directions: In a large pot over medium heat, cook beef until all red disappears, keeping beef in small chunks.
Pour off fat.
Add onion, green pepper, garlic, chili powder, oregano, basil and cumin and mix well.
Cook, stirring until onion and green pepper are tender, about 5 minutes.
Stir in sugar, salt, tomatoes, tomato sauce and tomato paste.
Mix well.
Bring to a boil, breaking up tomatoes with a fork.
Simmer slowly, covered, stirring occasionally until thickened and all flavors are blended, about 1 hour.
Add kidney beans, simmer covered, for about 15 minutes, or until hot.
Makes 12 to 16 servings.

_enrichment.description: A robust American chilli packed with tender ground beef, crisp bell peppers, and kidney beans in a spicy, tomato-based sauce. Hearty and warming, it’s a classic dish for chilly days and game-day gatherings.

title: Pasta With Tomato Vodka Sauce
ingredients: 1 (28 oz.) can plum tomatoes with juice
directions: Heat oil in a large skillet. Add onion; saute until tender (not brown), 5 minutes.
Stir in garlic.
Add tomatoes, vodka and red pepper; break up tomatoes with side of spoon.
Cook until sauce is reduced by 1/3 and thickened, 15 minutes.
Stir in cream; simmer, stirring (do not allow to boil) 5 minutes. Season with salt.

_enrichment.description: A velvety Italian classic with a twist—tomatoes, vodka, and cream create a rich, slightly spicy sauce that coats al dente pasta. The balance of tangy tomatoes and smooth cream makes this dish irresistibly comforting.

title: Beefsteak Pizzaiola
ingredients: 4 cloves fresh garlic, crushed and minced
salt and black pepper to taste
directions: While flank steak is partially frozen, slice into 1/4 x 2-inch strips. Allow to completely thaw.
Heat oil in a large skillet until almost smoking.
Sear steak on both sides.
Add onion, garlic, tomatoes, bell pepper and herbs.
Simmer, covered, for 1 hour, adding water if liquid boils off. Add mushrooms and wine. Continue to simmer until mushrooms are cooked. Serve with white rice.
Serves 6 people.

_enrichment.description: A hearty Italian dish where tender flank steak simmers in a robust tomato and red wine sauce with mushrooms and herbs. The slow-cooked flavours make it perfect for a cosy dinner or meal prep.

title: Italian-Style Baked Potatoes
directions: Peel potatoes; cut into approximately 1/4-inch thick slices. Mix seasonings and blend into olive oil.
Add potatoes and mix well.
Put potatoes into a greased baking pan.
Bake at 375° for 45 to 60 minutes or until potatoes are tender and golden brown.

_enrichment.description: Crispy on the outside and fluffy on the inside, these Italian-style baked potatoes are seasoned with garlic, herbs, and olive oil. A simple yet satisfying side or light meal for any season.

title: American Lasagne
directions: Brown beef and garlic in oil.
Add, then cover and simmer 20 minutes the tomato paste, tomatoes, salt, pepper and oregano. Cook noodles according to directions on package.
In an 11 x 7-inch dish, alternate layers of noodles, meat sauce, 12 ounces cottage cheese and Mozzarella cheese.
End with Mozzarella cheese on top.
Bake at 350° for 20 to 30 minutes.

_enrichment.description: A comforting American take on lasagne layered with rich beef sauce, creamy cottage cheese, and melted mozzarella. The perfect dish for a festive gathering or a hearty family meal.

title: Fettucini Broccoli
ingredients: 2 (20 oz.) pkg. frozen broccoli, thawed and drained or 1 head fresh broccoli
directions: Cook and drain noodles.
Meanwhile, in hot oil, saute garlic and broccoli for 5 minutes.
Add broth, basil, parsley, Parmesan cheese, cottage cheese and pepper.
Stir over low heat until blended (about 2 minutes).
Toss broccoli mixture with noodles. Serves 6 to 8 people.

_enrichment.description: A creamy, garlicky Italian-American dish where fettuccine meets tender broccoli in a brothy sauce with Parmesan and cottage cheese. Quick to prepare yet packed with flavour.

title: Herbed Spaghetti
directions: Combine ground beef, sausage, onion, and garlic in a Dutch oven; cook until meat is browned, stirring to crumble meat.
Drain off drippings.
Add remaining ingredients except spaghetti; bring to a boil.
Cover, reduce heat and simmer 2 hours, stirring occasionally.
Uncover and simmer an additional hour, stirring occasionally.
Remove bay leaf, and spoon over spaghetti.
Yield: 10 cups.

_enrichment.description: A hearty Italian-American ragù bursting with rich, savoury flavours from slow-cooked ground beef and pork sausage, simmered in a tangy tomato sauce with earthy mushrooms and aromatic herbs. Perfect for a comforting family meal.

title: Baked Catfish(Serves 4)
directions: Preheat oven to 400°.
Lightly oil a baking pan large enough to hold 4 catfish in a single layer.
Rub cut side of garlic into pan. Combine beaten egg and milk in shallow dish.
Combine bread crumbs, Parmesan cheese and seasonings in another dish.
Dip each fillet into egg mixture and roll in Parmesan mixture; coat completely. Arrange fillets on baking pan and drizzle remaining oil on top. Bake 20 minutes until fish is flaky.

_enrichment.description: Crispy, golden-baked catfish fillets with a Parmesan and herb crust, offering a delicate balance of light, flaky fish and rich, cheesy crunch. A classic Southern-inspired dish with Mediterranean flair.

title: Low-Fat Lasagna
ingredients: 2 pkg. low-fat Healthy Choice Mozzarella cheese
2 pkg. low-fat Healthy Choice Cheddar cheese
oregano, salt and pepper to taste
directions: Cook noodles; add olive oil to prevent them from sticking together.
Brown ground beef, onion and bell pepper. Drain fat from meat by putting in a strainer and "washing" meat with hot tap water.
Put meat back in skillet once drained and add mushrooms, sauce, oregano, salt and pepper.
Spray large pan with Pam.
Layer sauce first, noodles second, cottage cheese, then Mozzarella and/or Cheddar.
Start over with sauce.
Continue until pan is full.

_enrichment.description: A lighter take on lasagna, layered with lean beef, fresh vegetables, and low-fat cheeses for a creamy, comforting dish that doesn’t compromise on flavour. Ideal for health-conscious food lovers.

title: Garden Vegetable Pasta
ingredients: 1/8 tsp. garlic powder or 1 clove garlic, minced
1 c. cherry tomatoes, sliced in half
directions: In a 10-inch skillet over medium heat in hot oil, cook mushrooms and green onions with garlic until tender and liquid is evaporated, stirring often.
Stir in mixed vegetables, tomatoes, half and half, cheese and basil.
Add spaghetti.
Heat through, stirring occasionally.

_enrichment.description: A vibrant garden pasta dish featuring fresh vegetables, cherry tomatoes, and fragrant basil, tossed in a creamy Parmesan sauce. Light yet satisfying, perfect for a quick summer meal.

title: Turkey Slow Grilled
directions: Preheat covered barbecue grill to 300°.
Move coals to side and place pan large enough to catch all drippings under the grate. Wash and pat dry bird.
Blend herbs and butter to a paste. (Experiment with whatever herbs you like or, better yet, fresh herbs!)
Separate the skin from the breast from the back.
Insert paste and rub between the skin and meat.
Grill for 10 minutes per pound.
Fresh cooks faster!
Turkey is so moist and tender!

_enrichment.description: Juicy, herb-infused turkey slow-grilled to perfection, with tender meat and aromatic butter and herb paste. A festive centrepiece for gatherings or a special Sunday roast.

title: Too Easy Chicken Parmesan
ingredients: 2 lb. boneless, skinless chicken breasts, pounded thin
directions: Dip chicken into eggs, then into bread crumbs to coat thoroughly.
In large skillet, place chicken in hot oil and sprinkle with salt and pepper.
Brown chicken on both sides; remove from skillet and drain on paper towels.
Pour 1 cup sauce in a 13 x 9-inch baking dish.
Layer chicken and top with remaining sauce. Sprinkle with cheeses.
Bake for 45 minutes, or until thoroughly cooked.
Serves 8.

_enrichment.description: A delicious recipe for Too Easy Chicken Parmesan.

title: Pizza Beef Casserole
ingredients: 1/4 tsp. oregano, basil and seasoned salt
directions: Mix together the first 5 ingredients well; do not brown meat. Spread meat mixture into a 9 x 13-inch pan.
Spread with tomato sauce.
Place Mozzarella cheese on top.
Top with oregano, basil, seasoned salt and Parmesan cheese.
Bake in a 350° oven for about 60 minutes.
Nice served with tossed salad and Italian bread.

_enrichment.description: A delicious recipe for Pizza Beef Casserole.

title: Scalloped Zucchini And Potatoes
directions: Spread sliced potatoes over bottom of lightly greased 9 x 13-inch loaf pan.
Saute onions in oil with garlic until softened. Mix together basil, oregano and thyme.
Add 1/2 of mixture to onions.
Distribute onions evenly over potatoes; dribble with buttermilk.
Arrange one layer of zucchini slices to cover potato-onion mixture.
Sprinkle with remaining herbs; salt and pepper lightly.
Spread Cheddar cheese evenly over zucchini, then sprinkle on Parmesan cheese.
Cover with foil; bake in a 375° oven for 35 to 40 minutes, or until potatoes are tender.

_enrichment.description: A delicious recipe for Scalloped Zucchini And Potatoes.

title: Mom Mom'S Rice Lasagne
directions: Beat egg. Combine with Ricotta and season to taste with salt and pepper.
Cover bottom of casserole dish with sauce. Add a layer of rice. Add a layer of Ricotta.
Add sauce and a layer of Mozzarella and some Parmesan. Repeat until all ingredients are used.
Put sauce on top and sprinkle with Parmesan. Bake at 350° for 20 minutes or until bubbly.

_enrichment.description: A delicious recipe for Mom Mom'S Rice Lasagne.

title: Seafood Pizza Spread
directions: Drain crabmeat (if using canned).
Blend cream cheese, lemon juice, Worcestershire sauce, mayonnaise, onion and garlic powder. Spread on a round serving plate.
Spread cocktail sauce over cream cheese.
Sprinkle shredded crabmeat and shrimp on top. Refrigerate several hours before serving.
Serve with crackers.

_enrichment.description: A delicious recipe for Seafood Pizza Spread.

title: French Onion Soup
ingredients: 1 qt. beef stock (3 cans Campbell's beef stock)
1 qt. chicken stock (3 cans Campbell's chicken stock)
1 tsp. black pepper (you may wish to use less)
salt to taste (you may not want any)
directions: Heat butter in heavy kettle.
Add sliced onions; brown well, stirring constantly.
Add beef and chicken stock, the Worcestershire sauce, bay leaf, garlic salt and pepper.
Allow to simmer for 40 minutes.
Remove the bay leaf; add salt.
Prepare with a slice of toasted French bread (1/2-inch thick).
Add a thick slice of Swiss cheese (1/4-inch thick), overlapping the bowl.
Put under the broiler until cheese is melted and bubbly.

_enrichment.description: A quintessential French bistro classic, this rich and comforting onion soup is caramelised to perfection with a deep umami flavour. Topped with melted Gruyère cheese on toasted bread, it's a hearty dish that warms the soul.

title: Diane'S Quick Quiche
directions: To make a grilled cheese sandwich properly you must first decide what kind of bread to use, white, wheat, rye or raisin..raisin?
Then consider the kind of cheese you have available, American, Swiss, Cheddar, Limburger..Limburger?
You will also need butter, or oleo, or lard.. lard?
A kitchen countertop will be useful, as well as a knife, spatula, an iron skillet with a lid or cover, a stove and a helper to clean up the mess when you are through.

_enrichment.description: A quick and easy quiche inspired by American classics, featuring a flaky crust, creamy eggs, and a medley of Swiss cheese, bacon, and fresh herbs. Perfect for a light brunch or a weeknight dinner.

title: Cranberry Souffle
ingredients: 1 large or 2 small cans mandarin oranges
directions: Mix gelatin and salt thoroughly in small pan.
Add water and stir constantly over medium-low heat until gelatin is dissolved. Remove from heat and stir in lemon juice and rind.
Chill in refrigerator.
While gelatin is chilling, beat egg whites, gradually adding sugar and beating after each addition.
Set aside.
Remove chilled gelatin from fridge and beat it up a little until fluffy if too firm.
Add cranberry sauce, then fold the mixture into the egg whites.
Add oranges and nuts.
Pour into a lightly greased mold and chill thoroughly.
When ready to serve, dip mold in warm water and invert on plate.

_enrichment.description: A vibrant and airy cranberry soufflé with a tangy citrus twist, lightened with whipped egg whites and studded with walnuts and mandarin oranges. A festive dessert that’s as beautiful as it is delicious.

title: Victorian Baked French Toast
directions: Cook brown sugar, butter and corn syrup in small saucepan until butter melts.
Pour into 9 x 13-inch pan.
Layer bread in pan, squeezing together to fit.
Mix eggs, milk and cinnamon. Pour over bread (you can cover and refrigerate overnight).
Bake at 350° for 30 minutes or until center is done and lightly browned.

_enrichment.description: A nostalgic British-inspired baked French toast, drenched in a buttery caramel sauce and spiced with cinnamon. A decadent yet simple dish that’s ideal for lazy weekend brunches or special occasions.

title: Au Gratin Potatoes
ingredients: 4 medium potatoes, peeled and cut in 1/4-inch slices
directions: Spray 2-quart shallow baking dish with cooking spray and set aside.
Cover potatoes with cold water in a pot and bring to a boil.
Cook until potatoes are tender, about 10 minutes.
Drain and set aside.
While potatoes are cooking, heat oil over medium heat in a large nonstick skillet.
Add onions and garlic; cook until onions are softened and beginning to brown (about 15 minutes). Spread thin layer of potatoes in baking dish, salt, pepper and onion mixture.
Repeat layers ending with onions.
Pour in milk. Sprinkle with parsley flakes on top.
Bake until potatoes are slightly brown.
Bake (preheated) at 400° for about 20 minutes.

_enrichment.description: A classic French-style potato gratin, layered with creamy milk, crispy onions, and fragrant garlic. Baked until golden and bubbly, it’s a rich and comforting side dish perfect for any meal.

title: Quiche Lorraine
ingredients: 1 unbaked 9-inch pie crust (I use Pet Ritz deep dish pie shell)
directions: Sprinkle cheese and bacon into unbaked 9-inch pie shell.
Beat very well remaining ingredients together and pour over cheese mixture.
Bake in a preheated 375° oven for 45 minutes or until firm and browned.
Cool 10 minutes and then cut into wedges and serve hot.

_enrichment.description: A classic French savoury tart from Lorraine, blending rich Swiss cheese, smoky bacon, and a creamy egg custard in a buttery pastry shell. Its hearty, umami-packed flavours make it a timeless brunch or dinner staple.

title: French Silk Pie
directions: Cream butter and sugar on high speed for at least 10 minutes. Add melted chocolate and continue beating on high speed for another 10 minutes.
Add eggs, one at a time, beating on high speed for 10 minutes between each egg.
Add vanilla.
Mix well.
Mixture wil be very creamy and rather thin.
Pour into cooled, baked 8-inch pie crust and place in refrigerator until firm, 2 to 3 hours.
Serve very thin slices since this is very rich!
Garnish with whipped cream.

_enrichment.description: A decadent American-French dessert with a velvety chocolate filling, born from the 1950s. Its ultra-rich, silky texture and deep cocoa flavour make it a showstopper for special occasions.

title: Carol'S French Fried Shrimp
directions: Combine ingredients, except shrimp; beat.
Peel shell from shrimp, leaving last section and tail intact (optional). Butterfly shrimp by cutting almost through at center of back without severing ends.
Remove black vein.
Dry shrimp thoroughly between paper towels.
Dip into batter.
Fry in deep, hot fat (375°) until golden brown.
Drain on paper towels.
Serve immediately with lemon wedges and cocktail sauce or ketchup.

_enrichment.description: A Southern-inspired French classic of crispy fried shrimp, coated in a light, slightly sweet batter. The golden crunch and tender shrimp make it a beloved pub or seaside treat.

title: Asparagus Onion Quiche Squares
ingredients: 1 can cream of asparagus soup, undiluted
1 1/2 c. finely shredded Cheddar cheese, divided
directions: Line a 9x13 dish or pan with the crescent dough, sealing the edges of the crescents together.
Push dough about 1/2 inch up the sides of the pan.
In a mixing bowl, combine the onion, soup, 1 cup Cheddar, eggs, salt and lemon pepper seasoning.
Beat well. Pour into baking pan and sprinkle remaining cheese over the top. Bake in a 375 degree oven for about 30 minutes.
Remove from oven and allow to cool slightly before cutting into small squares.

_enrichment.description: A quick and comforting American twist on quiche, featuring tender asparagus, sharp cheddar, and a flaky crescent crust. Its light yet satisfying profile is perfect for spring brunches or meal prep.

title: Crab Quiche
ingredients: 2 (6 oz.) pkg. frozen crabmeat, thawed and drained or canned crabmeat, drained
directions: Prick bottom and sides of pastry with a fork.
Bake at 400° for 3 minutes; remove from oven and gently prick with a fork. Bake pastry shell an additional 5 minutes; set aside.
Saute onions in butter until tender; set aside.
Combine eggs and cream in large bowl, stirring well.
Stir in onions, crabmeat, cheese, sherry, salt and pepper.

_enrichment.description: A luxurious seafood quiche hailing from the American South, combining sweet crabmeat, sharp Swiss cheese, and a sherry-infused custard in a buttery pastry shell. Its refined yet comforting taste is ideal for festive gatherings.

title: Carrot Souffle
directions: Put cooked carrots in mixer, add other ingredients except the confectioner sugar.
Pour into a greased casserole dish.
Bake at 350° for 40 to 45 minutes.
Sprinkle top with 2 to 3 tablespoons confectioner sugar.

_enrichment.description: A light and fluffy carrot-based dessert soufflé, sweetened with sugar and enriched with eggs and butter. Perfect for a winter dessert.

title: Seafood Crepes(Serves 6)
ingredients: 8 oz. small scallops or cut up large scallops (fresh or frozen, thawed)
3 oz. small shelled shrimp (fresh or frozen, thawed)
directions: In a 2-quart saucepan, melt butter over medium heat.
Mix in flour; cook and stir 1 minute.
Gradually whisk in milk, sherry, chicken granules and salt; cook and stir until smooth (about 8 minutes).
Gradually stir in scallops, shrimp and mushrooms.
Cook, stirring gently, just until scallops and shrimp are cooked through; only about 10 minutes.

_enrichment.description: Delicate crepes filled with a rich seafood and mushroom mixture, baked with Gruyère cheese and topped with parsley. Ideal for a summer dinner party.

title: French Market Soup
ingredients: ham, sausage or chicken or a combination of leftover meats
2 cans tomatoes, chopped with own juice
directions: After soaking beans, add water and cook beans according to package directions for about 1 hour.
Add rest of ingredients and bring to a boil.
Cover and simmer for another 2 to 3 hours (or until beans are tender), stirring occasionally.
Great alone or can serve over rice.
Freezes well.

_enrichment.description: A hearty bean soup with ham, vegetables, and seasonings, simmered slowly for a rich, savoury flavour. Perfect for a winter meal.

title: French Toast Pockets
ingredients: 1 loaf Rhodes white bread, baked, cooled and sliced in pieces (may be baked a day ahead)
directions: Combine cream cheese, vanilla, pecans and 2 tablespoons sugar. Set aside.
Blend eggs, milk, nutmeg and 4 tablespoons sugar.
Set aside.
Combine cornflakes with coconut and set aside.
Cut through the top and along one side of each bread slice.
Spread a rounded tablespoon of cream cheese filling inside each pocket.

_enrichment.description: Sweet and creamy French toast pockets filled with a spiced cream cheese mixture and topped with crunchy cornflakes. Great for a weekend brunch.

title: Impossible Bacon Quiche
directions: Preheat oven to 400°.
Lightly grease a 10-inch pie plate. Sprinkle bacon, cheese and onion in pie plate.
Beat milk, Bisquick, eggs, salt and pepper in a medium size bowl with electric mixer until smooth, about 1 minute.

Pour into pie plate. Bake in hot oven (400°) for 35 minutes or until golden brown and knife inserted halfway between center and edges comes out clean. Garnish with tomato slices and bacon strips if you wish. Refrigerate remaining quiche.

_enrichment.description: A quick and easy quiche with crispy bacon, Swiss cheese, and onions, baked in a Bisquick crust. Ideal for a weeknight dinner.

title: Zucchini Quiche
directions: Saute zucchini in margarine with onion until tender.
Mix egg, 1/2 of cheese and spices in bowl.
Add zucchini and onion to egg mixture.
Spread crescent rolls (raw) in pie pan to make crust. Pour egg and vegetable mixture over crust.
Top with remaining cheese.
Bake at 350° until golden brown, approximately 20 minutes.

_enrichment.description: A savoury zucchini quiche with mozzarella and herbs, baked in a crescent roll crust. Perfect for a light summer meal.

title: Easy Quiche
directions: Grate Cheddar cheese.
Brown meat and drain off fat.
Blend mayonnaise, milk, eggs and cornstarch.
Stir in grated cheese. Bake for 35 to 40 minutes at 350° (also good to use ham and Swiss cheese or sausage and cheese).
Also you can spray your pie dish with Pam and not use pie shell.

_enrichment.description: A simple quiche with ground beef, Cheddar cheese, and a creamy egg custard, baked in a pie shell. Great for a quick dinner.

title: French Onion Soup In Crock-Pot
ingredients: 1 qt. beef bouillon or brown stock
directions: Pour bouillon or stock in crock-pot.
Cook onions slowly in large skillet in butter.
Cover and let cook about 15 minutes. Uncover and add salt, sugar and flour.
Stir well.
Add to stock in crock-pot.
Cover and cook on low 6 to 8 hours (high 3 hours). Before serving, top with grated cheese.
Recipe may be doubled for 5-quart pot.
If you have ovensafe bowls, place finished soup in individual bowls.
Place slice of Italian bread and slice of Mozzarella cheese on top and place bowl under broiler until cheese melts.

_enrichment.description: Classic French onion soup slow-cooked in a crock-pot, topped with melted Parmesan cheese. A winter favourite.

title: French Beef Stew With Vegetables
ingredients: 1 tsp. fresh or 1/2 tsp. dried thyme
directions: Preheat oven to 250°.
Place roast in a Dutch oven and arrange potatoes, carrots and onion around and over roast.
Sprinkle herbs and seasonings over top. Drizzle wine over top. Roast for 4 1/2 hours, covered.
Place meat and vegetables on platter. Place pan juices on high heat. Blend margarine and flour to a smooth paste and stir into juices.
Cook until thickened. Spoon some over roast and sprinkle with parsley.
Pour remaining gravy into gravy boat.
Makes 8 servings.

_enrichment.description: A slow-cooked beef stew with vegetables and red wine, served with a rich gravy. Perfect for a cosy autumn or winter meal.

title: Bacon And Swiss Quiche
directions: Fry bacon.
Spread frozen pie crust with 1 tablespoon melted butter.
Crumble bacon into bottom of pie and add Swiss strips. Mix 2 cups heavy cream with 4 eggs and 1/4 teaspoon salt and pepper.
Pour over bacon and Swiss.
Bake at 425° for first 15 minutes.
Drop temperature to 350° and bake another 30 minutes.

_enrichment.description: A rich quiche with crispy bacon and Swiss cheese, baked in a flaky pie crust. Ideal for a weekend brunch or dinner.

title: Eggplant Provencale
ingredients: 3 1/2 to 4 c. eggplant, peeled and diced
1 clove garlic, pressed or garlic salt
directions: Macerate eggplant with salt for about 30 minutes to remove liquid and soften.
Drain.
Saute in a film of olive oil, green pepper and onion; don't brown.
Set aside.
Dry eggplant on paper towels and saute in film of olive oil, 2 to 4 minutes, to brown. Add onion, pepper and remaining ingredients to eggplant.
Cook down to thicken.
Serve as a vegetable or over cooked pasta.

_enrichment.description: A Provençal-style eggplant dish with marinara sauce, garlic, and herbs. A light yet flavourful summer dish.

title: Prune Souffle
directions: Mix the first 6 ingredients.
Remove the stones from the cooked prunes and force the pulp through a sieve.
Add lemon juice and prune pulp.
Stir in dry ingredients. Add yolks of eggs, beaten until light and lemon colored.
Fold in whites beaten until stiff.
Turn into greased baking dish and bake in a slow oven. Set baking dish in pan of water.

_enrichment.description: A light and airy prune soufflé with nuts and spices, baked until golden. A unique dessert for special occasions.

title: Bea Steven'S Cottage Cheese Souffle Salad
ingredients: 1 c. chopped raw spinach or thawed frozen
directions: Dissolve Jell-O in water, vinegar and salt.
Add mayonnaise and beat until smooth.
Pour into refrigerator tray and place in freezing compartment 15 to 20 minutes until mixture is set about 1-inch from edges, but soft in center.
Turn into bowl and beat again until fluffy.
Fold in spinach, cottage cheese, celery and onion.
Turn into mold and chill until firm.

_enrichment.description: A creamy cottage cheese salad with lemon Jell-O, vegetables, and mayonnaise, set in a mould. A retro-style dish.

title: Sweet Potato Souffle
ingredients: 3 lb. sweet potatoes, peeled, cooked and mashed
directions: Use electric mixer on medium speed; blend in eggs with 1/4 cup brown sugar and 1/4 cup melted butter.
Add cinnamon, salt and 2 tablespoons orange juice.
Beat until fluffy and moist.
If needed, add more orange juice.
Pour into 2-quart casserole; cover with pecans.
Sprinkle remaining sugar and butter; bake, uncovered, 20 minutes in oven at 375°.

_enrichment.description: A sweet and spiced sweet potato soufflé with pecans, baked until golden. A perfect autumn or winter dessert.

title: Blintzes Or Crepes (Pancakes)
directions: Mix together all ingredients until smooth.
Grease an 8-inch skillet lightly with 1 teaspoon vegetable shortening, heat, pour enough batter to coat bottom.
Tilt skillet to cover evenly and cook until delicate brown.
Loosen and remove with spatula. Continue until all batter is used up.

_enrichment.description: Thin, delicate pancakes or blintzes, perfect for filling with sweet or savoury ingredients. A versatile brunch dish.

title: Salmon Quiche
ingredients: 1 can (7 3/4 oz.) salmon, well drained or 1 c. cooked salmon
directions: In small skillet, melt butter; add onion, celery and green pepper.
Saute until onion is translucent.
Arrange vegetable mixture evenly in bottom of baked pie shell.
Crumble salmon in an even layer over vegetables; sprinkle evenly with cheese.

_enrichment.description: A savoury pie featuring a flaky pastry shell filled with a creamy egg custard, tender salmon, and melted cheese, garnished with fresh dill and green onion.

title: French Breakfast Puffs
directions: In a mixing bowl, combine flour, 1/2 cup sugar, baking powder, nutmeg, and salt.
Make a well in the center of the dry ingredients.
In another bowl, Beat egg slightly; stir in milk and 1/3 cup melted butter or margarine, add egg mixture to flour mixture.
Stir just until moistened (the batter may be lumpy). Lightly grease muffin cups.
Fill cups about 2/3 full with batter. Bake in a 350° oven for 20 minutes or until muffins are golden. Meanwhile, in a shallow bowl combine the 1/4 cup sugar and cinnamon.
Immediately dip tops of hot muffins into the 1/4 cup melted butter or margarine, then into the cinnamon-sugar mixture until coated.
Serve warm.
Serves 12.

_enrichment.description: Sweet, spiced muffins with a tender crumb, topped with a cinnamon-sugar butter glaze for a delightful breakfast treat.

title: Poteau French Dressing
directions: This should be made just before serving.
Rub the bottom of a small bowl with onion or garlic.
Put in 1/2 teaspoon of salt, a dash of Tabasco sauce and a tablespoon of cold water (to dissolve salt).
Stir until salt is completely dissolved.
Now gradually add, stirring constantly, 6 tablespoons of oil and 1 teaspoon of vinegar or lemon juice.
Beat quickly until well mixed.

_enrichment.description: A zesty, tangy French dressing made fresh with lemon juice, oil, and a hint of Tabasco, perfect for salads or marinades.

title: Cauliflower Au Gratin
directions: Divide the cauliflower into sprigs.
Soak in salt water for a while and rinse well.
Boil up in slightly salted water.
Make a white sauce with the butter, flour and milk, adding enough cauliflower water to make a soft, smooth dough.
Allow to cool slightly and beat in the egg yolks one by one.
Finally, fold in the stiffly beaten egg whites to which the corn flour has been added.
Season to taste and line the bottom of a ovenproof dish with the gratin mixture.
Add the cauliflower and pour over the remainder of the gratin mixture.
Bake at 350° for about 1 hour testing with a knitting needle.
Serve with cold or melted butter.

_enrichment.description: A rich and creamy cauliflower bake with a golden, cheesy topping, combining tender florets in a silky white sauce.

title: Eggplant Souffle
directions: Peel eggplant and cook in boiling water 7 to 10 minutes. Drain and season with dash of onion powder and dill.
Mix remaining ingredients with 1/3 cup cracker crumbs and combine with eggplant.
Put in 2-quart greased casserole.
Sprinkle remaining cracker crumbs on top and dot with butter and dash of paprika. Bake at 350° for 30 minutes or until bubbly.
Serves 4 to 6.

_enrichment.description: A light and fluffy eggplant soufflé with a delicate herb flavour, baked until golden and served warm.

title: Breakfast Souffle
directions: Brown and drain sausage.
Beat eggs with milk, mustard and salt.
Cube bread and layer with sausage and cheese in a 9 x 13-inch baking pan.
Pour egg mixture over layers; cover with foil and refrigerate overnight.
Bake 45 minutes at 350° (20 minutes with foil on and 25 minutes with foil off).
Do not overbake.

_enrichment.description: A hearty breakfast bake with layers of sausage, cheese, and bread soaked in a creamy egg custard, baked to perfection.

title: Vegetable Quiche
directions: Saute onions, zucchini and garlic in margarine or oil. Prebake 1 pie crust until slightly cooked.
Mix together cheese, eggs, milk and dash of Tabasco sauce.
Add sauteed vegetables to egg mixture.
Pour into crust.
Bake at about 350° until set, about 45 to 60 minutes.
Sprinkle Parmesan cheese on top after it is baked.

_enrichment.description: A vibrant vegetable quiche with zucchini, onions, and tomatoes in a creamy egg custard, topped with Parmesan cheese.

title: Ham And Cheese Quiche
directions: Mix sour cream, milk, Bisquick, eggs, pepper and salt together; stir in ham, cheese and chilies.
Pour into deep pie plate.
Cook until inserted knife comes out clean at 400° (40 minutes).
Let stand 5 to 10 minutes before cutting.

_enrichment.description: A classic ham and cheese quiche with a rich custard filling, baked in a deep pie crust for a satisfying meal.

title: Cottage Cheese Quiche
directions: In blender, combine cottage cheese, eggs, milk, paprika and salt.
Line 10-inch pie shell with caraway seed and your favorite shredded cheese.
Pour in cottage cheese mixture.
Bake in 450° oven on lowest rack for 10 minutes.
Reduce heat to 350° and bake 20 to 25 minutes longer or until done when knife inserted in center comes out clean.

_enrichment.description: A simple yet flavourful quiche with cottage cheese, eggs, and a hint of caraway, baked in a crisp pastry shell.

title: Stuffed French Bread
directions: Slice off top of loaf of bread and carve out center (keep). Brown and drain ground beef, sausage, onion and bell pepper. Combine egg, milk and bread (center).
Add to meat.
Sprinkle grated Cheddar cheese on bottom of bread.
Add meat mixture on top of cheese.
Add cubes of Velveeta on top of meat.
Put on top loaf. Brush with butter.
Wrap in foil and bake at 350° for 30 minutes. Do not overbake.
Take out of foil and slice.

_enrichment.description: A hearty stuffed French bread loaf filled with seasoned ground beef, sausage, and melted cheese, perfect for sharing.

title: Avocado Corn Quiche
ingredients: 3 ears fresh or frozen corn on the cob, scraped of kernels
3/4 c. grated Havarti cheese (or any white cheese)
directions: *Using your favorite pie crust recipe fit the dough into a 10-inch false-bottom quiche pan.
Line with foil and fill with rice.
Bake 10 minutes in a preheated 400° oven.
Remove foil and rice and bake 5 minutes longer or until lightly colored.

_enrichment.description: A creamy avocado and corn quiche with a buttery crust, combining rich textures and fresh flavours.

title: Basic Crepes
directions: In mixer bowl, beat eggs lightly.
Add melted margarine or butter and mix.
Add remaining ingredients and beat until smooth. (Batter will be thin.)
Pour scant 1/4 cup butter into lightly greased and heated 8-inch crepe pan or skillet.
Tilt pan to coat bottom evenly with batter.
Cook over medium heat until brown; turn crepe to brown second side.
Cool pan slightly between crepes.
Spoon filling onto center of crepe and roll.

_enrichment.description: Thin, delicate French pancakes with a smooth, silky texture, ideal for both sweet and savoury fillings.

title: Ham And Onion Quiche
directions: Bake pie shells at 450° for 6 minutes.	Always prick bottom and sides of crusts with a	fork before baking.
Remove shells from oven; reduce heat to 350°.
Toss ham in 1 tablespoon flour, then sprinkle ham over bottom of pie shells.
Sprinkle Cheddar cheese over ham.
Beat remaining ingredients together and pour mixture into shells.
Bake at 350° for 45 minutes or until knife inserted comes out clean.

_enrichment.description: A savoury quiche with ham, cheese, and a hint of spice, baked in a flaky pastry shell for a comforting meal.

title: Leprechaun Quiche
ingredients: 8 slices bacon, diced and crisply fried
directions: Cook spinach according to package directions.
Unroll refrigerator rolls; press into 10-inch pie pan to form crust. Sprinkle Cheddar cheese evenly over the crust.
In a bowl, combine eggs, milk, Worcestershire sauce, salt, onion salt and pepper. Stir in creamed spinach, mushrooms and half the bacon.
Pour over crust.
Sprinkle with Parmesan cheese.
Bake in preheated 350° oven for 20 minutes.
Sprinkle remaining bacon over top of quiche. Bake an additional 20 to 25 minutes, or until knife inserted in center comes out clean.
Serves 6 to 8.

_enrichment.description: A festive quiche with creamed spinach, bacon, and cheese, baked in a crescent roll crust for a quick and easy meal.

title: Crepes(French Pancakes)
directions: Measure flour, sugar, baking powder and salt into bowl.
Stir in remaining ingredients.
Beat with rotary beater until smooth. For each crepe, lightly butter 8-inch skillet; heat over medium heat until butter is bubbly.
Pour scant 1/4 cupful batter into skillet.
Immediately rotate pan until batter covers bottom.
Cook until light brown; turn and brown other side.
May spread applesauce, strawberries, jelly or other spread and roll up and sprinkle with sugar.

_enrichment.description: Light and airy French pancakes with a tender crumb, perfect for sweet toppings or savoury fillings.

title: Fluffy French Toast
directions: Place 1 cup milk in shallow dish.
Combine beaten eggs, flour, salt, and baking powder in another shallow dish; add 1/3 cup milk and blend.	Melt shortening in skillet.
Dip bread, one slice at a time, into milk, then into egg mixture.
Turn to coat both sides of bread.
Place bread in skillet; cover and cook over low heat until bread is golden brown on one side.	Turn, cover, and cook until other side is golden brown.
Serve immediately.

_enrichment.description: A classic French-inspired breakfast dish with a light, airy texture, perfect for a leisurely weekend morning.

title: Grilled Cheese Sandwich
ingredients: 4 oz. cold Brie, cut into 1/8-inch thick slices
8 slices firm textured white bread, crusts removed
directions: In a heavy medium skillet, melt the butter over high heat. When the butter begins to brown, add the mushrooms and toss to coat.
Saute, stirring occasionally, until the mushrooms have browned around the edges, 2 to 3 minutes.
Add the green onions and cook for 1 minute longer. Transfer the mushrooms to a plate and season with salt and pepper.
Divide the cheese among 4 slices of bread.
Top each with the mushrooms, leaving about 1/4-inch from the edge of the bread. Add a tarragon sprig and cover with the remaining 4 slices of bread.
Preheat a cast-iron ridged griddle or skillet for 10 minutes over moderate heat. Grill the sandwiches for 4 minutes per side or until the bread is lightly browned and the cheese has melted.
Serve halved as a first course or quartered as an hors d'oeuvre.

_enrichment.description: A comforting American classic featuring melted Brie and sautéed mushrooms between buttery white bread, grilled to golden perfection.

title: French Pork Chops
ingredients: 3 scallions (green onions and tops), cut up
directions: Place pork chops into the butter mixture and saute until brown.
Remove the chops from butter and add the following to the butter: wine and mustard.
Mix all the liquid together and boil about 2 minutes until syrupy, then add whipping cream.
Boil 2 minutes.
Pour over meat on each individual plate and serve with saffron rice with a few baby French peas on top.
Garnish with a little chopped tomato placed on top of the meat.

_enrichment.description: Tender French pork chops glazed with a mustardy white wine and cream sauce, served with saffron rice for an elegant yet simple meal.

title: Easy Hamburger Quiche
ingredients: 1 1/2 c. shredded Cheddar or Swiss cheese
directions: Brown meat in skillet on medium heat.
Drain fat and set aside. Blend mayonnaise, milk, eggs and cornstarch until smooth.
Stir in meat, cheese, onion and pepper.
Turn mixture into pastry shell. Bake at 350° for 35 to 40 minutes or until golden brown on top and knife inserted in center comes out clean.
Garnish with sliced tomatoes.
Serve.

_enrichment.description: A quick and easy quiche with ground beef, Swiss cheese, and green onions, baked in a pastry shell for a hearty brunch or light dinner.

title: Chicken And Broccoli Au Gratin(Heart Healthy)
ingredients: 1 pkg. frozen broccoli spears, cooked and drained
1 chicken, cooked and cut in large cubes
1 c. (4 oz.) shredded low-fat or regular Monterey or sharp Cheddar cheese
directions: Melt butter in skillet.
Saute onions until transparent; remove from heat.
Stir in flour, salt, curry powder and pepper. Drain mushrooms, reserving liquid and add water to make 1/2 cup liquid.
Gradually stir in flour in the skillet.
Blend evaporated milk until smooth.
Add mushrooms; cook and stir over medium heat until sauce thickens.
Arrange broccoli and chicken in bottom of 13 x 9-inch baking dish.
Pour sauce over broccoli and chicken. Top with cheese.
Bake at 375° for 20 minutes or until bubbly around edges.
Serves 6.

_enrichment.description: A heart-healthy twist on a classic au gratin, featuring tender chicken and broccoli in a creamy curry-infused sauce.

title: Haddock Au Gratin
directions: Heat the oven to 400°.
In a small bowl, combine all ingredients except the fish.
Place the fish on individual squares of greased aluminum foil.
Spoon the crumb mixture over the fish. Seal the foil securely and bake for 25 to 30 minutes or until the fish flakes easily.
Makes six servings.

_enrichment.description: A simple and comforting British-style baked haddock topped with a cheesy breadcrumb crust, perfect for a light yet satisfying meal.

title: Crustless Vegetable Quiche
ingredients: 1 1/2 (6 oz.) shredded low-fat cheese (6 grams fat or less per oz.)
directions: In a covered medium saucepan, cook zucchini and onion in 2 to 3 tablespoons water for 5 to 6 minutes; drain.
In a large bowl, combine egg substitute, milk, flour, nutmeg, white pepper and zucchini mixture.
Pour into an 11 x 7-inch glass baking dish. Put glass baking dish into a 13 x 9-inch pan.
Bake at 325° for 1 hour.
Let stand for 5 to 10 minutes before serving.
Makes 4 servings.
Serving size: 3 1/2 x 5 1/2-inches.

_enrichment.description: A lighter, crustless quiche packed with vegetables and cheese, baked until golden for a healthy yet indulgent dish.

title: Copper Mountain Quiche
ingredients: 1 bunch green onions, trimmed and sliced
directions: Combine butter, cream cheese and 1 cup flour with a pastry blender until crumbly.
Press into pie plate to form crust. Refrigerate.
Preheat oven to 350°.
Cook spinach until tender. Drain and blot on paper towels to remove all excess water.
In a large bowl combine spinach and 2 Tablespoons flour.
Add remaining ingredients and mix well.
Pour mixture into chilled shell and bake 1 hour or until set.

_enrichment.description: A rich and creamy quiche with spinach, cheddar, Swiss cheese, and bacon, baked in a buttery crust for a decadent brunch dish.

title: No Fry French Toast(Served At Prayer Breakfast)
directions: Spray a baking sheet with nonstick spray coating.
In a shallow bowl, combine egg, milk and vanilla.
Dip bread slices in egg mixture just long enough to coat both sides.
Place in baking sheet.
Bake in a 450° oven about 6 minutes or until bread is lightly browned.
Turn bread over and bake 5 to 8 minutes more or until golden.

_enrichment.description: A healthier take on French toast, baked instead of fried, with a hint of orange and honey for a sweet, fluffy breakfast.

title: Green Beans Au Gratin
directions: Steam green beans over lightly salted water until tender.
Set aside.
Melt butter in skillet and saute onions until brown. Slowly add flour, water, salt, pepper and lemon juice.
Let simmer 3 to 4 minutes.
Stir in beans and sour cream.
Mix thoroughly. Place the mixture in a buttered casserole and top with Cheddar cheese and bread crumbs.
Bake at 350° for 1/2 hour.

_enrichment.description: A creamy, cheesy green bean bake with a crispy breadcrumb topping, perfect as a side dish or light main.

title: Bacon And Cheese Quiche
ingredients: 8 slices thick sourdough bread, take off crust
12 slices bacon, cooked and crumbled in large pieces
directions: Arrange half of the bread in a greased 9 x 13-inch pan.
Then layer on the bacon, half of the cheese, the rest of the bread, then the rest of the cheese.
Beat together with a wire whisk eggs, milk and seasonings.
Pour over bread, then drizzle melted butter over the top.
Cover and refrigerate overnight.
Bake at 350° for 50 to 60 minutes.
Cut into serving size pieces and serve with tossed green salad.

_enrichment.description: A hearty bacon and cheese quiche with layers of bread, perfect for a filling breakfast or brunch dish.

title: French Market Doughnuts
ingredients: 1 pkg. active dry or cake yeast
directions: Scald milk; add granulated sugar, salt and nutmeg.
Cool to lukewarm.
Sprinkle or crumble yeast into warm water, stirring until yeast is dissolved (use lukewarm water for cake yeast).
To lukewarm milk mixture, add oil, egg and dissolved yeast, blending with spoon.
Add flour gradually, beating well.
Cover with waxed paper, then clean towel and let rise in warm place until double in size.

_enrichment.description: Light, fluffy doughnuts dusted with powdered sugar, inspired by French patisserie techniques for a sweet treat.

title: French Potato Salad
ingredients: 8 to 10 medium all-purpose potatoes (do not use Idaho or Russets as they crumble when sliced)
directions: Scrub the potatoes and boil them in their jackets in salted water until just tender when pierced with a very sharp kitchen fork.
Drain and shake over low heat to dry thoroughly.
Peel them as hot as you can handle them.
The potatoes should still be warm when the dressing is added.
Slice them in 1/8-inch rounds and place in a large mixing bowl.
As you put in each layer of potatoes, sprinkle with some of the wine.
Next, beat the vinegar, salt and mustard powder together in a small bowl with a wire whisk or electric mixer.

_enrichment.description: A classic French potato salad with a tangy wine and vinegar dressing, served warm for a refreshing side dish.

title: Vegetable Souffle
directions: According to what vegetables you decide to use, blanch mixed vegetables for 3 minutes and cook squash in boiling water for 10 minutes.
Drain well.
In saucepan over heat, melt butter, then add flour and stir in milk.
Cook until thickened.
Remove from heat and set aside.
Beat egg yolks until fairly stiff and add to sauce.
Add seasonings and mix well.
Add vegetables of choice. Beat egg whites until stiff and fold into mixture.

_enrichment.description: A light and airy vegetable soufflé with a creamy cheese sauce, perfect for a sophisticated yet simple meal.

title: No Bake French Cream Cake
ingredients: 2 c vanilla wafer crumbs divided (about 1/2 pound)
directions: Place 1 cup crumbs in ungreased 8 inch square pan. Thoroughly cream butter and sugar.
Add eggs and beat well. Spread carefully over crumbs.
Combine cream, nuts and pineapple. Spread over creamed mixture, then sprinkle with remaining crumbs. Let stand 18 to 24 hours in refrigerator.
Cut into squares and serve.
May top with a scoop of vanilla ice cream.
Serves 12 - 16.

_enrichment.description: A no-bake French-inspired cream cake with layers of vanilla wafer crumbs, custard, and whipped cream, topped with nuts and pineapple.

title: Oven French Fries
directions: Preheat oven to 475°.
Scrub potatoes, cut into long strips about 1/2-inch wide.
Dry strips thoroughly on paper towels.
Toss in a bowl with oil as if making a salad.
Toss with optional spices.
Spread potatoes in a single layer on a cookie sheet and place in preheated oven for 35 minutes.
Turn strips periodically to brown on all sides.
If a crispier, browner potato is desired, run under broiler for a minute or two.
Sprinkle with salt. Yield: 6 servings, 80 calories per serving.
This is a low cholesterol, reduced fat recipe.

_enrichment.description: Crispy oven-baked potato fries seasoned with aromatic herbs and Parmesan, perfect as a side dish or snack.

title: French Toast With Brandied Lemon Butter
ingredients: 12 slices bread (French), sliced and left out overnight to dry out
directions: In a shallow dish, beat eggs, sugar, salt, milk and vanilla. Soak bread in mixture.
Heat butter over medium-high heat and cook each slice until lightly browned on each side.
Serve with maple syrup or Brandied Lemon Butter and lemon slices.
Sprinkle with powdered sugar.
Serves 6.

_enrichment.description: Golden-brown French toast served with brandied lemon butter and a dusting of powdered sugar, ideal for a leisurely brunch.

title: Classic French Silk Pie
ingredients: any 9-inch pie shell (I like a chocolate crumb crust)
3 oz. unsweetened chocolate, melted and cooled
directions: Beat sugar and butter until light and fluffy.
Stir in vanilla, cream of tartar and chocolate.
Beat in eggs until light and fluffy, about 3 minutes.
Pour into pie shell.
Refrigerate to set, 3 to 4 hours, or cover with plastic wrap and freeze at least 8 hours.
If pie is frozen, remove from freezer 15 minutes prior to serving.
Beat whipping cream and powdered sugar in a chilled 1 1/2-quart bowl until stiff.
Top pie with cream and garnish with chopped nuts or chocolate curls.

_enrichment.description: Silky-smooth chocolate pie with a rich, velvety texture, topped with whipped cream for a decadent dessert experience.

title: Shrimp And Crabmeat Au Gratin
ingredients: 1/4 lb. shrimp, boiled, peeled and chopped
directions: Saute celery and onions in butter until wilted.
Blend in flour, mixing well.
Gradually add milk, stirring constantly.
Add egg yolk, salt and black pepper.
Cook over medium heat for 5 minutes.
Put crabmeat and shrimp in a mixing bowl; pour the cooked sauce over the crab and shrimp and mix well.
Transfer half of mixture to lightly-greased 2-quart casserole dish.
Top with half the cheese; repeat layers.
Sprinkle top with bread crumbs. Bake at 375° for 10 to 15 minutes or until lightly browned. Yield:
4 to 6 servings.

_enrichment.description: A creamy seafood gratin with crabmeat and shrimp, baked until bubbly and golden for a luxurious main course.

title: Country Sausage And Potatoes Au Gratin
ingredients: 1 lb. smoked sausage, cut into 1/2 inch slices
1 pkg. Betty Crocker au gratin potatoes
4 medium carrots, cut into 2-inch julienne strips (about 2 c.)
1 pkg. (10 oz.) frozen chopped broccoli, thawed and drained
directions: Cook and stir sausage and onion in oil in 10-inch or 12-inch skillet until onion is tender, about 5 minutes.
Stir in potatoes, sauce mix, water and pepper.
Heat to boiling; reduce heat.
Cover and simmer, stirring occasionally, 10 minutes.
Stir in carrot strips; cover and simmer until carrots are crisp-tender and potatoes are tender, about 10 minutes.
Stir in broccoli and cheese.
Cover and cook until broccoli is heated through and cheese is melted, 5 to 10 minutes.
Makes 4 to 5 servings.

_enrichment.description: Hearty sausage and potato gratin with melted Cheddar, perfect for a comforting family meal.

title: Quiche Stuffed Mushrooms
directions: Rinse mushrooms, pat dry and remove stems.
Chop stems (makes about 1 1/2 cups) and set aside.
In large skillet, saute bacon until crisp; drain on paper towel, crumble and set aside.
Pour bacon drippings from skillet.
In the same skillet, melt butter. Use about 4 tablespoons of melted butter to brush the outsides of the mushroom caps and place in large shallow 13 x 9 x 2-inch baking pan.
To the butter remaining, add onion and saute, then add mushroom stems and saute 2 minutes longer.
Remove from the heat. In bowl, combine eggs, cream, cheese and salt.
Stir in bacon and mushroom mixture.
Fill caps.
Bake at 350° for 30 minutes.

_enrichment.description: Mushroom caps stuffed with a savoury mixture of bacon, cheese, and eggs, baked to perfection.

title: Julia Child'S Sweet Onion Quiche
ingredients: 3 large eggs, blended with enough milk or cream to make 1 1/2 c. liquid
1/4 c. lightly pressed down grated cheese
directions: Preheat oven to 375°.
Heat olive oil in a large saute pan. Saute sliced onions until soft, but not browned.
In a separate pan, heat butter and saute shallots briefly.
Add to onions and continue cooking until the mixture is tender.
Season with salt, pepper and nutmeg; let cool.

_enrichment.description: A classic quiche with sweet onions, cheese, and a buttery crust, offering a delicate balance of sweet and savoury.

title: Tomatoes Au Gratin(Low-Fat)
directions: Cut the tomatoes in half crosswise.
Gently squeeze the pieces to remove the excess juice.
Place cut-side up in a small casserole dish.
Bake at 350° for 10 minutes.
In a small bowl, mix the bread crumbs, Parmesan, parsley, lemon juice, oil and garlic powder.
Carefully spoon the mixture over the tomatoes and into the cavities left by the seeds.
Bake for 15 minutes or until the tomatoes are softened and the crumbs are lightly browned.

_enrichment.description: Low-fat tomatoes topped with a crispy breadcrumb and Parmesan mixture, baked until golden.

title: French Dip Sandwiches
directions: Remove all fat from roast and put roast in slow cooker. Combine all remaining ingredients except bread and pour over roast. Add water to almost cover roast.
Cover and cook on low for 10 to 12 hours or until tender.
Remove meat from broth; reserve broth. Shred meat with a fork.
Cut rolls in halves, spread with butter and grill until brown.
Place meat on rolls; dip in reserved broth while eating.

_enrichment.description: Tender beef roast slow-cooked in a savoury broth, served on toasted rolls for a hearty sandwich.

title: French Pastry Pie Crust
directions: Mix flour, salt, Crisco and sugar until it resembles corn meal.
In small bowl, put egg, water and vinegar.
Mix well with fork.
Add to flour mixture and mix until well blended, then roll out for crusts.
Makes 4 single crusts or 2 double crust pies. Freezes well.

_enrichment.description: A versatile, flaky pastry crust that can be used for pies or tarts, with a hint of sweetness.

title: Miss June'S To Die For Chocolate French Silk Pie
directions: Cream butter and sugar until light and fluffy.
Add melted chocolate, cocoa powder and vanilla; blend well.
Add 1 egg at a time, beating for 10 minutes after each addition (total 30 minutes).
Pour into shell and chill at least 4 hours.
Garnish with Cool Whip and toasted sliced almonds, if desired.

_enrichment.description: An indulgent chocolate silk pie with a graham cracker crust, topped with whipped cream and almonds.

title: Baked Vegetables Au Gratin
directions: In skillet, melt butter and saute onion and garlic salt.
In casserole, mix beans, corn, celery, green pepper and basil.
Add sauteed onion and mix well.
Mix 1 cup of cheese in mixture.
Take bread cubes and place along outer edge of casserole, leaving the middle open.
Sprinkle the rest of cheese over top of it all. Bake at 350° for 1 hour.

_enrichment.description: A creamy vegetable gratin with melted cheese and bread cubes, baked until bubbly and golden.

title: French Onion Soup With Cheese Toast
ingredients: 7 c. beef broth or 4 (14 1/2 oz.) cans ready to serve beef broth
2 c. dry white wine or chicken broth
13 slices toasted French bread (1/2 inch thick)
13 oz. Swiss or Gruyere cheese, sliced
directions: Melt butter in 5-quart Dutch oven or stock pot over medium heat.
Stir in onions and garlic.
Cook 20 to 30 minutes or until onions are very soft.
Stir in broth, wine, salt and pepper. Cook, uncovered, for 40 to 60 minutes or until flavors are blended.
Just before serving, toast bread; place cheese on hot bread.

_enrichment.description: Caramelised onion soup topped with cheesy toast, a comforting classic with deep, savoury flavours.

title: Potato Crust Quiche
ingredients: 1 Tbsp. fine dry bread crumbs or wheat germ
directions: Cook potatoes, covered, in boiling salted water until tender. Drain and mash.
Stir in butter and spoon into a greased 9-inch pie plate.
Spread over bottom and up sides, building up sides with a spoon to form a crust.
Arrange vegetables in bottom of potato crust.
Sprinkle with cheese.
In a small bowl, combine eggs, milk, salt and pepper.
Pour over cheese and sprinkle with bread crumbs or wheat germ.
Bake at 375° for 40 to 50 minutes.

_enrichment.description: A quiche with a potato crust, filled with mixed vegetables and melted cheese for a hearty meal.

title: Seafood Souffle
ingredients: 12 large, cooked and deveined shrimp, cut up or 1 lb. shrimp
Old Bay seasoning to taste (sprinkled on top)
directions: Line bread on the bottom of a slightly greased 9 x 13-inch glass dish.
Combine the next 9 ingredients and pour on top. Sprinkle with Old Bay seasoning.
Bake at 350° for 40 minutes. Let sit 10 minutes.
Serve.

_enrichment.description: A light and airy seafood soufflé with crabmeat and shrimp, baked to perfection.

title: Eggplant Quiche
ingredients: 1 small eggplant, pared and cubed (approximately 4 c.)
1 (10 1/2 oz.) can cream of chicken soup
directions: Partially bake crusts in preheated 450° oven for 6 minutes. Cook eggplant covered in boiling, salted water for 8 to 10 minutes; drain well.
Cook onion and pepper in butter until tender.
Blend in flour, soup, salt, sugar, oregano and black pepper.
Heat until bubbly.
Remove from heat; stir into beaten eggs.
Fold in tomato, cheese and eggplant.
Pour into pie shells; top with Parmesan cheese (sprinkle on top).
Bake for 30 minutes at 350°.

_enrichment.description: A savoury quiche with tender eggplant, bell pepper, and cheese in a buttery crust, perfect for a light dinner or brunch.

title: Beef Quiche
directions: Brown the meat; drain and set aside.
Blend mayonnaise, milk, eggs and cornstarch until smooth.
Stir in the meat, grated cheeses, onion, salt and pepper.
Turn into the pie shell.
Bake at 350° for 35 to 45 minutes or until a knife inserted in the center comes out clean.

_enrichment.description: A hearty quiche with ground beef, Swiss and Cheddar cheeses, and a creamy egg custard, ideal for a comforting meal.

title: Baked French Toast With Blueberry Sauce
ingredients: 1 (8 oz.) loaf French or Italian bread
5 c. (approximately 1 1/2 lb.) fresh or frozen blueberries
directions: Slice bread into 12 to 14 slices about 3/4-inch thick.
Place on a 10 1/2 x 15 1/2 x 1-inch cookie sheet.
Whisk egg, milk, baking powder and vanilla together and slowly pour it over the bread, turning to coat the bread completely.
Cover with plastic wrap and refrigerate for 1 to 2 hours or overnight.

_enrichment.description: A classic French-inspired baked French toast topped with a tangy blueberry sauce, perfect for a leisurely brunch.

title: Crustless South Of The Border Quiche
directions: Preheat oven to 400°.
Butter a 10 x 15 x 2-inch baking pan. In large bowl, beat eggs until slightly thickened; add flour and baking powder and blend well.
Add Jack cheese, Cheddar cheese, cottage cheese, chiles and butter; stir until smooth.
Pour into prepared pan and bake at 400° for 15 minutes.
Lower heat to 350° and continue to bake for 45 minutes or until quiche is set and lightly golden on top.
Allow to cool slightly, cut into 1 1/2-inch squares and serve hot or at room temperature.

_enrichment.description: A Tex-Mex-style crustless quiche with melted cheeses and green chiles, offering a spicy and cheesy twist on a classic dish.

title: Pleated Potatoes
directions: Cut potatoes crosswise into 1/4-inch slices, taking care not to cut all the way through so that potato remains intact.
Place cut side up in a shallow pan coated with cooking spray.
Combine remaining ingredients, stirring well.
Brush potatoes with margarine mixture.
Bake at 400° for 1 hour or until tender and slices have fanned out.
Baste occasionally with pan drippings. Yield:
6 servings (90 calories per serving).

_enrichment.description: Thinly sliced potatoes fanned out and baked until tender, brushed with a herby margarine mixture for a crispy, flavourful side.

title: Crustless Quiche
directions: Saute mushrooms and onions in shortening.
Mix all in bowl with whisk; reserve 2 ounces of the cheese.
Pour into greased 9 or 10-inch pie plate; bake in 425° oven for 25 minutes.
Remove from oven and spread remaining cheese on top; bake 5 minutes more.

_enrichment.description: A simple crustless quiche with mushrooms, onions, and Cheddar cheese, baked until golden and served warm.

title: Easy Tuna Quiche
directions: Preheat oven to 450°.
Spread tuna over bottom of pie shell. Sprinkle cheese and onion over tuna.
Top with sliced mushrooms. Beat together eggs, milk, lemon juice and seasonings; pour over tuna mixture.
Bake on a cookie sheet for 15 minutes.
Reduce heat to 350°; bake an additional 12 to 15 minutes or until center is set.
Crab or shrimp may be substituted for tuna.
Serves 6.

_enrichment.description: A quick and easy tuna quiche with a creamy egg custard, melted cheese, and a flaky crust, perfect for a light lunch.

title: Impossible Chicken Quiche
directions: Mix together the first 5 ingredients. Spread into greased deep dish pie pan.
Combine the remaining ingredients in blender.
Pour over the other mixture. Bake at 400° for 40 minutes.
Let stand 5 minutes before cutting.

_enrichment.description: A one-dish wonder with chicken, broccoli, and cheese in a Bisquick batter, baked until golden and fluffy.

title: French Fruit Au Gratin
directions: Mix fruits and cognac and let marinate for 2 hours at room temperature.
Pour mixture into 2-quart shallow baking dish.
In saucepan, mix egg yolks, sugar and cream and stir over low heat until thickened.
Do not boil.
Stir in vanilla.
Pour mixture evenly over undrained marinated fruit.
Place under preheated broiler and broil 8 to 10 minutes or until fluffy and golden brown. Serve warm.
Makes 6 to 8 servings.

_enrichment.description: A decadent French dessert of baked fruit in a rich custard sauce, infused with cognac and vanilla for a luxurious treat.

title: Monte Cristo Au Gratin
ingredients: 3 eggs, lightly beaten with 2 Tbsp. water
directions: Place 2 slices ham and 2 slices turkey between 2 slices of bread.
Dip sandwich in egg mixture.
In a frying pan, heat oil and butter.
Fry both sides of sandwiches until golden brown. Place in baking dish.
In a saucepan, combine cheese, milk and mayonnaise.
Heat, stirring constantly, until cheese melts and mixture is smooth.
Pour over sandwiches; broil 3 to 5 minutes, 6 inches from broiler, until bubbly.

_enrichment.description: A sweet and savoury French sandwich of ham and turkey, fried and baked with a cheesy sauce for a crispy, indulgent dish.

title: Low Carb Cheeseburger Quiche Lunch Snacks
ingredients: 1/2 c. half and half or heavy cream
1/4 tsp. fresh ground black or to taste
salt to taste (careful the bacon has salt)
directions: Preheat oven to 350°.
Brown hamburger in a heavy skillet; add onion.
Remove and mix in bowl with bacon pieces, breaking up any larger clumps with a fork or pastry mixer until you have a fine mix.
Drain well of any excess grease and press into the bottom of a deep-dish pie pan.
Set aside.
Combine remaining ingredients in mixer bowl and whip well.
Pour mixture over beef "crust" and bake 40 to 45 minutes until top is browned and "set".
Cool for 15 to 20 minutes to slice.
This can be packaged in Ziploc or plastic containers for meals quickly microwaved over the next 3 to 5 days. (Does not freeze well, though.)
Makes 6 servings.

_enrichment.description: A low-carb quiche with lean beef, bacon, and cheese, baked in a pie crust for a protein-packed lunch snack.

title: Cheesy Potato Souffle
ingredients: 3 medium-size red potatoes (1 1/2 lb.), peeled and sliced
directions: Cook potatoes in boiling salted water to cover 8 minutes or until tender.
Drain and mash potatoes.
Add sour cream; beat at medium speed of an electric mixer until smooth.
Beat egg yolks until thick and lemon colored.
Add yolks, cheese, onion, chives and pepper to potato mixture; stir well.
Beat egg whites (at room temperature) and baking powder until stiff peaks form.
Fold into potato mixture.
Spoon into a buttered 1 1/2-quart souffle dish. Bake at 350° for 50 minutes or until set.
Serve immediately. Yield: 6 servings.

_enrichment.description: A fluffy potato soufflé with melted cheese and herbs, baked until golden and served as a rich side dish.

title: Herbed Cheese Spread
ingredients: 2 Tbsp. minced fresh chervil or 1/4 tsp. dried chervil
3/4 tsp. minced fresh tarragon or 1/4 tsp. dried tarragon
directions: In a mixing bowl, beat cream cheese and butter until smooth. Add the parsley, chives, chervil, garlic, tarragon and lemon-pepper; mix well.
Transfer to a serving dish.
Cover and refrigerate for 4 hours or overnight.
Remove from the refrigerator 15 minutes before serving.
Serve with crackers. Yield: 1 1/2 cups.

_enrichment.description: A creamy herbed cheese spread with fresh herbs, perfect for spreading on crackers or bread as a starter.

title: Crabmeat Crepes
directions: Coat a large nonstick skillet with cooking spray; add margarine.
Place over medium-high heat until margarine melts.
Add mushrooms, green onions and 1 teaspoon thyme; saute 2 to 3 minutes or until vegetables are tender.
Reduce heat to low; stir in flour. Cook 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Gradually add milk.
Cook over medium heat, stirring constantly, until thickened and bubbly. Remove from heat; stir in crabmeat and next 5 ingredients.

_enrichment.description: Delicate crepes filled with lump crabmeat in a creamy mushroom sauce, garnished with fresh herbs for an elegant dish.

title: French Chocolates
ingredients: shredded coconut, chopped nuts or powdered sugar
1 (12 oz.) pkg. Baker's semi-sweet real chocolate chips
directions: Lightly grease 13 x 9-inch or 9-inch square pan.
Mix spread, sugar and milk in heavy 2 1/2 to 3-quart saucepan; bring to full rolling boil on medium heat, stirring constantly.
Continue boiling 5 minutes on medium heat or until candy thermometer reaches 234°, stirring constantly to prevent scorching.
Remove from heat.
Gradually stir in chips until melted.
Add remaining ingredients; mix well.
Pour into prepared pan.
Cool at room temperature; cut into squares.
Makes about 3 pounds.

_enrichment.description: Rich French chocolates made with condensed milk, chocolate chips, and coconut, set in a firm yet melt-in-the-mouth texture.

title: French Toast With Orange Butter
directions: Place bread in 13 x 9 x 2-inch pan.
Combine eggs, milk, salt, nutmeg and vanilla; beat well.
Pour mixture over bread. Cover and refrigerate overnight.
Melt 1 tablespoon butter in electric skillet at 300°.
Remove six slices of bread from dish and cook in butter 10 to 12 minutes on each side or until cooked through. Repeat procedure with remaining butter and bread.
Serve hot with Orange Butter.
Makes 6 servings.

_enrichment.description: A classic French-inspired breakfast dish featuring thick slices of bread soaked in a rich egg and milk mixture, spiced with nutmeg and vanilla, then cooked until golden and served with a zesty orange butter.

title: Chicken Provencale
ingredients: 4 red potatoes, cut into 1 1/2 inch chunks
2 small red onions, cut into 1/2 inch wedges
directions: Preheat oven to 400°.
In bowl toss potatoes, onions, tomatoes and olives with oil until well blended.
Remove skin from chicken, except wings.
Place chicken, vegetable mixture and bay leaves in large roasting pan.
Sprinkle with salt, thyme and oregano.
Bake until juices run clear when chicken is pierced with fork and vegetables are tender, about 45 minutes.
Remove bay leaves before serving.
Makes 8 servings, 201 calories per serving.

_enrichment.description: A hearty Provençal one-pot dish where chicken is roasted with potatoes, tomatoes, onions, and olives, infused with aromatic herbs like thyme and oregano for a rustic, Mediterranean flavour.

title: Scallops Au Gratin
directions: Rinse scallops in cold water. Cook scallops in water, uncovered, for 3 minutes.
Drain. Saute onion, green pepper and celery in butter until tender. Combine sauteed mix, scallops, bread crumbs and pepper.
Place in an 8 x 8-inch pan or casserole dish. Pour cream over all. Cover with aluminum foil. Bake at 400° for 40 minutes. Remove foil.
Sprinkle with cheese and bake 10 minutes longer.

_enrichment.description: Succulent scallops baked in a creamy sauce with sautéed vegetables, topped with melted Cheddar cheese and breadcrumbs for a crispy, golden finish.

title: Nanny Schembre'S Favorite Ratatouille
ingredients: 3 cloves garlic, peeled and chopped fine
1 large eggplant, peeled and cubed (salt to keep from turning brown)
5 large ripe tomatoes, peeled and sliced, or 1 (28 oz.) can crushed tomatoes
directions: Lightly saute garlic and onions in olive oil.
Dust vegetables with flour, except tomatoes.
Add vegetables to skillet along with tomatoes, salt, pepper, parsley, oregano and basil.
Cook over medium heat for 30 minutes.
Stir, turn heat down to low and simmer 1 hour.
Serve as a side vegetable or cold as a salad for lunch.

_enrichment.description: A traditional Provençal vegetable stew featuring zucchini, eggplant, peppers, and tomatoes, slow-cooked with garlic and herbs for a vibrant, flavourful side dish.

title: French-Toasted Ham And Cheese
directions: In cup, stir marmalade and mustard until mixed.
Set aside. Cut ham slices to fit on half of the bread slice; cut cheese slices to fit on top of ham, then top with a dollop of marmalade mixture and another bread slice.
Preheat oven to 450°.
In 15 1/2 x 10 1/2-inch jellyroll pan, melt butter; remove pan from oven. In pie plate with fork, beat milk and egg until mixed.
Dip sandwiches, one at a time, into egg mixture to coat all sides. Place sandwiches in one layer in jellyroll pan.
Bake until sandwiches are golden brown on both sides, or about 20 minutes, turning once.
Cut each sandwich in half.
Serve hot.

_enrichment.description: A decadent twist on French toast, combining ham and cheese between slices of rye bread, dipped in an egg mixture, and baked until golden, served with orange marmalade.

title: Ham And Eggs Au Gratin
directions: Cook onion in butter until tender, but not browned.
Blend in flour and salt; cook until bubbly.
Add milk; cook, stirring constantly, until melted.
Add eggs, potatoes, ham and parsley. Pour into 1 1/2 or 2-quart baking dish; top with remaining cheese. Sprinkle with paprika.
Bake at 350° for 35 to 40 minutes or until top is lightly browned.
Garnish with additional egg slices. Yields 6 servings.

_enrichment.description: A comforting gratin dish featuring eggs, potatoes, and ham baked in a creamy cheese sauce, topped with extra egg slices and paprika for a rich, savoury flavour.

title: French Coffee Cake
directions: Cream butter and sugar; add egg and sour cream.
Mix in dry ingredients except coconut.
Grease 8-inch tube pan or square pan. Line bottom of pan with all the coconut and pour in batter. Sprinkle top with sugar and cinnamon.
Bake at 350° for 30 minutes.

_enrichment.description: A moist and tender coffee cake with a hint of coconut, featuring a buttery crumb and a delicate balance of sweetness and sour cream tang.

title: Swiss Turkey Quiche
ingredients: 2 c. cubed cooked turkey or chicken
directions: Preheat oven to 425°. Bake pastry shell 8 minutes; remove from oven.
Reduce oven temperature to 350°.
In medium bowl, toss cheese with flour and bouillon; add remaining ingredients.
Mix well.
Pour into prepared shell. Bake 40 to 45 minutes or until set, topping with French fried onions during last 5 minutes.
Let stand 10 minutes before serving.
Refrigerate leftovers.

_enrichment.description: A classic Swiss-inspired quiche filled with tender chicken, Swiss cheese, and a medley of vegetables, baked in a flaky pastry shell.

title: Au Gratin Potato Casserole(Texas-Style)
ingredients: 1 (2 lb.) bag frozen Southern hash browns, thawed
directions: Mix 1 cup melted butter, salt and pepper; add onion and saute. Add soup, sour cream, cheese and potatoes; mix well.
Place in a large greased casserole dish (9 x 13-inches).
Melt 1/2 stick butter and add cornflakes; mix well.
Sprinkle over casserole. Bake at 350° for 45 minutes.
Serves 15 to 20.

_enrichment.description: A Texan-style potato casserole with a creamy, cheesy sauce, topped with crispy cornflakes for a satisfying, indulgent side dish.

title: Bernaise Sauce
directions: Stir egg yolks, lemon and wine vigorously in 1 1/2-quart saucepan.
Add 1/4 cup butter.
Heat over very low heat, stirring constantly until butter is melted and sauce is thickened.
Stir in onion, tarragon and chervil leaves.
Serve with fish or steak. Yields 3/4 cup of sauce.

_enrichment.description: A luxurious French sauce made with egg yolks, butter, white wine, and herbs, perfect for elevating steak or fish with its rich, tangy flavour.

title: French Puffs
ingredients: 1/2 c. sugar, mixed with 2 Tbsp. cinnamon (more or less cinnamon as desired)
directions: Mix together thoroughly the shortening, sugar and egg.
Set aside.
Sift together flour, baking powder, salt and nutmeg.
Add to shortening, sugar and egg mixture alternately with the 1/2 cup milk.
Fill muffin pans 2/3 full.
Bake at 375° for 20 minutes. Makes 10 to 12 muffins.
When thoroughly baked, roll in the melted butter and then in the sugar/cinnamon mixture.

_enrichment.description: Light and airy French puffs with a delicate nutmeg flavour, baked until golden and perfect for a sweet breakfast or snack.

title: Country French Chicken
ingredients: 4 to 6 boneless, skinless chicken breast halves
1 envelope dry vegetable soup and recipe mix
directions: In a large skillet, melt butter or margarine over medium heat. Add chicken; saute 5 minutes.
Stir in water, soup mix and lemon. Reduce heat to low; cover and simmer 15 minutes or until chicken is tender.
Remove chicken and discard lemon.
Combine sour cream, cornstarch and dill weed.
Add to skillet and bring to a boil, stirring constantly, until thickened.
Return chicken to skillet until serving time.
Serve over rice or pasta.
Yields 4 to 6 servings.

_enrichment.description: A rustic French-style chicken dish simmered in a creamy, herby sauce with lemon and dill, offering a comforting, home-style meal.

title: California Quiche
directions: Saute mushrooms, celery and onion in butter until limp, set aside.
Beat eggs; add milk, flour, salt, paprika and Tabasco and beat well.
Stir in bacon, cheeses and wine in mushroom
mixture. Cover and chill.

_enrichment.description: A California-inspired quiche packed with mushrooms, bacon, and a blend of Swiss and Cheddar cheeses, baked in a flaky pastry shell.

title: Rice Au Gratin Supreme
ingredients: 1 (10 1/4 oz.) can cream of mushroom soup (undiluted)
2 c. (8 oz.) shredded Cheddar cheese, divided
directions: Combine first four ingredients in a large bowl; mix well. Combine soup, mayonnaise, 1 cup cheese and milk.
Stir into rice mixture.
Spoon mixture into lightly greased, 2-quart casserole. Bake at 350° for 25 minutes.
Sprinkle with remaining 1 cup of cheese and bake 5 more minutes.
Makes 8 servings.

_enrichment.description: A creamy rice casserole with a cheesy, crispy topping, featuring a medley of vegetables and a rich mushroom sauce.

title: Artichoke Parmesan Quiche
ingredients: Pastry for 1 deep dish crust (9 inches)
1/2 c. grated parmesan cheese plus additional for garnish
salt & pepper & onion powder to taste
directions: Preheat oven to 450 degrees.
Line a 9 inch deep dish pie plate with pastry.
Prick bottom & sides several times with fork. Bake 7 to 8 minutes until it begins to brown; set aside.
Decrease oven temperature to 400 degrees.

_enrichment.description: A savoury quiche with artichokes and a blend of Parmesan and Swiss cheeses, baked in a flaky pastry shell for a rich, umami-packed dish.

title: Miniature Shrimp Quiches
directions: Brush tart shells with a mixture of beaten egg and a little milk for a brown glaze.
Divide the Parmesan cheese, chopped shrimp and Swiss cheese among the tart shells.
Mix egg yolks, cream, Tabasco and salt; fill shells with mixture.
Bake in 325° oven for 15 minutes.

_enrichment.description: Delicate, bite-sized quiches filled with shrimp, Swiss cheese, and a creamy egg mixture, baked in flaky tart shells for an elegant starter.

title: Bananas Foster French Toast
directions: Combine eggs, cream and vanilla.
Combine sugar and cinnamon. Dip bread in egg mixture, then sprinkle both sides with sugar mixture.
Heat 1 tablespoon of butter and 2 teaspoons of oil in large, nonstick skillet.
Add bread in batches and cook until golden brown, 1 1/2 minutes on each side, adding oil as necessary. Transfer to plate and keep warm.
Add remaining 3 tablespoons of butter to skillet.
Toss bananas with remaining cinnamon-sugar mixture.
Add bananas to skillet and cook over high heat until they begin to caramelize, 2 minutes.
Add water and cook until mixture is slightly thick; pour over French toast and sift confectioners sugar on top.
Makes 6 servings, each containing 550 calories and 31 g fat.

_enrichment.description: A decadent twist on French toast, featuring caramelised bananas tossed in cinnamon-sugar and served with a rich, buttery sauce.

title: Quiche Divan
ingredients: 1 (10 oz.) pkg. frozen broccoli, cooked and drained
directions: Place broccoli in the bottom of the pastry shell.
Cover with chicken and cheese.
Beat together eggs, cream, salt and pepper. Pour over the broccoli, chicken and cheese.
Bake at 375° for 35 to 40 minutes or until knife inserted near center comes out clean. Cool slightly before serving.

_enrichment.description: A classic French quiche with a creamy egg and cream filling, layered with tender chicken, Gruyère cheese, and steamed broccoli.

title: Garden Pie Or Quiche
directions: Steam the first 3 ingredients until tender.
Beat together milk, Bisquick, eggs, salt, pepper and thyme.
Grease a 9-inch pie tin, put vegetables in pie tin.
Spread shredded cheese over this. Pour Bisquick mixture over this.
Bake at 400° for 35 minutes (ovens vary, so play it by ear).
Let stand 5 minutes before cutting.
If using cauliflower instead of broccoli, use 1/2 teaspoon curry powder instead of thyme.

_enrichment.description: A hearty vegetable pie with steamed broccoli, peppers, and onions, topped with a fluffy Bisquick and egg mixture and melted cheese.

title: French Bread Sandwich
ingredients: turkey or turkey-like loaf, cut in cubes
directions: Cut French bread in half, then into sections.
Spread mayonnaise.
Place a piece of lettuce on each piece, then the filling (turkey, celery and apples mixed with mayonnaise, aji and a little sugar).
On filling, put cubed cranberry sauce and almond slivers.

_enrichment.description: A crunchy French bread sandwich filled with turkey, crisp apples, celery, and a sweet-tangy cranberry sauce, topped with almond slivers.

title: Onion And Fennel Soup Au Gratin
ingredients: 1 packet instant onion broth and seasoning mix, dissolved in 3/4 c. warm water
1 1/2 oz. reduced fat Swiss cheese, shredded
directions: In 1 quart nonstick saucepan, melt margarine; add onions and fennel.
Cook over medium-high heat, stirring frequently, until onions are translucent, about 2 minutes.
Add 1/2 cup water, the dissolved broth mix and pepper; bring mixture to a boil.
Reduce heat to low and let simmer until flavors blend, about 15 minutes.

_enrichment.description: A comforting French onion and fennel soup topped with a golden bread and Swiss cheese crust, baked until bubbly and golden.

title: Manicotti With Spinach And Cheese Crepes(12 Entree Crepes)
ingredients: 2 (10 oz.) pkg. frozen chopped spianch, thawed and squeezed free of all liquid
2 c. (1 lb.) Ricotta cheese or low-fat cottage cheese
directions: Put spinach in mixing bowl with salt, pepper, garlic powder, onion powder, nutmeg, Ricotta cheese, eggs, 1 1/2 cups Jack cheese and Parmesan cheese.
Stir to mix well.
Divide mixture
evenly among crepes and roll up.
Place crepes in a single layer in greased shallow baking dish.
Cover with foil.
Dish can be refrigerated at this point.
Before serving, bake in 375° oven for 15 to 20 minutes or until thoroughly heated.
Remove foil and sprinkle with remaining Jack cheese.
Bake, uncovered, 5 minutes more or until cheese has melted.

_enrichment.description: Spinach and cheese-filled crepes rolled and baked in a creamy sauce, a classic Italian-inspired French dish.

title: Crab And Shrimp Quiches
ingredients: 6 oz. can crab, rinse and drain well
directions: Bake pie shells until light brown.
Line the shells with the Swiss.
Combine the beaten eggs, sour cream and chowder in a bowl; mix well.
Stir in shrimp, crab meat, mushrooms and seasonings. Pour evenly into pie shells.
Bake at 350° for 1 hour or until knife inserted in center comes out clean.
Let stand 5 minutes before cutting.

_enrichment.description: Rich and savoury quiches packed with crab, shrimp, mushrooms, and Swiss cheese in a creamy egg and sour cream filling.

title: Potatoes, Onion, Mushroom Au Gratin
directions: Cook potatoes the night before with jackets on.
Cool, peel and cut in cubes.
Heat butter in large kettle and saute mushrooms until brown.
Stir in flour, milk, cream or evaporated milk, consomme and grated cheese.
Also add seasoned salt, pepper and Worcestershire sauce.
Cook, stirring until smooth and thick. Remove from heat and add potatoes and drained onions.
Mix. Spoon into shallow pan or 4-quart casserole.
Cover in fridge overnight. Bake at 350° for 1 hour.

_enrichment.description: A creamy, cheesy potato and mushroom bake with a golden crust, perfect for a hearty side or main dish.

title: Connie'S Easy French Bread
directions: Put yeast in 1/2 cup of warm water and mix.
Add sugar, salt, shortening and hot water.
Stir in the flour, one cup at a time, and mix after each cup.
