After all the flour is added, let dough set for 15 minutes, then stir again and let set again for 15 minutes.
Do this 4 times (takes 1 hour).
Let dough set, covered, until it doubles.
Form into 1 loaf and put on floured baking sheet.
Brush the top with cold water and make 5 slashes diagonally in top of loaf with sharp knife.
Let loaf rise for 30 minutes and bake in a 350° oven for 30 to 40 minutes.

_enrichment.description: A classic French bread recipe with a soft, airy interior and a crisp crust, made with yeast and simple ingredients.

title: French Poufs
directions: Combine shortening, 1/2 cup sugar and egg in bowl, mixing well.
Sift next 4 ingredients together.
Add to creamed mixture alternately with milk, beating well after each addition.
Fill greased muffin cups 2/3 full.
Bake at 350° for 20 to 25 minutes. Dip immediately in melted butter and then in mixture of 1/2 cup sugar and cinnamon.

_enrichment.description: Light, buttery French pastries dusted with cinnamon sugar, perfect for a sweet treat with tea or coffee.

title: Mom'S French Chef Meat Loaf
directions: Fry out the salt pork.
Add onion and cook until golden brown. Add pork and onion to other ingredients.
Mix thoroughly.
Bake in loaf pan in hot oven (450°) for 15 minutes, then decrease heat to moderate (350°) and bake for 30 minutes longer, or until done.
Or, microwave on High for 20- to 25 minutes, or until done.
Serve hot or cold.
This is a very good, juicy meat loaf, not dry.

_enrichment.description: A moist and flavourful meatloaf with a tangy tomato glaze, baked to perfection for a classic family meal.

title: Green Bean Au Gratin
ingredients: 1 pkg. frozen or a 16 oz. can of French style green beans
directions: Make a white sauce with the first five ingredients.
When thick, add cheese and melt.
Stir in green beans and pour into buttered 1 quart casserole dish.
Sprinkle with Parmesan cheese, almonds and paprika.
Bake uncovered 30 minutes at 350°.
Put onion rings on top during last 5 to 10 minutes of cooking time.

_enrichment.description: A creamy green bean bake topped with melted cheese, almonds, and a sprinkle of paprika for a crunchy, savoury side dish.

title: Creole Cauliflower Au Gratin
ingredients: 2 c. mashed cooked tomatoes (canned tomatoes can be used)
3 c. cooked chopped cauliflower (frozen cauliflower can be used)
directions: Melt butter in a saucepan; add onion and green pepper and brown lightly.
Blend in flour and add tomatoes and salt and pepper.
Heat to boiling point and cook 3 minutes, stirring constantly.
Add cauliflower and heat thoroughly.
Place mixture in a casserole and cover with shredded cheese.
Bake at 350° until cheese has melted and mixture is hot and bubbly.
Makes 6 servings.

_enrichment.description: A spicy Creole-style cauliflower bake with melted cheddar cheese, perfect as a hearty vegetarian side or main.

title: Chicken In Tarragon Sauce(French)
ingredients: 6 pieces skinless chicken (2 drumsticks, 2 thighs and 2 boneless breasts)
1 tsp. potato starch, dissolved in 1 Tbsp. water
directions: Place drumsticks and thighs along with wine, onion, stock, bay leaves, thyme, salt and pepper in a large saucepan and bring to a boil.
reduce heat; cover and boil gently, about 10 minutes.
Add breast meat; cover and boil another 10 minutes.
Transfer meat to a dish; keep warm.

_enrichment.description: Tender chicken pieces simmered in a delicate tarragon and white wine sauce, served with a creamy finish.

title: Crepe A La Caviar
directions: Blend milk and eggs with whisk, add flour and oil and mix until smooth.
Cover and refrigerate for at least 1 hour.
Lightly butter a small frypan and pour just enough batter to coat bottom of pan. When it starts to bubble, loosen edges with small spatula, pick up crepe with fingers and flip over.
Cook 20 seconds more.
Wrap cooked crepes in napkin and keep warm in the oven.

_enrichment.description: Delicate French crepes topped with luxurious caviar and a dollop of sour cream, a refined starter for special occasions.

title: Crab And Shrimp Quiche
directions: Mix crab and shrimp (rinsed and shredded) together and set aside.
Cook shallots in oleo in small saucepan until soft, 5 minutes.
Beat eggs with whisk; add milk, vermouth and hot sauce. Mix together.
Add crab and shrimp and additional milk to make 3 cups.
Pour into baked pie shell and bake at 375° for 30 to 35 minutes or until knife in center comes out clean.
Makes 6 servings.

_enrichment.description: A rich and creamy quiche filled with succulent crab and shrimp, perfect for a sophisticated dinner party.

title: Mushroom Quiche
ingredients: dash of basil, thyme, cayenne pepper and paprika
directions: Saute 2 cups chopped mushrooms and 1 cup saltine crackers (crushed).
Remove from pan and press into 9-inch pie plate to form crust.
Saute 1 cup scallions and place on crust.
Grate 1 1/2 cups Swiss cheese and place on top of scallions.
Blend together 3 eggs, 1 cup cottage cheese, basil, thyme and cayenne pepper.
Pour mixture into pie plate and sprinkle paprika on top.
Bake at 325° for 25 to 35 minutes.
Remove from oven; wait 10 to 15 minutes before cutting.

_enrichment.description: A savoury quiche with a crispy cracker crust, mushrooms, scallions, and Swiss cheese, ideal for a comforting meal.

title: Provencal Vegetable Soup
ingredients: 2 Tbsp. chopped fresh basil or 1 Tbsp. dried
directions: In 2 1/2 qts. water cook together leek, onion, green beans, potatoes, carrots, tomatoes and salt.
Cook for 45 minutes or until tender.
In the last 1/2 hour, add zucchini.
If you're using any frozen vegetables, add them the last 15 minutes.
Add salt and pepper to taste.
Prepare a garlic paste by crushing together garlic and basil.
Gradually work in tomato paste, parmesan cheese, and olive oil.
Add a few Tbsp. of the broth to the garlic paste, mix well, then stir the paste into the soup.
Heat 5 minutes. Serves 8.

_enrichment.description: A hearty Provençal vegetable soup packed with seasonal produce, garlic, and basil, a classic French autumn dish.

title: Quiche Provencale
ingredients: 6 to 8 slices bacon, fried and crumbled
1 1/2 c. milk or half and half
directions: In a skillet over medium heat, saute onion, green pepper and mushrooms in oil.
Cook spinach by directions on package; drain well.
Beat together eggs, flour and milk until well blended.
Add vegetables, garlic salt, basil and pepper.
Spread bacon and grated cheese over pie shell in pan.
Pour other ingredients over cheese and bacon.
Bake in preheated 375° oven until knife inserted near center comes out clean, 30 to 35 minutes.
Let stand 5 minutes before serving.

_enrichment.description: A rustic Provençal quiche with sautéed vegetables, bacon, and cheese, bringing the flavours of southern France to your table.

title: Quiche Noel
directions: Line a 9-inch quiche dish or pie pan with pastry; trim excess pastry around edges.
Prick bottom and sides of pastry with a fork.
Bake at 400° for 3 minutes; remove from oven and gently prick with a fork.
Bake 5 minutes longer; let cool on rack.

_enrichment.description: A festive Christmas quiche with chicken, Cheddar, and pimiento, perfect for holiday gatherings.

title: Ham And Egg Breakfast Souffle
ingredients: 4 medium green tomatoes, cored and sliced crosswise to 1/4-inch thick slices
directions: Fry bacon; drain and crumble.
Reserve 2 tablespoons bacon grease.
Mix together cornmeal, cheese, salt and pepper.
In a skillet, heat 1 tablespoon bacon grease with half of cooking oil. Coat tomato slices well with cornmeal mixture.
When grease is sizzling hot, cook tomatoes on both sides until crisp and lightly brown.
Repeat with remaining.
Drain on paper towel.
Transfer fried tomatoes to a large plate; sprinkle with crumbled bacon. Serve hot.

_enrichment.description: A fluffy breakfast soufflé with ham, cheese, and crispy green tomatoes, a hearty start to the day.

title: La Crepe Francaise
directions: Place flour and eggs in mixing bowl.
Add salt and sugar; mix well.
When ingredients get too heavy to mix, add milk and mix well.
Add vanilla and melted butter; mix well.
Cover bowl; let stand for 1 hour.
Using heated skillet or pan, use a small amount of Crisco to grease pan.
When pan is hot (not too hot), spread pancake mix in pan and brown both sides (golden brown only). Serve with warm maple syrup.
Serves 4.

_enrichment.description: Classic French crêpes with a light, buttery batter, simple yet elegant for any occasion.

title: Overnight French Toast Deluxe
ingredients: 1/4 c. butter or margarine, divided in half
directions: Place bread in a 13 x 9 x 2-inch baking dish.
Combine eggs, milk, sugar, salt, orange juice and vanilla; beat well.
Pour mixture over bread slices and turn slices to coat evenly.
Cover and refrigerate overnight.
In the morning, melt 2 tablespoons margarine in a large skillet; remove 4 slices bread from dish and saute 4 minutes or until browned on each side.
Repeat procedure with remaining margarine and bread.
Sprinkle with xxxx sugar and serve immediately.
Makes 4 servings.

_enrichment.description: A decadent overnight French toast with orange and vanilla, best served warm with a dusting of powdered sugar.

title: Escalloped Eggplant Au Gratin
directions: Peel and cube eggplant.
Cook in salted water until soft. Drain well; add bread cubes, butter, eggs, salt, pepper and all but 1/2 cup of cheese.
Sprinkle half the fine bread crumbs over bottom of buttered baking dish.
Add eggplant mixture.
Cover with remaining bread crumbs and cheese.
Cover with lid or foil and bake 30 minutes at 375°.
Remove cover and brown for 5 minutes.

_enrichment.description: A creamy, cheesy eggplant gratin with a crispy breadcrumb topping, a comforting vegetarian dish.

title: Spinach Quiche In A Rice Crust
ingredients: 1 pkg. frozen spinach, chopped and drained
directions: Cook rice in water until tender; drain and mix with 1 beaten egg and tablespoon cheese.
Press firmly into 9-inch pie pan to form crust.
Bake crust 3 minutes in 425° oven.
Saute onion in margarine until slightly brown.
Add to spinach, nutmeg, pepper and lemon juice.
Put in crust; sprinkle with remaining cheese. Mix 2 eggs with milk and pour into quiche.
Bake 10 minutes at 425°.
Lower temperature to 350° and bake 30 minutes longer.
Done when center is firm.
Makes 6 servings, approximately 185 calories per serving.

_enrichment.description: A spinach quiche with a rice crust, light yet flavourful, perfect for a healthy meal.

title: Turkey Souffle Salad
ingredients: 1 to 2 Tbsp. vinegar or lemon juice
1/3 c. chopped cucumber or green pepper
directions: Dissolve gelatin and salt in boiling water; add cold water, mayonnaise, vinegar, onion and pepper.
Beat until well blended. Pour into an 8-inch square pan; freeze 15 to 20 minutes or until firm, about 1-inch from edge but soft in center.
Spoon mixture into bowl and whip until fluffy; fold in turkey and chopped vegetables.
Pour into a 4-cup mold or individual molds.
Chill in refrigerator until firm, 30 to 60 minutes; unmold.
Garnish with salad greens and serve with additional mayonnaise, if desired. Makes about 4 cups or 4 entree servings.

_enrichment.description: A refreshing turkey soufflé salad with gelatin, vegetables, and mayo, a light and wobbly dish for summer picnics.

title: French Butter Cookies
ingredients: 2 1/4 c. all-purpose flour (do not pack, level off)
directions: In a large bowl combine sugar, soda, cream of tartar, salt, vanilla, butter and egg.
Mix with mixer on low speed for about 1 minute or until well blended.
Gradually add flour; blend at low speed until well mixed.
If desired, tint with a few drops of food coloring.

_enrichment.description: Buttery, melt-in-the-mouth French butter cookies, a simple yet indulgent treat.

title: Mushroom Tofu Quiche
ingredients: 12 oz. tofu (fresh bean curd), drained and cut up
1 (7 oz.) can whole kernel corn with sweet peppers, drained
1 medium tomato, cut in thin wedges
directions: Combine tofu, egg whites, whole egg, milk, oregano, garlic powder, 1/2 cup cheese, 1/4 teaspoon pepper and 1/8 teaspoon salt in blender.
Cover and blend or process until smooth.
Stir in corn and dried onion.
Prepare a 9-inch pie plate by spreading margarine over the bottom and sides and sprinkling with bread crumbs.
Pour tofu mixture into pie plate.
Bake, uncovered, at 350° for 30 to 35 minutes (a knife inserted near the center should come out clean). Arrange tomato wedges on top of quiche and sprinkle with remaining cheese.
Bake until cheese is melted (about 3 minutes).
Garnish with parsley.
Makes 4 servings.
NER: whole kernel corn with sweet peppers

_enrichment.description: A vegan-friendly tofu quiche with mushrooms, cheese, and corn, a lighter take on a classic dish.

title: Baked Blueberry French Toast
directions: Place bread into a jelly roll pan.
In a medium bowl, mix eggs, half-and-half and vanilla together, pour over bread, cover and refrigerate overnight.
Combine sugar, cinnamon, allspice and cornstarch together, fold in blueberries and place in a greased 13 X 9 baking pan.
Place chilled bread mixture on top of blueberry mixture; drizzle butter over bread.
Bake at 450 degrees for 30 minutes or until golden brown.
Makes 2 to 4 servings.

_enrichment.description: A sweet and spiced blueberry French toast bake, perfect for a leisurely weekend brunch.

title: Corn Quiche
directions: Cut monterey jack cheese into cubes.
Combine eggs and sour creme in a large bowl.
Add corn, bread crumbs, green chillies, salt and pepper.
Mix well.
Stir in monterey jack cheese cubes. Spoon into a greased 10 inch quiche pan or a square baking pan Bake at 350 or until a knife inserted in center comes out clean Sprinkle with cheddar cheese.
Bake 5 or 10 minutes longer.
Cool for 10 minutes before serving.

_enrichment.description: A savoury American-style quiche with sweetcorn, green chillies, and melted cheese, baked in a buttery crust for a rich, creamy texture.

title: Swiss Pie(Similar To A Quiche)
ingredients: 1/2 lb. grated or sliced thin cheese (i.e., Swiss)
1/2 c. cream (light or half and half)
1 Tbsp. spinach, mushrooms, onions, chopped broccoli, whatever
directions: Put grated cheese in a pastry crust (if adding extras, first mix in with cheese); then sprinkle with flour.
Mix milk, light cream, eggs, salt and pepper together, then pour over the cheese in the pie crust.
Scatter with plenty of bread crumbs and dot with butter.
Bake 30 minutes (first 15 minutes in hot 450° oven, second 15 minutes at low 325°).
Test with knife.
If it comes out clean, then it's done.

_enrichment.description: A Swiss-inspired pie with a pastry crust, filled with a creamy egg and cheese mixture, topped with breadcrumbs and butter for a golden finish.

title: Chicken And Asparagus Au Gratin
directions: Mix bread cubes with cheese and 1/4 cup of the melted butter. Line a 7 x 11 x 1 1/2-inch dish with half of the bread cubes. Arrange asparagus on cubes.
Melt remaining butter over flame, blend in flour, salt and pepper, then add milk, stirring until well mixed.
Cook until thick.
Add chicken and pour over asparagus.
Sprinkle top with rest of bread cubes and cheese mix. Bake at 375° for 25 minutes, or until brown.
Serves 8.

_enrichment.description: A hearty American gratin with tender chicken and asparagus, smothered in a creamy cheese sauce and topped with crispy breadcrumbs.

title: Tomato Quiche
ingredients: 1 c. shredded cheese (sharp Cheddar, Provolone or Mozzarella)
directions: Roll pie dough to fit an 8-inch pie pan and 1/2 inch up sides of pan.
Brush with soft or melted margarine.
Peel tomatoes; cut in wedges and arrange, overlapping, over entire crust.
Sprinkle with Jane's salt and any herbs you like.
Mix together onion, mayonnaise (or your preferred version), yogurt and cheese.
Spread over tomatoes.
Bake 10 minutes at 425°, then 20 minutes at 350° or until fully baked and set firm.
Should be served warm.

_enrichment.description: A tangy tomato quiche with a buttery crust, topped with a creamy cheese and yogurt mixture, perfect for a light summer meal.

title: Broccoli Rice Quiche
directions: Combine rice, 1 egg and 1/2 cup cheese, mixing well.
Press mixture into a greased 9-inch pie plate; set aside.
Cook broccoli and drain well.
Combine broccoli, 1/4 cup cheese and remaining ingredients.
Pour broccoli mixture into rice-lined pie plate. Bake at 375° for 50 minutes or until set.

_enrichment.description: A comforting quiche with rice, broccoli, and cheese, baked until golden and served with a fluffy texture.

title: Crustless Salmon Quiche
directions: Toss salmon, cheese and onion with flour; spread in greased pie plate.
Beat eggs slightly; beat in remaining ingredients. Pour egg mixture over salmon mixture.
Cook uncovered in 350° oven until knife inserted in center comes out clean, 35 to 40 minutes.
Let stand 10 minutes before cutting.

_enrichment.description: A crustless quiche with flaky salmon and Swiss cheese, baked until tender and served with a silky egg custard.

title: Shrimp Quiches(24 Mini Quiches)
ingredients: 1 (8 oz.) pkg. refrigerated butter flake rolls or flake biscuits
1/2 c. whipping cream or half and half
directions: Lightly grease 2 dozen 1 3/4-inch mini muffin cups.
Separate flake rolls into 12 equal pieces.
Cut each in half.
Press each into bottom and sides of muffin cups, making small pinched lip. Divide shrimp evenly among pastry shells.

_enrichment.description: Mini quiches with shrimp, Swiss cheese, and dill, baked in flaky pastry cups for an elegant appetiser.

title: Crustless Zucchini Quiche
ingredients: 15 oz. part skim milk Ricotta cheese
directions: Preheat oven to 350°.
Grease 9-inch quiche pan with 1 teaspoon butter. Steam zucchini until very soft (about 10 minutes).
Saute onion in 2 teaspoons butter until transparent. When cool, mix with Ricotta cheese, Cheddar cheese, milk, eggs, salt and pepper.
Fold in zucchini and pour into quiche pan.
Bake for about one hour, or until quiche is golden and firm to the touch.
Yields six servings.

_enrichment.description: A crustless quiche with tender zucchini, ricotta, and cheddar, baked until golden and served warm.

title: Janel'S French Chicken
directions: Preheat oven to 350°.
Combine salt, pepper, flour, powdered milk and sugar in plastic bag.
Shake 2 or 3 pieces of chicken at a time and arrange into greased dish.
Cut butter into small chunks and place on top of individual pieces.
Sprinkle French dressing in a pattern over chicken, sprinkle with paprika.
Cook uncovered in a 350° oven for 1 hour until chicken is done.

_enrichment.description: A French-inspired chicken dish with a crispy coating, baked until golden and served with a tangy dressing.

title: Autumn Potato Gratin
ingredients: 3 medium red potatoes, peeled and sliced thin
2 sweet potatoes (1 lb.), peeled and sliced thin
directions: Arrange half of potato slices in deep 10-inch pie plate.
Put onion slices on top.
Sprinkle with chervil, summer savory, pepper, salt, half of the butter and oil.
Arrange remaining sweet potato slices in overlapping pattern around edge of pan.
Arrange remaining white potato slices in overlapping pattern in center. Drizzle remaining oil over top.
Cover with foil and bake at 350° for 30 to 35 minutes.
Uncover and bake 30 minutes more or until golden and tender, basting occasionally with butter.
Serves 6.

_enrichment.description: A rich potato gratin with sweet and red potatoes, layered with herbs and butter, baked until golden and tender.

title: Fabulous Flako Pie(Quickie Quiche)
ingredients: 2 c. shredded Swiss cheese, Cheddar or Monterey Jack
1 1/2 c. chopped, cooked chicken or turkey
1 (10 oz.) pkg. frozen chopped broccoli, thawed and drained or spinach
directions: Heat oven to 400°.
Grease a 10-inch pie plate (Crisco works great).
Combine cheese, chicken, broccoli and onion in blender container or in bowl of electric mixer.
Mix at high speed until smooth.
Pour over ingredients in pie plate.
Bake 25 to 30 minutes or until knife comes out clean. Cool 5 minutes before serving. Garnish with tomato wedges, if desired. Makes 8 servings.

_enrichment.description: A quick quiche with Swiss cheese, chicken, and broccoli, baked in a flaky crust for a creamy, savoury dish.

title: Simplified Quiche
ingredients: 1/2 c. Cheddar cheese or Swiss or both
directions: Line bottom of pie shell with bacon, cheese, onion and bell pepper.
Blend other ingredients in blender on high for one minute.
Pour over bacon, cheese, etc.
Bake at 350° for approximately 55 minutes or until a knife comes out clean.

_enrichment.description: A simplified quiche with bacon, cheese, and vegetables, baked in a buttery crust for a hearty meal.

title: French Apple Cake
ingredients: 3 large apples, peeled and cut into small pieces
directions: Combine cooking oil, applesauce, sugar and eggs and beat 3 minutes at medium speed.
Add to this mixture the flour, salt, baking soda and vanilla.
Beat until smooth.
Fold in apples (peeled and cut into small pieces) and pecans.
Bake in cake pan, greased and floured for 1 hour and 20 minutes at 325°.

_enrichment.description: A moist French apple cake with tender apples and pecans, baked until golden and served warm.

title: Palacinke(Serbian Crepes)
directions: Warm milk and water on stove; add flour, sugar, salt, vanilla extract and eggs into large bowl with warmed milk and water. Batter should be very thin consistency.
Cover 6-inch skillet with Pam or oil.
Drip enough batter onto thinly covered surface and fry over medium heat.
Turn over once until brown on both sides.

_enrichment.description: Thin Serbian crepes with a delicate texture, perfect for breakfast or dessert, served with sweet toppings.

title: Spicy Sweet Potato Souffle
ingredients: 2 lb. sweet potatoes, peeled, cooked and mashed
directions: Heat oven to 375°.

In bowl of electric mixer, beat mashed sweet potatoes with softened butter, light brown sugar, bourbon, cayenne pepper and ground cinnamon 2 minutes or until fluffy. Stir in light cream and 3 egg yolks.
In separate bowl, using electric mixer, beat egg whites on high speed 5 minutes or until stiff peaks form; fold into sweet potato mixture.
Pour mixture into buttered 1 1/2-quart souffle dish; sprinkle with chopped pecans.
Bake 45 minutes or until souffle is puffed and golden brown on top.

_enrichment.description: A spicy sweet potato soufflé with a light, airy texture, topped with pecans for a festive dessert.

title: French Muffins
directions: Combine shortening, half of the sugar, and egg; mix thoroughly.
Sift together flour, baking powder, salt, and nutmeg. Stir into egg mixture alternately with milk.
Beat well after each addition.
Fill greased muffin tins 2/3 full.
Bake at 350 degrees for 20 to 25 minutes or until golden brown.
Immediately roll in melted butter and then in mixture of remaining sugar and cinnamon.
Serve hot.

_enrichment.description: Classic French muffins with a buttery, spiced flavour, perfect for breakfast or a light snack. The recipe includes a cinnamon-sugar coating for extra sweetness.

title: Easy Seafood Crepes
ingredients: 1 family sized cream of mushroom soup
directions: In skillet, saute mushrooms, crab meat and broccoli with salt and pepper in margarine or butter.
Place this mixture in center of flat tortilla.
Sprinkle cheese on top and roll.
Continue until all tortillas have been filled.
Place in oblong pan. Spread soup (sour cream may be added to soup for a creamier texture) over all tortillas and bake at 350° for 30 minutes.

_enrichment.description: A quick and easy seafood dish using tortillas, crab meat, and mushrooms in a creamy soup sauce, ideal for a light dinner or meal prep.

title: Caramel French Toast
ingredients: 1/4 c. plus 2 Tbsp. white corn syrup
directions: Combine brown sugar, margarine and corn syrup in a heavy saucepan.
Cook over medium heat, stirring constantly, about 5 minutes or until bubbly.
Pour syrup into bottom of greased 9 x 13-inch glass baking dish.
Arrange bread slices over syrup.

_enrichment.description: Rich and indulgent caramel French toast with a gooey syrup layer, perfect for a decadent weekend brunch or special occasion.

title: Rapee Morvandelle For 4(Quiche)
ingredients: 2 Tbsp. olive oil or cooking oil
3 medium potatoes, peeled and grated (squeeze out excess water; hash browns or shredded, frozen taters can be used)
directions: Cook onion, oil and butter until tender; add diced cooked ham and brown.
Beat together eggs, garlic, parsley, chives, grated cheese, milk, salt and pepper.
Add ham and onion.
Add potatoes (or hash browns or frozen taters) and mix., Heat 2 tablespoons butter in large pie plate (glass preferably) or skillet.
When butter foams, add egg and potato mixture.
Dot with butter.
Bake 30 to 40 minutes in 375° oven.

_enrichment.description: A hearty Morvan-style quiche with potatoes, ham, and Swiss cheese, offering a comforting and savoury meal for any season.

title: French Fried Onions
directions: Sift flour and salt together; add evaporated milk, salad oil and egg whites, beating until smooth.
Add water, making medium thin batter.
Peel and slice onions about 1/4-inch thick; dip into batter (coat well) and lower into hot fat (375°).
Fry until golden brown.
Remove with slotted spoon or fork; drain on paper towel and salt.

_enrichment.description: Crispy French fried onions made from scratch, a versatile snack or topping for salads and casseroles, ideal for autumn and winter.

title: Spinach And Potato Gratin
ingredients: 2 - 10 oz. pkg. frozen chopped spinach, thawed and squeezed dry
3 lbs. all purpose potatoes (9 medium), peeled and cut into 1/4 in. thick slices
directions: Preheat oven to 350°.
Grease shallow 3 quart casserole.
In 10 inch skillet, melt margarine or butter over medium heat - add shallots and cook 5 minutes or until tender, stirring occasionally.
Remove skillet from heat and stir in spinach, nutmeg, 1/4 tsp. salt and 1/4 tsp. pepper.

_enrichment.description: A creamy spinach and potato gratin with Gruyère cheese, perfect for a comforting autumn or winter dinner.

title: French Picnic Loaf
directions: Combine all ingredients except watercress and French bread in large bowl; mix well.
Shape into loaf in rectangular baking pan. Bake in preheated 325° oven until meat thermometer inserted into center registers 170°, about 1 1/2 hours.
Cool on wire rack; refrigerate until cold.
Garnish with watercress; serve with French bread.

_enrichment.description: A savoury French picnic loaf made with ground meats, mushrooms, and spices, ideal for a picnic or light meal.

title: Scallops Provencal
directions: Melt butter in large skillet and saute garlic over medium heat about 3 minutes.
Add wine and simmer 3 minutes.
Add tomatoes, parsley, salt and pepper and simmer 10 to 12 minutes until very thick.
Add scallops to pan and cook 3 to 5 minutes or until scallops are opaque.
Do not overcook.
In small bowl, mix bread crumbs and Parmesan.
Transfer scallops to ovensafe pan and sprinkle with crumb mixture.
Lightly brown under the broiler. Serve immediately.

_enrichment.description: Elegant scallops cooked in a Provençal style with tomatoes, garlic, and white wine, perfect for a summer dinner party.

title: Pinto Bean, Sausage And Fennel Gratin
ingredients: 1 b. dried pinto or red kidney beans, soaked in enough cold water to cover them by 2-inches overnight or quick soaked
1 lb. Italian sausage, casings discarded and meat chopped
3 c. thinly sliced fennel bulb (about 1 large bulb)
1 large red bell pepper, cut into thin strips
10 oz. can artichoke hearts, rinsed, drained well and each heart cut into 8 pieces
1 Tbsp. unsalted butter, cut into bits
directions: In a kettle combine the beans with enough cold water to cover them by 2-inches and simmer them, covered, for 1 to 1 1/2 hours or until they are tender.
Drain the beans in a colander set over a large bowl and reserve the cooking liquid.
In a large heavy skillet, cook the sausage over moderate heat, stirring and breaking up any lumps, until it is cooked through and transfer it with a slotted spoon to a bowl.

To the fat remaining in the skillet, add the oil and in the fat cook the onion, fennel, bell pepper, basil, thyme, salt and pepper to taste over moderate heat, stirring until the vegetables are softened.
Add the broth and simmer the mixture, covered, for 5 to 10 minutes or until the vegetables are tender.

_enrichment.description: A hearty gratin with pinto beans, Italian sausage, and fennel, offering a rich and savoury meal for autumn or winter.

title: "Philly" Brunch Quiche
ingredients: 1 (8 oz.) pkg. Philadelphia cream cheese, cubed
directions: Preheat oven to 350°.
Combine cheese and milk in saucepan and stir over low heat until smooth.
Gradually add cream cheese mixture to eggs; mix until well blended.
Saute onion in margarine. Add onion and remaining ingredients to mixture; mix well.
Pour into pastry shell and bake 35 to 40 minutes or until set. Excellent served with fresh fruit and hot rolls.
Serves 8.

_enrichment.description: A creamy Philadelphia cream cheese quiche with ham and pimento, perfect for a brunch or light dinner.

title: French Toast Casserole(Serves 12)
directions: Lightly spray a 9 x 13 inch baking dish with nonfat cooking spray.
Arrange bread in bottom of baking dish.
Combine remaining ingredients in a food processor or blender and process until all ingredients are blended.
Pour liquid mixture over bread; cover with plastic wrap and refrigerate one hour or overnight.

_enrichment.description: A make-ahead French toast casserole with a spiced custard, ideal for a festive brunch or holiday breakfast.

title: Joann'S Zucchini Quiche
directions: Saute zucchini, onion and margarine until tender.
Add basil, oregano, garlic powder and parsley flakes to zucchini mixture. Mix together Mozzarella cheese and eggs.
Spread rolls into pie plate.
Spread mustard over crust.
Add zucchini mixture, then pour cheese and egg mixture on top.
Bake at 350° for 18 to 20 minutes.

_enrichment.description: A zucchini quiche with mozzarella and herbs, perfect for a light summer dinner or meal prep.

title: Ham And Cheese Breakfast Souffle
ingredients: 18 to 20 slices white bread, crusts cut off
directions: In buttered 9 x 13-inch pan, place enough slices of bread to cover the bottom.
(I piece them to make sure it's all covered.) Cover the bread with ham and cheese.
Place remaining slices of bread over all, piecing as needed.
Beat eggs, milk and mustard until mustard dissolves.
Pour over bread.
Cover and refrigerate overnight.
Before baking, cover top with corn flakes and drizzle 1 stick melted margarine over top.
Bake for 1 to 1 1/2 hours in a 300° oven.
Will hold for additional hour at 200°.
Serves 8 to 12.

_enrichment.description: A fluffy ham and cheese breakfast soufflé, ideal for a weekend brunch or special occasion.

title: Marinated French Dip
ingredients: 1/3 c. ReaLemon lemon juice from concentrate
2 tsp. Wyler's or Steero beef flavor instant bouillon or 2 beef flavor bouillon cubes
6 French bread rolls, cut in half
directions: In large shallow baking dish, combine all ingredients, except brisket and bread; add brisket.
Cover; marinate in refrigerator 6 hours or overnight, turning occasionally.
Cover; roast at 325° for 2 to 2 1/2 hours or until tender, turning after 45 minutes. Remove meat from baking dish; reserve cooking juices.
Cut brisket into thin slices; serve on rolls with juices for dipping. Refrigerate leftovers.
Makes 6 to 8 servings.

_enrichment.description: A tender marinated French dip sandwich with beef brisket, perfect for a cosy winter meal or festive gathering.

title: Chicken Breasts In Sour Cream Almond Sauce
ingredients: 6 whole chicken breasts, halved and boned
directions: Saute chicken in butter until golden brown, about 3 minutes per side.
Remove from skillet. Add onion and garlic.
Cook and stir 2 to 3 minutes.
Add tomato paste and flour.
Stir until blended and smooth.
Gradually add chicken stock and sherry. Cook and stir about 5 minutes or until slightly thickened.
Return chicken to skillet.
Add almonds, tarragon, salt and pepper. Cover and simmer 30 minutes or until tender.

_enrichment.description: Chicken breasts in a creamy almond and tarragon sauce, offering a rich and elegant meal for autumn or winter.

title: Blended Gazpacho
directions: Remove garlic skins; peel away tomato skins and blend 1 clove garlic and 3 tomatoes in your electric blender.
Now add 1 onion (cut in quarters), 1 green pepper (cut in quarters) and 1 cucumber (peeled and cut in cubes) to blender mixture and blend again. Strain mixture into a large serving bowl.
Blend together eggs, vinegar, salt, cayenne and tomato juice long enough to mix thoroughly.
Stir into the serving bowl and chill in refrigerator. Add oil before serving.
Chop remaining tomato, onion, cucumber and green pepper in very fine pieces (some blenders will do this). Keep these 4 vegetables in separate piles.
Then put into 4 small bowls.
Heat butter with remaining clove of garlic; toss in bread cubes and fry until crisp and golden.
Put these into a small bowl.

_enrichment.description: A chilled Spanish-inspired soup blending tomatoes, cucumber, and peppers with a creamy egg and vinegar dressing, perfect for hot summer days.

title: Skillet-Creamed Potatoes
directions: Cut potatoes into 1/2-inch cubes.
Add sour cream, onion, chopped olives, salt and pepper into large pan.
Add potatoes; heat, stirring frequently, until the cream bubbles and potatoes are heated through.
Garnish with paprika and parsley.

_enrichment.description: Creamy potatoes with a tangy kick, featuring sour cream, olives, and a sprinkle of paprika for a comforting side dish.

title: Scrambled Eggs, Spanish Style
directions: Slice green peppers; slice onion thinly.

In 12-inch skillet over medium-high heat in tablespoon of hot olive or salad oil, cook green peppers, onion, garlic, dried thyme leaves and 1/2 teaspoon salt until vegetables are tender-crisp; add tomatoes and hot pepper sauce.
Cook 2 to 3 minutes until tomatoes are softened.
Spoon mixture onto warm large platter to keep warm.

_enrichment.description: Fluffy scrambled eggs with a Spanish twist, incorporating tomatoes, peppers, and a hint of thyme for a vibrant breakfast or brunch.

title: Spectacular Chicken Casserole
ingredients: 1 (5.2 oz.) pkg. wild rice with herbs
1 (16 oz.) can French-style green beans, drained
1 (10 3/4 oz.) can cream of celery soup
1 (8 oz.) can sliced water chestnuts, drained
directions: Preheat oven to 350°.
Prepare a 2-quart baking dish with Pam.
Cook rice for 5 minutes less than package directions. Mix remaining ingredients with rice; sprinkle with paprika. Bake, uncovered, for 30 minutes.
Makes 10 to 13 servings.

_enrichment.description: A hearty casserole combining wild rice, chicken, and water chestnuts in a creamy sauce, baked to golden perfection.

title: Jo'S Quick Spanish Rice
directions: In a large pot, heat the vegetable oil until very hot.
Stir in Sofrito and stir for 20 seconds.
Add rice; stir until all ingredients are well mixed.
Add black beans, water, salt, garlic powder, pepper and envelopes of Sazon; stir and bring to a boil. Add olives and/or Vienna sausages (optional).
Reduce heat to low. Cover and simmer for 20 to 25 minutes (until all water is absorbed).
Stir rice once with a large fork after 10 to 15 minutes.
When rice is done, turn off heat and let stand for 5 minutes.
Stir rice once again after 5 minutes and enjoy!

_enrichment.description: A quick and flavourful Spanish rice dish with black beans, sausage, and olives, simmered to a rich, savoury finish.

title: Spanish Rice Steaks
directions: Coat steaks with flour.
In skillet, brown steaks in oil. Transfer to lightly greased 9-inch square baking dish.
Season with salt and pepper; set aside.
Saute onion slices; add Spanish rice and sugar and mix well.
Spoon rice mixture over steaks; sprinkle with parsley flakes.

_enrichment.description: Crispy steaks topped with a sweet and savoury Spanish rice mixture, garnished with parsley for a simple yet elegant meal.

title: Spanish Shells
directions: Preheat oven to 350°.
Cook and drain pasta shells. Brown and drain ground beef.
Combine picante sauce, water, tomato sauce and taco seasoning.
Chop chilies and grate cheese. Stir 1/2 cup sauce into ground beef along with chilies, 1/2 cup cheese and 1/2 can fried onions.
Pour remaining sauce into bottom of 1 1/2-quart baking dish.
Stuff cooked shells with ground mixture. Arrange shells in dish.
Pour remaining sauce over shells.
Bake, covered, at 350° for 25 minutes.
Top with remaining cheese and fried onions.
Bake, uncovered, for 5 minutes.

_enrichment.description: Baked pasta shells stuffed with seasoned ground beef and cheese, smothered in a tangy tomato sauce for a comforting family dish.

title: Paella(Low-Fat)
ingredients: 1 (8 oz.) can stewed tomatoes, chopped (reserving liquid)
1/2 lb. medium shrimp, peeled and deveined
1 small red pepper, cut into strips
1 small green pepper, cut into strips
directions: Heat oil in Dutch oven over medium-high heat until hot.
Add chicken and stir until browned.
Add rice, onion and garlic. Cook, stirring, until onion is tender and rice is lightly browned. Add broth, tomatoes, tomato liquid, paprika, ground red pepper and saffron.
Bring to a boil over high heat; stir.
Reduce heat to low.
Cover and simmer 10 minutes.
Add shrimp, pepper strips and peas.
Cover and simmer 10 minutes or until rice is tender, liquid is absorbed and shrimp are opaque.

_enrichment.description: A lighter take on paella, featuring tender chicken, shrimp, and rice infused with saffron and paprika for a vibrant one-pot meal.

title: Spanish Chicken Casserole(Lower Fat)
directions: Saute chopped onion in 3 tablespoons chicken broth over low heat.
Add 14 ounces chicken broth and sour cream; continue to simmer.
Add flour and cornstarch; mix with a wire beater to blend.
Stir in water chestnuts, green chiles and mushrooms.
Cut tortillas into fourths.
Line a large casserole dish, sprayed with nonstick cooking spray, with part of the tortilla pieces.
Fold chicken into sauce.
Spoon chicken sauce over tortillas and sprinkle 1/2 of grated cheese on top.
Repeat layers and top with cheese.
Bake at 350° for 40 to 45 minutes.

_enrichment.description: A creamy, lower-fat casserole with chicken, mushrooms, and green chiles, layered with tortillas and topped with melted cheese.

title: Spanish Bean Soup(Florida Cracker Style)
directions: In a large heavy pot, put in bone and cover with water.
Add 2 onions and garlic. Bring to a low boil and cook for 30 minutes. Now add pepper, herb seasoning, chorizos and garbanzo beans. Simmer for 45 minutes.
Add potatoes and the last onions.
Taste, add more garlic if needed. Simmer for 1 hour.
Serve with warm Cuban bread.

_enrichment.description: A hearty Florida-style bean soup with garbanzos, chorizo, and potatoes, simmered with ham for a deeply savoury winter dish.

title: Spanish Rice With Turkey
directions: Cook turkey, onions and pepper until done, stirring constantly.
Stir in remaining ingredients.
Bring to a boil. Cover. Reduce heat and simmer 30 minutes, stirring occasionally. Serves 4.
Counts as 3 proteins, vegetable and 1 bread.

_enrichment.description: A simple Spanish-style rice dish with ground turkey, tomatoes, and peppers, seasoned with oregano and red pepper for a zesty meal.

title: Spanish Chicken
ingredients: 4 whole chicken breasts, cooked and boned
directions: Cut boned chicken into bite size pieces.
Mix soup, broth, chiles, onion and chicken.
Layer a 13 x 9-inch pan with Doritos, half of mixture, another layer of chips, remainder of mixture and final layer of chips.
Top with cheese and dash of paprika.
Bake at 325° for 45 minutes.

_enrichment.description: A cheesy, crunchy chicken bake layered with Doritos, green chiles, and melted cheese for a fun, indulgent family meal.

title: Low-Fat Spanish Rice
directions: In medium saucepan, over high heat, bring broth to a boil. Add rice, cover and reduce to low setting.
Cook 50 minutes or until rice is tender.
Saute garlic, onion, green pepper and celery in 3 to 4 tablespoons juice from tomatoes in a large skillet over medium-high heat for 5 to 7 minutes or until softened.
Add tomatoes to skillet with remaining juice.
Stir in seasonings and honey and bring to a boil.
Cover and reduce heat to simmer.
Cook for 10 minutes.
When rice is tender, stir into tomato mixture.
Heat until hot, approximately 5 minutes.

_enrichment.description: A lighter Spanish rice dish with brown rice, tomatoes, and peppers, seasoned with chili powder and honey for a balanced flavour.

title: Gazpacho Cooler
ingredients: 3/4 c. peeled cucumbers, coarsely chopped and seeded
directions: In blender, process tomatoes, cucumber, pepper, onion and garlic until smooth.
Stir in tomato juice, vinegar and dill weed. Add hot sauce and black pepper to taste.
Refrigerate at least 1 hour or until chilled.
Pour into glasses and garnish with sprouts.
Serves 6.

_enrichment.description: A refreshing gazpacho cooler with blended tomatoes, cucumber, and peppers, spiked with vinegar and hot sauce for a tangy sip.

title: Molded Chicken Salad
directions: Dissolve Jell-O in 1 cup hot chicken broth.
Stir well.
Add another cup chicken broth, grated onion, salt and lemon juice. Mix well using a wire whip.
Beat in mayonnaise, chicken, celery, green pepper and pimiento.
Pour into mold; sprinkle with paprika and chill.

_enrichment.description: A jellied chicken salad with a creamy mayonnaise dressing, set in a mould and garnished with paprika for a retro-style dish.

title: Company Spanish Rice
directions: Heat oil in large skillet.
Add garlic, celery and beef; brown, stirring constantly.
Mix rice, tomatoes, tomato paste, salt, bay leaf and cayenne pepper with hot water.
Add to garlic and celery; stir well and cover.
Bring to boil; reduce heat and add beef bouillon.

_enrichment.description: A hearty Spanish-inspired rice dish with ground beef, tomatoes, and spices, perfect for a filling weeknight meal.

title: Chicken Ring With Mushroom Sauce
ingredients: 2 chickens, seasoned and cooked tender (about 4 lb. each)
1 qt. milk or chicken stock (half and half may be used)
directions: Cut up chicken; combine ingredients in order given and mix well.
If stock is used in place of milk, use less salt.
Pack mixture in two generous sized buttered ring molds and bake in slow oven (325°) for 45 to 60 minutes.
Allow chicken rings to stand for 10 minutes or longer in a warm place (this insures easy removal), then turn onto two large platters and fill centers with mushroom sauce.

_enrichment.description: A classic British-style chicken dish baked in a ring mould with a creamy mushroom sauce, ideal for a dinner party.

title: Spanish Bean Soup
ingredients: 2 cans chickpeas or garbanzo beans, drained
directions: Heat oil on medium; saute onion and garlic for 1 minute.
Add remaining ingredients.
Bring to a boil, then reduce to a slow simmer for at least 2 hours.
Let cool slightly before serving for maximum flavor.
Serve with toasted Cuban or French bread for dipping.

_enrichment.description: A rustic Spanish bean soup with chickpeas, potatoes, and sausage, simmered for hours for deep flavour.

title: Arroz Con Pollo(Chicken And Rice - Spanish)
ingredients: 1 large chicken or pkg. thighs and legs
directions: Rub chicken with oregano mixture.
Brown chicken in lard. Reduce heat to moderate fire and add onions and green pepper; cook for 10 minutes.
Add tomato sauce and olives and cook for 10 minutes more.
Pour 2 cups of rice into 3 cups of boiling water; cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add chicken and sauces to rice and cook at moderate flame 15 minutes.
Cover pot and cook on low for another 15 to 20 minutes.
While cooking, turn rice from bottom to top with large spoon, not too often.
Serves 6.

_enrichment.description: A vibrant Spanish chicken and rice dish with olives and peppers, a one-pot wonder for a family meal.

title: Spanish Beans
directions: Brown about a pound of ground chuck in small amount of oil. When meat is browned, add onion.
Let this cook for several minutes.
Next, add celery and bell pepper, then a can of tomatoes. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Let these ingredients cook slowly for about 5 minutes.
At this point, if mixture is too juicy, it may be thickened by adding about a tablespoon of flour to make a smooth paste.
Add red kidney beans.
Simmer for 30 minutes.

_enrichment.description: A robust Spanish-style bean dish with ground beef and tomatoes, slow-cooked for maximum flavour.

title: Paella With Peas And Carrots
ingredients: 3 pounds chicken breasts, thighs and legs
1 (28-ounce) can whole tomatoes, undrained and chopped
1 (15-ounce) can soft-shelled or steamer clams in shell, drained, or 6 fresh cherrystone clams
1/2 cup frozen English peas and carrots
directions: Brown chicken in hot oil in a Dutch oven; remove chicken.
Add onion and garlic to pan; cook over medium-high heat, stirring constantly, until tender.
Add chicken, tomatoes and salt.
Cover and simmer 30 minutes.

_enrichment.description: A traditional Spanish paella with chicken, shrimp, and vegetables, infused with saffron for a fragrant dish.

title: Spanish Dip
directions: Brown meat and onion; add remaining ingredients and simmer. Put in pie plate and sprinkle 1/2 pound grated cheese.
Heat at 350° for 15 to 20 minutes.
Remove and top with sour cream and 1/2 pound cheese.
Return to oven for 5 minutes until cheese melts. Serve with chips or soft tortillas.

_enrichment.description: A creamy Spanish-style dip with ground beef and beans, topped with cheese and served with tortilla chips.

title: Marguerite'S Spanish Cream Dessert
ingredients: 1 c. light cream or half and half
toppings: chocolate sauce, crushed peaches, strawberries or raspberries, etc. (optional)
directions: In medium saucepan, stir in gelatine, 1 tablespoon sugar and salt.
Add milk blended with egg yolks.
Set over low heat and stir constantly for 5 minutes.
Remove from heat and stir in light cream or half and half and vanilla.
In separate bowl, beat egg whites until stiff, gradually beat in 4 tablespoons sugar.
Fold egg white mixture into gelatine mixture, blending well.
Pour into individual dessert dishes and chill until firm.
Serves 4 to 6.

_enrichment.description: A light and airy Spanish cream dessert, perfect for a summer treat or a dinner party finale.

title: Weight Watcher Gazpacho Cooler
ingredients: 1 c. mixed vegetable juice or tomato juice
3 Tbsp. freshly squeezed lime or lemon juice
directions: Pour vegetable juice into an ice cube tray, making 10 cubes. Freeze until solid.
Cut 2 slices from cucumber and reserve for garnish.
Pare and seed remaining cucumber, then cut into pieces. In blender, combine cucumber, pepper, onion, lime juice, vinegar, garlic and hot sauce.
Process on medium speed until smooth.
Stir in seltzer.

_enrichment.description: A refreshing Spanish-inspired gazpacho cooler with cucumber and lime, ideal for hot summer days.

title: He-Man Spanish Rice
directions: In skillet cook beef, onion, green pepper and garlic until vegetables are tender.
Pour off fat.
Add remaining ingredients. Cover, bring to a boil and cook over low heat 15 minutes or until rice is tender.
Makes 4 servings.

_enrichment.description: A quick and easy Spanish rice dish with ground beef and tomatoes, ready in under 30 minutes.

title: Quick And Easy Spanish Rice And Beef
ingredients: 1 (10 oz.) pkg. Birds Eye sweet corn or mixed vegetables
directions: Brown meat in a large skillet. Add tomatoes, water, corn and seasonings. Bring to a boil and boil about 2 minutes.
Stir in rice. Cover. Remove from heat.
Let stand 5 minutes. Fluff with fork.
Makes 4 servings.

_enrichment.description: A simple Spanish-style rice and beef dish with tomatoes and spices, perfect for a weeknight meal.

title: Wita'S Arroz(Spanish Rice)
directions: Brown uncooked rice in oil in skillet, stirring constantly. Add onion, salt, garlic and comino; brown for 1 more minute.
Add tomato sauce and water.
Cover with tight fitting lid and simmer for approximately 20 minutes or until rice is fluffy and liquid absorbed.
Do not stir rice while simmering.

_enrichment.description: A classic Spanish rice dish with tomato sauce and spices, cooked without stirring for perfect texture.

title: Spanish Pinto Beans
ingredients: 1 pkg. (1 lb. or more) pinto beans
directions: Boil beans until tender. In a large skillet put olive oil and heat for 3 to 4 minutes.
Add the other ingredients and saute for 5 minutes.
Pour this sauce over the beans and cook for 10 minutes.
For better flavor, add cut up lean ham to the sauce.

_enrichment.description: A hearty Spanish pinto bean dish with tomatoes and peppers, slow-cooked for deep flavour.

title: Microwave Spanish Rice
directions: In a 3-quart casserole dish, crumble beef.
Add remaining ingredients and mix well, cutting tomatoes to distribute evenly. Cover.
Microwave on High 12 to 14 minutes, stirring after 6 minutes.
Makes 4 to 6 servings.

_enrichment.description: A quick microwave Spanish rice dish with ground beef and tomatoes, ready in under 20 minutes.

title: Granny'S Spanish Roast
ingredients: 1 (3 to 5 lb.) chuck or venison roast
directions: Salt and pepper the roast. Brown in 3 tablespoons of oil or shortening.
Mix garlic, chili powder, ketchup, Worcestershire sauce and water.
Pour this over the browned roast.
Add onions. Bake at 350° for 2 1/2 to 3 hours until tender.
Serve with rice. Gravy is delicious.

_enrichment.description: A savoury Spanish-style roast with a tangy sauce, slow-cooked until tender and served with rice.

title: Texas Style Spanish Rice
directions: In a skillet, saute onion and green pepper in oil for about 2 minutes.
Add rice and stir until coated with oil.
Add tomatoes, turmeric, cumin, salt, garlic powder and water.
Bring to a boil. Reduce heat and simmer, covered, about 20 minutes or until liquid is absorbed.
Add cilantro if desired.
Yield: 6 servings.

_enrichment.description: A hearty Tex-Mex twist on Spanish rice, featuring aromatic spices and a touch of turmeric for colour. Perfect as a side or light main.

title: Spanish Rice(Arroz Con Tomate)
directions: In skillet, cook green pepper, onion, garlic, basil and rosemary in hot oil until vegetables are tender.
Stir in rice, chopped tomatoes, salt, pepper and 2 cups water.
Cook, covered, over low heat for about 20 minutes, or until rice is done.
Makes 4 servings.

_enrichment.description: A classic Spanish tomato-infused rice dish with fragrant herbs like basil and rosemary. Simple yet flavourful.

title: Spanish Style Eggplant
ingredients: 1 medium eggplant (4 c., peeled if desired)
4 c. fresh tomato wedges, or salt-free canned
directions: Saute eggplant, green pepper, onion and mushrooms in spray-coated or non-stick fry pan.
Add water as needed, a tablespoon at a time, to prevent sticking.
Add remaining ingredients and simmer until eggplant is tender and flavors are blended.
This may be served as a vegetable or as a topping over brown rice.
Yields seven 1-cup servings.

_enrichment.description: A rich, spiced vegetable stew with eggplant as the star, featuring Mediterranean herbs and a touch of smokiness.

title: Dot'S Spanish Omelet(Breakfast Or Brunch)
directions: Beat these first 3 ingredients together well.
Grease both sides of an omelet pan lightly with margarine and place over medium heat.
Divide egg mixture in half and pour part in each side of the omelet pan.

When 3/4 of the way done, spoon some Prego sauce (with meat), grated sharp Cheddar cheese, some chopped onion and some chopped green pepper over each side of the divided omelet. Then, flip omelet over, and cook a few seconds, more, so that the Prego sauce and other ingredients have a chance to warm through, thoroughly.
Place on dish and enjoy!

_enrichment.description: A fluffy, cheesy omelette with a hint of Spanish-inspired flavours, ideal for a leisurely brunch.

title: Spanish Pork Chops And Rice
ingredients: 4 (1-inch thick) pork chops (about 2 lb. in all)
1 green bell pepper, cut into chunks
1/3 c. black olives, pitted and cut up
directions: Brown chops in oil over medium-high heat for about 8 minutes. Remove.
Add onions and green pepper; cover and cook 3 minutes. Add tomato, garlic, spices, salt and rice; stir 1 minute.
Add stock; cover and simmer 5 minutes.
Add chops and any accumulated juices.
Cover and cook until rice is done and chops are cooked through, about 20 minutes.
Sprinkle with olives and parsley.

_enrichment.description: Succulent pork chops simmered with aromatic spices and served with a fragrant Spanish-style rice.

title: Spanish Rice And Chicken
ingredients: 1 (2 1/2 to 3 lb.) broiler/fryer chicken, cut up
directions: Place chicken in greased 13 x 9 x 2-inch baking pan.
Combine garlic salt, celery salt and paprika; sprinkle over chicken. Bake, uncovered, at 425° for 20 minutes.
Remove chicken from pan. Combine rice, onion, green pepper and parsley; spoon into the pan.
In a saucepan, bring broth, tomatoes, salt and chili powder to a boil.
Pour over rice mixture; mix well.
Place chicken pieces on top.
Cover and bake for 45 minutes or until chicken and rice are tender.
Yield:
4 to 6 servings.

_enrichment.description: Tender chicken baked with a medley of spices and served atop a bed of Spanish-style rice.

title: Fern'S Gazpacho
directions: In a blender, puree tomatoes, onion, green pepper and cucumber.
Add tomato juice, cumin, garlic, salt and pepper.
Put in a bowl; cover and chill.
Before serving, add oil and vinegar. Serve accompanied by side dishes of chopped vegetables.
Garnish with croutons.

_enrichment.description: A refreshing, chilled Spanish gazpacho with a blend of fresh vegetables and zesty vinegar.

title: Lowfat Spanish Rice With Smoked Sausage
ingredients: 1 1/2 c. chopped low fat smoked sausage
directions: Combine the bell pepper, onion, and broth.
Cook until peppers and onions are tender.
Add sausage, rice, tomato sauce, water, salt, and black pepper.
Cover and cook over low heat for about 8 to 10 minutes or until the rice has absorbed the liquid. Taste and adjust seasoning, if needed.
Makes 6 generous servings. with about 1 gram of fat per serving.

_enrichment.description: A lighter take on Spanish rice with smoky sausage, using low-fat ingredients for a guilt-free dish.

title: Vegetable Paella
ingredients: 1 can artichoke hearts, drained and chopped
3 peppers (green and red), cut into strips
directions: In a flat bottomed pan, saute onions in oil over low heat until tender.
Stir in tomatoes, leeks, artichokes, peppers, asparagus and peas.
Add 1 cup stock.
Bring to a boil and simmer 10 minutes. Blend in garlic, parsley and remaining stock.
Season to taste. Add rice and cook over medium heat for 20 to 25 minutes, until rice is cooked and all liquid has evaporated.
Stir in saffron and let stand 5 minutes.
Serves 4.

_enrichment.description: A vibrant paella packed with seasonal vegetables and saffron-infused rice, a true Spanish classic.

title: Spanish Style Beef And Rice Casserole
ingredients: 11/4 lb. boneless beef chuck shoulder steak, cut 3/4-inch thick
directions: Cut steak into 1/4-inch wide strips; cut strips into 2-inch pieces.
Heat oven to 350°.
Heat oil in large skillet over medium-high heat 5 minutes.
Cook and stir beef, bell pepper, onion and garlic 2 to 3 minutes or until beef is no longer pink; place in 2-quart casserole.
Stir in rice, chili powder, salt and pepper.
Add enough water to tomatoes to measure 2 cups; add to casserole.
Cover tightly and bake 50 minutes or until rice is tender.
Remove from oven; stir in peas.
Makes 4 servings.

_enrichment.description: A comforting casserole of tender beef and rice, spiced with chilli and tomatoes for a hearty meal.

title: Spanish Brown Rice
directions: Brown garlic, rice and onion in olive oil.
Add pepper and brown.
Add chili powder, tomatoes and water.
Bring to a boil and cook on low heat until all liquid is absorbed and rice is tender, about 45 minutes.
Check after 25 minutes and add more water if needed.
Serve with grated cheese on the side to be added just before eating.

_enrichment.description: A nutty brown rice dish with a hint of chilli and tomatoes, served with melted cheese.

title: Congealed Gazpacho Salad With Avocado Cream
ingredients: 1 small jar diced pimento, drained and diced
directions: Sprinkle gelatin over 1 cup tomato juice in a medium saucepan; let stand 1 minute.
Cook over low heat, stirring until gelatin dissolves.
Remove from heat; stir in remaining tomato juice, white wine vinegar and next 4 ingredients.
Chill until the consistency of unbeaten egg white.
Fold in tomato and the next 3 ingredients. Spoon into 10 slightly oiled 1/2-cup molds.
Chill until firm. Unmold onto lettuce-lined salad plates.
Top with Avocado Cream. Garnish, if desired.
Yields 10 servings.

_enrichment.description: A chilled, jellied gazpacho salad with a creamy avocado topping, perfect for warm days.

title: Spanish Paella
ingredients: 2 lb. boned chicken breasts, skinned, sauteed, cut into 1-inch strips and set aside in a bowl
1 chorizo sausage, cooked well, drained of fat, mashed and set aside in a bowl
1 lobster tail, cleaned and cut into chunks
2 lb. fresh fish (halibut, scallops, sea bass), cooked, sauteed in butter and set aside in a bowl
directions: Preheat oven to 400°.
In a large paella pan or iron skillet, saute onion and red bell pepper in butter until softened.
Add rice to pan and cook 10 minutes.
Do not brown.
Add more butter, if necessary.
Stir in chicken broth and saffron and bring to a boil, stirring occasionally.
Reduce to a low simmer and cook, uncovered, for 20 minutes or until rice is tender.

_enrichment.description: A luxurious paella with seafood, chicken, and saffron, showcasing the best of Spanish coastal cuisine.

title: Garden Gazpacho
ingredients: 2 large ripe tomatoes, peeled, seeded and cut into chunks (1 1/4 lb.)
1 2/3 c. (15 oz. container) Contadina refrigerated light garden vegetable sauce
directions: Position knife blade in food processor bowl; add tomatoes and pulse 10 to 15 times or until finely chopped.
Combine tomatoes, sauce and next 6 ingredients in a large glass container; cover and refrigerate 4 hours.
Top each serving equally with croutons and serve immediately.
Yield: 4 servings (105 calories, 9 from fat, per 1 1/4 cup serving).

_enrichment.description: A quick and refreshing gazpacho with a kick of hot sauce, topped with crispy croutons.

title: Spanish Cocoa Cake
directions: Mix together flour, cocoa, salt and baking soda.
Cream shortening or oil with sugar until fluffy; add eggs and vanilla and beat thoroughly.
Add dry ingredients and milk alternately in small amounts.
After each addition mix well.
Pour into greased pan and bake at 350° for 30 to 35 minutes.

_enrichment.description: A rich, moist chocolate cake with a hint of vanilla and sour cream for extra tenderness.

title: Spanish Piccadilly
directions: Chop the onion, pepper and garlic.
Fry in olive oil about 5 minutes. Add meat. Stir and cook good for 10 minutes. Add sauce, raisins, olives and tomatoes.
Cook on low heat 40 minutes, covered.
Serve over rice!

_enrichment.description: A hearty Spanish-inspired stew with ground beef, sweet and savoury elements, served over rice for a comforting meal.

title: Spanish Meat Balls
directions: Mix well the first 9 ingredients; shape into small balls.
Put into a large greased casserole.
Heat soup, water and remaining chili powder; pour over meat balls.
Bake, covered, at 375° for 2 hours.
Yields 6 servings.

_enrichment.description: Juicy meatballs baked in a spiced tomato broth, perfect for a cosy family dinner with a touch of warmth.

title: Spanish Rice
directions: Brown onions lightly in shortening.
Add meat.
Break up particles so meat mixture is very fine.
When brown, drain and add peppers, tomato juice and water.
Add rice. Season to taste, stirring well.
Bring to a boil.
Reduce heat.
Let simmer about 30 minutes or until peppers are tender.

_enrichment.description: A simple yet flavourful Spanish-style rice dish with ground beef, peppers, and tomato juice for a satisfying side or light meal.

title: Spanish Rice(Arroz Espanol)
directions: In medium skillet, saute chopped peppers in margarine until done.
Add tomato sauce, bouillon and spices.
Bring to a boil and simmer for 5 minutes.
Remove from heat and add rice.
Simmer for 5 minutes.
Mix well and serve.

_enrichment.description: A quick and aromatic Spanish rice dish with bell peppers and spices, ready in under 20 minutes for a vibrant side.

title: Gazpacho Soup
ingredients: 1 (46 oz.) can tomato juice or V-8 juice
directions: Grate or chop vegetables fine by hand or use a food processor.
Mix the remaining ingredients thoroughly and combine with vegetables.
Refrigerate several hours or overnight.
Makes 8 servings.

_enrichment.description: A refreshing, chilled Spanish soup packed with fresh vegetables and tangy seasonings, ideal for hot summer days.

title: Spanish Noodles
directions: Cut bacon into pieces and brown.
Add ground beef and cook until all red is gone.
Add noodles, onion, salt, pepper, chili sauce, peppers and tomatoes.
Be sure noodles are moistened.
Cook on high until steam appears.
Turn burner down and continue cooking 45 minutes.

_enrichment.description: A hearty noodle dish with ground beef, bacon, and peppers in a spicy tomato sauce, perfect for a filling weeknight meal.

title: Easy Gazpacho
directions: Rub inside of bowl with garlic.
Combine soups and seasonings. Add chopped vegetables.
Mix well and chill several hours.
Even better the next day.
Serves 6.

_enrichment.description: A simplified version of gazpacho with creamy tomato bisque and fresh vegetables, chilled for a refreshing meal.

title: Spanish Rice And Shrimp
ingredients: few drops liquid red pepper seasoning
directions: Saute onion, green pepper and garlic in oil in nonstick skillet for 3 minutes.
Add rice, chili powder, cumin, salt and pepper.
Cook, stirring, 1 minute.
Add water to chicken broth to make 2 1/4 cups.
Add to skillet.

_enrichment.description: A vibrant Spanish rice dish with shrimp, peppers, and spices, offering a light yet flavourful main course.

title: Spanish Cream
ingredients: fruit to put on after it is put on plate to serve (optional)
directions: In top of a double boiler, scald milk.
Slowly add gelatin and honey.
Pour a few tablespoonfuls over egg yolks, then add egg mixture to milk mixture in boiler.
Place over hot water and cook until thick and smooth.
Remove from heat.
Add salt and vanilla, then fold in egg whites.
Pour into a large mold and chill.
The mixture will form 2 layers as it sets.

_enrichment.description: A silky Spanish cream dessert made with milk, eggs, and honey, lightly set for a delicate finish.

title: Quick Spanish Rice
ingredients: 1 (28 oz.) can whole tomatoes, chopped and undrained
directions: Brown the beef in a skillet; crumble.
Drain off fat.
Add remaining ingredients except rice, stir to mix well; bring to a boil.
Add the instant rice; cover and remove from heat.
Let stand for 5 minutes.

_enrichment.description: A quick and easy Spanish rice dish with ground beef and tomatoes, ready in under 15 minutes for a speedy meal.

title: Spicy Spanish Rice
directions: Brown beef.
Add onion, green pepper, salt, brown sugar, chili powder, bay leaf and rest of the ingredients.
Cook, covered, until rice and vegetables are done.
Stir often.
Serves 6 to 8.

_enrichment.description: A spicy and slightly sweet Spanish rice dish with ground beef and peppers, offering a bold flavour profile.

title: Spanish Cabbage
directions: Fry bacon; break in pieces.
Saute onion and green pepper. Chop cabbage.
Add tomatoes, salt and pepper and vinegar.
Put all in one pot and boil until cabbage is done.

_enrichment.description: A simple and tangy Spanish cabbage dish with bacon, peppers, and tomatoes, perfect as a side or light meal.

title: Spanish Pork Chops
directions: Roll pork chops in flour and onion mixture.
Brown pork chops in oiled skillet.
Add whole tomatoes, salt and pepper.
Cover with tomato sauce and simmer until tender.

_enrichment.description: Juicy pork chops simmered in a rich tomato sauce with onions, a classic Spanish dish for a hearty meal.

title: Chicken In Wine(Serves 6)
directions: Place chicken pieces in flat baking dish.
Combine flour and 1/4 cup sour cream.
Blend until smooth; add remaining sour cream, salt, soup and wine.
Pour over chicken.
Sprinkle with almonds, pimientos and paprika.
Bake at 325° about 1 1/2 hours or until chicken is tender.

_enrichment.description: Tender chicken baked in a creamy wine and mushroom sauce, garnished with almonds and pimientos for a festive touch.

title: New World Spanish Corn Pudding
directions: Blend thawed, uncooked corn with milk at high speed until liquid.
If blender is not available, force through a strainer; set aside.
Mix vanilla, sugar, margarine, cinnamon and salt.
Add eggs and mix with corn.
Pour in an ungreased pan at 350° until firm, 25 to 45 minutes (time varies depending on size of pan).
It will be soft set.
Sprinkle with cinnamon while still hot if desired.
Cool in pan.
Serve cold.

_enrichment.description: A sweet and creamy corn pudding with a hint of cinnamon, inspired by New World and Spanish flavours for a comforting dessert.

title: Pork Chop Spanish Rice
ingredients: 2 oz. sharp American cheese, shredded (1/2 c.)
directions: Trim excess fat from chops.
Slowly brown chops in melted shortening about 15 to 20 minutes; drain off excess fat.
Combine salt, chili powder and pepper.
Sprinkle over meat.
Add rice, onion and chopped green pepper.
Pour tomatoes over.
Cover and cook over low heat 35 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add green pepper and cook 5 minutes or until meat is tender.
Sprinkle with cheese.
Makes 5 servings.

_enrichment.description: A hearty dish featuring pork chops served with a flavourful Spanish-style rice cooked with tomatoes, peppers, and spices. Perfect for a comforting family meal.

title: Spanish Coffee Cake
directions: Mix flour, sugars, cinnamon, salt and oil until crumbly.
Take out 1 cup of mixture and add pecans to it to make the topping.
To remaining mixture, add baking soda, baking powder, egg and 1/2 cup buttermilk.
Beat 2 minutes.
Add 1/2 cup buttermilk.

_enrichment.description: A moist and crumbly coffee cake with a cinnamon-pecan topping, ideal for brunch or a sweet treat with afternoon tea.

title: Spanish Rice Casserole
directions: Mix ground beef, salt and pepper.
Form into small balls. Heat in a large skillet the margarine and meat balls.
Brown slightly.
Remove meat balls.
Add to skillet the rice, onion and green pepper.
Cook over medium heat, stirring constantly until lightly browned.
Add 1 cup of water, tomatoes and mushrooms; mix well.
Add meat balls and bring to a boil.
Cover and reduce heat. Simmer, covered, for 30 minutes.

_enrichment.description: A baked casserole combining ground beef, rice, and vegetables in a savoury tomato-based sauce, perfect for a filling family dinner.

title: Spanish Corn Bread
ingredients: 1 c. shredded cheese (keep half of cheese back)
directions: Mix all ingredients together.
Have pan greased and hot.
Pour half of batter in a pan.
Sprinkle with half of cheese.
Pour rest of batter in pan and bake to almost done.
Then sprinkle rest of cheese over all and finish baking.

_enrichment.description: A moist and cheesy cornbread with a slightly crispy top, often served as a side dish with Mexican-inspired meals.

title: Spanish Tomato Rice
directions: Brown beef; drain.
Add onions and peppers.
Cook until tender.
Add remaining ingredients. Cover and simmer for 45 minutes.
Serve with Mexican cornbread and fried beans.

_enrichment.description: A rich and slightly sweet tomato rice dish with ground beef and peppers, often served with cornbread and beans for a hearty meal.

title: Spanish Burgers
directions: Brown onion and hamburger; drain.
Add rest of ingredients together in skillet; cook 5 minutes.
Bake in a shallow pan at 375° for 30 minutes.
Place in buns and serve.

_enrichment.description: Juicy hamburgers with a Spanish-inspired twist, featuring tomato juice and spices, served in buns for a quick and satisfying meal.

title: Spanish Hominy
ingredients: 2 mild jalapeno peppers or to taste (can use canned peppers)
directions: In large saucepan, saute onion in margarine until slightly browned.
Blend in flour and milk; stir.
Add hominy, cheese and peppers.
Cook over low heat until cheese has melted and is thick. Serve with Mexican food or great at a fish fry.
Serves 6 to 8.

_enrichment.description: A creamy and slightly spicy side dish made with hominy, cheese, and jalapeños, perfect for pairing with Mexican cuisine.

title: Spanish Potatoes
directions: Boil potatoes.
Cool and peel.
Cut into 1/2-inch thick round slices.
Combine remaining ingredients in a separate bowl.
Coat the bottom and sides of a shallow 2-inch baking dish with butter. Arrange a layer of sliced potatoes on the bottom and spread with mayonnaise combination.
Repeat and top with another layer of spread.
Bake in a 375° oven for about 15 minutes or until all cheese topping melts.
Cut into squares.
Serves 6.

_enrichment.description: Boiled potato slices layered with a creamy, tangy spread and topped with cheese, baked until golden and bubbly.

title: Crock-Pot Spanish Rice
directions: Brown beef in skillet and drain off fat.
Put all ingredients in crock-pot (3 1/2 or 4-quart model).
Stir thoroughly.
Cover and cook on low 7 to 9 hours (high 3 hours).

_enrichment.description: A slow-cooked rice dish with ground beef, tomatoes, and spices, perfect for a hands-off meal that fills the home with enticing aromas.

title: Spanish Style Rice
directions: Combine tomato sauce, water, onion, green pepper, butter, salt, sugar, mustard and pepper in saucepan.
Cover and bring to a full boil.
Stir in rice.
Remove from heat; cover and let stand 5 minutes.
Stir with a fork.
Makes 3 1/2 cups or 4 servings.

_enrichment.description: A simple yet flavourful rice dish cooked with tomato sauce, peppers, and spices, ready in under 30 minutes for a quick meal.

title: California Gazpacho
ingredients: 3 medium tomatoes, peeled, seeded and diced
1 medium size green pepper, seeded and diced
directions: Combine all ingredients in a large bowl, stirring well.
Cover and chill 8 hours or overnight.
Ladle into soup bowls.
Yields 6 cups (53 calories per 1-cup serving).

_enrichment.description: A refreshing and vibrant cold soup made with fresh tomatoes, cucumber, peppers, and herbs, perfect for hot summer days.

title: Spanish Omelet
directions: In medium skillet cook and stir green pepper and onion in butter over low heat until onion is tender.
Stir in tomato sauce, sugar and seasoning.
Simmer 10 minutes, until sauce is thickened. Fills approximately 2; 3 egg omelets.

_enrichment.description: A thick and savoury omelette filled with peppers, onions, and a rich tomato sauce, ideal for a light yet satisfying meal.

title: Spanish Rice(Serves 6)
directions: Brown hamburg; add onion and celery.
Cook until tender, not brown.
Put in casserole.
Add rice, bean sprouts, mushrooms and juice on both.
Add tomatoes and seasoning.
Bake at least 1 hour or longer at 325°.

_enrichment.description: A baked rice casserole with ground beef, bean sprouts, and mushrooms in a savoury tomato sauce, perfect for a family dinner.

title: Slow Cooker Spanish Rice
directions: Brown beef in skillet and drain off fat.
Put all ingredients in crock-pot.
Stir thoroughly.
Cover and cook on low for 7 to 8 hours.
May be cooked on higher temperature if you are home to stir (about 3 hours).
Serves 6.

_enrichment.description: A slow-cooked rice dish with ground beef, tomatoes, and spices, ideal for a hands-off meal that delivers rich, comforting flavours.

title: Beefy Spanish Rice
directions: In a large skillet, cook ground beef, onion, green pepper and garlic until the meat is browned; drain.
Stir in the next eight ingredients; bring to a boil.
Reduce heat; cover and simmer for 20 minutes or until the rice is tender.
Stir in tomato paste and heat through.
Serve with French bread.
Yield:
4 to 6 servings.

_enrichment.description: A hearty and flavourful rice dish with ground beef, tomatoes, and herbs, served with French bread for a satisfying meal.

title: Alice'S Spanish Pork Chops
ingredients: 6 pork chops, center cut and extra thick
directions: Brown pork chops in vegetable oil.
Place in large casserole. Top with remaining ingredients.
Cover and bake at 350° for 1 to 2 hours or until tender.
This is a meal in itself.
Serve with tossed salad.

_enrichment.description: A hearty one-pot Spanish-inspired dish featuring pork chops slow-cooked with vegetables and tomato soup for a tender, savoury meal.

title: Spanish Coffee
directions: Use red wine glasses with a large bowl.
Heat water in glasses in microwave.
Make one drink a a time.
Place the Cointreau and sugar in the glass and swirl, then flame, caramelizing the sugar. Add coffee.
Add rest of liquor.
Top with whipped cream, sugar and chocolate jimmies.
Makes 1.
Turn off the lights when flaming; a great show and a great drink!

_enrichment.description: A flaming Spanish coffee cocktail made with brandy, coffee, and chocolate, served in a large-bowled glass for a dramatic presentation.

title: Spanish Spaghetti
directions: Heat oil; add onions and green peppers.
Cook until soft.
Add ground beef, salt, black pepper and chili pepper.
Cook until browned.
Add tomato sauce, tomato paste and 4 cups tomatoes and 1 teaspoon sugar.
Cook together 15 minutes on medium heat.
Break spaghetti into pieces and cook according to directions on package. Do not overcook.
Drain and add to sauce mix and put into casserole.
Put into oven at 350° for 20 minutes.

_enrichment.description: A robust Spanish-style spaghetti with ground beef, tomatoes, and chilli peppers, offering a rich, meaty sauce over pasta.

title: Spanish Green Beans
directions: Cook beans in boiling salt water until tender; drain.
Heat olive oil in large skillet.
Add onions, parsley, garlic, ham and bacon; cook until ham and bacon are done.
Add wine, nutmeg and salt.
Add beans and small amount of water, if necessary.
Cover; simmer until heated through.
Coarse black pepper sprinkled on top can be added.
Makes 6 servings.

_enrichment.description: Tender green beans simmered with ham, bacon, and white wine, finished with nutmeg for a savoury, aromatic side dish.

title: Spanish Bar Cake
directions: Put apple pie mix into blender with eggs, apple pie spice and cloves.
Blend until smooth.
Dump into 2-quart mixing bowl and beat in cake mix with electric mixer on medium speed, blending thoroughly.
Remove beaters.
Stir in raisins and nuts.
Pour into greased and floured 9 x 13 x 2-inch pan.
Bake at 325° for 50 to 55 minutes, until toothpick inserted in center comes out clean. Cool.
Frost.

_enrichment.description: A moist, spiced apple cake with raisins and nuts, blending sweet apple pie filling with a fluffy cake batter.

title: Sangria (Spanish Wine Punch)
directions: Combine juices, wine, rum and sugar.
Stir to dissolve sugar. Slice orange thinly.
Cut slices in half.
Add orange slices, maraschino cherries and cinnamon stick to wine mixture.
Let stand one hour.
Remove cinnamon stick.
Pour into water goblets one-quarter filled with cracked ice or small ice cubes.
Yields 12 to 24 servings.

_enrichment.description: A refreshing Spanish sangria with citrus juices, wine, and rum, garnished with fresh fruit and cinnamon for a vibrant punch.

title: Grandma Vi'S Spanish Yellow Rice
ingredients: 1 lb. medium shrimp, peeled and deveined
2 pkg. Spanish saffron (can be found in produce section at Weis Markets)
salt, pepper and dash of oregano to taste
directions: In 1/4 cup vegetable oil, simmer shrimp, onion and green pepper for 3 minutes.
Add tomato juice, saffron, sugar and bay leaf.
Cover and simmer 5 minutes.
Add water, salt, pepper and oregano.
Bring to a boil.
Add rice.
Reduce heat, cover and cook for 20 minutes.
Stir occasionally until rice is cooked.
Serves 6.

_enrichment.description: A fragrant Spanish yellow rice with shrimp, saffron, and tomato juice, offering a colourful, aromatic one-pot dish.

title: Spanish Steak
directions: Melt butter in heavy frying pan.
Add onions and cook several minutes.
Add remaining ingredients and simmer on top of stove for 1 hour.
Serves 6.

_enrichment.description: A simple Spanish-style steak simmered with onions, ketchup, and tomato juice for a tangy, savoury main course.

title: Spanakorizo(Spanish Rice)
ingredients: 2 (10 oz.) pkg. frozen cut leaf spinach, thawed and drained
directions: Saute onion in oil until transparent.
Add the rice, tomato sauce, spinach and parsley; stir well.
Add the water, salt and pepper and bring to boil.
Reduce heat; cover and simmer until rice is cooked.
Before serving, add the juice of half a lemon.

_enrichment.description: A Greek-inspired spinach rice dish with lemon and tomato sauce, offering a light yet flavourful one-pot meal.

title: Spanish Cornbread
directions: Mix all ingredients together.
Pour in pan that has been greased and dusted
with corn meal.
Bake in oven at 425° until brown.

_enrichment.description: A savoury cornbread with cheese, onion, and hot peppers, baked until golden and served as a hearty side or snack.

title: Black Bean Gazpacho(Soup)
ingredients: 3 c. chopped, peeled and seeded tomatoes
1 (12 oz.) can (1 1/2 c.) hot-style vegetable juice cocktail *
1 (15 oz.) can black beans or garbanzo beans, rinsed and drained
1/2 c. chopped seeded cucumber, yellow summer squash and/or zucchini
1/2 c. chopped green or yellow sweet pepper
directions: In a large mixing bowl, combine tomatoes, juice, ham, beans, cucumber or squash, chopped pepper, green onion and dressing. Cover and chill for 2 to 24 hours.
If desired, serve with bottled hot pepper sauce.
Makes 4 main-dish servings.

_enrichment.description: A chilled black bean gazpacho soup with tomatoes, cucumber, and Italian dressing, offering a refreshing, tangy starter.

title: Gazpacho Salad
directions: Sprinkle cucumbers with 1 teaspoon salt; let stand for 30 minutes.
Combine next 6 ingredients in large salad bowl.
Next add mushrooms and scallions.
Drain and pat dry cucumbers; add to the bowl, then add parsley, tomatoes and green pepper.
Cover; chill 4 hours.
Just before serving, add Swiss cheese and toss then.
Add garnish of hard-boiled egg slices.

_enrichment.description: A chilled gazpacho salad with cucumbers, tomatoes, mushrooms, and Swiss cheese, tossed in a tangy dressing.

title: Pop'S Spanish Rice
directions: Saute pepper and onion until tender.
Add beef; cook.
Add tomato sauce and chili powder.
(Needs to be extra spicy; rice will absorb hot taste.)
Add rice and 1 to 1 1/2 cups water. Stir.
Cover and cook for 20 minutes over low heat.
Stir occasionally.

_enrichment.description: A spicy Spanish rice with ground beef, bell peppers, and chilli powder, offering a bold, hearty side dish.

title: Gazpacho Dip
ingredients: 1 (4 oz.) can chopped black olives and liquid
1 (4 oz.) can chopped green chiles and liquid
directions: Blend oil and vinegar.
Add salt, garlic salt and pepper. Stir.
Mix with olives, green chiles, tomatoes, onions anad avocados.
Chill for several hours for flavors to mix.
Serve cold with tortilla chips.
Very good!

_enrichment.description: A chilled gazpacho dip with avocados, tomatoes, and olives, served with tortilla chips for a refreshing appetiser.

title: Arroz Con Pollo For 24
ingredients: 1 (24 oz.) pkg. frozen green peas, cooked and drained
directions: Bake chicken, uncooked, for 30 minutes.
Dissolve bouillon in water and add saffron; stir until dissolved.
Stir in onions, bay leaves, parsley and 4 teaspoons salt; heat to boiling.
Remove pans from oven.
