Remove chickens.
Drain fat from pans.

_enrichment.description: A large-batch Spanish chicken and rice dish with saffron, peas, and pimentos, offering a colourful, aromatic main course.

title: Spanish Delight
directions: Brown meat.
Saute it with the onion and bell pepper.
Add other ingredients except cheese and noodles.
Cook a while, then add the cheese and noodles.
Serve.

_enrichment.description: A hearty American casserole with a Spanish-inspired twist, blending cheddar cheese, beef, and creamy mushroom soup for a comforting dish.

title: Spanish Cauliflower
directions: Cook cauliflower in boiling water 5 to 6 minutes.
Drain well. Combine butter, sugar, salt, pepper and cracker crumbs.
Stir in green pepper, tomatoes, onion, 1 1/4 cups cheese and hot cauliflower.
Pour into greased 2-quart casserole dish.
Sprinkle with remaining cheese.
Bake at 350° for 1 hour.

_enrichment.description: A creamy, cheesy cauliflower bake with a crispy cracker topping, perfect for a cosy family meal.

title: Spanish Fudge
directions: In a large electric skillet, set at 280°, combine sugar, butter, cinnamon, salt and milk.
Bring to a boil; boil 5 minutes, stirring constantly.
Turn heat off and add remaining ingredients. Stir until well blended.
Pour into 8-inch buttered pan.
Cool before cutting.
Makes 2 pounds.

_enrichment.description: A rich, sweet fudge with marshmallows, chocolate, and nuts, offering a decadent treat for celebrations.

title: Sopaipillas (Spanish Fried Biscuit Puffs)
directions: Stir together flour, baking powder and salt.
Cut in shortening until mixture resembles cornmeal.
Gradually add the water, stirring with a fork (dough will be crumbly).
Turn onto lightly floured
board or surface; knead into a smooth ball.

_enrichment.description: Crispy, golden fried dough puffs drizzled with honey, a traditional Southwestern treat often served with savoury dishes.

title: Spanish Meal
directions: Brown ground beef in skillet.
Drain.
Add kidney beans, taco seasoning, mushrooms, taco sauce, jalapeno peppers and rice. Simmer.
Pour in baking dish.
Sprinkle cheese over the mixture and bake at 350° for 30 minutes.

_enrichment.description: A Tex-Mex-inspired baked casserole with ground beef, kidney beans, and melted cheese, ideal for a filling family dinner.

title: Spanish Rice With Shrimp
directions: Combine all ingredients, except shrimp, in 2-quart casserole. Cover.
Microwave at High (100%) for 10 minutes.
Reduce power to Medium (50%).
Microwave until liquid is absorbed and rice is tender, 28 to 35 minutes.
Stir in shrimp.
Let stand, covered, for 5 minutes.

_enrichment.description: A fragrant Spanish-style rice dish with shrimp, tomatoes, and herbs, cooked in a casserole for a one-pot meal.

title: Barbecued Spanish Ribs
directions: Bake at 325°.
Place meat in shallow pan.
In a bowl, combine salt, pepper, cayenne pepper, paprika, chili powder, catsup, water and vinegar.
Pour over meat.
Top with onion slices.
Bake for 1 hour, turn meat then continue baking 1 hour longer.
Baste meat occasionally with sauce while baking.

_enrichment.description: Tender spareribs slow-baked in a spicy, tangy barbecue sauce, infused with onions and a blend of warm spices.

title: Crab Gazpacho
ingredients: 1/3 c. each celery and onion, chopped
1/2 c. finely chopped green bell pepper
1/2 c. finely chopped red bell pepper
directions: Combine all ingredients, except crabmeat, in large bowl.
Cover and let stand refrigerated overnight or several hours.
Before serving, stir in crabmeat.	Adjust seasoning.
Serve with hearty bread.
Yields 4 to 6 servings.

_enrichment.description: A chilled seafood gazpacho with fresh crab, tomatoes, and herbs, offering a refreshing summer soup.

title: Molded Gazpacho(Lo-Cal; 5 Cups)
ingredients: 2 large tomatoes, peeled, seeded and chopped
3/4 c. green pepper, seeded and chopped
directions: Mix 1 cup tomato juice with gelatin and heat until dissolved. Add rest of tomato juice, wine vinegar and spices.
Chill until it begins to set, then mix with tomatoes, green onions, pepper, cucumber, celery and parsley.
Pour into mold and chill until firm. (Use ring mold.)

_enrichment.description: A light, jellied gazpacho mould with tomatoes, cucumbers, and peppers, perfect for a low-calorie starter.

title: Skillet Chicken Paella
ingredients: 1 1/4 lb. skinless, boneless chicken breasts, cut into bite size strips
1 Tbsp. olive oil or cooking oil
1/8 tsp. ground saffron or ground turmeric
1 (14 1/2 oz.) can stewed tomatoes, cut up
1 medium red sweet pepper, cut into strips
directions: Rinse chicken; pat dry with paper towels.
In a 10-inch skillet, cook chicken strips, half at a time, in hot oil for 2 to 3 minutes or until no longer pink.
Remove chicken from skillet. Add onion and garlic to skillet; cook until tender but not brown. Remove skillet from heat.
Add broth, uncooked rice, oregano, paprika, salt, pepper and saffron or turmeric.
Bring to boiling. Reduce heat.
Simmer, covered, about 15 minutes.
Add undrained tomatoes, sweet pepper and frozen peas to skillet.
Cover and simmer about 5 minutes more or until rice is tender.
Stir in cooked chicken.
Cook and stir about 1 minute more or until heated through.
Makes 6 main dish servings.

_enrichment.description: A vibrant skillet paella with chicken, saffron rice, and vegetables, capturing the essence of Spanish cuisine.

title: Spanakopeta(Spinach Squares)
ingredients: 2 pkg. frozen chopped spinach, thawed and drained
directions: Saut onions in butter until soft.
Add spinach and saut a few minutes longer; cool.
Add cream cheese, Feta, eggs, salt and pepper; mix well.
Place 10 layers of phyllo pastry sheets in a 12 x 16 x 2-inch pan, brushing each sheet well with melted butter. Add spinach mixture, then place another 10 layers of phyllo, buttering each sheet.
Bake at 350° for 30 minutes.
Cut into squares before serving.
You can brush the spanakopeta with a slightly beaten egg before baking for a nice luster.
I use margarine as all butter is very rich.

_enrichment.description: Flaky phyllo pastry filled with a creamy spinach and cheese mixture, baked until golden for a delightful snack.

title: Artichoke Pie
directions: Saute artichoke hearts, bread crumbs and garlic in olive oil until tender.
Scramble eggs in bowl and add remaining ingredients.
Put artichoke hearts in bottom crust; pour egg mixture over it.
Cover with top crust.
Bake at 350° for 45 minutes to 1 hour (golden brown).

_enrichment.description: A savoury artichoke and cheese pie with a buttery crust, offering a rich and satisfying bite.

title: *Orzo Salad
directions: Cook orzo according to package directions (al dente).
Drain and rinse with cold water. Drain again. Add Feta cheese, parsley, dill and tomato. In a jar with a tight fitting lid, combine oil, lemon juice, salt and pepper.
Cover and shake.
Pour over the salad and toss until the orzo is well covered. Refrigerate until cool. Toss again before serving.
Makes 8 servings.

_enrichment.description: A refreshing orzo salad with feta, herbs, and lemon, perfect for a light lunch or side dish.

title: Greek-Style Pasta Salad
ingredients: 8 oz. penne or other tubular pasta
1 medium cucumber, peeled, seeded and chopped
1 (2.2 oz.) can sliced ripe olives, drained
directions: In a large pot of boiling salted water, cook penne until just tender, 10 to 12 minutes; drain well.
Rinse with cold water and drain again.
Return pasta to pot.
Add olive oil and vinegar and toss to coat.
Add cucumber, olives, tomatoes, scallions, green beans, bell pepper, parsley, oregano, garlic, salt and pepper. Toss to mix well.
Sprinkle Feta cheese over salad.
Serve chilled or at room temperature.

_enrichment.description: A vibrant Greek-style pasta salad with olives, tomatoes, and feta, ideal for picnics or barbecues.

title: Koulourakia(Greek Butter Cookies)
directions: Preheat oven to 375°.
Cream butter gradually beating in sugar. Beat eggs until light; add to butter mixture and beat thoroughly. Add vanilla extract.
Add lemon rinds.
Sift together flour and baking powder.
Carefully blend into butter-egg mixture to make soft dough.

_enrichment.description: Buttery Greek butter cookies with a delicate lemon flavour, perfect with tea or coffee.

title: Crevettes Scampi
directions: Shell and devein shrimp, leaving tail intact; arrange in single layer on bottom of broiler pan.
Preheat broiler.
In small saucepan, heat olive oil, garlic, parsley, oregano, salt and red pepper.
Pour over shrimp; toss to coat.
Broil 4 inches from source of heat for about 5 minutes.
Sprinkle with lemon juice. Serves 6.

_enrichment.description: Succulent prawns sautéed in garlic-infused olive oil with a hint of chilli, finished under the grill and brightened with lemon for a quick, flavourful starter.

title: White Greek Cookies
directions: Cream butter well, then add sugar gradually.
Add egg yolks and vanilla, continuing to mix.
Fold in almonds and whiskey.

Add flour and baking powder and mix by hand until dough is formed. Then make small shaped cookies (round balls, flat half moons). Bake at 350° for 20 minutes (almost light gold) on ungreased cookie sheet.
Cool cookies before sifting confectioners sugar on top and bottom.

_enrichment.description: Buttery Greek almond cookies dusted with icing sugar, enriched with a touch of whiskey for a delicate, melt-in-the-mouth treat.

title: Eggplant Moussaka
ingredients: 1 large or 2 medium eggplants, cut into large chunks and unpeeled
directions: Steam eggplants for 2 to 3 minutes.
Saute onion, green pepper, garlic and celery in vegetable oil.
Add tomatoes and cook briefly (5 to 10 minutes).
Add to mixture the cracked wheat cereal and simmer 20 minutes or so until cereal is cooked.
(Add tomatoes or tomato sauce, if liquid evaporates).

_enrichment.description: A layered Greek casserole with roasted aubergine, spiced tomato sauce and cracked wheat, baked until golden and fragrant.

title: Pan Sauteed Spicy Shrimp
directions: Toss shrimp in the next 5 ingredients and coat with a little olive oil until moist.
Let marinate for a couple of hours or overnight.
When you are ready to cook the shrimp, add the salt, lemon juice and chopped scallions.
In a very hot skillet coated with oil, quickly saute the shrimp until firm.
Serve hot on a bed of greens as an appetizer, or deglaze the pan with some dry white wine and some butter to make a sauce and serve on pasta or rice.

_enrichment.description: Spicy, garlicky prawns quickly seared in a hot pan and served on greens, with a zesty lemon finish for a vibrant appetiser.

title: Shrimp And Sausage Rice
ingredients: 1 small box Uncle Ben's long grain rice, cooked
1 small can mushrooms (use fresh, if you want)
directions: Cook rice according to directions on box. Brown sausage. Saute onion, bell pepper and celery.
Mix rice, sausage and sauteed vegetables together.
Mix in soup and spices together. Use a 13 x 9-inch casserole.
Bake at 350° until bubbly.

_enrichment.description: A hearty one-pot dish of rice, sausage, shrimp and vegetables in a creamy mushroom sauce, baked until bubbling.

title: Chicken Pillows
directions: Cut chicken breasts in one-inch pieces.
Marinate in lemon juice, oil, oregano, salt and garlic.
Unwrap phyllo dough; place on wax paper and keep covered to keep moist.
Cut dough in half lengthwise forming 2 strips each 6-inches wide.
Fold in half crosswise; brush with melted butter.
Put two pieces of chicken at short end and roll; form a package.
Brush with melted butter and bake at 400° for about 15 minutes.

_enrichment.description: Tender chicken pieces wrapped in buttery phyllo pastry, baked until golden for a crisp, flaky parcel.

title: Eggplant And Feta Salad
ingredients: 1 medium eggplant, unpeeled and cut into 1-inch cubes
1 medium yellow squash, cut into 1-inch cubes
1 each red, green and yellow pepper, cored, seeded and cut into 1-inch cubes
freshly ground black pepper to taste
3/4 lb. Feta cheese, cut into 1/2-inch cubes
directions: Set the oven at 350°.
Pile the eggplant, squash, zucchini, pepper and onion in a large roasting pan and sprinkle them with oil.
Turn the vegetables in the oil so they are coated.
Bake the vegetables, tossing them a couple of times during the cooking, for 40 minutes or until tender.

_enrichment.description: A warm salad of roasted aubergine, courgette and peppers with feta, basil and oregano for a Mediterranean-inspired dish.

title: Pickled Garden Relish
ingredients: 4 oz. roasted red peppers or pimentos, drained and sliced
directions: Combine the vinegar, oil and seasonings in a large saucepan, stirring to dissolve sugar.
Add all other ingredients; toss to coat.
Bring to a boil on high heat; reduce to low and simmer, covered, for 10 to 20 minutes, or until cauliflower is crunchy but cooked.
Cool, then refrigerate a day before serving, stirring several times to marinate all ingredients equally.
Drain just before serving.
(Serve cold.)

_enrichment.description: A tangy, crunchy relish of pickled vegetables with olives and herbs, perfect for adding zing to sandwiches or platters.

title: Turkey Scalloppine
ingredients: 3/4 c. Marsala wine or dry sherry
directions: Skin turkey, slice meat across grain, as thin as possible. Salt and pepper slices, coat with flour.
Heat oil and butter in large skillet.
Lightly brown slices, a few at a time, pour off fat, return meat to skillet.
Add Marsala, thyme and oregano; cook until almost dry, turning slices gently as they cook (about 10 minutes).
Saute mushrooms in 2 tablespoons butter, add to turkey. Add beef broth.
Cook turkey over gentle heat until almost dry. Beat egg yolk with cream, remove skillet from heat.
Pour cream mixture over turkey slices, turn gently to glaze.
Makes 6 servings.

_enrichment.description: Thinly sliced turkey breast cooked in a rich Marsala wine sauce with mushrooms and cream for a luxurious main course.

title: Zucchini Stick Salad
directions: Slice zucchini and peppers into thin strips.
Heat oil in frying pan; stir in zucchini and peppers to coat evenly.
Cover and cook about 10 minutes, stirring occasionally until tender-crisp when pierced.
Do not overcook.
Remove from heat and stir in olives and green onions, 1 teaspoon garlic salt and pepper.
Turn into bowl.

_enrichment.description: A light salad of thinly sliced courgette and peppers with olives, capers and herbs, dressed with a tangy vinaigrette.

title: Grecian Shrimp
directions: Saute onion, lemon juice, oregano, butter in oil.
Add shrimp and cook until pink.
Before serving, add feta cheese in large chunks into hot mixture.
Serve hot over pasta or rice or serve cold on romaine lettuce.

_enrichment.description: Garlicky prawns finished with feta cheese, served hot over pasta or cold on lettuce for a simple Greek-inspired dish.

title: Arroz Con Pollo(Chicken And Rice)
ingredients: 2 1/2 lb. ready to cook chicken, cut up into serving size pieces
1 Tbsp. achote coloring or 1 envelope sazon goya
directions: Mix salt, garlic and oregano; rub chicken inside and out. Saute tomatoes, peppers and onion; add chicken and simmer 1/2 hour.
Add achote coloring, rice, olives and capers; mix well. Add liquid; stir and cover.
Cook until the rice is done and all water has been absorbed, about 25 minutes.
Serve hot.
Garnish with strips of pimentos and peas.

_enrichment.description: A vibrant Latin American one-pot of chicken and rice cooked with tomatoes, peppers and olives, coloured with achiote.

title: Peppered Rib-Eye Steaks
directions: Brush steaks lightly with olive oil.
In a small bowl, combine all seasonings.
Sprinkle seasonings over steaks and press into both sides.
Cover and chill for 1 hour.
Grill steaks, turning once, over medium-hot coals 14 to 18 minutes for rare; 18 to 22 minutes for medium; 24 to 28 minutes for well done.
Place on a warm serving platter; cut across the grain into thick slices. Garnish with orange slices and parsley, if desired.
Yields 8 servings.

_enrichment.description: Juicy rib-eye steaks seasoned with a peppery blend and grilled to perfection, served with orange slices and parsley.

title: Pepper Shrimp
ingredients: 1/2 c. fruity red wine, such as Beaujolais or Red Zinfandel
1/2 c. loosely packed flat parsley, chopped
2 lb. medium shrimp, peeled and deveined
directions: In a bowl, whisk together the wine, tomato sauce, chervil, lemon juice, mustard, oregano, salt and pepper.
Heat the oil in a skillet.
Cook the minced garlic over medium heat for 1 minute. Add the tomato sauce mixture and bring to a boil, then lower the heat and simmer for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Remove from heat and set aside to cool for 5 minutes.
Stir in chopped parsley. Place shrimp in a single layer in a nonmetal baking dish.
Pour the sauce over the shrimp and marinate, covered, for 3 hours at room temperature or overnight in refrigerator.
Preheat broiler.
Broil shrimp about 6 inches from the heat for 10 minutes or until cooked through.
Turn once.

_enrichment.description: Prawns marinated in a tangy red wine and tomato sauce with herbs, served chilled or warm as a sophisticated starter.

title: Spicy Steamed Clams
ingredients: 1/4 tsp. dried hot red pepper flakes or to taste
1 1/4 c. thinly sliced fennel bulb or celery, combined with 1/2 tsp. fennel seed
1 (14 oz.) can plum tomatoes, drained and chopped (reserve juice)
24 small, hard shelled clams, scrubbed well
2 Tbsp. minced fresh parsley leaves plus parsley sprigs (for garnish)
directions: In a heavy saucepan, cook the garlic, pepper flakes and oregano in the oil over moderately low heat, stirring, for 1 minute.
Add the onion and fennel; cook the mixture over moderate heat, stirring, until the fennel is softened.
Add the tomatoes with the reserved juice, wine and salt and pepper to taste; simmer the mixture, uncovered, stirring occasionally, for 5 minutes.
Add the clams; steam them, covered, for 5 to 7 minutes, or until they have opened.
Discard any unopened ones.

_enrichment.description: Clams steamed in a fragrant broth of garlic, fennel, tomatoes and white wine, served with crusty bread for soaking up the juices.

title: Marinated Pork Party Sandwiches
ingredients: cocktail rolls (I used Snowflake Rolls from Food Lion as a substitute)
directions: Combine first 6 ingredients; cover and chill at least 8 hours.(Mayonnaise mixture)
Trim fat from tenderloins.
Place tenderloins in a large heavy-duty, zip-top plastic bag.
Combine oil and next 6 ingredients; pour over tenderloins.
Seal bag; marinate in refrigerator 8 hours, turning bag occasionally.
Remove tenderloins from marinade, reserving marinade.
Place tenderloins on a rack in a shallow roasting pan.
Insert meat thermometer into thickest portion of 1 tenderloin.
Bake at 425° for 10 minutes. Reduce heat to 350° and bake an additional 30 minutes or until meat thermometer registers 160°, basting occasionally with marinade. Let stand 10 minutes.
Cut tenderloins into 1/4 inch slices and arrange on a serving platter lined with kale leaves, if desired. Serve with mayonnaise mixture and cocktail rolls.

_enrichment.description: Tender pork tenderloins marinated in a zesty mix of mayonnaise, white wine vinegar, and spices, then served in soft cocktail rolls for a crowd-pleasing sandwich.

title: Mama Susanna'S Chicken
directions: Pour half the cooking wine in bottom of casserole dish.
Put chicken, artichokes, olives, onion, garlic and oregano in and then add the rest of the wine.
Cover and bake at 375° for 1 hour. Garnish with Romano cheese.
Put over your favorite pasta.

_enrichment.description: Succulent chicken breasts baked with artichokes, olives, and white wine in a fragrant oregano sauce, perfect over pasta.

title: Recipe For Greek Salad
ingredients: 1 head lettuce, washed and cut into bite-sized pieces
3 tomatoes, washed and cut into bite-sized slices
3 or 4 green onions, washed, ends cut off and sliced diagonally
1/2 lb. Greek Feta cheese, crumbled or cut into small pieces
directions: Add ingredients in the order given, toss gently and serve with your favorite crackers.
I like to serve it with Wheatsworth stone ground wheat crackers.
Recipe yields 6 large salads or 10 small salads.
Enjoy!

_enrichment.description: A classic Greek salad with crisp lettuce, ripe tomatoes, feta, olives, and a tangy vinaigrette, served with hearty crackers.

title: Greek Summer Vegetable Salad
ingredients: 3 large ripe red tomatoes (about 2 lb.), seeded and coarsely cut into 1- to 1 1/2-inch pieces
1 small red onion, cut into 1-inch dice
1 red bell pepper, coarsely cut into 1-inch pieces
1 English (hothouse) cucumber, unpeeled, cut into 1-inch pieces
Salt and freshly ground black pepper
directions: Place the tomatoes, onions, bell peppers, and cucumbers on a serving platter.
In a bowl, whisk together the olive oil and vinegar.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Pour the vinaigrette over the vegetables.
Crumble the feta over the salad, sprinkle the olives and oregano over the top, and serve immediately.
Makes 8 servings.

_enrichment.description: A refreshing summer salad with juicy tomatoes, crunchy vegetables, and briny feta, drizzled with a bright red wine vinaigrette.

title: Possin A La Nicoise
ingredients: 4 small tomatoes, peeled and chopped (fresh or canned)
directions: Coat a baking dish with 1 teaspoon olive oil.
Place fish fillets in a single layer. Season with salt and pepper.
Add garlic, parsley and oregano to the mashed tomatoes.
Add wine and mushrooms, if desired. Spread the mixture on the fish and sprinkle the remaining 4 teaspoons olive oil over the fillets.
Dot with bits of
2 tablespoons butter.
Bake at 350° for 20 minutes, more or less depending on thickness of fish.
Serves 6.

_enrichment.description: Delicate fish fillets baked with a herby tomato and wine sauce, finished with butter for a rich, Mediterranean-inspired dish.

title: Octopus Casserole
ingredients: 1 lemon, cut into very small pieces with rind
directions: Boil octopus slowly 1 1/2 to 2 hours until tender when pierced. Rinse off black covering.
Cut up octopus into bite size pieces. Cut up both cheeses into bite size pieces.
Toss all ingredients together and place in casserole dish.
Season to taste.
Pour small amount of oil over all.
Bake at 350° for 30 minutes or until cheese is melted.

_enrichment.description: A hearty Greek casserole with tender octopus, olives, and two types of cheese, baked until bubbly and golden.

title: Marinated Olives
directions: Discard brine from the jar of olives and slice olives the long way 4 times.
Return olives to jar and fill with water.
Change water frequently for 1 day and keep refrigerated at all times. Drain the water and put olives in a big pot.
Add remaining ingredients.
Mix everything with olives and put back into jar. Refrigerate for at least 4 days, turning over frequently.

_enrichment.description: Marinated olives with a kick of garlic, oregano, and hot pepper, perfect as a tangy antipasto or snack.

title: Broccoli With Peppers
ingredients: 4 c. small broccoli florets (medium sized head)
1 small sweet red pepper, cored, seeded and cut lengthwise into 1/2-inch strips
directions: In a heavy skillet (12-inch), heat olive oil over moderate heat one minute.
Add onion, garlic, water, broccoli, red pepper, oregano and red pepper flakes.
Cook, stirring, 2 minutes.
Cover skillet; reduce heat to low and cook 6 to 8 minutes longer, stirring often, until broccoli is tender, but still crisp.

_enrichment.description: Quick and colourful stir-fried broccoli with sweet peppers and oregano, finished with a splash of water for tenderness.

title: Ripe Olive Rice
directions: Cook crumbled sausage until lightly browned.
Add onion and garlic; continue to cook until onion is tender.
Stir in rice. Add broth, tomatoes, mushrooms, olives, oregano, salt and pepper. Heat to boiling.
Reduce heat, cover and cook slowly for 20 minutes, if raw rice is used.
(Leftover, cooked rice may be used).
Heat thoroughly over low heat.
Serves 4 to 6.

_enrichment.description: A flavourful rice dish with sausage, olives, and tomatoes, slow-cooked with herbs for a comforting one-pot meal.

title: Seasoned Eggplant
directions: Spray large skillet with cooking spray, heat over medium heat until hot.
Saute vegetables until crisp-tender.
Add herbs and red pepper and cook for 2 minutes.
Add broth to skillet and heat to boiling, reduce heat and simmer, covered, until vegetables are soft and most of the liquid is absorbed, for about 20 minutes.
Add salt and pepper to taste.

_enrichment.description: Soft, seasoned eggplant with bell peppers and tomatoes, simmered in broth and herbs for a hearty side dish.

title: Greek Lemon Cake
directions: In large mixer bowl, combine cake mix and farina.
Add milk, eggs and oil.
Beat at medium speed of electric mixer for length of time given on cake mix package.
Stir in 3/4 cup nuts.
Divide batter evenly between two greased 9 x 9 x 2-inch baking pans. Bake in 350°
oven for 30 to 35 minutes or until cake tests done. Place on wire racks, not removing cakes from pans.

_enrichment.description: A moist and citrusy Greek lemon cake with walnuts, topped with a zesty lemon glaze for a refreshing dessert.

title: Spicy Pecan Apple Salad
ingredients: Greens - Boston Bibb, Red Leaf, Romaine
directions: Melt butter. Mix salt, cinnamon, cayenne pepper and pecans with butter.
Bake pecan mixture at 350 for 15 min. or until golden brown. Set aside. To prepare dressing mix together sherry wine vinegar, dry mustard, and olive oil.
Set aside. Toss together greens, chopped apple, and feta cheese.
Add pecans and dressing to greens mixture.

_enrichment.description: A spicy and sweet salad with crisp apples, pecans, and feta, tossed in a tangy mustard dressing.

title: Greek Chicken
ingredients: 4 boned and skinned chicken breast pieces
directions: Cut chicken into strips.
Marinate 5 to 10 minutes in mixture of oregano, pepper, lemon juice and salt.
Heat olive oil in skillet and saute minced garlic briefly.
Add chicken strips (and marinade if you want) and cook until just brown.
Serve over cooked rice with some steamed broccoli.

_enrichment.description: Greek-style chicken marinated in lemon and oregano, quickly seared and served with rice and steamed broccoli.

title: Cuban Chicken
ingredients: 1 (15 oz.) can tomato sauce or chopped tomatoes
4 small, thin skinned potatoes, in 1-inch chunks
directions: Simmer chicken in oil 15 minutes. Remove. To pan, add garlic, onion and green pepper.
Cook
4 minutes. Add tomato sauce, potatoes, spices, wine, raisins and olives.
Add chicken. Simmer about 35 minutes (until done). Stir in frozen peas. Cook until peas are done (2 to 3 minutes).

_enrichment.description: A Cuban-inspired chicken stew with potatoes, raisins, and olives, simmered in a rich tomato and wine sauce.

title: Cherokee Casserole(Yields 6 Servings)
ingredients: 1 (10 1/2 oz.) can cream of mushroom soup
1 c. Minute rice (no other kind will do)
3 slices American cheese, cut into 1/2-inch strips
directions: Brown meat in oil; add onion and cook until tender.
Stir in other ingredients in order given, except 3 olives and cheese. Bring to a boil; reduce heat and simmer 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Spoon into serving dish; top with cheese and 3 olives.
Broil until cheese is melted.

_enrichment.description: A hearty American casserole with ground beef, rice, and melted cheese, topped with olives for a comforting family meal.

title: Greek Pizza
directions: Lightly oil a cookie sheet with Pam spray and let frozen loaf defrost.
Spread dough out and cover with crushed tomatoes and remainder of ingredients.
Bake at 400° for approximately 20 to 30 minutes (until bottom of crust is lightly brown).
Each package will make 2 pizzas.

_enrichment.description: A quick and easy Greek-inspired pizza topped with tomatoes, feta, mushrooms, and olives on a crispy crust.

title: Greek Moussaka With Eggplant
ingredients: 1 lb. fresh tomatoes, peeled and sliced
directions: Saute the onion until soft. Add meat and tomatoes; cook 5 minutes. Season to taste and remove from heat. Add eggs, cheese and parsley. Saute the potatoes and eggplant in oil and butter until soft.
In a buttered, flat casserole, put a layer of eggplant, a layer of potatoes and a layer of meat and tomato mixture.
Repeat layers. Cover with bechamel (white sauce).
Place in preheated 325° oven and bake 20 minutes.
Serves 10.

_enrichment.description: A hearty Greek moussaka layered with eggplant, potatoes, and a rich béchamel sauce, baked to perfection.

title: Weight Watchers Chicken Ala Grecque
ingredients: 1 c. each: sliced onions, quartered mushroom caps and julienne cut red or green peppers
1/4 c. each: dry white wine and water
2 Tbsp. minced fresh mint or 1 Tbsp. dried mint
directions: In medium bowl, not aluminum, combine chicken and lemon juice and turn to coat with juice.
Let marinate 10 minutes.
Combine flour and oregano on plate or in plastic bag.
Remove chicken from marinade, allowing juice to drip into bowl; reserve juice.
Dredge chicken in flour mixture, coating both sides.

_enrichment.description: Tender chicken cutlets marinated in lemon and herbs, then cooked with feta and mint for a light yet flavourful dish.

title: Linguini With White Clam Sauce
directions: Saute garlic in saucepan in the oil until it floats.
(Do not allow to brown.) Add clam juice to stop cooking.
Add salt, pepper and oregano and simmer for 5 minutes.
Remove from heat and add clams and half of the parsley.
Cook the linguini according to package directions and reserve 1/2 cup of the cooking water.
Melt 1 tablespoon butter with a small amount of olive oil and gently fold into the cooked and drained linguini with the reserved water and remaining parsley.
Heat the clam sauce, but do not boil. Serve over the linguini.

_enrichment.description: A classic Italian dish of linguini tossed in a creamy white clam sauce with fresh herbs and clams.

title: Mary'S Chicken
directions: Thoroughly wash chicken and remove skin.
In a large kettle, cover bottom with oil; sprinkle parsley flakes to cover bottom; sprinkle garlic salt, dash of pepper, salt and oregano.
Place chicken on high heat for 1 minute.
Turn over, then high heat for 1 minute.
Put on lowest heat, cover and simmer for 1 hour, turning every 10 to 15 minutes.

_enrichment.description: Juicy chicken breasts simmered in olive oil with herbs until tender and infused with Mediterranean flavours.

title: Chilean Sea Bass
ingredients: 1 1/2 lb. sea bass fillets (remove bones, cut in chunks)
directions: Rinse bass in cold water and pat dry with paper towels. Lightly season fish with salt and pepper; coat with flour.
Heat oil in skillet.
Add fish and cook until lightly browned, about 1 minute per side.
Transfer to plate; set aside.
Add onion and garlic to skillet and cook about 1 to 2 minutes.
Add tomatoes, lemon juice, bay leaves, rosemary, oregano and coriander; cook on high for 3 to 4 minutes.
Add fish and gently press into tomato mixture.
Cover and cook over medium heat until fish is no longer opaque in center, 8 to 10 minutes.
Remove bay leaves and serve with rice.

_enrichment.description: Delicate Chilean sea bass cooked with aromatic herbs and tomatoes, offering a light yet flavourful meal.

title: Yiayia'S Spanakopita(Spinach Pie)
ingredients: 2 lb. spinach (fresh), rinsed, drained and chopped
directions: Brown onion in olive oil.
Beat eggs and add to spinach. Break up Feta cheese in crumbled pieces and mix with cottage cheese.
Add to spinach.
Add browned onions; season with pepper. Butter a 9 x 13-inch pan and place 10 sheets phyllo into pan, brushing each with melted butter.
Spread spinach mixture on top. Cover with 10 sheets of phyllo, buttering each phyllo as you add it.
Butter top well and cut into squares about 2 x 3-inches.
Bake at 350° for one hour.

_enrichment.description: A traditional Greek spinach pie wrapped in crispy phyllo dough, filled with feta and herbs.

title: Rigatoni With Shrimp And Feta Cheese(Serves 8)
ingredients: 3 (14 to 16 oz.) cans plum tomatoes and juice, chopped coarsely
1 1/2 lb. medium shrimp, shelled and deveined
directions: In a kettle, cook onion and garlic in oil over medium heat until softened.
Add wine and boil mixture for 1 minute.
Stir in tomatoes with juice, 1 tablespoon parsley, basil, oregano and salt.
Simmer mixture 10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until reduced and thickened somewhat.
Add shrimp and cook for 4 to 5 minutes or until shrimp are just firm.

_enrichment.description: A rich Italian pasta dish featuring rigatoni with shrimp and feta in a tomato-herb sauce.

title: Greek Style Kabobs With Pita Bread
ingredients: 1 lb. pork tenderloin (see note at bottom)
directions: Cut pork into bite size pieces.
Season with lemon juice, salt, pepper, oregano and olive oil.
Skewer meat and cook on the grill.
Brush pita bread with a bit of oil and grill as well. When the meat is tender, put 1 skewer on each pita and slowly draw out the skewer leaving the pieces of meat in the pita.
Add the onion, tomato, parsley and sauce.
Roll the pita.
A piece of grease-proof or wax paper can be rolled along the bottom half of the pita for a less messy sandwich.

_enrichment.description: Grilled pork skewers served with warm pita bread and a fresh salad, a classic Greek street food.

title: Tirpetakia(A.K.A.  "Three Feet" Or Triangles)
ingredients: 1/2 lb. small curd cottage cheese, drained
directions: Drain spinach; mix with cheeses.
Add the egg, some lemon peel, nutmeg, salt and pepper.
Place sheet of phyllo dough on a cutting board and brush with melted butter.
Cut the sheet into strips 3 inches wide.
Place 1 tablespoon of filling at bottom of each piece and roll up to form a triangle, moving one side to the other until the strip is rolled up.
Bake on a greased cookie sheet at 400° for 15 to 20 minutes or until lightly browned.

_enrichment.description: Crispy phyllo triangles filled with a savoury mix of spinach, feta, and cottage cheese.

title: Greek Spanakopita Recipe
ingredients: 2 (1 lb) pkgs frozen Spinach, thawed and drained well
directions: Squeeze remaining liquid from spinach and place in large bowl, tear into small pieces.
Beat eggs, crumble feta cheese and add to spinach.
Saute onion and garlic in olive oil until brown, add to spinach, season with pepper and oregano.
Butter a large oblong baking dish.

_enrichment.description: A classic Greek spinach pie with flaky phyllo layers and a filling of spinach, feta, and herbs.

title: Greek Tortellini Salad
ingredients: 1/2 c. olive oil or salad oil
directions: In a large bowl, combine cooked pasta, sweet peppers, onion and olives.
For dressing, combine vinegar, oil, mint, lemon juice, sherry, seasoned salt, garlic powder, black pepper and red pepper. Pour over salad; toss gently to coat.
Cover and chill for 4 to 24 hours.
Stir in cheese.
Use a slotted spoon to serve.
Makes 12 to 14 side-dish servings.

_enrichment.description: A refreshing pasta salad with tortellini, peppers, olives, and feta, tossed in a tangy dressing.

title: Chicken With Feta Stuffing
directions: Wash chickens and rub with lemon juice, salt and pepper.
Cut chicken livers in half and place in bowl.
Add cheese, garlic, olive oil, lemon juice and seasonings.
Let stand to marinate for 1 hour. Then stuff chickens and secure firmly with skewers. Arrange in baking pan and brush with melted butter.
Add water to pan and bake at 375°, basting and turning chickens frequently until tender and browned.
Serves 6 to 8.

_enrichment.description: Whole chickens stuffed with a rich feta and liver filling, roasted until golden and juicy.

title: Malmakarona(Honey Dainties - Greek)
directions: In mixer bowl, beat cooking oil into butter until blended. Beat in the 1/3 cup sugar.
Add orange juice, baking powder and baking soda; mix well.
Add enough of the flour, a little at a time, to make a medium-soft dough.
Shape dough into 2-inch ovals and place on an ungreased baking sheet.
Bake in 350°
oven for 20 to 25 minutes or until cookies are golden.
Cool on a wire rack.

_enrichment.description: Sweet honey-drizzled Greek cookies with a nutty crunch, perfect with coffee or tea.

title: Greek Meatballs
directions: Mix hamburger with rice, yellow onion, parsley, dill and egg. Mix and mold into small meatballs.
Roll each in flour.
In a large frying pan, put tomato juice and water.
Bring to heavy simmer and add butter.
Gently drop meatballs into this sauce, cover and simmer for 45 minutes.

_enrichment.description: Juicy Greek-style meatballs simmered in a tomato-herb sauce, served with rice or bread.

title: Greek Fried Meatballs(Keftethes)
ingredients: 2 lb. chopped beef (part lamb or pork)
1 c. oil or butter for frying
3 Tbsp. chopped parsley and mint leaves
directions: Brown onion in a little butter.
Place onions in a mixing bowl and add meat, bread crumbs, eggs, and seasonings.
Blend well and shape into size desired.
Roll lightly in flour and fry in very hot oil or butter.

_enrichment.description: Juicy Greek meatballs with a hint of parsley, perfect as a starter or light meal.

title: Greek Lamb Stew
directions: Saute onions and lamb in olive oil.
Add tomato sauce, water, bouillon, oregano, marjoram, pepper, bay leaf and garlic.
Simmer, covered, for 45 minutes.
Add green pepper and eggplant.
Cover and simmer 30 minutes or until vegetables are tender.
Add tomato wedges and olives; simmer 5 minutes.
Ladle into bowls and garnish with sour cream and sprinkle with paprika.

_enrichment.description: Hearty Greek lamb stew with tender vegetables, ideal for a comforting autumn or winter dinner.

title: Huntington Bay Dip
directions: Saute bread crumbs, garlic powder, onion, salt and oregano in oil until golden brown.
Mix bread crumb mixture with clams and juice.
Add chopped parsley and grated cheese.
Pour into a shallow casserole dish or several small, shallow pans.
Bake in oven at 375° for 30 to 35 minutes until dark brown.
Serve with crackers. If preparing ahead of time, clambake can be frozen before cooking.

_enrichment.description: Creamy clam dip with a golden crust, best served with crackers for a quick appetiser.

title: Greek Butterball Cookies
ingredients: 3/4 c. chopped blanched almonds, crushed with rolling pin
directions: Mix the flour, baking soda and almonds.
Set aside.
Heat butter slowly in a small deep saucepan.
Cool.
Carefully spoon off into a large bowl the clarified butter from top of cloudy solids which have settled at the bottom of the pan.

Add sugar, brandy, lemon juice, extract, oil and egg yolk to butter. Beat well. Add the flour-nut mixture in fourths, mixing thoroughly after each addition. Shape dough into 3/4-inch balls and place on ungreased cookie sheets.
Bake at
350° for 15 minutes.

_enrichment.description: Delicate Greek butterball cookies with a hint of almond, perfect with tea or coffee.

title: Weight Watcher'S Southwestern White Chili
ingredients: 12 oz. boneless, skinless chicken breasts, cut into pieces
1 (15 oz.) can white navy beans with jalapenos
directions: Heat oil in a 3-quart saucepan on high.
Add chicken and cook 4 to 5 minutes, stirring often.
Remove the chicken with a slotted spoon, cover and keep warm.
Add onions, beans and spices.
Cook 2 minutes and stir in broth.
Simmer for 30 minutes, stirring frequently.
Stir in cooked chicken and simmer for 10 minutes. Garnish with Monterey Jack cheese and green onions.
(Counts as 3 proteins, 2 breads, 1 fat and 5 optional calories.)

_enrichment.description: Light and flavourful Southwestern white chilli, ideal for a quick weeknight meal.

title: Sweet Red Pepper Salad
ingredients: 1/2 c. olive oil or vegetable oil
directions: Place whole peppers on a broiler pan; broil 4 inches from the heat until the skins blister, about 2 to 3 minutes.
With tongs, rotate pepper slightly; broil until all sides are blistered and lightly blackened.
Place pepper in a brown paper bag; close bag and let stand for 15 to 20 minutes.
Peel off the charred skin and discard; remove stem and seeds.
Cut peppers into 1/4 inch wide strips.
In a shallow container, combine the oil, parsley, garlic, oregano and salt; add peppers and toss.
Cover and chill for 3 to 4 hours.
Serve on lettuce leaves if desired.
Yield: 6 servings.

_enrichment.description: Sweet and smoky roasted red pepper salad, a refreshing summer side dish.

title: Spanakopita (Spinach Pie)
ingredients: 2 sprigs dill or 1/2 tsp. dried dill
directions: Clean spinach.
Scald in boiling water briefly and drain it well.
Saute the scallions in 3 tablespoons of butter until soft. Add spinach, parsley, dill, salt and pepper and cook for about 5 minutes.
Place the spinach mixture in a well-greased 10-inch pie plate.
Sprinkle the cheese evenly over the top.

_enrichment.description: Flaky spinach and feta pie, a classic Greek dish perfect for any occasion.

title: Pseudo-Southwestern Quinoa
ingredients: 1 3/4 c. water 1/2 t. dulce or kelp powder
1 avocado, diced and drenched with lime juice
2 ears fresh corn on the cob, cut off corn
1/4 c. olive oil or flax seed oil
directions: First of all quinoa (pronounced "keen-wa") is an alkalizing grain.
It is hardy and contains the highest amount if protein of any grain.
It cooks quickly and makes a delicious, crunchy dish. It is high in calcium, phosphorus, iron, B vitamins, and vitamin E and has an ideal balance of amino acids.
Wash quinoa thoroughly to remove the saponin, the rust-colored coating that protects this seed against insects, birds, and the sun.
While cooking, the germ on the outside of the grain unfolds like a partial spiral giving the finished cooked product a lovely appearance.

_enrichment.description: Nutritious and crunchy quinoa salad with a Southwestern twist, great for meal prep.

title: Greek Roast Chicken
directions: Rub the chicken inside and out with the cut surface of the lemon.
Rub outside with two tablespoons softened butter.
Place on rack in a roasting dish and bake in a 375° oven for 1/2 hour per pound.
Remove chicken and keep warm.

_enrichment.description: Succulent Greek roast chicken with lemon and herbs, a festive centrepiece for any meal.

title: *Oven Roasted Peppers
directions: Preheat oven to 400°.
Cut each pepper into quarters.
Remove the seeds. Place in large roasting pan. Add olive oil, oregano, salt and pepper. Toss mixture to coat well.
Arrange peppers in one layer. Roast 25 minutes or until tender, stirring occasionally.
(Peppers can be cut into 8 pieces each.)

_enrichment.description: Sweet and tender roasted peppers with herbs, a versatile side dish for any season.

title: Herbed Grill Vegetables
ingredients: 1 large sweet pepper, cut in strips
1 tsp. fresh thyme or 1 tsp. dried
1 tsp. fresh oregano or 1/2 tsp. dried
directions: In a medium bowl, toss the red pepper, zucchini and squash with thyme, oregano and black pepper.
Drizzle 1 teaspoon of oil over vegetables and toss well.
Spread vegetables in a single layer on rack.
Cook vegetables 8 inches from heat until golden brown on both sides (3 to 4 minutes each side).
Yields 4 servings.

_enrichment.description: Grilled vegetables with fresh herbs, a light and healthy summer dish.

title: Greek Lamb Shanks
ingredients: 1 (11 oz.) can condensed tomato soup (no water)
directions: Sprinkle meat with salt, pepper and garlic.
Coat with flour. Brown all sides in butter or oil in frying pan.
In a casserole dish, mix soup and wine until blended. Stir in bay leaf, rosemary, basil, crushed garlic and onion slices.
Add browned shanks and baste thoroughly.
Cook in covered casserole at 350° for 2 hours. Turn and baste after the first hour.
Serve with "Near East" long grain and wild rice.
Meat will be very tender and can easily be removed from the bones. Serves 2 with leftovers.

_enrichment.description: Tender Greek lamb shanks braised in a rich tomato and wine sauce, perfect for a special dinner.

title: Shrimp, Feta And Tomato Salad
ingredients: 1 c. sliced celery, green pepper or cucumber (optional)
directions: In large heavy skillet, heat 2 tablespoons of oil over moderate heat.
Saute shrimp for 2 minutes; stir until firm. Transfer to large bowl; let cool.
In small bowl, whisk the lemon juice, vinegar, zest, oregano, salt and pepper.
Whisk 4 tablespoons of oil.
Toss shrimp, Feta, tomatoes and olives. Chill.
Serves 4.

_enrichment.description: Fresh shrimp and feta salad with a tangy dressing, a light and refreshing summer dish.

title: Greek Lentil Soup
ingredients: 1 (1 lb. 12 oz.) can Italian plum tomatoes
directions: Wash lentils; drain well.
Combine with water, onion, garlic, salt, pepper and oil in a large kettle; bring to boiling.
Add tomatoes and bay leaves.
Lower heat; cover and simmer for 45 minutes or until lentils are tender.
Serve with lemon juice or wine vinegar if you wish.

_enrichment.description: Hearty Greek lentil soup with a rich tomato base, a comforting winter dish.

title: Crock-Pot No-Bean Chili
ingredients: 3 1/2 lb. boneless beef chuck or round, trimmed and cut in pieces
directions: Heat 2 tablespoons oil and saute beef.
Saute onions, green peppers, jalapeno peppers and chili powder; add seasonings and stir.
Remove from pan and add beer; pour in crock-pot with meat. Cover and cook 6 to 7 hours on medium-low.
Skim fat, thicken by adding cornmeal.
Cook additional 15 minutes uncovered.
Makes 6 to 8 servings.

_enrichment.description: Spicy and hearty no-bean chilli, a quick and easy meal for any day.

title: Souvlakia(Greek Skewered Steak)
ingredients: 1/2 lb. meat per person (steak, lamb, veal or pork)
1 onion, quartered or tiny whole onions
directions: Cube meat into 1-inch pieces. Wash and prepare vegetables. Skewer each one in order:
green pepper, mushroom stem, onion, meat, tomato and continue until skewer is 3/4 full.
Cap with mushroom.
Lay in a flat pan and sprinkle with the rest of the ingredients.
Broil 5 minutes on one side. Turn and broil 4 minutes on the other side.
Can serve on rice pilaf.

_enrichment.description: Greek skewered steak with colourful vegetables, grilled to perfection and served on rice pilaf.

title: Pollo Aquascalientes
ingredients: shake or two of red pepper
directions: Cook chicken in olive oil in a deep heavy pot, cast-iron is best, 15 to 20 minutes, turning.
Sprinkle remaining ingredients over chicken.
Cover; turn down heat to a strong simmer.
Cook another 45 to 60 minutes until chicken is very tender.
Serve chicken on the side, with broth in a bowl.
Sprinkle broth with basil or serve together in a large soup bowl.
Sprinkle with basil.

_enrichment.description: Mexican-inspired chicken stew with tender meat, simmered in a rich, spiced broth and served with fresh basil.

title: Wilted Spinach Salad(Serves 6)
directions: Chop the cleaned spinach.
Remove the pits from the olives. Combine all ingredients, except the olive oil in a metal bowl. Heat the olive oil in a saucepan until almost smoking.
Pour the hot oil over the salad and toss quickly.
Add more vinegar if necessary.

_enrichment.description: A quick and tangy wilted spinach salad with olives, feta, and croutons, finished with hot olive oil.

title: Greek Honey Puffs
directions: Dissolve yeast in tepid water.
Combine the rest of the ingredients in a large bowl.
Add the yeast mixture to ingredients and beat at high speed until a smooth dough is formed.
Cover and place in warm place until batter rises 2 to 3 times original amount.
When dough has risen, moisten 1 hand with water and take a handful of dough.
Squeeze your fist until a small ball is formed and with a spoon, ease it into the hot oil.
Fry a few at a time.
Remove when brown with slotted spoon; drain on paper towel. Transfer to serving dish and pour hot honey over balls.
Can sprinkle with cinnamon and nuts.

_enrichment.description: Light and fluffy Greek honey puffs drizzled with honey and sprinkled with cinnamon and walnuts.

title: Spanakopeta
directions: Cook onions, scallions and parsley with olive oil.
Add spices and cook on low heat, stirring constantly.
In a bowl crumble cheese and add milk and eggs.
Pour over chopped spinach and mix. Add cooled herbs and mix well.

_enrichment.description: A classic Greek spinach and feta pie with crispy filo pastry, perfect for a light meal or snack.

title: Vegetable Fiesta! Con Queso (With Cheese)
ingredients: 1 small red, yellow, or green bell pepper, seeded and cubed
directions: Heat oil over medium to high heat.
Add onion, bell pepper, mushrooms and zucchini.
Stir one to two minutes.
Add garlic and oregano.
Stir fry one more minute or until crisp-tender.
Add wine and broth; stir fry a few seconds until evaporated. Add salt to taste.
Remove pan from heat and place cheese slices on top of the vegetables.
Cover the pan for about 2 minutes so the warmth of the vegetables will melt the cheese.

_enrichment.description: A vibrant vegetable stir-fry with melted cheese, white wine, and chicken broth for a cheesy, savoury dish.

title: Bean And Cheese Chili
ingredients: 1 can pinto or red kidney beans, drained
1 c. (4 oz.) shredded Monterey Jack cheese
1/4 c. loosely packed snipped fresh cilantro
directions: Heat oil in a 2 to 3-quart saucepan over medium heat.
Add onions and green pepper and, stirring often, cook 5 to 6 minutes until onions are clear.
Stir in tomatoes, cumin, oregano, garlic, salt and sugar.
Bring to a boil; reduce heat to medium low and simmer uncovered about 15 minutes to blend flavors.
Stir in beans; simmer 15 minutes longer until beans are hot and chili has thickened slightly.
Stir in black pepper.
Ladle into bowls and sprinkle with cheese and cilantro.
Serve with sour cream, if desired.
Makes 4 servings.

_enrichment.description: A hearty bean and cheese chilli with pinto beans, tomatoes, and spices, topped with melted cheese and fresh cilantro.

title: South-Of-The-Border Special
ingredients: minced garlic or garlic powder to taste
directions: In large frypan, brown ground beef and onions, add green peppers, cooking slightly.
Then add tomato sauce and the next six ingredients.
Simmer at least five minutes.
Serve over tortilla chips and top with shredded lettuce, diced fresh tomatoes, chopped onions, green peppers, shredded sharp cheese, sour cream and salsa, if desired.

_enrichment.description: A Tex-Mex-inspired ground beef dish with kidney beans and olives, served over crispy tortilla chips.

title: Spinach Pie(Spanakopita)
directions: Wash spinach; cut off stems.
Boil 5 minutes, drain and squeeze out water.
Saute onion in olive oil, then add oil and onion to spinach, parsley, dill, salt, pepper and nutmeg.
Cool and add eggs, Feta, Parmesan cheese and cottage cheese. Mix evenly.

_enrichment.description: A flaky Greek spinach and cheese pie with layers of filo pastry, perfect for a light meal or snack.

title: Cuban Style Black Beans
ingredients: 1 tsp. black pepper or to taste
directions: Sort beans; allow them to soak in water overnight or 8 hours. Next morning, rinse beans, then cover beans with water plus about 3 inches.
Add 1 bay leaf, ham bone or ham.
Pressure cook for 5 minutes or in a regular pot about 45 minutes on medium-high heat (watch for water to dry out).
In a skillet, saute until translucent the onions and bell pepper in the olive oil.
After this has cooked, add the salt, pepper, cumin, garlic and oregano. Add to beans and simmer for about 10 to 15 minutes.
Serve over white rice or as a soup.

_enrichment.description: Cuban-style black beans with ham, bell peppers, and spices, served over rice or as a soup.

title: Eggplant Casserole(Moussaka)
directions: Brown the onions and hamburger in half of the butter.
Add parsley, tomatoes, wine, salt and pepper to taste and cook on low flame until liquid is absorbed.
Set aside to cool, then add eggs, cheese and 3 tablespoons bread crumbs.
Mix well; season to taste. While this is being prepared, slice eggplants lengthwise.
Fry each in butter, browning each side.
Sprinkle the bottom of a 9 x 12-inch pan with bread crumbs, then place a layer of eggplants over this.
Cover with meat mixture, then add another layer of eggplants and meat again, until all is used, ending with eggplant. Top with "Krema" sauce.
Bake in oven at 375° to 400° for one hour.

_enrichment.description: A rich Greek moussaka with layers of eggplant, spiced meat, and creamy béchamel sauce.

title: Flavorful Tofu(Kids Will Eat)
ingredients: dash of chili pepper to taste
dash of black pepper to taste
directions: Cut tofu into 1/2-inch thick slices and allow to dry on paper towels.
Start to cook brown rice.
In large saute pan saute onions in olive oil until translucent.
Add mushrooms, red wine and spices and continue to cook until mushrooms are soft.
Coat tofu with wheat germ by placing wheat germ in shallow dish and gently pressing tofu.
Add tofu to saute, moving vegies aside to place tofu directly on pan.
Pour can of tomatoes over all and gently mix vegies onto top and in with the tomatoes.
Allow to cook, covered, on medium heat for 7 minutes.
Serve with rice.

_enrichment.description: A kid-friendly tofu dish with mushrooms, red wine, and herbs, served over brown rice.

title: Saucy Barbecue Shrimp
ingredients: 1 1/2 lb. medium or large uncooked, unpeeled shrimp, slit down the back
directions: Wash shrimp well and slit shell down back with paring knife. (Do not remove shell).
Combine all ingredients (except bread) in saucepan.
Simmer for 10 minutes.
Add shrimp to sauce.
Mix thoroughly.
Refrigerate for 2 to 3 hours.
Stir 2 or 3 times. Bake shrimp in saucepan, uncovered at 300° turning twice until they just turn pink (about 15 to 18 minutes).
Do not overbake. Serve in generous soup bowls with chunks of French bread to soak up the sauce.
Garnish with lemon slices.

_enrichment.description: Succulent barbecue shrimp simmered in a spicy, garlicky sauce, served with bread.

title: Garlic Oregano Chicken
directions: Place chicken in oblong pan (13 x 9 x 2-inch).
Mix remaining ingredients, except lemon slices; pour over chicken.
Cook, uncovered, in a 375° oven, spooning oil mixture over chicken occasionally, for 30 minutes.
Turn chicken; cook until thickest pieces are done, about 30 minutes.
Garnish with lemon slices.

_enrichment.description: Juicy garlic and oregano chicken roasted until tender, with a zesty lemon glaze.

title: Hot Golden Olive Bites
ingredients: 1 c. extra large canned pitted ripe olives
directions: Stuff each pitted olive with the tiny cubes of Cheddar cheese. Combine biscuit mix, butter, milk and spices to form a dough. Mold a teaspoon of dough around each olive, covering it completely.
Place on baking sheet and bake in a preheated 400° oven 10 to 12 minutes.
Serve hot.
Makes 4 dozen.

_enrichment.description: Bite-sized cheesy olive puffs with a buttery biscuit crust, perfect for parties or snacks.

title: Chicken Curry
directions: Rub chicken breasts with curry powder and put in baking dish. Pour on orange juice sweetened with a little honey.
Bake until done in very slow oven (or adjust for microwave).
Thicken juices with cornstarch.
Serve with brown or white rice.
(Slow oven is 300° and would take about 1 1/2 hours, depending on the number of chicken breasts.)

_enrichment.description: A hearty British-style chicken curry with tender chicken breasts baked in a spiced sauce, served with rice. Perfect for a comforting family meal.

title: Indian Corn Dinner
ingredients: 2 Tbsp. flour, mixed with 2 Tbsp. water
directions: Saute chopped onions and peppers in butter until tender, about 5 minutes at low heat.
Add meat and brown well.
Add corn, soup and seasonings.
Thicken with flour mixture and pour into greased casserole dish and bake in 350° oven for 20 minutes.

_enrichment.description: A simple American-style casserole with ground beef, corn, and tomato soup, baked until bubbly. Ideal for a quick and filling dinner.

title: Pakistani Chicken Curry
directions: Saute the onions, garlic and ginger in the shortening until the onion is golden brown.
Add the pieces of chicken, yogurt, tomato paste and the spices.
Mix well and fry for about 15 minutes by which time it should become rather dry.
Sprinkle in about 2 cups of hot water and allow to cook until chicken is tender.
If necessary, add more water half way through.
Makes about 6 servings.

_enrichment.description: A rich and aromatic Pakistani chicken curry with tender chicken pieces in a spiced yogurt and tomato sauce. Best served with naan or rice.

title: Indian Chicken Curry
directions: In a large saucepan, melt butter and saute onion, celery and apples for 5 minutes.
Stir in curry and flour.
Gradually stir in chicken broth, cream of coconut and half and half.
Stir over moderate heat until sauce bubbles and thickens.
Stir in chicken and season to taste with salt and pepper.
Serve over rice.

_enrichment.description: A creamy and mildly spiced Indian-style chicken curry with apples and coconut, served over rice. A delightful fusion of flavours.

title: Curried Vegetable Medley
ingredients: 1 c. green beans, cut into 1-inch pieces
directions: In 10-inch skillet, over medium heat in hot oil, cook cauliflowerets, beans, mushrooms and carrots with garlic, curry and cumin until vegetables are tender-crisp, stirring often.
In small bowl, stir together vegetable juice, cornstarch and pepper; gradually stir into skillet.
Cook until mixture boils and thickens, stirring constantly.
Sprinkle with almonds.
Makes 4 cups or 8 servings.

_enrichment.description: A vibrant vegetable medley with cauliflower, green beans, and carrots in a spiced curry sauce, garnished with almonds. A healthy and colourful dish.

title: Potatoes And Tomatoes With Fresh Coconut
ingredients: 6 cloves garlic, peeled and finely chopped
1 1/4 lb. fresh tomatoes, peeled and diced into 1/2-inch pieces or 16 to 20 oz. tin Italian tomatoes
directions: Peel potatoes, then dice into 3/4-inch pieces and put into bowl of cold water.
Heat oil in heavy 3-quart pot over medium-high flame.
When hot, add finely chopped garlic.
Stir about 5 seconds before adding red pepper and cumin seed.
Stir for another 3 seconds.
Garlic should brown lightly, red pepper should darken and cumin seed should sizzle.
Lower heat to medium.
Add grated coconut and stir for 10 to 15 seconds.

_enrichment.description: A fragrant and slightly spicy potato and tomato dish with fresh coconut, garlic, and cumin. A comforting side or light main.

title: Baked Indian Pudding
ingredients: 1 c. molasses or golden table syrup
directions: Heat oven to 325°.
Butter a deep 2-quart casserole.
Heat milk and sugar in a saucepan over low heat until tiny bubbles appear around edge of pan. Gradually stir in corn meal; cook, stirring constantly, until smooth and slightly thickened.
Remove from heat.
Stir in eggs, cinnamon, ginger, salt and molasses (syrup).
Stir together thoroughly.
Pour into prepared casserole. Bake, uncovered, 1 to 1 1/2 hours.
Serve hot as is, or with vanilla ice cream.
Makes 6 servings.

_enrichment.description: A classic American baked pudding with cornmeal, molasses, and spices, offering a warm and comforting dessert.

title: Mums Indian Relish
directions: Grind all of vegetables after washing.
Measure all of the vegetables putting them in a stainless steel large kettle. Sprinkle 1 cup of non-iodized salt over the vegetables.
Let stand overnight.
Drain off all the juice on the vegetables.
Add 2 quarts vinegar and bring to a
boil; cook until tender.
Mix the sugar, turmeric, mustard and flour and make a thin paste with a small amount of water.
Pour the paste over the ground vegetables. Mix well.
Let stand 5 minutes and ladle into clean hot jars and seal. Makes 10-12 pints.
Cover and let cool before storing in cool place.

_enrichment.description: A tangy and spiced Indian-style relish made with tomatoes, peppers, and onions, perfect as a condiment or side.

title: Chutney Chicken Spread
ingredients: 2 Tbsp. grated onion or sliced scallion
directions: Process chicken in food processor for 5 seconds, using steel blade.
Add cream cheese, chutney, curry, onion, mayonnaise and pepper.
Process 5 to 10 seconds, until well blended.
Place in an au gratin dish; spread with almonds.
Bake at 350° for 15 minutes. Serve with large corn chips.

_enrichment.description: A creamy and spiced chicken spread with chutney and almonds, baked until golden. Ideal for parties or a quick snack.

title: Turkey Keema
directions: Heat oil and fry onion until golden brown.
Add ginger and garlic, fry for a few more minutes.
Add ground turkey, tomatoes and all the spices; fry for a few more minutes.
Add 2 to 3 cups water and cook until turkey is tender and gravy is thick.

_enrichment.description: A flavourful Turkish-style minced turkey dish with spices, tomatoes, and peppers, served with rice or flatbread.

title: "Dr. Ziment'S Prescription Garlic Chicken Soup For Colds And
directions: Peel garlic cloves and place with other ingredients in a medium-size saucepan.
Heat to boiling, then reduce to simmer. Simmer for 30 minutes, breathing fumes during cooking.
If desired, strain soup, discarding solid ingredients, or puree it after removing bay leaf.
Divide the soup into 4 portions, each of which should be taken at the beginning of a meal, one to three times a day.

_enrichment.description: A soothing garlic and herb chicken soup with a hint of curry, designed to ease cold symptoms. A comforting remedy.

title: Indiana Chili
directions: Fry ground beef and onion.
Drain grease and set aside.
Boil water. Add macaroni until tender. Drain water from macaroni and then add tomato juice, ground beef mixture and spices.
Bring to a boil again, then remove from heat.
Serve in soup bowl along with crackers and peanut butter for a cozy, warm-up meal.

_enrichment.description: A hearty American chili with ground beef, macaroni, and spices, served with crackers and peanut butter for a nostalgic meal.

title: Easy Indian Curry
ingredients: 1 lb. medium shrimp, cleaned and deveined
1/2 pkg. cream of coconut or 1/2 can coconut milk
directions: Boil shrimp in 2 cups of water with a little salt for about 5 minutes.
Drain and keep aside.
Using a blender, combine onion, tomatoes, green chili, lemon juice, sugar, paprika, curry powder, salt and garlic into a paste.
Heat oil in large pot and saute ground paste and the coconut over low heat, stirring constantly until you get the aroma of all the spices and the oil floats to the top (about 15 minutes).
Add 1 cup water and bring to a boil. Lower heat and add shrimp.
Simmer gently until liquid is reduced a little.
Serve with boiled white rice.
Serves 6.

_enrichment.description: A quick and easy Indian-style shrimp curry with coconut and spices, served with fluffy white rice. A delightful weeknight meal.

title: Eggplant In Spicy Tomato Sauce
ingredients: 1 piece fresh ginger (1 1/2 x 1-inch), peeled and coarsely chopped
directions: Preheat broiler.
Halve eggplant lengthwise and then cut crosswise into 1/2-inch slices.
Brush slices on both sides with about 3 tablespoons of the oil and arrange them in a single layer in large broiling tray.
Broil 3 inches from heat source, about 7 minutes; turn with spatula and broil another 7 minutes, or until eggplant is nicely browned.

_enrichment.description: A spicy and aromatic eggplant dish in a tomato-based sauce with ginger, garlic, and fennel. A flavourful vegetarian option.

title: Chutney Spread
directions: Mix cream cheese, Cheddar cheese, sherry, Worcestershire sauce, curry and salt. Blend well.
Line loaf pan with wax paper. Smooth mixture into pan.
Refrigerate 3 hours or overnight. Remove from pan. Layer chutney, green onion, cashews and coconut.
Serve at room temperature with Bagel Chips or Wheat Thins.

_enrichment.description: A creamy and tangy cheese spread with chutney, sherry, and spices, served with crackers. A perfect appetiser for gatherings.

title: "Skordalia" Garlic Sauce
directions: Pound the garlic with the salt and walnuts until well blended and creamy.
If you like the taste of chopped nuts, just mix the chopped nuts with the salt and garlic, which you put through a garlic press.
Add the bread crumbs with oil, a little at a time, then the vinegar and finally the cold water.
Put enough water to make the mixture soft like a paste.

_enrichment.description: A creamy Greek garlic dip made with walnuts and breadcrumbs, perfect for dipping bread or pairing with grilled meats.

title: Chicken Curry Salad
directions: Drain pineapple, reserving 3 tablespoons liquid.
Mix reserved 3 tablespoons liquid, dressing, mustard, curry and salt in a large bowl.
Add pineapple and remaining ingredients; mix lightly. Refrigerate.
Spoon chicken mixture over lettuce-lined platter, if desired.
Makes 4 servings.

_enrichment.description: A sweet and tangy American chicken salad with pineapple, curry, and almonds, ideal for a refreshing summer lunch.

title: Indian Salmon Croquettes
ingredients: 1 medium green pepper diced, small pieces
directions: In large bowl, drain and pulverize salmon.
Add all ingredients; mixing thoroughly.
Mixture should be moist and not too chunky.
Pour top olive oil into heavy iron skillet and heat over medium heat.
Shape croquettes to desired size, dust with flour on both sides and cook, turning them once until golden brown on both sides, about 4 to 5 minutes.
Serve hot.

_enrichment.description: Crispy Indian-inspired salmon croquettes with a hint of chutney, offering a delightful contrast of textures.

title: Microwave Curried Rice With Almonds
ingredients: 2 Tbsp. tight olive oil or butter
directions: Put onions and oil into 3-quart casserole. Cover tightly and microwave on High for 2 to 3 minutes. Wash rice briefly in a sieve under cold, running water; drain well.
Add to onions in a casserole along with rest of ingredients, except almonds. Cover tightly
and microwave on High for 7 minutes; stir.
Cover and microwave until liquid is almost absorbed, 8 to 10 minutes longer. Stir. Cover and let stand 10 minutes.
Stir again and top with almonds.

_enrichment.description: A quick and fragrant microwave rice dish with almonds, curry, and raisins, perfect for a hassle-free meal.

title: Curry-Cajun Chicken
ingredients: 6 skinless, boneless chicken breast halves (about 1 1/2 lb.)
directions: In a 3-quart rectangular baking dish, combine honey, water, mustard, melted margarine or butter, Cajun seasoning, curry powder, lemon juice and garlic powder; mix well.
Add chicken, turning to coat.
Arrange in a single layer.
Bake, uncovered, in a 350° oven for about 30 minutes or until chicken is tender and no longer pink.

_enrichment.description: A sticky and spicy baked chicken dish with Cajun and curry flavours, ideal for a flavourful family dinner.

title: Curry Stew
ingredients: 2 lb. beef stew meat, cut in 1 1/2-inch cubes
directions: In Dutch oven, thoroughly brown meat in 2 tablespoons hot shortening, turning often.
Add 2 cups hot water; bring to a boil. Add salt, pepper and garlic; cover and simmer until meat is tender, about 1 1/2 hours, stirring occasionally to keep from sticking. Add potatoes and carrots.
Cover and cook for 30 to 40 minutes; test to see if vegetables are tender, then add celery and onions. Add more water if necessary.
Cover and simmer for 15 minutes. Blend 1/3 cup cold water into the flour and curry powder.
Stir slowly into hot stew mixture.
Let simmer another 15 minutes. Serve stew over rice.
Makes 6 to 8 servings.

_enrichment.description: A hearty British-style curry stew with beef and vegetables, slow-cooked to perfection and served with rice.

title: Curry Rice
directions: In large pan, simmer pork chop cubes in salted water (enough to cover) 45 to 60 minutes.
Add more hot water; enough to make as much curry as you want; then put in onions, carrots and potatoes. Salt and pepper to taste.
Bring to boil, lower heat and simmer 2 hours, stirring occasionally, covered.
Add the green pepper and simmer another 15 minutes.
Add flour to water to make sauce for thickening.
Stir into vegetables.
Add curry powder.
Serve over regular cooked rice, not instant.
Leftovers freeze nicely.

_enrichment.description: A comforting curry rice dish with pork, vegetables, and a rich sauce, perfect for a cosy meal.

title: Indiana Picnic Cake
directions: In a bowl, sift first 2 ingredients, add butter and nuts, and mix all together.
Take out 1 cup, set aside.
Add to bowl the rest of the ingredients.
Beat well and put in greased square pan 9 x 9-inch, then sprinkle the cup of crumb mixture on top.
Bake in preheated oven at 350° for 45 to 50 minutes.

_enrichment.description: A moist and nutty American picnic cake with brown sugar and buttermilk, ideal for a sweet treat.

title: India Chicken Curry
directions: Simmer onion and celery in butter.
Add flour; mix thoroughly. Add chicken stock and cook until smooth.
Add tomato juice, seasonings and chicken.
Arrange rice in a ring and pour hot curried chicken in the center.

_enrichment.description: A classic Indian chicken curry with a rich tomato-based sauce, served with rice for a satisfying meal.

title: Spiced Black Beans
ingredients: 6 cardamon pods, pull off pods and use seed
directions: In a 4 to 5-quart pan, over medium heat, cook onions in oil until limp and lightly browned, stirring often.
Add chicken broth, beans, tomato paste, spices and herbs.
Cover, bring to boil over high heat; reduce to simmer until beans mash easily, about 3 1/2 hours.
(Soaking beans overnight can reduce cooking time to 2 1/2 hours.)
Uncover and boil beans on high until reduced to about 5 1/2 cups, about 30 minutes, stirring to prevent scorching.

_enrichment.description: A spiced black bean stew with aromatic herbs and spices, perfect for a hearty vegetarian meal.

title: Chicken Panang Curry
ingredients: 1 (14 oz.) can Taste of Thai coconut milk
1 Tbsp. Taste of Thai Panang Curry Base
1 lb. boneless chicken, cut in strips
2 Tbsp. Taste of Thai seasoning sauce
2 tsp. Taste of Thai garlic chili pepper sauce (use this cautiously; you might prefer a little less)
directions: In a large saucepan, combine coconut milk and curry base and cook until blended over medium heat.
Add chicken, seasoning sauce and lemon juice and simmer for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally. Add remaining ingredients, except mushrooms and garlic chili pepper sauce.
Cover and simmer for 5 minutes.
Add mushrooms and garlic chili pepper sauce to taste.
Cover and simmer for 2 minutes.
Serve hot with rice.

_enrichment.description: A rich and creamy Thai Panang curry with chicken, bell peppers, and mushrooms, served with rice.

title: Mildred'S Apple-Tomato Chutney
directions: Peel and core apples and stew them in a very small quantity of water until they are tender and pulpy.
Cut up tomatoes; chop onions and garlic.
(Put through the coarse blade of food grinder.)
Add to apples.
Add sugar.
Tie the mustard seed in muslin and add to the mixture, along with spices and seasoning. Add the vinegar and cook gently for about 2 hours or until mixture is of required consistency.
Put into jars and seal.
Use water bath.
Makes about 4 1/2 pints.

_enrichment.description: A sweet and tangy apple-tomato chutney with a hint of spice, perfect for pairing with cheese or meats.

title: Meat With Rice And Lentils
ingredients: 1 lb. beef, cut in two-inch cubes
directions: Combine meat with yogurt and all the spices and leave to marinate for 2 to 3 hours.
When you're ready to cook, fry the onion in a large stew pot with oil.
Add the marinated meat mixture and simmer until liquid is slightly thickened.
Set aside.

_enrichment.description: A fragrant Middle Eastern dish of meat with rice and lentils, marinated in yogurt and spices.

title: Indian Summer Apple Cake
ingredients: 2 medium apples, peeled, cored, and chopped
directions: Blend together eggs, oil, and sugar until creamy.
Add flour, a small amount at a time, blending well after each addition. Batter will be stiff.
Fold in apples, coconut, and pecans.
Place in greased and floured 10 inch tube pan;
bake at 350 degrees about one hour or until wooded pick inserted in center of cake comes out
clean.
Cool in pan for 30 minutes; remove cake.

_enrichment.description: A moist and fruity apple cake with coconut and pecans, perfect for a cosy autumn dessert.

title: Indian Curried Beans
ingredients: 3 c. dry black-eyed peas (prepared as below)
4 ripe tomatoes (spaghetti sauce may be substituted)
few cloves minced or crushed garlic
directions: Heat oil in a nonstick frying pan.
Add garlic, ginger and chopped onion and stir-fry for 5 minutes.
Add diced tomatoes and cook 10 minutes, stirring frequently.
Add curry powder and prepared beans.
Bring mixture to a boil and cook on medium heat for 10 more minutes.
Garnish with Garam Masala and finely chopped cilantro.

_enrichment.description: A quick and flavourful black-eyed pea curry with tomatoes and spices, ideal for a speedy meal.

title: Kurma(Quick Chicken Curry From South Africa)
ingredients: 2 1/2 to 3 lb. fryer chicken, cut up
directions: Combine all ingredients in Dutch oven or large, heavy pan. Bring to a boil; reduce heat to low.
Cover and simmer for 1 hour or until chicken is done, stirring occasionally.
Serve with hot rice.
Makes 6 servings.

_enrichment.description: A quick and hearty South African chicken curry with a spicy kick, perfect for a comforting family meal.

title: Indian Cornbread
directions: Mix ingredients except cheese and bacon drippings.
Pour half of bacon drippings in mixture and the other half in skillet.
Pour half of mixture in skillet, then sprinkle with cheese.
Add rest of mixture, then top with remaining cheese.
Preheat oven to 350°. Bake 45 minutes.
Let set 30 minutes before serving.

_enrichment.description: A savoury American cornbread with a cheesy, bacon-infused twist, ideal for a cosy brunch or side dish.

title: Chicken & Beer
directions: Place cut-up chicken in a 4 quart cooking pot.
Add all the ingredients to pot.
Add all the ingredients to pot and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat and simmer until tender, approximately 1 hour. Add liquid if ingredients are getting thick.
Should be a medium gravy consistency.

_enrichment.description: A rustic South African chicken and beer stew with a rich, medium gravy consistency, great for a hearty dinner.

title: John'S Curry
ingredients: 2 lb. beef or chicken, cut into cubes
1 or 2 pieces garlic, crushed or 1 tsp. garlic powder
1 1/2 tsp. curry mix or curry powder
directions: Saute onions, bell pepper and garlic in 2 tablespoons of margarine or butter.
Set aside.
Brown meat in frying pan. Transfer to a large pot.
Make a gravy in frying pan with drippings.
You may want to thicken it with small amount of flour. Add all ingredients to meat and simmer until meat is done. Cook rice as directed on package.
Cut up boiled egg, 1 tomato, pineapple, banana, raisins and coconut and place in small dishes. Serve on top of curry and rice.

_enrichment.description: A sweet and spicy South African beef curry with a medley of fruits and vegetables, perfect for a festive occasion.

title: Roasted Spiced Turkey Breast
ingredients: 1 Shady Brook Farms split turkey breast
8 oz. nonfat or low-fat plain yogurt
1/2 tsp. hot red pepper sauce as desired
directions: Combine all ingredients, except turkey, in large shallow pan or bowl.
Add turkey; marinate for 1 hour in refrigerator, turning once.
Discard unused marinade.
Cover the breasts with foil; roast for 30 minutes in a 350° oven.
Baste occasionally. Uncover; roast for an additional 30 to 40 minutes, until brown.

_enrichment.description: A fragrant roasted turkey breast marinated with fresh coriander, ginger, and curry spices, ideal for a festive centrepiece.

title: Chicken Cilantro
directions: Heat oil; add turmeric and garlic and saute onion.
Add chicken, salt and pepper; lightly brown.
Add all water and chicken cubes; boil for 30 to 45 minutes, until chicken is tender. Add cilantro, green onions and potatoes.
Simmer until potatoes are cooked.
Serve with rice and green salad or shred cooked chicken and serve with flour tortillas and beans.

_enrichment.description: A vibrant South African chicken curry with turmeric, cilantro, and potatoes, served with rice for a satisfying meal.

title: Curried Turkey Salad With Georgia Peach Chutney
ingredients: 2 c. baked turkey, cut into bite-sized pieces
1 bunch green onions, finely sliced (tops too)
2 c. cooked white or brown rice, cooked in chicken broth
1 tsp. curry powder (or more to taste)
directions: Combine turkey, onions, chestnuts, rice and raisins in a large bowl.
Prepare a sauce using remaining ingredients, except peanuts.
Fold sauce into turkey mixture. Cover with plastic wrap and refrigerate at least 3 to 4 hours or overnight to blend flavors.
Serve on curly leaf lettuce and garnish with peanuts.

_enrichment.description: A refreshing turkey salad with curry spices, peaches, and peanuts, perfect for a light summer lunch or picnic.

title: Curry Spiced Rice
directions: In 3-quart casserole dish, combine onion and butter.
Cover with heavy-duty plastic wrap; microwave on High for 3 minutes. Stir in remaining ingredients; cover and microwave on High for 5 minutes.
Stir well.
Cover and microwave for another 20 minutes or until all liquid is absorbed.
Let stand 4 to 5 minutes.
Makes 6 servings.

_enrichment.description: A fragrant South African rice dish with curry spices, turmeric, and allspice, cooked in a casserole for a comforting side.

title: Indian Pilaf
directions: Heat oven to 350°.
Melt butter in skillet.
Add onion and rice; cook and stir until rice is yellow and onion is tender. Stir in next 5 seasonings.
Pour into ungreased 2-quart casserole. Heat chicken broth to boiling; stir into rice mixture.
Cover tightly.
Bake for 30 to 40 minutes or until liquid is absorbed and rice is tender.

_enrichment.description: A fragrant Indian-style pilaf with basmati rice, spices, and chicken broth, baked to perfection for a hearty side dish.

title: City Bakery'S Burgers(Veggie Burgers)
ingredients: 1/2 tsp each ground cumin and ground ginger
1 C each coarsely chopped mushrooms and cooked thin-skinned potatoes
1 C carrots diced in 1/4 inch pieces
1 C soft whole wheat bread crumbs
directions: In a 10-12 inch nonstick frying pan over medium heat, cook onion and garlic in butter, stirring often, until onion is golden; about 8 minutes.
Add cumin and ginger; stir for 1 minute.
Add mushrooms, potatoes, carrots and chopped cilantro.
Stir often until carrots are tender to bite, about 7 minutes.
Add flour and stir for 3 minutes.
Remove from heat and let cool slightly.
Then mix in eggs and bread crumbs.
Add salt and pepper to taste.

_enrichment.description: A hearty veggie burger with mushrooms, carrots, and spices, served in a bun for a satisfying meat-free meal.

title: Corn Curry
directions: Combine margarine, vegetables and curry powder in a skillet. Cook over low heat for 8 to 10 minutes or until vegetables are tender.
Stir in sour cream and pepper.
Heat thoroughly, stirring often; do not allow to boil.
Yields 6 servings.

_enrichment.description: A creamy South African corn curry with green peppers, pimientos, and a touch of sour cream for a rich, comforting dish.

title: Fried Corn Patties
directions: In blender or food processor, combine corn and water.
Do not puree.
Add eggs and process briefly.
Put this mixture in bowl. Add all remaining ingredients, except oil (it is for frying), mixing well.
Heat oil, about 1/2 to 1-inch deep in skillet.
Drop 1 to 2 tablespoons of mixture into heated oil.
Fry to 2 to 3 minutes on each side or until golden brown. Drain on paper towels.

_enrichment.description: Crispy South African corn patties with green onions, celery, and spices, fried to golden perfection for a savoury snack.

title: Indiana Date Pudding
directions: Boil the brown sugar and water for 3 minutes.
Pour into a 9 x 13 pan.
Mix together the sugar and oleo, then add milk, flour, baking powder, chopped nuts and diced dates.
Spoon batter mixture by spoonfuls into water/brown sugar mixture.
Bake at 350° until brown on top.
