_enrichment.description: A rich and moist American date pudding with brown sugar and nuts, baked until golden for a decadent dessert.

title: Heavenly Chicken Salad
directions: Combine chicken, onion, celery, pineapple and raisins.
Blend mayonnaise, sour cream, curry powder, chutney and lemon juice. Combine with chicken.
Chill.
May be garnished with slivered almonds, cherry tomatoes, carrot strips or olives.

_enrichment.description: A creamy and fruity South African chicken salad with pineapple, apple, and raisins, perfect for a light lunch or picnic.

title: Turkey Pitas
ingredients: 2 or 3 plum tomatoes, chopped in bite size pieces
directions: Mix first 5 ingredients together, let marinate overnight. Brown meat, drain off fat.
Add cumin and garlic.
Stir in cheese just before serving (hot or cold).
Cut 8 pita pockets in half. Put equal amount of each mixture.

_enrichment.description: A fresh and zesty turkey pita with tomatoes, herbs, and Parmesan cheese, ideal for a quick and flavourful lunch.

title: Medallions Of Pork With Wild Mushrooms And Berries
ingredients: 2 lb. medallions of pork or 4 boneless pork chops
directions: Melt 1 stick of butter with the chopped garlic.
Add the mushrooms; saute over medium heat until partially cooked.
Add the cranberries, blueberries and Port; continue to cook for another 5 minutes, or until mushrooms are soft.
With a slotted spoon, remove the mushrooms and berries; set aside on a warm platter.

_enrichment.description: Tender pork medallions paired with a rich sauce of wild mushrooms and a sweet-tart berry reduction, finished with a touch of butter and garlic.

title: Smothered Chicken With Vidalias
ingredients: 1 pkg. dried chicken noodle soup mix
1 medium Vidalia onion, sliced thinly and separated
directions: Combine flour, salt and pepper in a plastic food storage bag. Add chicken and shake to coat well.
In 10-inch skillet, melt shortening or oil and brown chicken.
Drain off fat and sprinkle soup mix and onion over chicken in skillet.
Mix together milk and water and add to skillet.
Cover and cook over low heat about 30 minutes or until chicken is done.
If dish seems too dry, add more liquid.
Serve over rice.

_enrichment.description: Juicy chicken breasts smothered in a creamy onion sauce, made with sweet Vidalias and a hint of soup mix for depth.

title: Carole'S Chicken
directions: Set aside chicken breasts.
Mix all ingredients in a bowl. Place chicken in a deep dish or bowl and pour marinade over chicken.
Marinate chicken overnight or for several hours.
Grill chicken over hot fire for 3 minutes each side.

_enrichment.description: Marinated chicken breasts grilled to perfection with a zesty lime and spice blend, featuring turmeric and rosemary for a fragrant kick.

title: Curried Green Beans And Potatoes
ingredients: 1 lb. small, unpeeled red potatoes, halved lengthwise and sliced (about 3 c.)
1 Tbsp. chopped unsalted dry roasted peanuts
directions: Place potato slices in a medium saucepan.
Cover with water; bring to a boil over medium-high heat.
Cover; cook for 5 minutes. Add green beans; cook, uncovered, for 8 minutes, or until the vegetables are tender.
Drain; set aside.
Combine curry powder and the next 4 ingredients in a large nonstick skillet; cook over low heat for 5 minutes.
Stir in yogurt and garlic; cook until mixture is warm.
(Do not overcook, or yogurt will separate.) Combine potato mixture and yogurt mixture in a large bowl; toss gently.
Sprinkle with peanuts.
Yields 6 servings (serving size: 1 cup).

_enrichment.description: A fragrant curry of green beans and potatoes, spiced with cumin and ginger, finished with yogurt for a creamy, tangy touch.

title: Tandoori Chicken(Serves 10 People)
directions: Skin the chicken pieces and score them deeply.
Mix thoroughly chili and curry powder, tomato paste, salt and yogurt.
Marinate chicken pieces in the mixture for four to five hours.
(If desired, this can be marinated in the refrigerator overnight.)
Arrange the chicken pieces in the baking pan and pour the remaining excess marinade over chicken.
Bake at 375° for 45 minutes, occasionally basting.
Drain well; brush melted butter and grill or broil for 5 minutes.
Delicious Tandoori Chicken is ready!

_enrichment.description: Succulent chicken pieces marinated in a spiced yogurt blend, baked until tender and basted for a smoky, aromatic finish.

title: Wetha See Byan (Dry Pork Curry)(Origin: Burmese)
ingredients: 3 Tbsp. light sesame oil or corn oil
1 stalk lemon grass or 2 strips lemon rind
directions: Cut pork into 5 cm (2-inch) pieces.
Excess fat should be removed or the curry will be too rich, but a little fat left on the meat is quite acceptable.
Puree and cook onion, garlic, ginger, chili and turmeric.
When well cooked, add pork and simmer gently in its own juices until tender.
Add crushed or finely chopped lemon grass or rind, tamarind juice and fish sauce.
Stir well. Cook until all water has evaporated and the oil separates from the gravy.
The flavour of the curry can be varied by adding extra chili powder for a hot curry, or stirring in a piece of hot Indian mango pickle.
Slices of lemon, chopped fresh coriander leaves or chopped spring onions can be added at the end of cooking, if desired.

_enrichment.description: A dry Burmese pork curry with bold flavours of lemongrass, tamarind, and fish sauce, simmered until rich and aromatic.

title: Shrimp Masala(Pakistan)
ingredients: 2 c. fresh shredded coconut, soaked for 1 hour in 3 c. water
directions: Grind together the garlic, ginger, coriander, cumin and red pepper to a paste.
Heat oil in a pan; add onion.
Saute onions. Add ground paste and cook until oil separates about 15 minutes on a low temperature.
Strain coconut.
Add coconut milk to the onions and bring to a boil, then simmer over medium low heat 15 minutes.
Add shrimp and simmer until shrimp is done, about 8 to 10 minutes.

_enrichment.description: Shrimp cooked in a fragrant Pakistani masala with coconut milk, coriander, and cumin, delivering a creamy, spicy depth.

title: Beets With Tomato And Onion - Indian Style
ingredients: 3/4 lb. beets, peeled and cut into chunks
2 medium tomatoes, seeded and chopped (or canned equivalent)
directions: Heat the oil in a saucepan.
Add the cumin, garlic and onion and cook until the onions are soft.
Add the flour and cayenne and cook for a minute more.

Add the beets, tomatoes, salt and water and simmer, covered, for 30 minutes or until beets are cooked. Uncover and cook for 7 minutes to thicken the sauce. I took these to a potluck once and after most of the guests had gone, the host was seen polishing off the leftovers straight from the pot.

_enrichment.description: Beets and tomatoes slow-cooked with cumin and cayenne, creating a rich, earthy side dish with a hint of heat.

title: Gourmet Pork Medallions
directions: Cut pork tenderloin into 8 thin slices.
Gently pound to 1/4-inch thick medallions.
Mix flour with 1/4 teaspoon each salt and pepper.
Coat medallions with mixture.
In a skillet, saute medallions in 3 tablespoons of the butter until golden brown on both sides.
Remove from skillet and keep warm in a 200° oven.
Add remaining butter to skillet and saute onion, scallops and mushrooms for 3 to 4 minutes.
Add Chablis and 1/4 of the heavy cream. Simmer until sauce is thickened.
Taste and add salt and pepper as needed.
Whip the remaining cream until stiff and fold into Hollandaise Sauce to make mousseline.

_enrichment.description: Elegant pork medallions seared to golden perfection, served with a silky mushroom and Chablis cream sauce.

title: Low-Fat Curry Dip
ingredients: garlic powder and parsley to taste
other spices of your choice to taste
directions: Separate squash from its shell and place in a 9 x 12-inch greased baking dish.
Mix in carrots, margarine and seasonings. Top with Mozzarella.
Bake at 350° for 10 to 15 minutes or until cheese is melted and dish is heated through.
Makes 6 to 8 (1/2 cup) portions.

_enrichment.description: A low-fat curry dip made with tofu, mango, and spices, baked until bubbly and served with crudités or crackers.

title: Savory Herb-And-Garlic Pork Chops
directions: Place the chops in the freezer until partially frozen, about 20 minutes.
Trim off all visible fat.
In a cup, combine the garlic, cumin, salt substitute, coriander, red pepper and black pepper.
Spread the mixture thickly on both sides of the chops. Place on a plate.
Cover with plastic wrap and refrigerate 2 to 8 hours.

_enrichment.description: Herb-and-garlic-crusted pork chops with a hint of apple for sweetness, served alongside braised red cabbage and roasted potatoes.

title: Indian Chicken Breasts
directions: In a blender, combine all ingredients except chicken.
Puree until smooth.
Place chicken in shallow dish.
Pour marinade over and turn to coat.
Cover and let stand at least 15 minutes. Preheat grill or large cast-iron skillet over medium heat. Spray cooking surface. Cook 4 minutes per side.

_enrichment.description: Chicken breasts marinated in a spiced yogurt blend, grilled or pan-seared for a juicy, aromatic main.

title: Sandy'S Chicken Curry
directions: Put chicken, stock, ginger and salt in a pan; simmer for about 30 minutes.
Heat oil in a small skillet; saute onions until golden.
Blend in garlic, peanut butter, curry powder and milk. Cook mixture over low heat for about 4 minutes.
Mix the flour and a small amount of water together until smooth.
Remove chicken pieces from stock; add flour mixture to stock.
Cook over medium heat, stirring constantly, until smooth and thickened.
Pour onion mixture into sauce and blend well.
Stir in the lemon juice and remaining seasonings.
Add chicken pieces to the sauce and heat through gently.

_enrichment.description: A rich and creamy chicken curry with peanut butter and warm spices, simmered until thick and fragrant.

title: White Bean Chicken Chili
ingredients: 1 lb. boned chicken thighs, cut into 1-inch pieces
directions: Heat oil in Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add onion, garlic, cumin, oregano, aniseed and red pepper and saute for 5 minutes. Push onion to one side of pan.
Season chicken with salt and pepper.
Saute 5 minutes.
Drain beans; reserve 1/2 cup bean liquid.
Add broth, beans, chilies, cream and reserved bean liquid to chicken.
Simmer for 10 minutes or until chicken is tender. Season to taste with salt and pepper.
Top with cheese and sprinkle with cilantro on individual serving.
Can be prepared a day ahead and refrigerated.
Serves 4 to 6.

_enrichment.description: A hearty white bean and chicken chilli with cumin, oregano, and a touch of cream for richness.

title: Cuban Chicken Fricassee
directions: Heat olive oil in medium heat in a large saucepan.
Add chicken breast, salt, cumin, garlic powder, potatoes, bell pepper and onion.
Stir until chicken turns white.
Add cooking wine, tomato sauce, water, bouillon cube and cilantro.
Cook until potatoes become soft, about 30 minutes.

_enrichment.description: A Cuban-inspired chicken fricassee with bell peppers, potatoes, and a tomato-wine sauce, finished with fresh cilantro.

title: Sole Curry
ingredients: 2 lb. sole fillets, cut crosswise into 1 1/2-inch strips
directions: In a wide frying pan over medium heat, melt butter.
Add shallots, curry and fish.
Turn heat to high and gently shake. Turn fish over carefully.
Cook for a total of 3 to 5 minutes until fish flakes in thickest portion.

_enrichment.description: A delicate French-inspired sole dish with a warm curry butter sauce, finished with cream and lemon for a rich yet light meal.

title: Veal Medallions With Chanterelle Mushrooms And Champagne
ingredients: 2 lb. veal tenderloin, sliced into 8 (4 oz.) filets
1 medium tomato, sliced in half and coarsely chopped
directions: Place a large skillet on the stove on high heat and add 3 tablespoons of the olive oil.
Place the Chanterelles into the skillet and saute until tender, about 5 minutes.
Season with salt and pepper.
Add 2 more tablespoons of oil to the skillet and add the button mushrooms and shallots.
Saute until the mushrooms are browned, about 5 minutes.
Add the bay leaves, thyme and tomato. Stir the mushrooms well and season, if necessary.
Pour in 1/4 cup of the champagne, the reduced beef stock and stir well to combine the ingredients.

_enrichment.description: Elegant veal medallions paired with earthy chanterelle mushrooms and a luxurious champagne sauce, perfect for a dinner-party.

title: Mix Vegetable Curry
directions: Heat a sauce pan on high heat.
Put some oil in it.
Add onions and cook on med heat until lightly brown and until the oil separates.
Add tomatoes and mix vegetable and cook until tender and oil comes on top.

_enrichment.description: A hearty vegetable curry with coconut and spices, offering a creamy, spicy, and slightly sweet flavour profile.

title: Vidalia Onion Cake
ingredients: 2 c. all-purpose flour, sifted before measuring
directions: Mix baking soda with tomato soup; let stand while mixing other ingredients.
Cream shortening and sugar.
Add cloves and nutmeg to flour; sift together.
Combine soup mixture and flour.
Add onions, nuts and raisins (drained).
Bake at 325° in greased and floured or otherwise prepared tube pan or Bundt pan for about an hour or until done with toothpick test.
Remove from pan as soon as heat from pan will allow; cool upside down.

_enrichment.description: A moist, spiced cake with caramelised onions, nuts, and raisins, baked in a Bundt pan for a comforting dessert.

title: India Curry
ingredients: 2 lb. ground beef or mixed beef and lamb
directions: Fry onions, chili and curry powder in enough fat to brown.
Add salted meat and press flat in frying pan; brown on both sides. Chop into small pieces and add to tomatoes, beans and diced potatoes.
Cook for 10 minutes.
Bring salted rice to boil and boil for 15 minutes.
Serve on plates with sauce on top.
Serves 6. Serve with Fritos.

_enrichment.description: A robust Indian-inspired beef curry with potatoes and string beans, served over rice and crispy Fritos for texture.

title: Rowena'S Curry Chicken
directions: Coat chicken and brown in oil; remove.
In drippings over medium heat, cook garlic, onions, green pepper, celery, curry and salt for 5 minutes.
Stir in tomatoes and raisins.
Add chicken. Cover and simmer until chicken is done, about 45 minutes.
Brown almonds in butter, shaking skillet occasionally.
Sprinkle almonds and parsley over chicken.
Serve with hot rice or noodles.

_enrichment.description: A fragrant chicken curry with almonds, raisins, and a touch of sweetness, simmered until the chicken is fall-off-the-bone tender.

title: Indian Bread(Makes 10 To 12 Servings)
directions: Sift together the dry ingredients.
Combine the milk and molasses.
Add the liquid ingredients to the dry ingredients and beat until smooth.
Pour into a well-greased 2-quart steam mold, cover and place on a rack in a deep kettle that has a close fitting lid.
Pour enough boiling water into the kettle to come about halfway up the mold.
Cover the kettle and steam the mold for 3 hours.
Remove the mold from the kettle and let stand 20 minutes.
Remove the cover and let stand 10 minutes longer. Loosen the edges with spatula; invert onto plate.
Let stand until bread unmolds.
Serve with lots of butter.

_enrichment.description: A steamed bread with molasses and nutmeg, traditionally served with molasses or butter for a sweet, spiced treat.

title: Indian Puddinga Yankee Favorite!
directions: In a large saucepan, scald the milk.
Using a wire whisk, gradually stir in the cornmeal.
Cook over medium heat, stirring occasionally, until the mixture thickens and coats a metal spoon, about 10 minutes.
Stir in the butter or margarine; remove from heat.

_enrichment.description: A classic New England pudding with cornmeal, molasses, and warm spices, offering a dense, custard-like texture.

title: No-Fuss Curry Chicken
ingredients: 1 large McIntosh apple, peeled and sliced
directions: Wash and dry chicken.
In oblong 3-quart baking dish (13 x 8 3/4 x 1 3/4-inches), stir together onion, apple, tomatoes, salt and curry powder.
Top with chicken, skin side up, in a single layer.
Sprinkle chicken with paprika.
Bake, uncovered, in 400° oven until chicken is tender and skin crisp, about 1 hour.

_enrichment.description: A fuss-free roast chicken dish with caramelised onions and apples, baked with curry and paprika for a fragrant main.

title: Lime Chutney
ingredients: 1 1/2 c. minced lime peel (rind and pith)
1/4 c. minced, peeled fresh ginger root
directions: In stainless steel or enamel kettle, combine lime juice, peel, vinegar, sugar, ginger root, onion and raisins.
Grind together the coriander seed, mustard seed, peppercorns and cloves.
Add mixture to kettle with cinnamon, cardamom, cayenne and salt.
Bring to a boil over moderate heat.
Simmer, stirring occasionally, for 40 to 50 minutes until sauce thickens.
Ladle the chutney into sterilized jars.
Makes 1 quart.
Best if stored in 1/2-pint jars.

_enrichment.description: A tangy, spiced lime chutney with raisins and warm spices, perfect for pairing with meats or cheese boards.

title: Chicken Stir-Fry(Curry)
ingredients: 1 lb. boneless chicken breasts, cut in 1-inch pieces
1 c. salted peanuts (may use unsalted or dry roast)
directions: In large skillet, over medium-high heat, heat 2 tablespoons peanut oil.
Add chicken and garlic; stir-fry for 3 minutes or until chicken is done.
Remove from skillet.
Heat remaining oil, stir-fry broccoli and onions for 2 to 3 minutes.
Drain pineapple, reserving liquid.

_enrichment.description: A quick and colourful chicken stir-fry with broccoli, pineapple, and a curry-infused sauce, served with peanuts for crunch.

title: Little Indian Dish
directions: Saute beef, onion and green pepper until meat is done.
Add mushroom soup and stewed tomatoes and cook on low heat until onion and green pepper is done.
Add cooked long grain rice and blend well.
Pour into casserole dish, then garnish top with grated cheese.
Place in a 400° oven until cheese melts.
Also good warmed over.
Add the catsup and chili powder when adding the soup and stewed tomatoes.

_enrichment.description: A comforting casserole with ground beef, rice, and a creamy mushroom-tomato sauce, topped with melted cheese.

title: Indian Slaw
directions: Mix the cabbage, carrots, onions and peppers together.
Take out 1 tablespoon sugar, then sprinkle rest over cabbage mixture. Bring oil, vinegar, celery seed, salt and the 1 tablespoon of sugar to a rapid boil and pour over the cabbage.
Refrigerate for 9 days.
(May be eaten sooner, but is better after 9 days.)

_enrichment.description: A crunchy, tangy slaw with cabbage, carrots, and peppers, sweetened and dressed for a refreshing side dish.

title: India Potatoes And Tomatoes
ingredients: 3 large potatoes, cooked, peeled and diced
directions: Fry mustard seeds in hot oil (be careful they can burn quickly).
Add diced potatoes and fry 5 minutes.
Add tomatoes, green pepper, turmeric, grated ginger, cayenne, salt and coriander leaves.
Cook slowly until tomatoes are done.
Remove from fire and serve hot.

_enrichment.description: A fragrant potato and tomato dish with mustard seeds, turmeric, and ginger, offering a spicy, earthy flavour.

title: Chicken Curry Casserole
ingredients: 1 1/2 lb. chicken breasts, cooked and cut in bite-size pieces
directions: Cook rice according to directions; spread in bottom of large baking dish. Cover with frozen peas.
In separate bowl, combine soups, mayonnaise and curry powder; then spread 1/2 this mixture over peas.
Next, place chicken pieces in dish.
Spread remaining mixture over top.
Sprinkle cheese and bake in 375° oven until heated through, about 45 minutes to 1 hour.
Great dish to make ahead of time and freeze.
Serves 8.

_enrichment.description: A creamy chicken curry casserole with rice and peas, baked until bubbly and golden with melted cheese on top.

title: Christa'S Tomato Chutney
ingredients: small piece ginger and any kind of fruit you have left (even raisins)
directions: Put tomatoes in bowl and sprinkle them with salt.
Cover bowl for 24 hours, put in fridge.
Mix the curry, flour and mustard in a bowl, add enough vinegar to make a paste.
Drain tomatoes, rinse off excess salt and place them in a preserving pan.
Simmer gently until just boiling, then cook for 5 minutes.
Dissolve the sugar with the remaining vinegar and add to the pan.
Simmer about 30 minutes.
Add the flour paste and cook for 5 minutes, stirring constantly.
Pour in hot sterilized jars and seal with vinegar proof tops.

_enrichment.description: A tangy, slightly sweet tomato chutney with warm spices, perfect for pairing with meats or cheese boards.

title: Tandoon Chicken (Roasted Chicken)(Indian)
directions: Take a big mixing bowl and mix all the ingredients in it.
Mix it very nicely that it becomes like a paste.
Then mix chicken drumsticks in it.
Keep the whole pan in the refrigerator for 4 to 6 hours before eating.

_enrichment.description: Tender chicken drumsticks marinated in a creamy, spiced yogurt and sour cream mixture, infused with garlic and ginger.

title: Hamburgers, Indian Style
directions: Mix the ground lamb and salt thoroughly.
Form into 2-inch diameter meat patties and brown in olive oil until a rich deep brown.
Turn down heat and add onions, all spices and tomatoes. Simmer for 45 minutes.
Cover; stir during this time.

_enrichment.description: Juicy lamb patties seasoned with aromatic spices, slow-cooked with tomatoes and onions for a rich, hearty dish.

title: Chicken Curry(Recipe From India)
directions: Brown onion and celery in butter.
Add flour and blend.
Add chicken broth; cook until thick, stirring constantly.
Add tomato juice, Worcestershire sauce, seasonings and chicken.
Heat thoroughly.
Serve over the cooked rice.
Serves 8.

_enrichment.description: A classic British-style chicken curry with a creamy, spiced sauce, served over fluffy rice.

title: Vidalia Onion Casserole
directions: In greased casserole, arrange onions.
Mix together other ingredients except cracker crumbs and pour over onions.
Bake at 350° for 30 to 40 minutes.
Top with cracker crumbs.

_enrichment.description: A rich, cheesy casserole with caramelised onions, water chestnuts, and a crispy cracker topping.

title: Indiana Lemon Angel Pie
directions: Meringue shell:
Beat egg whites with cream of tartar in medium bowl until foamy.
Beat in sugar, one tablespoon at a time, until meringue forms, still glossy peaks.
Lightly butter a 9 inch pie plate.
Spoon meringue over bottom and side
to form a shell. Bake in a slow oven at 275 degrees for 1 hour.
Turn oven off and allow meringue to cool in the oven.

_enrichment.description: A light, airy lemon meringue pie with a crisp shell and tangy lemon filling.

title: Indian Black-Eyed Peas And Potatoes
directions: In skillet heat oil.
Add mustard seed.
Cover and cook until seed begins to pop.
Remove from heat.
Remove lid, stir in cumin. Add onion and garlic.
Cook and stir until onion is tender but not brown.
Stir in undrained black-eyed peas, potatoes, ginger, turmeric and red pepper.
Bring to boil.
Reduce heat, simmer for 15 minutes.
Serves 4 to 5.

_enrichment.description: A hearty, spiced stew of black-eyed peas and potatoes, flavoured with ginger and turmeric.

title: Vidalia Onion Seafood Casserole
directions: Saute onions, celery and green pepper in margarine.
Add pimento, cream of celery soup, milk and cream cheese.
Heat slowly until blended.
Add rice and shrimp; mix well.
Bake at 350° for 30 minutes.
Place crushed crackers on top the last 10 minutes of baking.
(Cover top of casserole with crumbs.)

_enrichment.description: A creamy seafood casserole with shrimp, rice, and Vidalia onions, topped with crispy cracker crumbs.

title: Curried Butternut Squash Bisque(A Spicy First Course Soup)
ingredients: 3 lb. butternut squash, peeled, seeded and cubed
salt and freshly ground pepper to taste
directions: In a heavy soup pot, heat oil over medium heat.
Add onions and garlic; saute for 2 to 3 minutes or until slightly softened.
Stir in curry and cumin and cook for 1 minute.
Add chicken stock, cider, rice and squash; bring to a boil.
Reduce to low heat and cover, then simmer for 30 to 40 minutes, or until squash is tender. Pour mixture through a strainer set over a large bowl.
Puree the solids in a food processor or blender until very smooth.
Return the puree and liquid to the saucepan.
(May be prepared to this point and stored, covered, in refrigerator for up to 2 days.)
To serve, heat gently and season with salt and pepper.
In small bowl, stir together yogurt and milk and add a tablespoon to each serving, drawing a knife through to make swirls.
Serves 8.

_enrichment.description: A spiced butternut squash soup with a silky texture, finished with a swirl of yogurt.

title: Indian Corn
directions: Cut bacon into small pieces; fry until crisp, drain, reserving 2 tablespoons fat.
In reserved fat, saute green pepper and onion until tender.
Add corn, salt and pepper; heat thoroughly over medium heat.
Add cream; cook 1 minute, stirring.
Stir in bacon. Serve immediately.
Serves 4.

_enrichment.description: Sweet corn kernels sautéed with bacon, onions, and green peppers, finished with a touch of cream.

title: Indian Corn Stew
directions: Cook onions and green pepper with margarine in frying pan for 5 minutes.
Add meat and allow to brown, stirring often.
Add tomatoes, corn, soup, sugar and salt.
Simmer for 15 minutes. Stir in flour and water.
Cook a few minutes and serve.

_enrichment.description: A hearty stew of ground beef, corn, and tomatoes, simmered in a creamy tomato soup base.

title: Crisp Pickle Slices
ingredients: a small amount of green food coloring
directions: Do not pare cucumbers; slice 1/8-inch thick.
Add onions and salt.
Cover with cracked ice and mix thoroughly.
Let stand 3 hours; drain thoroughly.
Combine remaining ingredients and bring to a boil.
Drop cucumber slices into mixture.
Cook until they become transparent.
Seal in hot, sterilized jars.
Makes 4 pints.

_enrichment.description: Crisp, tangy pickle slices with onions, celery, and mustard seeds, preserved in a spiced vinegar brine.

title: Chicken Medallions
ingredients: 1/2 c. melted butter (this can be omitted for less fattening dish)
directions: Cut chicken into 1-inch nuggets.
Combine other ingredients, except butter.
Dip chicken in butter, then into cheese/crumb mixture.
Spread on foil covered baking sheet and bake at 400° for 10 minutes.

_enrichment.description: Golden, crispy chicken medallions coated in Parmesan and breadcrumbs, baked to perfection.

title: Curry Chicken With Pineapple In Coconut Milk
ingredients: 1 1/2 to 2 lbs. boneless chicken cut into bite size pieces or shrimp, peeled
1 6 oz. can bamboo shoots, cut into slivers
1-2 oz. curry paste, about 1 teaspoon, adjust to taste (do not substitute powder)
2 20 oz. cans pineapple tidbits with juice
2 tsp. dried basil or 2 tbsp. fresh basil, cut up
1 14 oz. can baby corn (on cob) drained, cut into bite size pieces (optional)
directions: Saute cut chicken in oil. If using shrimp, DO NOT cook through, it will be tough. Add curry paste and mix to coat.
In a large pot, combine remaining ingredients. Add cooked chicken or shrimp (do not drain). Boil for 3 minutes, longer is you want carrots crisp-tender.
Serve over rice, saffron rice is best. It's better the next day!

_enrichment.description: Tender chicken simmered in a fragrant coconut milk and curry sauce with pineapple and vegetables.

title: Indian Cookies
directions: Combine dry ingredients in large bowl, then mix together.
Make a well in middle of bowl.
Place eggs and water/milk gradually into well.
When mixing, include dry ingredients from sides.
Place flour on flat surface to roll out and cut-out round cookies.
Bake in 325° oven for 10 to 12 minutes.
Makes 2 dozen.

_enrichment.description: Spiced, nutmeg-infused cookies with a soft, chewy texture, perfect with tea or coffee.

title: Curry Chicken Divan
directions: Boil chicken with salt until tender.
Cook broccoli, carrot and onion; drain.
Place chicken and vegetables in buttered baking dish.
Mix other ingredients, spread on top and cover with bread crumbs.
Drizzle with melted butter on top.
Bake at 350° for 25 to 30 minutes.

_enrichment.description: A creamy, comforting American casserole with tender chicken, vegetables, and a rich curry-infused sauce, topped with cheesy breadcrumbs.

title: Curry Sausage Couscous
directions: Prepare couscous as directed on package.
Cook sausage, onion, nuts, curry powder, salt and garlic in 10-inch skillet until pork is no longer pink.
Drain.
Stir in couscous and parsley.
Cook about 2 minutes, or until mixture is hot.

_enrichment.description: A fragrant and nutty couscous dish with spiced pork sausage, perfect for a quick yet flavourful meal.

title: Easy Chicken Curry
ingredients: 1 can Campbell's cream of chicken soup
1 can Campbell's cream of mushroom soup
directions: In skillet, melt butter.
Add chicken; brown for 5 minutes with a dusting of pepper.
While chicken is cooking, combine all other ingredients.
Mix well.
Coat bottom and sides of a casserole dish with mixture.
Place chicken in dish.
Garnish with desired amount of broccoli trees.
Cover all with remainder of mixture.
Cover dish; bake at 350° for 1 hour.
Serve over egg noodles.

_enrichment.description: A simple and tangy chicken curry with a creamy sauce, ideal for a fuss-free weeknight dinner.

title: Indian Casserole
directions: Brown beef, salt, onion and green pepper.
Add tomatoes and hominy.
Pour into buttered casserole dish and bake at 350° for 40 minutes.
Remove from oven and sprinkle cheese on top.
Return to oven and bake 15 minutes.

_enrichment.description: A hearty Mexican-inspired casserole with hominy, beef, and noodles, baked until bubbly and cheesy.

title: Indian Fry Bread
directions: Dissolve yeast in warm water.
Add sugar and salt.
Allow to stand 5 minutes covered with towel.
Add flour and oil; stir flour into liquid mixture.
Turn out on floured board and knead until dough's texture is smooth.
Put dough into greased bowl, cover, and let rise for 1 1/2 hours or until doubled in bulk. When doubled, punch down and knead a few moments in 1/2 c. cornmeal. Separate into two balls.
Work with one ball at a time.
Cover resting ball with towel.
Roll dough into 12 inch diameter circle, 1/2 inch thick.
Cut into 2 inch squares.
Let pieces of dough rest while oil heats in iron skillet to boiling point.
Fry 5 to 6 pieces at a time until golden brown, just a few moments.
If there are leftovers, warm for breakfast and shake in confectioner's sugar.

_enrichment.description: A soft, pillowy Indian fry bread, perfect for serving with dips or as a base for tacos.

title: Indian Bread
directions: Dissolve yeast in water.
Mix flour, sugar and corn meal together.
Add yeast and enough water to make a sticky dough.
Drop by teaspoons into hot lard (deep fat fry).
Fry until crispy brown on both sides.
Remove to a paper towel covered pan.
Serve with butter and maple syrup or peanut butter or jelly.
Also good with ham and eggs or tomato gravy.
Lots of ways to serve them.

_enrichment.description: Crispy, deep-fried Indian bread with a slightly sweet and savoury flavour, best served hot.

title: Quick Shrimp Curry
ingredients: 1 c. condensed cream of mushroom soup
directions: Cook onion, garlic and curry powder in butter until onion is tender.
Stir in soup, milk, shrimp and peas.
Cook over low heat for 5 minutes; stir frequently.
Serve over rice.
Serve with fruit salad.

_enrichment.description: A quick and aromatic shrimp curry with a creamy sauce, served over fluffy rice.

title: Black Bean Casserole
directions: Soak beans 4 to 8 hours, then cook by bringing to a boil. Simmer until done.
Cook rice by instructions on bag.
Cook beans with coriander, cumin and garlic.
Place beans in a large casserole dish.
Add rice, then chopped tomatoes with juice.
Add cheese and olives.
Bake at 350° for 30 minutes.

_enrichment.description: A flavourful black bean casserole with rice, tomatoes, and cheese, baked until golden.

title: Red Snapper With Honey Mustard Sauce
directions: Rinse fish and pat dry.
Place in a baking dish.
Combine first 7 ingredients.
Stir with a wire whisk until well blended.
Pour mixture over fish, cover and marinate 30 minutes to an hour in refrigerator.
Remove fish from marinade.
Coat grill with vegetable spray and grill fish over hot coals or broil 6 minutes on each side or until fish flakes.
Baste with extra marinade.

_enrichment.description: Tender red snapper marinated in a sweet and tangy honey-mustard sauce with aromatic spices.

title: Cold Chicken Curry
ingredients: 8 c. cooked chicken, cut into large chunks
1 c. thinly sliced bell pepper strips
directions: Cook rice; refrigerate until cool.
Toss rice, cauliflower and French dressing; cover and refrigerate for 2 hours.
In a large bowl, combine the mayonnaise, curry powder, salt and pepper.
Stir in milk.
Add chicken and toss.
Refrigerate for 2 hours.
In a large bowl, mix the rice and chicken.
Add green pepper, celery and onion.
Turn on platter lined with romaine leaves.
Top with almonds.

_enrichment.description: A refreshing cold chicken curry salad with rice, cauliflower, and a creamy curry dressing.

title: Tandoori Chicken
directions: Skin chicken.
Slash chicken flesh.
Combine ingredients in bowl.
Add chicken, turning to coat.
Cover and refrigerate several hours or overnight, turning occasionally.
Let chicken stand 1 hour at room temperature before cooking.
Preheat oven to 450°.
Arrange in shallow baking pan.
Bake 15 minutes; turn and coat with marinade.
Bake until chicken is fork-tender.

_enrichment.description: Juicy tandoori chicken marinated in yogurt and spices, grilled to perfection.

title: Chicken Mandalay
ingredients: 1 jar junior size baby apricots or peaches
directions: Shake first 3 ingredients in bag with chicken until coated. Saute in oil until golden brown.
Mix remaining ingredients in bowl and add to chicken.
Simmer until chicken is cooked.
Serve with rice.

_enrichment.description: Tender chicken cutlets coated in a spiced flour mixture and simmered in a tangy sauce.

title: Vidalia Onion Pie
directions: Preheat oven to 350°.
Mix cracker crumbs and 4 tablespoons butter; press into 8-inch pie plate.
Melt 2 tablespoons butter. Saute onions until tender.
Beat other ingredients, except cheese, together.
Pour onions over cracker crust. Pour other mixture over onions.
Sprinkle with grated cheese and dot with paprika or parsley flakes.
Bake 35 minutes.

_enrichment.description: A savoury Vidalia onion pie with a buttery cracker crust, topped with cheese and herbs.

title: Chicken Breast With Honey Chutney And Pears
ingredients: 6 chicken breast halves, boned, skinned and trimmed
directions: Preheat oven to 350°.
Combine pears and lemon juice. Melt margarine in skillet.
Add chicken; saute. Transfer to shallow glass baking dish.
Combine remaining ingredients.
Pour over chicken.
Place pears and lemon juice combination on top.
Bake 30 minutes or until done.

_enrichment.description: Succulent chicken breasts glazed with honey chutney and paired with tender pears.

title: Coriander And Mint Chutney
ingredients: 3 to 4 hot chili peppers, to taste (fewer if you do not want it hot)
directions: Clean and wash coriander, mint, chili, tomato and onion.
Put small amounts of each in blender and blend.
Add yogurt and salt a little at a time.
Blend until smooth.
Store in a container with a tight lid.
Will last approximately 2 to 3 weeks, just stir each time you use it.

_enrichment.description: A vibrant and cooling coriander and mint chutney, perfect for pairing with grilled meats or snacks.

title: Holiday Hot Nuts
directions: Preheat oven to 350°.
In a large bowl, toss nuts with oil. In a small bowl, combine all spices except salt.
Add the spices to nuts, tossing nuts to cover evenly with spices.
Spread the nuts on a baking sheet in a single layer.
Bake for 10 minutes. If desired, sprinkle with salt.
Makes about 3 cups.

_enrichment.description: Spiced mixed nuts roasted with a blend of warm, aromatic spices for a festive snack or party appetiser.

title: Chicken Tikka
ingredients: 1 (3 lb.) chicken, boned and cut into 8ths
directions: In a large mixing bowl, blend spices together with yoghurt, salt, lemon juice, coloring and tomato puree.
Add chicken pieces and coat well.
Leave overnight or for at least 3 hours.

_enrichment.description: Marinated chicken pieces grilled or baked for a tender, flavourful starter or main course with a yogurt-based spice blend.

title: Queema (Ground Beef) Or Beef
directions: Cut and fry onion in vegetable oil until red.
Add meat and other ingredients.
Let it cook on high heat for about 5 minutes, then turn the heat down.
Let it cook until the meat is tender.

_enrichment.description: Slow-cooked beef stew with warm spices, onions, and yogurt for a rich, hearty dish perfect for colder months.

title: Curry In A Hurry
ingredients: 1 c. diced chicken, pork or turkey
directions: In saucepan, cook the apple, onion and curry in butter until onion is tender. Stir in soup, water and meat.
Heat, stirring occasionally.
Serve over rice and garnish with coconut.
Serves 4.

_enrichment.description: A quick and creamy curry with apple, onion, and coconut, served over rice for a comforting meal.

title: Red Beef Curry(Low Sodium)
ingredients: 1 lb. round or sirloin steak, cut into 3/4-inch cubes
directions: Brown beef in shortening.
Add all seasoning and tomato paste. Stir to blend.
Stir in enough water to cover meat (about 1 1/4 cups).
Cover and simmer until meat is tender, about 45 minutes to 1 hour.
Remove cinnamon stick.
Serve over brown rice.

_enrichment.description: A low-sodium beef curry with warm spices, tomato paste, and thyme, simmered until tender and served with brown rice.

title: Polynesian Turkey
ingredients: 2 c. (15 1/2 oz. can) pineapple chunks, drained
directions: In a large bowl, combine first 7 ingredients.
Toss lightly. Cover and chill at least 2 hours.
Before serving, slice banana. Add to turkey mixture with nuts.
Serve in lettuce cups.
Garnish with coconut and oranges.

_enrichment.description: A tropical-inspired turkey salad with curry, pineapple, banana, and coconut, served in lettuce cups for a light meal.

title: Medallions Of Pork With Riesling Sauce
ingredients: 1 (12 oz.) pork tenderloin, cut into 1-inch thick rounds
3 Tbsp. balsamic vinegar or red wine vinegar
1 1/2 tsp. minced fresh thyme or 1/2 tsp. dried
1 1/2 tsp. minced fresh oregano or 1/2 tsp. dried
1/2 stick chilled unsalted butter, cut into pieces
directions: Season pork with salt and pepper.
Coat in the flour; shake off excess.
Melt 2 tablespoons butter in heavy skillet over medium-high heat.
Add onion and garlic and saute until golden brown, about 5 minutes.
Transfer to bowl.
Melt remaining 2 tablespoons butter in same skillet over medium-high heat.
Add pork and saute about 4 minutes per side.
Transfer pork to plate. Add onion mixture, Riesling, raisins, vinegar, green peppercorns and herbs to same skillet and boil until sauce thickens, about 4 minutes.
Add pork to skillet to heat through.
Divide pork among plates.
Add 1/4 cup butter to sauce in skillet and whisk just until melted.
Mix in pine nuts.
Spoon sauce over pork.

_enrichment.description: Pan-seared pork medallions with a rich Riesling sauce, raisins, and herbs for an elegant main course.

title: Vidalia Onion Casserole(Serves 6)
ingredients: 3 c. Vidalia onions, wedged and parboiled
directions: Melt butter; stir in flour, broth and evaporated milk.
Stir constantly until mixture begins to thicken and is smooth.
Stir cheese into sauce.
Drain onions and add to sauce with salt and pepper.
Pour into buttered 1 1/2-quart casserole dish.
Cover with cracker crumbs.
Bake at 350° for 30 minutes.

_enrichment.description: A creamy, cheesy casserole with caramelised Vidalia onions, perfect for a comforting side dish or light meal.

title: Turkey Medallions With Orange Teriyaki Sauce(Light And Easy)
directions: In a 1-quart saucepan over medium heat in 2 teaspoons corn oil, cook celery, onion, salt and ginger until tender.
In medium bowl, mix turkey, vegetable mix, horseradish and 1 tablespoon chopped parsley until blended.
With slightly dampened hands, shape turkey mixture into 8 medallions.

_enrichment.description: Light turkey medallions with a teriyaki-orange sauce, served with stir-fried vegetables for a quick and healthy meal.

title: Curried-Yogurt Chicken
directions: In small bowl, beat orange juice, yogurt, soy sauce and honey with wire whisk until smooth.
Rinse chicken and pat dry.
Place chicken in small baking dish.
Pour yogurt mixture over chicken; cover and refrigerate 30 minutes to overnight.
Heat oven to 350°. In medium skillet, heat oil over medium heat; add garlic.
Saute until lightly browned.
Keep chicken in dish; spoon off yogurt mixture and add to garlic.
Stir in curry, paprika, ginger, cumin and cornstarch.
Cook, stirring until mixture thickens into a sauce, about 10 minutes.
Pour sauce over chicken in baking dish; sprinkle with raisins.
Bake 30 minutes or until chicken is fork-tender.
Serve over hot rice.

_enrichment.description: Chicken thighs marinated in a spiced yogurt and orange sauce, baked until tender and served with rice.

title: Andale-Dip
ingredients: 1 (16 oz.) can tomatoes, drained and chopped (reserve liquid)
directions: Mix all ingredients.
If too dry, add some of reserved tomato juice.
Refrigerate overnight.
Serve with Doritos or Fritos. Yields 2 cups.

_enrichment.description: A zesty dip with green chilies, olives, tomatoes, and vinegar, perfect for chips or crudités.

title: Medallions Of Pork Tenderloin
ingredients: 2 lb. pork tenderloin, well trimmed, cut crosswise into 1-inch thick medallions
directions: Dry meat with towels.
Combine salt, pepper and flour; dredge medallions.
Shake off excess.
Beat eggs with water.
Dip medallions in eggs and coat with bread crumbs.
Press meat with heel of palm to adhere bread crumbs; dry for 10 minutes.

_enrichment.description: Crispy breaded pork medallions pan-fried and served with a lemon-wine sauce and mushrooms.

title: Cowboy Casserole(Former Dallas Cowboy, Don Meredith)
ingredients: garlic powder, chili powder or Lawry's salt or all 3
directions: Cook spaghetti until just tender.
Brown onion in butter; add ground beef and cook until browned.
Add salt, pepper and seasonings.
Add soups and Worcestershire; mix spaghetti and meat sauce.
Cover and simmer 15 minutes.
Put into casserole dish. Top with cheese and heat in oven until cheese melts.

_enrichment.description: A hearty casserole with spaghetti, ground beef, and creamy tomato soup, topped with melted cheese.

title: Curry Puffs
directions: Saute beef, onion and garlic in butter.
Add curry powder, water and salt.
Simmer for about 30 minutes. Roll out and cut pastry to desired size (2 to 3-inches in diameter).

_enrichment.description: Savoury curry puffs filled with spiced ground beef, perfect as a snack or appetiser.

title: Indian Tapioca Pudding
directions: In the top of a double boiler, heat 3 cups milk. While the milk is heating, mix: cornmeal, quick tapioca, sugar, salt, cinnamon and ginger.
Pour the hot milk over this mixture in a saucepan and add molasses.
Bake in a greased casserole or in a pan set in another pan of water in a 350° oven, stirring every 15 minutes until done, about 2 hours.

_enrichment.description: A creamy, spiced tapioca pudding with molasses and ginger, baked until set for a comforting dessert.

title: Nenek'S Curry
directions: Saute onions and garlic in oil until soft.
Add enough water to make a paste.
Add curry powders and let simmer 10 minutes.
Add potatoes, tofu and chicken seasoning, covering with water.
Cook until potatoes are soft.
Just before serving, add last 2 ingredients.

_enrichment.description: A hearty Malaysian-style curry with tender potatoes, tofu, and chicken, enriched with coconut cream and a tangy lemon finish.

title: Lobster Cakes With Corn Chowder
ingredients: Salt, pepper and hot sauce to taste
directions: Mix together first 10 ingredients.
Fold in lobster meat and season to taste.
Add enough cracker crumbs until all blends together.
Form 8 small cakes.
Preheat oven to 357 degrees.
Place cakes on lightly greased baking sheet.
Bake 10 to 12 minutes.
To serve, place lobster cake in soup plate.
Pour chowder around. Enjoy!

_enrichment.description: Delicate lobster cakes paired with a creamy, spiced corn chowder for a luxurious yet simple seafood dish.

title: Hot Madras Beef Curry
ingredients: 6 green chili peppers, chopped (jalapeno or other)
2 Tbsp. curry powder, more as desired
directions: Heat the butter in large saucepan and fry the onions until golden brown.
Next add green chilies, turmeric, chili powder, cumin, garlic, coriander, curry and tomato paste.
Fry for 10 minutes, stirring constantly, making sure the masala does not stick to the bottom of the pan.
Add lemon, salt and pepper.
In large pan, brown the round steak in butter.
Add water and bouillon cubes.
Let simmer for 20 to 30 minutes.
Mix in the masala sauce.
Simmer for 2 or more hours, adding water as needed. Serve over rice or noodles.
This is a very spicy dish.
You can use more or less chilies to suit your personal taste.

_enrichment.description: A robust Indian-style beef curry with warm spices, slow-cooked until the meat is tender and the sauce rich.

title: Tomato Chicken Curry
ingredients: 1 whole chicken, cut up, or 4 to 6 chicken breasts
directions: Saute onions and garlic in olive oil.
Add spices.
Remove garlic.
Fry chicken in spice mixture for about 30 minutes.
Add tomatoes; simmer for another 30 minutes.
Add yogurt just before serving.

_enrichment.description: A fragrant tomato-based chicken curry with a creamy yogurt finish, balancing warm spices and tender chicken.

title: Joan'S Spicy Hot Black-Eyed Peas
directions: Cook bacon in large skillet until crisp.
Remove bacon; crumble and set aside or substitute oil.
Stir next 11 ingredients into bacon drippings or oil in skillet.
Bring to boil.
Reduce heat; simmer 20 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Pour mixture into serving dish. Sprinkle bacon and parsley over top.
Makes 6 servings.

_enrichment.description: A spicy Southern-style dish of black-eyed peas simmered with tomatoes, peppers, and warm spices.

title: Vidalia Onion Rings
directions: Slice onions 1/8 inch thick.
Sprinkle with sugar and add ice water to cover.
Soak for 1 hour.
Make batter of flour, salt, water, vegetable oil and beaten egg whites.
Drain onion rings and dip into batter.
Fry at 375° until brown.

_enrichment.description: Crispy, sweet Vidalia onion rings with a light, airy batter for a perfect pub-style snack.

title: Polynesian Shrimp Curry
ingredients: 1 lb. unpeeled medium size fresh shrimp
1/2 c. butter or margarine, melted and divided
directions: Combine milk and coconut in small saucepan; bring to boil. Reduce heat and simmer 2 minutes.
Strain mixture; set milk and coconut aside.
Peel and devein shrimp.
Saute shrimp and pineapple chunks in 1/4 cup butter in large skillet 3 to 5 minutes or until shrimp turn pink, stirring frequently.
Drain shrimp mixture and discard liquid.
Set shrimp mixture aside.
Combine remaining 1/4 cup butter and curry powder in large skillet; cook over low heat, stirring constantly, 2 minutes.
Add green onions and celery; cook, stirring constantly until crisp-tender.
Combine flour and garlic salt; add to onion mixture.
Cook 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Gradually add reserved coconut milk and chicken broth; cook over medium heat, stirring constantly, until thickened and bubbly.
Stir in shrimp mixture and reserved coconut.
Serve over rice.
Yield:
4 servings.

_enrichment.description: A tropical-inspired shrimp curry with pineapple and coconut, served over fluffy rice.

title: Gold Medal Banana Cake
directions: Melt shortening, add beaten eggs and sugar.
Sift flour, baking powder, soda, salt and add to mixture.
Add vanilla and bananas; mix well.
Pour mixture into a tube pan and bake at 350° until done.
Test with toothpick.

_enrichment.description: A moist, spiced banana cake with a tender crumb, perfect for afternoon tea or dessert.

title: Mince Curry With Peas(Ground Beef)
directions: Heat oil and cook onions until transparent. Add tomatoes; cook 10 minutes. Add all spices and simmer for 15 minutes.
Crumble beef into sauce, stirring. Cook, covered, for 20 minutes. Add potatoes and cook until almost tender, about 15 minutes. Add frozen peas. Cook for 10 more minutes.
Serve over rice.

_enrichment.description: A fragrant mince curry with potatoes and peas, slow-cooked with warm spices and served over rice.

title: Masoods' Kheema Biryani(Saffron Rice With Ground Lamb)
ingredients: 2 c. basmati rice or other long grain rice, washed and drained
directions: Preheat oven to 350°.
In small bowl, drop saffron threads in; add boiling water and soak for 10 minutes.
Meanwhile, in heavy saucepan, bring 2 cups of water to boil.
Pour in rice and add salt.
Cook uncovered for 10 minutes.
Strain and put rice aside. Separately, heat oil.
Add onions, cumin seed and salt; stir for 3 or 4 minutes.
Add turmeric and cumin for 1 minute, then add lamb. When cooked, add fresh coriander.
Heat remaining 1/4 cup oil and in 4-quart casserole, stir in fennel seed and rice until coated. Spread 1/3 of rice mixture on bottom of casserole.
Layer 1/2 the saffron mixture and 1/2 lamb mixture.
Spread the other half of the rice over the lamb and add the meat and then add the remaining rice mixture.
Put rest of saffron over mixture.
Add chicken stock and pour in casserole and bring to boil over high heat. Cover casserole with sheet of foil and fold down.

Set lid on top of foil and bake in the middle of the oven for 25 minutes, or until rice is tender and all the liquid is absorbed in casserole. Fluff the rice with fork and serve the biryani directly from the casserole or serve on platter.

_enrichment.description: A luxurious biryani with saffron-infused basmati rice and tender ground lamb, layered with fresh herbs.

title: Brownies(Chocolate Indians)
directions: In top of double boiler, melt chocolate with margarine.
Add to sugar; stir well.
Add eggs, one at a time; beat well.
Add vanilla and flour; stir into chocolate mixture.
Add nuts.
Grease and flour a 16 x 7 1/2-inch pan.
Pour into pan.
Bake at 350° for 25 to 30 minutes.
(Do not overbake.)

_enrichment.description: Rich, fudgy brownies with a dense, chewy texture and a deep chocolate flavour.

title: Vegetable Curry
ingredients: 6 to 8 potatoes, small and cut up
directions: Place vegetables on a large plate.
Sprinkle with mixed spices and salt.
Melt butter in a heavy pan.
Saute the onions and garlic.
Add the spiced veggies and stock.
Cover and bring to a boil, simmer for about 20 minutes until all vegetables are tender. Serve with hot cooked rice.
Enjoy!

_enrichment.description: A vibrant vegetable curry with cauliflower, potatoes, and peas, spiced and simmered in a creamy sauce.

title: Bombay Tuna
ingredients: 6 1/2 oz. can tuna, packed in water and well drained
1/2 tsp. curry powder or to taste
directions: In medium bowl, combine tuna, apple, celery, green onion, almonds and currants.
Stir in mayonnaise, chutney, mustard and curry powder.
Refrigerate.
Great for summertime entertaining!

_enrichment.description: A refreshing tuna salad with apple, celery, and almonds, dressed in a tangy curry-mayonnaise sauce.

title: Dallas' Chocolate Chip
directions: Cream butter, vegetable shortening, sugar and brown sugar for 5 minutes.
Add next 9 ingredients.
Put teaspoon size clumps on cookie sheet.
Bake at 350° for 10 to 15 minutes.

_enrichment.description: Chewy oatmeal chocolate chip cookies with a buttery, spiced dough and a hint of lemon.

title: Icebox Relish
ingredients: 4 or 5 green and red bell peppers
a few hot peppers, cut in large pieces
directions: Cut cabbages, onions, tomatoes and green and red bell peppers finely.
Add salt and mix.
Let set for 30 minutes or longer. Squeeze dry with hands or in a bag.
Add remaining ingredients. Keep in refrigerator.
Keeps several weeks.

_enrichment.description: A tangy, crunchy relish with cabbage, tomatoes, and peppers, perfect for sandwiches or snacks.

title: Aunt E'S Curried Chicken Bites
directions: Cut chicken breasts into bite size pieces and place in pan with small amount of butter.
Cover with wax paper and place in 400 degree oven for 6 to 8 minutes. (Chicken is done if it springs back when pressed with finger.)
Combine with other ingredients. Refrigerate several hours or overnight before serving.

_enrichment.description: Tender chicken bites marinated in a creamy, tangy curry sauce with a hint of spice and fresh herbs.

title: Prepared Mustard
directions: Mix dry ingredients and add vinegar.
Make a paste.
Add rest of the vinegar and cook.
If too thick, add a little more vinegar and water to taste.
This can be doubled if needed.

_enrichment.description: A smooth, tangy mustard paste with a mild curry and turmeric kick, perfect for sandwiches or dressings.

title: Curry-Cajun Spiced Chicken
directions: In a 9 x 13-inch pan, combine honey, water, mustard, melted oleo, Cajun seasoning, curry powder, lemon juice and garlic; mix well.
Add chicken, turning to coat.
Arrange in single layer. Bake, uncovered, in a 350° oven for 30 minutes, or until chicken is tender and no longer pink.

_enrichment.description: Juicy chicken glazed with a sweet and spicy blend of honey, mustard, and Cajun-curry seasoning.

title: Quick Turkey Curry
ingredients: 1 (10 1/2 oz.) can condensed cream of mushroom soup
directions: Cook onion in butter or margarine.
Add soup and milk; heat and stir until smooth.
Stir in sour cream and curry powder.
Add turkey; heat, but do not boil.
Garnish with snipped parsley. Serve over hot cooked rice.
If desired, offer curry condiments of chutney, raisins and toasted slivered almonds.
Makes 4 servings.

_enrichment.description: A quick and creamy turkey curry with a rich, spiced sauce, best served over fluffy rice.

title: Mom'S Sugar Cookies(Canaan Elementary Favorite)
directions: Cream together sugar, shortening, vanilla, lemon extract and eggs.
Add sifted dry ingredients and mix well.
Bake at 350° for 10 to 12 minutes on a cookie sheet.

_enrichment.description: Classic buttery sugar cookies with a hint of vanilla and lemon, soft and chewy.

title: Tindall Goulash
directions: Brown hamburger.
Cook macaroni.
Grease casserole with butter. Layer 1/2 macaroni, Velveeta, hamburger, corn, tomatoes, other 1/2 of macaroni and top with Velveeta.
Bake at 350° for 45 minutes.

_enrichment.description: A comforting, cheesy goulash with tender macaroni, ground beef, and sweet corn.

title: Mock Indian Pudding
directions: Crumble Shredded Wheat into a greased baking dish.
Beat eggs, add milk.
Stir in molasses, salt and cinnamon.
Pour over biscuits.
Dot with butter.
Bake at 350° for 35 to 45 minutes. Serve plain or with whipped cream.
Ice cream is also good.

_enrichment.description: A traditional molasses-based pudding with a spiced, custard-like texture, served warm or with cream.

title: Almond Curry Fruit
directions: All fruits must be well drained.
Place drained fruits in layers in a baking dish; sprinkle with almonds.
Combine melted margarine, brown sugar and curry powder, mixing well; pour this mixture over fruit and bake at 325° for 1 hour.

_enrichment.description: A sweet and spiced fruit salad with almonds, caramelised with brown sugar and curry powder.

title: Candied Carrots
ingredients: approximately 6 to 8 average sized carrots, diced
directions: Combine first 8 ingredients in a small bowl; stir well.
Cook corn according to package directions.
Transfer corn to a serving platter and brush margarine mixture over corn.

_enrichment.description: Glazed carrots with a warm blend of spices, honey, and a touch of heat from cayenne.

title: Meat Curry
ingredients: 1 c. apple, cut in small cubes
3 c. meat, cut in bite size pieces
directions: Melt butter in saucepan and add onion, garlic, celery and apple.
Cook, stirring often, for about 5 minutes.
Blend flour, curry powder and mustard; sprinkle it over vegetables.
Stir.
Add bay leaf and chicken broth; rapidly stir with wire whisk.
When mixture has thickened, let simmer for 15 minutes.
Stir bottom often to prevent from sticking.
Add meat, milk and finely chopped chutney; simmer 4 to 5 minutes longer.
Serve with rice.

_enrichment.description: A rich, aromatic meat curry with tender chunks, apples, and a velvety sauce.

title: Mushroom Curry
ingredients: 4 tsp. fresh snipped chives or basil
directions: Rinse the mushrooms in cold water; drain well.
Cut in halves or if large in quarters.
Peel the potatoes.
Melt butter in saucepan and fry onions, herbs and ginger for about 5 minutes. Add the salt, turmeric and chili powder.
Fry for 2 more minutes, then add sliced tomatoes.
Mix thoroughly and cook for 30 minutes over medium heat.
If you want liquid, cook covered; if not, cook uncovered.
Add the lemon juice and curry powder and simmer gently for 10 minutes.
Remove from heat.
Serve hot.

_enrichment.description: A hearty mushroom and potato curry with a fragrant blend of spices and a tangy finish.

title: After-The-Holidays Salad
directions: In a mixing bowl toss first 6 ingredients.
In a small bowl, combine all remaining ingredients, except lettuce leaves.
Pour over turkey mixture and mix gently.
Chill.
Serve on a bed of lettuce if desired.
Yield:
4 servings.

_enrichment.description: A refreshing salad with leftover turkey, pineapple, grapes, and a creamy curry-chutney dressing.

title: Beans-N-Rice That Fill You Up
ingredients: 1 (15 oz.) can black beans, rinsed and drained
directions: In nonstick pan, heat oil; add 1 cup onion and 1/2 cup green pepper and cook until tender.
Add rice, cumin, red pepper and coriander.
Mix and heat 2 minutes.
Add beans; heat.
Add uncooked onion and tomato if desired.
Approximately 125 calories per 1/2 cup; less than 2 grams of fat.

_enrichment.description: A hearty, protein-packed rice and bean dish with a spiced tomato base.

title: Curry Appetizer
directions: Blend cream cheese and sour cream until smooth.
Add curry powder.
Blend well.
Add remaining ingredients, except chutney. Mix well.
Line a jello mold with plastic wrap and pour in mix. Refrigerate until set.
After it is unmolded, spread chutney on top.
Serve with water crackers.

_enrichment.description: A creamy, curried appetiser with bacon, peanuts, and raisins, set in a jello mould.

title: Chick 'N Bean Soup
ingredients: 1 (about 16 oz.) can whole tomatoes, undrained and coarsely chopped
2 whole chicken breasts (4 pieces), skinned, boned and cut into 3/4-inch pieces
1 (16 oz.) can garbanzo beans (chickpeas), rinsed and drained
1 c. dry small pasta shells or elbow macaroni
directions: Heat oil in large saucepan or Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add onion and garlic; cook and stir 3 minutes.
Add broth, tomatoes, carrots and picante sauce.
Bring to a boil; reduce heat.
Cover and simmer 15 minutes.

_enrichment.description: A chunky, spiced chicken and bean soup with tender vegetables and a tomato base.

title: Lyndal'S Chili
directions: Cook ground beef, onions and drain off grease.
Add tomato juice, tomato paste, kidney beans, salt, pepper, paprika and chili powder.
Simmer in pot for several hours.

_enrichment.description: A hearty American chilli with ground beef and kidney beans, slow-cooked for deep flavour. Perfect for a cosy family meal.

title: Vegetable Fish Curry(Serves 4 To 6)
ingredients: 1 lb. frozen fish fillets, cut in 1 1/2-inch cubes
2 medium tomatoes, cut in 1/2-inch cubes (2 c.)
1 large zucchini, cut in 1/2-inch cubes (2 c.)
directions: Heat 1 1/2 cups water and wine in large skillet over medium heat.
Add fish; cover and simmer 3 to 4 minutes or until fish flakes easily.
Drain, reserving 1 1/2 cups liquid.
Heat corn oil over medium heat in large skillet.
Add tomatoes, zucchini, onion and garlic.
Cook 5 minutes or until crisp-tender, stirring occasionally.
Stir in reserved liquid.

_enrichment.description: A light and fragrant fish curry with fresh vegetables, ideal for a summer evening. Served with fluffy rice.

title: Curry-Onion Dip
directions: Combine first 9 ingredients.
Cover and refrigerate 8 hours or overnight.
Serve in a pepper cup with vegetables or boiled shrimp. Garnish, if desired.
Yields 2 1/4 cups.

_enrichment.description: A creamy, tangy dip with a hint of curry, perfect for crudités or seafood. Best prepared ahead for maximum flavour.

title: Tanzania Chicken
directions: Cut up chicken and stew, keeping meat on bones.
Mix together all remaining ingredients.
Pour over chicken and simmer together. Thicken a bit with cornstarch.
Serve over rice.

_enrichment.description: A fragrant Tanzanian chicken stew with turmeric and curry spices, served over steamed rice.

title: Grandma Coy'S Curry Pickles
ingredients: 6 large or 24 small cucumbers, sliced (leave rind on)
directions: Wash cucumbers and drain.
Combine salt and water.
Pour over cucumbers.
Let stand 5 hours. Drain and rinse thoroughly.
Mix remaining ingredients.
Heat to boiling.
Pour over cucumbers. Heat to boiling and pack into sterile jars. Seal immediately!
(Do not have to use canner!)

_enrichment.description: Crunchy pickled cucumbers with a warm curry and celery flavour, a tangy side for cheese boards.

title: Dalena'S Shorty Annie
directions: Brown hamburger, pepper and onion.
Add all other ingredients and simmer for 30 minutes.
Serve with cornbread or crackers. Parmesan cheese added on top of individual bowls is good also.

_enrichment.description: A simple, savoury beef and tomato sauce with a touch of Parmesan, great with cornbread.

title: Indiana Style Corn Dogs
directions: In bowl mix cornmeal, flour, baking powder, salt, sugar, milk, egg, paprika, mustard and pepper.
Pour mixture into tall glass. Skewer hot dogs with wooden skewers; dip in mixture.
Deep fry at 375° until golden brown.

_enrichment.description: Crispy cornmeal-coated hot dogs, a classic American fairground treat.

title: Vidalia Onion Chips
directions: Separate each wedge into chips.
Place in ice water.
Beat together the milk and egg.
Remove chips from water; dip in flour, then egg mixture.
Dip in bread crumbs and seasoning.
Fry in hot oil until brown; drain.

_enrichment.description: Thin, crispy onion chips with a Cajun kick, perfect for sharing with drinks.

title: Sri Lankan Chicken Curry
ingredients: 1 c. flour with salt and pepper to taste
directions: Combine lemon juice with curry powder, red pepper and ginger; set aside.
Melt butter in stock pot.
Add all onions and garlic to saute.
Add lemon juice-spice mixture.
Saute until onions are soft.
(At any point, you can add more curry powder or red pepper.)

_enrichment.description: A rich Sri Lankan chicken curry with coconut milk and warming spices, served with rice.

title: Indian Bars(Brownies)
directions: Melt butter and chocolate.
Add sugar and eggs; mix thoroughly. Sift flour with baking powder.
Stir into creamed mixture.
Mix in vanilla and nuts.
Bake in greased and floured 9 x 13-inch pan at 350° for 25 to 30 minutes.
Check after 20 minutes.
Do not overcook!
Should be slightly gooey in the middle.

_enrichment.description: Fudgy chocolate brownies with a hint of vanilla and pecans, a classic American dessert.

title: Easy Apple Dumplings
directions: Peel, cut apples into quarters.
Roll out biscuits.
Cut each in half.
Put 1 quarter apple in each biscuit half.
Fold dough over apple, place in a buttered sheet pan or large casserole dish. Mix next 5 ingredients; pour over dumplings, cut butter over top. Bake in a 350° oven until apples are done.
Take from oven, baste and continue baking until golden brown.
May be served with Cool Whip.
Yields 8 to 10 portions.

_enrichment.description: Tender apple dumplings baked in flaky pastry, a comforting autumn dessert.

title: Egg Drop Soup
ingredients: 1 lb. lean pork, cut in strips
1 (8 oz.) can sliced water chestnuts, drained
directions: In a large saucepan or Dutch oven, heat oil over medium heat. Add onion, garlic, ginger and pork; cook and stir until tender. Stir in broth, soy sauce, pepper and sesame oil; bring to a boil. Add carrots; simmer 15 minutes.
Add all remaining ingredients except eggs; bring to a boil.
Reduce heat to low.
Stir in eggs with a fork to separate into strands.

_enrichment.description: A silky, comforting egg drop soup with pork, vegetables, and a hint of sesame.

title: Sweet And Sour Red Cabbage
ingredients: 1 head red cabbage, cut into small pieces
directions: In a pot of water with 1 tablespoon salt, boil shredded cabbage until just tender.
Cook it quickly, perhaps 10 minutes at most, to retain its Vitamin C.
Drain; stir in chopped onion that has been glaceed in a nonstick frypan.
Add salt, pepper, sugar and vinegar; stir at least two minutes until the vinegar permeates the cabbage, which will become limp.
Serve hot with Best Herbed Pork Roast.

_enrichment.description: Sweet and sour red cabbage with caraway, a tangy side dish for roasted meats.

title: Easy Chicken And Dumplings
ingredients: 1 chicken, cooked and cut into small pieces
directions: Layer chicken in bottom of casserole dish and pour melted butter or margarine over chicken.
Next, mix flour with milk and pour over chicken.
Mix soup and broth and pour over top of casserole and gently stir across top; smooth out.
Bake at 350° for 45 minutes or until golden brown and done in center.

_enrichment.description: A creamy, comforting chicken and dumplings casserole, perfect for cold evenings.

title: The Best Sweet And Sour
directions: Cook peppers and onions in oil until tender-crisp.
Blend syrup drained from pineapple with vinegar, sugar, water, catsup and cornstarch.
Add to vegetables and cook, stirring until sauce boils and thickens.
Add drained pineapple.
Serve over fried chicken or leftover chunked pork that has been fried or fried tofu chunks.
Serves 6 to 8.

_enrichment.description: A classic sweet and sour sauce with pineapple and peppers, great with fried chicken or pork.

title: Sesame Chicken Wings
ingredients: freshly ground black pepper to taste
2 lb. chicken wings, wing tips cut off
directions: In a bowl, whisk together the soy sauce, the garlic, the ginger, the honey and the pepper.
Add the wings; toss them to coat and let them marinate, covered and chilled, for 2 hours. Line a baking pan with foil and arrange the wings, drained (reserving the marinade) in one layer on the foil.
Brush the wings with the
oil; sprinkle them with the sesame seed and bake them in the middle of a preheated 400° oven, basting them occasionally with the reserved marinade, for 30 to 35 minutes or until they are browned.
Serves 4 as an hors d'oeuvre.

_enrichment.description: Crispy chicken wings coated in a sweet and savoury sesame glaze, baked to perfection and garnished with toasted sesame seeds.

title: Oriental Rice
ingredients: 1 (6 oz.) pkg. long grain and wild rice
directions: Place rice, seasoning packet, butter, water chestnuts, mushrooms, onion and soy sauce in a 3-quart casserole.
Microwave on High (100%) for 3 to 4 minutes or until onion is tender.
Stir halfway through cooking time.
Pour in chicken broth.
Cover with a tight fitting lid or plastic wrap.
Microwave on High for 5 to 7 minutes or until boiling.
Stir, then microwave on Medium (50%) for 40 to 45 minutes or until rice is tender and liquid is absorbed. Yield:
6 to 8 servings.

_enrichment.description: Quick and easy microwave rice dish with water chestnuts, mushrooms, and soy sauce for a light, savoury side.

title: Beef, Beans And Dumplings
ingredients: 1 (15 1/2 oz.) jar spaghetti sauce with mushrooms
directions: In casserole dish, crumble beef and sausage; add onion and garlic.
Cook, covered, on High for 4 to 6 minutes in microwave. Drain fat.
Stir in sauce, undrained beans, water and pepper. Cook, covered, on High 7 to 9 minutes or until thoroughly heated, stirring once.
Arrange biscuits around outer edges, leaving center open.
Sprinkle the center with cheese and parsley.
Cook, uncovered, on High 2 to 3 minutes or until biscuits are done, turning at 1 1/2 minutes.

_enrichment.description: Hearty casserole with ground beef, sausage, and pinto beans, topped with fluffy biscuits and melted cheese.

title: Mike'S Fried Rice
ingredients: 1 to 2 c. leftover meat (barbecue chicken, pork chops, ham, etc.)
directions: Heat wok or large frying pan on high.
Add oil, then chopped green onions, including most of green parts.
Stir-fry for 1 minute.
Add leftover meat that has been cubed; stir-fry another minute.
Add cooked mixed vegetables; season to taste with garlic powder and ginger.
Stir-fry 1 minute more, then add rice and mix well.
Add scrambled eggs which have been mashed well with a fork. Sprinkle with soy sauce to taste; stir-fry for 2 more minutes. Enjoy.

_enrichment.description: Classic fried rice stir-fried with green onions, leftover meat, and eggs for a quick and satisfying meal.

title: Chinese Pepper Steak(Crockery Cooking)
ingredients: 1 c. fresh or canned drained bean sprouts
2 green peppers, cut in thin strips
1 Tbsp. cornstarch with 2 Tbsp. cold water
directions: Slice steak diagonally (against the grain) in narrow strips. In skillet, brown steak in smoking hot oil.
Combine with garlic, salt, pepper and sugar in crock-pot.
Cook on low for 6 to 9 hours.
Turn control to High.
Add bean sprouts, tomatoes and green peppers.
Dissolve cornstarch in water.
Stir into pot, cover and cook on high 20 minutes until thickened and clear. Sprinkle with onion.
Serve hot with rice or chow mein noodles.

_enrichment.description: Slow-cooked pepper steak with tender beef strips, garlic, and green peppers in a savoury sauce.

title: Hot Dogs With Fried Rice And Tomatoes
directions: Melt butter or bacon fat.
Add rice and saute until yellow. Add onion and cook 2 minutes.
Add tomatoes, seasonings and herbs. Cover and cook 15 to 20 minutes until rice is tender.
Arrange hot grilled hot dogs on hot platter and pour rice and tomato mixture around them.
Yields 6 servings.

_enrichment.description: Comforting dish of fried rice with tomatoes, onions, and spices, served alongside grilled hot dogs.

title: Lite Oriental Chicken
ingredients: 1 (8 oz.) can water chestnuts, rinsed and drained
2 whole chicken breasts, skinned and boned
directions: Mix together 1/2 cup broth, soy sauce, sherry and cornstarch. Set aside; cut chicken into 1-inch cubes.
Heat 3 tablespoons chicken broth in large pan or wok and brown chicken quickly. Remove from pan and keep warm.
Add garlic, mushrooms and snow peas; stir 2 minutes until mushrooms are golden brown.
Return chicken to pan and add water chestnuts and bamboo shoots.
Pour well mixed sauce over all and cook 2 to 3 minutes until chicken is done and vegetables are crisp and tender.

_enrichment.description: Light and flavourful chicken stir-fry with bamboo shoots, snow peas, and mushrooms in a tangy sauce.

title: Aunt Ethel'S Old Fashioned Chicken And Dumplings
directions: Stir flour, eggs and oil together.
Then add a little ice water at a time, until mixture forms a ball.
Sprinkle ball with flour for easy handling and divide into 3 balls.
Roll each ball out on well-floured board, real thin.
Cut into 2-inch squares and drop in simmering broth where chicken was cooked.
Should have at least 2 quarts of broth.
Repeat with each ball, rolling thin, cutting into squares and dropping in broth.
Stir well and simmer about 10 minutes.
Add cut-up chicken.
If broth seems too thin, thicken with a little flour dissolved in water.

_enrichment.description: Traditional chicken and dumplings with tender chicken, vegetables, and fluffy homemade dumplings.

title: Chinese Barbecued Pork
directions: Cut pork into long strips about 2 inches wide and 2 inches thick.
Soak the first six ingredients for half a day or overnight.
Rub the meat (at room temperature) with garlic, sugar and honey.
Heat oven at 400°.
Roast the pork for 35 minutes, turning strips over once during roasting process.
Remove pork from oven and slice.

_enrichment.description: Sweet and sticky Chinese barbecued pork with a caramelised glaze, perfect for a festive meal.

title: Marinated Tofu Salad
ingredients: 1 1/2 lb. tofu, parboiled, drained and dried
1 head Chinese cabbage or equivalent, shredded
directions: In a shallow pan combine oils, lemon juice, shoyu and vinegar.
Combine with a wire whisk.
Cut tofu in half horizontally, then diagonally to form thin triangles about 1/4-inch thick.
Place into the bowl with the marinade and chill for at least an hour.
Arrange the cabbage on center of the plates.
Top with tofu.
Arrange the watercress around the edges. Place some shredded carrots in the center, with the scallions. Pour the marinade over all with a sprinkle of gomahsio.

_enrichment.description: Refreshing salad with marinated tofu, crisp cabbage, and carrots, drizzled with a tangy dressing.

title: Sweet And Sour Cashew Chicken
ingredients: 1/3 c. green bell pepper, cut into strips
1/3 c. red bell pepper, cut into strips
4 boneless, skinless chicken breast halves, cut into small chunks
1 (12 oz.) jar pineapple ice cream topping (about 1 c.)
directions: Saute the peppers in the butter and oil until tender but not mushy. Remove from pan and set aside.
Add the chicken to the skillet.
If needed, add a little more butter and oil. Cook until the chicken is lightly browned and tender.
Add peppers back to skillet along with the ice cream topping, sugar, ginger, and vinegar.
Stir to mix well.
Combine the soy sauce and cornstarch in a small bowl and stir until blended.
Add to skillet, mixing well.
Serve over rice and sprinkle the toasted cashews over the top. Makes 4 servings.

_enrichment.description: Sweet and sour chicken stir-fry with bell peppers, pineapple, and cashews for a vibrant, tangy dish.

title: Sweet And Sour Eggplant
directions: In a large frying pan over medium heat, warm oil.
Add onion and eggplant and saut about 10 minutes, stirring occasionally. Add bay leaves, raisins and vinegar.
Cover, reduce heat and and cook until vinegar evaporates, about 10 minutes. Add water a little at a time and continue cooking for about 30 minutes. Remove bay leaves.
Add the diced tomato and season to taste with salt and pepper.
Serves 6 for a side dish.

_enrichment.description: Sweet and sour eggplant with raisins, tomatoes, and a tangy vinegar sauce for a rich side dish.

title: Chicken Or Beef Hekka
ingredients: chicken, cut up in 1 x 1-inch pieces
beef, cut up in thin 2-inch long slices
directions: Cook chicken and beef in soy sauce until brown.
Add flour and water to cover meat.
Add ginger and garlic cloves to your taste. Before adding watercress, take garlic and ginger out.
Add sugar to your taste.
If you like bamboo shoots, add some sliced pieces. Serve over rice.

_enrichment.description: Hearty hot pot with chicken, beef, watercress, and bamboo shoots in a savoury broth, served over rice.

title: Sweet And Sour Stir-Fried Beef
ingredients: 1 medium green pepper, cut in 1-inch pieces
directions: Trim excess fat from beef; cut beef with grain into 2-inch strips.
Cut strips across grain into 1/8-inch slices.
Mix cornstarch and 1 tablespoon water.
Heat oil in 12-inch skillet or wok over medium-high heat until hot.
Add beef and onion; cook and stir until beef is brown, about 3 minutes.
Stir in pineapple, 1/2 cup water, the sugar, vinegar, instant bouillon and soy sauce. Heat to boiling.
Stir in cornstarch mixture.
Cook and stir 1 minute. Stir in green pepper and tomatoes; cook and stir 1 minute. Serve with hot, cooked rice.
Makes 5 servings.

_enrichment.description: Stir-fried beef with pineapple, tomatoes, and a sweet and sour sauce for a vibrant, tangy dish.

title: Oriental Celery And Peas
ingredients: 1 can (8 1/2 oz.) water chestnuts, sliced and drained
directions: Bring water to boiling in large saucepan; add celery and peas. Cover; cook 5 minutes or until celery is almost tender.
Drain and leave vegetables in saucepan.
Add salt, pepper, soy sauce, wine and water chestnuts to vegetables in saucepan.
Toss together.
Cook 2 minutes longer.

_enrichment.description: Light and crunchy stir-fry of celery, peas, and water chestnuts in a savoury soy and wine sauce.

title: Shrimp Fried Rice
directions: Combine shrimp, green onion, cornstarch, 1 teaspoon salt and pepper in a large bowl. Toss to mix.
Stir-fry shrimp mixture in hot oil in wok for 3 to 5 minutes or until shrimp turn pink. Remove from wok. Stir rice, eggs and 1 1/2 teaspoons salt together in a bowl.
Pour into hot wok.
Stir-fry over medium-high heat for 2 minutes or until eggs are cooked. Add shrimp mixture. Stir-fry until mixed.

_enrichment.description: A quick and flavourful Chinese-inspired fried rice with succulent shrimp, eggs, and spring onions, perfect for a weeknight meal.

title: Sausage Stir-Fry
ingredients: 4 to 5 pieces cooked link sausage (about 1 lb.), sliced in rings
2 c. weak beef broth (made with bouillon cubes)
directions: Heat a wok or large pot until hot.
Add a small amount of oil to coat bottom.
When oil is hot, add cabbage, onion and celery and stir-fry until it is about half done.
Add sausage rings and continue stirring over high heat until sausage is heated through. Add V-8 juice, beef broth, soy sauce and pepper.
Cook until heated.
Thicken with cornstarch and a little of the reserved beef broth.
Heat through.
Add sesame oil just before serving.
Serve over hot, fluffy rice.

_enrichment.description: A hearty stir-fry featuring sausage, cabbage, and celery in a savoury broth, thickened for a comforting dish.

title: Mrs. Tran'S Fried Rice
ingredients: 1 small onion, peeled and finely chopped
directions: Scramble eggs in butter and set aside.
In wok or large frypan over medium heat, saute onion in oil until transparent.
Add vegetables, and cook, stirring for 3 minutes.
Add sugar, fish sauce and soy sauce and mix well.
Add rice and cook about 5 minutes, stirring frequently until all food is hot.
Chop up scrambled eggs; mix in and serve.

_enrichment.description: A classic fried rice recipe with eggs, peas, and carrots, seasoned with fish sauce and soy for an authentic taste.

title: Sweet And Sour Meatballs
ingredients: seasonings to taste: salt and pepper, garlic powder, parsley
directions: Mix ground beef, egg, seasonings and bread crumbs with 1/3 can of tomato sauce.
Add more sauce and/or breadcrumbs until mixture is moist and well mixed.
Place remaining tomato sauce in medium saucepan with sugar and lemon juice.
Mix and place over medium-high heat.

_enrichment.description: Juicy meatballs in a tangy sweet and sour tomato sauce, a simple yet delicious dish for a cosy dinner.

title: Japanese Fried Rice
ingredients: 1 Cup uncooked long grain converted or parboiled rice (not instant or quick white rice)
directions: Cook the rice following the instructions on the package. This should take about 20 minutes.
Pour the rice into a large bowl to let it cool.
Scramble the eggs in a small pan over medium heat.
chop scrambled chunks of egg into small pea-sized bits with your spatula while cooking.
When the rice is cooled, add the peas, carrots, eggs and onion to the bowl. Carefully toss all of the ingredients together. Melt the butter in a large skillet over medium/high heat.
When the butter has completely melted, dump the rice mixture into the pan and add the soy sauce plus a dash of salt and pepper.
Cook the rice mixture for 6 to 8 minutes, stirring often.

_enrichment.description: A Japanese-style fried rice with eggs, peas, and carrots, seasoned with soy sauce for a light yet satisfying meal.

title: Oriental Omelet(From Joslin Diabetic Clinic)
