directions: Combine chicken, bean sprouts, water chestnuts and soy sauce and mix well.
Set aside.
Mix eggs, water, green pepper, onion, salt and pepper with a fork.
Heat butter or margarine in a 10-inch skillet.
Pour in egg mixture.

With spatula, draw cooked portions of egg toward center so that uncooked portions flow to bottom. Cook until bottom is set and lightly browned and top is creamy. Arrange chicken mixture on half of omelet.
With spatula, fold other half over and turn out on serving platter.
Yields 2 servings.
Each serving equals 3 ounces of meat and 1 vegetable.

_enrichment.description: A fluffy omelette packed with chicken, bean sprouts, and water chestnuts, offering a protein-rich breakfast or brunch option.

title: Sweet And Sour Tofu And Vegies
ingredients: 1/3 can (2/3 c.) pineapple chunks with juice
1/2 Tbsp. malt or molasses or 1 tsp. honey
1 large carrot, sliced thinly or chopped small
directions: Dissolve cornstarch in 2 tablespoons of water; add 1/4 cup pineapple juice, 1/4 cup water, vinegar, honey, soy sauce, ginger, and pepper; set aside.
Heat oil in a wok or large skillet on high heat.
Add onion and stir until strong smell starts to go away. Add carrot and stir a little more.
Add tofu and stir until lightly browned.
Add green pepper and salt.
Stir a minute more, then reduce heat to medium and cook, uncovered.
Colorful, rich and juicy with a Chinese flavor.
A meal in itself with rice or chow mein noodles.

_enrichment.description: A vibrant sweet and sour stir-fry with tofu and mixed vegetables, offering a balance of tangy and savoury flavours.

title: Beef Stir-Fry With Mushrooms
ingredients: 5 green onions, bias-sliced into 1-inch lengths (1 c.)
directions: Partially freeze beef; cut on the bias into thin bite-size strips.
For marinade, in a medium mixing bowl, stir together water, soy sauce, wine, cornstarch and molasses.
Add beef, stirring to coat well.
Cover and marinate at room temperature for 30 minutes or in the refrigerator for 2 hours, stirring occasionally.
Drain well, reserving marinade.
Set aside.

_enrichment.description: Tender beef strips stir-fried with mushrooms and green onions in a rich soy-based sauce, served over rice.

title: Chinese Pepper Steak
directions: Slice steak in narrow strips.
Brown steak in 2 tablespoons oil.
Combine with garlic, salt, pepper, soy sauce and sugar for 6 to 8 hours in crock-pot on low.
Turn to high and add vegetables. Dissolve 1 tablespoon cornstarch in 2 tablespoons cold water. Stir into pot.
Cook on high 15 to 20 minutes or until thickened. Serve over noodles or rice.

_enrichment.description: A classic Chinese pepper steak with tender beef, crisp vegetables, and a glossy sweet and sour sauce.

title: Hot And Sour Soup
ingredients: 1 lb. boneless, skinless chicken breast, cut in 1-inch cubes
directions: In a 5-quart pot, heat oil.
Add chicken and saute until meat is not pink.
Add water, mushrooms, cabbage, bamboo shoots and bouillon cubes.
Bring to boil.
Reduce heat and simmer 1/2 hour. In small bowl, dissolve cornstarch and stir with fork until smooth.
Stir into soup.
Add vinegar, soy sauce, salt and pepper. Stir soup until slightly thickened and enjoy.
Unused soup can be frozen for a quick meal anytime.

_enrichment.description: A comforting hot and sour soup with mushrooms, chicken, and bamboo shoots, offering a tangy and spicy broth.

title: Chicken 'N Dumplings
directions: Boil fryer until tender. Remove from broth and set aside to cool. Mix dry ingredients.
Cut in shortening.
Add beaten eggs and enough milk to make a stiff dough. Roll out really thin on heavily floured board.
Cut into pieces (pizza cutter works great!).
Drop into rapidly boiling broth.
Cover and simmer on very low heat for 5 to 10 minutes. Remove skin and bones from chicken and pull into bite size pieces.
Add chicken to pot and season to taste with black pepper.
I pour about a cup of sweet milk in pot just as it's finished cooking.

_enrichment.description: A classic American comfort dish of tender chicken in a creamy broth with fluffy dumplings, perfect for a cosy meal.

title: Oriental Brussels Sprouts
ingredients: 1 lb. fresh or frozen Brussels sprouts
directions: Cook bacon until crisp.
Remove from pan and drain on paper towel.
Stir soy sauce into the bacon drippings.
Add chestnuts, garlic and broth.
Bring to a boil and add Brussels sprouts. Return to a boil; cover and reduce heat, cooking about 8 minutes or until sprouts are just tender.
Combine cornstarch and water and add to the pan liquid.
Cook, stirring, until thickened. Sprinkle reserved bacon over the top to serve.
Serves 6 to 8.

_enrichment.description: Crispy bacon and Brussels sprouts stir-fried with water chestnuts in a savoury soy-based sauce.

title: Meat Loaf With Sweet And Sour Sauce
directions: In a saucepan, mix tomato sauce with brown sugar, vinegar and mustard.
Stir until sugar is dissolved.
Combine egg, onion, crackers, meat, salt, pepper and 1/2 cup of the tomato sauce mixture.
Mix lightly but thoroughly and shape into loaf.
Put into baking dish and pour remainder of tomato sauce over mixture over it.
Bake in 350° oven for 1 hour, basting occasionally. Note:
This recipe may be cut in half and it works fine.

_enrichment.description: A classic meatloaf with a tangy sweet and sour tomato glaze, a hearty dish for a family dinner.

title: Chinese Beef-N-Rice
directions: In large skillet cook and stir rice in oil over medium heat until golden brown.
Add salt, water, bouillon cubes and soy sauce. Cover tightly; simmer 20 minutes.
Stir in onions, celery, green peppers and meat.
Cover tightly; simmer 10 minutes or until liquid is absorbed and rice tender.

_enrichment.description: A one-pot dish of beef and rice with onions, celery, and peppers, offering a hearty and flavourful meal.

title: Kyoto Stir-Fry(Serves 6.)
ingredients: 1 (16 oz.) can Great Northern beans, drained and rinsed or 2 c. pre-cooked beans
1 1/2 c. broccoli, cut into 1-inch pieces (fresh or frozen)
directions: Place thinly sliced beef into wok or large skillet; add cooking oil and brown over medium heat.
Add all vegetables and stir constantly; cook until all vegetables are tender.
Remove from heat.
In small saucepan, combine cooking sherry and cornstarch over low heat.
Stir until smooth.
Stir in soy sauce and all spices.
Add sauce to the meat and vegetable mixture over medium to high heat and simmer 5 minutes.
Serve immediately.

_enrichment.description: A vibrant stir-fry with sirloin, beans, and mixed vegetables in a savoury soy-based sauce, served over rice.

title: Oriental Cocktail Kabobs
ingredients: 1 lb. pkg. brown and serve sausages
directions: Cook sausages according to package directions and cut into thirds.
Alternate pieces of pineapple, sausage, water chestnuts, green pepper and mushrooms on long toothpicks.
Combine remaining ingredients; heat skillet.
Add kabobs, cover and simmer 10 minutes.
Remove from skillet; serve warm as a dinner or appetizer.
Yields 40 to 50.

_enrichment.description: A playful and colourful appetiser featuring skewered sausages, pineapple, and vegetables in a sweet-savoury soy-ginger glaze.

title: Poor Man'S Chow Mein
directions: Brown beef, onions and celery (I usually begin beef in a small amount of water and after awhile, pour away drippings).
To the beef, add onions and celery; continue browning.
Mix in casserole dish the soups, water, rice and soy sauce.
Add the browned beef mixture and stir.
Bake for 1 hour at 350°.
Place topping of dry chow mein noodles on each serving.

_enrichment.description: A hearty, budget-friendly casserole of ground beef, rice, and celery in a creamy soup-based sauce, topped with crispy noodles.

title: Mongolian Beef(Spicy)
ingredients: 2 to 3 green onions, sliced into 2-inch diagonal slices
directions: Deep fry beef in oil.
In wok, stir-fry beef, red peppers and onion in sesame oil. Add approximately 2 tablespoons each of soy sauce, oyster sauce and wine sauce.
(All can be found in any Asian grocery store.)
Season with black pepper and pinch of sugar.
Stir-fry quickly to blend flavors.
Place crisp fried rice noodles on serving dish.
Cover with beef mixture and serve.

_enrichment.description: Crispy fried beef stir-fried with peppers and onions in a glossy, spicy-sweet sauce, served over rice noodles.

title: Zelma'S Never Fail Dumplings
directions: Beat eggs and shortening.
Add dry ingredients and enough milk to make a stiff dough.
Knead dough on floured surface.
Roll out very thin, cut in strips and drop into boiling broth with a teaspoon of Accent seasoning. When dumplings are almost done, add a few drops yellow food coloring and 1 cup of cream.

_enrichment.description: Light, fluffy dumplings dropped into boiling broth for a comforting, no-fail addition to soups or stews.

title: Basic Stir-Fry
ingredients: 10 oz. boneless, skinless chicken breasts, cut in strips
1/2 c. each carrots and celery, diagonally sliced
1/2 c. green bell peppers, sliced in strips
directions: In glass bowl, combine chicken, soy sauce and garlic powder; stir well and set aside.
Heat 2 teaspoons oil in skillet or wok. Stir in all vegetables and stir-fry 3 to 5 minutes until onions are translucent.
Remove to bowl.
Heat remaining 2 teaspoons oil. Add chicken and stir-fry until chicken is cooked, about 5 minutes.
Return vegetables to skillet or wok.
Stir in cornstarch mixture and continue cooking until sauce thickens.
Divide mixture evenly.
Makes 4 servings.

_enrichment.description: A quick and vibrant stir-fry of chicken and mixed vegetables in a glossy soy-garlic sauce, perfect for a weeknight meal.

title: Fresh Tofu Loaf
directions: Beat eggs slightly.
Add mushroom soup; beat and blend with milk and soy sauce.
Mash tofu coarsely, and combine all ingredients; mix well.
Bake in oiled loaf pan, set in a pan of hot water, at 350° about 45 minutes, or until a knife inserted in the center comes out clean.
Let stand 10 minutes before unmolding.

_enrichment.description: A savoury tofu loaf with mushrooms, water chestnuts, and wholemeal breadcrumbs, baked in a water bath for a moist texture.

title: Cabbage Stir-Fry
ingredients: 1/2 c. chopped red or green bell pepper
directions: Heat oil in wok or large skillet until hot.
Add cabbage, onion, pepper, garlic powder, sugar, salt and pepper.
Stir-fry until cabbage is just tender and light green, about 3 minutes. Add water and cover; remove from heat.
Let stand 3 to 5 minutes. Yields 4 to 6 servings.

_enrichment.description: A simple, quick stir-fry of cabbage and peppers with a touch of garlic and sugar, finished with a splash of water for tenderness.

title: Beef Casserole(Chinese Style)
ingredients: 1 c. crushed potato chips or corn flakes
directions: Cook beef until brown and crumbly.
Turn into a 1 1/2-quart casserole.
Arrange thawed peas over meat, then cover with celery. Mix together soup, cream, seasonings and onion.
Pour over celery. Sprinkle with chips or flakes.
Bake at 375° for 30 minutes or until hot and bubbly.

_enrichment.description: A comforting casserole of ground beef, peas, and celery in a creamy mushroom sauce, topped with crispy potato chips.

title: Chinese Vegetables
directions: In a skillet or wok, cook diagonally sliced celery, bean sprouts and onion in oil until tender.
Add remaining ingredients, except rice.
Cook over low heat, stirring until thickened.
Serve over rice with additional soy sauce.

_enrichment.description: A colourful medley of stir-fried vegetables in a light soy-cornstarch sauce, served over steamed rice.

title: Great-Great Granny Delia'S Chicken And Dumplings
directions: Season chicken and brown in shortening.
Cover with water and cook until tender.
While the hen is cooking, prepare the dumplings.
Mix dry ingredients together.
Add eggs and stock. Make into dough stiff enough to roll out thin and cut in narrow strips.
Drop dumplings into boiling pan of chicken and stock. Cover and simmer for about 20 minutes.

_enrichment.description: Classic Southern-style chicken and fluffy dumplings simmered in a rich broth for a heartwarming, nostalgic dish.

title: Stir-Fry Shrimp For Two
directions: Heat a small wok or skillet over medium-high heat and add cooking oil.
Add shrimp; toss and fry for 30 seconds.
Add scallops; stir and cook another 10 seconds.
With a slotted spoon, remove seafood to a plate.
Add to wok the broccoli and carrots and fry for 30 seconds; add bell pepper, snow peas and tomato and cook while tossing for an additional 30 seconds. Return seafood to wok.
Add sauce mixture and quickly toss until thickened.
Add sesame seed, if desired. Serve over rice.

_enrichment.description: Elegant stir-fried shrimp and scallops with crisp vegetables in a glossy soy-sesame sauce, served over rice.

title: Sweet And Sour Pork
ingredients: 3/4 to 1 c. pineapple juice from pineapple
directions: Brown pork in oil in large skillet.
Combine water, vinegar, brown sugar, cornstarch, salt, soy sauce and juice from pineapple. Cook until clear and slightly thickened, about 2 minutes.
Add sauce to pork in skillet and cook over low heat for 1 hour or until pork is done.
Add pineapple chunks, green peppers and onion.
Continue cooking 2 minutes longer.

_enrichment.description: Crispy pork pieces coated in a tangy, sweet-sour sauce with pineapple, peppers, and onions for a vibrant main course.

title: Chicken With Hoisin Sauce
ingredients: 1 medium green pepper, cut in pieces
directions: Cut chicken into 1/2-inch strips and then 1/2-inch squares. Place in bowl with cornstarch and toss.
Pour in sherry, soy and toss.
Heat wok; pour in 1 tablespoon oil and stir-fry peppers, water chestnuts, mushrooms and salt for 2 to 3 minutes.
Remove, set aside and add remaining 3 tablespoons oil and stir-fry chicken.
Add hoisin sauce and stir well with chicken.
Add vegetables back in and cook 1 minute.
Add nuts and stir to heat through.
Serve hot.

_enrichment.description: Tender chicken stir-fried with peppers, mushrooms, and water chestnuts in a glossy hoisin sauce, finished with cashews.

title: Midwestern Stir-Fry
ingredients: 1/2 lb. smoked sausage, cut into 1/4-inch slices and halved
1/2 c. green or red pepper strips
directions: Mix together salad dressing, mustard and milk until well blended; set aside.
Combine sausage, peppers and onions in a medium bowl.
Microwave on High for 2 minutes.
Stir in squash. Continue microwaving on High for 2 minutes or until vegetables are crisp-tender.
Add salad dressing mixture; mix lightly.
Microwave on Medium (50%) for 2 to 3 minutes or until thoroughly heated, stirring after 2 minutes (do not overcook).
Serve over rice. Makes 4 servings.

_enrichment.description: A Midwestern-inspired mix of sausage, peppers, and squash in a creamy dressing, served over rice for a hearty side dish.

title: Ame'S Potluck Rice
directions: In very large container (roaster pan), heat oil; add meat, stir-fry until done and push meat to one side of pan.
Add celery; stir until clear.
Push aside with meat.
Add onion; cook until golden and push aside with meat mixture.
Add carrots, stir carefully all ingredients together.
Add rice and stir carefully with wooden spoon.
Add water chestnuts, peppers and green onion. Mix carefully.
Add soy sauce mix carefully with wooden spoon and cover until ready to eat.

_enrichment.description: A colourful rice dish stir-fried with meat and vegetables, finished with soy sauce and water chestnuts for a satisfying one-pot meal.

title: Vegetable Stir-Fry(Time:  About 45 Minutes Before Serving)
directions: Cut carrots and celery into 3 inch long matchstick thin strips.
Slice onion thin.
Cut broccoli into 2 x 1/2-inch pieces. Cut mushrooms into quarters or cut in half if small.
In 5-quart Dutch oven or 12-inch skillet over high heat, in hot salad oil, cook carrots, celery, onion and broccoli, stirring quickly and frequently, about 3 or 4 minutes.
Add mushrooms, water, salt and sugar; cover and cook 5 or 6 minutes longer, stirring occasionally, until vegetables are tender-crisp.
Contains 95 calories per serving.
Yield: 8 servings.

_enrichment.description: A quick and healthy vegetable stir-fry with tender-crisp broccoli, mushrooms, and carrots, lightly seasoned with salt and sugar for a simple yet flavourful dish.

title: Chinese Pepper Steak Coca-Cola
ingredients: 1 1/2 lb. top round or sirloin steak (boneless)
1 c. beef broth (bouillon, canned and undiluted)
1 c. green bell pepper, cored, seeded and cut into thin strips
directions: Trim all fat from the meat and cut into pencil-thin strips. In a deep skillet or Dutch oven, heat oil, garlic and salt.
Add the meat and brown over high heat, about 10 minutes, stirring occasionally with a fork.
Add the beef broth.
Cover and simmer for 15 to 20 minutes or until the meat is fork-tender.
Stir in the green pepper strips, celery, onion and 1/2 cup of Coca-Cola. Cover and simmer for 5 minutes.
Do not overcook; the vegetables should be tender-crisp.
Peel the tomatoes; cut into wedges and gently stir into meat.

_enrichment.description: A classic Chinese-American dish featuring tender strips of beef cooked in a sweet and tangy Coca-Cola sauce with crisp vegetables like bell peppers and celery.

title: Healthy Sweet And Sour Chicken
directions: Cut chicken into 1 inch cubes.
Marinate for 45 minutes in soy sauce, sesame oil and ginger.
Mix ketchup, water, vinegar and sugar and set aside.
Put marinated chicken and cornstarch in Ziploc bag and shake until well coated.
Preheat skillet or wok, add 1 tablespoon vegetable oil and add chicken breasts a few at a time.
Brown chicken, then remove from skillet.
Put in 1 teaspoon vetetable oil in skillet and add vegetables, stir constantly and cook for 5 mintues.
Pour sweet and sour sauce over vegetables; bring to boil, add chicken and cook 10 minutes to thicken.
Serve with rice. 6 to 8 servings.

_enrichment.description: A lighter take on sweet and sour chicken with marinated breast pieces, crisp vegetables, and a glossy sauce made from ketchup, vinegar, and sugar.

title: "Stir-Fry Vegetables"
ingredients: 1/2 medium bell pepper, sliced in rings or strips
1/2 medium brown onion, sliced in rings or strips
directions: In large skillet, heat oil over medium heat.
Add all vegetables, except onion, mushrooms and bell pepper.
Cook until vegetables are almost to the desired tenderness, then add the onion, mushrooms and bell pepper, adding as much soy sauce as desired to taste.
Cook up to 1/2 hour or until done to taste. Serve with rice, chow mein noodles or both.

_enrichment.description: A simple stir-fry of seasonal vegetables like cauliflower, broccoli, and carrots, lightly cooked in olive oil and served with rice or noodles.

title: Chicken Cashew
ingredients: 2 chicken breast, boned and cut into pieces
directions: Heat canola oil in skillet.
Place chicken breast in skillet and sprinkle with 1/2 tsp. salt. Cook 5 to 10 minutes, stirring frequently.
Remove from skillet. Put onions, celery and 1/2 cup chicken broth in skillet. Cook uncovered 5 minutes or until slightly tender.
Combine sugar, cornstarch, soy sauce, cooking sherry and remaining chicken broth.
Pour over the vegetables and stir until the sauce thickens. Add cooked chicken, bamboo shoots, water chestnuts and cashews. Stir together and heat through. Serve with rice.
Serves 4 to 6.

_enrichment.description: A rich and nutty stir-fry with tender chicken, crunchy vegetables, and a glossy sauce, finished with cashews for added texture and flavour.

title: Sweet And Sour Asparagus
directions: Drain asparagus.
Combine rest of ingredients, except asparagus, and cook over medium heat for about 45 minutes. Remove cinnamon stick and cloves.
Pour the liquid over asparagus.
Cover and refrigerate immediately. Let marinate at least overnight.

_enrichment.description: A sweet and tangy marinated asparagus dish with a spiced vinegar syrup, perfect as a chilled side or appetiser.

title: Deluxe Pork Chop Suey
ingredients: 3 c. or 1/2 lb. fresh bean sprouts
1/2 c. sliced canned mushrooms or 1 c. fresh, sliced
directions: Marinate strips of pork and onion in soya sauce for 20 minutes. Dissolve cornstarch in water.
Heat oil until it begins to smoke in a wok or skillet.
Add pork and onion and stir-fry until pork is browned. Cover and cook 3 more minutes until thoroughly cooked.
Uncover and add bean sprouts and celery. Stir and cook 2 minutes. Add rest of ingredients and stir-fry thoroughly.
Cover and cook 3 more minutes.
Uncover and add cornstarch solution, stirring thoroughly, before adding to the wok.
Shut heat off and stir all food together. Serve over rice. Serves 4 to 6.

_enrichment.description: A deluxe version of pork chop suey with marinated pork, crisp vegetables, and a savoury sauce, served over rice.

title: Stir-Fry Cashew Chicken
ingredients: 4 chicken breasts, boneless, skinless and sliced
directions: Stir together soy sauce, sugar, cornstarch and chicken broth. In wok or frypan, stir-fry chicken for about 3 minutes.
Add garlic, green onions, green pepper and mushrooms and continue to stir-fry for about 2 minutes.
Lower heat and stir in soy sauce mixture.
Cook until sauce thickens.
Serve hot over cooked rice. Sprinkle with cashew nuts.

_enrichment.description: A quick and flavourful stir-fry of chicken, vegetables, and cashews, finished with a glossy soy-based sauce and served over rice.

title: Sweet 'N Sour Stir-Fry
ingredients: 1 small green bell pepper, cut into chunks
3/4 c. sweet and sour French dressing
directions: In medium skillet, heat oil and cook carrots, snow peas and green bell pepper over medium heat, stirring frequently 5 minutes or until crisp-tender.
Add water chestnuts, tomato, cucumber and sweet 'n sour spicy French dressing blended with brown sugar and soy sauce.
Simmer, covered, 5 minutes or until vegetables are tender.
Top, if desired, with sesame seeds.
Yields 6 servings.

_enrichment.description: A vibrant sweet and sour stir-fry with crisp vegetables and a tangy dressing, topped with sesame seeds for extra crunch.

title: Flour Tortilla Dumplings
directions: Boil chicken in a pot with chopped celery, onion and bouillon cubes.
Remove chicken from bones and cut into bite-size pieces. Add 2 cans soup to broth.
Cut tortillas into strips and add.
Add chicken and cook for about 30 minutes.

_enrichment.description: Comforting dumplings made with flour tortillas, tender chicken, and a creamy soup broth, simmered until the tortillas are soft and flavourful.

title: Beef With Dumplings
directions: Trim excess fat from roast.
In Dutch oven or large skillet, slowly brown meat on all sides in hot shortening.
Add next 7 ingredients.
Cover.
Cook slowly 2 1/2 hours or until meat is tender.
Place biscuits on meat. Sprinkle with parsley.
Cover tightly and steam 15 minutes or until "dumplings" are done. Remove meat (pot roast) and "dumplings" to warm platter. Make pot roast gravy if you want to.
Makes 6 to 8 servings.

_enrichment.description: A hearty beef stew with tender meat and flaky dumplings, slow-cooked with tomatoes, vinegar, and spices for a rich, comforting dish.

title: Easy Stir-Fry
ingredients: 1 to 2 c. cubed chicken, beef or pork
1 (16 oz.) pkg. frozen broccoli, cauliflower and carrots
1 (16 oz.) pkg. frozen Oriental vegetable mix
brown sugar and soy sauce to taste
directions: Marinate meat in marinade and/or vinegar for about 1 hour. Preheat vegetable oil in wok or large pan on stove.
Add meat and marinade to pan and quickly brown outside of meat, stirring so all sides brown.
Cover and steam for about 3 minutes.
Add vegetables; stir until coated with liquid.
Cover and steam for 5 minutes or until vegetables are desired tenderness.
Add brown sugar and soy sauce to taste.
Serve over rice.

_enrichment.description: An easy stir-fry with marinated chicken, frozen broccoli, and a tangy soy-vinegar sauce, quickly cooked for a weeknight meal.

title: Mock Apple Dumplings
ingredients: 2 1/2 c. peeled, cored and diced apples
directions: Blend flour, baking powder, salt, sugar and shortening like pie crust.
Add milk and mix thoroughly until forms a soft dough. On a floured surface, pat dough, then roll out to a rectangular shape with 1/4-inch to 1/2-inch thickness.
Spread softened margarine over the dough.
Sprinkle cinnamon, sugar, apples, and more cinnamon and sugar.
Roll up like a jelly roll.
Cut in 2-inch slices and place in 9 x 13-inch pan (sprayed).
Pour Syrup (recipe follows) over slices.
Bake at 425° for 30 minutes or tops are golden brown and center is firm.
Serve warm or cold.

_enrichment.description: A mock apple dumpling dessert with a flaky pastry crust, cinnamon-spiced apples, and a soft, cake-like texture.

title: Sweet And Sour Steak
ingredients: 1 small can pineapple chunks (reserving 3 Tbsp. liquid)
directions: Brown steak on both sides in oil in frying pan.
Combine salt, pepper, dry mustard, minced onion, water and vinegar and pour over steak.
Cover tightly and cook about 1 1/2 hours over low heat. Drain orange sections and pineapple chunks, reserving 3 tablespoons liquid and blend in brown sugar and cornstarch.
Put steak on platter to keep warm.
Put fruit and sugar mixture in pan with drippings and cook 2 or 3 minutes.
Pour over meat and serve.

_enrichment.description: A sweet and tangy steak dish with orange and pineapple segments, slow-cooked in a spiced vinegar sauce for a rich, fruity flavour.

title: Kung Pao Chicken
directions: Cut chicken breasts into 1-inch squares; marinate chicken in 1 tablespoon soy sauce and 1 1/2 tablespoons cornstarch, mixed. Quickly stir-fry chicken in hot vegetable oil.
Drain and remove chicken from heat.
Mix in a separate bowl 2 tablespoons soy sauce, cooking wine, sugar, Chinese brown vinegar, 1 teaspoon cornstarch, sesame oil, salt and hot pepper sauce; set aside.
Stir-fry minced ginger in remaining oil.
Add chicken and the sauce; set aside until thickened and thoroughly heated.
Remove from heat; mix in peanuts.

_enrichment.description: A fiery Sichuan-inspired chicken dish with peanuts, ginger, and a spicy soy-vinegar sauce, offering a balance of heat and umami.

title: Chicken Stew With Parsley Dumplings
directions: Heat chicken, 4 cups of water, onion, bouillon and pepper to boiling in Dutch oven and reduce heat.
Simmer, uncovered, 30 minutes.
Add carrots and simmer 10 minutes.
Mix flour and 1/2 cup water. Stir flour mixture and celery into chicken. Cook, stirring constantly, until thickened.
Combine Bisquick, milk and parsley.
Drop by spoonfuls onto boiling stew. Cook, uncovered, over low heat 10 minutes.
Cover and cook an additional 10 minutes. Serves 4 to 6.

_enrichment.description: A hearty British-style chicken stew with tender dumplings, perfect for a cosy autumn or winter meal.

title: Oriental Chicken
directions: Cut chicken into 1/4-inch strips, cutting across the grain. Heat oil and add chicken until they go white (about 3 minutes). Add green pepper, onion, celery, water chestnuts, broth, salt and ginger.
Cover and cook about 6 minutes.
Mix soy sauce with cornstarch.
Add to skillet with bean sprouts.
Turn heat down and simmer few minutes.
Serve over hot rice.
Serves 4.

_enrichment.description: A quick and flavourful stir-fry with chicken, vegetables, and a glossy soy-based sauce, served over rice.

title: Bread Dumplings(Semmelknodel)
directions: Finely slice bread rolls and soak in milk for 15 minutes.
Fry onion and parsley in butter; add to soaked bread.
Mix in eggs, flour, salt and nutmeg.
Bring water to a boil with salt.
Form a test dumpling and cook in slowly simmering water 10 to 15 minutes.
If it breaks apart, add bread crumbs to mixture to make it drier.
If it is too firm, add a little more milk to mixture. Serve with goulash or roast beef.

_enrichment.description: Soft, pillowy German bread dumplings served in a light broth, a classic accompaniment to roast meats.

title: Chinese Roast Pork Or Chicken
ingredients: 2 lb. boneless pork loin, trimmed of excess fat or boneless chicken breasts
directions: Quarter the pork lengthwise into 4 large strips.
Pierce the meat with a fork.
Whisk the other ingredients together in a bowl. Line a roasting pan with heavy-duty aluminum foil.
Arrange the pork or chicken in a single layer.
Pour the marinade sauce over the meat and allow it to marinate for at least 4 hours, or overnight, in the refrigerator.

_enrichment.description: Succulent roasted pork or chicken marinated in a sweet and savoury Chinese-inspired sauce.

title: Chinese Whatsit(Or You Name It Casserole)
directions: Saute beef; add salt and pepper to taste and a dash of sugar. Place cooked beef in a 9 1/2 x 13-inch baking casserole.
Combine celery and onion and layer evenly over beef.
Break up frozen peas and make another layer.
(Celery, onion and peas may be combined to make 1 layer together.)
Spread crushed potato chips over vegetables.
Dilute soup with water and pour over all ingredients. Bake at 350° for 30 minutes.

_enrichment.description: A comforting American casserole with layers of beef, vegetables, and crispy potato chips.

title: Chinese Chicken Slaw
ingredients: 2 c. each: shredded green and red cabbage
1 (8 oz.) can sliced water chestnuts, drained
1 (6 oz.) pkg. frozen Chinese pea pods, thawed and drained
1/4 c. each: julienne carrots and sliced green onion
directions: Whisk dressing and soy sauce together and set aside.
In large bowl, combine remaining ingredients (except chow mein noodles). Pour dressing over salad and toss well.
Chill until ready to serve.
Serve topped with Chinese noodles.
(This is really good over hot white rice, too.)

_enrichment.description: A refreshing Asian-inspired slaw with shredded chicken, crunchy vegetables, and a tangy dressing.

title: Shrimp And Scallop Casserole
directions: Marinate seafood in soy sauce 15 minutes. While seafood is marinating, melt butter in saucepan. Add parsley and crushed garlic. Remove seafood from sauce.
Place in a shallow casserole or baking dish. Top with mushrooms, water chestnuts and bean sprouts.
Pour butter mixture on top.
Bake at 350° for about 25 minutes.
(Test shrimp for doneness.)

_enrichment.description: A delicate seafood casserole with shrimp and scallops in a light soy-garlic sauce.

title: Szechuan Noodle Toss
ingredients: 2 large red peppers, seeded, cored and julienned
1 lb. spinach leaves, washed, drained and cut in 1-inch pieces
1 (8 oz.) can sliced water chestnuts, drained
directions: In a 5-quart saucepan, over high heat, bring 3-quarts water, the salt and 1 tablespoon of the vegetable oil to a rolling boil. Add pasta, stirring to separate strands.
Cover pan until water returns to boiling.
Uncover and cook pasta 7 to 9 minutes or until pasta is al dente; that is, cooked but firm to the bite.
Pour hot pasta and water into a colander that has been set in the sink. Drain pasta thoroughly.
Rinse with cold running water and drain pasta again.
Transfer cooled pasta to a large bowl and set aside.

_enrichment.description: A spicy, aromatic noodle dish tossed with vegetables, tofu, and a sesame-soy dressing.

title: Egg Foo Young
ingredients: 1 c. cooked ham, pork, chicken, etc. (I use leftover roast and roast gravy)
1 Tbsp. La Choy brown molasses Chinese brown sauce
directions: Mix first 6 ingredients.
Drop by 1/2 cup into heated oil in skillet.
Do not crowd omelets.
Turn once when lightly browned. Transfer drained patties to warm platter.
Serve omelets over rice topped with gravy made from last 6 ingredients (cook until thickens) or leftover roast gravy or canned gravy to which soy sauce and Chinese brown sauce has been added.
Chinese noodles, green onions, celery, etc., make nice additions to meal.

_enrichment.description: A savoury Chinese omelette packed with pork, bean sprouts, and a rich gravy.

title: Coconut Chinese Chews
directions: Mix ingredients in order named.
Spread it thin on a greased and floured pan.
Bake at 375° until done, not allowing the edges to become hard.
Cut into 1-inch squares and roll between your palms into ball shapes. When cool, ice each and roll thickly in shredded coconut.

_enrichment.description: Sweet, chewy coconut squares with dates and nuts, a simple baked treat.

title: Broccoli-Carrot Stir-Fry
directions: For sauce, in a bowl, stir together orange juice, dry sherry and cornstarch; set aside.

Preheat a wok or large skillet over high heat. Add cooking oil (add more cooking oil as necessary during cooking). Stir-fry ginger root in hot oil for 15 seconds. Add carrots and stir-fry for 1 minute. Add broccoli and stir-fry for 3 to 4 minutes or until
crisp-tender. Push vegetables from the center of the wok or skillet.
Stir sauce; add to center of wok. Cook and stir until thickened and bubbly. Cook and stir for 1 minute more. Stir in vegetables and walnuts to coat. Serve immediately. Makes 4 servings.

_enrichment.description: A vibrant stir-fry with broccoli, carrots, and walnuts in a citrusy orange-sherry sauce.

title: Fried Rice (Japanese)
directions: Mix eggs, 1 tsp. DASHI sauce, sugar and pepper until well blended.
Heat skillet, add sesame oil, and egg mixture.
Scramble and remove from skillet.
Add vegetable oil to the hot skillet. Stir in green onion, ginger and egg mixture.
Add cooked rice and ham until crisp and hot.
Mix with 2 Tbsp. DASHI sauce and a little pepper to taste.
If you prefer, you can use any kind of meat instead of ham.

_enrichment.description: A classic Japanese fried rice with ham, eggs, and a hint of sesame, quick and satisfying.

title: Ginger Chicken Stir-Fry
ingredients: 1 lb. skinned, boned chicken breast, cut into 1 1/2-inch pieces
1 (16 oz.) pkg. frozen broccoli, cauliflower and carrot mix
1/3 c. dry wine or chicken broth
directions: Combine teriyaki sauce and 1/4 cup water in shallow baking dish; add chicken pieces, coating well.
Cover and refrigerate 2 hours.
Drain marinade from chicken.
Heat oil in large skillet or wok; stir-fry chicken and onion until chicken is slightly browned.
Stir in vegetables, ginger and wine.
Cover and simmer 4 to 5 minutes or until vegetables are tender-crisp.
Combine cornstarch and water in small bowl until smooth.
Gradually add to skillet, stirring constantly until thickened.
Serve over rice. Yields 6 servings.
Low fat!

_enrichment.description: A teriyaki-glazed chicken stir-fry with ginger and vegetables, served over brown rice.

title: Sweet And Sour Loaf
ingredients: 1/4 c. crushed bread crumbs or crackers
directions: Mix tomato sauce, sugar, vinegar and mustard until sugar dissolves.
Mix egg, onion, cucumber, beef, salt (optional), pepper and 1/2 cup tomato sauce mixture.
Shape in a loaf pan and pour the rest of the tomato sauce over the top.
Preheat oven to 400° and bake for about 40 to 45 minutes.

_enrichment.description: A sweet and tangy meatloaf with a tomato-based glaze, a homely American classic.

title: Quick Saucy Chicken Stir-Fry
ingredients: 3 c. (1 lb.) boneless, skinless chicken breasts, cut into thin strips
directions: Heat 1 tablespoon oil in wok or large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add vegetables and garlic. Stir-fry 4 to 5 minutes or until tender. Remove vegetables from pan.
Add remaining oil and chicken to pan.

Stir-fry 4 minutes or until tender. Reduce heat. Stir in vegetables and remaining ingredients; simmer 1 minute, stirring occasionally. Serve over the cooked rice. Sprinkle with crushed red pepper flakes.

_enrichment.description: A quick and colourful stir-fry with chicken and mixed vegetables in a savoury sauce.

title: Beef And Broccoli
ingredients: 2 1/2 lb. chuck roast or round steak
directions: Slice meat in strips.
Quickly cook beef in hot oil until meat loses its redness.
Remove meat and place in a shallow bowl. Sprinkle with 2 tablespoons soy sauce and garlic powder.
Prepare brown gravy mix according to package directions and add to pan juices with remaining soy sauce.
Add meat and juices to gravy. Cook slowly for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Cook broccoli until just a fraction underdone.
Drain.
Add to meat and gravy with bean sprouts and water chestnuts.
Stir gently; heat through. Serve over rice.

_enrichment.description: A quick and savoury Chinese stir-fry featuring tender beef strips and crisp broccoli in a rich soy-based gravy.

title: Fried Rice (Chinese)
ingredients: 2 slices cooked bacon, drained and diced
directions: Use chopsticks or a fork to separate rice grains.
In a small bowl, combine the eggs, soy sauce, sesame oil, salt and pepper. Set aside.
Heat wok and add oil around the rim (this seals the wok and heats the oil quickly).
Add shrimp, bacon, and green onion.
Immediately add rice and stir-fry over high heat; after a few minutes, the grains will separate.
While stirring rice, pour in egg mixture.
Stir-fry one minute.
Serve rice in a warm Chinese bowl to retain heat during serving.
Serves 6.

_enrichment.description: A classic Chinese fried rice dish with fluffy grains, scrambled eggs, and a medley of shrimp, bacon, and green onions.

title: Moo Goo Gai Pan(Chinese Chicken And Mushrooms)
ingredients: 2 Tbsp. dry sherry or cooking sherry
directions: Skin and bone chicken breasts.
Slice across breasts into 1/8-inch slices.
In a medium bowl mix chicken, soy sauce, sherry, cornstarch, ginger, sugar and garlic powder.
Set aside.
Thinly slice mushrooms.
Cut each green onion crosswise into 3-inch pieces.
In a 12-inch skillet or wok over medium-high heat in 1/4 cup hot salad oil, cook mushrooms and green onions, stirring quickly and frequently until mushrooms are tender (about 2 minutes).

With a slotted spoon, remove mushroom mixture to bowl. In same skillet or wok, over high heat, in 3 tablespoons hot salad oil, cook chicken mixture, stirring frequently and quickly, until chicken is tender (about 2 or 3 minutes).
Return mushroom mixture to skillet and heat through.
Serve over rice.

_enrichment.description: A delicate Chinese stir-fry of thinly sliced chicken breast and mushrooms in a light, savoury sauce.

title: Jabchai(Chop Suey)
directions: Put noodles into boiling water of more than 180° and cook 5 to 6 minutes.
Rinse noodles with cold water and drain 2 or 3 times. Stir-fry sliced meats and vegetables.
Flavor the rinsed noodles with such various spices as sesame oil, sesame powder, soy sauce and sugar.
Add vegetables and meat.

_enrichment.description: A Korean-inspired stir-fried noodle dish with tender meat, vegetables, and a nutty sesame flavour.

title: Sweet And Sour Meatballs With Rice(About 10 Servings)
ingredients: 1 (16 oz.) can sauerkraut, drain and snip
directions: Beat eggs in soup and water with fork.
Add ground beef and shape in about 40 meatballs.
Combine remaining ingredients (except rice).
Pour 1/2 mixture in 13 x 9 x 2-inch baking dish. Brown meatball mixture in 10-inch skillet, about 1/3 at a time. Remove and drain off fat.
Add on top of
sauerkraut in baking dish. Pour remaining sauerkraut mixture over meatballs.
Cover with foil and bake at 325° in oven for 20 minutes.
Uncover, bake 15 to 20 minutes longer.
Serve over hot rice.

_enrichment.description: A hearty American casserole featuring sweet and sour meatballs with sauerkraut, served over rice.

title: Chinese Vegetable Pouches
directions: Pour 1 tablespoon oil around top of a preheated wok, coating sides.
Heat on high for 1 minute.
Add carrot and cook 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add cabbage and cook, stirring constantly, 5 minutes or until tender.
Stir in greens and next 6 ingredients. Mix cornstarch and water, stirring until smooth; add to cabbage mix.
Cook, stirring constantly, 1 minute or until thickened.
Let cool.

_enrichment.description: Crispy Chinese vegetable parcels filled with a savoury mix of cabbage, carrots, and cashews.

title: Chop Suey Casserole
directions: Brown the pork in the soy sauce.
Add the celery and onion. Add the mushroom soup, mushrooms, bean sprouts and noodles.
Put into casserole dish and bake at 350° for 1 hour or until pork is done.
During the last 10 minutes of baking, sprinkle chow mein noodles on top.

_enrichment.description: A comforting American casserole with tender pork, mushrooms, and bean sprouts, topped with crispy noodles.

title: Spanish Fried Rice
directions: Soak rice in hot water for 1/2 hour; drain well.
Spread in big shallow pan to dry a little, about 1 hour.
Cook bacon until crisp; crumble and set aside.
Add rice to drippings and cook until rice is lightly browned.
Add crumbled bacon, beef broth, tomato sauce, green pepper, onion, garlic and cumin seed.
Cover; cook over low heat, stirring now and then, until rice is done and liquid absorbed, about 30 minutes (add extra water if needed).

_enrichment.description: A Spanish-inspired fried rice with smoky bacon, tomatoes, and cumin, offering a rich and aromatic flavour.

title: Asparagus And Chicken Salad
ingredients: 2 c. cooked chicken, cut into thin strips
directions: Wash and peel asparagus; cut diagonally into 1-inch pieces. Heat oil in a heavy saute pan.
When very hot, add the asparagus and stir-fry for 2 minutes, stirring constantly with a large spoon.
Add the seasoned salt, salt and pepper, stirring another 30 seconds.
Pour in chicken broth and soy sauce.
Cover; cook 1 minute longer.
Remove from heat.
Add the chicken and mix. Transfer to a serving platter.
Cover; refrigerate until serving time.
Just before serving, add the vinegar and toss until well blended.

_enrichment.description: A light and refreshing salad featuring tender asparagus and chicken in a tangy soy-vinegar dressing.

title: Spaghetti With Beef And Mushroom Stir-Fry
ingredients: 1 1/2 to 2 lb. boneless beef sirloin, cut in thin strips
4 Tbsp. oil (to heat wok or skillet)
1 medium red and yellow bell peppers, sliced
1 lb. mushrooms, sliced, or 2 (6 oz.) jars, drained
directions: Combine soy sauce, oil, garlic and ginger in a mixing bowl. Add beef strips and toss well to coat.
Cover and marinate for 1 hour.
In wok or skillet, heat 4 tablespoons oil.
Add beef mixture and broccoli; stir-fry for 2 minutes.
Add onions, pepper strips and mushrooms; stir-fry until vegetables are crisp and tender.
Blend water, cornstarch, bouillon and salt; stir into beef mixture.
Cook until thickened; keep warm.
Prepare spaghetti as directed and drain.
Add beef mixture and toss to mix.
Serve with soy sauce, if desired.

_enrichment.description: A hearty Italian-American stir-fry of beef sirloin and vegetables in a savoury soy-ginger sauce, served over spaghetti.

title: Sweet And Sour Beef
ingredients: 6 to 8 oz. beef, cut into bite-size pieces
2 to 3 carrots, peeled and thinly sliced
1 green pepper, cut into bite-sized chunks
directions: Brown meat in skillet.
Remove and set aside.
Combine brown sugar and cornstarch in skillet.
Add vinegar, soy sauce and juice from canned pineapple.
Cook, stirring constantly until thickened. Add pineapple chunks and meat.
Simmer until 5 minutes before serving time.
If necessary, add water, pineapple juice or apple juice to thin the sauce.
Five minutes before serving time, stir in carrots and green pepper.

_enrichment.description: A classic Chinese sweet and sour beef dish with tender meat, pineapple, and crisp vegetables.

title: Hot Chicken Salad Oriental
ingredients: 1 (8 oz.) can water chestnuts, drained, sliced
directions: In bowl, stir together first 4 ingredients until blended. Stir in chicken, celery, water chestnuts and onion.
Add macaroni; toss to coat well.
Spoon into 2-quart casserole.
Top with chow mein noodles.
Bake in 375° oven 20 to 25 minutes or until heated. Makes 4 to 6 servings.

_enrichment.description: A creamy American casserole featuring shredded chicken, macaroni, and a tangy soy-lemon dressing, topped with crispy noodles.

title: Turkey Soup With Egg Dumplings
directions: Sift flour with salt.
Stir into butter in medium-size mixing bowl.
Add eggs beating until well blended.
Bring broth to boil in large saucepan or kettle with salt, pepper, thyme and turmeric. Add onion, corn, limas and turkey.
Return soup to boiling. Drop dumpling batter by teaspoonsfuls into boiling soup to make 12 dumplings.
Lower heat to keep soup simmering; cover and cook 20 minutes or until dumplings are cooked through.
Serve in warm soup plates or bowls.
Sprinkle with finely chopped parsley if you wish.
Serves 6.

_enrichment.description: A comforting turkey soup with fluffy egg dumplings, corn, and lima beans in a savoury broth.

title: Stir-Fry Minced Chicken With Vegetables
directions: Cut chicken breasts into cube size, then add soy sauce, cornstarch and cooking wine and soak for a while.
Meanwhile, cut green peppers, celery, water chestnuts, carrots, bamboo shoots and onions into cube size.
Stir-fry chicken in 3 tablespoons oil for 3 minutes; remove to a bowl.
Heat the other 3 tablespoons oil to fry onions, bamboo shoots, water chestnuts, celery, carrots and green peppers.
Then add chicken to the pan.
Mix well.

_enrichment.description: A quick and flavourful Chinese stir-fry of minced chicken with crisp vegetables in a light soy sauce.

title: Cabbage Crunch(Chinese Salad)
ingredients: 1 head of cabbage, shredded or 2 pkg. pre-shredded
1 c. vegetable oil (olive oil is best)
directions: Break noodles into small pieces.
Noodles and almonds may be toasted, if desired.
Place noodles, almonds and sunflower seeds in a plastic bag or sealable container.
Combine sugar, vegetable oil, vinegar and Soy sauce
and mix well to make dressing. Combine ingredients immediately prior to serving as it will not keep, the dry ingredients get soggy.

_enrichment.description: A crunchy Chinese-inspired salad with cabbage, Ramen noodles, almonds, and a tangy soy-vinegar dressing.

title: Sweet And Sour Cabbage Slaw
directions: Mix together vinegar and sugar.
Heat until sugar dissolves; set aside to cool.
Shred together in food processor the cabbage, onion and green pepper.
Add celery seed, mustard seed and salt to cabbage mixture and add black pepper to taste.
When vinegar mixture is cooled, pour over cabbage mixture and mix well.
Chill in a closed container.
This will keep in refrigerator for 2 to 3 weeks.

_enrichment.description: A tangy and slightly sweet cabbage slaw with a crunchy texture, perfect as a refreshing side dish.

title: Chinatown Red-Cooked Pork Treasures
ingredients: 2 lb. boneless pork loin, cut into 3/4-inch cubes
directions: In a 1-gallon self-sealing plastic bag, combine all ingredients and mix well.
Seal bag and marinate in the refrigerate 8 to 12 hours or overnight.
Heat oven to 350°. Remove pork cubes from marinade, discarding marinade.
Pat pork dry with paper towel.
Place cubes in a single layer not touching in a shallow baking pan and bake at 350° for 25 to 30 minutes until pork is just tender and lightly browned.
Remove to serving platter or chafing dish and serve hot as an appetizer.
Makes 16 servings.

_enrichment.description: Tender pork loin marinated in a rich blend of sauces and spices, then slow-cooked to perfection for a melt-in-the-mouth appetiser.

title: Chicken Almond
directions: Skin uncooked chicken breast; cut in thin strips.
Sprinkle with 2 tablespoons soy sauce; marinate for 10 minutes.
Beat flour, salt and eggs until smooth.
Dip chicken in batter; fry in hot oil (370°) until browned.
Remove chicken and keep warm.
Add chicken broth to skillet.
Mix in bamboo shoots, chestnuts, celery and 2 tablespoons monosodium glutamate.
Bring to boil; cover and cook 5 minutes.
Blend cornstarch and cold water; add to chicken mixture, stirring constantly, until mixture thickens and bubbles. Add chicken, almonds and salt to taste.
Cut lettuce in strips and toss with mixture just before serving over hot, fluffy rice.

_enrichment.description: Crispy battered chicken strips served with a creamy almond sauce, offering a delightful mix of textures and flavours.

title: Peanut Butter And Pork Stir-Fry
ingredients: 1 medium red or green pepper, cut in 1-inch pieces (1 c.)
1 medium onion, sliced and separated into rings (1/2 c.)
directions: In wok or large skillet, heat 1 tablespoon oil.
Stir-fry pork 3 minutes or until pink is gone.
Remove from pan.
Add rest of oil, carrots, pepper, celery and onion; stir-fry for 3 minutes. Add beans; stir-fry for 1 minute or until veggies are tender-crisp.

_enrichment.description: A quick and hearty stir-fry combining tender pork with crunchy vegetables in a rich peanut butter sauce.

title: Chinese Chicken
directions: In a large skillet, heat oil until hot.
Place water chestnuts, celery, carrots and cabbage in skillet.
Cover; cook over medium-high heat for about 2 minutes.
Add baked chicken. Pour chicken broth in center of mixture.
Cover and steam 1 minute.
Dissolve cornstarch in water.
Add soy sauce; toss with chicken-vegetable mixture to thicken sauce to cover all ingredients.
Serve immediately over rice, noodles or chow mein noodles.

_enrichment.description: A light and flavourful steamed chicken dish with crisp vegetables, served with a silky sauce.

title: Spiced Peach Dumplings
directions: In saucepan, mix peaches, sugar, butter and lemon juice. Bring to boil.
In large bowl, mix all other ingredients, except nuts.
Drop this mixture by spoonfuls into hot peach mixture. Sprinkle with nuts.
Cover and cook over low heat for 15 minutes. Serve hot with light cream or ice cream.

_enrichment.description: Warm, spiced peach dumplings with a flaky biscuit topping, served with a dollop of light cream or ice cream.

title: Stir Fry Pepper Steak
directions: Saute garlic and 2 Tbsp. oil until golden brown.
Add ginger and pepper to the garlic mixture.
Meanwhile slice frozen Steak-Ums into thin strips and add to browned garlic and cook until done.
Remove from wok into another dish.

_enrichment.description: A classic stir-fry of thinly sliced steak and peppers in a savoury sauce, offering a satisfying crunch and tender bite.

title: Chinese Dish
ingredients: 1 (6 oz.) can mushrooms (1 1/3 c. with juice)
directions: Brown and
cook meat in shortening; add celery, onions, water and mushrooms with liquid.
Mix soy sauce and cornstarch together, then gradually pour in cooked ingredients to thicken.
Add drained bean sprouts.

_enrichment.description: A simple yet flavourful stir-fry featuring tender meat, mushrooms, and bean sprouts in a glossy soy sauce.

title: Barbecued Beef(Pulgogi)
ingredients: 1 1/3 lb. top round or tenderloin of beef
directions: Slice the beef thinly and score lightly with a knife to make it more tender.
Cut it into bite size pieces and marinate it in the sugar and rice wine.
Mix the marinated beef thoroughly with the soy sauce, chopped garlic, sesame salt and sesame oil.
Broil the seasoned beef over hot charcoal on a grill or in a frypan.

_enrichment.description: Marinated beef slices grilled to perfection, offering a sweet and savoury flavour with a tender, juicy texture.

title: Lucky Seven Stir-Fry
ingredients: 3/4 lb. boneless skinless chicken breast or boneless tender beef, cut into thin strips
directions: Combine meat, cornstarch and soy sauce; let stand 10 minutes. Stir-fry meat in 2 tablespoons hot oil in wok or large skillet over high heat 2 minutes; remove.
Heat remaining 1 tablespoon oil in same pan.
Add vegetables; stir-fry 2 minutes.
Combine Lucky Seven Stir-Fry Sauce and red pepper; pour mixture over vegetables. Cook, stirring, until sauce boils and thickens.
Stir in meat to combine.

_enrichment.description: A quick and colourful stir-fry of chicken and frozen vegetables in a rich, spicy sauce, served over fluffy rice.

title: Turkey Stir-Fry
ingredients: 1 1/2 lb. raw boneless turkey, cut into strips
directions: In large skillet or wok, stir-fry turkey in oil over medium-high heat until no longer pink, approximately 5 to 6 minutes.
Remove turkey and keep warm.
Stir-fry onion, carrot, green pepper and mushrooms until crispy and tender.
In small bowl, combine chicken broth, cornstarch, soy sauce and ginger. Add to the skillet; cook and stir until heated through.
If desired, serve over rice.

_enrichment.description: A light and flavourful stir-fry of turkey and crisp vegetables in a silky sauce, perfect for a quick meal.

title: Princess Chicken
ingredients: 1/2 c. dry sherry or white wine
2 c. vegetables in bite-size pieces (broccoli, carrots, celery, bell pepper, pea pods)
directions: Cut chicken into bite-size pieces.
Mix 1/4 cup soy sauce, 1/4 cup sherry, 3/4 cup water and 8 teaspoons cornstarch together. Toss over chicken and let marinate 20 minutes.
Mix remaining soy sauce, sherry, sugar, sesame oil, rice vinegar, 1 tablespoon water and cornstarch.
Heat peanut oil in a wok or skillet.

Stir-fry red pepper flakes for 1 or 2 minutes (depending on desired hotness). Add chicken and marinade and stir-fry until chicken changes color. Add remaining mixture, water chestnuts and vegetables and stir-fry until sauce thickens and vegetables are desired tenderness and chicken is cooked.
Serve over rice.

_enrichment.description: Succulent chicken pieces stir-fried with water chestnuts and vegetables in a rich, slightly spicy sauce, served over rice.

title: Beef And Peppers Stir-Fry
ingredients: 1 lb. beef top round steak, cut 3/4-inch to 1-inch thick
directions: Slice steak crosswise into 1/8-inch strips. Combine water, sherry and soy sauce.
Pour over beef and marinate while cutting peppers into thin strips.
Quickly cook peppers in hot oil in large frying pan or wok 3 to 4 minutes, stirring constantly. Remove from pan.
Pour off and reserve marinade.

_enrichment.description: A quick and flavourful stir-fry of thinly sliced beef and peppers in a glossy, savoury sauce.

title: Ginger Dip
directions: Drain and chop chestnuts.
Finely chop parsley and chop crystallized ginger.
Combine all ingredients; cover and chill for at least 8 hours.
To reduce fat and cholesterol, fat-free mayonnaise and nonfat sour cream alternatives may be substituted. Serve as a dip with fresh vegetables.
Yields 3 cups.

_enrichment.description: A creamy and tangy dip made with mayonnaise, sour cream, and ginger, perfect for serving with crispy snacks.

title: Mandarin Chicken Salad
directions: Poach chicken in simmered water 10 to 15 minutes or until no longer pink.
Drain, set to cool.
When cool enough to handle, remove skin and bones and shred (be sure to blanch the broccoli!).
Stir water into peanut butter until smooth.
Add soy sauce, vinegar, sesame oil, wine and sugar.
Stir in 1 tablespoon of onions and ginger mix.
Pour over chicken and broccoli-noodles in a large bowl and toss.
Garnish with peanuts.

_enrichment.description: A refreshing salad featuring shredded chicken, crunchy vegetables, and a nutty peanut dressing, served over angel hair pasta.

title: Don'S Shrimp, Cashew And Rice Fry
ingredients: 1 box Uncle Ben's long grain and wild rice
directions: Prepare the rice according to the box directions, set aside. Heat the oil and soy sauce in a large skillet.
Saute the cashews, mushrooms and water chestnuts until hot.
Add shrimp and heat well. Mix in the rice and heat until the liquid is absorbed.
Serve with egg rolls and Hardee's garden salad.

_enrichment.description: A quick and savoury stir-fry featuring shrimp, cashews, and mushrooms, served with rice and a side salad for a simple weeknight meal.

title: Fried Won Ton
directions: Chop all ingredients fine.
Place in bowl and mix soy sauce, sesame oil, cornstarch and water.
Cook to thicken.
Wrap meat mixture in won ton skins. Cook in hot oil. Drain and serve with sweet and sour sauce.

_enrichment.description: Crispy fried wontons filled with a spiced pork and prawn mixture, served with a sweet dipping sauce for a delightful snack or starter.

title: Chinese Pork Dumplings
ingredients: 1/2 c. finely chopped canned bamboo shoots
1/4 c. finely chopped green onions (with tops)
directions: Soak mushrooms in warm water until soft, about 30 minutes; drain.
Rinse in warm water; drain.
Remove and discard stems; chop caps finely.
Mix mushrooms, pork, bamboo shoots, green onions, egg white, cornstarch, salt, 2 teaspoons soy sauce, 1/2 teaspoon sesame oil and the white pepper.

_enrichment.description: Delicate dumplings filled with ground pork, mushrooms, and bamboo shoots, offering a tender and flavourful bite perfect for a dinner party.

title: Sweet And Sour Stew
ingredients: diced potatoes, carrots and celery to taste
directions: Combine flour, m.s.g. and pepper.
Coat beef with flour mixture. Brown in oil in kettle.
Combine water, catsup, brown sugar, vinegar, Worcestershire sauce and salt.
Pour over beef. Add onion and cook over low heat for about 45 minutes.
Add remaining vegetables.
Simmer for 1 hour and 30 minutes or 2 hours until beef is done and vegetables are tender. Yields 6 servings.

_enrichment.description: A hearty stew with tender beef, potatoes, and onions in a rich sweet and sour gravy, ideal for a comforting family meal.

title: Uncle Bob'S Chicken Stir-Fry(Low-Fat)
ingredients: 1 whole chicken breast (skinless), cut into chunks
1 jar sweet and sour sauce (Yosita brand is good)
directions: In a pan sprayed with Pam, add all ingredients except sweet and sour sauce.
You can add a little water to the pan to help steam the vegetables.
When vegetables are almost done, add sweet and sour sauce.
Heat and serve over noodles or rice.

_enrichment.description: A low-fat stir-fry with chicken, vegetables, and a tangy sweet and sour sauce, served over noodles or rice for a quick and healthy meal.

title: Shira-Ae With Chicken
directions: Cut chicken into small pieces.
Slice carrots, bamboo shoots and Konnyaku in 1-inch pieces.
Cook in water, sake, sugar and soy sauce mixture for 15 minutes and cool.
When cool, mix with tofu mixture from the Vegetable Shira-ae recipe.

_enrichment.description: A delicate Japanese dish featuring chicken and vegetables simmered in a sweet soy-based broth, served chilled with tofu for a refreshing meal.

title: Wilda'S Sweet And Sour Pork
ingredients: 1 1/2 lb. pork steak, cut in 1-inch cubes
1 (No. 2) can pineapple chunks in heavy syrup
1 small green pepper, cut in strips
directions: Brown pork in fat; add water, cover and simmer 1 hour.
Drain pineapple and save syrup.
Combine brown sugar, vinegar and soy sauce; add to pork.
Mix cornstarch well with pineapple juice and add.
Continue cooking, stirring until gravy thickens. Add pineapple, green pepper and onion.
Cook 2 to 3 minutes and serve over rice with soy sauce.

_enrichment.description: A classic sweet and sour pork dish with pineapple, peppers, and a glossy sauce, served over rice for a vibrant and satisfying meal.

title: Pork Chow Mein
directions: Cut pork into 4 x 1/2 x 1/4-inch strips.
Place in a bowl. Add garlic and 2 tablespoons soy sauce.
Cover and refrigerate 2 to 4 hours.
Meanwhile, combine broth, cornstarch, ginger and remaining soy sauce; mix well and set aside.

_enrichment.description: A quick and flavourful stir-fry with pork, vegetables, and a savoury sauce, served over rice for a simple yet satisfying meal.

title: Pork Chops Hong-Kong
ingredients: 1 (4 oz.) can water chestnuts, drained and sliced
directions: Brown chops, pour off fat.
Place chops in baking pan; arrange onion, mushrooms and water chestnuts on top.
Combine remaining ingredients and pour over chops.
Cover and bake at 350° for 45 minutes or until tender.

_enrichment.description: Baked pork chops topped with onions, mushrooms, and water chestnuts in a tangy sauce, offering a tender and flavourful main course.

title: Chinese Hot Dish
directions: Brown the first 3 ingredients.
Mix and place the remaining ingredients in a casserole dish. Add the hamburger.
Bake at 350° for 1 hour.
Salt hamburger only slightly as soy sauce is salty. Put Chinese noodles on top of casserole for the last 15 minutes.

_enrichment.description: A creamy casserole with ground beef, vegetables, and creamy soups, baked until bubbly and served with Chinese noodles for a comforting meal.

title: Sweet And Sour Pork(Microwave)
ingredients: 1 (13 1/4 oz.) can pineapple chunks and juice
1 medium green pepper, cut into strips
directions: Toss together pork and cornstarch in 2-quart glass casserole. Stir in remaining ingredients, except green pepper.
Cover with glass lid or plastic wrap.
Microwave on roast for 25 minutes. Stir in green pepper.
Re-cover and continue cooking on roast for 5 to 10 minutes or until tender.
Let stand, covered, 5 minutes before serving.
Makes 4 to 6 servings.

_enrichment.description: A quick microwave sweet and sour pork dish with lean pork, pineapple, and peppers, offering a tangy and tender meal in minutes.

title: Haluski With Dumplings
directions: Chop cabbage and saute in butter.
While cooking, mix flour, eggs, salt, milk and 1/2 cup of water.
Add more water if needed. Drop small amounts in boiling water for 5 minutes.
Drain and add to cabbage.
Brown onion in butter and pour over cabbage and dumplings.

_enrichment.description: A hearty dish of cabbage and dumplings, browned with onions for a comforting and filling meal perfect for colder months.

title: Chicken Casserole Oriental
ingredients: 1 medium green pepper, cut in strips
1/2 c. coarsely broken walnuts or almonds
directions: Drain mushrooms, reserve 3 tablespoons liquid.
Melt margarine in skillet; saute onion until tender.
Stir in green pepper, celery, chicken broth and soy sauce.
Add chicken and mushrooms. Mix cornstarch and liquid from mushrooms; stir into chicken mixture.
Add nuts, water chestnuts and rice.
Pour into 2-quart greased casserole.
Bake in preheated 400° oven for 20 minutes or until rice is soft.
Yield: 10 to 12 servings.

_enrichment.description: A creamy casserole with chicken, mushrooms, and vegetables in a savoury sauce, served over rice for a comforting and flavourful meal.

title: Sweet And Sour Cucumber Salad(Diabetic:  Low-Fat)
directions: Boil water, vinegar, sweetener, pickle and salt.
Place cucumbers and celery slices in a glass jar.
Pour boiling liquid over. Cover and refrigerate overnight. Drain before serving. Yields 2 cups.
Contains 20 calories per serving.

_enrichment.description: A refreshing and low-fat cucumber salad with a tangy vinegar dressing, perfect for a light summer side dish.

title: Almond Chicken(Stir-Fry)
directions: Skin, bone and cut up chicken breasts into 1/2-inch cubes. In a bowl, mix ginger, honey and cornstarch and blend in water, soy sauce and sherry.
Thaw pea pods.
In a large skillet, heat oil over medium heat.
Add almonds, stirring and cooking for about 3 minutes.
Add chicken and cook just until meat turns white. Pour in sherry mixture and cook until sauce thickens.
Add pea pods and stir until hot and glazed.
Serve over rice.
(Cashews may be substituted for the almonds.)

_enrichment.description: A stir-fry featuring almond-glazed chicken with ginger and honey, served with vegetables for a sweet and savoury meal.

title: Sweet And Sour Frankfurters
directions: Cut franks in quarters and cook slowly for 5 to 8 minutes. Chop onion coarsely.
Cut green pepper in long thin strips and quarter tomatoes.
Heat fat in a skillet; add onion, green peppers, tomatoes, pineapple and pineapple syrup.
Mix cornstarch with water.
Add to mixture in skillet.
Flavor with vinegar and stir in drained franks.

_enrichment.description: A tangy and slightly sweet dish featuring frankfurters cooked with onions, peppers, tomatoes, and pineapple in a glossy sauce. Perfect for a quick weeknight meal.

title: Sweet And Sour Appetizers
ingredients: 1 (10 oz.) can pineapple chunks (save 1/2 c. pineapple juice)
directions: Mix all liquid ingredients (vinegar, pineapple juice and water), brown sugar, salt and cornstarch in saucepan.
Bring to boil over low heat until the mixture thickens.
Add browned, chopped sausage, green peppers and pineapple.
Serve with toothpicks, so that the guests may poke a sausage or pineapple chunk.
I usually put this in a crock-pot to keep warm at parties. It is always a hit.

_enrichment.description: A party-friendly appetiser with sausages, pineapple, and peppers in a sticky sweet-and-sour sauce. Ideal for serving with toothpicks at gatherings.

title: China Town Chicken With Zucchini
ingredients: 1/3 c. sliced green onions with tops
2 whole boned chicken breasts, sliced in small strips
1 c. water, mixed with 1 Tbsp. cornstarch
directions: Cook rice as directed, then mix in green onions.
While rice is cooking, saute chicken and garlic in oil until chicken is white.
Add soy sauce and cook for 2 to 3 minutes, stirring some. Add zucchini and water chestnuts.
Cook until zucchini is crisp-tender (2 to 3 minutes).
Add water-cornstarch mix and stir until thick and clear.
Serve over rice-onion mixture.

_enrichment.description: A light and flavourful stir-fry of chicken and zucchini served over fluffy brown rice, with a glossy soy-based sauce. A healthy and satisfying meal.

title: Szechuan Shrimp
directions: Combine scallions, bamboo shoots, ginger, garlic and Tabasco in small bowl.
In second bowl, combine sugar, catsup, sherry, soy sauce and sesame seed.
In a third bowl, mix cornstarch and water. Heat oil to 400°.
Add shrimp to hot oil; cook about 2 minutes. Remove to strainer.
Heat 2 tablespoons of oil.
Add contents of first bowl, then second bowl, shrimp and then cornstarch with water.
Serve over rice.

_enrichment.description: Spicy and aromatic Szechuan-style shrimp with a glossy sauce, featuring ginger, garlic, and sesame. A bold and exciting dish for adventurous eaters.

title: Roy'S Chicken
ingredients: 3 whole chicken breasts, split and boned or 6 chicken legs and thighs
directions: Mix the soy sauce, wine, hoisin, ginger, lemon peel, lemon juice and pepper.
Pour into a large sturdy plastic bag.
Add the chicken; seal bag and refrigerate at least 4 hours or overnight. Before cooking, drain chicken and discard marinade.
Arrange chicken side down in a single layer in a greased 9 x 13-inch baking pan.
Bake uncovered in a 400° oven for 15 minutes.
Turn chicken over, skin side up, and cook another 10 minutes for breasts (20 minutes for legs and thighs) or until meat is no longer pink when slashed.
Or instead of cooking, barbecue over a low fire.

_enrichment.description: Tender chicken breasts marinated in a rich blend of soy sauce, hoisin, and white wine, then baked to perfection. A simple yet flavourful dish.

title: Chinese Style Fried Rice
directions: In a small saucepan, bring 1 1/4 c water to a boil.
Stir in rice and remove from heat.
Cover and let stand 5 minutes. Meanwhile using a 10 in skillet, cook egg (slightly beaten) in 3 Tbsp, butter.
Add 1/3 c chopped onion and the rice.
Saute, stirring over medium heat until mixture slightly browned.
Mix 1/4 c water with 2 to 3 Tbsp soy sauce and stir into the rice.
Makes 4 servings.

_enrichment.description: A classic fried rice dish with a savoury soy sauce kick, featuring fluffy rice and a hint of buttery richness. A quick and satisfying meal.

title: Chinese Chicken And Rice
directions: Dip chicken in flour and brown in hot oil.
Drain and arrange in another skillet.
Add remaining ingredients and bring to a boil. Simmer 15 minutes or until chicken is thoroughly done.
Thicken with a mixture of cornstarch and water.
Serve over hot, cooked rice.

_enrichment.description: A hearty one-pot dish of tender chicken simmered with mushrooms, water chestnuts, and a glossy soy-based sauce, served over fluffy rice.

title: Oriental Wings(Makes 24 Appetizer Servings)
directions: Preheat oven to 450°.
Cut each wing at joints; discard tips. Wash, drain and dry wings.
Mix together soy and hoisin sauces, rice vinegar, honey, Cajun seasoning and garlic.
Dip wings in sauce.
Arrange in single layer on foil-lined baking pan.
Bake 20 minutes; remove from oven and turn, spooning any remaining sauce over wings.
Return to oven and bake until dark brown, 20 to 25 minutes.
Can be made ahead, refrigerated and reheated in foil at 350° for 10 to 15 minutes.
Serve hot or at room temperature.

_enrichment.description: Crispy baked chicken wings coated in a sticky, spicy-sweet sauce with hoisin and Cajun seasoning. A crowd-pleasing appetiser for any occasion.

title: Lo Mein Salad
ingredients: 1 lb. thin spaghetti, cooked al dente
directions: Bring all liquid ingredients and garlic to a boil.
Toss with warm rinsed spaghetti.
Fold in sesame seed.
Chill 24 hours. Before serving, add scallions, lightly steamed broccoli, mushrooms and snow peas.
(You can lightly saute these in corn oil if you prefer.)
You can substitute or add ingredients such as carrots and asparagus.
You can turn this into a main dish by adding ground beef, pork, chicken or shrimp.

_enrichment.description: A chilled noodle salad with a tangy, nutty dressing, featuring crisp vegetables and a sesame seed garnish. A refreshing and light dish.

title: Chicken And Cashew Nuts
directions: After cutting chicken into cubes, marinate with 1/2 teaspoon salt.
Cut onion and squash into cubes.
Mix sauce:
soy sauce, oyster sauce, sesame oil and tapioca powder with 1/2 cup of water and set aside.
Saute onion until browning in 2 tablespoons of vegetable oil, then add marinated chicken, stirring until it is cooked.
Add cut squash and onion; stir until half cooked, then add mixed sauce.
Keep it at a boil.
Add toasted cashews and chili before you serve.
Serves 6 to 8 people.

_enrichment.description: A savoury stir-fry of tender chicken and cashew nuts with a glossy sauce, featuring a balance of sweet, salty, and umami flavours.

title: Couscous Stir-Fry
ingredients: 1 (12 oz.) pkg. frozen non-cholesterol egg substitute, thawed
directions: About 30 minutes before serving, cut each zucchini lengthwise into quarters, then crosswise into 1/4-inch thick slices.
Dice red pepper. Cut each mushroom into quarters.
In a 2-quart saucepan, heat 2 1/4 cups water to boiling.
Add couscous; cover and let stand 5 minutes.
In a 5-quart Dutch oven over medium-high heat, in 1 tablespoon hot salad oil, cook zucchini and red pepper until tender, stirring occasionally.
Remove to a large bowl.
In 1 more tablespoon hot salad oil, cook the mushrooms until tender. Remove to same bowl.
In 1 more tablespoon very hot salad oil, scramble non-cholesterol egg substitute into nickel size pieces. Add couscous and vegetables to scrambled eggs.
Stir in soy sauce. Spoon into bowl. Garnish with 2 tablespoons chopped green onion, if you like.
Makes 4 servings.

_enrichment.description: A quick and easy couscous stir-fry with crisp vegetables and a savoury soy sauce. A light yet satisfying dish for busy evenings.

title: Pot Stickers
directions: For filling:
Squeeze cabbage to remove as much moisture as possible; place in large bowl.
Add pork, onion, water, soy sauce, cornstarch, ginger, sesame oil and sugar; mix well.
Place 1 rounded teaspoonful filling in center of each pot sticker wrapper.

_enrichment.description: Homemade pot stickers with a juicy pork filling, featuring ginger, garlic, and sesame oil. A delicious and comforting dish.

title: Chow Mein Chop Suey
ingredients: 4 to 5 pieces pork steaks, sliced in 2-inch strips
1 pkg. dried mushrooms, soaked until soft and sliced
directions: Boil Samin with 2 tablespoons of bacon drippings until done. Rinse in cold water and drain.
Samin should be separated and set aside.
Brown strips of pork steaks, including bones (for flavor). Add carrots and water, covering carrots, cooking about 15 minutes on medium heat, then add string beans and mushrooms.
Cook another 15 minutes, then add onions last, about 10 minutes.
Add more water to cover vegetables and simmer about 10 minutes.
Mix shoyu and cornstarch to make gravy (slightly thick); set aside on low heat.
Saute Samin in sesame seed or olive oil until lightly browned.
Spread Samin on platter and pour chop suey over.

_enrichment.description: A hearty chow mein with tender pork, crisp vegetables, and a glossy soy-based sauce. A satisfying and flavourful dish.

title: Stir-Fry Ground Meat With Celery And Broccoli
ingredients: 4 stalks celery, cut in 1/4-inch slanted slices
directions: In heavy large skillet or wok at high heat stir-fry meat until done, then add vegetables and stir-fry until broccoli turns color, about 2 or 3 minutes.
Add spices and remaining ingredients, except cornstarch and water.
Stir to blend.
Add the cornstarch mixture and reduce heat to simmer.
Cook until sauce thickens. Remove from heat and serve over rice or noodles.

_enrichment.description: A quick and easy stir-fry of ground turkey with crisp celery and broccoli in a glossy soy-based sauce. A healthy and satisfying meal.

title: Chinese Noodles With Broccoli(Low-Fat)
directions: Heat sesame oil in a saucepan.
Add the minced garlic; cook, stirring, over medium-high heat until garlic is just golden.
Stir in ginger; continue cooking for 5 minutes.
Stir in chicken broth and hoisin sauce.
Bring to a boil, reduce heat and simmer 2 to 3 more minutes.
Serve over pasta with a sprinkling of low sodium soy sauce and a scattering of chopped scallions or red pepper flakes.
Toss broccoli with pasta.

_enrichment.description: A light and aromatic noodle dish with tender broccoli and a savoury hoisin sauce. A quick and satisfying meal.

title: Chicken Teriyaki
ingredients: 1 Tbsp. freshly grated ginger or 1 tsp. ground ginger
2 medium green peppers, cut into 1-inch squares
1 1/2 lb. chicken breasts, boned, skinned and cut into 4 x 1/2-inch strips
directions: Combine first 6 ingredients in large shallow dish; stir with ladle.
Add vegetables and chicken; toss to coat.
Let marinate in refrigerator for several hours.
Toss occasionally.
Thread vegetables on 12 (6-inch) skewers alternating vegetables and chicken, weaving chicken strips in an accordion fashion.
Broil about 6 inches from heat, about 3 minutes on each side.
Baste chicken and vegetables thoroughly with marinade while broiling. Makes 6 servings (2 skewers each).

_enrichment.description: A Japanese-inspired dish featuring chicken marinated in a sweet and savoury teriyaki sauce, grilled on skewers with colourful vegetables for a vibrant and flavourful meal.

title: Teriyaki Meatballs
ingredients: 1 1/2 c. quick oats or bread crumbs
directions: Mix first 6 ingredients and form into small balls.
Bake 10 minutes at 400°; turn and bake 5 more minutes.
Combine and heat brown sugar, vinegar, seasoning, green pepper and pineapple.
When sugar dissolves, stir in bouillon and cornstarch.
Cook, stirring constantly until thick and clear.
Stir in meatballs and heat thoroughly.

_enrichment.description: Juicy meatballs coated in a tangy lemon teriyaki glaze, baked to perfection and served with a sweet and spicy pineapple sauce for a delightful contrast.

title: Teriyaki Beef Broil
directions: Partially freeze steak, slice diagonally across grain into 1/4-inch strips.
Place steak in shallow container with soy sauce, vegetable oil, molasses, ginger, mustard and garlic.
Cover and chill for 1 hour.
Thread steak on 9-inch bamboo skewers.
Place skewers on lightly greased rack in a broiler pan.
Broil 4-inches from heat for 6 to 7 minutes, turning once.

_enrichment.description: Tender strips of beef marinated in a rich teriyaki sauce, threaded onto skewers and broiled for a quick and flavourful meal.

title: Teriyaki Microwave Chicken
directions: Combine water, soy sauce and teriyaki sauce in a deep microwavable bowl or casserole dish.
Place chicken breast side up in bowl.
Sprinkle onion, green pepper and spices over chicken. Marinate 15 minutes to 1/2 hour.
Turn chicken breast side down before placing in microwave.
Microwave on High 7 minutes per pound (average 35 minutes).
Turn breast side up halfway through cooking time.
Liquid left in bowl can be simmered for fat removal and thickened for gravy.
Serve over rice.

_enrichment.description: A convenient microwave method for cooking chicken in a teriyaki sauce, infused with aromatic spices for a quick and easy meal.

title: Cabbage Ramen Slaw
directions: Brown last 4 ingredients together.
Mix cabbage, onions, chicken, noodles and almonds, then sprinkle seasoning from noodles and mix.
Add oil and vinegar and mix again.
The flavors mix together better if it's refrigerated overnight.

_enrichment.description: A crunchy and refreshing slaw made with cabbage, ramen noodles, and almonds, tossed in a tangy dressing for a light and satisfying side dish.

title: Japanese Fruitcake
directions: Cream butter and gradually add sugar at medium speed on electric mixer.
Add eggs, one at a time, beating well each time. Combine flour and baking powder.
Add and mix after alternately adding flour to creamed mixture with milk, beginning and ending with flour.
Stir in vanilla.

_enrichment.description: A moist and spiced fruitcake with a lemon coconut frosting, perfect for festive occasions or as a comforting dessert.

title: Skillet Chicken Teriyaki
directions: Mix soy sauce, water, sugar, garlic and ginger together. Saute chicken in oil, about 10 minutes.
Remove chicken; drain oil.
Return chicken to pan; pour sauce over the pieces.
Bring to a boil, then cover.
Simmer for 20 minutes, turning occasionally. Serve with sauce over rice.

_enrichment.description: A simple skillet dish featuring chicken simmered in a homemade teriyaki sauce, served over rice for a comforting and flavourful meal.

title: Low-Fat Teriyaki Potatoes
ingredients: 1 1/2 lb. new potatoes (about 10) or about 5 medium red potatoes
directions: Scrub potatoes.
Cut new potatoes into quarters or the medium potatoes into 1-inch pieces.
Place the potatoes in a 1 1/2-quart microwave-safe casserole.
Add the margarine, teriyaki sauce, garlic salt, Italian seasoning and black and red pepper; toss to combine.
Cover and cook on High for 12 to 15 minutes or until the potatoes are tender, stirring twice during the cooking.
Stir again before serving.
Garnish with the rosemary, if desired.
May be served as is or with nonfat sour cream.
Serves 5.
Contains 155 calories and 2 grams fat per serving.

_enrichment.description: Low-fat potatoes tossed in a teriyaki glaze with aromatic herbs, microwaved until tender for a quick and healthy side dish.

title: Teriyaki Potatoes
ingredients: 1 Tbsp. margarine or butter, cut into pieces
directions: Wash potatoes, then cut into 1-inch pieces.
Place potatoes in a 1 1/2-quart microwave-safe casserole.
Add margarine or butter, teriyaki sauce, garlic salt, Italian seasoning and the red and black pepper.
Toss to combine.
Cover; microcook on High for 12 to 15 minutes (14 to 16 minutes for low-wattage ovens), or until potatoes are tender, stirring twice during cooking.
Stir before serving.
Garnish with rosemary.
Makes 5 side dish servings.

_enrichment.description: Classic teriyaki-glazed potatoes with a blend of herbs and spices, microwaved until tender for a simple and satisfying side dish.

title: Teriyaki Shish Kebabs
ingredients: 2 lb. boneless beef sirloin steak (1 1/2 inches thick), cut into 1 1/2-inch cubes
1/2 fresh pineapple, trimmed and cut into 1-inch chunks
2 to 3 small zucchini, cut into 1-inch chunks
1/2 lb. whole fresh mushrooms (medium size work best)
1 large green or sweet red pepper, cut into 1-inch pieces
directions: Combine the first 5 ingredients; toss with beef.
Cover; refrigerate overnight.
Drain beef, reserving marinade.
Thread meat, pineapple and vegetables alternately on long skewers.
Grill over hot coals for 15 to 20 minutes, turning often, or until meat reaches desired doneness and vegetables are tender.
Simmer the marinade in a small saucepan over low heat for 15 minutes.
Remove meat and vegetables from skewers.
Serve with marinade.

_enrichment.description: Grilled beef and vegetable skewers marinated in a teriyaki sauce, served with a simmered glaze for a smoky and sweet dish.

title: Teriyaki Chicken Bits
ingredients: 2 boneless skinless chicken breasts, cut into 1-inch pieces
directions: Combine teriyaki sauce and sugar in small bowl.
Add chicken and marinate 30 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Remove chicken; reserve 2 tablespoons of marinade.
Combine reserved marinade, water and cornstarch; set aside.
Heat oil in hot wok or skillet over medium heat.
