Stir-fry chicken and sesame seed for 2 minutes. Add cornstarch mixture.
Cook and stir until mixture boils and thickens and chicken is tender, about 1 minute.

_enrichment.description: Quick stir-fried chicken pieces coated in a glossy teriyaki sauce, garnished with sesame seeds for a simple and flavourful dish.

title: Teriyaki Kabobs
ingredients: 2 Tbsp. rice wine or chicken broth
directions: Place chicken chunks and marinade in Ziploc bag and squeeze out air.
Refrigerate 3 to 4 hours.
Alternate on skewers with any or all of the following:
small onions or onion chunks, whole fresh mushrooms, green pepper chunks, small canned potatoes, pineapple chunks or tomato wedges.
Cook on a barbecue grill or broil 3 to 4 inches from heat, turning after 5 to 6 minutes. Serve with sweet and sour sauce.

_enrichment.description: Chicken and pineapple skewers marinated in a teriyaki sauce, grilled or broiled for a sweet and tangy barbecue-style dish.

title: Beef Teriyaki Kabobs
ingredients: 2 Tbsp. dry sherry or apple juice
1 tsp. grated ginger root or 1/4 tsp. ground ginger
directions: Partially freeze beef.
Thinly slice across the grain into long strips 1/8 inch thick.
For marinade, combine soy sauce, sherry, molasses, mustard, ginger root, garlic and 1/3 cup water. Pour over meat.
Marinate for 15 minutes at room temperature.

_enrichment.description: Thinly sliced beef marinated in a teriyaki sauce, threaded onto skewers and cooked for a quick and flavourful meal.

title: Ramen Salad
ingredients: 4 to 5 diced green onions and stems
3 Tbsp. sesame seed, toasted with almonds
directions: Mix diced cabbage, crumbled noodles, onions, almonds, seed and chestnuts.
Mix together packet of chicken flavoring from Ramen package, oil, vinegar and sugar.
Pour over cabbage mixture and toss.
Can be made well in advance.

_enrichment.description: A crunchy and savoury salad made with cabbage, ramen noodles, and almonds, tossed in a tangy dressing for a light and satisfying dish.

title: Teriyaki Hamburgers
ingredients: 2 tsp. grated fresh ginger root or 1/2 tsp. dry powdered garlic
directions: Combine soy, pineapple juice, garlic, ginger, Worcestershire and sugar.
Make 8 hamburger patties and place in plastic bag along with the pineapple slices.
Pour in marinade, seal and refrigerate for 1 to 2 hours.
Drain patties and pineapple reserving marinade for basting.
Grill and serve with lettuce and green pepper rings.
Serves 8.

_enrichment.description: Juicy hamburgers marinated in a teriyaki and pineapple glaze, grilled and served on buns for a sweet and savoury meal.

title: Shrimp Tempura Batter
directions: Combine flour, cornstarch, baking powder and baking soda in a bowl.
Add salt, egg and water; stir until flour is moistened. Batter should be slightly lumpy.
Dip shrimp in batter.
May also be used with broccoli and sweet potatoes.

_enrichment.description: A light and crispy batter for frying shrimp, perfect for a quick Japanese-inspired starter or snack.

title: Japanese Fruit Cake
directions: Mix eggs, milk, flour, sugar and butter together.
Put 1/3 mixture in greased and floured 9-inch cake pan.
Mix last 6 ingredients.
Add to the other 2/3 of batter.
Bake in two 9-inch cake pans at 325°.

_enrichment.description: A spiced fruit cake with raisins and pecans, baked in two layers for a festive dessert.

title: Ramen Cole Slaw
directions: Mix the oil, vinegar and sugar with the seasoning packet for the dressing.
Mix cabbage, red peppers, nuts and onions with dressing.
Add broken Ramen noodles just before serving.

_enrichment.description: A crunchy and tangy slaw with Ramen noodles, nuts, and a sweet-sour dressing for a refreshing side.

title: Chicken Meatballs Japanese Style
directions: Mix chicken, 1 tablespoon sugar, 1 tablespoon soy sauce and egg in mixing bowl.
Sprinkle on cornstarch and stir in well.
In a saucepan, combine the broth, remaining soy sauce, sugar and sherry.
Bring to a boil.
Form meatballs with hands or drop from spoon into boiling broth.
Poach 5 to 6 minutes.
Remove with slotted spoon.
You may then poach green beans or other vegetables in broth.
Thicken broth with a cornstarch and water paste, if desired.
Serve over rice.

_enrichment.description: Tender chicken meatballs poached in a savoury broth, infused with Japanese flavours.

title: Teriyaki Beef Jerky
directions: Mix sauce well and pour over thin sliced beef.
Let marinate at least 4 hours.
Place strips on wire rack over cookie sheet. Bake at 150° for 11 to 12 hours.
Blot excess grease.
Store in container with a lid at room temperature.

_enrichment.description: Lean beef strips marinated in teriyaki sauce and baked until chewy and flavourful.

title: Teriyaki Chicken
ingredients: 2 1/2 tsp. ground ginger or 1 Tbsp. freshly ground ginger
1 tsp. garlic powder or 2-3 cloves garlic, crushed
directions: In a disposable plastic bag, combine soy sauce, sugar, vegetable oil, ginger, garlic powder, black pepper.
Mix until sugar is dissolved.
Wash and cut chicken wings and add to the soy sauce marinade.
Marinate for at least 1 hour or longer.
Place chicken wings on cookie sheet.
Discard marinade.
Bake at 400° for 35-40 minutes.

_enrichment.description: Chicken wings marinated in a sweet-savoury teriyaki sauce, then baked for a sticky glaze.

title: Chicken Teriyaki Chunks
ingredients: minced garlic to taste (I use 2 Tbsp.)
directions: Mix together soy sauce, honey, ginger and garlic.
Simmer in saucepan.
Cut chicken into 1-inch chunks.
Dip chicken in beaten egg, then dip chicken into flour.
Fry the coated chicken nuggets in oil until browned.
Dip chicken nuggets into soy sauce mixture, then bake them in 325° oven for 20 minutes.
Sprinkle chicken with sesame seed, if desired.
Serve with rice and whatever sauce remains.

_enrichment.description: Crispy chicken nuggets coated in a teriyaki glaze, baked until golden and sticky.

title: Tempura Batter
directions: Sift dry ingredients in bowl.
Gradually stir in water.
Add fresh vegetables, shrimp, etc.
The batter will be thin. Deep fry until golden brown.

_enrichment.description: A light and airy tempura batter for frying vegetables or seafood to a golden crisp.

title: Ramen Noodle Salad
directions: Cook noodles for 3 minutes and break into small pieces.
Chop or grate cabbage.
Mix drained noodles, cabbage, nuts, chicken and peas in a large bowl.
Mix flavor packages, oil, vinegar and sugar.
Pour over cabbage mixture and blend together.
Top with sunflower seeds and chill.
Makes a lot.

_enrichment.description: A crunchy and refreshing salad with Ramen noodles, cabbage, and a tangy dressing.

title: Tori Age(Fried Chicken - Japanese-Style)
ingredients: 2 lb. boneless thighs, cut in bite size pieces or quartered
2 Tbsp. green onions (white part only), finely chopped
directions: Mix all ingredients thoroughly.
Let stand at least 2 to 3 hours.
Deep fry at 350°.
Serve with mustard/shoyu sauce, if desired.

_enrichment.description: Crispy fried chicken thighs marinated in a savoury-sweet sauce, served with a tangy dip.

title: Baked Mochi
directions: Mix dry ingredients, except potato starch.
Add water and coconut milk gradually until well mixed.
Add vanilla and coloring and pour into greased 9 x 13-inch pan.
Cover with foil and bake in 350° oven for 1 hour.
Do not underbake.
In fact, overbake. Cool completely.
Cut into smaller pieces and roll in potato starch.

_enrichment.description: A chewy and slightly sweet baked mochi cake with a soft, pillowy texture.

title: Japanese Ingredients Used In Recipes
ingredients: 7 oz. beef or pork, thinly sliced
directions: Miso is a seasoning in paste form made from soybeans.
It has a salty and unique flavor and is used primarily in miso soup.
It is also used for marinating meats and fish before cooking and as a seasoning in boiled and sauteed dishes.

_enrichment.description: A guide to essential Japanese ingredients like miso, soy sauce, and sake for authentic flavours.

title: Japanese Fruitcake Pie
directions: Mix melted butter and sugar; let cool to room temperature. Add eggs; mix thoroughly.
Add remaining ingredients and pour evenly into unbaked pie shells.
Bake for 30 to 40 minutes in a 375° oven.

_enrichment.description: A rich and fruity pie with a buttery crust, packed with raisins and nuts.

title: Teriyaki Chicken Kabob
directions: In a large frying pan, put 2 tablespoons of oil.
Heat up first, then put sliced chicken breasts in until brown and slightly cooked.
Then put chopped onion and bell peppers and cook for about 5 minutes.

_enrichment.description: Grilled chicken and vegetable skewers glazed with teriyaki sauce for a sweet-savoury main.

title: Sesame Teriyaki Bites
directions: Cut steak into 24 cubes.
Drain pineapple, reserving 1/4 cup juice.
Combine juice, teriyaki sauce, honey and ground ginger. Marinate steak in mixture overnight.

_enrichment.description: Bite-sized steak cubes marinated in teriyaki and pineapple, served with sesame and tomatoes.

title: Sweet And Sour Teriyaki
directions: Cook rice according to directions on box; set aside.
Brown ground beef in 12-inch skillet; drain.
Mix all ingredients, except rice, in beef.
Bring to a boil, reduce heat, cover and simmer for 25 to 30 minutes.
Pour over cooked rice; mix well.

_enrichment.description: A hearty sweet and tangy beef stir-fry with pineapple and vegetables, served over rice for a comforting meal.

title: Secret Teriyaki Sauce(Enough For 2 Lbs.  Meat)
directions: Combine sugars, shoyu, water, garlic, and ginger in a 1-2 quart saucepan.
Bring to a boil and boil for 5 minutes.
Remove pot from heat and cool.
Whisk in oils to cooled marinade.
Add 2-4 lbs. sliced beef flank or other meat, thinly sliced, and marinate overnight.
Grill meat over charcoal for 3-4 minutes each side.
Serve with rice.

_enrichment.description: A rich, glossy teriyaki marinade for grilling flank steak, balancing sweet and savoury notes.

title: Beef Teriyaki And Rice
ingredients: 3 c. bite size vegetables (broccoli, peppers and mushrooms)
directions: Mix soy sauce, brown sugar and seasonings.
Add beef; let stand 10 minutes to marinate.
Stir-fry beef in hot oil in skillet until browned.
Add vegetables; stir-fry until tender-crisp.
Mix broth and cornstarch; add to skillet.
Bring to a boil.
Boil 1 minute.
Prepare 4 servings of Minute rice as directed on package. Serve beef mixture over rice.
Serves 4.

_enrichment.description: Quick and flavourful beef stir-fry with vegetables, thickened with a cornstarch broth for a glossy finish.

title: Matt'S Teriyaki Chicken
ingredients: 1 1/2 to 2 lb. white or dark chicken cut into 3 inch x 3/4 inch x 3/4 inch strips
directions: Cook chicken to about 4/5 the way done.
Mix in bowl honey, lime juice, teriyaki sauce, garlic, and ginger.
Add sauce to chicken and green onion.
Serve over rice.

_enrichment.description: Juicy chicken glazed with a sticky teriyaki sauce, finished with lime for a bright, tangy kick.

title: Teriyaki Beef Ribs
directions: Separate beef ribs.
Set aside.
Heat and stir remaining ingredients until sugar dissolves.
Let cool.
Marinate ribs in sauce for two hours or overnight.
Next day, lift ribs from marinade, gently shaking off excess.
Barbecue, basting occasionally with remaining marinade.
Makes 6 servings.
(Sauce is also good for Teriyaki Steak.)

_enrichment.description: Tender beef ribs marinated in a spicy-sweet teriyaki sauce, perfect for a weekend barbecue.

title: Venison Teriyaki
ingredients: 2 lb. venison round steak (1 1/2 inches thick maximum)
directions: Slice meat diagonally across grain 1/4 inch deep.
Mix rest of ingredients for marinade.
Pour over meat.
Refrigerate overnight.

_enrichment.description: Venison steak marinated overnight in a rich teriyaki sauce, ideal for a hearty autumn meal.

title: Japanese Spaghetti
directions: In a heavy casserole or pot, cook bacon until crisp.
Remove bacon from pot and drain on paper towels.
Reserve bacon drippings in pot.
Add cabbage to bacon drippings; simmer over low heat, covered, until soft.
Add mushrooms, soy sauce, garlic, seasonings and scallions (including the green tops).
Simmer until vegetables are soft.
Toss with spaghetti and crumbled bacon.
Makes 8 servings.

_enrichment.description: A comforting Japanese-style spaghetti dish with crispy bacon, cabbage, and mushrooms in a savoury sauce.

title: Japanese Fruit Cake(This Recipe Makes Six Thin Layers!)
directions: Cream butter and sugar.
Add eggs, one at a time.
Add milk, flour and baking powder.
Set aside 1/2 the batter.
Take the other half batter and bake 3 layers (8 or 9-inch round pans) at 350° for approximately 20 to 25 minutes.
(Watch - ovens vary!!)
Add the raisins, nuts, cloves and cinnamon to the set aside batter.
Bake these 3 layers the same way as others.

_enrichment.description: A layered Japanese fruit cake with raisins, nuts, and warm spices, baked in thin layers for a delicate texture.

title: Japanese Chicken
directions: Prepare chicken and salt.
Marinate chicken in mixed ingredients for 2 or 3 hours.
Cook at 325°, covered, until tender.
Uncover and cook down.

_enrichment.description: Chicken marinated in soy sauce and spices, slow-cooked until tender and finished with a glossy glaze.

title: Teriyaki Cornish Hens
ingredients: 6 oz. long grain and wild rice mix, cooked
directions: In 1-quart casserole, combine cornstarch, brown sugar, dry mustard and ginger.
Blend in teriyaki sauce and orange juice; stir until smooth.
Cook or microwave until thickened, stirring every minute.
Preheat oven to 350°.
Remove giblets from hens; rinse with cold water and pat dry.
In small mixing bowl, combine rice and apricots.
Stuff hens with rice mixture; close cavities and secure.
In 3-quart oblong glass baking pan, place hens, breast side up, on trivet.
Cook at 350° for 70 minutes in convection oven or longer in regular oven.
Baste with teriyaki sauce every 15 minutes.
Serves 4.

_enrichment.description: Cornish hens glazed with a teriyaki-orange sauce, served with rice and dried apricots for a festive touch.

title: Annette'S Grilled Teriyaki Chicken
ingredients: 4 to 6 boneless skinless breasts of chicken
2 1/2 small cans Hunt's tomato sauce (empty and fill 1/2 can with water)
pastry dough (use No-Fail Pie Crust recipe)
directions: Combine cooking oil and margarine in a large pot and heat. When oil is hot, add onions.
Saute onion mixture until tender and transparent.
Add garlic powder and black pepper.
Stir well to blend seasoning.
Add tomato sauce and water.
Simmer for 20 minutes until a red gray sauce forms.
Cook 10 minutes more.
Set aside.

_enrichment.description: A creamy, tangy chicken dish with a rich tomato-based sauce, perfect for a comforting family meal.

title: Tonkatsu - Pork Cutlets
directions: Slice pork tenderloins into 1/2-inch thick pieces.
Sprinkle with salt and pepper to taste.
Let stand for 10 minutes. Coat with flour. Dip in beaten eggs and dredge lightly in panko.
Deep fry in hot oil until
golden brown.
Garnish with finely shredded cabbage.
Serve with Tonkatsu Sauce.

_enrichment.description: Crispy Japanese pork cutlets served with shredded cabbage and a tangy tonkatsu sauce.

title: Baked Squash Sacramento
ingredients: 1/4 c. chopped or diced roasted almonds
directions: Mash squash; stir in butter, sugar, cream, seasonings and eggs.
Fold in part of almonds, reserving a few for garnish.
Pour into greased 1-quart casserole.
Top with remaining almonds.
Bake in 375° (moderately hot) oven for 30 to 40 minutes.
Makes 6 servings.

_enrichment.description: A creamy, spiced baked squash dish with a crunchy almond topping, perfect for autumn gatherings.

title: Japanese Salad
directions: Combine chicken, lettuce, green onion, almonds and poppy seed. Meanwhile, mix dressing in salad bottle.
Mix sugar, salt, pepper, vinegar and oil.
Pour over salad.
Top with noodles.

_enrichment.description: A refreshing Japanese salad with chicken, lettuce, almonds, and a poppy seed dressing.

title: Chicken Ramen Salad
directions: Brown almonds, sesame seed and sunflower seeds in 2 tablespoons oil.
Chop cabbage, green onion and bell pepper fine. Mix all together.
Add Top Ramen (uncooked), broken into small pieces.

_enrichment.description: A crunchy, nutty salad with cabbage, green onions, and crispy ramen noodles, tossed in a tangy dressing.

title: Teriyaki Steak Skewers
directions: Mix all ingredients well.
Let stand for about 30 minutes. Cut steak into strips about 1/4-inch thick by 1-inch wide.
Add to sauce. Stir to coat. Let stand 2 hours!!

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Teriyaki Steak Skewers.

title: Ginger Chicken Teriyaki
directions: Marinate chicken wings overnight in mixture of soy sauce and grated ginger.
Heat oil and fry wings until well done, dark brown.
Cut up bell pepper, onions and tomatoes; add to chicken. Simmer until done.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Ginger Chicken Teriyaki.

title: Teriyaki Chicken Wings
directions: Mix soy sauce, garlic, ginger, brown sugar, m.s.g. and pepper in large bowl with tight seal.
Add chicken wings; refrigerate for 12 to 16 hours.
Turn bowl upside-down after 6 hours.
After 12 hours, place chicken onto a sheet pan and place in oven at 375° for 45 minutes.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Teriyaki Chicken Wings.

title: Steak Teriyaki
ingredients: 1 1/2-inch piece fresh ginger, peeled and grated
directions: Place steaks in shallow pan.
Mix together, blending well, brown sugar, soy sauce, ginger, garlic and sesame oil.
Pour over steaks.
Cover and refrigerate at least 3 hours or overnight. Turn steaks occasionally.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Steak Teriyaki.

title: Mixed Vegetable Tempura
directions: Pour vegetable oil at least 2-inches deep into wide skillet. Heat to 375°.
Beat the eggs with wire whisk; blend in 1/2 cup water.
Sprinkle the cake flour evenly over the liquid.
Stir in flour with whisk only until moist and large lumps disappear.
Mix the shrimp, mixed vegetables and onion into flour mixture.
Drop by spoonfuls into hot oil.
Fry until lightly golden.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Mixed Vegetable Tempura.

title: Grilled And Chilled Japanese Eggplant
ingredients: 16 small Japanese eggplants (no longer than 5 inches)
1 c. chicken broth (homemade or low sodium canned)
directions: Prepare a charcoal fire.
Grill the eggplants over hot coals until soft, about 10 to 15 minutes, turning frequently.
Remove and set aside until cool enough to handle.
Combine the chicken broth, bonito flakes, soy sauce and rice wine in a saucepan. Simmer for 5 minutes.
Add the ginger and remove from the heat. Use a sharp knife to slice the eggplant lengthwise into 4 slices, without cutting through the stem end.
Fan each eggplant out on a platter and drizzle with the sauce.
Refrigerate until completely cool, or up to 24 hours, before serving.
Yield: 8 servings.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Grilled And Chilled Japanese Eggplant.

title: Chicken Teriyaki(Serves 4)
directions: Heat heavy frying pan.
Grease it with vegetable oil.
Add chicken and saute over high heat evenly until it gets golden brown on both sides.
Combine seasonings for teriyaki sauce.
Drain oil from frying pan.
Pour teriyaki sauce over chicken and cook, covered tightly, over low heat for about 7 minutes.
Turn chicken pieces twice while cooking.
Increase heat and continue cooking, uncovered, glazing chicken on both sides with teriyaki sauce. Reserve some of the sauce to top the chicken when served.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Chicken Teriyaki(Serves 4).

title: Chicken Katsu
directions: Debone chicken thighs.
Place in bowl; add sake and sprinkle with salt.
Let sit for about 10 to 15 minutes.
In a separate bowl, beat 1 to 2 eggs and add salt and pepper to taste.
Dip chicken thighs into egg batter, then roll in panko flakes and deep fry in hot oil.
Cut into bite size strips and serve with tonkatsu sauce if desired.
Makes about 3 to 4 servings.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Chicken Katsu.

title: Flank Steak Teriyaki Barbecue
ingredients: 1/4 c. each: soy sauce and honey
2 Tbsp. each: vinegar and finely chopped onion
directions: Combine all ingredients except steak and pour over flank steak. Marinate 8 hours or overnight.
Turn occasionally.
Barbecue steak over hot coals, turning once until done, about 8 minutes on each side for medium rare.
Baste occasionally while grilling.
Carve slices thin, cutting on diagonal from top to bottom of steak.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Flank Steak Teriyaki Barbecue.

title: Island Teriyaki
directions: Slice steak in 1/4-inch wide strips while still frozen. Marinate in mixture of soy sauce, brown sugar, olive oil, ginger, pepper and garlic.
Leave overnight in refrigerator.
Put on bamboo skewers, weaving meat every 2 inches.
Put on barbecue. Cook to taste.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Island Teriyaki.

title: Top Ramen Salad
directions: Prepare vegetables: shred cabbage, slice onions (including stems), grate carrots, use uncooked frozen peas, dice celery and drain water chestnuts.
Toss together.
Add chopped, cooked chicken (2 cans prepared).
Break up Ramen (uncooked) in small pieces, using handle of knife before opening package.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Top Ramen Salad.

title: Neil Stuart'S Japanese Clam And Water Chestnut Chowder(Serves 4)
directions: Melt the butter in a saucepan over moderate heat.
Add leeks and garlic and cook, stirring occasionally, for 3 minutes.
Add the mushrooms and continue cooking for 3 additional minutes.
Stir in flour until it absorbs all the liquid in the pot, then add teriyaki, clam juice, chopped clams, water chestnuts and green onions.
Bring the mixture to a boil and, lowering the heat, simmer for 20 minutes.
Add lemon juice and black pepper and serve.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Neil Stuart'S Japanese Clam And Water Chestnut Chowder(Serves 4).

title: Tonkatsu
directions: Take the soy sauce and dip 1 spoon of sugar in sauce; mix. Put pork cutlet in soy sauce.
Crack egg and stir.
Put pork cutlet in egg and then into bread powder.
Wait 10 minutes or else bread powder comes off.
Put cutlet in boiling oil until it fries properly, not too soft or too hard. Slice cabbage and put on dressing.
Serve with tonkatsu sauce, cabbage and rice.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Tonkatsu.

title: Mom'S Japanese Cake
directions: Add together first 6 ingredients.
Take half of recipe and put coconut, cinnamon, cloves and raisins in.
Bake other half plain.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Mom'S Japanese Cake.

title: Japanese Pie
directions: Mix margarine, sugar and eggs.
Add remaining ingredients; pour into unbaked pie shell.
Bake at 325° for 40 to 45 minutes.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Japanese Pie.

title: Japanese Fruit Pies(Makes 3)
directions: Mix all ingredients well.
Pour into 3 unbaked 9-inch pie shells.
Bake at 350° for 40 minutes or until crust edges are golden brown.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Japanese Fruit Pies(Makes 3).

title: Teriyaki-Style Chicken
directions: Mix and whip the ingredients except chicken.
Flatten chicken breast; marinate in sauce several hours or overnight.
Broil, adding some marinade.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Teriyaki-Style Chicken.

title: Chicken Drummettes Teriyaki
directions: Cut large drummette sections from chicken wings and reserve rest for another use.
Combine remaining ingredients in a large, glass dish or plastic bag.
Add the drummettes and marinate. Refrigerate overnight.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Chicken Drummettes Teriyaki.

title: Japanese Cookies
directions: Cream the sugar with the butter.
Add the remaining ingredients.
Shape into two rolls; chill until firm.
Cut 1/4-inch thick and bake 30 minutes at 300°.
Sprinkle with powdered sugar while warm.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Japanese Cookies.

title: Yakitori(Japanese)
ingredients: 1 small piece fresh ginger root, grated
directions: Soak chicken in combined sauces for 20 minutes or longer.
Put 4 or 5 pieces of chicken on bamboo stick.
Simmer down remaining sauce until quantity is half.
May need to use a little cornstarch in water to thicken.
Broil on charcoal grill.
Brush several times.
(Buy mirin in Oriental food store; purchase Oriental grater.
Saki is found in wine specialty stores.)

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Yakitori(Japanese).

title: Teriyaki Burgers
ingredients: 1/4 tsp. crushed ginger root or 1/8 tsp. ground ginger
directions: Shape ground beef into 4 patties, each about 3/4 inch thick. Mix remaining ingredients.
Spoon onto patties.
Turn patties; let stand 10 minutes.
Grill patties about 4 inches from medium coals, turning once, until desired doneness, 5 to 7 minutes on each side for medium.
Serve on toasted sesame seed buns.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Teriyaki Burgers.

title: Ramen Skillet
directions: Brown ground beef.
Sprinkle seasoning packets on cooking ground beef.
Drain off grease.
Add tomato sauce, salsa and onion. Stir well, then add broken Ramen noodles.
Stir in just a little. Cover and simmer until noodles are tender, stirring occasionally. When noodles are done, stir until all is combined.
Sprinkle grated cheese on top.
Cover, just long enough to melt.
Serve with salad and garlic bread.
Serves 4 to 6.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Ramen Skillet.

title: Teriyaki Salmon
directions: Preheat broiler.
Place salmon fillets on rack in broiling pan.
In cup mix teriyaki sauce, ginger root and sesame oil. Brush mixture over salmon fillets.
Broil salmon 3 to 5 inches from source of heat, without turning until fish flakes easily when tested with a fork, about 10 minutes.
Garnish with parsley sprigs.
Makes 4 main dish servings.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Teriyaki Salmon.

title: Yakitori(Japanese Skewered Chicken)
ingredients: 2 chicken breasts, cooked and deboned and cut into 1-inch pieces
directions: Combine sake (sherry), soy sauce and sugar in medium saucepan. Pare the ginger and cut into paper thin slices; add to saucepan. Bring to boiling.
Lower heat and simmer 15 minutes, or until sauce is reduced to 1 1/4 cups.
Preheat broiler or hibachi.
Dip skewered chicken pieces into sauce.
Broil 2 minutes.
Brush again with sauce and serve garnished with watercress.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Yakitori(Japanese Skewered Chicken).

title: Easy Chicken Teriyaki
ingredients: 2 chicken breast halves, skinned and fat removed
directions: Stir all the ingredients, except the chicken, together in a baking dish or pan.
Add the chicken pieces and turn a few times to coat well with the sauce.
With the flesh side facing down in the sauce, cover and bake at 375° for 45 to 50 minutes.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Easy Chicken Teriyaki.

title: Teriyaki Chicken Salad
ingredients: 1 pound boneless, skinless chicken cut into small strips
1 red bell pepper - roasted or 1 jar of roasted red bell peppers
1 bottle `Annie's Shiitake and Sesame Vinaigrette' dressing
directions: Marinate chicken in plastic storage bag with enough Teriyaki sauce to cover all pieces.
Place in refrigerator for 30 minutes to 2 hours.
Cook marinated chicken in olive oil in skillet on medium heat until for about 10 minutes or until chicken is done (no pink inside).
Let chicken cool.
If you don't have a jar of red peppers, roast red bell pepper on grill or in skillet, turning about every 2 minutes until skin turns mostly black.
Let cool and peel off skin.
Slice pepper into 1/4 inch strips.
If you have jar of peppers, take them out one at a time and slice them into 1/4 inch strips until you have about one cup.
Cut or shred chicken into small bite size pieces.
Cut quartered artichoke hearts in half.
Take off any `tough' leaves.
In a large bowl, add lettuce, chicken, peppers, artichokes, cheese and noodles.
Toss well and serve with dressing on the side.
Serves 4 - 6 people.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Teriyaki Chicken Salad.

title: Gau (Chinese Mochi Cake)
ingredients: 5 c. Mochiko flour (need 2 boxes; 1 box holds 3 1/4 c.)
1 1/2 c. coconut milk, thawed (12 oz. can frozen)
directions: Combine Mochiko, sugar and baking soda.
Add coconut milk and water.
Mix well.
Spray a 9 x 13-inch pan with Pam.
Pour batter into a greased pan and top with sesame seed.
Bake at 350° for 60 minutes.
Cool and cut into squares.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Gau (Chinese Mochi Cake).

title: Sigiyaki(Fried Eggplants In Miso Sauce)
ingredients: 4 Japanese eggplants or 2 small western eggplants
directions: Slice eggplants about 1/2 inch thick.
Mix miso, honey, Sake, ginger and water in a pot and simmer over low heat, stirring for about 3 minutes.
Remove from heat.
Dry eggplant slices with a towel.
In a skillet, heat the sesame oil.
Add eggplant slices. Cover and fry both sides until brown.
Arrange eggplant slices on a plate.
Pour the miso sauce on top.
Sprinkle with sesame seed.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Sigiyaki(Fried Eggplants In Miso Sauce).

title: Shrimp Tempura
directions: Blend and refrigerate 30 minutes.
Heat 3-inches oil in deep pot or 2-inches if using frypan.
Test oil with drop of batter. When drop goes to bottom of pan and pops back up light brown, oil is hot enough.
Dip cleaned, shelled shrimp in batter and fry until done.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Shrimp Tempura.

title: Teriyaki Walnuts
directions: In 15 x 10-inch jelly roll pan, mix well sugar, ginger and garlic salt.
Set aside.
In large bowl with fork, beat egg whites with teriyaki sauce until blended.
With rubber spatula, stir in walnuts until well coated.
Transfer to jelly roll pan.
Stir with spatula until nuts are coated with sugar mixture.
Bake in preheated 250° oven.
Stir occasionally until coating is dry. Store airtight for up to 3 weeks.
Makes 2 cups.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Teriyaki Walnuts.

title: Micro Chicken Teriyaki
directions: Mix together all ingredients except chicken.
In an 11 3/4 x 7 1/2 x 1 3/4-inch dish, add chicken and coat with sauce.
Marinate 30 minutes or overnight in refrigerator.
Drain off most of the sauce.
Cover with wax paper.
Microwave on High for 22 minutes, or until tender.
Turn the chicken over and brush with drippings halfway through cooking.
Let stand 5 minutes before serving.
If using chicken breasts, reduce time.
Watch carefully.

_enrichment.description: A quick and easy Japanese-inspired chicken dish with a sweet and savoury teriyaki glaze, perfect for a weeknight meal.

title: Japanese Fruitcake 1
directions: Cream butter and sugar until fluffy.
Add egg yolks and beat until smooth.
Alternate milk and sifted dry ingredients.
Add vanilla; beat until very smooth.
Fold in stiffly beaten egg whites.
Divide batter into 2 parts.
Put 1/3 in one bowl and 2/3 in another.
To the 1/3 add cinnamon, cloves, allspice and 1/4 pound raisins.
Put batter into greased and floured 9-inch pans.

_enrichment.description: A rich and spiced Japanese fruitcake with a moist texture, ideal for festive occasions or as a comforting dessert.

title: Jello Mochi
ingredients: 2 cans soda (use same flavor as jello)
directions: Combine dry ingredients.
Add unchilled soda, eggs and butter. Mix well.
Line pan with waxed paper and bake at 350° for 1 hour. Cool and cut.
Yield: 1 (9 x 13-inch) pan.

_enrichment.description: A playful fusion dessert combining Jello and mochi for a colourful, chewy treat that’s fun to make and eat.

title: Japanese Fruit Pie
directions: Mix all ingredients together in order given.
Pour into unbaked 9-inch
pie
shell	and bake at 300° for 30 to 40 minutes.

_enrichment.description: A simple Japanese-inspired fruit pie with a creamy, tangy filling, best served warm as a comforting dessert.

title: Japanese Fruit Pies
directions: Blend
eggs, sugar and butter.	Stir in raisins, coconut and pecans.
Mix
well	and
add
vinegar, continuing to stir. Pour into pie shells.
Bake 35 to 45 minutes at 350°.

_enrichment.description: A no-fuss Japanese fruit pie with a rich, custard-like filling, perfect for a quick yet satisfying dessert.

title: Japanese Fruitcake Pie(Makes 2)
directions: Mix
butter,
sugar and eggs together (after butter has cooled to
room temperature).
Mix thoroughly.
Add remaining ingredients
and pour evenly into unbaked pie shells.
Bake 30 to 40 minutes in a 350° oven.

_enrichment.description: A decadent Japanese fruitcake pie with a buttery, moist filling, ideal for sharing at gatherings or as a festive treat.

title: Teriyaki Steak Marinade
directions: Mix together.
Pour over meat.
Marinate several hours or overnight.
Barbecue or broil meat.

_enrichment.description: A versatile teriyaki marinade for steak, balancing sweet, tangy, and umami flavours for a delicious grilled dish.

title: Japanese Mock Sukiyaki
directions: Brown meat in 2 tablespoons oil.
Add broth, vegetables and sauce.
Cover
and	simmer	10
minutes.
Combine
1/4 cup water and cornstarch.
Stir in sauce and serve over rice.

_enrichment.description: A Japanese-inspired sukiyaki-style dish with tender beef and vegetables in a savoury broth, perfect for a cosy meal.

title: Chicken Ramen
ingredients: 1 pkg. Campbell's chicken flavored Ramen noodle soup
directions: Cook chicken, debone and cut into small pieces (reserve 1/2 cup
liquid).
Cook
noodles; drain and add packet.
Cook broccoli;
drain.

_enrichment.description: A creamy, comforting chicken ramen dish with a curry twist, combining noodles, chicken, and vegetables for a hearty meal.

title: Japanese Fruit Cake Pie
directions: Beat eggs.
Add other ingredients.
Pour into 2 unbaked pie shells.
Bake at 300° for 50 to 55 minutes.

_enrichment.description: A light and fruity Japanese fruitcake pie with a delicate custard filling, ideal for a summer dessert or afternoon tea.

title: Light Tempura Batter
directions: In bowl, stir together first 5 ingredients.
Add water and egg.
Stir until smooth.
May add basil or dill.
Use for shrimp, chicken and all kinds of vegetables.
Heat oil to 375°.

_enrichment.description: A crisp and airy tempura batter that coats seafood and vegetables beautifully, ensuring a light and crunchy texture.

title: Pipinola Narazuke
directions: Peel
pipinola	skin; cut into squares.
Soak in salted water for
day.
Pat dry the pipinola; soak into sake kasu sauce
for 4	to
5 days.
Rinse the sake kasu; cut into bite size pieces and serve.

_enrichment.description: A traditional Japanese pickled pipinola dish with a sweet and tangy flavour, perfect as a side or condiment.

title: Tempura
directions: Slice vegetables to desired thickness or leave whole or into bite
size chunks.
For vegetables, can use string beans, onion, cauliflower,
broccoli,
eggplant
or asparagus or try something new.

_enrichment.description: A classic Japanese tempura with crispy battered vegetables and seafood, served with a dipping sauce for a delightful dish.

title: Cole Slaw With Ramen Noodles
ingredients: 2 pkg. noodles from Ramen 3 oz. soup mix, broken into pieces
directions: Heat oil.
Lightly brown almonds, sesame seed and Ramen noodles.
Set aside.
Thinly shred cabbage.
Chop fine the green onions and green pepper.
Combine all together.

_enrichment.description: A crunchy and refreshing coleslaw with toasted almonds and ramen noodles, adding a unique texture to a classic side.

title: Hawaiian Teriyaki Steak
ingredients: chuck beef roast, cut in thin strips, partially frozen (easier to cut)
directions: Mix together soy sauce, sugar, ginger and garlic powder.
Add meat strips and marinate overnight in refrigerator.
Cook on barbecue grill on bamboo sticks after draining.

_enrichment.description: A Hawaiian-inspired teriyaki beef dish with a sweet and savoury glaze, grilled to perfection for a flavourful main course.

title: Marinated Cod Teriyaki(Microwave)
directions: In small bowl combine teriyaki sauce, water, sherry, garlic and ginger.
Arrange fillets in 8 x 8-inch baking dish.
Pour mixture over fillets.
Turn fish over to coat both sides.
Cover and refrigerate 1 hour.
Microwave uncovered on High for 4 to 5 minutes (flakes easily with fork).

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Marinated Cod Teriyaki(Microwave).

title: "Grilled" Or "Broiled" Teriyaki Chops(Serves 4)
directions: Whisk together all ingredients, except chops.
Marinate chops in mixture in refrigerator for 1 to 2 hours.
Remove.
Broil or grill chops for 12 to 15 minutes.
Brush with marinade; turn once.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for "Grilled" Or "Broiled" Teriyaki Chops(Serves 4).

title: Chicken Tempura Delight
ingredients: 2 lb. chicken breasts, cut into 1-inch pieces
directions: Mix first 4 ingredients and chill for 1 hour.
Dip chicken in batter, then roll in flour. Brown in oil and drain.
Serve with sweet and sour sauce.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Chicken Tempura Delight.

title: Teriyaki Beef Bits
ingredients: 1 lb. beef tenderloin or flank steak
directions: Cut beef into 1-inch cubes.
Combine sherry, honey, soy sauce and lemon juice in deep bowl.
Add meat and stir to coat well. Marinate for 30 minutes.
Spread meat pieces apart in baking pan. Bake at 475° for 6 to 8 minutes.
Makes 3 dozen.
Great served with steamed rice.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Teriyaki Beef Bits.

title: Beef Dish - Japanese Style
directions: Trim fat from beef and cut up into pieces a little larger than bite size.
Brown quickly, cover and simmer on low heat for about 1 hour.
Do not add any liquids.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Beef Dish - Japanese Style.

title: Beef Teriyaki
ingredients: 1 tsp. finely chopped ginger or 1/2 tsp. ginger powder
8 oz. steaks, cut in strips 1/4 inch thick
directions: Combine all ingredients but the steaks and mix well.
Pour over steak strips.
Marinate overnight in refrigerator.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Beef Teriyaki.

title: Miso Soup
directions: Cook shrimp in water for 10 minutes.
Bring water to a boil and let simmer.
Put miso in strainer (over a pot) and pour shrimp water through until all miso is dissolved.
Cut tofu into 1/2-inch cubes and add to stock.
Bring to a boil.
Remove from fire and add finely chopped green onions.
Saimin noodles may also be added at the same time as the tofu for a heavier soup.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Miso Soup.

title: Japanese Style Spareribs
directions: Cut spareribs into serving size pieces.
Place in baking dish. Combine remaining ingredients and pour over spareribs.
Let marinate for one hour.
Bake at 350° for 1 hour.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Japanese Style Spareribs.

title: Teriyaki Grilled Chicken
directions: Preheat grill.
In a shallow dish, combine soy sauce, sherry, oil and ginger.
Place chicken in marinade and let stand 5 minutes on each side. Grill until browned on both sides and cooked through, about 7 to 10 minutes a side.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Teriyaki Grilled Chicken.

title: Teriyaki Meat Marinade
directions: Mix all ingredients together.
Pour over meat and marinate at room temperature for at least one hour, turning meat several times.
Great on the grill.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Teriyaki Meat Marinade.

title: Teriyaki Steak Strips
ingredients: 2 to 2 1/2 lb. flank steak, cut in 1-inch strips
1/4 c. chopped scallions with tops (or use onion salt)
1 clove garlic, minced or garlic salt
directions: Combine all ingredients
(except flank steak).	Marinate flank steak overnight.
Grill.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Teriyaki Steak Strips.

title: Teriyaki Steak
ingredients: 6 cloves garlic or 3/4 tsp. minced garlic
London broil steak, cut into strips
directions: Marinate overnight.
Grill or broil in oven.
Also great with chicken.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Teriyaki Steak.

title: Japanese Pecan Pie
directions: Mix all together.
Put into 9-inch unbaked pie shell.
Bake at 350° for 45 minutes.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Japanese Pecan Pie.

title: Teriyaki Meat Loaf
directions: Mix meat
and
onion.
Combine egg and milk.
Add bread crumbs, then
combine with meat.
Mix soy sauce, sugar, garlic and mayonnaise in bowl.
Stir 1/2 sauce into meat loaf.
Shape meat loaf.
Halfway through cooking, pour remaining sauce over loaf. Bake at 350° for 45 to 50 minutes.
Serve with rice!

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Teriyaki Meat Loaf.

title: Grandpa'S Teriyaki Sauce
directions: Combine all of the ingredients.
Use to marinate 2 to 3 pounds of meat.
Cook as directed.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Grandpa'S Teriyaki Sauce.

title: Teriyaki Veggies
directions: Melt
butter in saucepan; add teriyaki	sauce
and
then spices.
Bring to quick boil, then pour
evenly
over
veggies. Cook 5 to 10 minutes on high before serving.
(Depends
on	how crisp you like vegetables.)
Serves 6 to 8.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Teriyaki Veggies.

title: Teriyaki Sauce
ingredients: 1 Tbsp. sake or any liquor (can be omitted)
directions: Mix ingredients well.
Marinate meat (chicken, beef or ribs) 1 hour.
Can be marinated overnight.
Baste meat with sauce while broiling.
Yields 1 1/4 cups.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Teriyaki Sauce.

title: Bibingka(Baked Mochi Flour)
ingredients: 2 cans (16 oz. each) coconut milk (fresh coconut milk preferable)
directions: Mix all dry ingredients together.
Combine fresh milk and coconut milk.
Add to dry ingredients.
Fold in tsubushian.
Pour into a greased 9 x 13-inch pan.
Bake at 350° for 1 hour.
Cut into desired serving size.
Serve either hot or cold.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Bibingka(Baked Mochi Flour).

title: Hawaiian Baked Mochi
ingredients: 1 can Tsubushi-an (sweet red bean paste; optional)
directions: Preheat oven to 350°.
Grease a 9 x 13-inch pan.
Mix all ingredients together and spread into pan.
Bake at 350° for 1 hour.
Cool and cut into squares.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Hawaiian Baked Mochi.

title: Teriyaki Marinade
ingredients: 1 c. orange juice or pineapple juice
directions: Mix all ingredients together.
This is a good recipe for cheaper cuts of steak.
Also goes well on chicken or ground beef.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Teriyaki Marinade.

title: "Make Your Own" Teriyaki Sauce
ingredients: 1 c. sherry (the real kind) or dilute to 1/2 c. sherry and 1/2 c. water
1 lemon, squeezed and seeded (use juice only)
salt and pepper to taste (to season meats as they are cooking)
1 fresh ham with the bone in it (9 to 14 lb.)
2 Tbsp. corn oil or vegetable cooking oil
2 large onions, peeled and left whole
1 whole chicken or if white meat is preferred, use breast filets
1 can Campbell's cream of chicken soup
2 deep dish pie shells, at room temperature
directions: Preheat oven to 350°.
Remove the skin from the chicken.
Place in a pot with enough water to cover the chicken.
Boil in a medium pot until tender.
Add the Veg-All and the cream of chicken soup. Mix well.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for "Make Your Own" Teriyaki Sauce.

title: Adobe Bread(Brought Up From Ft. Sutter In Sacramento.)
ingredients: 2 small bunches arugula (parsley-like but very spicy)
1 large pkg. Rhodes frozen dinner rolls
directions: Add salt, cream, sugar and oil to warm potatoes.
Cool before adding flour.
Chill.
Divide into balls and roll out one at a time, as thin as possible, adding flour as needed.
Bake on medium to hot griddle, turning often, until done.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Adobe Bread(Brought Up From Ft. Sutter In Sacramento.).

title: Teriyaki
directions: For marinade, combine soy sauce, salad oil, molasses, ginger, dry mustard and minced garlic cloves; mix well.
If marinade is to be used on steak, score meat on both sides.
Marinate overnight and let stand 15 minutes at room temperature before cooking on grill.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Teriyaki.

title: Teriyaki Marinade Sauce
directions: Mix all ingredients.
Pour over meat and let stand at least 2 hours.
Refrigerate.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Teriyaki Marinade Sauce.

title: Japanese Jello
directions: Dissolve together the first 3 ingredients.
Boil together sugar and water.
Add sugar and water mixture to dissolved jello mixture.
Stir well.
Pour into pan; refrigerate until set.
Cut into shapes.
Easy to eat with fingers.
Great for children!!

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Japanese Jello.

title: Yaki Soba
directions: Chop shrimp and scallions.
Cut pork, chicken and beef into small strips.
Heat wok with 2 tablespoons oil.
Add shrimp, pork, chicken and beef.
Stir-fry.
Remove.
To wok, add water.
Add noodles.
Cook until soft (3 to 4 minutes).
Add seasoning (from noodle package), the meat, shrimp, spinach and scallions.
Cook 1 minute.
Remove from heat.
Serve hot.
Serves 2 to 4.
Amount of noodles in recipe may be doubled or tripled.
If so, add an additional 3/4 cup water for each package of noodles.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Yaki Soba.

title: Japanese Honey Cake(Kasutera)
directions: Preheat
oven
to
Beat together eggs, sugar and honey until very thick.
Add flour, beating well.
Pour batter into a greased 9 x 12-inch pan and bake 45 minutes.
But, stir the cake with
a
spoon
after the first 15 minutes of baking time. Continue
baking
in
ordinary
manner
next half hour. Allow cake
to
cool
in	pan.
Dust
cake with confectioners sugar, cut into strips and serve.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Japanese Honey Cake(Kasutera).

title: Thai Noodle Salad
ingredients: 1 bunch scallions, hollow green tops discarded
directions: Mix together in large serving bowl first 5 ingredients to make dressing.
While cooking linguine according to package directions, wash scallions and slice thinly on the diagonal. Trim and wash snow peas.
Drain cooked linguine, add to dressing in bowl and toss well.
Toss in scallions and snow peas.
Toast sesame seed to golden brown in heavy skillet over moderate heat, stirring often.
Toss into salad.
Serve warm or chilled.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Thai Noodle Salad.

title: Tom Kha Kaichicken Coconut Soup
ingredients: 2 to 4 Slices 2 Tbsp. Galangal Root (Laos Root)
directions: Cut up the chicken meat either diced or in strips, chop lemon grass and kaffir leaves in food processor or hand chop finely. Cut the shallots to 1/4 inch strips, cut scallions to 1 inch pieces, chop cilantro with stems, no roots.
Peel and slice the galangal root in thin strips 1/2 inch long.
Heat pan on medium, add the kaffir, lemon grass, shallots, whole dried chilis, and galangal, stir until light brown.
Add the chicken broth and increase the heat to high and bring to a boil.
Add chicken, coconut and milk, fish sauce and 1/2 teaspoon chili paste.
Add the chili paste in increments and taste before adding the second half.
Heat almost to boiling, remove from the heat, add lime juice, cilantro and scallions.
Serves 4.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Tom Kha Kaichicken Coconut Soup.

title: Thai Lemon Grass Soup
ingredients: 10 hot chilies, just broken with pestle
directions: Heat chicken broth in a pot.
Cut lemon grass into 1-inch lengths.
Add lime leaves, lemon grass, galangal and chicken to the broth.
Heat until chicken is cooked through, then add lime juice, fish sauce, chilies and mushrooms.
Simmer for 5 minutes. Garnish with cilantro and serve.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Thai Lemon Grass Soup.

title: Thai Beef Kabobs
directions: Cut meat into strips at least 2 inches thick.
Partially freeze the strips and then cut across the grain in long strips about 1/4-inch thick.
Mix together all other ingredients and use to marinate meat for up to 2 hours.
Be sure to stir occasionally. Skewer meat strips with onion chunks and grill to your liking.

_enrichment.description: Juicy beef skewers marinated in a savoury-sweet Thai-inspired sauce, grilled to perfection and served with fresh herbs. Ideal for a summer barbecue.

title: Pad Thaismall Width Noodles
directions: Soak the noodles in cold water overnight or 15 minutes in hot water.
Shell and devein shrimp.
Chop scallion and cilantro. Grind or crush the peanuts.

Preheat the oil in the WOK on medium-high, then add garlic and shrimp then stir until the shrimp turns white, add the egg and stir till cooked, add the noodles and all of the seasonings, stir or toss well.
Turn heat off, add bean sprouts and scallions.
Serves 4.

_enrichment.description: Classic Thai stir-fried noodles with shrimp, eggs, and a tangy-sweet sauce, garnished with peanuts and fresh herbs. A quick and flavourful dish.

title: Thai Pork And Peanuts(Appetizer)
ingredients: 1 head leafy green lettuce, rinsed and separated
directions: Stir coconut in frying pan until crisp; set aside.
Cook onion, ginger and garlic in oil.
Saut pork in same pan.
Spoon off fat.
Add lime juice, cilantro, peanuts, coconut, chilies and soy sauce.
Put in a bowl and serve with lettuce leaves to wrap pork.

_enrichment.description: A crispy, spicy pork salad with peanuts, lime, and fresh herbs, served in lettuce leaves for a refreshing appetiser.

title: Thai Chicken
directions: Combine first 8 ingredients.
Mix well.
Arrange chicken in shallow baking dish, single layer.
Spread sauce on top. Marinate for 2 hours.

_enrichment.description: Tender chicken marinated in a rich peanut-hoisin sauce, perfect for grilling or baking. A crowd-pleasing dish with bold flavours.

title: Linguini With Thai Chicken
ingredients: 1 lb. skinless boneless chicken breast (cut bite-size)
1 medium onion, sliced thin and separated into rings
directions: For sauce, combine and blend broth, wine, soy, cornstarch, peanut butter and pepper in a bowl and reserve.
Preheat large skillet over high heat.
Add oil.
Stir-fry garlic and ginger 15 seconds.
Add onion; stir-fry 3 minutes.
Remove and reserve vegetables.
Add chicken and stir-fry until done.
Make a well by pushing chicken to sides and stir in sauce in the center.
Cook, stirring until sauce is thickened and bubbly.
Cook 2 more minutes.
Add vegetables until coated and hot.
Serve over noodles with green onions on top and cilantro as additional garnish.

_enrichment.description: Linguine tossed with stir-fried chicken in a creamy peanut sauce, finished with fresh herbs and a hint of spice.

title: Thailand Chicken
directions: Cut chicken into bite size pieces and brown lightly.
Mix remaining ingredients and pour over chicken.
Simmer 15 minutes and serve over rice.

_enrichment.description: A comforting one-pot dish of chicken simmered in a creamy mushroom sauce, served over rice. Simple and satisfying.

title: Thai Fruited Rice
ingredients: cold, cooked rice (6 c. or so)
directions: Stir-fry in 2 tablespoons of oil.
Toss in wok while cooking. Can keep warm in low oven.
Good served with chicken, pork or fish.

_enrichment.description: Fragrant rice tossed with fresh fruit, vegetables, and a light soy dressing. A vibrant and colourful side dish.

title: Thai Cucumber Salad
directions: Trim ends of cucumbers and scrape with fork to make a decorative pattern.
Quarter the cucumbers lengthwise and remove seeds.
Cut them crosswise into thin slices.
Put in bowl and add chili.
In small saucepan, combine sugar and vinegar; bring to boil.
Cook, stirring, until the sugar has dissolved.
Cool to room temperature and pour sauce over the cucumber mixture.
Chill and sprinkle with cilantro.
Serves 4.

_enrichment.description: A refreshing salad of cucumber slices tossed in a sweet and spicy vinegar dressing, finished with fresh herbs.

title: Thai Ribbons
directions: Soak 30 (6-inch) bamboo skewers.
Combine teriyaki sauce, ginger, oil, garlic and red pepper.
Cut steak across grain into 1/4-inch thick slices.
Place meat in plastic bag.
Pour marinade over steak and marinate in bag up to 2 hours.
Thread slices on bamboo skewers.
Place on rack in broiler pan 3 to 4-inches from heat.
Broil 2 minutes on each side.
Sprinkle with sesame seed. (Can substitute chicken or pork.)

_enrichment.description: Thinly sliced beef marinated in teriyaki sauce and grilled on skewers for a quick and flavourful meal.

title: Phat-Thai
directions: Soak the
rice stick (like noodles) in warm water until tender, but
not
soft.
In
a
large wok, saute garlic in hot oil, add
shrimp and cook until just pink.	Remove shrimp, set it aside
and	add
chicken.
Remove chicken after browned and cook the
eggs, scrambled, chopping the eggs with spatula when firm. Remove
the
eggs
and stir-fry the tofu.
Remove tofu, add noodles,
vinegar,	paprika
and
cayenne
pepper
sauce; cook for
minutes, stirring constantly.
Mix in previously cooked ingredients
and bean sprouts.
Toss well and cook for an additional 3 to 4 minutes.	Serve with a topping of crushed peanuts and (optional) lemon wedges on the side.

_enrichment.description: A classic Thai stir-fry of rice noodles with shrimp, chicken, eggs, and tofu, tossed in a tangy-sweet sauce.

title: Mexican Casserole
ingredients: 1 (12 oz.) can pet milk (can use fat free)
directions: In 9 X 13 dish spree fritos or doritos to cover well.
Brown and mix hamburger and onion.
Add rotel, soups and milk.
Pour over chips, cover with cheese and bake at 350 degrees until bubbly (25 to 30 minutes).

_enrichment.description: A hearty Mexican-inspired casserole layered with tortilla chips, seasoned ground beef, and melted cheese. Perfect for a cosy meal.

title: Enchiladas
directions: In a saucepan, combine tomato paste, water, garlic and chili powder; simmer 20 minutes.
In skillet, brown ground beef with half of the onions; drain fat.
Stir in 1 1/2 cups of cheese and 1 cup tomato paste mixture.
Spoon 1/4 cup tomato paste mixture in 3-quart shallow baking dish.
Soften tortillas, one at a time, by dipping in and out of remaining mixture.
Spoon beef mixture down center of each tortilla; roll up.
Place seam side down in baking dish.
Pour remaining tomato paste mixture over top.
Sprinkle with remaining cheese and onions.
Bake at 350° for 20 minutes. Makes 6 servings.

_enrichment.description: Classic beef enchiladas smothered in a rich tomato-chili sauce and melted cheese. A comforting Mexican favourite.

title: Chicken And Tortilla Casserole
directions: Boil chicken; bone and cut up meat.
To the meat, add onion and green pepper.
Mix soups, tomatoes, chili powder and garlic salt.
Dip each tortilla in chicken broth, then in a buttered 9 x 12-inch baking dish.
Place a layer of tortilla, 1/2 the chicken, 1/2 the soup mixture and 1/2 cheese.
Repeat layers; bake 30 to 40 minutes in 350° oven until cheese melts.

_enrichment.description: A layered casserole of tortillas, chicken, and creamy tomato soup, topped with melted cheese. A satisfying family meal.

title: Cream Tacos
directions: Fry hamburger with onion.
Layer corn tortillas (cut in 1/4) in pan.
Put 1 teaspoon hamburger on each piece, 1 teaspoon green chili on top of hamburger, then 1 teaspoon tomato sauce, top with grated cheese.
Repeat layers until all ingredients are gone.
Pour can of milk over all.
Cover with foil.
Bake in oven at 350° for 30 to 35 minutes until milk evaporates.

_enrichment.description: Crispy tacos layered with seasoned beef, green chilli, and melted cheese, baked until bubbly. A quick and tasty meal.

title: Taco Salad
directions: Combine first 7 ingredients in crock-pot; mix well.
Add salt and pepper.
Cook on low for 3 to 4 hours or until done to taste. Heat cheese and tomatoes in saucepan until cheese melts, stirring constantly.
Layer lettuce, ground beef sauce, tomatoes, cheese sauce and crushed corn chips on individual plates.
Yield:
12 servings.

_enrichment.description: A hearty taco salad with seasoned ground beef, melted cheese, and crushed tortilla chips, served over fresh lettuce.

title: Southwest Taco Dip
ingredients: 2 (15 oz. each) cans black beans, drained and rinsed
directions: Combine beans, turkey, onion and chili powder in a skillet. Cook over medium heat until turkey is brown.
Stir occasionally to mash beans and break up turkey.
Stir in salsa and place in microwave-proof dish.
Sprinkle with cheese and microwave on High 1 to 2 minutes until cheese is melted.
Combine sour cream, cilantro, salt and pepper and spread on top.
Sprinkle with avocado, tomato and green onions.
Serve with tortilla chips.
Makes 7 cups.

_enrichment.description: A layered Tex-Mex dip with spiced turkey, black beans, and melted cheese, topped with fresh avocado and herbs. Perfect for sharing with friends.

title: Taco Casserole
ingredients: 2 c. shredded Southwestern Blend cheese (Pepperjack, Cheddar, Monterey Jack)
directions: Preheat oven to 350 degrees.
Combine ground chuck and onions in large skillet. Cook until meat is browned.
Drain excess grease. Add the taco seasoning, salsa, Mexicorn, mayonnaise, and sour cream.
Mix well and remove from heat. In a casserole dish (about 2 quart size), layer half of the meat mixture, chips and cheese. Repeat layers.
Bake for about 20 minutes or until bubbly.

_enrichment.description: A hearty casserole with seasoned beef, salsa, and cheese, baked until bubbly and golden. Ideal for a family dinner.

title: Creamed Tacos
directions: Cook ground beef, onion and bell pepper together in a skillet; drain meat.
In double boiler, mix the Ro-Tel tomatoes, evaporated milk and Velveeta cheese; heat until cheese melts.
Layer ingredients from skillet on bottom of ungreased casserole dish, then spread cheese ingredients on top of meat, then layer flour tortillas.
Repeat layers until all ingredients are used.
Bake together in a 350° oven for 30 minutes.

_enrichment.description: A creamy, cheesy taco bake with ground beef, peppers, and tomatoes, baked until golden and bubbly. A comforting dish for any occasion.

title: Chicken-Mexican Casserole
ingredients: 1 (10 1/2 oz.) can cream of chicken soup
2 large firm, ripe tomatoes, sliced thinly
1 (6 to 8 oz.) pkg. corn chips or tortilla chips
2 (5 oz. each) cans boned chicken, chunked or 2 c. cooked and cubed chicken
directions: In a mixing bowl, place soup, chiles, onion and water.
Stir to blend well.
Lay 1/2 corn chips in 2-quart casserole.
Top with 1 can or 1 cup chunked chicken, then half of the tomato slices. Pour half of the soup mixture over chicken mixture. Sprinkle with half of the cheese.
Repeat layers in same order, ending with cheese.
Bake, uncovered, on High in microwave or reheat 8 to 10 minutes with cover on.
Serves 6.

_enrichment.description: A layered casserole with chicken, corn chips, and cheese, baked until hot and bubbly. Quick and easy to prepare.

title: Tamale Bake Casserole
ingredients: 1/2 c. or 2 oz. shredded sharp cheese
directions: Drain tamales; reserve sauce.
Remove papers and slice tamales crosswise; set aside.
Combine tamale sauce, flour, chili powder and garlic salt.
Add eggs, corn, olives and tamales.
Turn into 10 x 9 x 2-inch dish.
Bake in a 350° oven 40 minutes or until set. Sprinkle cheese on top and bake 3 minutes or until cheese melts.

_enrichment.description: A baked tamale casserole with eggs, cheese, and olives, topped with extra cheese for a rich finish. A unique twist on classic tamales.

title: Baked Vegetables Tamale
directions: Mix corn, tomatoes, corn meal, cheese, onion and green pepper. Let stand 30 minutes.
Add eggs, milk and seasoning.
Pour into buttered baking dish at 325° for 1 1/4 hours or until firm in the center.
This may be served from the dish or may be baked in a loaf and unmolded onto a platter.
Buttered peas may be used as a garnish with sections of hard-cooked eggs on the platter.

_enrichment.description: A baked vegetable tamale with corn, cheese, and eggs, served warm with peas and eggs. A wholesome and filling dish.

title: Guacamole Viva
directions: Peel and mash avocado with a fork in a medium glass bowl.
Add lemon juice and sour cream, mixing well.
Stir in garlic, onion and tomato.
Season with celery salt, oregano and cayenne pepper. Place avocado pit in the center; cover and chill until serving. Remove pit before serving.

_enrichment.description: A creamy, zesty guacamole with avocado, sour cream, and spices, served with tortilla chips. A refreshing starter or snack.

title: King Ranch Chicken Enchiladas
ingredients: 3 chicken breasts (skinless, boneless), cooked and cut into bite-size pieces
directions: Cover a 9 x 13-inch buttered Pyrex dish with the Doritos chips. Cook chicken and cut into bite-size pieces; place over the Doritos. Mix together soups, milk and picante sauce.
Pour over chicken and Doritos.
Sprinkle grated Cheddar cheese on top.
Bake at 350° for 30 minutes.
Best made at least 6 hours before serving.
Serves 6.

_enrichment.description: A Tex-Mex classic with shredded chicken, Doritos, and cheese, baked until crispy and golden. A crowd-pleasing dish for any gathering.

title: Fiesta Salad
directions: Combine cooked rice, tuna, celery, green pepper, lemon juice and onion in a mixing bowl.
Blend the seasonings with the sour cream.
Stir into the tuna/rice mixture.
Chill for 1 hour. Garnish with tomatoes and serve with corn chips.

_enrichment.description: A refreshing salad with rice, tuna, and fresh vegetables, dressed with a creamy, spiced sour cream. Perfect for a light lunch.

title: Chicken Enchiladas
directions: Mix sour cream and soups; add 1/2 the cheese and mushrooms, along with the chiles.
Divide the soup mixture in half and add to the shredded chicken.
Fill the flour tortilla shells and roll. Place in a 9 x 13-inch pan.
Pour the remaining soup mixture over the top of tortillas and sprinkle with green onions and remaining cheese.
Bake for 30 minutes at 350°.

_enrichment.description: A classic enchilada dish with shredded chicken, cheese, and creamy soups, baked until golden and bubbly. A comforting meal for any day.

title: Microwave Chili
ingredients: 2 (8 oz.) cans tomato sauce or 1 (8 oz.) can tomato sauce and 1 c. beef broth or consomme
directions: Combine meat, onion and garlic in a large casserole.
Cover and cook at High setting for 10 minutes or until meat loses its reddish color, stirring once or twice.
Add remaining ingredients, mixing thoroughly.
Cover casserole and heat until mixture comes to a boil.
Reduce microwave temperature to Slow or Defrost and cook for about 1 hour, stirring a few times.
After cooking, allow chili to stand for a few minutes before serving.

_enrichment.description: A quick and easy microwave chili with ground beef, tomatoes, and spices, ready in under an hour. Perfect for a cosy night in.

title: Sour Cream Enchiladas
ingredients: Salt, pepper and garlic to taste
directions: Combine soups, chicken, chilies, sour cream, salt, pepper, and garlic.
Simmer for a few minutes.
Place a small amount on a limp flour tortilla.
Sprinkle with a few green onions and cheese.
Roll tortilla and place in baking dish.
Repeat with desired number of tortillas.
Pour remaining sauce over tortillas. Sprinkle remaining cheese and onions on top. Bake at 350° until bubbly (about 30 minutes).

_enrichment.description: A creamy enchilada bake with chicken, cheese, and green chilies, baked until golden and bubbly. A comforting dish for any occasion.

title: Mexican Garden Casserole
ingredients: 1 c. chopped zucchini or other squash
directions: In medium saucepan, combine corn, onion, green pepper, celery, carrots and water; bring to a boil.
Reduce heat and simmer for 5 minutes, covered.
Do not drain liquid from vegetables.
In a large mixing bowl, combine squash, tomato, 3/4 cup of the cheese, cornmeal, milk, eggs, pepper and hot sauce.
Add undrained vegetables to cornmeal mixture; mix well.
Turn into greased 9 x 12-inch casserole pan.
Top with remaining cheese.
Bake, uncovered, in a 350° oven for 45 to 50 minutes or until heated thoroughly.

_enrichment.description: A vegetable-packed casserole with corn, zucchini, and cheese, baked until golden and bubbly. A wholesome and filling dish.

title: Mexican Bean Dip
directions: In pan, layer refried beans, then guacamole.
Next, mix sour cream, salad dressing and taco seasoning together.
Spread on guacamole.
Layer the green onions, tomatoes, olives and cheese. Serve with tortilla chips.

_enrichment.description: A layered bean dip with guacamole, sour cream, and cheese, served with tortilla chips. A perfect starter for any Mexican feast.

title: Mexican Chocolate
directions: In large saucepan, heat all ingredients except milk and cream over low heat, stirring until chocolate is melted.
Heat to boiling; reduce heat and simmer 4 minutes, stirring constantly. Stir in milk; heat over low heat, stirring occasionally.
Beat with rotary beater until foamy.
Top serving with whipped cream. Makes 8 (2/3-cup) servings.

_enrichment.description: A rich and spiced Mexican hot chocolate with unsweetened chocolate, coffee, and cinnamon, topped with whipped cream. A decadent dessert.

title: Mexican Chicken Casserole
directions: Preheat oven to 350°.
Boil chicken and strip off bone.
Saute onion in 2 tablespoons butter and mix with chicken.
Take 3/4 cup of chicken broth, put in a bowl and add cream of mushroom, cream of chicken soup and Ro-Tel and blend them together.
Take 9 x 13-inch casserole dish.
Put chicken in dish.
Sprinkle layer of chips, layer of cheese mixed together, layer of chips, then pour soup and Ro-Tel mixture on top and layer with rest of cheeses. Bake 30 minutes.

_enrichment.description: A hearty Mexican-inspired casserole with tender chicken, creamy sauces, and melted cheese, baked until bubbly and golden.

title: Mexican Layered Dip
directions: Spread bean dip on shallow serving tray (make sure the sides are at least 1-inch high).
Mash avocados and add lemon juice, salt and pepper.
Spread on top of bean dip.
Mix sour cream, mayonnaise and taco seasoning mix.
Spread on top of avocado layer (or bean dip layer if avocado layer is omitted).
Layer in order: chopped green onions, chopped black olives and tomatoes.
Sprinkle Cheddar cheese on top.
Chill at least 3 hours before serving. Enjoy with Tostitos or Doritos.

_enrichment.description: A layered dip with creamy, tangy, and fresh flavours, perfect for sharing with friends or as a starter.

title: Mexican Dip
ingredients: medium size jar Pace picante sauce
directions: Spread refried beans in bottom of 9 x 9-inch pan.
Mash up avocados; adding lemon juice, garlic salt, salsa and Miracle Whip. Spread that mixture on top of refried beans.
Next, layer of sour cream.
Over sour cream, pour all the salsa.
On top of salsa put chopped green onions, chopped tomato and chopped black olives. Chill in refrigerator.
Serve with chips.
Do not make ahead of time.

_enrichment.description: A creamy and zesty Mexican dip layered with refried beans, avocado, and sour cream, served with crispy tortilla chips.

title: Mexican Style Chicken Kiev
ingredients: 1 (7 oz.) can diced green chiles, divided into 8 portions
1/4 lb. Jack cheese, cut into 8 strips
directions: Pound chicken pieces to about 1/4-inch thickness.
Put chiles and Jack cheese strip in center of each chicken piece.
Roll up and tuck ends under.
Combine bread crumbs, Parmesan cheese, chili powder, salt, cumin and pepper.
Dip each stuffed chicken in shallow bowl of 6 teaspoons melted butter and roll in crumb mixture.
Place chicken, seam side down in 9 x 13-inch baking dish and drizzle with a little melted butter.
Cover and chill 4 hours or overnight.
Bake uncovered at 400°, 20 minutes, or until done. Serve with tomato sauce, if desired.
Makes 8 servings.

_enrichment.description: Succulent chicken rolls stuffed with spicy green chiles and melted cheese, breaded and fried to a golden crisp.

title: Papaya Salsa & Brie With Tortilla Wedges
ingredients: 3 c. Ripe Papaya, peeled and diced
3/4 c. Kiwi Fruit, peeled and diced
4 oz. Round Brie or 8 oz. En Croute
directions: Cut enough tortillas into wedges (like pie) and bake at 350° for 10 minutes, until crisp, then set aside.
Combine the papaya and the next 6 ingredients.
Set aside.
Place the Brie on a baking sheet.
Bake for 10 minutes at 350° until thoroughly heated.
Dip the chips made from the tortillas in the fruity salsa and accompany it with a chunk of warmed cheese.
MMMMMM!!!!!! Serves 16.

_enrichment.description: A sweet and tangy papaya salsa served with warm, melty Brie and crispy tortilla wedges for dipping.

title: Eight Layer Taco Dip Appetizer
ingredients: 1 small can jalapeno peppers, finely chopped (green chile peppers)
1 (6 oz.) can pitted black olives, chopped
directions: In blender or food processor, process beans to a paste consistency.
In a shallow serving dish with a rim, layer the ingredients as follows:
beans, sour cream, avocado dip, cheese, peppers, olives, scallions and tomatoes.
Serve with taco chips.

_enrichment.description: A vibrant eight-layer dip with creamy, cheesy, and fresh ingredients, ideal for sharing at gatherings.

title: Las Palmas Turkey Enchiladas
ingredients: 3 c. (12 oz.) shredded Monterey Jack cheese, divided usage
1 (19 oz.) can Las Palmas green chile enchilada sauce
directions: In small bowl, combine turkey, 2 cups of cheese, green chiles and onion.
In small skillet, bring enchilada sauce to a boil; remove from heat.
Dip each tortilla into heated sauce to soften. Spoon 1/3 cup of turkey mixture and 2 tablespoons of sour cream down center of each tortilla.
Roll.
Place tortillas seam side down in a 12 x 8-inch baking dish.
Pour remaining heated enchilada sauce over top.
Sprinkle with remaining 1 cup of cheese.
Bake 20 minutes at 350°.
Makes 4 servings.

_enrichment.description: Tender turkey enchiladas filled with spicy green chiles and cheese, smothered in a rich enchilada sauce.

title: Vegetable Burritos(Low-Fat)
directions: Cook mushrooms, onion, green pepper and garlic in olive oil in a large, nonstick skillet over medium high heat, stirring constantly until tender.
Remove from heat; drain.
Combine cooked vegetables, drained kidney beans, olives and pepper.
Spoon about 1/2 cup bean mixture evenly down center of each tortilla. Top with 1 tablespoon sour cream, 1 tablespoon salsa and 1 tablespoon cheese; fold opposite sides over filling.
Coat a large nonstick skillet or griddle with cooking spray.
Heat over medium-high heat until hot.
Cook tortilla, seam side down, 1 minute on each side or until thoroughly heated.
Top each tortilla with 1 tablespoon salsa.

_enrichment.description: Low-fat vegetable burritos packed with mushrooms, peppers, and beans, wrapped in a soft tortilla.

title: Taco Seasoning Casserole
directions: Brown ground beef, onion and bell pepper.
Add taco seasoning. Pour into casserole dish, sprayed with Pam.
Mix corn bread according to directions on package, with 1/2 can onion rings. Pour on top.
Grate cheese and add rest of onion rings on top. Bake at 400° for about 10 minutes.

_enrichment.description: A comforting casserole with seasoned ground beef, peppers, and corn, topped with a cheesy cornbread crust.

title: Black "Eyes Of Texas" Casserole
ingredients: 1 (15 1/2 oz.) can jalapeno black-eyed peas, drained
1 (10 oz.) can tomatoes with green chilies
directions: Saute ground beef, onion and garlic until lightly browned, stirring to crumble the meat.
Stir in remaining ingredients except tortillas and cheese.
Alternately layer the mixture of meat and tortillas, beginning and ending with meat mixture, in a greased 13 x 9 x 2-inch pan.
Sprinkle with cheese and bake at 350° for 35 minutes or until bubbly.
Freezes well.
Yields 8 servings.

_enrichment.description: A spicy and cheesy casserole with black-eyed peas, ground beef, and tortillas, baked until bubbly.

title: Beef Enchiladas
directions: Brown ground beef and drain. Add onion and taco seasoning according to package directions.
Add 1/2 cup grated cheese to meat and onion mixture. Set aside. Combine soups and enchilada sauce.
Top each tortilla with 1 heaping tablespoonful of meat mixture. Place, seam side down, in a 9 x 13-inch baking dish. Pour sauce over tortillas and top with remaining cheese.
Bake in preheated 350° oven for 25 to 30 minutes.

_enrichment.description: Classic beef enchiladas with a creamy sauce, melted cheese, and tender ground beef, wrapped in soft tortillas.

title: Beef Burritos
directions: Cook hamburger; drain and then add burrito mix, refried beans and water, mixing well.
Cook for 20 to 30 minutes.
Heat shells in oven or microwave oven for 2 to 3 minutes.
Add lettuce, meat mix, tomatoes, cheese and taco sauce to burrito.
Before you roll your burrito up, you can also add the other ingredients:
black olives, green onion and sour cream to the filling.
Roll up and serve.

_enrichment.description: Hearty beef burritos stuffed with seasoned meat, beans, and fresh toppings, wrapped in a soft tortilla.

title: Mexican Burgers
ingredients: 1 medium very ripe avocado, seeded, peeled and cut up
1 small tomato, peeled, seeded and chopped (1 1/3 c.)
dash of bottled hot pepper sauce or ground red pepper
1 lb. lean ground beef or ground turkey
4 hamburger buns, split and toasted on the grill
directions: In a medium mixing bowl, mash avocado.
Stir in tomato, the 1 tablespoon onion, lemon juice, salt, garlic powder and hot pepper sauce.
Cover and chill in the refrigerator until serving time. Meanwhile, in another medium mixing bowl, combine crushed corn chips, milk, 2 tablespoons onion and Worcestershire sauce.
Add meat; mix well.
Shape the meat mixture into four 3/4-inch thick patties.
Arrange patties on the cooking grill 11 to 12 minutes or until well done, turning once halfway through grilling time. Serve patties topped with tomato slices and guacamole on lettuce-lined buns.
Makes 4 servings.

_enrichment.description: Juicy burgers with a creamy avocado and tomato topping, served on soft buns with crispy corn chips.

title: Tamale Balls And Sauce
directions: Mix all ingredients with hands and form into small balls the size of large marbles.
Drop into sauce and cook slowly for 2 hours.
Can cook in a 325° oven.
Serve hot from chafing dish. Yields approximately 150 balls.

_enrichment.description: Small, spicy tamale balls simmered in a rich tomato-based sauce, served hot from the chafing dish.

title: White Chili
ingredients: 2 pkg. (9 oz. each) frozen white corn in a pouch, thawed
4 (4 oz.) cans diced green chilies, not drained
2 (15 oz.) cans Great Northern beans, undrained
directions: Mix water, 1 teaspoon lemon pepper and cumin seed in a large saucepan, heat to a boil.
Add chicken breast halves, reduce heat to low; cover and simmer until chicken is fork tender and juices run clear, 20 to 28 minutes.
Remove chicken from bones; cut into 1-inch pieces.
Return chicken to saucepan.

_enrichment.description: A creamy and slightly spicy white chili with tender chicken, white beans, and corn, perfect for chilly evenings.

title: Viva La Chicken Casserole
directions: Salt chicken; wrap in foil and bake 1 hour at 400°.
Cool and cut into bite size pieces.
Break 6 tortillas in buttered 9 x 13-inch baking dish.
Spread half of the cut up chicken over the tortillas.
Mix the rest of the ingredients (except the cheese) together and pour half the mixture over the chicken and tortillas. Repeat layers and cover with shredded cheese.
Refrigerate overnight.
Remove from fridge and let it come to room temperature.
Bake at 300° for 1 1/2 hours.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Viva La Chicken Casserole.

title: 7 Layer Mexican Pie
ingredients: 1 brick New York sharp Cheddar cheese or Monterey Jack
sliced or chopped large black pitted olives
directions: In deep dish pie or quiche pan, smear refried beans on bottom. Cover with salsa.
Plop guacamole mixture over this and smooth. Add sour cream on top, then cover generously with New York sharp Cheddar cheese.
Decorate the top with the black olives and chopped scallions.
Serve with a basket of restaurant-style corn tortilla chips for dunking into.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for 7 Layer Mexican Pie.

title: Cheesy Beef Enchiladas
ingredients: 1 (16 oz.) jar thick 'n chunky salsa (mild, medium or hot)
directions: Brown ground beef; pour off excess fat.
Add taco seasoning mix and water.
Put 3 to 4 tablespoons taco meat in each flour tortilla.
Roll and place seam down in a glass casserole.
Top with thick 'n chunky salsa, Cheez Whiz and Colby cheese. Microwave 6 to 8 minutes, until cheese has completely melted. Serve with cucumbers and sliced tomatoes.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Cheesy Beef Enchiladas.

title: Taco Squares
directions: Preheat oven to 375°.
Brown meat and onions in a skillet, drain grease, if necessary.
Stir in the taco seasoning mix, beans and tomato sauce.
Simmer 5 minutes.
Unroll dough into 4 long rectangles.
Place in ungreased 15 x 10-inch jellyroll pan.
Press over bottom and 1 inch up sides to form crust.
Bake at 375° for 25 to 30 minutes or until golden brown.
Sprinkle with cheese. Return to oven until cheese is melted, about 2 to 3 minutes.
Cut into squares and top with chopped lettuce, tomatoes, taco sauce, sour cream, etc.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Taco Squares.

title: Kansas Style Enchiladas
directions: Cook hamburger meat and chorizo together; drain grease off. Combine with vegetables and 1 cup of cheese.
Dip tortillas in hot oil until soft, then dip in heated enchilada sauce.
Fill dipped tortilla with mixture, then roll and place in baking dish.
Spread with remaining sauce and cheese.
Put in oven until cheese melts and enchiladas are hot.
This is my Mother's recipe.
Enjoy.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Kansas Style Enchiladas.

title: Refried Bean Casserole
directions: Heat oil and saute onion and pepper until well softened. Combine with beans, sour cream, chili powder and garlic powder. Turn into 1 1/2-quart casserole.
Top with cheese.
Bake at 350° for 20 minutes or until hot and bubbly.
Serves 4 to 5.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Refried Bean Casserole.

title: Cumin Avocado Rice
directions: Cook rice according to directions, adding butter, bouillon cubes and cumin, but omitting salt.
Peel, seed and coarsely chop avocado.
Stir in rice with picante sauce and green onions.
Serve cold with additional picante sauce.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Cumin Avocado Rice.

title: Spicy Nachos
directions: Mix together the first 5 ingredients; let sit for 15 minutes. Pour the Doritos in long pan.
Pour sauce over Doritos.
Sprinkle the shredded cheese over all.
Put under broiler for 3 minutes, until cheese melts.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Spicy Nachos.

title: Chicken Enchilada Soup
ingredients: 15 oz. can black beans, drained and rinsed
directions: Combine cornstarch and water in small bowl.
Stir together all remaining ingredients, except cilantro, in saucepan.
Add the cornstarch water mixture; bring to a boil, stirring occasionally. Add cilantro.
Garnish with tortilla chips and shredded Cheddar cheese.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Chicken Enchilada Soup.

title: Firecracker Casserole
ingredients: 1 can black beans, rinsed and drained
directions: Cook the meat in skillet until it is no longer pink, drain. Add beans, chili pwd., cumin and salt.
Transfer to a 9 by 13 in. baking dish.
Arrange tortillas over the top; combine soup and tomatoes, then pour over the tortillas.
Sprinkle with cheese. Bake uncovered at 350° for 25-30 min. Yields 8 servings.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Firecracker Casserole.

title: Taco Soup With Black Beans
ingredients: 1 lb. ground beef, browned and drained
1 (15 1/2) oz. can red kidney beans, undrained
1 envelope dry Hidden Valley ranch dressing mix
directions: Combine all ingredients except chips, shredded cheese and sour cream in slow cooker.
Cover.
Cook on low 4 - 6 hours or serve as soon as thoroughly heated.
Garnish individual servings with chips, cheese and sour cream.
