_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Taco Soup With Black Beans.

title: Chicken And Sour Cream Enchiladas
directions: Cook and cube chicken.
Mix together all ingredients, except cheese and tortillas.
Put a little of the mixture in the bottom of pan (so they don't stick).
Put 2 tablespoons of mixture in tortilla and some of the cheese, roll and place in pan.
After all are rolled up, spread rest of sauce and cheese on top.
Bake for about 30 minutes at 350°.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Chicken And Sour Cream Enchiladas.

title: Mexican Delight
directions: Brown ground beef.
Chop onion and saute with ground beef.
Add remaining ingredients and cook for 30 minutes or until rice is done.
Serve over Fritos corn chips or tortillas.
Top with fresh chopped onions, grated cheese, lettuce, diced avocado, ripe chopped olives, Ranch Style "white cream" dressing and sour cream, taco sauce, etc.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Mexican Delight.

title: Chicken Tortillas(4 Servings)
ingredients: 1 1/2 cans green chilies, seeded and diced
directions: Cut chicken into large pieces.
In bowl combine onion, soups, salsa and milk, blending well.
In a 1 1/2-quart buttered casserole, layer half the tortillas, chicken, sauce and cheese. Sprinkle with green chilies.
Repeat layers once more ending with cheese on top.
Cover; refrigerate for 24 hours.
Remove cover. Bake at 300° for 1 hour.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Chicken Tortillas(4 Servings).

title: Mexican Style Liver
ingredients: 1 (12 oz.) can whole kernel corn, drained
directions: In large skillet, cook bacon until crisp.
Drain, reserving 3 tablespoons drippings in skillet.
Crumble bacon; set aside.
Cook onion and garlic in drippings for about 5 minutes or until onion is tender, but not brown.
Combine flour, chili powder and salt. Cut liver into thin strips; toss with flour mixture to coat.
Add liver to onion in skillet; brown quickly on all sides.
Stir in crumbled bacon, undrained tomatoes and whole kernel corn.
Simmer, covered, until mixture is heated through.
Serve with tortillas or over hot, cooked rice.
Makes 6 servings.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Mexican Style Liver.

title: California Tamale Casserole
directions: Brown meat, add minced garlic, onion and celery.
Cook until onion is transparent.
Add corn, salt, chili powder and tomatoes and boil slowly about 15 minutes.
Slowly stir in olives. Put in a greased casserole dish, top with cheese. Bake at 350° about 45 minutes.

_enrichment.description: A hearty American-Mexican fusion casserole with ground beef, corn, and cheese, perfect for a comforting family meal.

title: Hoppin Juan Skillet Cakes
ingredients: 1/4 c. Weight Watchers fat-free mayonnaise dressing
2 Tbsp. plus 2 tsp. Weight Watchers ultimate 90 plain nonfat yogurt
directions: Preheat oven to 400°.
Spray baking sheet with nonstick cooking spray.
In medium bowl, coarsely mash black-eyed peas. Add rice, onion, bread crumbs, mayonnaise dressing, chili powder, cumin and red pepper.
Divide mixture into 8 (1/3 cup) portions. Shape each portion into a 1/2-inch thick patty.

_enrichment.description: Crispy skillet cakes made with black-eyed peas and brown rice, seasoned with Mexican spices for a protein-rich dish.

title: Mexican Meat Filling
directions: In large skillet cook ground chuck, onion, garlic and cayenne over medium-high heat, stirring often until meat is no longer pink, about 5 minutes.
Add chili powder, cumin and salt.
Cook 1 minute.
Add tomato sauce and cook, stirring often, until thickened, about 5 minutes.

_enrichment.description: A simple and flavourful Mexican-style meat filling for tacos, burritos, or enchiladas, with a rich blend of spices.

title: Taco Dip Ambrosino
ingredients: 1 jar taco sauce (hot or mild, to taste)
directions: Cream together with a fork cream cheese and cottage cheese until blended.
Layer mixture in bottom of a glass pie plate or serving bowl/plate.
Add layer of taco sauce, vegetables and Cheddar.
Alternate layers until supplies are exhausted. Finish with Cheddar and garnish with parsley.
Serve with taco chips to scoop.

_enrichment.description: A creamy and layered taco dip with cheese, avocado, and olives, served with crispy tortilla chips for scooping.

title: Chicken Taco Casserole
directions: Boil chicken breasts.
(Skin, debone and tear into small pieces.)
Mix chicken pieces, cream of chicken soup (not diluted), onions and peppers.
Grease a 9 x 13-inch pan with corn oil. Layer chips, soup mixture and cheese until soup mixture is gone. Final layer of chips and sprinkle top with cheese.
Cover with foil.
Cook at 350° for 30 minutes.
Remove foil; cook for 5 more minutes.
Allow to cool for 10 minutes and serve.

_enrichment.description: A cheesy, crunchy casserole with chicken, Doritos, and peppers, baked until bubbly and golden.

title: Mmmmm Impossible Taco Pie
directions: Cook ground beef, onions and seasoning mix together until meat turns color; drain.
Put in a greased 8 x 8-inch square pan. Spread beans on top of meat.
Mix Bisquick, milk and eggs; pour over beans.
Bake at 400° until brown.
Sprinkle cheese on top and put back into oven until cheese is melted.
Serve with remaining ingredients.

_enrichment.description: A quick and easy taco pie with ground beef, beans, and Bisquick batter, topped with melted cheese.

title: Mexican Taco Dip
ingredients: 1 large pkg. Cheddar cheese, Monterey Jack or Taco Mix cheese, grated
1 large bag white corn tortilla chips
directions: Mix cream cheese, sour cream and taco seasoning all together. Shape into a mound on a plate.
Spread salsa sauce over top. Sprinkle chopped ingredients over top.
Sprinkle cheese over top last.
Serve chilled with corn tortilla chips.

_enrichment.description: A chilled Mexican taco dip layered with cream cheese, sour cream, and fresh vegetables, served with tortilla chips.

title: Skillet Enchiladas
ingredients: 1 lb. ground beef, chicken or turkey
1 (10 1/2 oz.) can condensed cream of mushroom soup
2 Tbsp. seeded chopped canned green chillies or 1 can diced green chillies
directions: In a 10-inch skillet, brown meat and onion; drain any excess fat (electric skillet at 350°).
Stir in soup, enchilada sauce, milk and chillies.
Reduce heat to 220°.
Cover and cook 20 minutes, stirring occasionally.

_enrichment.description: Enchiladas made in a skillet with ground beef, tortillas, and a creamy sauce, topped with cheese and olives.

title: Mexican Beef Casserole
directions: Cook ground beef, onion, green pepper and garlic together and drain.
Add next 8 ingredients and simmer until slightly cooked. Add meat mixture to noodles and add grated cheese.
Sprinkle with Parmesan cheese and cover with foil.
Bake 30 minutes at 375°.

_enrichment.description: A hearty Mexican beef casserole with egg noodles, olives, and mushrooms, baked until bubbly.

title: Mexican Chicken Wings
directions: Combine ingredients (first seven) and rub on chicken wings. Let stand for 15 minutes.
Saute wings in oil and butter until lightly browned.
Drain excess fat.
Add garlic, jalapeno and beer and simmer until liquid has turned to a glaze.

_enrichment.description: Spicy and aromatic chicken wings marinated in Mexican spices and beer, glazed to perfection.

title: Corn Bread Or Mexican Corn Bread
directions: Mix the dry ingredients.
Beat eggs. Add milk and stir into dry mixture.
Add the Crisco and mix thoroughly.
Pour into "hot" cast-iron skillet or corn bread stick pan with about 1 tablespoon oil. Bake in preheated oven at 450° or until golden brown.

_enrichment.description: A classic American cornbread with a hint of Mexican flair, perfect as a side or snack.

title: Salsa Cheese Bake
directions: Cook garlic in oil 1 minute.
Stir in next 6 ingredients. Heat to boiling constantly stirring.
Reduce heat & simmer until thickened about 3 minutes.
Cook & drain sausage, stir in 1 c. salsa mixture.
In ungreased casserole dish layer 1/2 of cheese (sliced 1/4 inches) then sausage mixture.
Top with 1/2 cheese. Bake uncovered at 400 degrees 8-10 minutes.
Heat remaining salsa mixture & pour over cheese.
Serve with chips.

_enrichment.description: A sweet and savoury cheese bake with sausage, salsa, and spices, topped with tortilla chips.

title: Fabulous Beef Fajitas
ingredients: 1/2 c. prepared Italian salad dressing, divided
1 lb. boneless sirloin steak, cut into 1/4 inch strips
1 medium onion, sliced and separated into rings
directions: In a large resealable plastic bag, combine 1/4 cup salad dressing and chili powder; add steak.
Seal and turn to coat; refrigerate for 30 minutes.
In a skillet, saute peppers and onion in remaining salad dressing until crisp and tender.
Remove and keep warm.	Drain steak, discarding marinade in skillet, saute steak for 6-10 minutes or until no longer pink.
Return vegetables to pan.
Heat through and spoon meat and vegetables down center of tortillas; fold in sides.
Yield:
4-6 servings.

_enrichment.description: Sizzling beef fajitas with peppers and onions, served in warm tortillas for a vibrant meal.

title: Mexican Cornbread With Ground Beef
directions: Combine cornmeal, milk, salt, bacon drippings, eggs, soda and corn in mixing bowl. Set aside. Saute ground beef in skillet. Drain on paper towels. Grease a large black iron skillet and heat. Sprinkle bottom with a thin layer of cornmeal and brown slightly. Pour half of the batter in hot skillet.
Sprinkle cheese evenly over batter, then sprinkle on the meat, onion and peppers.
Pour remaining batter on top. Bake at 350° for 45 to 60 minutes. Serves 8 to 10.
Serve with a tossed green salad, iced tea and fruit dessert for a complete meal.

_enrichment.description: A moist and cheesy Mexican cornbread with ground beef and peppers, baked to golden perfection.

title: Green Enchiladas
ingredients: 1 pkg. dry green onion dip mix
directions: Brown ground beef with salt and garlic.
Drain well. Set aside.
Make sauce: heat soup, milk and Velveeta until melted. Add green chiles, dip mix and pimentos.
Add cheese and onions to meat.
Mix well.
Soften tortillas by dipping them, one at a time, in hot oil in shallow skillet. Drain. Fill with meat mixture. Roll and place in a greased 10 x 13 casserole dish.
Pour cheese sauce over top.
Cover and bake at 350° for 30 minutes.

_enrichment.description: Green enchiladas filled with seasoned beef, topped with a creamy green chile sauce and melted cheese.

title: Chicken In Green Sauce-Guatemala(Jocon)
ingredients: 1 cup green onions chopped, green part only
directions: Cook chicken in the water with 1 tsp. of salt in a covered pot, about 30 minutes, or until chicken is soft.
Remove chicken and set aside.
Save broth and soak tortillas in in it.
Toast sesame seeds and squash seeds in a skillet until they lightly brown.
In a food processor, grind the seeds. add the cilantro, green onions, hot chiles, tomatillos and 1 cup of the broth.
Add the tortillas and process into a smooth paste.
Brown the chicken in a lightly oiled pan for 5 minutes over medium heat.
Add the paste mixture and the remaining broth, about 2 cups.
Simmer over low heat for 15 minutes until the sauce is reduced to a thick, rich, green consistency. Serve over rice if desired.
Makes 4-6 servings.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Chicken In Green Sauce-Guatemala(Jocon).

title: Scotch Crunchies
ingredients: 1 to 2 jalapenos, diced (if you like it mild, only 1)
directions: On high heat, place tomatoes and jalapenos directly on stovetop until slightly roasted.
Remove from heat.
Peel and slice. Combine with avocados, garlic, onions, cilantro, garlic salt and pepper into a food processor.
On low speed, blend ingredients until they appear blended into a lumpy texture, not creamy.
(I prefer to hand mix in a mocahete, which adds just the right authentic flavor!)
Serve with tortilla chips or as a topping on your favorite Mexican dish!
Muy Bueno!

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Scotch Crunchies.

title: Fresh Salsa
directions: In a large frying pan, cook all the chiles until black on each side, then cut chiles down the middle and remove all the seeds. Dice chiles real small.
Put into a large saucepan.
Chop onions and tomatoes real small.
Put into pan with chiles.
Add the Pace salsa to other ingredients in pan.
Also, add tomato sauce, stewed tomatoes and 1 cup of water.
Let simmer for about 1 hour.
Make sure to add about 1 tablespoon of garlic salt.
Make sure to mix all ingredients well before simmering.
Makes about 6 pounds of salsa.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Fresh Salsa.

title: Oven-Fried "Chicken" Chimichangas
ingredients: frozen chicken-like slices (amount depends on how much you wish to make)
1 (4 oz.) can chopped green chilies, drained
directions: Grind or chop "chicken"; add cheese and green chilies, set aside.
Wrap tortillas in damp paper towels; microwave at High 15 seconds or until hot.
Brush both sides of tortillas, one at a time, with vegetable oil (keep remaining tortillas warm).
Place a scant 1/2 cup of "chicken" mixture just below center of each tortilla.
Fold in left and right sides of tortilla to partially enclose filling.
Fold up bottom edge of tortilla; fold into a rectangle and secure with a wooden pick.
Repeat with remaining tortillas and "chicken" mixture.
Place on a lightly greased baking sheet.
Bake at 425° for 10 minutes or until crisp and lightly browned.
Serve with shredded lettuce, salsa and sour cream. Yields 4 servings.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Oven-Fried "Chicken" Chimichangas.

title: Fiesta Chicken Bake
ingredients: 2 c. cooked chicken or turkey, cubed
directions: Place chips on the bottom of a glass casserole dish.
Scatter the chicken pieces on top of chips.
Mix soups and chiles together and pour over all.
Top with tomatoes; sprinkle cheese over top. Bake at 350° for 25 to 30 minutes.
Garnish with sour cream and guacamole before serving.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Fiesta Chicken Bake.

title: Microwave Nachos Grande
ingredients: 1 large bag tortilla chips (your choice of brands)
directions: On large microwavable platter, start layering as follows: tortilla chips, Cheddar cheese, refried beans, hamburger, sour cream, tomatoes, green onions and black olives.
After completing first layer of everything, begin with second layer in same order. When ingredients are gone, top with guacamole and jalapeno peppers.
Entire platter can be heated in microwave.
Serve immediately.
Great party snack (but a little messy)!

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Microwave Nachos Grande.

title: Stir-Fry Fajitas
ingredients: 1 lb. boneless, stir-fry beef or chicken
1 large green pepper or 2 Ortega peppers
directions: In a large skillet, fry meat in salad oil until tender.
Add garlic, onion and rest of ingredients.
Cook until desired tenderness.
Serve mixture on warm tortillas topped with salsa and sour cream to your taste.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Stir-Fry Fajitas.

title: Chili Verde
ingredients: 3 pork steaks (lean meat), cut into 1/2-inch strips
2 small cans whole green chilies, chopped or sliced
directions: Fry meat until golden brown.
Pour unwanted grease out.
Saute onions and garlic with the meat. Add chilies, jalapeno, cumin, salt, water and tomatoes. Bring to a boil.
Lower heat and simmer until pork is done.
It's delicious with beans, rice and tortillas.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Chili Verde.

title: Winter Guacamole
ingredients: 2 small jalapeno chiles, stemmed, seeded and minced
4 sun dried tomatoes (packed in oil), drained and minced
5 slices bacon, cooked crisp and coarsely crumbled
directions: Pit and peel avocados; mash pulp in medium bowl to chunky consistency.
Add lime juice, scallions, jalapeno chiles and tomatoes, then fold in cheese, bacon and coriander.
Season to taste with salt.
Serve as soon as possible to keep bacon crisp. Use your favorite chips for dipping.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Winter Guacamole.

title: Chicken Tortilla Casserole(6 To 8)
ingredients: 1 lb. medium Cheddar cheese, grated or 1/2 Cheddar and 1/2 Jack
directions: Simmer chicken breasts until tender.
Save a little broth. Shred chicken.
Mix soup ingredients (soups, salsa, milk and broth).
Grease 9 x 13-inch Pyrex dish.
Cut tortillas into quarters and start layering tortillas on the bottom, then chicken pieces, then soup mixture and top with cheese.
Repeat layers. Cover with foil and refrigerate 24 hours.
Bake at 325° for 1 hour or until bubbly.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Chicken Tortilla Casserole(6 To 8).

title: Bulk Chili
directions: Cook meat over medium heat for 45 minutes.
Add chili powder, salt, paprika, cumin, black pepper and garlic.
Stir well and continue to cook for 15 minutes.
Add water to cover and stir well; bring back to boil.
Stir in cracker meal slowly, stirring constantly, to avoid lumping, to desired thickness.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Bulk Chili.

title: Mexican Salad Or Dip(Layered)
ingredients: 1 c. or more green onions, chopped
8 oz. or more sharp grated cheese
directions: Mix together the avocados, salt, pepper and lemon juice.
Mix the sour cream, taco seasoning mix and mayonnaise or salad dressing.
Mix the green onion, black olives and tomatoes.
In a 13 x 9 x 2-inch dish, layer each each of the following:
bean dip, avocado mix, sour cream mix, green onion mix and top with the cheese.
Serve with Fritos or taco chips.
Makes a lot and keeps well.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Mexican Salad Or Dip(Layered).

title: Bobby'S Nachos
directions: Place chips on individual serving plates while heating chili and cutting Velveeta into cubes.
Spoon chili on top of chips and place cubes of Velveeta on top of chili.
Spoon picante sauce over these items, as desired.
Heat in microwave for 2 minutes or until cheese is melted.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Bobby'S Nachos.

title: Super Nachos
ingredients: 10 1/2 oz. can bean dip with jalapenos
directions: In a large skillet, cook meat until brown; drain.
Add seasonings and water; simmer for 20 minutes.
Spread bean dip over large 12 x 14-inch pizza pan.
Sprinkle with cheeses; top with meat and chopped chilies and bake until bubbly.
Arrange lettuce, tomatoes and tortillas around edge and serve.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Super Nachos.

title: Hot Taco Salad
directions: Break lettuce into large bowl and add tomato wedges.
Brown ground beef with onion and green pepper.
Add seasonings to taste (salt, pepper and garlic salt).
Over very low heat, heat cheese and mushroom soup until cheese is smooth, stirring occasionally. Sprinkle ground beef on top of lettuce and tomatoes, then crumble Fritos on top.
Pour cheese sauce over salad and toss.
Serve with hard rolls.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Hot Taco Salad.

title: Southwestern Bean Salad
ingredients: 1 (15 1/2 to 16 oz.) can pink beans, rinsed and drained
1 can black beans, rinsed and drained
1 (16 oz.) can black-eyed peas, rinsed and drained
directions: In large bowl with wire whisk or fork, mix olive oil, vinegar, cumin and salt.
Add celery, scallions, pink beans, black beans and black-eyed peas.
Gently toss to coat well.
To serve, stir in celery leaves.

_enrichment.description: A vibrant and zesty bean salad with a tangy dressing, perfect for a light summer meal or potluck. Packed with protein and fibre from pink beans, black beans, and black-eyed peas.

title: Taco Twist
directions: Brown beef and add taco seasoning (saving 1 tablespoon), peppers and tomato sauce.
Let simmer for a few minutes.
Cook and drain macaroni and add cheese, sour cream and remaining taco seasoning to macaroni.
Pour into a greased 9 x 9-inch baking dish.
Top with hamburger mixture and sprinkle Cheddar cheese on top.
Bake at 350° for 30 minutes.

_enrichment.description: A comforting, cheesy casserole with a taco-inspired twist, blending ground beef, macaroni, and melted Cheddar for a hearty family dinner.

title: Beef Tostadas
directions: Preheat oven to 450°.
Place tortillas on a heavy baking sheet; put another baking sheet on top of tortillas.
Bake 8 to 10 minutes, or until tortillas are crisp.
In a nonstick skillet over medium-high heat.
Combine onion and ground round.
Saute until meat is browned.
Drain off fat and add cumin, salt, oregano, chili powder, garlic powder and hot pepper sauce.
Stir until well mixed.
For each tostada, spread 1/3 cup meat on crisped tortilla. Add 1/4 cup cabbage, 2 tablespoons Salsa Cruda and 2 tablespoons shredded cheese.
Serves 6.

_enrichment.description: Crispy corn tortillas topped with seasoned ground beef, fresh cabbage, and melted cheese, served with salsa for a satisfying Mexican street-food experience.

title: Mexican Lasagne
directions: Brown hamburger; drain.
Add salt and pepper to taste and onion, if desired.
In saucepan, heat milk, soup and enchilada sauces until blended.
Grease 9 x 13-inch pan.
Place 3 tortillas in bottom, top with a layer of meat, cheese, then soup mixture. Make 3 layers of this making sure to reserve enough soup mixture to pour over top.
End with cheese on top.
Bake at 350° for 25 to 30 minutes.
Top with shredded lettuce, tomatoes and sour cream, if desired.

_enrichment.description: A layered casserole inspired by Mexican and Italian cuisines, featuring seasoned beef, tortillas, and a creamy enchilada sauce, topped with melted cheese.

title: 9 Layer Mexican Dip
directions: Peel and pit avocado, mash and mix with 3 tablespoons of Miracle Whip, set aside.
Mix together sour cream, 1 cup of Miracle Whip and 1/2 package of taco seasoning.
Set aside.
On a large platter spread on avocado mix, refried beans, sour cream mix, Cheddar cheese, hot sauce, mushrooms, black olives, green onions and tomatoes.
Serve with tortilla chips, Fritos or Doritos.

_enrichment.description: A layered dip with avocado, refried beans, cheese, and sour cream, served with crispy tortilla chips for a crowd-pleasing appetiser.

title: Mexican Pinwheels
ingredients: 1 (1 oz.) pkg. dry Hidden Valley Ranch dressing
directions: Mix together cream cheese, Ranch dressing and green onions. Spread on the flour tortillas.
Drain vegetables, pat extra liquid out on paper towels.
Sprinkle equal amounts on the 4 tortillas. Roll tortillas up tightly to form logs and refrigerate at least 2 hours before serving.
Slice in 1/2-inch pinwheels and garnish with salsa or guacamole.

_enrichment.description: Savoury pinwheels made with cream cheese, ranch dressing, and vegetables, rolled in flour tortillas and sliced for a fun, shareable snack.

title: Texas Pancakes
ingredients: 3/4 c. fresh or canned corn kernels, drained
directions: In skillet heat butter.
Add onion and garlic; cook over low heat until tender, but not brown.
Turn into mixing bowl; cool. Stir in egg yolks, mixing to blend well.
Combine flour, salt, pepper and chili powder.
Stir into onion mixture.

Stir in corn. Peel and grate potatoes; place in cold water for 5 minutes. Squeeze as dry as possible, then drain thoroughly on paper towels. Beat egg whites with cream of tartar until stiff but not dry.
Add potatoes to corn mixture; blend well.
Fold in egg whites.
In skillet heat about 1/4 inch oil.
Form batter into 3 inch pancakes. Cook until brown; flip over.

_enrichment.description: Fluffy pancakes with a Southwestern kick, featuring corn, potatoes, and a hint of chilli, served with salsa for a hearty breakfast or brunch.

title: Black Bean And Corn Salad
ingredients: 1 (15 oz.) can black beans, drained and rinsed
1/4 c. chopped sweet onion, Vidalia or red
directions: Combine olive oil, vinegar, mustard, cumin and cilantro; set aside. combine beans, corn, onion and tomatoes.
Drizzle dressing over bean mixture.
Stir together gently to avoid breaking the beans.
May be topped with shredded Monterey Jack cheese if desired.
Serves 4 to 6. Should go with Chicken Tostada recipe.

_enrichment.description: A fresh and zesty salad combining black beans, sweet corn, and tomatoes with a tangy dressing, perfect for a light summer lunch or side dish.

title: Mexican Turkey Casserole
ingredients: 2 pt. cooked turkey, drain and reserve broth
2 c. each diced onion and bell pepper, saute and set aside
1 (10 3/4 oz.) can each 99% fat free cream of chicken and cream of mushroom soup
1 (8 oz.) can tomato sauce (no salt added)
1 (4 oz.) can mild chopped green chilies (undrained)
8 to 10 oz. no fat grated Cheddar cheese
directions: Place tortilla chips in 12 x 15 x 2-inch Pam sprayed pan. Crush chips slightly.
Add cut up turkey.
Blend 1 cup broth and remaining ingredients (except cheese) and pour over turkey.
Top with cheese.
Cover with foil and bake in 350° oven about 45 to 50 minutes.

_enrichment.description: A hearty casserole with shredded turkey, tortillas, and a creamy tomato-chilli sauce, topped with melted cheese for a comforting family meal.

title: Dove Load Burritos
directions: In large skillet over medium heat, cook dove meat and onion in 1/2 the butter; season with lemon pepper.
Cook for 10 minutes; set aside.
In same skillet melt remaining butter and fry potatoes adding lemon pepper
and garlic salt to taste.
Place meat in with potatoes; adding salsa and chili peppers.
Simmer 15 minutes; stirring frequently.
Spoon into warm tortillas; cover with cheese and green taco sauce.
Fold and serve.

_enrichment.description: Savoury burritos stuffed with seasoned dove meat, potatoes, and salsa, wrapped in flour tortillas for a rustic and flavourful meal.

title: Noodles Mexicana
directions: In large skillet, brown burger, onion, pepper and garlic in oil.
Stir in salt, corn, olives, tomatoes and sauce, cheese and paprika.
Cook noodles as directed; drain.
Combine noodle and burger mixture; pour into 2 1/2-quart casserole.
Cover; bake at 350° for 30 minutes.

_enrichment.description: A hearty noodle casserole with a Mexican-inspired twist, featuring a vegeburger, olives, and tomatoes in a creamy tomato sauce.

title: Apple Enchiladas
directions: Spoon fruit filling evenly down the center of each tortilla. Sprinkle evenly with cinnamon.
Roll up and place seam side down in a lightly greased pan.
Bring butter, sugar, brown sugar and water to a boil, in a medium saucepan.
Reduce heat and simmer, stirring constantly for three minutes.
Pour over enchiladas.
Let stand for 30 minutes.
Bake at 350 degrees for 20 minutes.

_enrichment.description: Sweet and spiced apple-filled tortillas baked in a caramel sauce, offering a delightful fusion of Mexican and American flavours for dessert.

title: Thunderbolt Potatoes
ingredients: 1 c. fresh yellow corn, cut from cob (about 2 ears)
4 large baking potatoes, peeled and quartered (about 2 3/4 lb.)
directions: Cook corn and garlic in butter in skillet over medium-high heat, stirring constantly, until tender; set aside.
Cook potatoes in boiling water 20 minutes or until tender.
Combine potatoes, milk, salt, pepper, chili powder and cumin in a large bowl.
Beat at medium speed with an electric mixer until smooth.
Stir in corn mixture and chiles.
Garnish with chili powder, if desired.
Yields 4 to 6 servings.

_enrichment.description: Creamy mashed potatoes with sweet corn, chilli, and garlic, offering a comforting side dish with a Southwestern twist.

title: Tuna Burritos
directions: Mix the flour, baking powder, water, butter and salt to make the dough.
Knead the dough into a soft mass.
Form the mass like a Mexican tortilla.
For each made-up tortilla, add some tuna; fold them in half and, with a fork, pinch the edges to tighten.
Deep fry until golden brown.

_enrichment.description: Crispy fried tuna-filled tortillas, a simple yet satisfying snack or light meal with a Mexican-inspired dough.

title: Zingy Tortilla Bites
ingredients: 1 to 4 jalapenos, seeded and finely chopped
1 (2 oz.) can chopped black olives, drained (optional)
directions: Blend the cream cheese and sour cream in an electric mixer or processor.
Add the next 7 ingredients and mix well.
Spread the cream cheese mixture on each tortilla and roll up tightly as for jelly roll.
Place the tortilla rolls on a tray and cover lightly with a damp towel and plastic wrap to prevent drying.
Chill for 8 to 10 hours.
Cut the tortilla rolls into 1/2 to 3/4-inch slices and arrange on a serving platter.
Serve with the picante sauce. Yield:
10 dozen.

_enrichment.description: Creamy and zesty tortilla bites filled with a tangy cheese mixture, perfect for parties or as a light appetiser.

title: Quick Enchiladas
ingredients: 1 pkg. tortillas (flour or corn both taste good)
1 qt. tomato sauce or spaghetti sauce (to moisten)
1/2 to 1 c. diced chicken or hamburger (optional)
directions: Mix beans, rice, tomato sauce and optional ingredients in a bowl.
Wet bottom of 10-inch round pan or 9 x 12-inch pan with at least 2 inches deep of tomato sauce.
Place tortillas flat on the bottom of the pans.
Cover entire surface by overlapping the tortillas.
Cover tortillas with bean mixture.
Two layers of tortillas and bean mixture work fine, ending with a layer of tortillas and grated cheese on top.
Place in a 350° oven until hot.
(I usually put on a lid for awhile or cover with aluminum foil until tortillas are hot, then remove foil for the last few minutes.)

_enrichment.description: A quick and hearty Mexican-inspired casserole with layers of tortillas, spiced beans, and cheese, perfect for a family meal.

title: Speedy Taco Bake
ingredients: 1 (15 1/4 oz.) can whole kernel corn, drained
directions: Cook ground beef and onion until beef is brown.
Drain.
Spoon into an ungreased 13 x 9 x 2-inch baking dish.
Stir in taco seasoning mix (dry), tomato sauce and corn.
Sprinkle with cheese. Stir remaining ingredients until blended.
Pour over beef mixture.
Bake for 35 minutes or until golden brown at 350°. Serve with sour cream, chopped tomato and shredded lettuce.

_enrichment.description: A speedy taco-inspired bake with ground beef, cheese, and a Bisquick topping, ideal for a quick family dinner.

title: Nacho Beef Casserole
ingredients: 2 c. tortilla chips nacho cheese flavored, broken into pieces (not crushed)
directions: Heat oven to 350°.
Brown ground meat in a small amount of butter or margarine.
Drain grease.
Stir into ground meat the picante sauce, hominy, chili powder and water.
In an 11 3/4 x 7 1/2 x 1 3/4-inch Pyrex dish, layer 1/2 of meat mixture, chips and cheese; repeat layer.
Bake 20 minutes in a 350° oven or until bubbly and thoroughly heated.
Makes about 6 servings.

_enrichment.description: A layered Mexican casserole with ground meat, hominy, and tortilla chips, baked until bubbly and golden.

title: Homestyle Chili
ingredients: 1 (28 oz.) can whole tomatoes (undrained), cut up
2 (15.5 oz.) cans red kidney beans, drained and rinsed
directions: In medium skillet, brown meat with onion and drain.
Add all remaining ingredients, except kidney beans.
Bring to a boil. Reduce heat, cover and simmer for 1 hour.
Add kidney beans; simmer until thoroughly heated.
Makes 7 (1 1/2-cup) servings.
To cook in a slow cooker, cook on high setting for 3 to 4 hours.

_enrichment.description: A classic, slow-cooked chilli with ground beef, peppers, and kidney beans, perfect for a cosy winter meal.

title: Chicken Victoria
ingredients: 1 green, red or yellow bell pepper, chopped
directions: Brown chicken pieces in hot oil for 5 minutes; drain off fat. Add desired La Victoria salsa and water.
Reduce heat; cover and simmer for 30 minutes.
Uncover; add corn and bell pepper (add more water if necessary).
Cover and cook 10 minutes more.
Makes 4 servings.

_enrichment.description: A simple and flavourful Mexican-inspired chicken dish with salsa, corn, and bell peppers, ready in under an hour.

title: Mock Enchilada
directions: Brown beef in hot fat.	Add
onion and cook until soft. Mix in salt, pepper and chili.
Place alternate layers of corn chips, chili mixture and cheese in a greased casserole, ending with corn chips.
Bake at 350° for 15 minutes.

_enrichment.description: A mock enchilada casserole with layers of corn chips, chilli, and cheese, baked until crispy and golden.

title: Ten Layer Mexican Dip
directions: In the bottom of a large platter, spread all of the bean dip. The next layer will be the sour cream. Top that layer with the guacamole.
Next sprinkle the taco seasoning mix.
On top of that, will be the shrimp layer. The black olives go on top of the shrimp.
Spoon the salsa over the olive layer.
Next make a layer using the chopped green onions.
On top of the green onions, you will make a layer of chopped tomatoes.
The last layer will be the shredded Cheddar cheese.
You may omit any layer that you personally do not care for.

_enrichment.description: A vibrant Mexican seven-layer dip with beans, shrimp, salsa, and cheese, perfect for sharing at a party.

title: Mexican Cornbread Salad
ingredients: 1/3 c. French onion dip (sour cream base)
directions: Combine crumbled cornbread, peas, sausage, cucumber and tomato.
Toss to mix.
In small bowl, combine mayonnaise and dip. Mix well and add to cornbread mixture, stirring until well mixed. Spoon onto a bed of shredded lettuce
Makes about 8 servings.

_enrichment.description: A Southern-inspired salad with crumbled cornbread, peas, sausage, and vegetables, dressed with mayonnaise.

title: Molded Guacamole Ring
directions: Soften gelatin in cold water and dissolve in boiling water. Add lemon juice, onion and seasonings.
While gelatin mixture is still liquid, stir in avocado.
Fold in mayonnaise.
Pour into 10-inch ring mold and chill until firm.
Unmold and fill center with chilled seafood or chicken salad.
Surround with cherry tomatoes.
Serves 12.
This may also be served on a tray surrounded with tortilla chips for an appetizer.

_enrichment.description: A chilled avocado ring mould filled with chicken salad and surrounded by cherry tomatoes, a refreshing appetiser.

title: Fiesta Crab Crisps
directions: Cut tortillas into 6 wedges each and spray both sides with cooking spray.
Place pieces on an ungreased baking pan and bake in a preheated 400° oven for 10 minutes or until crisp. Combine remaining ingredients in a medium bowl and mix well.
Spread a rounded tablespoonful of crab mixture on each tortilla wedge; place wedges on an ungreased baking pan.
Broil for 5 minutes until lightly brown.
Serve immediately.
Serves 6.

_enrichment.description: Crispy tortilla wedges topped with a zesty crab mixture, broiled until golden and served immediately.

title: Chicken Taco Rice
ingredients: 1 lb. boned chicken breasts, cut in strips
1 medium red pepper, cut in strips
directions: Cook and stir chicken in hot oil until lightly browned.
Add broth, tomato sauce and seasoning mix. Bring to a boil.
Reduce heat, cover and simmer about 5 minutes.
Add corn and red pepper. Bring to a full boil. Stir in rice. Cover and remove from heat. Let stand 5 minutes. Fluff with fork and serve with cheese, tortilla chips and sour cream, if desired.

_enrichment.description: A one-pot Mexican-inspired rice dish with chicken, corn, and cheese, ready in under 30 minutes.

title: Real Chili
ingredients: 2 to 3 slices thick bacon, cut into 1/4-inch pieces
1 lb. lean (sirloin tip, round) beef, cut into 1/2-inch cubes
1 whole red chili pepper, seeded and chopped or 1/4 to 1/2 tsp. cayenne pepper
directions: Saute bacon until crisp, drain grease or sop it up with bread and feed to the dog.
Add garlic and beef and brown at high heat (stir a lot).
Add onions, stir until transparent.
Add bouillon and 1 1/2 cans beer, drink the rest of the beer.
Add the spices. Bring to a boil, then simmer until beef is done (1 to 1 1/2 hours). Serve on rice with beans on the side.
Yields approximately 4 servings.

_enrichment.description: A rich and smoky beef chilli with bacon, beer, and spices, simmered until tender and full of flavour.

title: Mexican Potato Salad(Microwave)
ingredients: 2 cans tiny new potatoes, drained and quartered
directions: In a 2-quart microwave-safe casserole, place potatoes.
In a small microwave-safe bowl, combine picante sauce, lime juice, oil, salt and pepper.
Cook, uncovered, on High for 45 to 60 seconds or until heated through.
Add sauce to potatoes.
Stir in tomatoes, olives, onion and parsley.
Warm all when ready to serve.

_enrichment.description: A quick microwave potato salad with picante sauce, lime, and fresh vegetables, perfect for a light meal.

title: Helen'S Tortilla Soup
ingredients: 2 large tomatoes, peeled and diced (fresh or canned)
Grated cheese - Monterrey Jack or Jack mixed with Cheddar
Corn on the cob, cut in 3/4 inch slices, optional (use 1 slice per bowl)
directions: Saute onion and garlic in oil.
Add broths, tomato sauce, cumin, chili powder, jalapenos and Worcestershire. Bring to a boil, cover and simmer for 1 hour.
Add chicken and tomatoes. Cook 15 minutes. Cut corn tortillas in narrow strips.
Fry crisp. Place a few tortilla strips in the bottom of each bowl.
Fill with hot soup.
Top with more chips and shredded cheese.
(Optional - squeeze fresh lime juice on top.) Avocado slices may be served with this soup.

_enrichment.description: A comforting tortilla soup with chicken, peppers, and crispy tortilla strips, served in a bowl.

title: Taco Stacko
directions: Blend refried beans with taco mix.
Spread in 9-inch pie plate.
Mash avocados and combine with sour cream and lemon juice. Spread mixture over beans.
Sprinkle with cheese, tomatoes, chilies, olives and green onions.

_enrichment.description: A layered Mexican taco stack with refried beans, avocado cream, and fresh toppings, served in a pie plate.

title: Mexican "Maze-In" Bread
ingredients: 1 (4 oz.) can chopped, drained green chiles
directions: Mix together the corn meal, salt and soda and blend.
Stir in shortening, sour cream, corn and eggs and mix well.
Add chiles and cheese; stir.
Pour mixture into a greased 9 x 13-inch pan and bake at 375° for 35 to 40 minutes.

_enrichment.description: A savoury Mexican cornbread bake with sweetcorn, cheese, and green chiles, perfect for a hearty side dish.

title: Chicken Tamale Pie
directions: Mix soup, beans, chilies, chili powder, onions and chicken in a saucepan and heat.
Mix muffin mix as directed.
Add 1/2 cup Cheddar cheese.
Pour into casserole dish and pull up onto sides. Pour heated mixture within 1/4 inch of edge.
Bake at 350° for 25 minutes.
Garnish with cheese, onions and cherry tomatoes.

_enrichment.description: A comforting Mexican-inspired casserole with chicken, beans, and cheese, topped with a cheesy muffin crust.

title: White Bean Chili
ingredients: 1 (16 oz.) can whole tomatoes (undrained), coarsely chopped
1 (15 1/2 oz.) can Great Northern beans, drained and rinsed
directions: Spray large nonstick skillet with cooking spray; heat over medium heat until hot.
Add chicken; cook and stir until browned, breaking into pieces with fork.
Remove chicken; drain fat from skillet.
Add celery, onions and garlic to skillet; cook and stir over medium heat 5 to 7 minutes or until tender.
Sprinkle with chili powder, cumin, allspice, cinnamon and pepper; cook and stir 1 minute.
Return chicken to skillet.
Stir in tomatoes with juice, beans and chicken broth; heat to a boil.
Reduce heat to low and simmer, uncovered, 15 minutes.

_enrichment.description: A fragrant white bean chilli with warm spices, tender chicken, and a rich broth, ideal for a cosy meal.

title: Tortilla Appetizer Wedges
ingredients: 1 (4.25 oz.) can ChiChi's diced green chilies
1 Tbsp. minced red and green jalapeno peppers
salsa or picante sauce or salsa verde
directions: In medium bowl, combine cream cheese and sour cream; mix well. Stir in chilies, onions and jalapenos.
Season with salt and pepper.
Spread tortilla with about 1/4 cup cream cheese mixture; top with another tortilla.
Continue spreading and stacking tortillas until stack is 4 tortillas high.
Top with another tortilla that has not been spread with cream cheese mixture. Make another stack with remaining 5 tortillas.
Wrap tortilla stacks in plastic wrap.
Refrigerate at least 1 hour.
Cut each stack into 18 wedges.
Arrange pinwheel fashion around cup of salsa, picante sauce or salsa verde.
Makes 36 appetizer servings.

_enrichment.description: Crispy tortilla wedges layered with a creamy cheese and chilli dip, perfect for sharing as a starter.

title: Ranch-Style Frijoles
directions: Soak beans in cold water overnight.
Drain, wash and cover with about 2 inches of water.
Add salt and boil over moderate heat for about 1 hour, adding water if needed.
Add diced onion, diced garlic, pepper, cumin seed, chopped chilies, taco sauce and tomatoes.
Cook over medium heat for an hour or longer, until beans are tender.
For spicier beans, add 1 or 2 teaspoons of red chili powder.

_enrichment.description: Slow-cooked pinto beans with smoky bacon, chilli, and tomatoes, a classic Mexican side dish.

title: Texas Chili Beef
ingredients: 1 red pepper, seeded and cut into 2 x 1/2-inch strips
1 tsp. chili powder or to taste
1 (17 oz.) can cannellini beans, drained and rinsed
directions: Heat the oil in a large heavy bottomed skillet, add the onions, red pepper and garlic, if using, and cook gently for 5 minutes, until the onion and pepper are soft.
Add the beef, turn the heat to high and cook until the meat is evenly browned, stirring with a wooden spoon to remove lumps.
Remove from the heat and stir in the flour, chili powder, cumin and oregano.
Add the tomato paste, the tomatoes with their juice and the beef broth.
Return to the heat and bring to a boil, stirring.
Season with salt, if desired, but not with pepper (chili powder flavors the dish).
Cover the pan and simmer for approximately 40 minutes.
Stir in the cannellini beans and simmer for a further 5 minutes to heat through.
Turn into a warmed serving dish and serve at once.
Serves 4.

_enrichment.description: A rich and hearty Texas-style chilli with ground beef, beans, and warm spices, perfect for a cold day.

title: Chuck Wagon Chili Soup
ingredients: 1 small butternut squash, peeled, seeded and cut in 3/4-inch chunks (approximately 4 c.)
directions: Saute beef in a 5-quart pot over medium heat 4 minutes or until brown.
Discard fat.
Saute onions and garlic in pot 4 minutes or until soft.
Stir in chili powder and cumin, then squash, broth and 1 extra cup water.
Cover and gently boil 15 minutes until squash is almost tender.
Add tomatoes and beans; bring to a boil.
Reduce heat; cover and simmer 15 minutes to develop flavors.
Serve with shredded Cheddar cheese and sliced scallions.

_enrichment.description: A chunky chilli soup with ground beef, butternut squash, and beans, ideal for a warming meal.

title: Black Bean Tortilla Pinwheels
ingredients: 1/4 c. chopped, drained pimento green olives or black olives
directions: Beat cream cheese and sour cream in bowl until blended.
Stir in Monterey Jack cheese, olives, onion, salt and garlic powder. Cover and refrigerate 2 hours.
Place beans in food processor or blender; process until smooth.
Spread each tortilla with a thin layer of beans.
Spread thin layer of cream cheese mixture over beans.
Roll up tortillas lightly.
Wrap in plastic wrap. Refrigerate until chilled.
Cut 3/4-inch slices and serve with salsa.

_enrichment.description: Savoury tortilla pinwheels filled with black beans and a creamy cheese mixture, great for sharing.

title: Mexican Chili Bake
ingredients: 2 cans diced tomatoes and green chilies
directions: In large skillet, cook onions, green peppers and garlic in butter until tender but not brown. Stir in rice, tomatoes and seasoning.
Heat thoroughly.
Saute beef; stir beef into rice mixture.
Spoon half of beef-rice mixture into buttered, shallow 2-quart casserole dish.
Sprinkle with 1/2 cup cheese and 1/2 cup corn chips.
Repeat layers.
Bake at 350° for 20 minutes, or until heated thoroughly.

_enrichment.description: A layered Mexican-inspired bake with rice, beef, and cheese, topped with crispy corn chips.

title: Taco Crescent Pie
directions: Brown ground chuck and drain.
Add taco mix, water and olives. Simmer 5 minutes. Separate rolls and put in ungreased 9 x 10-inch pie plate to make crust.
Sprinkle 1 cup crushed chips over bottom crust. Spoon on meat mixture, the sour cream, cheese and top with remaining corn chips.
Bake at 375° for 15 to 20 minutes. Slice and serve. Top individual servings with as much lettuce, tomato and taco sauce as desired.
Freezes well.

_enrichment.description: A quick and easy taco pie with ground beef, crescent rolls, and crispy tortilla chips.

title: Creole Flounder
directions: Heat oven to 500°.
Place fillets in single layer in baking dish, 13 1/2 x 9 x 2-inch.
Stir together remaining ingredients except pepper rings. Spoon over fillets.
Bake 5 to 8 minutes or until fish flakes easily with fork.
Remove fillets to warm platter. Garnish with green pepper rings. Makes 4 to 6 servings.

_enrichment.description: A zesty Creole-style flounder bake with tomatoes, peppers, and a hint of lemon.

title: All-American Apple Pie
directions: If apples lack tartness, sprinkle with the 1 tablespoon lemon juice.
In mixing bowl, combine sugar, flour, cinnamon and nutmeg (for a very juicy pie, omit the flour).
Add sugar mixture to the sliced apples; toss to mix.
Fill pastry-lined pie plate with apple mixture; dot with butter or margarine.
Cut slits in top crust for escape of steam; place pastry atop filling.
Seal and flute edge.
Sprinkle some sugar atop, if desired.

_enrichment.description: A classic American apple pie with a flaky pastry and spiced filling, perfect for dessert.

title: Cajun Crawfish Cornbread
directions: In bowl, combine cornmeal, salt and soda.
In medium bowl, beat eggs; chop onions and jalapeno peppers.
Grate cheese and add beaten eggs, onions, peppers, cheese, oil, corn and crawfish tails. Combine this mixture with cornmeal mix and mix well.
Pour into greased 12 x 14-inch baking dish.
Bake at 375° for 55 minutes or until golden brown.

_enrichment.description: A spicy Cajun cornbread with crawfish tails, cheese, and jalapeños, ideal for a Southern-style meal.

title: Chicken Creole
directions: Heat vegetable oil in a heavy skillet until hot.
Stir in 1/4 cup flour.
Stir constantly until mixture is about the color of a copper penny, about 5 minutes.
Reduce heat.
Add pepper and onion; cook until tender (about 10 minutes), stirring constantly. Add chicken, tomatoes, Worcestershire sauce, parsley, garlic, soy sauce, sugar, pepper, salt and hot sauce.
Cover and simmer about 20 minutes.
Serve over rice.

_enrichment.description: A rich and flavourful Creole chicken stew with tomatoes, peppers, and a hint of soy sauce.

title: Corn Creole
directions: Saute onion, garlic and bell pepper in bacon fat.
Add corn. Add egg beaten with milk.
Add corn meal and season to taste. Stir mixture over medium fire until thick.
Pour into a buttered casserole.
Sprinkle top with bread crumbs and dot with butter. Bake 1 hour at 350°.

_enrichment.description: A creamy corn casserole with a crispy breadcrumb topping, a classic Southern side dish.

title: Southern Oven-Fried Chicken
directions: Preheat the oven to 350°.
Remove the skin and fat from the chicken and discard.
Cut the chicken into six or eight serving-size pieces, then debone the breast.
(Reserve the wings, back and bones from the breast to use for stock.)
In a small bowl, combine the next seven ingredients.
Mix well and reserve 1/2 teaspoon of the seasoning mix; sprinkle the remaining seasoning over the chicken and set aside.

_enrichment.description: Crispy, oven-baked chicken coated in a spiced cornmeal crust, offering a healthier twist on classic Southern fried chicken.

title: Baked Cajun Cabbage
directions: Boil cabbage 10 minutes, uncovered, until tender-crisp. Drain.
Place in casserole. Blend oleo and flour in saucepan over medium heat. Add onion, celery, pepper, salt and cayenne pepper. Saute 10 minutes.
Add the milk and Cheddar Cheese. Blend well until melted.
Pour over cabbage. Top with the bread crumbs and bake at 350° for 30 minutes.
(I use hot Mexican Velveeta cheese.)

_enrichment.description: A creamy, cheesy Cajun-inspired cabbage bake with a crispy breadcrumb topping, perfect for a hearty side dish.

title: "Southern Living" Bar Cookie
ingredients: 1 (18.25 oz.) pkg. German Chocolate Cake Mix with Pudding
directions: 1. Combine caramels and 1/4 c. evaporated milk in small saucepan.
Cook over low heat stirring occasionally until smooth. Set aside. 2. In a bowl combine cake mix, butter, egg, and remaining milk.
Spoon half of mixture into greased 13 x 9 x 2 pan spreading evenly. 3. Bake at 350° for 6 minutes.
Remove from oven; sprinkle with chocolate morsels and pecans. 4. Spoon caramel mixture on top.
Carefully spoon remaining cake mixture over caramel layer. 5. Bake at 350° for 20-25 minutes.
Cool on wire rack and cut into bars.
Yields about 3 dozen.

_enrichment.description: A decadent layered bar cookie with caramel, chocolate cake, and pecans, ideal for sharing at gatherings.

title: My Mom'S Southern Pound Cake
directions: Halfway through mixing cake, turn oven to 200°.
Cream butter, Crisco and sugar; add eggs, mixing well.
Sift together flour, baking powder and salt.
Add the dry ingredients alternately with the milk, vanilla and lemon to the creamed mixture (beginning and ending with the flour mixture).
Bake in a well-greased and floured 10-inch tube pan.
When cake goes in the oven, turn to 325° for 1 1/2 hours.
(You may need to cover cake loosely with aluminum foil the last 1/2 hour of baking to keep it from getting too brown.)

_enrichment.description: A classic Southern pound cake with a tender crumb and rich buttery flavour, traditionally baked in a tube pan.

title: Barbecued Beef Brisket
directions: Sprinkle beef with onion salt, celery salt and garlic powder. Mix liquid smoke and Worcestershire sauce together and pour over meat.
Cover with foil.
Refrigerate several hours, turning once. Bake covered at 300° for 4 to 4 1/2 hours.
Pour BBQ sauce over beef and bake uncovered for another 30 minutes.

_enrichment.description: Slow-cooked beef brisket with a smoky, barbecue glaze, resulting in melt-in-the-mouth tender meat.

title: Cajun Chicken
ingredients: 1 lb. chicken breasts, cut in strips
directions: Saute chicken in oil for 5 minutes; combine paprika and next 7 ingredients.
Add to skillet for 1 minute, until chicken is cooked.
Combine bouillon with 1 cup hot water; add cornstarch. Add to chicken mixture.
Cook until slightly thickened.
Add mushrooms and scallions; stir in parsley.

_enrichment.description: Juicy chicken breasts simmered in a spicy Cajun sauce with mushrooms and scallions, offering bold flavours.

title: Cajun Turkey
ingredients: 4 oz. bottle crab and shrimp boil
directions: Mix together m.s.g., cayenne pepper, paprika, white pepper and celery salt.
Rub into turkey cavity and all over the skin.
(Be careful and don't touch your hands to your eyes!)
Separate garlic bud and clean cloves.
Slit holes all over turkey and insert cloves.
Also place cloves of garlic in stomach cavity of turkey. Mix a 4 ounce bottle of shrimp and crab boil with 4 (4 ounce) bottles of water and inject into turkey all over the body.
Place turkey in stainless steel roasting pan and cover with foil. Refrigerate for 24 to 48 hours, occasionally basting with juices in pan.
When ready to cook, heat oil.
Place whole turkey in oil and fry until tender.

_enrichment.description: A Cajun-spiced roast turkey with garlic cloves inserted for extra flavour, perfect for festive occasions.

title: Barbecue Coleslaw
ingredients: liquid red pepper seasoning to taste
directions: Combine vinegar and sugar in small saucepan.
Cook over low heat just until sugar dissolves.
Stir in barbecue sauce, water, mayonnaise, mustard powder and red pepper seasoning.
Combine cabbage and carrots in large bowl.
Pour dressing over; toss to combine.

_enrichment.description: A tangy barbecue coleslaw with a creamy dressing, balancing sweet and spicy notes for a refreshing side.

title: Creole Pork Chops
directions: Salt and pepper chops; brown well in skillet, then put in baking dish.
Combine remaining ingredients and pour over chops. Bake, uncovered, at 325° for 1 1/2 hours, or until tender.
Turn chops after an hour.
Have a barbecued taste.

_enrichment.description: Pork chops baked in a tangy, barbecue-style sauce with a hint of ginger, offering a savoury-sweet glaze.

title: Tex-Mex Turkey Chili
ingredients: 1 (8 oz.) can tomatoes and green chilies (undrained)
1 (15 oz.) can red kidney beans, drained
directions: Combine onion, garlic and oil in a 2-quart glass bowl.
Cover with plastic wrap.
Microwave on High for 3 minutes or until onion is tender, stirring every minute.
Add chili powder and the next 4 ingredients; stir well.
Microwave, uncovered, on High 6 to 9 minutes, stirring every 3 minutes.
Add the remaining ingredients. Microwave, uncovered, on High for 6 minutes until thoroughly heated.

_enrichment.description: A hearty Tex-Mex turkey chilli with kidney beans and spices, ideal for a warming meal.

title: Shrimp Creole Microwave
directions: Place butter in 2 1/2-quart casserole.
Heat full power 20 to 30 seconds or until melted.
Stir in green pepper, onion and celery. Cook full power 6 to 8 minutes until vegetables are tender. Stir halfway through. Sprinkle vegetables with flour and stir to blend.
Mix in tomatoes. Cook full power until heated, 4 to 6 minutes. Stir halfway through cooking time. Blend in remaining ingredients. Cook full power about 6 minutes, or until shrimp are fully cooked.
Stir twice during time.
Remove bay leaf.

_enrichment.description: A quick and flavourful Creole-style shrimp dish with tomatoes and spices, cooked in the microwave for convenience.

title: Baked Bluefish Fillets With Creole Sauce
directions: Mix the first 5 ingredients together in a 2-quart pan and saute together for 10 minutes over medium heat.
Add remaining ingredients, all but bluefish fillets and mix well over medium-low heat until vegetables are fully cooked, about 15 minutes.
While this is cooking, bake bluefish fillets in a 400° oven until fully cooked, approximately 30 minutes.
When fish is cooked, portion on plates and top with Creole sauce.

_enrichment.description: Bluefish fillets baked and topped with a rich Creole sauce featuring celery, peppers, and herbs.

title: Cajun-Style Chicken Nuggets
ingredients: 1 envelope Lipton onion or onion-mushroom soup mix
2 lb. boneless chicken breast, cut into 1-inch pieces
directions: In large bowl mix onion soup mix, bread crumbs, chili powder, cumin, thyme and pepper.
Dip chicken in bread crumb mixture; coating well.
In large skillet heat 1/2-inch oil and cook chicken over medium heat turning once until tender.
Drain on paper towel. Serve warm and could be served with assorted mustards.
Makes about 5 dozen.

_enrichment.description: Crispy Cajun-style chicken nuggets with a spiced breadcrumb coating, perfect for a quick snack or meal.

title: Creole Muffins
directions: Sift flour, baking powder, salt, sugar and corn meal together. Combine eggs and milk and add to dry ingredients, stirring only enough to moisten.
Add oil, butter, peppers, onion, pimiento and cheese.
Combine, stirring only enough to moisten.
Do not overstir.
Place in greased muffin tins. Bake at 400° for 20 to 25 minutes.
Makes 3 1/2 to 4 dozen.

_enrichment.description: Savoury Creole muffins with peppers, cheese, and cornmeal, offering a spicy kick in every bite.

title: Cajun Shrimp
ingredients: 1 3/4 lb. medium shrimp, peeled and deveined
directions: In large skillet, melt 1/2 cup butter.
Add onions, salt, garlic, peppers, basil, thyme and oregano; stir well.
Add shrimp; saute until pink, about 1 minute, shaking the pan.
Add mushrooms, stock and remaining butter.

_enrichment.description: Buttery Cajun shrimp sautéed with garlic, herbs, and mushrooms, served over rice for a satisfying meal.

title: Creole Tomatoes
directions: Cook bacon until crisp.
Drain, chop and set aside.
In the skillet, leave just enough bacon grease to saute onions until transparent.
Add to this the pulp, scooped carefully with teaspoon, from the tomatoes (do not scrape shells too thin).
Add the mashed eggs and enough bread crumbs to make a firm mixture. Season with butter, salt, pepper and parsley (if necessary, add a little canned tomato juice).
Add bacon last and stuff tomatoes with mixture.
Sprinkle tops with buttered bread crumbs.
Place stuffed tomatoes in a baking dish and bake at 350° until thoroughly heated and buttered bread crumbs are browned.
Do not cook long enough for tomatoes to lose shape.
Serve hot.

_enrichment.description: A hearty Southern dish featuring tomatoes stuffed with a savoury mixture of scrambled eggs, bacon, and breadcrumbs, baked until tender.

title: Southern Yeast Biscuit Dough
ingredients: 2 pkg. (1/4 oz. each) active dry yeast
directions: Combine the water and sugar in a small bowl.
Stir in the yeast. Set aside until the mixture becomes bubbly. In a large mixing bowl, combine the flour and baking soda.
Stir to mix. Cut in the shortening using a pastry blender, a fork or your fingertips until the mixture resembles cornmeal.
Add the yeast mixture and buttermilk. Stir with a large wooden spoon until well mixed.
This will not be a smooth dough.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate the dough until needed; it will keep about 1 week. Makes about 8 cups of dough.

_enrichment.description: A classic Southern biscuit dough made with yeast, buttermilk, and shortening, perfect for flaky, tender biscuits.

title: Southern Style Matzo Ball Soup
ingredients: 2 large potatoes, cut in large pieces
directions: In large pot, boil 2 quarts water.
Add in order the broth, potatoes, carrots and onions.
Let cook at boiling.
Combine eggs and oil; beat well.
Add matzo meal and stir well.

Refrigerate matzo mixture for at least 15 minutes, then take teaspoon size portions of matzo meal, roll into balls and drop into broth. Reduce to simmer for 30 minutes or until balls are fluffy and veggies are tender.
Add peas and butter.
Serve hot with rolls or cornbread.

_enrichment.description: A comforting Southern soup featuring matzo balls, potatoes, carrots, and onions in a rich chicken broth.

title: Southern Key Lime Pie
ingredients: 1/2 to 3/4 tsp. finely shredded key lime peel or 1 1/2 tsp. finely shredded Persian lime peel
1/3 c. lime juice (8 to 10 key limes or 2 to 3 Persian limes)
directions: Separate egg yolks from whites; set whites aside for meringue.
Place the egg yolks in a medium mixing bowl; with a fork or whisk beat egg yolks till combined.
Gradually stir in the condensed milk and lime peel.
Add lime juice and water, stirring well (the mixture will thicken).
Pour into the baked pastry shell.
Bake in a 325° oven for 30 minutes.
Remove from oven and transfer to a wire rack.
Increase oven temperature to 350°.

_enrichment.description: A tangy and sweet lime pie with a creamy custard filling and fluffy meringue topping, a Southern classic.

title: Creole Rice
ingredients: 2 (10 oz.) cans tomatoes with green chiles, undrained
directions: Drain tomatoes, reserving 1 cup juice.
Combine juice, water and next 5 ingredients in a large saucepan.
Bring to a boil; add vegetables and rice.
Cover, reduce heat and simmer 20 minutes or until rice is tender and liquid is absorbed.
Yield: 4 to 6 servings.

_enrichment.description: A simple yet flavourful Creole rice dish with tomatoes, green peppers, and herbs, cooked to perfection.

title: J'S Southern Gumbo
ingredients: 1 (10 oz.) can diced tomatoes with green chili peppers
directions: To make a roux, heat oil in a heavy pot or saucepan.
Sprinkle flour into oil, stirring constantly.
Cook over low heat for 40 to 45 minutes, stirring often.
Cook until the mixture is the color of caramel and has a gravy like consistency.
Add onion, bell pepper, banana peppers and garlic to the roux; cook 15 minutes. Add tomato sauce, diced tomatoes and okra; cook an additional 15 minutes.
Add shrimp and cook for 10 minutes.
Season to taste.

_enrichment.description: A rich and spicy gumbo with shrimp, okra, and a dark roux, served over rice for a hearty meal.

title: Creole Bean Soup
directions: Panfry pork until brown on all sides, then add onion and cook until soft.
Blend in flour; add water slowly, stirring constantly, to keep mixture smooth.
Add potatoes, celery, carrots, tomatoes and salt.
Cover and cook gently until vegetables are soft.
Add beans, simmer about 10 minutes.
Stir in green pepper and parsley and serve.

_enrichment.description: A creamy bean soup with salt pork, vegetables, and beans, simmered to a rich and hearty consistency.

title: Macaroni Ala Creole
directions: Cook macaroni in plenty of water with salt.
Drain water off. Cook hamburger and onions in bacon drippings until nicely browned. Add chopped mushrooms.
Add macaroni and cook 5 minutes longer. Add seasoning and green pepper (chopped) to the tomatoes (chopped).
Simmer 10 minutes; then add to the macaroni mixture in the frying pan or baking dish.
Set in oven to brown for 20 minutes at 375°.
Serve hot.
Serves 8.

_enrichment.description: A baked macaroni dish with ground beef, mushrooms, and tomatoes, topped with cheese for a comforting meal.

title: Creole Steak
directions: Cook steak in small amount of oil until brown.
Add onions, pepper, garlic, rice, water and tomatoes.
Turn fire down and let steam until rice is done.
Then add salt and red pepper to taste. You may need to add more water, depending on your taste.

_enrichment.description: A simple Southern steak dish cooked with rice, tomatoes, and peppers for a flavourful one-pot meal.

title: American Shortcake
ingredients: sweetened whipped cream or frozen whipped topping
directions: Mix berries with sugar; set aside.
Mix dry ingredients with rind and cut in shortening until crumbly.
Stir in milk just until blended.
Turn out on lightly floured surface and knead 10 to 12 times.
Pat out to 1/2-inch thickness and cut into 3-inch circles. Place 1-inch apart on ungreased cookie sheet and bake at 450° until golden brown, 10 to 12 minutes.
Split and fill with whipped cream and berries.

_enrichment.description: A classic American shortcake with fresh berries and whipped cream, perfect for a summer dessert.

title: Beef-Rice Creole
directions: Cook and stir ground beef in 10-inch skillet until light brown; drain.
Sprinkle beef with flour and salt.
Stir in green pepper, onion and parsley; cook and stir over low heat until onion is tender, about 5 minutes.
Stir water and tomatoes (with liquid) into beef mixture.
Break up tomatoes with fork.
Heat to boiling. Reduce heat; cover and let stand 5 minutes.
Yields 4 servings.

_enrichment.description: A hearty beef and rice dish with tomatoes and peppers, simmered to a rich and flavourful consistency.

title: American Spaghetti
ingredients: 2 cans tomatoes (1 large, approximately 24 oz. and 1 small 16 oz.)
directions: Saute bacon, green pepper, onion and mushrooms 5 minutes.
Add ground beef and cook until brown. Add salt and pepper to taste. Add tomatoes (break up).
Combine with spaghetti.
Pour into large baking dish and bake 30 minutes at 350°.
Top with cheese, if desired.

_enrichment.description: A baked spaghetti dish with ground beef, bacon, and tomatoes, topped with cheese for a comforting meal.

title: Catfish Creole
ingredients: 1/2 c. each chopped celery and green pepper
1 large can (1 lb. 12 oz.) whole tomatoes
1 Tbsp. each Worcestershire sauce and paprika
1/4 tsp. each thyme and hot sauce
2 lb. skinned, dressed catfish, cut in 1 1/2-inch thick steaks
directions: Melt butter in wide frying pan over medium heat. Add onion, celery, green pepper and garlic. Cook until soft, about 7 minutes. Add tomatoes and their liquid (break up tomatoes with a spoon), lemon slices, Worcestershire sauce, paprika, bay leaf, salt, thyme and liquid hot sauce.
Cook, stirring occasionally, for about 15 minutes or until sauce is slightly thickened.
Press fish pieces down into sauce.
Cover pan and simmer until fish flakes. Serve over hot cooked rice.

_enrichment.description: A Creole-style catfish dish with a rich tomato and vegetable sauce, served over rice.

title: Tex-Mex Won Tons
ingredients: cooking oil or shortening (for deep frying)
directions: For Filling:
In a large skillet, cook ground beef, onion and green pepper until meat is brown and vegetables are tender.
Drain off fat.
Stir beans, cheese, catsup, chili powder and cumin into meat mixture; mix well.

_enrichment.description: Tex-Mex-style won tons filled with spiced beef, beans, and cheese, fried until crispy.

title: Southern Green Beans
directions: Slice bacon into 1/2-inch pieces and saute until crisp. Drain, reserving 1 1/2 cups fat.
Saute onions in fat until golden.
Cook green beans in kettle with onions, vinegar and pepper until beans are desired degree of doneness.
Add bacon bits and chopped peanuts.
Makes 50 (2 1/2 ounce) servings.

_enrichment.description: A Southern-style green bean dish with crispy bacon, peanuts, and a tangy vinegar dressing.

title: Shrimp Creole Soup
ingredients: 1 pouch Campbell's chicken rice soup mix
generous dash of hot pepper sauce
1/2 lb. medium shrimp, peeled and deveined
directions: In 2-quart saucepan over medium-high heat, cook celery, green onions and garlic in oil until celery is tender, stirring often. Stir in soup mix, tomatoes with their liquid, water, bay leaf, sugar, thyme, pepper and hot pepper sauce.
Heat to boiling, stirring occasionally.
Reduce heat to low.
Cover; simmer 5 minutes.
Stir in shrimp; cover.
Simmer 5 minutes more or until shrimp are opaque.
Remove bay leaf before serving.

_enrichment.description: A hearty soup featuring shrimp in a rich tomato-based broth with aromatic vegetables, perfect for a comforting meal.

title: Cajun Dirty Rice
ingredients: 1 (8 oz.) pkg. Swanson frozen chicken livers, thawed and chopped
1 pouch Campbell's onion soup and recipe mix
directions: In 10-inch skillet over medium-high heat, cook livers, green pepper, celery and garlic in butter until vegetables are tender, stirring often.
Stir soup mix, water and hot pepper sauce into skillet.
Heat to boiling; stir in rice.
Reduce heat to low. Cover; simmer 20 minutes or until rice is tender.
Makes 6 servings.

_enrichment.description: A spicy and flavourful rice dish made with chicken livers and vegetables, offering a taste of Cajun cuisine.

title: Southern Banana Pudding
ingredients: 1 (3 1/4 oz.) pkg. Jell-O brand vanilla flavor pudding
directions: Combine Jell-O pudding, egg yolks and milk in saucepan.
Cook and stir over medium heat until mixture comes to a full boil. Remove from heat.
Arrange layer of vanilla wafers on bottom and sides of 1 1/2-quart baking dish.
Add a layer of banana slices and of pudding.
Continue layering wafers, bananas and pudding, ending with pudding.

_enrichment.description: A classic Southern dessert layered with vanilla wafers, bananas, and creamy custard, ideal for a sweet treat.

title: Southern Fried Chicken With Gravy
directions: Sift together flour, baking powder and salt.
Beat eggs until fluffy.
Add milk, salad oil and lemon juice.
Add this mixture to flour mixture and blend.
Pour 2 inches of oil into a large, heavy skillet and heat.
Dip chicken pieces into batter and drop into hot oil.
Reduce heat to medium and fry about 45 minutes, turning frequently to brown all sides of chicken.
Remove chicken to platter.
Pour most of oil from skillet.
Add amount of milk desired for gravy, heat, stirring brown particles from bottom of skillet.

_enrichment.description: Crispy fried chicken served with a rich gravy, a Southern classic that’s both indulgent and satisfying.

title: Southern Yam Casserole
ingredients: 2 (1 lb.) cans yams, drained or 4 medium yams, cooked, peeled and halved
1 medium apple, pared, cored and sliced
directions: Arrange yams, apple and dates in greased, shallow, 1 1/2-quart casserole.
Sprinkle with lemon juice, flavoring, nutmeg, walnuts and brown sugar.
Dot with butter or margarine.
Bake in 350° (moderate) oven 20 to 25 minutes.

_enrichment.description: A sweet and nutty casserole featuring yams, apples, and dates, baked to perfection for a comforting side dish.

title: Tex-Mex Stew
directions: In Dutch oven, brown meat with garlic and chopped onion. Drain off grease.
Drain and coarsely chop the tomatoes.

Reserve juice. Add tomatoes, juice, picante sauce, cumin, salt and pepper to meat mixture. Bring to a boil. Reduce heat. Add corn and beans. Cover and simmer for 20 to 30 minutes. Serve with additional picante sauce.
One dish dinner needs only a pan of cornbread or nacho chips.

_enrichment.description: A Tex-Mex-inspired stew packed with ground beef, beans, and tomatoes, offering a hearty one-pot meal.

title: Southern Corn Soup
ingredients: 3 1/2 c. fresh corn kernels (8 to 10 ears) or frozen corn
fresh oregano and fried tortilla triangles (garnish)
directions: Combine corn and chicken broth in blender or food processor and puree.
In a 3-quart saucepan, combine butter and corn mixture and simmer slowly 5 minutes, stirring to keep corn from sticking to bottom of pan.
Add milk, garlic, oregano, salt and pepper and bring to boil.
Reduce heat and add green chilies and chicken. Simmer 5 minutes.
Remove soup from heat and add cheese and baking soda (to prevent curdling).
Stir until melted.

_enrichment.description: A creamy corn soup with tender chicken and a hint of spice, blending Southern and Southwestern flavours.

title: Microwave Okra Creole
directions: In a 2-quart casserole dish, heat onion and butter 3 1/2 to 5 minutes until onion is tender.
Add okra, tomatoes, Worcestershire sauce and pepper sauce.
Heat, covered, 22 to 25 minutes until vegetables are tender, stirring twice.
Season with salt and pepper.
Let stand 5 minutes before serving.

_enrichment.description: A quick and easy Creole-style okra dish with tomatoes and spices, perfect for a light yet flavourful meal.

title: Early American Cornbread
directions: Stir in cornmeal to boiling water and mix well.
Add shortening, molasses and salt and set aside to cool.
Dissolve the yeast in the warm water for 5 minutes.
Add flour and cornmeal mix; knead until smooth and put in greased bowl in warm place and let rise until double in size.
Knead; let rise again.
Bake at 375° for 1 hour.

_enrichment.description: A traditional cornbread recipe with a moist texture and slightly sweet flavour, ideal for any meal.

title: Southern Beef And Greens Soup
ingredients: 1/2 lb. collards or spinach, thinly sliced
directions: Place beef, water, salt and pepper into Dutch oven; cover tightly and simmer 1 1/2 hours.
Add greens, basil and marjoram and continue cooking, covered, 30 minutes.
Cut potatoes into cubes.
Add potatoes, onion and tomatoes to meat and cook for 40 minutes longer.
Remove shanks; cut meat from each and return meat to soup.
Makes 5 to 6 servings.

_enrichment.description: A nourishing soup with tender beef shanks and greens, simmered with potatoes and tomatoes for a hearty dish.

title: Southern Chocolate Pie
directions: In saucepan, beat egg yolks slightly; add 1 cup sugar slowly until mixed and creamy.
Sift dry ingredients and add to sugar mixture.
Mix until all blends; add milk and vanilla gradually and cook on low heat until thick.
Pour into baked pie shell.
Beat egg whites until stiff.
Add 1/4 cup sugar and 1 teaspoon cream of tartar.
Top pie with meringue and brown at 400°.

_enrichment.description: A decadent chocolate pie with a silky custard filling and a flaky crust, a must-try for chocolate lovers.

title: Southern Hash
directions: Brown onion and bell pepper in butter; remove from pan.
Brown ground beef until redness is gone.
Add onion, bell pepper, rice, tomatoes, salt, pepper and chili powder.
Pour into greased dish. Bake at 350° for 1 hour.

_enrichment.description: A rustic Southern hash with ground beef, rice, and tomatoes, baked until golden and full of flavour.

title: Spicy Shrimp Creole
directions: Saute onion, green pepper, celery and garlic in butter in a Dutch oven until tender.
Stir in tomatoes, tomato sauce, Worcestershire sauce, oregano, thyme and red pepper.
Cook over medium heat stirring occasionally about 15 minutes or until desired consistency.
Stir in shrimp and simmer over medium heat 5 to 10 minutes or until shrimp is done.
Serve over rice.
Makes 4 to 6 servings.

_enrichment.description: A spicy and aromatic shrimp dish served over rice, showcasing bold Cajun flavours.

title: Shrimp Creole(For 4)
ingredients: 1 tsp. chili powder or to taste
1 lb. medium shrimp, peeled and cleaned
directions: Melt 2 teaspoons margarine in a heavy saucepan and add celery, onion and green pepper.
Saute until onions are clear.
Add tomatoes and seasonings and simmer 20 minutes.
Add shrimp and cook for 3 minutes or until shrimp turns pink.
Sprinkle with parsley. Serve on rice.

_enrichment.description: A classic Creole shrimp dish with tomatoes and herbs, served over rice for a simple yet flavourful meal.

title: Cajun Stewed Okra
ingredients: 2 ears of corn cut from cob
directions: Soak okra in cold water about 30 minutes before cooking.
Drain and cut into pieces.
Sprinkle with salt, pepper, garlic powder and season all.
Drop okra into a bag into which you have placed the flour.
Shake bag coating the okra.
In large skillet covered with oil and heated, drop okra in and fry until slime is gone.
Add all dry ingredients while stirring over low heat to keep from sticking. When okra is tender add all other ingredients cover and cook over low heat adding water as needed to keep from sticking.
Stir often. You may add more salt, pepper, etc, to reach flavor desired. Should cook at least 1 hour.

_enrichment.description: A Cajun-style okra stew with tomatoes and corn, offering a spicy and hearty side dish.

title: Butterflied Southern Citrus Barbecue
ingredients: 1 Tbsp. grated grapefruit or lemon peel
directions: Place lamb in large glass or enamel bowl.
In measuring cup, combine remaining ingredients; pour over lamb.
Cover and refrigerate several hours or overnight.
Drain lamb, reserving marinade.

Grill lamb 4 to 6 inches above coals or source of heat for 1 hour and 15 minutes or until meat thermometer inserted in thickest part registers 140° for rare or 150° to 155° for medium. Baste with marinade.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Butterflied Southern Citrus Barbecue.

title: Buffalo Cookies
ingredients: 1 c. oats (quick or old fashioned), uncooked
directions: Preheat oven to 350°.
Grease cookie sheet. Combine Crisco, sugar, brown sugar, milk and vanilla in a large bowl. Beat at low speed until blended.
Add eggs and beat at medium speed until blended. Combine flour, baking powder, baking soda and salt. Add gradually to creamed mixture at low speed.
Stir in oats, cornflakes, chocolate chips, nuts and coconut.
Using a 1/4 cup measuring cup, which is filled and leveled off with a knife, drop mixture onto cookie sheet 3-inches apart.
Bake for 13 to 15 minutes or until lightly browned around the edges but still slightly soft in the center. Cool 3 minutes on cookie sheet before removing to rack with a wide, thin pancake turner.
Makes 2 to 2 1/2 dozen cookies.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Buffalo Cookies.

title: Southern Angel Biscuits(Regular Recipe)
directions: Dissolve the yeast in warm water; set aside.
In a medium mixing bowl, mix 3 1/2 cups of flour with other dry ingredients; cut in shortening.
Stir in buttermilk, yeast mixture and egg; blend thoroughly.
Turn out onto a lightly floured surface; knead slightly, adding remaining flour, if necessary.
Roll out dough to 1/2-inch thickness.
Cut with a 2-inch biscuit cutter, dipping cutter into flour as needed.
Place on a lightly greased baking sheet; let dough rise slightly.
Bake at 400° for 12 to 15 minutes or until lightly browned.
Yield:
about 24 biscuits.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Southern Angel Biscuits(Regular Recipe).

title: Buffalo Claws
directions: Whisk together all ingredients in a small bowl except for crab.
Arrange claws on a broiling pan and brush both sides of each claw with butter mixture.
Broil 5 to 6 inches from heat for 3 to 4 minutes, or just until crab is hot.
Remove from oven and brush lightly with remaining butter mix.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Buffalo Claws.

title: Southern Cream Cookies(5 Dozen)
directions: Cream together shortening and sugar.
Add and mix well eggs, vanilla and sour cream.
This can be the store bought regular or nonfat or the best is soured whipping cream.
Add after mixed together the flour, salt, baking powder and baking soda.
Add nutmeats.
Drop from teaspoon onto lightly sprayed (vegetable oil) cookie sheet.
Spray the bottom of a glass or a cookie press.
Dip into a mixture of 3 tablespoons sugar and 1 teaspoon cinnamon. Press cookies flat.
Bake in moderate oven at 350° for 15 minutes.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Southern Cream Cookies(5 Dozen).

title: Cajun Cole Slaw
directions: Slice cabbage.
Mix all ingredients together and add to the cabbage.
Thin the mayonnaise with the beaten egg white and add to the slaw.
(A little vinegar and/or sugar may be added according to your personal taste.)

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Cajun Cole Slaw.

title: Cajun Rush'S Chargrilled Shrimp
ingredients: jumbo shrimp (15 to 20 count; 6 per person)
directions: Peel shrimp, except for final tail section (provides a handle for eating).
Mix all other ingredients in a flat casserole or baking dish and marinate shrimp in resulting sauce for a few minutes.
When chargrill is good and hot, place shrimp on grill. Watch carefully, shrimp cook quickly (2 to 3 minutes).
Turn shrimp after a minute or so and baste with marinade.
Remove from grill as soon as they lose their translucency and turn whitish. Don't overcook! Baste again and serve immediately.
Shrimp should be juicy and full of flavor.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Cajun Rush'S Chargrilled Shrimp.

title: Southern Maryland Oyster Stew
directions: Place oysters in saucepan, add water and cook until oysters are plump and edges curl.
Remove oysters and add to milk.
Add salt and pepper, then strained oyster juice.
Add butter, heat and serve with crackers.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Southern Maryland Oyster Stew.

title: Apple Cake American
directions: Grease an 8-inch square pan.
Slice 6 apples and spread in the bottom of the pan.
Sprinkle with 1/2 cup sugar and cinnamon.
Mix remaining ingredients together in a bowl and pour over apples. Bake 60 minutes at 300°.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Apple Cake American.

title: American Goulash
directions: Cook macaroni according to package directions and drain well. Brown meat, onion and pepper.
Add cooked and drained macaroni, seasonings, tomatoes and tomato soup.
Pour into greased 2-quart casserole.
Mix crumbs with melted margarine and sprinkle over top.
Grate cheese over top of casserole.
Bake at 350° for 30 minutes.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for American Goulash.

title: Gumbo A La Creole
ingredients: chicken, smoked pork sausage and shrimp
directions: In large gumbo pot, make a roux with oil and flour.
Cook over medium heat, stirring constantly, until roux is a dark caramel color.
Remove from fire and add remaining ingredients, except shrimp, 1/2 cup green onions, file and rice.
Boil for about 2 hours, or until meat is tender and gumbo is thickened.
In the last 15 minutes of boiling, add reserved green onions and shrimp. Remove from fire and add file.
Serves 5 or 6.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Gumbo A La Creole.

title: Turkey Creole
ingredients: 3 c. cut up, cooked turkey or chicken
