directions: In 12-inch skillet over medium heat, in hot salad oil, cook onion, green pepper and garlic until onion and green pepper are tender-crisp, about 5 minutes.
Add 2 cups water, tomatoes with their liquid, chili powder, salt, sugar, oregano leaves and pepper.
Heat to boiling.
Stir in rice.
Reduce heat to low; cover skillet and simmer for 25 minutes or until rice is tender and liquid is absorbed.
Stir in turkey and heat thoroughly. Yields 4 servings.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Turkey Creole.

title: Cajun Red Beans And Rice Salad
ingredients: 1 tsp. Louisiana hot sauce or hot pepper sauce
directions: In large bowl, combine rice, red beans, tomato, green bell pepper, red onion and garlic; mix well.
In small jar with tight-fitting lid, combine the oil, vinegar, chili powder, seasoned salt, salt, pepper and hot sauce and shake well.
Pour dressing over salad and toss gently to coat.
Cover and refrigerate 1 hour to blend flavors.
Makes 12 servings.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Cajun Red Beans And Rice Salad.

title: Cajun Heath Cake
directions: Sift together flour and sugar; set aside.
Place margarine, cocoa and water in a pot; bring to a boil.
Pour hot mixture on flour and sugar; mix well.
Add buttermilk, soda, vanilla and eggs. Mix well.
Pour in greased and floured oblong pan (9 x 13-inch). Bake 20 to 25 minutes at 400°.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Cajun Heath Cake.

title: Southern Pecan Pie(9-Inch Pie)
directions: Mix together eggs, sugar, corn syrup, butter, vanilla, and pecan pieces.
Pour filling into prepared pie shell.
Arrange the pecan halves around the inside edge.
Bake at 400° for 10 minutes. Reduce heat to 350°.
Bake for 40 minutes longer or until the center is firm.
Cool to room temperature before cutting.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Southern Pecan Pie(9-Inch Pie).

title: Tex-Mex Bean Dip
directions: In large 11 x 13-inch casserole dish, layer bean dip; set aside.
Combine avocados, lemon juice, salt and pepper.
Mix well and spread over bean dip; set aside.
Combine salsa dressing, sour cream and taco seasoning.
Mix well and spread over avocado mixture, then layer chopped green onion, diced tomatoes, Cheddar cheese and black olives.
Serve with toasted corn chips.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Tex-Mex Bean Dip.

title: Cajun Corn Soup
ingredients: 1 can (14 1/2 oz.) Cajun style stewed tomatoes
2 pkg. (16 oz. each) frozen whole kernel corn
directions: In a large Dutch oven, saute onion, green pepper and green onions in oil until tender, about 5 minutes.
Add flour and cook until bubbly.
Add water, stewed tomatoes, chopped tomatoes and tomato paste; mix well.
Stir in the corn, ham, sausage, hot pepper sauce, cayenne pepper and salt.
Bring to boil, stirring frequently.
Reduce heat; simmer uncovered for 1 hour, stirring occasionally.
Yields 12 to 14 servings.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Cajun Corn Soup.

title: Eggs Creole
directions: Cook the onion and green pepper in oil in a large frypan, until the onion is lightly browned.
Add the tomatoes and water; heat to boiling.
Add the uncooked rice, salt and pepper.
Cover; cook over low heat until rice is tender, 25 to 30 minutes.
Stir occasionally with a fork to keep from sticking.
If the rice becomes dry, add a little more water.
Drop eggs on rice.
Cover; simmer 5 to 10 minutes, or until eggs are as firm as desired.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Eggs Creole.

title: 15 Bean Soup - Cajun Style
ingredients: 1 pkg. Cajun 15 bean soup (located in the dried bean/pea section)
1 small pkg. Italian sausage (I use mild)
directions: Soak beans.
Drain and add more water and 1 tablespoon salt. Cook until almost tender (time varies, usually 1 to 2 hours). Brown Italian sausage and drain.
Add to beans along with other ingredients.
Use seasoning packet with caution; it makes the beans very hot.
Continue to boil on medium to low heat.
Serve with cornbread.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for 15 Bean Soup - Cajun Style.

title: Tex-Mex Dip(7 Layers)
directions: This dish is assembled in layers.
Spread bean dip in 10-inch pie plate.
Mix next four ingredients and spread on top of layer one.
Mix next three ingredients and spread on top of layer two. Each of next four ingredients serves as a layer, in order found in ingredients list.
Serve with round taco chips.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Tex-Mex Dip(7 Layers).

title: Southern-Style Jambalaya
ingredients: 1/2 lb. boned chicken breasts, cut into pieces
directions: Cook and stir chicken in hot butter in large skillet until lightly browned.
Add sausage, green pepper, onion and celery; cook until vegetables are just tender, about 5 minutes.
Mix in tomatoes and water; bring to a full boil.
Stir in rice, salt and hot pepper sauce.
Cover; remove from heat.
Let stand 5 minutes. Fluff with fork; sprinkle with parsley.
Makes 4 servings.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Southern-Style Jambalaya.

title: All-American Hot Dish(Makes 6 Servings)
directions: Cook and stir meat and onion in large skillet until meat is browned.
Drain off fat.
Stir in corn (with liquid), tomato sauce, olives, noodles, water, oregano, salt, pepper and cheese. Heat mixture to boiling.
Lower heat and simmer, uncovered, stirring occasionally, 20 minutes or until noodles are tender. Sprinkle with parsley.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for All-American Hot Dish(Makes 6 Servings).

title: Elbow Macaroni Creole
directions: Fry sausage and remove from pan, saving 4 tablespoons fat. Saute onions and pepper; add tomatoes and seasonings and simmer 15 minutes.
Add sausage.
Cook elbow macaroni.
Alternate layers of macaroni and sausage mixture.
Bake with bread crumbs on top at 350° for 30 minutes.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Elbow Macaroni Creole.

title: Creole Style Lentils
directions: Cook carrots, celery and onion in hot margarine in medium size saucepan until lightly browned, about 10 minutes.
Add tomatoes, salt, sugar, pepper, pepper sauce and lentils.
Cover; simmer for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Serve over rice.
Makes about 5 1/2 cups.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Creole Style Lentils.

title: Dixie Southern Pecan Pie
directions: Beat eggs lightly in a bowl.
Mix in corn syrup, sugar, butter, vanilla and salt.
Stir in chopped pecans.
Spoon mixture into pie shell, spreading pecan halves over mixture.
Bake at 400° for 15 minutes.
Reduce oven temperature to 350° and bake 30 to 35 minutes more, until a wooden pick, inserted in center, comes out clean.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Dixie Southern Pecan Pie.

title: Easy Chicken Creole
ingredients: 2 boneless chicken breasts, sliced into bite-size pieces
2 c. total sliced red/yellow/green bell peppers and onions
1/2 to 1 Tbsp. dry Cajun seasoning to taste
hot sauce (if desired; Tabasco green sauce is good, but not too hot)
directions: Heat olive oil in a large frying pan over medium heat.
Add chicken and saut until no longer pink (about 5 minutes).
Add peppers and onions and saut 5 more minutes, stirring as needed. Add Cajun seasoning and diced tomatoes.
Cover and simmer until tender but vegetables are still crispy (about 5 more minutes). Serve over hot rice.
Great with hot French bread and a green salad.
Serves 4.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Easy Chicken Creole.

title: Southern Fritters
directions: Sift flour, cornmeal, baking powder, salt and sugar together. Beat eggs with milk.
Add to sifted ingredients.
Quickly stir in melted butter or margarine and drop by teaspoonfuls into hot, deep fat.
Turn once to brown all over.
These puff up into round balls.
Serve very hot with maple syrup or honey or dust with powdered sugar.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Southern Fritters.

title: Ranch House Cookie
directions: Combine shortening, eggs and vanilla.
Sift together flour, baking powder, soda and salt.
Add both sugars to shortening, egg mixture, stirring completely.
Add flour mixture and remaining ingredients, mixing well after each addition.
Roll in small balls the size of walnuts and bake at 350° for 12 to 15 minutes.
Makes approximately 100 cookies.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Ranch House Cookie.

title: Southern Chicken Fricassee
directions: Melt butter.
Fry butter and flour in sauce pan on medium heat till dark brown.
Immediately add water and stir till smooth. Add remaining ingredients.
Cook on low heat until chicken is tender. You can either cook dumplings in the fricassee gravy or serve over cooked rice.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Southern Chicken Fricassee.

title: American Fruit Tart
directions: On lightly floured surface, roll pastry to 12-inch circle. Place in 10-inch tart pan or quiche dish (9-inch pie plate may be substituted).
Prick bottom and sides of pastry with fork.
Bake at 400° for 9 to 11 minutes or until golden brown.
Cool.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for American Fruit Tart.

title: Beef And Rice Creole(Method:  One Pot)
directions: Dice green pepper and onion; set aside.
Cook hamburger until light brown, stirring continuously to crumble meat into small pieces.
Drain grease from meat into a heat-resistant can. Sprinkle meat with flour.
Add green pepper, onion, salt, pepper, parsley and garlic.
Cook over low heat until onion is tender. Break up tomatoes with a fork.
Stir broken up tomatoes and water into meat mixture; heat to a boil.
Reduce heat, cover and simmer 5 minutes.
Stir in rice.
Remove from heat; let stand, covered, 5 to 7 minutes.

_enrichment.description: A hearty one-pot dish combining minced beef with rice, tomatoes, and peppers in a savoury Creole-style sauce. Perfect for a comforting family meal.

title: Southern Corn Fritters
directions: Drain corn well, reserving 1/4 cup liquid.
Sift together flour, baking powder, salt, parsley flakes and paprika.
Combine eggs, melted butter or margarine and liquid drained from corn. Mix well.
Add to dry ingredients all at once and stir until smooth.
Fold in drained corn.
Drop batter from a tablespoon into deep fat, heated to 375° and fry a few at a time, until golden brown on all sides.
Remove from fat; drain on absorbent paper. Keep hot while frying remaining fritters.
Roll in confectioners sugar just before serving.
Makes 20 fritters.

_enrichment.description: Crispy golden fritters made with sweet corn, eggs, and a hint of spice, dusted with sugar for a delightful contrast. Ideal for a quick snack or brunch.

title: Southern Persimmon Pudding
directions: Sift dry ingredients together.
Add eggs to persimmon pulp, then add dry ingredients, butter and milk.
Add nut meats and mix well.
Bake in a greased dish at 350° until firm, about 1 hour. Serve with hard sauce, if desired.

_enrichment.description: A moist and spiced pudding made with ripe persimmons, perfect for a cosy autumn dessert. Serve warm with a dollop of hard sauce.

title: Low-Fat Microwave Tex-Mex Turkey Chili
ingredients: 1 (8 oz.) can no salt added tomato sauce
1 (10 oz.) can no salt added tomatoes and green chiles (undrained)
directions: Combine onion, garlic and oil in 2-quart glass measure.
Cover with plastic wrap; microwave on High 3 minutes or until onion is tender, stirring every minute.
Add chili powder and next 4 ingredients.
Stir well.
Microwave, uncovered, on High 6 to 9 minutes or until slightly thickened, stirring every 3 minutes. Add remaining ingredients; stir well.
Microwave, uncovered, on High 6 minutes or until thoroughly heated.

_enrichment.description: A low-fat, microwave-friendly Tex-Mex chilli packed with ground turkey, kidney beans, and warming spices. Quick and healthy for a weeknight dinner.

title: Creole Cauliflower
directions: Melt butter in saucepan; add onion and green pepper and brown lightly.
Blend in flour, stirring, then add tomatoes, salt and pepper.
Heat to boiling point and cook three minutes, stirring constantly.
Add cauliflower and heat through. Place mixture in casserole, top with cheese and bake at 350° until cheese melts and is lightly browned.

_enrichment.description: Cauliflower simmered in a rich tomato and cheese sauce, topped with melted cheddar for a comforting side dish. A great autumnal accompaniment.

title: Red Beans And Rice(Southern Dish)
directions: Wash beans and soak overnight or from 4 to 5 hours in cold water. Drain water. Place in a pot and cover with cold water. Heat slowly.
Add seasonings with meat, onion and chopped garlic. Boil at least 2 hours or until beans are tender.
Add rice.
Mix well and let steam 10 minutes.
Serves 6 to 8.

_enrichment.description: A classic Southern dish of red beans slow-cooked with ham, peppers, and spices, served over fluffy rice. A hearty meal for colder months.

title: Southern Tea Cakes(Cookies)
directions: Mix dry ingredients.
In separate bowl, mix the beaten eggs, oil, vanilla and sugar.
Pour egg mixture into dry ingredients. Drop from teaspoon onto ungreased cookie sheet; flatten with greased bottom of glass.
Bake until golden brown in a 350° oven.

_enrichment.description: Buttery, spiced tea cakes with a soft texture and a hint of nutmeg. A simple yet indulgent treat, perfect with tea.

title: Cajun Cornbread Skillet Casserole
directions: Brown beef in skillet.
Add taco mix, taco sauce and water. Mix eggs, cornmeal, salt, buttermilk, vegetable oil and corn. Grease a 10-inch iron skillet.
Pour half of the cornbread mixture in bottom of pan.
Add onion and bell pepper, then add meat mixture.
Pour remaining cornbread mixture on top. Bake at 350° for 45 to 60 minutes.

_enrichment.description: A savoury cornbread casserole layered with spiced beef, peppers, and onions. A hearty one-dish meal for a cosy dinner.

title: "Southern Living'S" Chocolate Sour Cream Pound Cake
directions: Beat butter at medium speed with an electric mixer about 2 minutes or until soft and creamy.
Gradually add sugars, beating at medium speed 5 to 7 minutes.
Add eggs, one at a time, beating just until yellow disappears.
Combine flour, baking soda and cocoa; add to creamed mixture alternately with sour cream, beginning and ending with flour mixture.
Mix at lowest speed until just blended after each addition.
Stir in vanilla.
Spoon batter into a greased and floured 10-inch tube pan.
Bake at 325° for 1 hour and 20 minutes or until a wooden pick inserted in center comes out clean. Cool in pan on a wire rack 10 to 15 minutes; remove from pan and cool completely on a wire rack.
Sprinkle with powdered sugar if desired.

_enrichment.description: A rich, moist pound cake with a deep chocolate flavour and a tender crumb, enhanced by sour cream for extra richness.

title: Baked Fish Creole
ingredients: 1 1/2 c. canned or fresh tomatoes, chopped
directions: Place fish fillets in greased shallow baking dish (the dish should be small enough so that the sauce completely covers fillets.
In small skillet melt butter and saute until tender, green pepper, onion and garlic.
Add and heat tomatoes.
Pour Creole sauce over top of fillets.
Place thin slices of lemon over top.
Bake uncovered in 425° oven for 20 minutes or until fish flakes easily.

_enrichment.description: Delicate fish fillets baked in a tangy Creole tomato sauce with peppers and onions, finished with lemon slices. A light yet flavourful main.

title: Cajun Rump Roast
directions: Make several deep slits in top and sides of roast with a paring knife.
Fill slits with garlic cloves and green onions. Rub roast with salt and red cayenne pepper.
Place in a large cooking bag that is in a large shallow oblong pan.
Place peeled potatoes, carrots and mushrooms around roast.
On top of roast, sprinkle 1/2 green onions, parsley and mint.
Mix wine, water, bitters and soy; pour around roast.
Seal bag; poke with holes. Bake 2 hours or so at 325° until done.

_enrichment.description: A robust Cajun-style rump roast slow-cooked with garlic, herbs, and vegetables in a flavourful wine and soy sauce marinade. Perfect for a special dinner.

title: Pork Chops, Rice Creole
directions: Season pork chops well on both sides with salt and pepper. Brown well in hot skillet.
Remove chops and drain off all but 1 tablespoon fat.
Slice 2 onions and brown with garlic, minced in the fat.
Add hot water to the pan and condensed tomato soup.
Add chopped green onion tops, if you have them (or use extra onion at point mentioned), diced celery, chopped green pepper, chopped parsley and raw rice.
Season with salt, pepper, bay leaf, thyme and marjoram.
Mix well and pour over chops in casserole.
Cover and bake in moderate oven at 350° for 1 1/4 hours or until meat and rice are tender.
If rice becomes dry toward end of baking time, add a little hot water.

_enrichment.description: Pork chops braised in a rich Creole sauce with rice, tomatoes, and herbs. A satisfying one-pot meal for a cosy evening.

title: Mama'S Shrimp Creole
ingredients: 1 lb. fresh mushrooms, cleaned and sliced
directions: In a large saucepan, combine tomatoes and seasonings.
Add vegetables and stir until thoroughly moistened. Cook over medium-high heat until vegetables are tender, stirring occasionally. Combine cornstarch and water.
Add shrimp.
Heat and stir just until boiling.
Remove bay leaf. Serve over rice.
Makes 6 servings.

_enrichment.description: Shrimp simmered in a spiced tomato sauce with mushrooms and peppers, served over fluffy rice. A vibrant and flavourful dish for a summer evening.

title: Smothered Cajun Chicken
directions: Marinate chicken in soy sauce, salt and hot sauce for 2 or 3 hours.
In a large skillet or Dutch oven, put oil, onion, pepper and garlic and saute until tender.
Add parsley and white wine to chicken.
Marinate and add water.
Cover and cook slow 1 to 1 1/2 hours or until done.

_enrichment.description: Tender chicken smothered in a spicy, herby sauce with onions and peppers. A comforting Southern classic for a hearty meal.

title: Fiery Cajun Shrimp
ingredients: 4 to 5 lb. fresh shrimp (medium to large) in shells
directions: Combine all ingredients, except shrimp and sliced lemons, in Dutch oven or large saucepan.
Bring to a gentle boil, stirring frequently; reduce to simmer after 1 minute.
Simmer about 20 minutes.
Pour a small amount of sauce in bottom of a casserole (to cover bottom), then make layers of shrimp alternating with lemon slices.
Pour sauce over all.
Bake at 375° for about 20 minutes, depending on size of shrimp.
Stir halfway through to ensure all shrimp are cooking.
Be sure to serve with fresh French bread for sopping up this delicious hot sauce.

_enrichment.description: Succulent shrimp baked in a fiery, buttery sauce with garlic, rosemary, and a kick of Tabasco. A bold and zesty dish for seafood lovers.

title: Creole Bread Pudding
ingredients: approximately 10 slices raisin bread, torn to make 4 c.
directions: Preheat oven to 350°.
Butter a 2-quart glass casserole dish. In large bowl, combine bread and milk and let soak.
In medium bowl, beat eggs slightly; stir in sugar, butter, vanilla and salt. Add to bread mixture; stir to mix.
Pour into dish; sprinkle with cinnamon.
Bake 45 minutes or until knife inserted comes out clean.
Serve warm with brandy sauce.

_enrichment.description: A rich, custardy bread pudding made with raisin bread, perfect for a cosy dessert with a hint of cinnamon. Serve warm with a drizzle of brandy sauce.

title: Southern Peach Cobbler
directions: Cut shortening into flour, salt and 1 tablespoon sugar with pastry blender.
Add just enough ice water to hold dough together. Roll out on floured board to about 1/8-inch thick.
Line the sides and bottom of a 2 to 2 1/2-quart baking dish with crust.
Flute edges of sides.
Cut remaining dough into 1 x 1 1/2-inch pieces. Set aside.

_enrichment.description: A classic Southern dessert featuring juicy peaches baked under a buttery, flaky crust, best enjoyed warm with a scoop of vanilla ice cream.

title: Southern Refrigerator Rolls
directions: To sweet milk, add sugar and shortening.
Put in saucepan and bring to boiling point; let cool until lukewarm.
Add yeast which has been dissolved in the warm water.
Stir in 6 cups flour.
Let rise in a warm place 2 hours.
Then add 5 more cups flour, salt and baking powder, which have been sifted together.
Grease top of dough and cover carefully with waxed paper.
Place in refrigerator until needed.
Allow 1 1/2 to 2 hours for final rising after rolls are in the pan.
Bake in moderate oven at 375° for 20 minutes.

_enrichment.description: Soft, pillowy rolls with a slightly sweet flavour, perfect for a family meal or as a side with butter. Best served warm from the oven.

title: Buffalo Chips(Cookies)
directions: Using a large bowl, blend butter and sugars.
Stir in eggs and vanilla.
Sift salt, flour, soda and baking powder together.
Add to bowl; mix well.
Stir in oats, corn flakes, chocolate chips, pecans and coconut.
Use an ice cream scoop to place 6 cookies on lightly greased cookie sheet.
Bake for 12 to 15 minutes at 350°. Cookies will be soft and chewy if you don't bake until brown. Makes about 36 very large cookies.

_enrichment.description: Chewy, oat-filled cookies packed with chocolate chips, pecans, and coconut, offering a delightful mix of textures and flavours.

title: Shrimp Creole(Total Cooking Time:  14 To 15 Minutes)
ingredients: 1 (16 oz.) can whole tomatoes, drained and chopped
1 (10 oz.) pkg. frozen cooked shrimp, thawed
directions: In 2-quart microproof casserole, combine green onions, celery, green pepper, butter and garlic.
Cook, covered, on High (Maximum power) 3 minutes or until green onions are transparent.
Stir in remaining ingredients.
Cook, covered, on 80 (Reheat) 5 minutes. Stir.
Cook, covered, on 80 (Reheat) 6 to 7 minutes or until hot. Let stand, covered, 5 minutes before serving over hot cooked rice. Makes 4 to 6 servings.

_enrichment.description: A quick and spicy Creole-style shrimp dish with a medley of vegetables, perfect for a weeknight dinner with rice.

title: Cajun Catfish
directions: Combine salt, pepper and hot sauce to create marinade.
Season catfish with salt and pepper to taste and marinate for 2 hours in refrigerator.
Put cornmeal into paper bag.
Drop catfish in bag, one at a time, and shake bag until fish are completely coated. Fry fish in deep, hot olive oil (375°) until fish floats to surface and are golden brown.
Drain on paper towels and serve hot.
Serves 4.

_enrichment.description: Crispy-coated catfish fillets marinated in a spicy, tangy blend, offering a satisfying crunch with every bite.

title: Potato Casserole Americana
ingredients: 8 to 10 potatoes, peeled, cubed and cooked
directions: Beat sour cream and cream cheese in a large bowl until blended.
Add hot potatoes; beat in milk and garlic, gradually beating until light and fluffy.
Season to taste with salt and pepper.
Spoon potatoes into a 2-quart casserole; dot with butter and sprinkle with paprika.
Bake, uncovered, at 350° until brown. Makes 10 servings.

_enrichment.description: A creamy, cheesy potato casserole with a golden, paprika-dusted top, ideal for a hearty side dish or main course.

title: Mom'S Stuffed Green Peppers With Homemade Creole Sauce
directions: Sauce:
Saute green pepper, onion and garlic in oil.
Simmer 5 minutes until soft, not browned.
Add remaining ingredients and simmer 40 to 50 minutes, until thick.
Add this mixture to 1 pound ground meat browned and sauteed with 1 tablespoon of grated onion and 1/3 cup finely chopped celery.
Add salt and pepper to taste. Cool 5 minutes or until vegetables are soft.
Add 1 1/2 cups cooked rice.
Moisten with Creole sauce, you may not want all of it.

_enrichment.description: Green peppers stuffed with seasoned ground meat and simmered in a rich, spiced Creole sauce, a comforting dish for any occasion.

title: Cajun Shrimp Squash Casserole
ingredients: 3 lb. yellow squash, sliced in 1/2-inch rounds
directions: In large black cast-iron skillet, saute onion in margarine until clear.
Add squash, sugar and spices.
Cover skillet and simmer on low fire until squash begins to wilt.
Add raw shrimp. Cover and simmer until shrimp turn red and curl.
Do not overcook. Serve as is or if you a real Cajun, serve over steamed rice.

_enrichment.description: A rustic Cajun-style casserole of tender squash and succulent shrimp, lightly spiced and best served with steamed rice.

title: The All American Dessert
ingredients: 1 (8 serving size) pkg. Jell-O and gelatin (any red flavor)
1 (8 serving size) pkg. Jell-O brand berry blue flavor gelatin
1 (8 oz.) tub Cool Whip nondairy whipped topping, thawed
4 c. all butter pound loaf, cubed
directions: Stir 2 cups boiling water into each flavor of gelatin in separate bowls until dissolved.
Stir 1 cup cold water into each bowl.
Pour into separate 13 x 9-inch pans.
Refrigerate at least 3 hours or until firm.
Cut into 1/2-inch cubes.
Layer gelatin cubes, whipped topping, cake and strawberries in 3-quart serving bowl.
Garnish with remaining whipped topping and Jell-O star jigglers.

_enrichment.description: A layered, colourful dessert of gelatin cubes, whipped topping, cake, and strawberries, perfect for a festive gathering.

title: Buffalo Chili Con Carne
ingredients: 1 lb. cubed or coarsely ground buffalo meat
2 cans (16 oz. each) diced tomatoes with liquid
1 (15 1/2 oz.) can dark red kidney beans, rinsed and drained
1 (15 oz.) can pinto beans, rinsed and drained
directions: In a large kettle or Dutch oven, brown meat in oil; drain. Add onion and green pepper; saute for 5 minutes.
Stir in remaining ingredients and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat, cover and simmer 1 1/2 to 2 hours or until the meat is tender.
Yields 6 servings (1 1/2 quarts).

_enrichment.description: A hearty, spicy chilli made with buffalo meat, beans, and chillies, ideal for a cosy meal on a chilly evening.

title: Southern Peanut Brittle
directions: Cook peanuts, sugar, syrup and salt in a heavy iron skillet, stirring constantly until dark golden brown (hard crack stage). Remove from heat.
Working quickly, add butter, vanilla and baking soda.
The soda should be added last as it will foam up.
Stir completely.
Pour onto a greased cookie sheet.
Do not try to spread it out; let it move by itself.
It will be thick and airy. Cool completely before breaking into pieces.
The easiest way to break it is to hit the bottom of the cookie sheet on a firm surface.

_enrichment.description: A crunchy, sweet peanut brittle with a hint of vanilla, perfect for sharing or as a festive treat.

title: Sugar-Free Oatmeal Cookies
directions: Preheat oven to 350°.
Mix pancake mix, oats and baking powder together.
Stir in remaining ingredients in order given and mix well.
Drop by teaspoon onto a sprayed cookie sheet (the cookies may also be dropped into muffin tins).
Bake 7 to 10 minutes or until lightly golden around edges.

_enrichment.description: Oatmeal cookies with a sugar-free twist, offering a chewy texture and a hint of vanilla, ideal for a guilt-free snack.

title: Tex-Mex Dip(Great For Super Bowl Or Pool Parties)
directions: Combine avocados, lemon juice, salt and pepper.
Combine sour cream, mayonnaise and taco seasoning.
Layer as follows:
bean dip, avocado mixture, sour cream mixture, onions, tomatoes, olives and Cheddar cheese.
Refrigerate overnight.
Set out at room temperature two hours before serving to allow mixture to soften. Yields 8 to 10 servings.

_enrichment.description: A creamy, layered Tex-Mex dip with avocado, sour cream, and cheese, perfect for sharing at parties or game days.

title: Fish Creole(Low Calorie Recipe)
ingredients: 1 lb. sole or haddock fillets, defrosted
directions: Rinse fish and pat dry with paper towels.
In 9 x 12-inch dish, arrange in dish with the thickest pieces to the outside edge of dish.
In 1-quart glass measure, stir together tomato sauce, mushrooms, green peppers, celery, water, minced onion and bouillon.
Pour evenly over fish.
Cover tightly.
Bake in 350° oven for about 30 to 40 minutes, until fish is opaque.

_enrichment.description: A light and flavourful low-calorie fish dish with a tangy tomato sauce, perfect for a healthy yet satisfying meal.

title: Southern Comfort Sausage Cake(In Honor Of Princess Patty)
directions: Bring the raisins and the water to a boil before adding to the other ingredients.
Stir or mix with hands the sausage and light brown sugar together.
Add the boiled raisins and water.
Mix all the dry ingredients together and add to the sausage-raisins mixture alternately with the Southern Comfort.
Then add the nuts. Stir all well and pour into a 9 x 13 x 2-inch Pyrex baking dish, or in a Bundt pan, well-greased and bake 1 hour (more or less) in a 375° preheated oven.
When cake is done and cooled for perhaps 10 minutes one can pour a glaze over.

_enrichment.description: A moist, spiced sausage cake with sweet raisins and walnuts, baked until tender and optionally glazed. A hearty Southern American treat.

title: Southern Cornbread Stuffing
ingredients: 1 pkg. herb seasoned stuffing mix (about 4 c.)
directions: In large saucepan or Dutch oven, melt bacon drippings until hot.
Add celery and onions.
Cook over medium-low heat until tender.
Stir in stuffing mix, cornbread, pecans and seasonings. Add eggs and toss lightly.
Pour hot broth over entire mixture. Toss.
Makes about 14 cups, about 220 calories per 1/2 cup serving.

_enrichment.description: A rich, savoury stuffing made with cornbread, bacon, and pecans, perfect for roast poultry or as a side dish.

title: Creole Green Beans
directions: Saute green pepper and onion in hot bacon drippings until tender. Add flour, salt, sugar and paprika.
Stir until blended. Add tomatoes and simmer for about 15 minutes.
Stir in green peas; heat through and serve.

_enrichment.description: A tangy, slightly sweet dish of green beans and peas cooked with tomatoes and paprika, offering a Creole twist.

title: Cajun Fried Turkey
directions: Liquify all ingredients, except turkey, in blender.
Inject liquified ingredients into turkey.
Marinate turkey in refrigerator 24 hours.
Deep fry turkey in pot 4 minutes per pound at 350°.

_enrichment.description: A whole turkey marinated in a spicy Cajun blend and deep-fried for a crispy, flavourful centrepiece.

title: Hershey'S Great American Chocolate Chip Cookies
ingredients: 2 c. Hershey's milk chocolate chips (11.5 oz. pkg.)
directions: Heat oven to 375°.
Stir together flour, baking soda and salt. In large bowl, beat margarine, sugar, brown sugar and vanilla until creamy.
Add eggs; beat well.
Gradually add flour mixture, beating well.
Stir in milk chocolate chips and nuts.
Drop by rounded teaspoonfuls onto ungreased cookie sheet.
Bake 9 to 11 minutes or until slightly browned.
Cool slightly; remove to wire rack.
Cool completely.
Makes 6 dozen cookies.

_enrichment.description: Classic American chocolate chip cookies with a chewy texture and rich chocolate flavour.

title: Cajun Chicken Creole
directions: Brown chicken in 3 tablespoons oil in deep skillet.
Remove, add onion, peppers and garlic.
Saute until onion is translucent. Add tomatoes, soy sauce and cayenne pepper; bring to a boil, lower heat and add chicken.
Cover and simmer about 1 hour.
Turn chicken 1/2 way through.
Serve over rice.

_enrichment.description: Tender chicken simmered in a spicy Creole tomato sauce with peppers and onions, served over rice.

title: Creole Seasoning Blend
directions: Combine all in blender or food processor.
Mix at highest speed for 30 seconds.
Store in tightly covered jar.
Uses:
gumbo, jambalaya, barbecue sauce, soups, stews, chicken, fish or shrimp.

_enrichment.description: A versatile Cajun seasoning blend perfect for adding heat and depth to gumbos, jambalayas, and grilled meats.

title: The Chewy Brownie Cookie
ingredients: 1 1/2 c. firmly packed light brown sugar
2 c. semi-sweet chocolate chips (12 oz. pkg.)
directions: Heat oven to 375°.
Combine Crisco, light brown sugar, water and vanilla in a large mixing bowl.
Beat at medium speed of electric mixer until well blended.

_enrichment.description: Chewy, fudgy cookies packed with chocolate chips for a rich, indulgent treat.

title: All American Barbecue Sandwiches
directions: In a Dutch oven, cook beef and onion until meat is browned and onion is tender.
Drain.
Combine ketchup, mustard, Worcestershire sauce, vinegar, sugar, salt and pepper.
Stir into beef mixture. Heat thoroughly.
Serve on buns.

_enrichment.description: Slow-cooked barbecue beef sandwiches with a tangy, sweet sauce, served on soft buns.

title: Cajun Fettuccine
directions: Saute chopped onions and celery in butter until
melted.
Add seasonings and meat.
Simmer 10 minutes.
Add evaporated milk. Simmer 10 minutes.
Add cheese, Velveeta and Parmesan cheese. Cook until thickens.
Serve over noodles.

_enrichment.description: Creamy, cheesy fettuccine tossed with Cajun-spiced chicken and a rich, velvety sauce.

title: Creole Peas
ingredients: 1 (10 3/4 oz.) can cream of mushroom soup
directions: Heat oven to 350°.
Butter 2-quart casserole.
Fry bacon crisp; drain and crumble.
Saute green pepper, onion and celery in drippings.
Add next 6 ingredients and mix.
Place in prepared dish in layers using 1/2 of hot mixture, 1/2 bacon crumbs, 1/2 sliced eggs and 1/2 of soup.
Repeat.
Sprinkle with bread crumbs.
Bake for 30 minutes.
Serves 6 to 8.

_enrichment.description: A creamy, savoury casserole of peas and vegetables baked with a crispy breadcrumb topping.

title: Southern Chicken Salad In Baked Pie Shell
ingredients: 2 c. pineapple chunks, halved and drained
directions: Poach and skin chicken; cut into 1-inch chunks.
Blend the mayonnaise and whipped cream.
Toss to coat chicken, celery, pineapple and nuts.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Mound in baked pie shell.
Chill; serve in wedges.

_enrichment.description: A Southern-style chicken salad with pineapple and pecans, served in a buttery pie shell.

title: Cajun Fettucine
directions: Melt butter in pan and saute onion, celery and garlic.
Add flour, cooking about 15 minutes until mixture is light tan in color.
Add half and half; add cheese.
Cook 10 minutes.
Add pimentos, mushrooms and seafood.
Cook about 20 minutes.
Mix with cooked noodles.
Place mixture in 9 x 13-inch pan.
Bake, uncovered at 375° for about 5 to 10 minutes until hot.

_enrichment.description: Luxurious fettuccine with seafood, mushrooms, and a creamy cheese sauce, infused with Cajun spices.

title: Southern Style Corn Bread Dressing
ingredients: 1 c. hot water or 1 c. chicken broth
directions: Excluding eggs, cook corn bread according to package directions.
After done, place cooked corn bread in large bowl. Add remaining ingredients.
Mix well.
Mixture should be loose. Place mix in baking dish.
Bake for 20 minutes or until firm and brown in oven at 350°.
Serve with favorite meat dish.
If desired, serve favorite gravy over dressing.

_enrichment.description: A classic Southern cornbread dressing with herbs and sage, baked until golden and served with roast meats.

title: Shrimp Creole
directions: Saute celery, onion and green pepper in 1/4 cup margarine until tender.
Add tomatoes, water and seasonings.
Cover and simmer 30 minutes.
Add shrimp and cook 4 to 5 minutes.
Mix cornstarch with water in a cup until all lumps dissolve.
Stir into sauce.
Cool and stir until mixture thickens.
Serve over rice.

_enrichment.description: A spicy, tomato-based Creole sauce with succulent shrimp, served over rice or with crusty bread.

title: Southern Style Chicken And Yams
ingredients: 4 large yams or sweet potatoes, peeled and thinly sliced
1 frying chicken, cut up or 6 chicken breasts, boned, skinned and halved
directions: Arrange yams and onion in a large shallow baking dish, about 3-quarts (9 x 13-inch).
Sprinkle with salt and pepper.
Place chicken on top of yams.
Sprinkle with walnuts.
Melt butter. Stir in mustard, honey and orange juice. Spoon over chicken and yams. Cover dish with foil.
Bake at 375° for 1 hour.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Southern Style Chicken And Yams.

title: Southern Squash Casserole
directions: Cook squash until tender; drain off water.
Add all other ingredients.
Pour into greased casserole dish.
Bake at 350° until top is golden brown and bubbly.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Southern Squash Casserole.

title: Southern Pecan Pie
directions: Beat together the sugar, corn syrup, salt, flour and eggs. Add the vanilla, margarine and pecan halves.
Pour into unbaked shell.
Turn rounded side of pecans up.
Bake in slow oven at 300° for 1 hour.
Cool before cutting.
Serve with ice cream or whipped cream, if desired.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Southern Pecan Pie.

title: Shrimp Creole Casserole
ingredients: 1/4 c. tomato sauce or canned tomato soup
1/2 tsp. onion juice or finely minced onion
directions: Melt butter; add shrimp and simmer 3 minutes.
Add rice, cream, tomato sauce and seasoning.
Heat to boiling.
Place in greased casserole.
Let stand a while before baking to improve flavor.
A few buttered crumbs may be placed on the top.
Bake at 350° for 30 minutes.
Serves 4.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Shrimp Creole Casserole.

title: Southern Spoonbread
directions: Pour boiling water over meal.
Let stand until cool.
Add sugar, salt and beaten egg.
Dissolve soda into buttermilk and add to batter; mix well.
Add melted shortening; pour in greased deep dish or skillet.
Bake at 400° for 25 to 30 minutes.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Southern Spoonbread.

title: Buffalo Chip Cookies
directions: Cream together oil, shortening and sugars.
Add eggs and vanilla.
Sift in flour, baking soda and powder.
Mix in oatmeal, Post Toasties, chocolate chips, pecans and coconut.
Put 6 scoops on each cookie sheet with an ice cream scoop.
Heat oven to 350°. Cook 10 to 15 minutes.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Buffalo Chip Cookies.

title: Peanut Pie(From:  "Southern Living" Magazine)
ingredients: 1 (8 oz.) container frozen whipped topping, thawed
directions: Combine cream cheese, confectioners sugar, peanut butter and milk in a large mixing bowl; beat at medium speed with an electric mixer until well blended.
Fold in whipped topping; spoon into crust and sprinkle with peanuts.
Chill 8 hours.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Peanut Pie(From:  "Southern Living" Magazine).

title: Southern Sweet Potato Pie
directions: In a large mixing bowl, combine the flour and sugar.
Add potatoes, eggs, corn syrup, nutmeg, salt, butter and evaporated milk.
Beat well.
Pour into pastry shell.
Bake at 350° for 55 to 60 minutes.
Yields 8 servings.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Southern Sweet Potato Pie.

title: Cajun Cabbage
ingredients: salt, pepper and cayenne pepper to taste
directions: Cook the bacon in iron skillet until crisp.
Add the onion and peppers.
Add the remaining ingredients.
Simmer for 45 minutes.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Cajun Cabbage.

title: American Meat Loaf
directions: Thoroughly combine all ingredients and shape into a loaf pan (8 x 4 x 1 1/2-inches).
Bake in 350° oven for 1 hour.
Allow meat loaf to stand 5 minutes before slicing.
Makes 6 servings.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for American Meat Loaf.

title: Southern Blackberry Cobbler
directions: Place berries in 9 inch square baking dish.
Combine sugar, cornstarch, and salt, mixing well.	Sprinkle mixture over berries. Dot with butter.
Roll out pastry to fit dish.
Place pastry over berries.
Brush with milk, and sprinkle with sugar.
Bake at 425° for 30 minutes.
Makes 6-8 servings.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Southern Blackberry Cobbler.

title: Southern Okra
ingredients: 3 medium sized tomatoes, peeled and quartered
directions: Cook okra in boiling salted water 10 minutes; drain.
Brown onion and green pepper in salad oil.
Add tomatoes and cook slowly for 5 minutes.
Add okra and remaining ingredients.
Cook over low heat until vegetables are just tender, stirring as little as possible.
One cup canned tomatoes may be used in place of fresh tomatoes.
Serves 4.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Southern Okra.

title: Southern Peanut Butter Pie
directions: Whip cheese until light and fluffy.
Beat in sugar and peanut butter.
Slowly add milk, blending thoroughly.
Fold in topping. Pour into pie shell.
Sprinkle with chopped nuts.
Freeze and serve frozen.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Southern Peanut Butter Pie.

title: Ranch Cookies
ingredients: 2 c. butterscotch and chocolate chips (1 c. each)
directions: Cream together shortening, brown sugar and eggs.
Add remaining ingredients.
Mix together and drop by tablespoons on greased cookie sheet (for smaller cookie, use teaspoon).
Bake at 375° for 12 minutes.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Ranch Cookies.

title: Southern Fried Chicken
ingredients: 1 egg plus 2 Tbsp. milk or water, beaten
directions: Wash chicken; drain on paper towel.
Season chicken with salt and pepper.
Dip in egg mixture.
Add spices to flour; coat chicken.
Fry slowly in hot oil until browned on both sides (about 35 minutes), turning as needed.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Southern Fried Chicken.

title: Cajun Hush Puppies
ingredients: 2 jalapeno peppers, seeded and chopped fine
directions: Combine dry ingredients.
Add buttermilk, water, oleo and egg. Mix well.
Stir in onion and peppers.
Carefully drop batter by teaspoonful into hot oil.
Fry 3 to 5 minutes until golden brown, turning once.
Yield:
2 1/2 dozen.

_enrichment.description: Crispy golden fritters made with cornmeal and bell peppers, seasoned with Creole spices for a taste of Louisiana's bayou. Perfect as a snack or side.

title: Baked Creole Eggplant
ingredients: Tabasco sauce and Worcestershire sauce to taste
directions: Parboil eggplant; drain.
Saute onion, celery and shrimp in butter in skillet.
Add tomato.
Cook to desired consistency.
Add salt, pepper, Tabasco sauce, Worcestershire sauce and bay leaf. Add eggplant.
Spoon into 4 x 7-inch baking dish.
Top with bread crumbs.
Bake at 350° for 20 minutes.
Yields 8 servings.

_enrichment.description: A hearty Southern dish featuring eggplant baked with shrimp, tomatoes, and Creole seasonings for a rich, savoury flavour. Ideal for a comforting dinner.

title: Cajun Chicken Casserole With Cornbread Crust
directions: Combine chicken, soup, celery, green pepper, onion and pimentos in a large bowl.
Stir well.
Pour into a 9 x 14-inch pan or individual casserole dishes.
Combine cornbread mix, chicken stock and melted butter in a large bowl.
Stir well.
Sprinkle over casserole.
Bake in a 350° oven until crust is golden and casserole is bubbly, for about 15 to 20 minutes.
Yields 10 servings.

_enrichment.description: A one-pan casserole of tender chicken in a creamy sauce, topped with a golden cornbread crust. A filling meal for any occasion.

title: Tex-Mex Salad
ingredients: 1 small can mandarin orange slices, drained
directions: Combine first 9 ingredients in bowl.
Using a fork, gradually mix oil into lime juice in small bowl.
Season with salt and pepper.
Add to salad with mint and mix well.
Line plates (or serving bowl) with lettuce leaves.
Mound salad on lettuce.

_enrichment.description: A vibrant Tex-Mex salad with fresh oranges, avocados, and peppers, dressed in a zesty lime and olive oil vinaigrette. Light yet flavourful.

title: Southern Peanut Pie
directions: Beat eggs until foamy.
Add sugar, syrup, butter, salt and vanilla.
Continue to beat until thoroughly blended.
Stir in peanuts.
Pour into unbaked pie shell.
Bake in preheated 375° oven for 45 minutes.
Serve warm or cold.
May be topped with whipped cream or ice cream.

_enrichment.description: A rich, nutty Southern pie with a buttery crust, filled with a sweet and gooey peanut custard. A decadent dessert for any occasion.

title: Tex-Mex Chicken Casserole
directions: Mix chicken broth, soups, chili powder, Ro-Tel, onions and bell pepper.
Place half of the chicken in casserole dish.
Pour half of the soup mixture on top.
Crumble half of the Doritos on top. Layer again in this order.
Top with grated cheese.
Bake at 375° for 30 minutes.
Serve topped with sour cream and black olives. Makes 1 large or 2 small casseroles.

_enrichment.description: A Tex-Mex twist on a classic casserole, layered with Doritos, chicken, and melted cheese. A fun and filling dish for family meals.

title: Southern Okra And Tomato
ingredients: 3 tomatoes, peeled and quartered or canned tomatoes
directions: Cook okra in boiling salted water for 10 minutes.
Drain some. Brown onion and green pepper in oil.
Add tomatoes and cook slowly 5 minutes.
Add okra and remaining ingredients.
Cook over low heat until vegetables are tender.

_enrichment.description: A Southern classic of tender okra and tomatoes, slow-cooked with onions and peppers for a hearty side dish. Perfect with rice or bread.

title: American Beauty Cake
ingredients: 2 oz. red food coloring plus 2 oz. water
directions: Mix the first 2 ingredients together and let stand until needed.
Cream together the shortening, sugar and eggs.
Add coloring mixture.
Add buttermilk and beat 1 minute.
Add flour and salt; beat 1 minute.
Remove from mixer.
Fold in by hand the vinegar and baking soda that has been smoothed together just before adding to cake mixture.
Pour into 2 greased and floured 8-inch cake pans.
Bake at 350° for 30 to 35 minutes or until done.

_enrichment.description: A moist, rich chocolate cake with a deep red hue, topped with a glossy chocolate icing. A classic American dessert for celebrations.

title: Southern California Layered Dip
directions: You will need a 9 x 13-inch glass pan.
In a bowl, peel and mash avocados.
Add salt and pepper to taste.
Add 1 tablespoon lemon juice to preserve green color.
Spread this mixture into the 9 x 13-inch pan.

_enrichment.description: A layered dip with avocados, salsa, and melted cheese, served with crispy tortilla chips. A crowd-pleasing starter for any gathering.

title: Jenny'S Eggs Florentine(American Meatless Breakfast)
directions: Blend last 6 ingredients in a blender or processor.
Gently heat resultant sauce in saucepan.
Place cheese slices on toast. Broil them until cheese melts.

_enrichment.description: A vegetarian breakfast featuring poached eggs on toast, topped with a creamy spinach and cheese sauce. A hearty start to the day.

title: All-American Burgers
ingredients: 1 clove garlic, minced or 2 Tbsp. onion, finely chopped
directions: Combine first eight ingredients in saucepan.
Bring to boil; simmer, uncovered, for 5 minutes.
Combine beef, salt and pepper; form into 4 (3/4-inch thick) patties.

_enrichment.description: Juicy beef burgers with a homemade tangy sauce, served on soft buns with melted cheese. A classic American meal for any day.

title: Cajun Fried Chicken
ingredients: 1 Tbsp. Cajun seasoning or mix 1 tsp. paprika, 1/2 tsp. each: garlic and onion powders, 1/4 tsp. salt and dried thyme leaves and 1/8 tsp. pepper
4 skinless, boneless chicken breast halves fillets, all pieces halved crosswise
directions: In large skillet, heat 1/2-inch oil to 400°.
In large bowl, mix flour, seasoning and 1 teaspoon salt.
In another bowl, mix egg, milk and pepper sauce.
Sprinkle chicken with remaining salt. Coat with egg mixture; drain off excess.
Coat with flour; shake off excess.
In hot oil, fry chicken 6 minutes or until browned and cooked.
Drain.

_enrichment.description: Crispy fried chicken coated in a spicy Cajun flour mix, fried to golden perfection. A Southern favourite for a hearty meal.

title: Buffalo Chips (Cookies)
directions: Blend butter and sugars.
Stir in eggs and vanilla.
Sift salt, flour, soda and baking powder.
Add to bowl.
Mix well. Stir in oats, cornflakes, chocolate chips, pecans and coconut. Place on lightly greased cookie sheet.
Bake for 12 to 15 minutes at 350°.
Cookies will be soft and chewy if you don't bake them until brown.
Makes 36 big cookies.

_enrichment.description: Chewy cookies packed with chocolate chips, pecans, and coconut, with a hint of vanilla. A sweet treat for any occasion.

title: Tex-Mex Dip
ingredients: 3 to 5 medium avocados, plus 1/2 tsp. salt
1 (8 oz.) pkg. sharp Cheddar cheese, grated
directions: Mash avocados with lemon juice, salt and pepper.
Combine sour cream, mayonnaise and taco mix in another bowl.
Spread bean dip on a large shallow platter.
Next, spread avocados and then sour cream mixture.
Layer top with chopped green onions, tomatoes (drained), olives and cheese.
Refrigerate.

_enrichment.description: A layered Tex-Mex dip with avocados, sour cream, and cheese, served with tortilla chips. A zesty starter for any gathering.

title: Southern-Style Chicken Gravy
ingredients: 1 (4 oz.) can sliced mushrooms, drained (reserve liquid)
1 (2 lb.) box frozen fully cooked fried chicken
directions: Saute onions and mushrooms in butter until golden.
Stir in soup, broth, mushroom liquid and seasonings.
Add chicken; cover and simmer 25 to 30 minutes.
Sprinkle with parsley.
Serve over Pilaf.
Makes 5 to 6 servings.

_enrichment.description: A creamy, savoury gravy made with mushrooms and onions, served over rice pilaf. A comforting side dish for a hearty meal.

title: Boudin Or Cajun Sausage
directions: Cut fresh pork butts into 1 1/2 pound pieces.
Boil all of the ingredients in 8 gallons of water.
When meat separates from bone, strain meat from stock.
Reserve stock.
Grind meat mixture in a meat grinder or food processor.
Cook 36 cups of rice.
Combine mixture with cooled rice and add enough stock to make it moist.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Boudin Or Cajun Sausage.

title: Southern Pie
directions: Cream together the butter and sugar.
Add egg yolks, vinegar, nuts and raisins.
Fold in stiffly beaten egg whites.
Bake in an unbaked crust for 45 minutes in a moderate 350° oven.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Southern Pie.

title: Cajun Red Beans And Rice
directions: Place beans in a large kettle; add water, cover and soak overnight.
Next day, add onion, green pepper, celery, ham, salt, cayenne and black pepper to beans in the soaking water.
Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to simmer.
Cover and cook 2 hours or until beans are tender.
Stir in onion tops.
Uncover and cook about 30 minutes longer.
Serve over hot rice.
Makes 8 servings.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Cajun Red Beans And Rice.

title: Ranchburgers(Family Size)
ingredients: 1 (30 oz.) chili beans in chili sauce (Kuners)
directions: Brown hamburger and onions; drain grease.
Add all spices; stir.
(Put the chili beans and sauce through blender first, then add.)
Cook on low for about 1 hour.
Serve on hamburger buns. Also makes great chili dogs.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Ranchburgers(Family Size).

title: Mama'S Cajun Gumbo
ingredients: 1 lb. hot links, cut in bite size pieces
2 lb. plus shrimp, peeled and cleaned
directions: Start a pound or so of hot, smoked stuffed sausage in a quart of water.
Add shrimp and crabmeat.
Let this mixture simmer while you dice celery, bell peppers and onions.
Fry these in bacon fat over low heat until done but not brown.
(A covered skillet is best for this.)
When done, add to seafood mixture and continue simmering.
Add tomatoes and tomato paste.
In bacon fat, brown four heaping tablespoons flour until very, very brown.
This roux is one of the flavor secrets of a good gumbo.
Add roux to simmering mixture very carefully to avoid excessive steaming.
Add one tablespoon of powdered gumbo file and taste.
Add more file and/or salt to suit your taste.
Serve the gumbo in large bowls over fluffy white rice.
Garlic-buttered French bread and tossed green salad usually complete the meal for our gumbo crowd.
Serves about 10.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Mama'S Cajun Gumbo.

title: Southwestern Spinach
ingredients: 1 c. liquid (spinach cooking water plus enough water or milk to make 1 c.)
1 (4 oz.) can mild green chilies, chopped
directions: Preheat oven to 350°.
Cook spinach according to package directions; drain, reserving liquid.
Add enough water or milk to make 1 cup of liquid; set aside.
Saute onion and celery in margarine until soft.
Blend in flour and cook at least 1 minute, stirring.
Add liquid slowly, stirring constantly to avoid lumps. Pour this mixture onto the drained spinach.
Add remaining ingredients.
Mix thoroughly.
Pour into greased baking dish. Bake 20 minutes in oven or place in microwave for 8 to 10 minutes.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Southwestern Spinach.

title: Garlic Cheese Grits
directions: Bring first 3 ingredients to a boil in a large saucepan; gradually stir in grits.
Return to a boil; reduce heat, and simmer, stirring occasionally, 15 minutes or until thickened.
Add Cheddar cheese and next 4 ingredients, stirring until cheese melts.
Remove from heat; let stand 10 minutes.
Stir in eggs, and pour into a lightly greased 11-inch by 7-inch baking dish or 2-quart casserole.
Sprinkle with paprika.
Bake grits at 350° for 1 hour or until set.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Garlic Cheese Grits.

title: Sausage Pie
ingredients: 16 small fresh link pork sausages (approximately 1 lb.)
4 to 5 medium tomatoes, peeled and chopped
directions: Place sausages on a rack in a baking pan.
Bake at 350° for 15 minutes or until lightly browned. Cut into 1-inch pieces.
Set aside.
In a skillet, saute peppers in oil for 3 minutes. Place in 3-quart casserole.
Add the sausages and the next 8 ingredients. Combine bread crumbs and butter. Sprinkle on top of casserole. Bake, uncovered, at 350° for 30 to 40 minutes or until heated through.
Yields 6 to 8 servings.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Sausage Pie.

title: Tavern Cheese Soup
directions: Bring potatoes, 1 1/2 cups water, celery, leeks and bouillon granules to a boil over high heat.
Reduce heat to low, cover and simmer for 30 minutes, or until potatoes are fork-tender.
Remove from heat (do not drain) and mash mixture until smooth.
Gently stir in cheese until melted.
Stir in milk, beer, Worcestershire sauce, salt, dry mustard and white pepper.
Stir over low heat until hot.
Makes 6 servings.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Tavern Cheese Soup.

title: Three-Cheese Ball
ingredients: 2 (3 oz.) jars process Cheddar cheese spread
1/2 c. snipped parsley or bottled parsley flakes
directions: Let cheeses soften at room temperature.
In medium bowl combine cheeses, onion and Worcestershire sauce; blend well.
Stir in 1/4 cup parsley and 1/2 cup pecans.
Shape into ball and place in bowl that has been lined with wax paper.
Refrigerate overnight.
About an hour before serving, remove ball from refrigerator and roll in mixture of 1/2 cup ground pecans and 1/4 cup parsley.
Place on serving dish, surround with crackers.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Three-Cheese Ball.

title: Betsy'S Microwave Broccoli-Cauliflower Casserole
directions: Cook and drain vegetables and put into a 2-quart casserole. Mix together the soup, eggs, onion, Worcestershire sauce and cheese, then stir this mixture into the vegetables.
Combine the melted butter and bread crumbs, then spread this over top of the casserole.
Bake in microwave at Medium-high for about 15 minutes or until heated thoroughly.
Makes about 8 servings.
Can easily be increased or decreased by amount of vegetables.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Betsy'S Microwave Broccoli-Cauliflower Casserole.

title: English Muffin Loaves
directions: In large bowl, combine 3 cups flour, yeast, sugar, salt and baking soda; set aside.
In medium saucepan, combine milk and water and heat until very warm (115° to 120°).
Add to flour mixture gradually, beating until batter is smooth.
Gradually add the remaining flour.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for English Muffin Loaves.

title: Cheesy Chicken Pie
ingredients: 1 (10 3/4 oz.) can cream of chicken soup
1 lb. chicken breasts, cubed and cooked
3 c. cooked, cut up vegetables (I use broccoli, cauliflower and sliced carrots)
1 (2.8 oz.) can Durkee French fried onions
directions: In a large bowl, combine soup, milk, spices, Worcestershire sauce, vegetables, cubed chicken and 1/2 can of onions.
Place in a 10-inch pie pan that has been prepared with Pam.
Bake at 350° for 20 minutes, then stir ingredients.
Sprinkle with remaining onions and 1 cup of cheese.
Bake for 10 minutes more or until cheese melts.
Serves 4 to 6 people.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Cheesy Chicken Pie.

title: Hamburger Stuffed Bread
directions: Cut off top of loaf of bread and hollow out, forming a crust shell.
Tear enough bread for 2 cups; set aside.
Brown beef and oil; add green pepper, Worcestershire sauce and cheese soup. Simmer 5 minutes.
Stir in bread crumbs.
Fill in bread crust. Place American cheese slices diagonally on top.
Bake 5 to 10 minutes or until cheese melts.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Hamburger Stuffed Bread.

title: Vegetable Dip In Rye Bread
directions: Mix all ingredients (except bread).
Cut top off bread (keep for lid).
Dig out center of bread (keep bread for dipping).
Fill bread with mixture.
Replace top of bread.
Triple wrap bread with foil and place in 350° oven for 1 1/2 hours (or until cheese is melted and mixture is hot all through).
Mix pieces of bread with 2 tablespoons of butter and place in oven for 10 minutes.
Serve dip warm with raw vegetables, crackers, bread sticks, etc.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Vegetable Dip In Rye Bread.

title: Potato Cheese Soup With Salmon
ingredients: 3 1/2 c. raw potatoes, peeled and sliced
2 c. (8 oz.) shredded sharp Cheddar cheese
1 (7 1/2 oz.) red or pink salmon, bones and skin removed
directions: In a 2-quart saucepan, melt the butter and saute onion and celery until tender but not brown.

Add potatoes and chicken broth; cover and cook on low heat until potatoes are tender. Puree potato mixture in a blender with 2 cups milk. Return to pan, add remaining 1 cup milk, cream, cheese, thyme, Worcestershire sauce and salmon.
Heat on low, stirring often until hot.
Season with salt and pepper.
Garnish with chopped parsley.
Yields 6 servings.

_enrichment.description: A creamy potato and cheese soup with a hint of thyme, enriched with milk and cream, and finished with fresh parsley. Perfect for a comforting winter meal.

title: Cheddar-Broiled Tomatoes(Serves 4)
directions: Toss together cheese and flour; set aside.
Heat together milk, Worcestershire, mustard, garlic salt and food coloring in saucepan.
Turn heat to low; add cheese, stirring until cheese is melted.
For each serving, pour 1/2 cup sauce over 2 tomato halves.
Garnish with parsley.
Contains 209 calories per serving.

_enrichment.description: Broiled tomatoes topped with a cheesy, mustard-infused sauce, offering a tangy and savoury bite. Ideal as a light starter or side dish.

title: Bread Basket Dip
ingredients: 10-inch round loaf bread (rye or pumpernickel work best)
directions: Combine all of the ingredients (except bread).
Put into hollowed out loaf of bread.
(When you hollow out the bread, save the top of bread so that you can put it back on top of the filled loaf.)
Wrap entire loaf of bread in aluminum foil and bake for 2 full hours at 300°.
Serve with bread cubes, crackers or raw veggies.
This is worth the effort.

_enrichment.description: A rich, creamy dip made with cream cheese, pumpkin, and Cheddar, served in a hollowed-out loaf of bread. Perfect for sharing at gatherings.

title: Easy Cheeseburger Casserole
ingredients: 1 lb. lean ground beef, browned and drained
1 c. chopped onions, cooked with meat
1 1/2 c. plus 2 Tbsp. Bisquick baking mix, divided
directions: Place cooked meat and onions into Pam-sprayed 2-quart baking dish.
Add catsup, Worcestershire sauce, mushrooms (if desired) and 2 tablespoons Bisquick.
Stir well and set aside.
Combine remaining Bisquick with enough water to form a soft dough.
With fingertips dipped in Bisquick, press dough into bottom and up sides of casserole.
Fill with meat mixture.
Sprinkle on cheese. Bake in preheated 425° oven 20 minutes.

_enrichment.description: A quick and easy casserole combining ground beef, Bisquick, and Cheddar cheese for a hearty, family-friendly meal. Ready in under an hour.

title: Stuffed Bread
directions: Slice top off of the French bread; scoop out and crumble the inside bread, leaving about 1/2 to 3/4-inch shell.
Brown ground beef, onion and pepper.
Add all of the remaining ingredients to the crumbled bread; stuff the shell.
Top with sliced American cheese; replace top of loaf.
Heat in oven for about 10 minutes.

_enrichment.description: A stuffed French bread loaf filled with a savoury mixture of ground beef, peppers, and Cheddar cheese soup. Great for a casual meal or snack.

title: Chili Burger(Quick And Easy)
directions: With the ground beef, prepare as many patties as needed. Place on baking sheet.
Sprinkle Worcestershire sauce to taste and broil in oven until done.
Heat chili mixed with chopped onions. Remove cooked patties and place on bun.
Pour chili and onion mixture on top of patties. Add cheese and onions.
Top with top of the bun.

_enrichment.description: A quick chili burger topped with melted Cheddar cheese and a spicy chili-onion mixture. Perfect for a fast, satisfying meal.

title: English Muffins In A Loaf
directions: Combine 3 cups flour, yeast, sugar, salt and baking soda. Heat liquids until very warm.
Add to dry mixture and beat well. Stir in enough remaining flour to make a stiff batter.
Spoon into two 8 1/2 x 4 1/2-inch pans that have been greased and sprinkled with cornmeal.
Sprinkle top of batter with cornmeal.
Cover, let rise in warm place for 45 to 60 minutes.
Bake at 400° for 25 minutes.
Turn out of pans while bread is still warm.

_enrichment.description: Homemade English muffins baked in a loaf pan, offering a soft, slightly sweet bread with a crumbly texture. Ideal for breakfast or brunch.

title: Speedy Beef Casserole For One
directions: In skillet, cook meat, onion and garlic until meat is brown and onion is tender; drain.
Stir in Worcestershire sauce, salt and pepper.
In saucepan, combine meat mixture, tomato sauce, rice and chili powder.
Cook over medium-high heat for about 10 minutes, or until hot and bubbly, stirring occasionally. Stir in half of the cheese until melted.
Spoon onto a plate.
Top with remaining shredded cheese.

_enrichment.description: A single-serving beef and rice casserole with a cheesy topping, seasoned with chili powder and Worcestershire sauce. Quick and comforting.

title: Cheese And Shrimp Casserole
ingredients: 1 1/2 lb. fresh or frozen shelled shrimp, cooked
directions: Cook mushrooms in butter or margarine for 4 to 5 minutes or until tender.
Stir in rice, cheese, cream, cocktail sauce, Worcestershire sauce, salt and pepper.
Cover and chill 6 shrimp. Half remaining shrimp lengthwise; stir into rice mixture.
Spoon mixture into a 12 x 7 1/2 x 2-inch baking dish or 2-quart au gratin dish.
Bake, covered, in a 350° oven for 30 minutes or until hot.
Garnish with reserved shrimp and parsley.
Serves 8.

_enrichment.description: A creamy casserole combining rice, Cheddar cheese, and shrimp, finished with a tangy cocktail sauce. A luxurious yet easy dish.

title: Meatball - Potato Scallop
ingredients: 1 (10 1/2 oz.) can condensed cream of mushroom soup
2 or 3 large potatoes, peeled, thinly sliced and cooked
1 (8 oz.) can whole kernel corn, drained
directions: Mix first 6 ingredients lightly, but thoroughly. Shape in 16 balls and brown on all sides in the oil in skillet.
Remove meatballs and pour off all but 1 tablespoon fat.
Add onion to skillet and saute for 2 to 3 minutes.
Add soup, Worcestershire and about 1/2 cup water.
Simmer 5 minutes.

_enrichment.description: A layered dish of meatballs, potatoes, and corn in a creamy cheese sauce, baked until golden. A hearty, comforting meal.

title: Apache Cheese Bread
directions: Cut the top off the bread, reserving top, and scoop out the inside. Combine remaining ingredients and mix well.
This will be a very stiff mixture.
Fill the bread with the cheese mixture, replace the top and place on a cookie sheet.
Bake in a 350° oven for 1 hour and 10 minutes.
Serve with tortilla chips or tear and dip with the bread.

_enrichment.description: A cheesy, stuffed bread loaf filled with cream cheese, Cheddar, ham, and green chilies. Perfect for sharing or as a snack.

title: Finger Cheesewiches
ingredients: 2 large loaves Pepperidge Farm sliced bread
3 jars (5 oz.) Kraft Old English sharp cheese
1 (8 oz.) jar Woody's sharp Cheddar cheese
directions: Have ingredients at room temperature and mix.
Remove crust from bread.
Spread cheese mixture on 3 slices and place them together, then cut into 3 strips and cut these 3 strips in half (end up with 6 pieces).
Frost sides and top with cheese mixture thinly.
Place cubes on cookie sheet and freeze.
When frozen, put into plastic bag and keep frozen.

_enrichment.description: Cheesy sandwiches cut into strips, perfect for parties or a quick snack. The mixture includes butter, Worcestershire sauce, and spices.

title: Party Cheese Balls
directions: Combine softened cream cheese and Cheddar cheese together. Add pimentos, green pepper, onion, Worcestershire sauce, lemon juice, cayenne and salt.
Mix well.
Chill for 1 hour.
Shape into a ball and roll into finely chopped pecans.

_enrichment.description: A classic cheese ball made with cream cheese, Cheddar, and pecans, rolled in finely chopped nuts. A crowd-pleasing appetiser.

title: Beefy Macaroni Skillet
ingredients: 2 c. cooked corkscrew macaroni or elbow macaroni
directions: In skillet over medium-high heat, cook beef and onion until beef is browned and onion is tender, stirring to separate meat. Spoon off fat.
Stir in soup, water, Worcestershire sauce and cheese; add macaroni.
Reduce heat to low.
Heat through, stirring occasionally.
Top with additional cheese, if desired.
Fast and easy.

_enrichment.description: A one-pan meal of ground beef, macaroni, and Cheddar cheese in a tomato-based sauce. Quick, easy, and family-friendly.

title: Stuffed Potatoes With Ham
directions: Clean and dry potatoes.
Rub skins lightly with oil and Worcestershire sauce.
Bake until tender (about 1 hour at 400°). Remove potatoes, let cool slightly, then slit open and scoop out potato.
Mash potatoes, then mix in sour cream, ham, onions, salt and pepper.
Stuff potato skins with potato mix.
Bake at 350° for 15 to 20 minutes, covered.
Remove cover and sprinkle with cheese; return to oven until cheese melts.

_enrichment.description: Baked potatoes stuffed with a creamy mixture of sour cream, ham, and Cheddar cheese. A comforting side or light main dish.

title: Party Sandwich For A Crowd
ingredients: 1 lb. thinly sliced, fully cooked ham
directions: Cut bread in half length-wise.
Hollow out top and bottom of loaves, leaving 1/2 inch shell.
Discard removed bread or save for another use.
Combine cheeses, onion, mayonnaise and Worcestershire sauce; spread over cut sides of bread.
Layer ham and roast beef on bottom and top halves; place pickles on top of bottom half. Gently press halves together and wrap in plastic wrap; refrigerate for at least 2 hours.
Cut into 1 1/2 inch slices.
12-14 servings.

_enrichment.description: A hearty sandwich loaf filled with layers of ham, roast beef, and a creamy cheese spread, perfect for feeding a crowd at a party or gathering.

title: Traditional English Muffins In A Loaf
directions: Grease and sprinkle with cornmeal two 8 x 4-inch loaf pans. Combine 3 cups flour with yeast, sugar, salt and soda.
Heat milk and water until very warm (120° to 130°).
Add to dry ingredients and beat well. Stir in remaining 3 cups flour to make a stiff batter.
Spoon into prepared pans.
Sprinkle tops with cornmeal. Cover and let rise in a warm place, free from drafts, for 45 minutes.

_enrichment.description: Soft, no-knead English muffins baked in a loaf pan, ideal for a quick breakfast or brunch with butter and jam.

title: Freckle Face Taters
directions: Quarter hot potatoes lengthwise and place in a bake and serve dish.
Sprinkle grated cheese over potatoes.
Melt butter in a skillet and add remaining ingredients; stir until mixture is moist.
Crumble mixture over potatoes and broil until cheese melts.
Makes 4 servings.

_enrichment.description: Creamy, cheesy potato wedges with a crispy breadcrumb topping, baked until golden and bubbling.

title: Crabby English Wedges
ingredients: salt, pepper and celery salt to taste
directions: Mix all ingredients and spread on English muffin halves.
Cut muffins in quarters or eighths and arrange on cookie sheet. Broil 5 minutes or until lightly browned. Can be prepared the night before and refrigerated.
Broil just before serving.
Excellent appetizer.
Can freeze for up to 30 days and use as needed.

_enrichment.description: A simple yet indulgent crab and cheese spread on toasted English muffins, perfect as a quick appetiser.

title: Cheddar Casserole
directions: In a small skillet, melt 2 tablespoons margarine over moderate heat.
When foam subsides, add the onion and cook 4 or 5 minutes until translucent but not brown.
Bring 2 cups water to boil in 1 quart pan.
Add salt and slowly pour in grits, without allowing water to stop boiling.
Boil 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Reduce heat to medium and cook 2 minutes.
Add the onions, Worcestershire and Tabasco, a little black pepper and 1 1/2 cups grated Cheddar combined with 3 tablespoons soft butter.

_enrichment.description: A rich, cheesy grits casserole with a crispy topping, baked until golden and bubbling.

title: Baked English Omelette
directions: Beat eggs with a wire whisk in bowl until smooth. Beat in milk. Stir in seasoning, Cheddar cheese, onion and bacon.
Melt butter in a 9-inch frying pan/omelette pan.
Pour egg mixture in pan. Sprinkle with Parmesan cheese. Bake at 400° for 20 minutes or until set and golden.
Garnish with additional bacon, if desired. Serve immediately.

_enrichment.description: A fluffy baked omelette with melted cheddar and crispy bacon, served hot for a satisfying breakfast or brunch.

title: Cheddar Dill Scones
directions: Heat oven to 400°.
In medium bowl, combine all ingredients, except butter, eggs and half and half.
Cut in butter until crumbly.
Stir eggs and half and half in just until moistened. Turn dough onto lightly floured surface.
Knead until smooth (1 minute).
Divide dough in half; roll each half into 8-inch circle. Cut each circle into 8 pie shaped wedges.
Place 1 inch apart on cookie sheets.
Bake for 15 to 20 minutes or until lightly browned. Makes 16 scones.

_enrichment.description: Savoury cheddar and dill scones with a tender crumb, perfect with butter or as part of a light meal.

title: Sloppy Joe Shepherd'S Pie
ingredients: 1 (15.5 oz.) can Sloppy Joe Sandwich Sauce
directions: Heat oven to 350.
In large skillet, cook ground beef and onion until beef is thoroughly cooked, stirring frequently. Drain.
Add mixed vegetables and sandwich sauce.
Mix well.
Bring to a boil.
Reduce heat and simmer for 5 minutes or until the vegetables are tender.
Spoon into an ungreased 8-inch square glass baking dish.
Cook mashed potatoes as directed on the package using hot water, milk, and margarine.
Let stand for 5 minutes. Spoon potatoes around the edges of the hot beef mixture. Sprinkle paprika over the potatoes.
Bake at 350 for 25 to 30 minutes or until bubbly and thoroughly heated.

_enrichment.description: A comforting shepherd’s pie with a rich, sloppy joe-style beef filling and creamy mashed potato topping.

title: Mary'S Easy Shepherd'S Pie
ingredients: 2 lb. frozen peas and carrots or other mixed vegetables
directions: In skillet, brown meat just until done. Drain off fat.
Place meat in large casserole dish.
Layer frozen vegetables over meat. Mix water with gravy mix; pour evenly over meat and vegetable layers.
Spread mashed potatoes over other layers. Dot with margarine, if desired.
Cover and bake in a 350° oven for 45 minutes.
Remove cover and bake an additional 15 minutes or until potatoes are slightly browned. Excellent one dish meal.

_enrichment.description: A simple shepherd’s pie with ground beef, carrots, and mashed potatoes, baked until golden and bubbling.

title: English Plum Pudding
directions: After all dry ingredients have been mixed together, add the soda with just enough water to make a mixture.
Lay out a 12 x 14-inch cloth and lightly flour before putting the mixture in it. Use 1/2 mixture in two 12 x 14-inch cloths.
Tie up into a ball. Boil in open pan for 3 hours.
Add water as needed.
Be sure to put a saucer or plate in bottom of pan.

_enrichment.description: A traditional British plum pudding boiled in a cloth, rich with spices and dried fruit, served with custard.

title: Vegetarian Shepherd'S Pie
ingredients: pinch of herbs (thyme and 1/4 tsp. mixed herbs)
directions: Soak the lentils for 12 hours.
Place in boiling water and cook until tender.
Heat the butter in a frying pan and fry the onions, peeled and cut into slices, until brown.
Remove them from the pan. Add the flour and fry a few minutes; gradually add the water in which the lentils have been cooked.
Stir and bring to the boil; add salt, pepper, herbs and flavoring.
Turn the lentils into a pie dish or meat tin.
Moisten with as much sauce as required.
Cover with plenty of well mashed potatoes and bake in the oven for 1 hour with the "regulo" at mark 7. The remainder of the sauce may be thinned with more vegetable stock and served with the pie.
Beans or peas may be used in place of the lentils.

_enrichment.description: A hearty vegetarian shepherd’s pie with lentils and mashed potatoes, baked until golden and comforting.

title: Christmas Morning "Wife Saver"
directions: In a buttered 9 x 13-inch glass dish, place 8 slices of bread. Cover with bacon, then cheese and top with bread to resemble a sandwich.
Beat eggs in bowl; add remaining ingredients.
Cover and refrigerate overnight.
In the morning, melt butter (or margarine) and pour over top.
Add crushed corn flakes.
Bake uncovered 1 hour at 350°.
Let sit 10 minutes before serving. Serves 8.

_enrichment.description: A make-ahead breakfast bake with bread, bacon, cheese, and eggs, topped with cornflakes for crunch.

title: Calico Dip
ingredients: 1 large jar chipped beef, cut in small pieces
directions: Cut off top or round loaf.
Pull inside of bread out (with fork) in dip-sized pieces.
Mix ingredients and spoon into hollow loaf.
Replace round top; wrap in foil and bake in oven at 300° for 1 1/2 hours.
Before serving, place small pieces of bread pulled from loaf on cookie sheet and place under broiler just long enough to lightly toast and harden pieces.
Remove lid from loaf and dip pieces into cheese mix.
When bread bits are gone, tear off pieces of loaf to eat.

_enrichment.description: A creamy cheese dip served in a hollowed-out bread loaf, perfect for sharing as an appetiser.

title: Marilyn'S English Pasties
ingredients: 5 lb. meat, cut small (beef stew or chuck roast)
directions: For dough, mix first 3 ingredients.
Mix well.
Add water and mix as little as necessary and use as little flour as you can when rolling out the dough.
Bake at 400° for 10 minutes, then at 350° for 40 to 45 minutes until crust is golden brown.
Make a small slit in the top of each pastie.
When you remove from oven, put a little water oleo mixture into slit (1 teaspoon oleo and 1/2 teaspoon water for each pastie).
Let sit for a few minutes.

_enrichment.description: Traditional British pasties filled with meat, potatoes, and rutabaga, baked in a flaky pastry crust.

title: Salmon Loaf(With Egg And English Pea Sauce)
directions: Drain salmon and reserve liquid, then break fish into small pieces.
Mix with all other ingredients until well blended.
Shape into a loaf and bake at 350° for 25 to 35 minutes.
Slice and serve hot with egg and pea sauce.

_enrichment.description: A light salmon loaf with a creamy egg and pea sauce, baked until tender and served hot.

title: Cheese Casserole
directions: Cut bread slices into 4 squares.
Fit 1/3 of bread tightly in bottom of greased 2-quart casserole.
Sprinkle with salt and pepper, 1/3 of onion and 1/3 of cheese.
Press down, repeat instructions twice.
Beat eggs, add mustard, Worcestershire sauce and milk.
Mix well.
Pour over casserole contents, cover and chill overnight.
Remove from refrigerator 2 hours before serving and drizzle with melted butter.
One hour before serving, bake in a preheated 325° oven for 40 to 50 minutes or until firm in center.
(If you double this recipe, use only 5 cups milk.) Serves 8.

_enrichment.description: A comforting British-style casserole layered with buttered bread, sharp Cheddar, and a creamy egg-milk mixture, baked until golden and bubbly.

title: Tasty Cheese Ball
ingredients: 1 (8 oz.) jar Cheddar cold pack smoky cheese food
directions: Mix the cream cheese, Cheddar cold pack and butter together until blended.
Add the chopped onion, green pepper (if desired), Worcestershire sauce and chopped dried beef.
Mix well; refrigerate 30 minutes to 1 hour.
Shape into one ball or two and roll in chopped pecans.
Wrap in Handi-Wrap, then foil.
Chill. May be made several days ahead of serving.
Good with crackers.
NER: Cheddar cold pack smoky cheese food

_enrichment.description: A smoky, tangy cheese ball made with cream cheese, smoked Cheddar, and Worcestershire sauce, rolled in nuts for a festive party snack.

title: Sesame-Cheddar Scones
ingredients: 2 Tbsp. each vegetable shortening and cold unsalted margarine, cut into bits
directions: Preheat oven to 425°.
Grease a large baking sheet.
In a large bowl stir together the flour, cornmeal and baking powder. Add the shortening and butter and blend with your fingers until the mixture resembles coarse meal.
Mix in the cheese.

_enrichment.description: Flaky, savoury scones studded with sharp Cheddar and sesame seeds, perfect with tea or as a light lunch.

title: Three-Cheese Pie
directions: Mix cheeses, onion and flour.
Spread in greased pie plate (10 x 1 1/2-inch).
Beat eggs slightly; beat in milk, salt, mustard and Worcestershire sauce.
Pour over cheese mixture.
Cook, uncovered, in a 350° oven until set, 35 to 40 minutes.
Let stand 10 minutes. Arrange tomato slices around edge of pie, overlapping slices slightly.

_enrichment.description: A rich, cheesy pie with layers of Cheddar, Mozzarella, and Monterey Jack, topped with tomatoes for a hearty main course.

title: Campfire Potatoes
ingredients: 5 medium potatoes, peeled and thinly sliced
directions: Place the potatoes and onion on a large piece of heavy-duty foil (about 20 x 20-inches); dot with butter.
Combine the cheese, parsley, Worcestershire sauce, salt and pepper; sprinkle over potatoes.
Fold foil up around potatoes and add broth.
Seal the edges of foil well.
Grill, covered, over medium coals, for 35 to 40 minutes or until potatoes are tender.
Yields 4 to 6 servings.

_enrichment.description: Tender foil-baked potatoes stuffed with Cheddar, onions, and herbs, grilled to perfection for a campfire-style meal.

title: English Mincemeat(5 Pounds)
ingredients: 1 lb. cooking apples, peeled and cored
finely grated rind of one lemon and juice
directions: Wash jam jars thoroughly.
Chop or coarsely mince raisins, sultanas (white raisins), almonds and apples and mixed peels into a bowl.
Stir in currants with sugar, suet and spices.
Mix all ingredients together with sherry, lemon rind and juice.

_enrichment.description: A traditional British mincemeat packed with dried fruits, spices, and sherry, perfect for festive pies or as a spread.

title: James' Broccoli Soup
directions: In large soup pot, cook broccoli in chicken stock with bay leaf until tender, about 20 minutes.
Melt butter, add flour and mix well.
When broccoli is done, remove bay leaf and puree in blender with chicken stock.
Return to pot.
