Add butter-flour mixture, parsley, thyme, sage, pepper and Worcestershire sauce; stir until blended.
Heat on low.

_enrichment.description: A creamy, herby broccoli soup with a touch of Cheddar and a silky egg yolk finish, ideal for a light winter meal.

title: Cheddar Puffs
directions: Grate cheese and cut butter into bits.
Add all other ingredients and stir the mixture with a wooden spatula until it forms a ball.
Knead the dough lightly to distribute the fat evenly and reform it into a ball.
Dust with flour, then chill for 1 hour (wrapped in waxed paper).
Roll out between two sheets of waxed paper into 1/4 inch rectangle and cut out rounds with a 1-inch fluted cutter.
Arrange these 1 inch apart on baking sheets.
Brush with 1 large egg beaten with a pinch of salt.
Sprinkle rounds with poppy seed and bake at 425° for 10 to 12 minutes, until puffed and golden.
Let cool for 5 minutes, then transfer to rack to cool completely.

_enrichment.description: Crisp, cheesy puffs with a flaky texture, made with sharp Cheddar and a hint of cayenne for a spicy kick.

title: English Toffee(Microwave)
directions: Combine sugar, butter, syrup and water in a 2-quart glass baking dish.
Microwave on High for 10 to 12 minutes until caramel colored, stirring twice.
Stir in vanilla quickly.
Pour in greased 9 x 12-inch dish.
Sprinkle chocolate on top and, when softened, spread evenly.
Sprinkle with nuts, if desired. Remember: Use only real butter!
When cool, break into pieces.

_enrichment.description: A quick microwave toffee with chocolate and walnuts, offering a rich, buttery sweetness in minutes.

title: Kids' Favorite Shepherd'S Pie
ingredients: 2 lb. ground beef, cooked and drained
directions: After meat is cooked and drained, put back in pan and add soups, milk and seasonings.
Heat until blended.
Put into 2 1/2-quart casserole and top with mashed potatoes.
Layer some shredded Cheddar on top and bake at 325° until heated through, about 45 minutes.

_enrichment.description: A kid-friendly shepherd’s pie with a creamy beef filling and mashed potato topping, baked until golden and bubbly.

title: English-Style Scones(Diabetic Recipe)
ingredients: 6 Tbsp. orange marmalade fruit spread (no sugar added)
directions: Beat 2 eggs, cream and vanilla.
In medium bowl, combine dry ingredients.
Cut in butter with 2 knives until coarse crumbs. Stir in dates/raisins.
Add egg mixture until moistened.
With floured hands, knead dough 4 times on lightly floured surface. Place dough on greased cookie sheet.
Pat into 8-inch circle. Gently score 3/4 way through into 6 wedges with sharp, wet knife. Beat 1 egg with 1 teaspoon water to brush over dough.
Bake at 375° for 18 to 20 minutes.

_enrichment.description: Light, diabetic-friendly scones with dates and raisins, lightly sweetened and served with marmalade or whipped cream.

title: English Farmhouse Cake
ingredients: 8 oz. coarse sugar (1 1/4 c. brown sugar)
1 lb. mixed fruit (raisins, currants and golden raisins)
directions: Heat oven to 250° to 300°.
Rub fat into flour with fingertips until it looks like breadcrumbs.
Add all dry ingredients; mix. Slightly beat eggs.
Add eggs to dry ingredients with enough milk to mix a stiff, but sticky dough.
Bake in one pan for 1 1/2 hours, or until cooked.

_enrichment.description: A dense, fruity British farmhouse cake with mixed spice and lard, baked low and slow for a moist, traditional treat.

title: Beef Cabbage Casserole
directions: In medium skillet, saute onion in butter until soft, but not brown.
Add beef and cook, breaking up meat, until pink is gone. In a 2-quart buttered casserole dish, layer the carrots and half of the cabbage.
Sprinkle with salt and pepper.
Add meat mixture, press down slightly and add remaining cabbage.
Add spaghetti sauce and Worcestershire sauce and mix.
Pour over top of casserole.
Cover and bake at 350° for 55 minutes.
Add cheese on top and bake, uncovered, for 5 minutes.
Serve with tomato slices.

_enrichment.description: A hearty casserole of ground beef, cabbage, and carrots in a tangy tomato sauce, topped with melted Cheddar.

title: Old English Casserole
directions: Place cooked noodles in a lightly greased 8-cup baking dish. Brown beef in a large skillet.
Remove meat to a bowl; drain all but 1 tablespoon fat from skillet.
Saute onion in fat in skillet until tender.
Return beef.

_enrichment.description: A classic American noodle casserole with ground beef, mushrooms, and green beans in a tomato sauce, baked with melted cheese.

title: Edna'S No Mistake Yellow Squash Casserole
directions: In saucepan, cook squash, onion and green and red sweet peppers in melted margarine over medium heat for 7 to 9 minutes or until vegetables are slightly softened.
Thoroughly mix mayonnaise, cheese, egg, sugar and seasonings into vegetables. Spoon into a 2 1/2 quart baking dish that has been sprayed with nonstick coating.
Sprinkle with cracker crumbs.
Bake at 350° for about 30 minutes or until bubbly and browned.
Makes 8 servings.

_enrichment.description: A creamy, cheesy squash casserole with a crispy cracker topping, perfect as a side dish for a comforting meal.

title: Fiesta Beans
ingredients: 1 or more tsp. crushed red chilies
directions: Pick over and rinse beans.
Place in large pot and cover with water so that water is about 2 inches over top of beans.
Mince garlic; cut salt pork into 1/4 x 1/4 x 1-inch pieces and add both to beans.
Bring to boil and simmer until tender (2 to 4 hours).

_enrichment.description: A hearty American bean dish with pink beans, salt pork, and a tangy-sweet-spicy sauce, perfect for a comforting winter meal.

title: Shepherd'S Pie(One Of My Husband'S Favorites.)
ingredients: 1 1/2 lb. ground beef or ground lamb
directions: Preheat oven to 350°.
Heat the butter in a saucepan and add onions, carrots and celery.
Cook, stirring until onions are wilted.
Add the 3 tablespoons of flour and stir with wire whisk. Add tomato paste, wine, beef broth and Worcestershire sauce.
Stir rapidly for two minutes and let simmer slowly for 10 minutes.
Set aside.

_enrichment.description: A classic British shepherd's pie with a rich beef and vegetable filling topped with creamy mashed potatoes, ideal for a cosy dinner.

title: English Muffins In A Loaf(Makes 2 Loaves)
directions: Combine 3 cups flour (may use 1 1/2 whole wheat and 1 1/2 white flour), yeast, sugar, salt and soda.
Heat milk and water to 120°. Add to dry mixture; beat well.
Stir in enough flour to make a very stiff batter.
Spoon into two 8 1/2 x 4 1/2 x 2 1/2-inch loaf pans that are greased and sprinkled with cornmeal.
Sprinkle tops with cornmeal.
Cover; let rise in warm place 45 minutes.
Bake at 400° for 25 minutes.
Remove from pans immediately and cool.

_enrichment.description: Homemade English muffins baked in a loaf pan, offering a soft and fluffy texture with a slightly crisp crust.

title: English Muffin Bread(Microwave)
directions: Combine 1 1/2 cups flour, yeast, sugar, salt and baking soda. Heat the liquids on High for 1 1/2 minutes with one quarter turn every 30 seconds.
Add to dry mixture and stir thoroughly.
Add the rest of the flour and stir.
Spoon into 8 1/2 x 4 1/2-inch bread pan (greased and sprinkled with cornmeal).
Cover with Saran Wrap and rise for 45 minutes.

_enrichment.description: Quick microwave-friendly English muffin bread with a tender crumb and a slightly crispy crust, perfect for a fast breakfast.

title: English Toffee Cheesecake
ingredients: 1/4 c. firmly packed light brown sugar
1 (14 oz.) can Eagle Brand sweetened condensed milk (not evaporated milk)
3 (1.4 oz.) milk chocolate covered English toffee candy bars, finely chopped
directions: Preheat oven to 300°. Combine crumbs, almonds, sugar and margarine.
Press firmly on bottom of a 9-inch spring-form pan. In a large mixer bowl, beat cheese until fluffy.
Gradually beat in sweetened condensed milk until smooth.
Add eggs and vanilla; mix well. Stir in toffee pieces.

Pour into prepared pan. Bake 50 minutes or until center is set. Turn oven off; remove from oven. Run a knife tip around edge of pan. Return to oven and let cool in oven with oven door slightly open. Chill thoroughly. Remove side of spring-form pan. Garnish as desired. Refrigerate leftovers. Makes one 9-inch cheesecake.

_enrichment.description: A decadent American cheesecake with a graham cracker crust, topped with English toffee and milk chocolate, ideal for festive occasions.

title: English Cider Cake(Makes 8 Servings.)
ingredients: 2/3 c. unfermented apple cider (no sugar added)
1 small apple (about 1/4 lb.), cored, pared and grated
2 Tbsp. plus 2 tsp. reduced calorie margarine (tub)
directions: Into medium mixing bowl, sift together flour, baking powder and seasonings; set aside.
Preheat oven to 375°.

In small mixing bowl, using mixer at medium speed, cream margarine with granulated sugar; add eggs, one at a time, beating well after each addition. Add egg mixture alternately with cider to flour mixture, stirring until batter is smooth.
Fold in fruits.
Line bottom of 7 3/8 x 3 5/8 x 2 1/2-inch loaf pan with paper.
Pour batter into pan; bake for 40 to 50 minutes (until cake tester inserted in center comes out dry).
Let cake cool in pan for 5 minutes, then remove to wire rack.
Let cool 10 minutes longer.
Using a sieve or tea strainer, sift confectioners sugar over loaf.
To serve, cut into 8 equal slices.

_enrichment.description: A moist and spiced British cider cake with apples and warm cinnamon, perfect for afternoon tea or a cosy dessert.

title: Old English Apple Pie
directions: Place apples in long baking dish.
Cover with white sugar. Mix softened butter, brown sugar, flour, cinnamon and nuts until it flakes about the size of a pea.
Sprinkle over top of apples and sugar.
Bake at 350° for 1 hour.

_enrichment.description: A traditional British apple pie with a buttery, flaky crust and a sweet-spiced filling, best served warm with custard.

title: Snack Attack Party Mix
ingredients: 1 1/4 c. bite size Cheddar cheese crackers
directions: Combine the first 5 ingredients in a 15 x 10 x 1-inch jelly roll pan; set aside.
Combine margarine, Worcestershire sauce and salt; drizzle over snack mixture, tossing gently.
Bake at 250° for 45 minutes, stirring every 15 minutes.
Yields 6 1/2 cups.

_enrichment.description: A savoury American party mix with crispy potato sticks, cheese crackers, and nuts, lightly seasoned and baked to perfection.

title: English Pea And Asparagus Casserole
directions: Put well-drained asparagus in buttered baking dish.
Chop eggs and put layer over asparagus, then layer with grated cheese.
Add drained peas.
Dot peas and asparagus with butter and season with salt and pepper.
Add milk to soup; mix well and pour over top of all.
Top with cracker crumbs.
Dot with butter and sprinkle lightly with paprika.
Bake in a preheated 350° oven until bubbly all over and crackers crumbs brown (this can be cut in half).

_enrichment.description: A creamy British casserole with tender asparagus, peas, and eggs, topped with crispy cracker crumbs and melted cheese.

title: English Muffin Loaf
directions: Combine 3 cups flour, yeast, sugar, salt and soda.
Heat liquid until very warm (120° to 130°).
Add to dry ingredients. Beat well.
Stir in rest of flour to make stiff batter.
Spoon into 2 8 1/2 x 4 1/2-inch loaf pans that have been greased and sprinkled with cornmeal.
Sprinkle tops with cornmeal and cover. Let rise in warm place for 45 minutes.
Bake at 400° for 25 minutes.
Remove immediately.

_enrichment.description: A simple British English muffin loaf with a soft interior and a slightly crispy crust, baked in a loaf pan.

title: Shrimp Casserole
ingredients: 1 pkg. Uncle Ben's wild rice mix
directions: Cook mushrooms in butter about 4 minutes.
Cook rice according to directions on box.
Mix all ingredients in buttered 2-quart casserole.
Bake at 350° for 30 minutes.
Makes 8 servings.

_enrichment.description: A creamy American shrimp casserole with mushrooms, rice, and cheese, baked until bubbly and golden.

title: Brunch Pie
ingredients: 1 can (10 oz.) cream of chicken soup
directions: Blend together the cracker crumbs and butter.
Press into a 9-inch pie plate using 8-inch pie plate.
Layer meat in shell except 10 to 12 strips.
Combine milk, soup and cheese; heat until cheese melts.
Add beaten eggs, mustard and Worcestershire sauce. Pour over meat.
Top with reserved meat strips.
Bake at 300° for 45 minutes.

_enrichment.description: A savoury British brunch pie with layers of ham, turkey, and a creamy cheese sauce, topped with a cracker crust.

title: Old English Cookies
directions: Mix first four ingredients.
Sift and add remaining ingredients. Mix well.
Drop even amounts on cookie sheets.
Bake at 350° for 8 to 10 minutes.

_enrichment.description: Classic British spiced cookies with a soft, chewy texture and warm cinnamon, nutmeg, and clove flavours.

title: Sylvia'S Shepherd'S Pie
ingredients: 5 large carrots, diced, cooked (2 cans, drained)
directions: Saute ground beef and onions together.
Salt and pepper to taste.
Add pinch of sage, 1 pinch of mustard.
Cook until brown; drain and wash off.
Peel diced boiled potatoes, then mash boil beans, corn and carrots separately.
Cook squash until tender (drain).
In square 9 x 9-inch pan.
Layer ground beef mixture, green beans, squash, corn, carrots, mashed potatoes.
Cover with cheese.
Bake at 325° until cheese is melted and browned.

_enrichment.description: A hearty British shepherd's pie variation with ground beef, mixed vegetables, and a cheesy mashed potato topping.

title: Mushroom Sauced Meatballs
ingredients: 1/2 c. fresh whole wheat bread crumbs
1 c. (4 oz.) Kraft natural shredded sharp Cheddar cheese
1/2 c. dry white wine or beef broth
directions: Mix meat, egg, bread crumbs, cheese, onion, Worcestershire sauce, parsley, basil and pepper.
Shape mixture into 12 meatballs. Brown on all sides in oil in a large skillet about 5 minutes. Remove meatballs from skillet.
Cook and stir mushrooms in same skillet 2 to 3 minutes or until tender.

_enrichment.description: A rich American meatball dish with mushrooms in a savoury sauce, served with a side of fluffy rice or mashed potatoes.

title: Broccoli-Rice Melange
ingredients: 3 c. fluffy cooked rice (1 c. before cooking)
1 (10 oz.) pkg. frozen chopped broccoli, thawed, heated and drained
directions: Combine rice and broccoli.
Mix egg, onion, Worcestershire sauce, salt and milk. Stir into the rice-broccoli mixture.
Pour into a greased 2-quart casserole.
Top with melted butter and shredded cheese.
Sprinkle with paprika.
Bake in slow oven (325°) for 30 to 40 minutes.
Makes 6 generous servings.

_enrichment.description: A comforting baked rice and broccoli dish with melted cheese, perfect for a hearty family meal.

title: Mom'S Special Party Cheese Balls
ingredients: 1 Tbsp. grated onion or onion flakes
directions: Cut cheese in small pieces or grate and allow to come to room temperature.
In mixing bowl, beat cheese and mayonnaise until smooth and creamy.
Add onion, Worcestershire sauce, paprika and parsley.
Continue to beat until thoroughly blended.
Shape into 2 balls.
Roll in nuts.
May garnish with olives, cherries or parsley.
Freezes well.

_enrichment.description: Creamy cheese balls rolled in pecans, ideal for parties or festive gatherings.

title: Weight Watcher Stuffed Peppers
directions: Saute ground turkey, onion and celery in oil in nonstick frypans sprayed with Pam.
Add cooked rice, spices, bouillon, V-8 and Worcestershire.
Mix well.
Spoon into pepper halves.
Garnish with a tomato slice and 1 teaspoon grated cheese.
Bake at 350°, covered, 1 hour.
Four servings (2 halves each) equals 4 protein and 1 bread.

_enrichment.description: Health-conscious stuffed peppers with lean turkey and brown rice, baked to perfection.

title: Roman Holiday
directions: Add to ground beef and salt and pepper:
celery, onion, green pepper and mushrooms.
Simmer until soft.
Cook spaghetti (broken in thirds) and add Cheddar cheese and tomato soup.

_enrichment.description: A hearty Italian-American casserole with ground beef, spaghetti, and melted cheese.

title: Beef And Noodle Casserole
ingredients: 1 (10 oz.) pkg. wide noodles, cooked and drained
2 cans (10 3/4 oz. each) cream of tomato soup (undiluted)
1 can (10 3/4 oz.) cream of mushroom soup
directions: In a large skillet, brown beef.
Remove beef and drain fat. In the same skillet, melt butter over medium heat.
Saute onion and pepper until tender.
Add beef, Worcestershire sauce, noodles and soups; mix well.
Spoon into a greased 3-quart casserole; top with shredded cheese.
Bake at 350° for 45 minutes.
Yields 8 servings.

_enrichment.description: A classic American casserole with ground beef, noodles, and creamy tomato soup.

title: English Cheese Melts
ingredients: 2 cans (2.25 oz. each) sliced or chopped olives
directions: Cut English muffins in half.
Set aside.
In large bowl mix together olives, cheese, onions, mayonnaise and salt.
Spread mixture evenly over 12 muffin halves.
Place on baking sheet. Broil 3 to 4 minutes or until cheese is bubbly and melted.
Cut in half before serving.
Garnish with parsley sprigs.

_enrichment.description: Quick and easy cheese-topped English muffins, perfect for a light meal or snack.

title: Cheese Ball Or Log
directions: Have cream cheese at room temperature.
Combine with all ingredients except nuts.
Form into ball or log.
Place in refrigerator and chill.
Take out and roll ball or log in nuts. Wrap in waxed paper or foil and place in refrigerator until time to serve.

_enrichment.description: A savoury cheese ball or log rolled in nuts, great for parties or as a snack.

title: English Muffin Bread
directions: Stir flour, sugar, yeast and baking soda together.
Heat 2 cups of milk and 1/4 cup water.
Add to dry mixture; beat well. Stir in enough flour to make stiff batter (about 2 1/4 cups).
May substitute wheat flour here, if preferred.
Spoon into 2 greased pans dusted with cornmeal.
Let rise for 45 minutes.
Bake at 400° for 25 minutes, until well brown.

_enrichment.description: A simple homemade bread with a soft texture, perfect for breakfast or brunch.

title: Apache Cheese Bread(Party Dip)
directions: Scoop out bread.
Mix all ingredients.
Place in scooped out bread.
Bake at 350° on sprayed cookie sheet for 45 minutes to 1 hour.
Or you may serve with crackers.

_enrichment.description: A party dip or bread loaf stuffed with cheese, ham, and green chillies for a festive touch.

title: Quick English Currant Bread
directions: Cream sugar and margarine.
Add eggs.
Combine flour, salt, spices, baking powder and baking soda.
Add raisins, currants and candied fruit.
Add buttermilk alternately with flour mixture. Make as moist as fruit cake.
Pour into 2 loaf pans, greased and lined with waxed paper.
Bake in a 350° oven for 1/2 hour, then at 300° for last half hour.
Cool on rack.
Wrap in foil and store in refrigerator.

_enrichment.description: A quick and moist currant bread with spices, perfect for breakfast or tea time.

title: Barbarakuchen(German Lemon Cake)
directions: Mix the butter until smooth; slowly add the sugar, eggs and the mixture of flour, cornstarch and baking powder.
Add the lemon extract.
Pour batter into greased loaf pan.
Bake 60 to 75 minutes in 350° oven.

_enrichment.description: A zesty German lemon cake with a tender crumb, ideal for dessert or afternoon tea.

title: German-Style Red Cabbage
directions: Melt butter in a large skillet over medium-high heat; add apple and onion and saute for 5 minutes.
Add cabbage and vinegar.
Sir together sugar and next 3 ingredients; sprinkle over cabbage.
Add wine; cover, reduce heat, and simmer, stirring often, 35 minutes. Yield:
6 servings.

_enrichment.description: A classic German side dish of red cabbage with apples and a tangy vinegar glaze.

title: German Pancake
directions: Preheat oven to 400°.
Blend it all together and put into a 10-inch skillet (cast-iron preferred).
Prepare skillet by smearing 4 tablespoons butter all around the inside.
Bake 25 to 35 minutes, until golden brown.

_enrichment.description: A light and fluffy German pancake baked until golden, perfect for breakfast or brunch.

title: German Chocolate Cake
directions: Melt chocolate in boiling water. Cool.
Set aside.
Cream the butter and sugar until fluffy. Add yolks, one at

a time, beating well after each. Blend in vanilla and chocolate. Sift flour with soda and salt. Add alternately with buttermilk to chocolate mixture, beating after each addition until smooth. Fold in beaten egg whites.
Pour into three 9-inch layer pans.
Line bottom of pans with wax paper.
Bake at 350° for 30 to 35 minutes.
Cool and frost tops only.

_enrichment.description: A rich and decadent German chocolate cake with layers of moist chocolate sponge.

title: German Meat Casserole
ingredients: 1 (10 1/2 oz.) can cream of mushroom soup
1 (3 1/2 oz.) can French fried onion rings
directions: Parboil carrots and potatoes for 20 minutes.
Saut onion in butter; add beef and cook until turns grey.
Butter a 2 1/2-quart casserole. Alternate layers, beef-onion mixture and vegetables. Mix catsup with soup and pour over casserole.
Saut mushrooms in 3 tablespoons butter and place on top of casserole.
Sprinkle onion rings over top.
Bake at 350° about 20 minutes or until onion rings are brown and crisp.
Yield:
6 large servings.

_enrichment.description: A hearty German meat casserole with potatoes, carrots, and mushrooms.

title: Homemade Pretzels
directions: Mix yeast and sugar with 1/4 cup water.
Set aside until bubbly.
Place flour and yeast mixture into a mixing bowl and blend to form a dough.
Knead on a floured surface for about 5 minutes, until smooth and elastic.
Place in a greased bowl. Cover and let rise 1 to 1 1/2 hours.
Punch down and shape the pretzels as follows:
divide dough into 12 pieces and shape into sticks 6 inches long or divide dough into 10 pieces and shape into knots.
Place on greased cookie sheets.
Cover and let rise 45 minutes.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Homemade Pretzels.

title: Fat Free Strawberry Pretzels
directions: Mix melted margarine with sugar, then combine with the pretzels; coat pretzels well.
Place pretzels in a 9 x 13-inch pan and bake at 375° for 5 minutes.
Cool well.
In a large bowl, combine Cool Whip, 3/4 cup sugar and cream cheese together.
Mix until smooth and spread over cooled pretzels.
Now combine Jell-O with hot water and mix until Jell-O is dissolved.
Add the strawberries and chill until Jell-O starts to thicken, then pour over cream cheese mixture and chill for 2 hours before serving.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Fat Free Strawberry Pretzels.

title: German Kuchen
ingredients: 1 c. shortening (use 1/2 c. Crisco and 1/2 c. butter for flavor)
directions: Cream shortening; add sugar, beaten eggs, flour, salt and vanilla.
Spread into a 9 x 12-inch greased pan.
Reserve 1 cup batter for top.
After spreading the portion of batter in bottom of pan, spoon pie filling on batter.
Drop the rest of the batter on top of filling, not completely covering it.
When baked, fruit will ooze up through batter.
Bake at 350° for 40 to 45 minutes.
Bake at 325° if using glass dish.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for German Kuchen.

title: German Apple Cake
directions: Mix all ingredients together with spoon (no mixer).
Batter will be stiff.
Spread into a greased and floured 9 x 13-inch pan. Bake at 350° for 45 to 60 minutes.
Serve with whipped cream, ice cream or cream cheese frosting.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for German Apple Cake.

title: German Schnitzel
directions: First, flatten meat to tenderize and make larger.
(This can be done by pounding meat with the flat side of meat cleaver or plate edge.)
Season with salt, pepper and garlic powder and spread with mustard.
Dip meat in egg on both sides and then dip in bread crumbs.
Melt margarine in skillet at medium-high heat.
When margarine is hot, add meat and fry until brown on both sides.
If egg is left over, scramble and place on top of meat.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for German Schnitzel.

title: Soft Pretzels(Easy Enough For Children To Make)
ingredients: kosher salt, sesame seed or poppy seed
directions: Dissolve yeast in warm water; let stand 5 minutes.
Combine next three ingredients; add to yeast mixture, mixing well.
Turn dough out onto a floured surface; knead until smooth and elastic (about 8 minutes).
Divide into 16 portions; roll each into an 8 to 10-inch rope.
Twist each into pretzel shapes, letters or curlicues; place on ungreased baking sheets.
Combine egg with water; stir well.
Brush pretzels with egg mixture; sprinkle with kosher salt or seed.
Bake at 425° for 12 to 13 minutes or until golden brown.
Yields 16 pretzels.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Soft Pretzels(Easy Enough For Children To Make).

title: German Steamed Slaw
directions: Fry bacon and reserve the grease.
Chop cabbage and onions. In a large, heavy pot, mix cabbage, onions, salt, pepper, celery seed and tomatoes.
Add sugar and vinegar to warm bacon grease and then add to the cabbage mixture.
Cook slow until cabbage is very soft and translucent.
Stir occasionally.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for German Steamed Slaw.

title: German Slaw
directions: Chop cabbage and onion and pour 1 cup sugar over it.
Combine 1 tablespoon sugar, salt, dry mustard, celery seed, vinegar and oil in small pan and bring to boil.
Pour the dressing over the cabbage immediately.
Cover and refrigerate until next day.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for German Slaw.

title: German-Style Pork And Sauerkraut
ingredients: 2 medium size carrots, cut diagonally into 1/4-inch slices
8 small red potatoes (about 1 1/4 lb.), each cut in half
directions: About 1 1/2 hours before serving, in 12-inch skillet over medium-low heat, cook bacon slices until browned.
Remove bacon to paper towels to drain. Set aside.
Cut a few diagonal slashes in each knackwurst or weisswurst.
In drippings in skillet over medium heat, cook sausages and pork chops, in batches, until browned.
Remove sausages and chops to plate.
In drippings in skillet, cook onion until tender, stirring occasionally. Add sauerkraut, carrots, apple juice and 1/2 cup water. Tuck in pork chops.
Over high heat, heat to boiling. Reduce heat to low, cover and cook 10 minutes.
Stir in potato halves; cook 20 minutes. Return sausages to skillet; cook 10 to 15 minutes until sausages are heated through and potatoes are tender.
Coarsely crumble bacon into skillet. Sprinkle with parsley.
Makes 6 servings.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for German-Style Pork And Sauerkraut.

title: Puffy German Pancakes
directions: Heat oven to 400°.
Spoon 2 tablespoons margarine into a 10-inch pie pan.
In a medium bowl, combine remaining ingredients; beat until smooth.
Place pie pan in oven to melt margarine and heat the pan.
Pour batter into prepared pie pan.
Bake 20 to 25 minutes, until edges are deep brown and puffy.
Loosen pancake with a knife or spatula; cut into wedges.
(Top with butter, syrup, powdered sugar, jam, fruit, sauteed apples, shredded cheese, crumbled bacon or cubed ham.)
Serves 4 to 6.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Puffy German Pancakes.

title: Baked German Potato Salad
ingredients: 2 qt. potatoes, boiled, peeled and sliced 1/8 inch thick (red ones are ideal)
directions: Fry bacon until crisp in medium skillet.
Remove with slotted spoon and reserve.
In there is not approximately 1/4 cup of fat left, add additional bacon fat or oil to make that amount.
Add celery and green onion and cook over medium heat 3 to 4 minutes. Add flour, salt and pepper and cook 2 minutes longer.
Add sugar, vinegar and water all at once and, stirring with a whisk, bring to boil and cook 1 minute.
Add parsley, celery seed and reserved bacon and combine.
Remove from heat.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Baked German Potato Salad.

title: German Style Pork Chops
ingredients: 1 c. flour (cornstarch and/or extra flour to thicken)
directions: Mix flour, salt, pepper and bread pork chops, then saute in skillet with butter to slightly brown both sides (1 to 2 minutes). In large pot, add water, soup mix, onion and beer.
Bring to simmer.
Then add breaded pork chops.
Simmer on low for 45 minutes to 1 hour.
Thicken with flour or cornstarch and serve over rice or noodles.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for German Style Pork Chops.

title: Ammonia Cookies(German Christmas Cookies)
ingredients: 3 tsp. powdered ammonia (you get this from a pharmacy)
directions: Mix the first 6 ingredients together until well blended. Combine flour, baking powder, soda and powdered ammonia; mix in the egg mixture.
Chill at least 2 hours or overnight.
Roll out and cut with cookie cutter.
Place on cookie sheets and bake at 350° for 10 to 12 minutes until light brown.
Makes 7 to 8 dozen.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Ammonia Cookies(German Christmas Cookies).

title: Classic German Chocolate Cake
directions: In small saucepan, over low heat, melt chocolate in water; stir.
In large bowl, beat sugar and shortening until fluffy. Beat in egg yolks and vanilla.
Stir in chocolate mixture.
In medium bowl, combine flour, baking soda and salt; add to batter alternately with buttermilk until blended.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Classic German Chocolate Cake.

title: German Chocolate Filling And Topping
directions: In medium saucepan, combine milk, egg yolks and butter.
Cook, stirring constantly, until mixture comes to a boil.
Remove from heat; add remaining ingredients.
Cool until thick enough to hold shape, stirring occasionally.
Frosts two 9-inch round layer cakes.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for German Chocolate Filling And Topping.

title: German Meatball And Noodle Casserole
ingredients: 1 (16 oz.) jar sweet-sour red cabbage, drained
directions: Combine beef, egg, onion, milk, bread crumbs, salt and pepper; mix well.
Shape into 1-inch meatballs.
In a medium skillet (medium-low heat) in hot salad oil, brown meatballs; add undiluted beef broth and simmer, covered 30 minutes.
In a large saucepan, cook noodles and drain.
Toss caraway seed and noodles.
Preheat oven to 350°.

_enrichment.description: A hearty German casserole featuring tender meatballs baked with egg noodles and sweet-sour red cabbage, perfect for a comforting family meal.

title: Molasses Cookies(Chewy German Cookie)
directions: In a saucepan, mix together the molasses, sugar, spices and water.
Bring to a boil and sustain for 3 minutes.
Remove from heat and cool.
Add nuts and the flour, mixed with baking powder. Mix well.
Let stand until cold.
Divide dough and roll out on board or cloth with a rolling pin or pat by hand. Cut into diamond shapes and place on greased cookie sheet. Bake at 350° for 10 to 13 minutes.
Makes 12 to 14 dozen.

_enrichment.description: Chewy, spiced molasses cookies with a rich blend of warm spices, ideal for a cosy afternoon treat or festive baking.

title: Deluxe German Potato Salad
ingredients: 5 lb. unpeeled red potatoes, cooked and sliced
directions: In a skillet, cook bacon until crisp.
Drain, crumble and set aside.
In the drippings, saute celery and onions until tender. Combine sugar and flour.
Add to skillet with vinegar, water, salt and mustard.
Cook, stirring constantly until mixture thickens.
In a large bowl, combine potatoes, carrots, parsley and bacon.
Pour the sauce over the potato mixture and stir gently to coat.

_enrichment.description: A classic German potato salad with crispy bacon, tangy vinegar dressing, and fresh herbs, served warm or cold.

title: Philly Pretzels
directions: In a large bowl, mix together yeast, water, sugar and salt. Stir in flour.
Knead on floured table until dough is smooth. Shape into animal or other desired shapes.
Brush with beaten egg. Sprinkle with coarse salt.
Bake in a 400° oven for about 15 minutes or until golden brown.

_enrichment.description: Soft, golden pretzels with a crisp crust, brushed with egg and sprinkled with coarse salt, perfect for snacking or sharing.

title: German Style Cabbage Rolls
directions: Boil cabbage heads until leaves look transparent, then remove from head and allow to cool.
Cut thick vein out so leaf won't split when cooking.
Mix together the following ingredients: ground beef, Italian sausage, rice, onion, garlic, salt and pepper. Form mixture into hot dog shapes just large enough to fit each cabbage leaf.
Place rolled mixture into cabbage leaf and roll, tucking sides as you go, then place into deep pot.

When a row is complete, top it with sauerkraut, and so on, until pot is full. Now, pour your tomato sauce over top, fill pot with water (enough to cover cabbage rolls), cover pot with lid and cook 2 to 4 hours.

_enrichment.description: Traditional German cabbage rolls stuffed with a savoury mix of beef, sausage, and rice, baked in a tangy tomato and sauerkraut sauce.

title: German Pancakes
directions: Mix dry ingredients together.
Stir in rest and beat with rotary beater until smooth.
Heat skillet and small amount of oil. Add a scoop of batter; rotate skillet until batter covers the entire area.
Cook on each side until brown spots appear.
Serve with sugar and applesauce; roll up to eat.

_enrichment.description: Fluffy German pancakes with a crisp edge, served rolled up with sugar and applesauce for a delightful breakfast or brunch.

title: German Crumb Cake
directions: Sift flour, brown sugar, white sugar, cinnamon and nutmeg together.
Combine Crisco and butter.
Rub both mixtures together with hands until it forms crumbs.
Take out 1 cup of this mixture for top of cake.
To the remaining mixture add eggs, buttermilk or sour milk and baking soda dissolved in vinegar.
Bake in greased and floured pie pans or cake pans.
Bake at 350° for 30 to 35 minutes.

_enrichment.description: A buttery crumb cake with a cinnamon-sugar topping, moist and tender with a delightful crunch from the crumb layer.

title: Pretzel Pie
directions: Mix the first 3 ingredients and bake at 400° for 8 minutes in a 9 x 13-inch pan.
Cream the cream cheese and sugar together and spread on cooled crust.
Mix jello and boiling water together. Add strawberries.
Let stand for 10 minutes.
Pour over cream cheese mixture and refrigerate for at least 2 hours.
When you are ready to serve, cover top generously with Cool Whip.

_enrichment.description: A no-bake pretzel pie with a sweet crust, creamy cheese layer, and fruity strawberry jelly topping, chilled until set.

title: German Sweet And Sour Cabbage
directions: Pour sugar over shredded cabbage and let stand for several minutes.
Combine other ingredients in saucepan; bring to a boil. Pour boiling mixture over cabbage; let stand overnight before using.
This will keep for at least 10 days.
Store in a covered dish, but not a metal container.
This is very good with ham for a buffet dinner.

_enrichment.description: Sweet and sour cabbage with a tangy vinegar dressing, celery, and a touch of mustard, perfect as a side for cold cuts or ham.

title: German Cheese Cake
directions: Place ingredients in order given in large mixing bowl.
Beat with mixer, start on low, move to medium and finish 10 minutes on high.
Batter should be smooth and liquid.
Grease and flour a round 10-inch baking pan 3-inches high; pour into pan and bake 1 hour in preheated 350° oven.
Leave cake in oven with door closed for 1 additional hour after heat is off.
Cool on rack in refrigerator.
Makes 20 servings.

_enrichment.description: A rich and creamy German cheesecake with a smooth ricotta and cream cheese filling, baked to perfection.

title: German Icebox Slaw
ingredients: 1 medium head cabbage, shredded or chopped
directions: In a large bowl, alternate layers of shredded cabbage and onion rings.
In a saucepan, boil together remaining ingredients and pour over cabbage and onion mixture.
Let stand 4 to 8 hours in refrigerator before serving.
The slaw may be kept in the refrigerator as long as 3 weeks.

_enrichment.description: A refreshing German slaw with crisp cabbage, onions, and a tangy mustard dressing, best served chilled after marinating.

title: German Apfel Kuchen(Apple Cake)
directions: Cut butter into flour.
Add 1/3 cup of sugar, 1 egg and the lemon peel.
Press into a ball.
Roll out on floured surface. Line bottom and partially up sides of a greased 12-inch spring-form pan with the dough.
Decoratively lay apples on pastry and bake for 35 minutes.
In a medium bowl, beat 3 eggs, cream, 1/2 cup sugar and vanilla.
Pour over apples and bake for 20 to 25 minutes until custard is set.

_enrichment.description: A classic German apple cake with a buttery pastry base, topped with thinly sliced apples and a light custard layer.

title: Apple Or Peach Strudel
directions: Into bottom of buttered baking dish (9-inch square or 11 x 13-inch), put thick layer of fruit.
Sprinkle with sugar and cinnamon (nutmeg if you like).
Dot with lumps of butter.
Mix together 1 cup sugar, baking powder, flour and salt.
Into this, break 1 egg; mix until crumbly.
Put this over fruit and bake at 350° to 375° until brown, about 20 to 30 minutes.
You can double the crumbs if you use a larger dish.
This is good and easy.

_enrichment.description: A simple yet delicious strudel with spiced apples or peaches, topped with a crumbly sugar and butter mixture.

title: The Wizard'S Giant Pretzels
ingredients: 1 cake compressed yeast or 1 pkg. dry yeast
directions: Dissolve yeast in warm water in
a large mixing bowl. Add sugar and salt.
Stir well. Add flour, one cup at a time, beating after each addition. Turn dough out onto a floured board and knead until smooth.
Cut off small chunks of the dough. Roll into 1/2-inch ropes and twist into pretzel shapes.
Dust cookie sheets with cornmeal.
Arrange pretzels on the sheets.
Brush tops of pretzels with beaten egg and sprinkle generously with salt.
Bake in a hot oven at 425° for 15 minutes or until brown.
Makes 6 or 8 large pretzels.

_enrichment.description: Giant, soft pretzels with a chewy interior and crisp crust, dusted with cornmeal for extra texture.

title: German Chocolate Cake(1993 Dairy Festival 1St Place)
directions: Grease and lightly flour two 8 x 1 1/2-inch round baking pans; set aside.
In a saucepan, heat chocolate and water until chocolate melts; cool.
Stir together flour, baking soda and salt. In a mixer bowl, beat butter or margarine on medium speed of electric mixer about 30 seconds.
Add sugar and vanilla; beat until fluffy.
Add egg yolks, one at a time, beating on medium speed for 1 minute after each.
Beat in chocolate mixture.
Add dry ingredients and buttermilk alternately to beaten mixture, beating after each addition.
Fold in egg whites.

_enrichment.description: A decadent German chocolate cake with moist layers, rich chocolate frosting, and a fluffy texture, a true crowd-pleaser.

title: My Mother-In-Law'S German Chocolate Icing
directions: Beat egg yolks; add milk, sugar and butter.
Cook until thick (about 12 minutes).
Take off heat; add remaining ingredients. Cool before putting on cake.
(This is delicious on Duncan Hines German chocolate cake mix.)

_enrichment.description: A rich, creamy icing made with milk, sugar, and egg yolks, topped with pecans and coconut, perfect for German chocolate cake.

title: Potica Strudel
directions: Dissolve yeast in about 4 tablespoons warm water and 1 teaspoon sugar.
Mix all of the other ingredients in a large bowl.
Add yeast mixture; mix then place on board.
Add a little flour and knead your dough until smooth.
Divide dough into 3 equal balls. Roll each one out and fill with your favorite filling, nut raisin, apple or cherry.
Roll up slowly with cloth.
Place on cookie sheet.
Let rise in warm place for about 1 hour.
Melt butter and paint on top of Potica Strudel.
Bake in a moderate oven at 325° for 15 minutes and then at 350° for an additional hour.

_enrichment.description: A traditional Slovenian strudel with a soft, buttery dough filled with your favourite sweet or savoury ingredients.

title: German Meat Balls
directions: Combine beef, bread, half onion, 1 1/2 teaspoons salt and pepper.
Mold into balls.
In Dutch oven, combine remaining onion, sugar, raisins, kraut, gingersnaps and tomato sauce.
Add meat balls; simmer for 1 1/2 hours.

_enrichment.description: Hearty German meatballs simmered in a tangy-sweet sauce with sauerkraut, raisins, and tomato sauce.

title: Pretzel Salad(This Was So Good!)
directions: Mix together pretzels, 3 tablespoons sugar and margarine. Spread evenly into 9 x 13-inch pan; bake at 350° for 10 minutes. Cool.
Cream together 1 scant cup sugar and cream cheese.
Fold in Cool Whip and layer on top of pretzel mix.
Cook pudding; cool and layer on top of 2nd layer.
Mix jello in hot water and let partially set up.
Add raspberries.
Pour over top of all and chill until set.

_enrichment.description: A layered dessert with a pretzel base, creamy cheese layer, vanilla pudding, and raspberry jelly, topped with fresh raspberries.

title: Annie'S German Plum Cake
directions: Cream the butter and sugar together, then add eggs until smooth.
Stir in sifted flour and baking powder and add vanilla. The batter will be thick.
Spread into a baking pan (9 x 13-inch), ungreased.
Cut plums in half, removing pits and rinse.
When dry, place plums, skin side down, on top of batter until it is covered. (I find it usually takes about 21 plums to cover the whole pan, 42 halves, 6 across and seven down).
Sprinkle with 1/4 cup sugar and bake in 350° oven for 1/2 to 3/4 hour until batter is browning on the edges.
If the plums are properly ripened, they will create a syrupy glaze over the cake.
This cake is like nothing else I've ever tasted.

_enrichment.description: A moist German plum cake with a buttery batter and fresh plums arranged on top, sprinkled with sugar.

title: German Green Beans
ingredients: 4 c. cooked or canned green beans, drained
directions: Fry bacon until crisp; remove from skillet, leaving drippings. Add onion and cook until tender.
Add flour, sugar, salt, vinegar and water.
Mix well and simmer until slightly thickened.
Add beans and cook for 5 minutes.
Crumble bacon on top just before serving.

_enrichment.description: Green beans cooked with bacon, onions, and a tangy-sweet sauce made with vinegar and sugar.

title: German Liver Dumplings
ingredients: 2 sub rolls (put under faucet, then squeeze water out)
directions: Mix together hamburger, liver, rolls, 1 onion, eggs, flour, bread crumbs, marjoram, salt and pepper; mixture will be soft but will shape in broth.
Form balls, big or small, and drop in hot broth.
Simmer for 20 to 30 minutes.
They will rise.
Remove when done; saute the second onion in butter and pour over dumplings.
Serve with sauerkraut or mashed potatoes.

_enrichment.description: Soft liver dumplings simmered in beef broth, served with sautéed onions and mashed potatoes or sauerkraut.

title: German Casserole
directions: Cook noodles and drain.
Stir in drained sauerkraut.
Mix ground beef, egg, bread crumbs, milk, salt and pepper until well blended.
Shape into 24 meatballs.
Brown in oleo in a large frying pan. Stir in tomatoes. Spoon half the noodle-sauerkraut mixture into an 8-cup baking dish. Top with half the meatball mixture. Repeat layers. Cover.
Bake in moderate oven at 350° for 45 minutes or until bubbly hot in the center.

_enrichment.description: A hearty German casserole with noodles, sauerkraut, and meatballs in a tomato-based sauce, baked until bubbly.

title: German Stollen
ingredients: 1 Tbsp. grated lemon peel or 1 Tbsp. lemon extract
directions: In large mixer bowl, combine yeast, 2 cups of flour and cardamon.
Heat milk, butter, sugar and salt just until warm, stirring occasionally to melt butter.
Add to dry mixture in bowl; add egg.
Beat at low speed with electric mixer for 1 minute, scraping sides of bowl.
Beat at high speed for 3 minutes.
By hand, stir in fruits and peel (or extract).
Stir in enough flour to make a soft dough that is easy to handle.
Turn out onto a lightly floured surface.
Knead until smooth and elastic, about 8 to 10 minutes.
Place in a greased bowl, turning to grease top. Cover and let rise in a warm place, free from draft until double, about 1 3/4 hours.

_enrichment.description: A festive German fruit bread filled with raisins, candied fruit, and lemon peel, flavoured with cardamom and topped with powdered sugar.

title: Moist German Chocolate Cake
directions: Sift dry ingredients.
Melt in saucepan: Baker's chocolate, butter, hot water and vanilla.
Add eggs and 1/2 cup dry mix, then combine all of the ingredients together.
Bake in 2 greased 9-inch pans for 35 to 45 minutes at 350°.
Frost as desired. Cool Whip works well on this cake.

_enrichment.description: A moist, rich chocolate cake with a tender crumb, perfect for any occasion and topped with whipped cream.

title: German Apple Coffee Cake
directions: Cream sugar, butter and egg.
Stir in milk and lemon extract. Blend in flour, baking powder and salt.
Spread in bottom on well-greased 2-quart baking pan.
Arrange apple slices on top. Cut butter into the last 4 ingredients, sprinkle over apples. Bake at 350° until it tests done with wooden toothpick (about 40 to 45 minutes).

_enrichment.description: A buttery coffee cake with fresh apple slices on top, flavoured with cinnamon and nutmeg, ideal for brunch or dessert.

title: 90 Minute Soft Pretzels
directions: Combine 1 cup flour, 2 tablespoons sugar, 1 teaspoon salt and yeast.
Add and beat for 2 minutes:
1 cup warm water and 1 tablespoon margarine or oil (120° to 130°).
Add 1/2 cup flour to mixture and beat 2 minutes.
Add 1 1/2 cups flour gradually to make soft dough; knead 5 minutes.

_enrichment.description: Soft, chewy pretzels with a golden crust, sprinkled with coarse salt, baked until puffy and golden.

title: Puffy Baked German Pancakes
ingredients: 4 eggs or 2 eggs and egg substitute to make up difference
directions: Preheat oven to 425°.
Heat 2 (9-inch) cast-iron skillets or heavy cake pans.
(You need sturdy pans that can hold and conduct heat.)
Mix the flour, sugar and salt.
Add milk and eggs, blending only until well combined, scraping down the sides of the bowl with a spatula.
Take the pans from the oven.
In each, put 2 tablespoons butter; swirl to coat with butter as it melts.

_enrichment.description: A light, puffy German pancake baked in a cast-iron skillet, dusted with powdered sugar and served with butter.

title: German'S Chocolate Angel Pie
directions: Beat egg whites with salt and cream of tartar until foamy.
Add sugar, 2 tablespoons at a time, beating well after each addition. Continue beating to very stiff peaks.
Fold in vanilla and nuts. Spoon into lightly greased 8-inch pie pan to form nest-like shell; build sides up 1/2-inch above edge of pan.
Bake in slow oven (300°) for 50 to 55 minutes.
Cool.

_enrichment.description: A no-bake pie with a meringue shell filled with chocolate pudding and whipped cream, topped with pecans.

title: German Beer Coffee Cake
directions: Mix flour, soda, cinnamon, allspice and cloves. Set aside. In a large bowl, cream the butter and sugar.
Add eggs, one at a time, beating well after each. Add flour mixture, alternating with beer, blending well after each addition.
Stir in dates and nuts. Pour into well-greased 12-cup fluted tube pan. Bake at 350° for 1 hour and 15 minutes.
Cool 10 minutes. Turn out on a rack to cool. Wrap in foil for 24 hours before serving. May sprinkle with confectioners sugar. Serve with Rum Butter Spread.

_enrichment.description: A spiced coffee cake with dates and walnuts, moistened with beer for a unique flavour, perfect with a cup of coffee.

title: Crabmeat Strudel
directions: Saute onions in olive oil, until golden brown.
Add cleaned spinach, salt, pepper and nutmeg; toss lightly.
Remove from heat; spinach should remain wilted.
When cold, add crabmeat, Ricotta cheese and one egg.
Mix well without breaking the crabmeat.
Lay out 2 stacks of 4 layers phyllo dough, side by side.
Place mix on top and roll dough, while brushing it with melted butter.
Place on buttered sheet pan and bake for 30 to 35 minutes at 375°. Serve with boiled potatoes and remaining melted butter.
Garnish with dill or chives.
Serves 5 to 7 persons.

_enrichment.description: A delicate, flaky strudel filled with sweet crabmeat and creamy ricotta, perfect for a light yet elegant starter.

title: Viener Schnitzel
directions: Pound veal until 1/4 inch thick.
Dredge with mixture of flour, Season-All, pepper and nutmeg.
Dip in mixture of egg and water, then into bread crumbs.
Let dry 30 minutes.
Brown veal on both sides in 1/2 cup of butter, cooking about 15 minutes. Remove veal to hot platter.

_enrichment.description: A classic Viennese dish featuring thin, crispy veal cutlets coated in golden breadcrumbs, served with lemon and parsley.

title: Pretzel Salad Dessert
ingredients: 1 (6 oz.) can crushed pineapple and juice
directions: Mix together crushed pretzels, 3 tablespoons sugar and melted butter; spread into a 9 x 13-inch pan, pressing firmly to bottom and sides of pan.
Bake at 400° for 7 minutes; let stand until cool.
Mix jello with boiling water until jello is dissolved.
Add pineapple with juice and frozen raspberries; chill until soft set. Mix together softened cream cheese, 1 cup sugar and thawed dairy topping.
Spread cream cheese mixture over cooled pretzel crust; pour jello mixture over the top and spread evenly.
Chill until firm.
Makes 10 to 12 servings.

_enrichment.description: A layered dessert with a sweet pretzel crust, creamy cheese layer, and tangy raspberry jelly, ideal for festive occasions.

title: German Potato Pancakes (Kartoffelpuffer)
directions: Peel and shred (or grate) potatoes and onion.
Place them in a large bowl.
In a small bowl, beat the egg.
Gradually add the flour, salt, parsley and nutmeg, beating with a wire whisk.
Pour the mixture over the shredded potatoes and onion, stirring to combine.

_enrichment.description: Crispy on the outside and tender inside, these German potato pancakes are a comforting side dish or vegetarian main.

title: German Cabbage Rolls
directions: Mix all ingredients except cabbage, tomato sauce and water together; make small balls.
Separate cabbage leaves and place in boiling water to soften.
Place one ball of meat in center of cabbage leaf; roll and secure with toothpick.
Place in Dutch oven; add tomato sauce and water.
Bring to boil and simmer about 1 1/2 hours.
Skim off fat as it is cooking.

_enrichment.description: Hearty cabbage rolls stuffed with a savoury meat and rice mixture, slow-cooked in tomato sauce for a comforting meal.

title: Hot Potato Salad(Or German Potato Salad)
directions: Cook potatoes in salted water until just tender.
Peel and cut into slices, about 1/4-inch thick.
Fry bacon and onion until tender.
Combine flour, salt, celery seed and sugar in small bowl; add water and vinegar.
Stir slowly into bacon-onion mixture; cook on low heat until mixture boils and is thick and smooth.
Add potatoes and parsley; stir gently.
Serves 4 to 6.

_enrichment.description: A warm, tangy potato salad with crispy bacon and a creamy dressing, a classic German side dish.

title: German Ice Cream Torte
ingredients: 1 (10 oz.) pkg. frozen red raspberries, thawed and drained
1/2 c. whipping cream, whipped and chilled
directions: Remove rim from 9-inch spring-form pan. Cut brown paper to fit bottom. Beat egg whites and cream of tartar until foamy. Slowly beat in superfine sugar, beating until stiff.
Spread evenly on paper. Bake 50 minutes at 275°. Turn off heat.
Leave meringue in oven 1 hour with door closed.
Remove paper. Return meringue to bottom of pan. Replace rim.

_enrichment.description: A decadent German ice cream torte with layers of meringue, chocolate, and whipped cream, perfect for special occasions.

title: German Potato Salad
directions: Peel and cut potatoes in thin slices.
Fry bacon until crisp. Remove bacon from grease and reserve 1/2 cup drippings to make dressing.
Blend sugar, flour, salt and pepper.
Stir into bacon drippings to make smooth paste.
Add vinegar and water.
Boil 2 to 3 minutes.
Combine sauce, potatoes and onions in skillet.
Turn skillet off.
Cover with a towel (not a lid) and let stand at room temperature 3 or 4 hours.
Sprinkle with bacon.

_enrichment.description: A warm, vinegar-based potato salad with crispy bacon, a staple in German cuisine, best served with sausages.

title: Raspberry Pretzel Salad
ingredients: 2 pkg. frozen raspberries (not the loose frozen raspberries)
directions: Mix crumbs, butter and 3 tablespoons sugar; press in pan. Bake for 10 minutes at 350°.
Cool.
Cream the cream cheese and sugar until smooth.
Spread on pretzel crust.
Dissolve jello in hot water; add frozen raspberries.
Let stand until thick and pour over top.
Let set.

_enrichment.description: A layered dessert with a pretzel crust, creamy cheese layer, and raspberry jelly, a sweet and tangy treat.

title: Pretzel Salad
directions: Mix pretzel crumbs, 3 tablespoons sugar and melted butter. Press into a 9 x 13-inch dish and bake 10 minutes at 350°.
Cool. Whip the cream cheese and sugar until light and fluffy.
Fold in Cool Whip.
Spread over crust and refrigerate.
Dissolve gelatin in boiling water.
Add frozen strawberries.
When mixture has thickened, pour over cream cheese layer.
Refrigerate at least 2 hours.
Even better if made a day ahead.

_enrichment.description: A layered dessert with a pretzel crust, creamy cheese layer, and strawberry jelly, a sweet and fruity treat.

title: Middle Eastern Style Dip
ingredients: 1 (10 oz.) can Campbell's condensed cream of celery soup
generous dash of ground red pepper
directions: In covered blender or food processor, combine all ingredients. Blend until smooth.
Serve at room temperature or chilled with pita bread.
Cut up fresh vegetables or use crackers for dipping.
Makes 2 1/4 cups.

_enrichment.description: A creamy, nutty Middle Eastern dip made with chickpeas and almonds, perfect for sharing with bread or vegetables.

title: Homemade Hummus
ingredients: 3 Tbsp. Frontier brand Mexican seasoning mix (or cumin, turmeric, chilies, onion powder, paprika, oregano and cayenne pepper)
directions: Soak beans in 6 cups water overnight, then rinse well.
Drain. Place beans in crock-pot (high) for 10 to 12 hours until quite soft.
Cook also with 3 cloves of the garlic, 5 cups of water, onion and Mexican seasoning mix.
When done, place beans in food processor (use a slotted spoon so most of broth is left in crock-pot).
Blend with lemon, remaining 3 cloves garlic, white miso, olive oil, cayenne and tahini.
Add more broth little by little as needed to make a smooth paste.
You can find miso and tahini at health food markets.
This makes great sandwiches with mayo, avocado, sprouts and grated carrots.

_enrichment.description: A smooth, creamy hummus made from scratch with garbanzo beans, tahini, and lemon, a classic Middle Eastern dip.

title: Dajaj Pilaf (Chicken)(Middle Eastern Feast Dish)
directions: Place chicken, water and spices in large heavy kettle (Dutch oven).
Boil over high heat, skimming fat for 5 minutes.
Reduce heat; simmer 1 hour.
Drain chicken; reserve stock. Debone chicken.

_enrichment.description: A fragrant Middle Eastern chicken and rice dish with apricots, raisins, and nuts, a festive and flavourful main.

title: Chickpeas With Tahini (Ground Sesame Seed)
directions: Place all ingredients in blender.
Mix until chickpeas are smooth.
Store hummus in covered container in refrigerator. Serve well chilled.
Garnish with chopped parsley.
More garlic may be added, if desired.
Thin with additional lemon juice if served as dip with chips.

_enrichment.description: A smooth, garlicky chickpea dip with tahini and lemon, a simple yet flavourful Middle Eastern appetiser.

title: Falafel And Tehina
directions: Drain can of chickpeas.
Mash.
Add next seven ingredients. Mix thoroughly.
Taking a spoonful of the mixture at a time, roll into balls about 1-inch in diameter.
Heat oil and deep-fry falafel until browned.
Drain.
Serve hot on a gyro bread or in a pita bread pocket with tehina on top Tehina recipe follows.

_enrichment.description: Crispy falafel balls made from chickpeas and spices, served with tahini sauce for a satisfying vegetarian meal.

title: Mashed Chickpea Appetizer(Humus)
directions: Open, drain and rinse canned chickpeas.
Mash in food processor or blender.
Add mashed garlic, salt and tahini paste. Blend until smooth.
Add juice of 2 lemons and mix.
If consistency is too thick to spread, add 1 tablespoon water.
Serve in a flat dish.

_enrichment.description: A creamy Levantine chickpea dip flavoured with garlic, tahini, lemon, and olive oil, traditionally served as a starter with warm flatbread.

title: Hummus(Chickpea Spread)
ingredients: 2 c. cooked, drained chickpeas or canned
directions: Start your food processor and then put in garlic.
As soon as garlic is minced, put in chickpeas, lemon juice, salt, tahini and cold water.
Blend until smooth.
Add more cold water if you want it thinner.
Empty contents into a shallow 6-inch wide bowl. Smooth contents.
Sprinkle with paprika and cumin, then drizzle oil over it.
Best served at room temperature.
Dip with pita bread.

_enrichment.description: A classic Middle Eastern chickpea and tahini spread, rich and nutty, garnished with paprika and cumin, perfect for dipping or spreading.

title: Split Pea Hummus
directions: Boil peas for 30 minutes or until soft.
Combine all ingredients in blender and process until smooth and creamy.
Add water, if necessary, for consistency.
Use as dip with chips or veggies.

_enrichment.description: A vibrant split pea hummus with fresh mint and lemon, offering a lighter alternative to traditional chickpea-based dips.

title: Hummus Bi Tahini(Chickpeas With Sesame Seed Paste)
directions: Soak the chickpeas overnight in cold water after checking for small stones and blemished peas.
Wash and rinse several times. Put in pot with 6 cups water and cook on high.
When they are easily mashed between two fingers, they're done.

_enrichment.description: A traditional Lebanese hummus made from slow-cooked chickpeas, blended with tahini, lemon, and garlic, served with parsley and olive oil.

title: Spicy Pumpkin Dip
ingredients: 1 1/2 c. canned chickpeas, drained and rinsed
directions: In a food processor, process pumpkin and chickpeas until fairly smooth.
Add remaining ingredients to food processor and process until smooth.
Season to taste.
Serve with pita chips. Makes 3 cups.

_enrichment.description: A spicy pumpkin and chickpea dip with tahini, garlic, and cumin, offering a warm, autumnal twist on classic hummus.

title: Hummus Bi Tahini
directions: Blend all ingredients in a food processor to a creamy paste. Add water or more lemon juice if dip feels too thick.
Pour into serving dishes.
Garnish with whole chickpeas and fresh parsley.

_enrichment.description: A simple yet flavourful Lebanese hummus with chickpeas, tahini, lemon, garlic, and cumin, garnished with whole chickpeas and parsley.

title: Hommus Tahini(Mid Eastern Recipe)
directions: Rinse chickpeas with cold water.
Put in pan and cover with water; boil for a few minutes until soft and drain.
Put chickpeas, tahini, garlic, lemon juice and salt in a blender and blend until smooth.
You can put homos in a shallow dish and serve with wedges of flat bread.
You can sprinkle red pepper and cumin over top, if desired.

_enrichment.description: A classic Middle Eastern hummus made from boiled chickpeas, tahini, garlic, lemon, and salt, served with flatbread.

title: Homos Bi Tahini
directions: Rinse and drain chickpeas and place in blender with other ingredients.
Blend well.
Sprinkle with a little olive oil. Garnish with parsley.

_enrichment.description: A straightforward Lebanese hummus with chickpeas, tahini, garlic, lemon, and salt, finished with olive oil and parsley.

title: Hummus
ingredients: 1 (15 oz.) can garbanzo beans, drained (reserve liquid)
directions: In small frying pan, toast sesame seed over low heat, shaking pan often, until golden brown (about 3 minutes).

Transfer seed to blender or food processor and add garbanzos, 2 tablespoons olive oil, lemon juice, garlic and 6 tablespoons reserved liquid. Whirl, adding more liquid if necessary, until hummus is smooth, but still thick.
Season with salt and pepper.
Place in shallow bowl.
Drizzle with remaining 2 tablespoons oil.
Makes 2 cups.

_enrichment.description: A homemade hummus made from toasted sesame seeds and chickpeas, blended with olive oil, lemon, garlic, and salt for a nutty, rich dip.

title: Hummus Party Dip
directions: In a blender or food processor fitted with the metal blade, combine 1/4 cup bean liquid and remaining ingredients.
Process until mixture is smooth paste.
Add more liquid if needed to make desired consistency.
Cover and refrigerate until chilled.
Serve with crackers or pita bread.
Use as a dip for crudites or use as a stuffing for celery.
Makes 1 cup.

_enrichment.description: A quick and easy hummus dip with garbanzo beans, lemon, garlic, and paprika, perfect for parties or a light snack.

title: Hummus Dip
directions: In a large bowl, crush garlic and salt together.
Add drained chickpeas, lemon (squeezed) and tahini sauce.
Mix with blender/hand blender or mixer until smooth.
Serve with Lebanese bread/pita chips.

_enrichment.description: A simple hummus dip with chickpeas, lemon, garlic, tahini, and salt, served with Lebanese bread or pita chips.

title: Humus
ingredients: 1 (15 oz.) can chickpeas, rinsed and drained (garbanzo beans)
2 large carrots, peeled and cut into sticks
directions: Place sundried tomatoes in small bowl.
Add hot water to cover; let stand 10 to 15 minutes or until tomatoes are softened. Drain well; cut tomatoes into quarters.
In food processor or blender, process chickpeas, yogurt, tahini, olive oil, lemon juice, garlic and ground pepper until smooth.
Stir in sundried tomatoes.
Refrigerate humus 3 hours or longer to blend flavors. To serve, spoon humus into small bowl; serve with pita wedges and celery, carrot and zucchini sticks for dipping.
Yield:
12 servings.

_enrichment.description: A rich and flavourful hummus with chickpeas, sundried tomatoes, nonfat yogurt, tahini, and garlic, served with fresh vegetables and pita bread.

title: Chickpeas Dip
ingredients: 1/3 c. lemon concentrate or juice of 2 lemons
directions: Reserve the liquid from chickpeas.
Cover peas with water and boil 5 minutes.
Drain water.
Place garlic and lemon juice in blender.
Process until garlic is liquid.
Add tahini.
Blend until smooth.
Add chickpeas and blend until smooth.
Salt to taste.
Spoon onto platter.
Sprinkle with paprika.

_enrichment.description: A quick chickpea dip with tahini, lemon, garlic, and salt, using the liquid from the chickpeas for a lighter texture.

title: Korean Beef
ingredients: 1-2 lbs. beef, cut in thin slices
directions: Put beef in bowl and sprinkle sugar on top.
Put in refrigerator overnight.
Mix together carrots, green onion, garlic, sesame oil, pepper, salt, soy sauce, and wine.
Let them set for a few hours.
Use an electric fry pan and cook the beef with the vegetables on med-high heat.
Serve over white rice. Note:
The electric fry pan can be put in the middle of the dining table and everyone can cook their own beef.

_enrichment.description: A sweet and savoury Korean beef dish marinated in soy sauce, sugar, and sesame oil, served over steamed rice with colourful vegetables.

title: Korean Salad
directions: Cook bacon; drain and crumble.
In large salad bowl, mix spinach, sprouts, water chestnuts, eggs and bacon.
In a jar, combine oil, sugar, catsup, vinegar, Worcestershire sauce and onion; shake well.
Sprinkle dressing on salad and toss.
Serve. Dressing refrigerates well and is good on any salad.

_enrichment.description: A classic Korean spinach and bean sprout salad with bacon, eggs, and a tangy dressing, offering a refreshing crunch.

title: Grilled Marinated Beef(Korean)
directions: Cut meat into serving pieces; score on both sides.
Place meat in shallow pan.
Mix soy sauce, onions, garlic, sugar, oil, sherry and pepper.
Pour over beef and let stand 1 hour at room temperature.
Turn once; drain.
Place meat over medium coals. Grill until desired doneness (allow 15 minutes for medium). Garnish with additional green onion, if desired.
Makes 4 to 6 servings.

_enrichment.description: A classic Korean barbecue dish featuring marinated sirloin steak grilled to perfection, offering a balance of savoury and sweet flavours.

title: Bul-Ko-Kee(Korean Barbecue Beef)
ingredients: 1 lb. beef (boneless top loin or sirloin steaks)
directions: Trim fat from beef and cut diagonally across the grain into 1/8-inch slices.
Mix remaining ingredients.
Stir in beef until coated.
Cover and refrigerate for 30 minutes; drain beef. Stir-fry until light brown over medium heat, 2 to 3 minutes. Serve over hot cooked rice.

_enrichment.description: Thinly sliced beef marinated in a sweet and savoury sauce, quickly stir-fried for a tender and flavourful dish.

title: Korean Barbecued Beef Short Ribs
directions: With bone side down, make a lengthwise cut in each piece, cutting beef halfway to bone. Cut crosswise at 1/2-inch intervals. Meat will look like a fan.
Combine remaining ingredients in a blender. Pour sauce over meat before broiling.
Place on hot grill and brown on all four sides.
Cook until brown and crusty.

_enrichment.description: Succulent beef short ribs marinated and broiled until caramelised, offering a rich and smoky flavour.

title: Korean Beef Stew
directions: Rinse the beef with cold water and score with knife.
Mix the soy sauce, sugar, kiwi, sesame oil, sesame seed, black pepper and garlic to make the seasoning sauce.
Marinate the meat in seasoning sauce for 15 minutes.
Cut the carrots and radish into 2-inch pieces.
Put the meat and sauce into a large saucepan and add 3 cups of water.
Simmer for 1 1/2 hours.
Add carrots and radishes; cook until tender.
Serve with rice.

_enrichment.description: A hearty Korean stew made with beef short ribs, vegetables, and a tangy marinade, simmered to tender perfection.

title: Bulgogi-Marinated Beef
ingredients: 3 to 4 lb. rump roast, cut into strips
2 Tbsp. oil (sesame seed oil is best)
directions: In a blender, blend garlic, onions and soy sauce.
Add the next 4 ingredients and blend.
Remove from blender and add sesame seed, green onions (to taste) and vinegar.
Pour sauce over meat and mix well.
Let the meat soak overnight in a covered bowl in the refrigerator.
Cook the next day in the crock-pot 1 hour on high and 2 to 3 hours on low.
Serve over rice.

_enrichment.description: Bulgogi, a Korean classic, featuring thinly sliced marinated beef slow-cooked until tender and infused with rich flavours.

title: Spinach Kimchee
ingredients: 2 Tbsp. Korean Ko Chu Chong paste (paprika paste)
directions: Wash and drain spinach.
Blanch spinach in boiling water about 60 seconds.
Rinse immediately in cold water, drain and squeeze. Add all ingredients after placing in glass bowl.
Mix by hand. Place in glass container.
Eat fresh with white rice or refrigerate!

_enrichment.description: A vibrant Korean spinach side dish with a spicy, garlicky kick, perfect for pairing with rice.

title: Haeran'S Korean Beef
directions: Cut beef tenderloin or lean beef into thin strips and sprinkle with sugar; set aside for 30 minutes.
Combine soy, pepper, garlic, onion, etc. with 1 tablespoon sugar; mix into beef and let marinate for 30 minutes more.
Place foil on a hot grill; puncture foil all over with a fork.
Place meat on foil and cook, stirring as you would stir-fry, until done.
Makes 4 to 6 servings.

_enrichment.description: Quickly grilled beef strips marinated in a sweet and savoury sauce, offering a tender and flavourful meal.

title: Pupu Korean
ingredients: 4 to 5 lb. drummettes or chicken wings
1 skin chili pepper, minced or 6 to 8 drops Tabasco sauce
directions: Salt chicken; dredge in flour or add salt and pepper to about half a bag of
a plastic bag (the kind that you get from the supermarket
with your vegetables).
Shake your drummettes in the bag (no
mess).
Deep
fry chicken.
Dip, while hot, in sauce made with remaining ingredients.

_enrichment.description: Crispy fried chicken drummettes coated in a sweet and savoury marinade, perfect for sharing.

title: Korean Chicken Or Beef
ingredients: 2 lb. thinly sliced chicken or beef
1 piece thinly sliced and minced gingerroot
directions: Mix all ingredients, except meat, in a large bowl.
Add the meat and mix thoroughly.
Marinate for at least 24 hours.
Then bake at 375° for 45 minutes or barbecue.

_enrichment.description: Chicken or beef marinated in a sweet and savoury sauce, baked or barbecued for a tender and flavourful dish.

title: Chop Chey(Korean Spaghetti)
ingredients: 1 lb. sirloin or round steak, cut into 2-inch strips
3 carrots, cut lengthwise into very thin strips
1 large onion, sliced and rings separated
directions: Brown meat in sesame seed oil.
Add carrots and cook until tender.
Add mushrooms, onion and green onions.
Boil spinach about 3 minutes (do not overcook).
Drain.
Cut spinach to break up bigger pieces.

_enrichment.description: A Korean stir-fry featuring noodles, beef, and vegetables, offering a mix of textures and flavours.

title: Bulgogi(Korean Style Marinated Thin Beef Steak)
directions: Mix sauce portions together.
Add meat and stir.
Allow mixture to set in refrigerator for 3 to 4 hours.
Stir-fry in small to medium batches over medium heat.
May be served on roll or a bed of rice.

_enrichment.description: Thinly sliced marinated beef stir-fried to perfection, offering a tender and flavourful dish.

title: Korean Barbecued Beef(4 Servings)
ingredients: 1 lb. beef boneless top loin or sirloin steak
directions: Cut beef diagonally across grain in 1/8-inch slices.
Mix remaining ingredients.
Stir in beef until well coated.
Cover; refrigerate 30 minutes.
Drain beef; stir-fry in 10-inch skillet or wok over medium heat 2 or 3 minutes.
Serve over hot cooked rice, if desired.

_enrichment.description: Thinly sliced beef marinated and stir-fried, served over rice for a quick and flavourful meal.

title: Korean Marinade Chicken
directions: Mix all ingredients together, except chicken; mix well.
Rinse chicken and place in a deep bowl.
Pour marinade over chicken. Cover; place in refrigerator overnight.
Shake occasionally.
Keep mixed.
When ready, prepare grill as usual.
Let briquets turn white as directed.
Place chicken on grill.
Use remaining marinade for basting.
Cook chicken until cooked thoroughly, approximately 20 to 30 minutes.

_enrichment.description: Chicken breasts marinated in a sweet and savoury sauce, grilled to tender perfection.

title: Korean Meatballs
directions: Combine all ingredients and mix well.
Shape into bite-size meatballs.
Broil for 1 to 2 minutes on each side, turning until evenly cooked.
Serve immediately or place over warmer to keep hot.
Serve with picks.
Double or triple for a large crowd. These are good plain or with the sauce.

_enrichment.description: Juicy meatballs seasoned with a sweet and savoury marinade, broiled until golden.

title: See-Kum-Chee Kook(Korean Spinach Soup)
ingredients: 1/4 lb. beef, cut into thin slices, 1-inch square
pepper to taste (Koreans like a lot!)
directions: Mix beef, onions, garlic, sesame seed, soy sauce, salt and pepper.
Saute in bottom of heavy pan with a little oil until meat is seared.
Add water; cook until meat is tender.
Add spinach and cook until tender.
Serves 6.

_enrichment.description: A comforting Korean spinach soup with tender beef, offering a light yet flavourful dish.

title: Korean Mon-Du
directions: Boil spinach and bean sprouts.
Dice onions.
Chop beef thinly.
Combine cooked vegetables, bean cake and beef.
Chop finely together.
Add sesame oil until mixture can make a ball. Add salt and pepper and mix more.
Put small mound on Mon-Du or egg roll square and make rectangle.
Seal edges tightly together with water.
Steam until dough is tender.
Dip in soy sauce and vinegar to taste.

_enrichment.description: A refreshing Korean side dish made with bean sprouts, spinach, and beef, seasoned with sesame oil and salt for a light yet flavourful bite.

title: Korean Fried Beef Strips
ingredients: 1 lb. beef flank steak or top round
cooking oil for deep fat frying
directions: Partially freeze beef, thinly slice across the grain into bite-size strips.
In a bowl, combine all measured ingredients. Soak meat for 30 minutes at room temperature.
Fry strips in oil until desired cooking time (how you prefer your meat cooked). Serve over cooked rice.

_enrichment.description: Tender Korean-style beef strips marinated in soy sauce, garlic, and spices, then fried to perfection for a quick and delicious meal.

title: Bul-Kogi(Korean Barbecue)
ingredients: 2 lb. lean beef (sirloin or top round), cut into 2-inch narrow strips
directions: Place in broiler pan, cover and let marinate at least 15 minutes or until ready to cook.
Uncover and broil about 10 minutes.
Serve over white rice.

_enrichment.description: Classic Korean barbecue featuring marinated lean beef, broiled until caramelised and served with white rice for a simple yet satisfying dish.

title: Kal Bi Korean Ribs
directions: Spread sugar on meat with sesame seed and onions.
Mix soy sauce, sesame oil and ginger powder.
Marinate for 6 hours or overnight.
Bake on barbecue grill.

_enrichment.description: Succulent Korean-style ribs marinated in a sweet and savoury blend of soy sauce, sesame oil, and ginger, then baked to tender perfection.

title: Barbecue Ribs(Korean Style)
directions: Cut the meat thin.
Mix all the ingredients and mix well.
Mix the thin sliced meat over sauce.
Marinate 2 to 3 hours.
Cook in grill and barbecue and taste it.

_enrichment.description: Korean-style barbecue ribs marinated in a tangy soy-based sauce, grilled to a smoky finish for a rich and flavourful dish.

title: Cumber Kimshi(Variation Of Traditional Korean Cabbage Kimshi)
directions: Slice cucumbers very thinly and wilt with salt.
Squeeze out water.
Add spices.
This keeps in the refrigerator for weeks.
It gets hotter the longer it sets.

_enrichment.description: A spicy and tangy Korean cucumber side dish, lightly pickled with garlic, sesame oil, and chilli for a refreshing crunch.

title: Morgan'S Korean Kal-Bi Ribs(From Morgan'S Restaurant, Bellevue, Wa)
ingredients: 1/2 tsp. red pepper flakes or powder
directions: Run all of the ingredients
in	blender for a minute or two. Combine flavors for 24
hours.
Marinate
to 5 pounds beef short ribs (or chicken pieces) at least overnight.	Grill meat or barbecue until
brown,
or	under broiler quickly.	(I use the crock-pot to cook meat, then brown on the grill.)

_enrichment.description: A rich and flavourful Korean-style short rib marinade, blended for a smooth texture and grilled to a caramelised finish.

title: Korean Barbecue Sauce(Kun Koki)
ingredients: 3 Tbsp. sesame seed, browned and pulverized
3 lb. boneless chicken or barbecue meat
directions: Mix
all ingredients, except chicken or meat.
Marinate boneless
chicken
or meat for 1 hour or more.
Broil, pan fry or charcoal.
Serves 6.

_enrichment.description: A versatile Korean barbecue sauce made with sesame, soy, and garlic, perfect for marinating chicken or meat before grilling.

title: Korean Style Namasu
directions: Chop
cabbage;	soak
and rub in salt water.
Rinse and remove excess water.
Mix all ingredients together;
pour over cabbage. Soak for 2 to 3 hours.

_enrichment.description: A refreshing Korean-style cabbage salad with a tangy vinegar and soy dressing, lightly spiced with garlic and chilli.

title: Korean Barbecue
ingredients: 3 lb. beef, sliced into thin strips
soy sauce, enough to cover beef
3 green onions or 1 large onion, chopped
directions: Combine all ingredients; mix well.
Marinate overnight or for 8 hours in a covered dish.
Preheat oven to 350°.
Bake for 1 hour in a covered dish.

_enrichment.description: Classic Korean barbecue beef marinated in soy, sugar, and garlic, then baked until tender and served with rice.

title: Asphodel Bread
directions: Combine biscuit mix, sugar and salt in a large bowl.
Add yeast which has been dissolved in warm milk.
Beat eggs with cream of tartar and add to the mixture.
Stir well, cover and let rise until double in bulk.
Stir down; pour into two greased loaf pans. Cover; let rise again until smooth.
Bake 40 minutes at 350°.

_enrichment.description: A simple and fluffy quick bread made with biscuit mix, yeast, and eggs, baked to a golden finish.

title: Vietnamese Egg Roll
ingredients: 1 lb. shrimp or chicken breasts, ground or finely chopped
2 c. fresh bean sprouts or cabbage
directions: All ingredients finely chopped.
In large bowl combine ingredients together well.
Fill each egg roll skin with 2 tablespoons mixture.
Fold 2 sides over mixture, brushing last fold with water.
Roll between palms to seal and shape.
When ready to cook, preheat oil to 325°.
Drop egg rolls into hot oil. Fry until golden brown on both sides.
Drain on paper toweling. Keep warm in oven until ready to serve.
Serve with Chinese hot mustard or a sweet-sour sauce.
Can be made in advance and frozen. Serves 8.

_enrichment.description: Crispy Vietnamese egg rolls filled with seasoned pork, shrimp, and vegetables, fried to a golden perfection.

title: Daphone Icebox Cookies
directions: Cover 9 x 13-inch pan with whole graham crackers.
Melt margarine; add white sugar and egg and milk mixture.
Cook until thick.
Stir in graham cracker crumbs, pecans and coconut.
Spread on graham crackers and cover with graham crackers.

_enrichment.description: A no-bake cookie recipe with graham crackers, coconut, and pecans, layered and chilled for a crumbly texture.

title: Oboe Player'S Casserole(Original Obtained From Fellow Oboe Player In Lima Symphony.)
directions: Cook beans and broccoli together about 8 minutes.
Drain. Stir together with next 4 ingredients.
Pour in casserole dish. Mix melted margarine with approximately 3 cups Rice Krispies. Sprinkle over top.
Bake 1/2 hour at 350°.
(May need to cover part of time.)

_enrichment.description: A creamy casserole with broccoli, mushrooms, and sour cream, topped with crispy Rice Krispies for texture.

title: Vietnamese Rice
directions: Cook rice and set aside.
Cook celery and onions in oil until tender.
Add eggs and scramble.
Add Spam, rice and cabbage; toss. Sprinkle with soy sauce and toss.
Cover and let stand for 5 minutes.
Serves 4 to 6 adults.

_enrichment.description: A Vietnamese-style fried rice with eggs, cabbage, and soy sauce, tossed with Spam for a hearty meal.

title: Spring Rolls
ingredients: 1/2 c. jelly noodles (a kind of transparent vermicelli made of mung bean)
4 eggs, slightly beaten, fried and chopped
directions: Heat vegetable oil in frying pan.
Add chopped garlic and fry for 2 minutes.
Put in ground pork, shrimp, crab meat, bean sprouts, eggs, green onion, celery, black mushrooms and jelly noodles.
Fry for 5 minutes.
Add soy sauce, nampla, salt and pepper

_enrichment.description: Crispy Vietnamese spring rolls packed with a savoury mix of pork, seafood, and fresh vegetables, lightly fried to golden perfection.

title: Vietnamese Fried Rice
directions: Wash the
rice.
Cook rice in 3 cups water.
Chop onion and cook
in frypan containing 1 tablespoon oil.
Place small pieces of	pork in oil.
After seasoning with salt, pepper and sugar, fry for
minutes.	Remove	pork and cook shrimp in same manner for only 5 minutes.
Remove shrimp from pan.
Cook peas.
Use another skillet to fry the cooked rice in 1 tablespoon of
oil. Add
pork,	shrimp	and peas to fried rice and cook on
medium heat
for 10 minutes.
You may add a pinch of Accent, if
