desired. Yellow food coloring may be added at the time rice is being cooked to add a more appetizing color.

_enrichment.description: A fragrant and slightly sweet Vietnamese fried rice with pork, shrimp, and peas, seasoned with umami-rich Accent for a comforting meal.

title: Phoney Champagne Punch
directions: Boil water and sugar for 1 minute.
Cool and add rest of ingredients.
Serves 12.

_enrichment.description: A refreshing and fizzy punch made with citrus juices and ginger ale, perfect for warm weather gatherings or festive occasions.

title: California Curried Couscous(Serves 6)
directions: Bring chicken broth with curry to boil in medium saucepan. Stir in couscous; cover and reduce to low heat.
Cook 5 minutes. Remove from heat and fluff with a fork.
Cover until needed. While couscous cooks, saute garlic, celery and onion in olive oil over medium heat until soft, but not browned, about 5 minutes. Combine chutney and lemon juice in small bowl.
Toss couscous with onion/celery mixture.
Stir pine nuts, currants and green onions into chutney mixture in small frying pan over low heat.
Pour over couscous and stir to combine.

_enrichment.description: A fragrant and nutty couscous dish with curry-infused chicken and a medley of dried fruits and nuts, served warm or cold.

title: Moroccan Lentil Stew
ingredients: 4 to 5 potatoes, cut in bite size pieces (leave peels on)
4 fresh tomatoes or may use 16 oz. can
directions: Put package of lentils in a large pot and cover with water. Add potatoes and carrots if desired.
Add salt; cover and cook 20 minutes.
Then saute chopped onion in oil with chopped tomatoes. Add ginger, turmeric and black pepper and continue to cook until onion is translucent and spices are well mixed.
Add this mixture to the cooked lentils and continue to simmer the stew gently for another 20 minutes.

_enrichment.description: A hearty Moroccan lentil stew with tender potatoes and carrots, spiced with ginger and turmeric for a warming, earthy flavour.

title: Couscous Porridge A La Golden Russets
directions: Heat the milk until just short of scalding, melting the butter in it, having added the salt already.
Dump in the couscous, then a generous handful of currants.
Remove from heat; stir it up, cover and let sit for five minutes.

_enrichment.description: A creamy and comforting porridge made with couscous, milk, and apples, finished with currants and a dollop of yogurt.

title: Couscous Salad
directions: Bring water to boil.
Stir in salt and add couscous.
Remove pan from heat and cover.
Let stand 5 minutes.
Transfer couscous to a large bowl and break up any clumps.
Toss couscous with remaining ingredients.
Add couscous dressing and mix well.

_enrichment.description: A light and refreshing salad with fluffy couscous, crisp cucumbers, and sweet currants, tossed with fresh parsley.

title: Moroccan Chicken And Couscous Casserole
ingredients: 5 Tbsp. chopped dried mixed fruit or raisins
1/2 c. water or fat-skimmed chicken broth
2 tsp. cumin seed or 1 tsp. ground cumin
1/2 tsp. ground cinnamon or apple pie spice
directions: Broil chicken, skin side up, to brown the skin and melt the fat.
Combine remaining ingredients in a casserole.
Arrange chicken pieces, skin side up, on top.
Cover casserole with lid or foil.

_enrichment.description: A Moroccan-inspired casserole with tender chicken and fluffy couscous, sweetened with raisins and spiced with cinnamon and cumin.

title: Curried Couscous Salad
directions: Cook chicken; cool and dice.
Cook couscous and cool.
Combine chicken, couscous, carrots, peas, red peppers and green onions. After soaking raisins in orange juice, drain and add raisins to couscous, reserving orange juice.
Combine vinegar, orange juice, curry, mustard, salt and pepper.
Shake or blend thoroughly.
Add oil and blend.
Pour over couscous and toss.
Chill until serving.

_enrichment.description: A vibrant couscous salad with diced chicken, crunchy vegetables, and a tangy curry-mustard dressing, perfect for picnics.

title: Moroccan Lamb Pies
directions: Melt butter, add onion and cook gently until soft.
Stir in cumin and almonds, and continue to cook until almonds are browned a little.
Add honey and raisins.
Drain mixture on paper towels. Mix with lamb.
Add salt and pepper.

_enrichment.description: Flaky phyllo pastry filled with spiced lamb, almonds, and raisins, drizzled with honey for a sweet and savoury bite.

title: Vegetable Couscous
ingredients: 1 tsp. olive oil or vegetable oil
directions: Heat all ingredients except couscous and cheese to boiling in a 2-quart saucepan.
Serve over couscous.
Sprinkle with cheese. Makes 6 servings.

_enrichment.description: A Mediterranean-inspired couscous dish with roasted vegetables, garbanzo beans, and a sprinkle of Parmesan for a savoury finish.

title: Couscous With Chickpeas And Feta Cheese
ingredients: 1 (16 oz.) can chicken broth or vegetable broth
directions: In medium saucepan, bring broth to boil.
Stir in couscous. Remove from heat; cover and let stand for 5 minutes.
Transfer couscous to large bowl and fluff with fork.
Let stand 5 minutes; fluff again. mix in chickpeas, scallions, cheese and olives.
Pour dressing over mixture and toss lightly with fork.
Serve on bed of lettuce.
Serves 6. Enjoy.

_enrichment.description: A light and zesty couscous salad with chickpeas, olives, and feta cheese, tossed in a tangy Italian vinaigrette.

title: Veggie-Topped Couscous
ingredients: 1 large onion, cut into thin wedges
1 tsp. olive oil or cooking oil
2 medium zucchini, quartered lengthwise and cut into 1/2-inch pieces (2 1/2 c.)
1 (15 oz.) can garbanzo beans, rinsed and drained
1 (14 1/2 oz.) can reduced sodium stewed tomatoes
1/4 c. chopped unsalted dry roasted peanuts
directions: In a large saucepan, cook onion and garlic in hot oil over medium-low heat until crisp-tender.
Stir in carrots, 1/2 cup water, basil, cumin, salt and, if desired, red pepper.
Bring to boiling.
Reduce heat.
Cover and simmer for 10 minutes.
Stir in zucchini, garbanzo beans and tomatoes.
Cover and cook for 2 minutes.
Stir together 2 tablespoons water and cornstarch.
Stir into tomato mixture.
Cook and stir until thickened and bubbly. Cook and stir for 2 minutes more.

_enrichment.description: A hearty vegetable-topped couscous with carrots, zucchini, and garbanzo beans, spiced with cumin and red pepper.

title: Mediterranean Couscous Salad
ingredients: 3/4 c. quick-cooking couscous (many supermarkets carry Far East brand)
3 oz. Feta cheese, cut into 1/4-inch dices
3 Tbsp. minced arugula or 1/4 c. minced watercress
directions: Bring stock to boil in small saucepan.
Mix couscous.
Cover and remove from heat.
Let stand 5 minutes.
Fluff couscous with fork.
Turn into medium bowl.
Cool to room temperature.

_enrichment.description: A Mediterranean couscous salad with feta cheese, arugula, and olives, dressed in a lemony olive oil vinaigrette.

title: Tabooley-Lebanese Parsley Salad
directions: Rinse wheat in water until water is no longer cloudy.
Soak for 5 minutes.
Squeeze dry by cupping hands and pressing between palms of hands.
Use food processor to pulsate parsley to save time or chop fine on cutting board.
Chop onions, mint leaves and tomatoes very fine.
Place vegetables in a large bowl; add wheat, lemon juice, oil, salt and pepper and mix well.
Serve with fresh small grape leaves or place salad on romaine lettuce leaves used as scoops.
Serves 6.

_enrichment.description: A fresh and zesty Lebanese parsley salad with bulgur wheat, tomatoes, and mint, dressed in lemon and olive oil.

title: Tabbouleh(A Middle Eastern Salad)
ingredients: 1/2 bunch mint, chopped or 2 Tbsp. dried mint
directions: Rinse burghul well; drain and place in a large bowl.
Add all chopped vegetables and mix well.
Add oil, lemon juice, salt and pepper.
Allow burghul mixture to set about 1 hour so juice will be absorbed by wheat.
Serve on fresh green lettuce with Pita bread.

_enrichment.description: A refreshing Levantine salad made with fine bulgur wheat, fresh herbs, and crisp vegetables, dressed with lemon and olive oil. Perfect for warm weather.

title: Tabouli (Lebanese Salad)
ingredients: 1 medium onion, minced fine or 4 green onions
directions: Place wheat in bowl; cover with warm water and set aside until cool.
Squeeze moisture out of wheat with hands.
Toss with chopped vegetables, salt, lemon juice and olive oil.
Refrigerate for at least 4 hours before serving.

_enrichment.description: A classic Lebanese tabouli salad featuring cracked wheat, tomatoes, and fresh herbs, marinated in lemon and olive oil. Best served chilled.

title: Lebanese Salad(Tabboule)
ingredients: 2 finely chopped large tomatoes (red and firm)
directions: Marinate the bulgur wheat in the lemon juice.
While it is marinating, wash vegetables thoroughly and chop.
Combine vegetables and bulgur wheat.
Add lemon juice, olive oil (1 tablespoon oil for 1 tablespoon lemon) and salt and pepper to taste.
Mix thoroughly.
Decorate with romaine lettuce (leaves can be used as spoon to serve oneself).

_enrichment.description: A vibrant Lebanese tabbouleh salad with bulgur wheat, parsley, mint, and lemon, offering a bright and zesty flavour profile.

title: Lebanese Carrots
ingredients: 1 medium onion, thinly sliced and separated
directions: Combine sauce ingredients.
Add salt and pepper to taste. Pour over vegetables.
Allow to sit (refrigerated) for 24 hours. Serve on cold platter as salad or relish.
Serves 8.

_enrichment.description: A tangy and slightly sweet Lebanese carrot salad with a rich tomato-based dressing, ideal for picnics or as a side dish.

title: Mahshy Bandora (Stuffed Tomatoes)(Lebanese)
ingredients: salt, pepper, cinnamon and red pepper
directions: Core tomatoes; reserve what is inside.
Fry onions, garlic and pine nuts with 1 tablespoon vegetable oil.
Add over the meat and seasoning.
Stir over medium heat until brown.
Fill tomatoes with meat mixture; arrange in the bottom of a pan.
Add the reserved tomato juice mixed with 2 tablespoons vegetable oil.
Bake in 325° oven for 15 minutes, until tomatoes are done.
Fry green peppers with 2 teaspoons vegetable oil until tender.
Add over 2 1/2 cups water with pinch of salt.
Let water boil before adding rice. Cover pan and cook at a very low heat for 1/2 hour, until rice is done.
Add mixed vegetables to rice; stir gently.
Cover pan and let it cook for 15 minutes longer.
Serve mixed rice with Mahshy Bandora.

_enrichment.description: Stuffed tomatoes filled with a savoury mixture of rice, meat, and vegetables, a traditional Lebanese dish often served at gatherings.

title: Tabbouleh Salad
directions: Combine bulgur and boiling water in large bowl.
Water should barely cover the bulgur.
Let stand for 30 minutes until water is absorbed.
Add tomatoes and remaining ingredients.
Toss.
Serve at room temperature or chilled.
Food processor works wonderfully well in chopping parsley, mint and onions.
Cucumber may be added.
It is best if seeded and diced.

_enrichment.description: A simple yet flavourful tabbouleh salad made with cracked wheat, fresh herbs, and lemon, perfect for a light meal or side dish.

title: Reda'S Lebanese Green Beans
ingredients: 2 lb. fresh green beans, washed and snapped
1 (14.5 oz.) can whole tomatoes, crushed by hand
directions: Saut onion in oil and garlic until transparent (about 5 to 10 minutes).
Add tomatoes, spices, salt and pepper; bring to a boil and add green beans.
Turn to simmer and cook until beans are tender.

_enrichment.description: A hearty Lebanese green bean dish cooked with tomatoes, garlic, and allspice, offering a warm and comforting flavour.

title: Tabbouleh(Middle Eastern Salad)
ingredients: 1/2 bunch mint, chopped fine or 2 Tbsp. dried mint
1 large cucumber, peeled and chopped fine
directions: Wash burghul.
Drain and place in a large bowl.
Squeeze out moisture by cupping your hands and squeezing.
Add all chopped vegetables and mix well.
Add oil, lemon juice, salt and pepper. (It's a long process, but chop the vegetables as fine as possible for the more true Tabbouleh.)
Allow burghul to soak and absorb the juice (about 1 hour).
Serve over fresh green lettuce.
Eat with lettuce leaves, tender grape leaves or with a fork.
Makes a refreshing appetizer or a picnic salad.
Makes 4 servings.

_enrichment.description: A traditional tabbouleh salad with fine bulgur wheat, fresh herbs, and vegetables, dressed with lemon and olive oil for a refreshing taste.

title: Tabbouleh Salad(No Mayonnaise)
directions: In a saucepan, pour boiling water over the bulghur wheat. Stir.
Place over low heat and cover.
Cook 20 minutes or until water is absorbed.
Chill wheat.

_enrichment.description: A no-mayonnaise tabbouleh salad made with bulgur wheat, fresh vegetables, and a tangy lemon dressing, ideal for a light meal.

title: Tabooley(Lebanese Wheat Salad)
directions: Soak the cracked wheat in water for 15 minutes; drain dry. Dice onions and tomatoes.
Cut the parsley into small pieces with kitchen shears.
Mix all well.
Serve on lettuce leaves.
Garnish with sprigs of fresh mint and black olives.
Serves 6.

_enrichment.description: A simple Lebanese wheat salad with cracked wheat, green onions, parsley, and tomatoes, dressed with lemon and oil.

title: Kibbeh Nayye(Raw Kibbi)
ingredients: 1 c. sliced spring onions, including green tops
directions: Make Kibbeh according to directions, using 2 cups burghul instead of 3.
After final kneading, place on a large oval platter and spread it out flat.
Round the edges and smooth the top, using hand dipped in iced water.
Make a depression in the center with thumb and with the side of forefinger make 2 grooves along and across kibbeh to the edges.
Alternately shape on individual dishes.

_enrichment.description: A raw Levantine dish made with fine bulgur wheat and spiced meat, served with fresh herbs and lemon wedges for a unique dining experience.

title: Lebanese Potato Salad
directions: Boil potatoes with skins on.
Do not overcook.
Cool until you can handle them.
Peel and salt while still warm.
Mix other ingredients thoroughly.
Add to diced potatoes.
Mix and chill until served.

_enrichment.description: A creamy and tangy Lebanese potato salad with fresh herbs, garlic, and lemon, perfect as a side dish or light meal.

title: Taboli Wheat Salad(Lebanese)
directions: Chop parsley and onions; add tomato.
Wash cracked wheat and soak in water approximately 10 minutes.
Drain.
Add remaining ingredients.
Toss and serve.

_enrichment.description: A refreshing Lebanese wheat salad with cracked wheat, fresh mint, and vegetables, dressed with lemon and olive oil.

title: Tabbouleh
directions: Cover cracked wheat with cold water.
Let stand 30 minutes; drain.
Press out as much water as possible.
Place cracked wheat, parsley, tomatoes, onions and mint in a glass/plastic bowl.
Mix remaining ingredients.
Pour over cracked wheat mixture and toss. Cover and refrigerate at least 1 hour.
Garnish with ripe olives if desired.
Serves 6 (175 calories per serving).

_enrichment.description: A classic Lebanese tabbouleh salad with cracked wheat, parsley, tomatoes, and mint, offering a bright and zesty flavour.

title: Khyar Bil-Laban (Lebanese)(Cucumber-Yogurt Salad)
ingredients: 1 Tbsp. dry mint or 3 or 4 fresh stems and leaves
directions: Mash garlic with salt and fresh mint in bowl.
Add yogurt, blending well.
Peel and cut cucumbers in half lengthwise, then slice in thin half rounds.
Add cucumbers to yogurt mixture.
Fold together gently.
This is especially refreshing in summer.
Serves 4.
Garnish with fresh mint.

_enrichment.description: A cooling Lebanese cucumber-yogurt salad with garlic and mint, perfect for hot summer days or as a refreshing side.

title: Tabbouleh With A Tang
ingredients: 1 pt. (16 oz.) yellow and/or red grape tomatoes
directions: Prepare couscous according to package directions.
Fluff with a fork.
Halve tomatoes and dice cucumber.
In large bowl, toss together couscous, halved tomatoes, diced cucumber, chopped mint and citrus vinaigrette.
Season with salt, pepper, extra lemon juice and hot pepper sauce to taste, if desired.
Chill.
Makes 4 servings.

_enrichment.description: A refreshing Levantine salad with fluffy couscous, juicy tomatoes, crisp cucumber, and aromatic mint, dressed in a zesty vinaigrette.

title: Turkish Vermicelli Soup
directions: Break vermicelli into 1/2-inch pieces.
Heat oil in large saucepan.
Add onion and vermicelli and cook, stirring constantly, until vermicelli is brown and onion is clear.
Add remaining ingredients and bring to boil. Simmer until vermicelli is cooked and carrots and celery are done, stirring often.

_enrichment.description: A comforting Turkish vermicelli soup with thin noodles, tender vegetables, and a savoury broth, finished with a squeeze of lemon.

title: Sutlach(Turkish Rice Pudding)
ingredients: 5 Tbsp. cornstarch, mixed with 1/2 c. milk
directions: Precook rice; cool.
In saucepan, mix milk, sugar and lemon peel.
Heat until almost boiling.
Slowly add the cornstarch and milk mix ture and continue to cook and stir until thickened. Remove from heat.
Add beaten egg yolk, vanilla and rice. Mix well and refrigerate.

_enrichment.description: A creamy Turkish rice pudding with fragrant vanilla and lemon, thickened with cornstarch and enriched with egg yolk.

title: Baklava (Greek Pastry)
directions: In saucepan, melt butter; set aside.
In bowl, mix sugar, cinnamon, cloves and pecans; set aside.
In large 13 x 9-inch pan, layer filo (3 or 4 sheets) and brush with butter.
Sprinkle with pecan mixture.
Layer more filo brushed with butter, pecan mixture, etc., ending with filo.
Cut into 4-inch square and cut 4-inch squares into 2-inch triangles.
Bake at 375° until golden brown.
Cool.

_enrichment.description: A classic Greek baklava layered with buttery filo pastry, spiced nuts, and honey syrup for a rich, flaky dessert.

title: Lamb And Veggie Kebabs
ingredients: 1 lb. leg of lamb, cut into 2-inch cubes
1 yellow squash, halved lengthwise and cut into chunks
directions: In large bowl, combine oil, lemon juice, parsley, oregano, salt and pepper.
Add lamb cubes; turn to coat in marinade. Cover.
Refrigerate 2 hours or overnight.

_enrichment.description: Juicy lamb cubes marinated in herbs and lemon, skewered with colourful summer vegetables for a tender, flavourful kebab.

title: Spiced Shrimp Kebabs
ingredients: 1 can (8 1/4 oz.) pineapple chunks in heavy syrup
dash of hot red pepper sauce
directions: Drain pineapple; reserve 1/4 cup syrup.
In skillet, heat oil over medium-high heat.
Saute garlic, spice and tomato paste 2 minutes.
Add shrimp; saute 2 minutes.
Stir in pineapple syrup and remaining ingredients; boil.
Add pineapple place in bowl. Accompany with picks to make kebabs.

_enrichment.description: Sweet and spicy shrimp kebabs with pineapple, ginger, and turmeric, served with a tangy lime dip.

title: Turkish Delight
directions: Cook sugar with water and corn syrup in a heavy bottomed saucepan until mixture forms a soft ball or reaches 240° on candy thermometer.
Keep hot.
Soften gelatin in lemon juice.
Dissolve cornstarch in 1/2 cup cold water and pour into hot syrup.
Simmer slowly until very thick; remove from heat and add gelatin and lemon juice.
Stir until gelatin dissolves.
Stir in desired flavoring and nuts.
Sprinkle a thick layer of confectioners sugar in an 8-inch square pan.
Pour in candy and let set in a cool dry place (not refrigerator) for 3 to 4 hours.
Top with a layer of sugar and cut into 1-inch squares.
Dredge lightly in additional sugar and store in airtight container.

_enrichment.description: A traditional Turkish delight made with sugar, lemon, and nuts, set in a chewy, jelly-like texture dusted with icing sugar.

title: Spinach Borek
ingredients: 1/2 lb. Gruyere or Romano cheese, grated
1 large bunch fresh, washed and chopped spinach
directions: Heat oven to 350°.
Use about 1 1/2 pounds of cheese.
You may use any combination or proportion as long as the total amount is about the 1 1/2 pounds.
Do not open the filo package until butter is melted and filling prepared.
Place a damp, well wrung out towel over the filo that you are about to use.
Have eggs and cheese at room temperature.
Put eggs, cheese, dill and cayenne in blender and mix well.
Put mixture into bowl and add spinach and nuts. Stir and blend.

Using a 2-inch wide paint brush dipped in melted butter, butter 8 or 10 sheets of filo dough, 1 at a time and place 1 on top of another in a large buttered 9 x 13 x 2-inch baking pan. Spread filling evenly over these sheets.
Butter 8 or 10 more filo sheets.
Place them on top of the filling.
Cut borek into serving size pieces.
If borek is difficult to cut, put pan into refrigerator for an hour; it will then be much easier to cut.
It can also be frozen at this point and baked later.
Bake for 30 to 40 minutes at 350° or until top is nicely browned.

_enrichment.description: A flaky spinach borek with creamy cheeses, dill, and pine nuts, baked in buttery filo pastry for a crispy, savoury bite.

title: Nutty Baklava
ingredients: 1/2 c. finely chopped Macadamia nuts, toasted
directions: Butter a 13 x 9 x 2-inch baking pan.
Cut phyllo in half crosswise.
Cut into half to fit into pan.
Discard trimmings. Cover phyllo with slightly damp towel.
Layer 10 sheets of phyllo in pan, brushing each sheet with melted butter.

_enrichment.description: A nutty twist on baklava with coconut, pecans, and allspice, layered in buttery filo for a crunchy, sweet dessert.

title: Baklava
directions: Mix together nuts, cinnamon, cloves and sugar; set aside. Brush 9 x 13-inch pan with melted butter.

Line bottom of dish with 10 sheets of filo, brushing each sheet with melted butter. Spread 1/3 of nut mixture evenly on top and cover with another 5 sheets of filo, brushing each sheet with melted butter.
Place 1/2 of the remaining mixture on top of filo.
Add 5 more sheets of filo.
Repeat this step once more, using remainder of filo, brushing each sheet with butter.

_enrichment.description: A classic baklava with walnuts, almonds, and warm spices, layered in buttery filo and drenched in honey syrup.

title: Beef And Vegetable Kebabs
directions: Cut the green pepper in half.
Remove and discard the core and seeds.
Cut the pepper flesh into 1-inch pieces with a sharp knife.

_enrichment.description: Tender beef and vegetable kebabs with bell peppers, onions, and mushrooms, marinated in lemon and herbs.

title: *Baklava("Pita Dulci")
directions: Butter a 12 x 17 x 1-inch baking pan. Butter 8 to 10 sheets of phyllo for bottom crust.
Sprinkle nuts (combined with sugar and cinnamon).
Sprinkle nuts on every sheet or every two sheets. Top with 8 sheets. Refrigerate.
Cut into diamonds. Bake at 350° for 60 minutes. (After 40 minutes, lower oven to 325° for remaining 20 minutes.)
Pour cooled syrup over hot Baklava.

_enrichment.description: A sweet and buttery Greek baklava with walnuts and cinnamon, cut into diamonds and drenched in syrup.

title: Meat Marinade For Shish-Kebabs
ingredients: 1/4 c. chopped onion or 1 Tbsp. minced onion
2 Tbsp. Worcestershire sauce or 1 Tbsp. Worcestershire sauce and 1 Tbsp. soy sauce
directions: Combine all ingredients and mix thoroughly.
Marinate 2 to 3 hours.
Stir occasionally.
For 2 pounds of cubed meat, 4 to 5 servings.

_enrichment.description: A simple yet flavourful marinade for shish kebabs, combining oil, vinegar, and spices to tenderise the meat.

title: Baklavas (Baklava)(Makes 40 Servings)
directions: In a medium saucepan, combine all ingredients, except lemon juice and honey.
Bring to a boil.
Simmer 20 minutes or until candy thermometer registers 224°.
Add lemon juice and honey; simmer 5 minutes.
Remove from heat; cool.

_enrichment.description: A rich syrup for baklava made with sugar, honey, and warm spices, perfect for drizzling over flaky pastry.

title: Shish Kebab
ingredients: 2 lb. lean beef, cut into 1-inch cubes (top round or sirloin)
directions: Marinate meat in dressing in plastic bag from 1 hour to overnight
(can
marinate
vegetables
also).
On each skewer, arrange beef, cherry tomato, onion, bell pepper and mushrooms, repeating until skewer is filled.
Grill over medium hot coals until
done,
turning
once.
Baste
with
marinade as needed. Serve with rice.

_enrichment.description: Classic shish kebabs with marinated beef, cherry tomatoes, onions, and peppers, grilled to juicy perfection.

title: Swedish Meat Balls
directions: Combine all ingredients except jelly and sauce.
Mix well and make balls.
Brown in skillet.
Combine jelly and chili sauce and mix well.
Pour sauce over meat balls and bake in a 350° oven for 45 minutes or place in large skillet on top of stove and simmer for 20 minutes.

_enrichment.description: Classic Swedish meatballs with a sweet and tangy sauce, perfect for a cosy family dinner.

title: Danish Cake
directions: Mix
shortening, sugar, eggs, flour, soda, salt, buttermilk, orange juice, nuts, dates, orange rind and vanilla.
Bake in a tube pan at 350° for 1 1/4 hours.
When done, while still hot, pour sauce over cake.

_enrichment.description: A rich Danish cake with a citrusy glaze, ideal for festive occasions or afternoon tea.

title: Quick Swedish Meatballs
directions: Combine meat, bread crumbs, cheese, soup mix, seasonings and 1/4 cup milk; mix thoroughly (with hands).
Form into small balls. Brown lightly; remove to a baking dish.
Drain excess grease, leaving small amount in skillet.
Blend in sauce mix and remaining milk (add more or less milk for desired thickness).
Cook until thick and bubbly.
Pour over meat balls.
Bake in a 350° oven for 30 to 45 minutes.

_enrichment.description: Quick and creamy Swedish meatballs with a stroganoff-inspired sauce, ready in under an hour.

title: Swedish Rye Bread
directions: Soften yeast in warm water.
Combine rye flour, molasses, shortening, salt and boiling water; blending well.
Cool to lukewarm; add softened yeast.
Stir in white flour gradually. Transfer to floured board, let rest 10 minutes.
Knead until shiny (about 10 minutes).
Cover and put in greased bowl to rise for 2 hours.

_enrichment.description: A dense and hearty Swedish rye bread, perfect for sandwiches or served with butter.

title: Irish (Norwegian) Soda Bread
directions: Preheat oven to 350°.
Grease an 8 1/2-inch pan (preferably an iron frying pan).
Mix dry ingredients.
Add raisins and caraway seed.
Mix well until raisins are coated with flour. Combine eggs, buttermilk and melted butter. Add to dry ingredients and mix lightly until moistened. Turn batter into pan and shape round. Bake 1 hour.
Serve slices buttered with a good cup of coffee or tea.

_enrichment.description: A traditional Irish soda bread with raisins and caraway, best enjoyed with butter and tea.

title: Swedish Meatballs
directions: Mix meat, eggs, onion, salt and pepper; shape into small balls.
Drop raw balls into sauce made from remaining ingredients. Gently simmer about 20 minutes until done.
These may be frozen and reheated for later use.
Serve in a chafing dish for party appetizers.

_enrichment.description: Swedish meatballs simmered in a tangy sauce, perfect for a party appetiser or quick meal.

title: Krum Kaga(Norwegian Pastry)
directions: In a bowl, sift together dry ingredients.
In second bowl, cream together the sugar and butter.
Blend in eggs and whipping cream.
Stir in dry ingredients. Drop by teaspoonfuls on hot krum kaga iron. When light brown, wrap around stick.

_enrichment.description: Norwegian pastry cookies with a crisp texture, traditionally cooked on a special iron.

title: Crock-Pot Swedish Cabbage Rolls
directions: Place cabbage leaves in microwavable dish with 2 to 4 tablespoons water and microwave for about 3 minutes or until cabbage is limp.
Combine egg, milk, onion, salt, pepper, meat and cooked rice.
Place about 1/4 cup meat mixture in center of each leaf; fold in sides and roll ends over meat.
Place in crock-pot. Combine tomato sauce with brown sugar, lemon juice and Worcestershire sauce.
Pour over cabbage rolls.
Cover and cook on low 7 to 9 hours.
Serves 6.

_enrichment.description: Slow-cooked Swedish cabbage rolls filled with turkey and rice, a comforting winter dish.

title: Swedish Raspberry Cake
directions: Cream butter and sugar.
Add eggs one at a time while beating. Combine flour, baking powder and salt.
Add to butter, sugar and egg mixture alternately with sour cream, baking soda and lemon rind.
Fold in dry raspberries.
Bake at 350° for 1 1/2 hours using a Bundt pan or angel food cake pan.
This makes a large, rich cake. May be frozen.

_enrichment.description: A moist Swedish raspberry cake with a rich texture, perfect for celebrations or tea time.

title: Danish Sesame Bran Bread
directions: Combine boiling water and cornmeal.
Combine 1 1/2 cups unbleached flour, 1 cup whole wheat flour, cereal, sesame seed, salt and yeast; blend well.
Heat milk, oil, molasses and honey until very warm.
Add warm liquid, cornmeal mixture and yogurt to flour mixture.
Blend well.
Stir in rest of whole wheat flour until stiff dough forms.
Knead in 1 to 1 1/2 cups all-purpose flour about 8 minutes.
Place in greased bowl; cover loosely with plastic wrap and cloth towel.
Let rise in warm place until light and doubled in size, 1 to 1 1/2 hours.

_enrichment.description: A wholesome Danish bread with sesame and bran, ideal for a healthy breakfast or snack.

title: Swedish Pot Roast
directions: Mix salt, pepper and cinnamon.
Rub spice mixture on both sides of roast.
Brown roast in some oil in a roaster.
Now mix the vinegar and brown sugar together and pour over the roast.
Add onions and bay leaves and pot in oven. Bake for 3 - 4 hours at 350 degrees.

_enrichment.description: A slow-cooked Swedish pot roast with a sweet and tangy glaze, perfect for a Sunday roast.

title: Swedish Nut Wafers
directions: Preheat oven to 350°.
Lightly butter a cookie sheet or jellyroll pan.
Cream butter, then gradually beat in the sugar until well blended.
Add egg, milk and vanilla; beat well.
Mix together flour, baking powder and salt; mix into first mixture. Spread dough onto the cookie sheet as thin as possible, just enough to cover pan.
Sprinkle with nuts and press them into dough gently.
Mark with sharp knife into rectangle (cookie size), bake about 12 minutes.

_enrichment.description: Crispy Swedish nut wafers with a buttery texture, a classic holiday treat.

title: Michael'S Danish Chocolate Chip Tart A' L'Orange
ingredients: 2 (4 oz.) pkg. graham crackers (mini tart crusts)
directions: Preheat oven to 350°.
In large bowl, combine Ricotta, eggs, sugar, butter and vanilla; beat well.
Stir in chocolate chips. Spoon 1/4 cup into each crust (mini).
Place on cookie sheet. Bake at 350° for 20 to 25 minutes (center will be soft).
Cool completely.

_enrichment.description: A decadent Danish chocolate tart with mandarin oranges, ideal for a special dessert.

title: Norwegian Fattigman
directions: Mix all of the ingredients using enough flour to make a soft dough.
Roll out thin and cut in strips in the shape of diamonds. Make a one inch slit in the middle of each diamond.
Loop one tip of the diamond through slit to make the cookie look like a diamond with a knot in the middle.
Fry in hot lard turning once.
Drain well on paper towels.
Cool and sprinkle with powdered sugar.

_enrichment.description: Norwegian fattigman cookies with a delicate texture, dusted with powdered sugar.

title: Norwegian Betasuppe
ingredients: 1 lb. ham hocks or 1/2 c. finely chopped ham
2 c. each: potatoes, diced, rutabaga and celery root
directions: Combine peas, ham hocks or ham, onion, thyme and water.
Add potatoes, rutabaga, celery root, leek and salt.
Combine ingredients in a large Dutch oven and simmer for 1 1/2 to 2 hours. Add vegies and salt and cook for another 30 to 45 minutes.
If using ham hocks, remove, trim, cut finely and return to the soup!

_enrichment.description: A hearty Norwegian pea soup with ham hocks, a traditional winter dish.

title: Pepparkakor(Thin Swedish Gingersnaps)
directions: In small bowl, sift flour and mix remaining dry ingredients (first 5 items).
In medium bowl, cream butter until soft. Gradually add sugar, creaming until fluffy.
Add well beaten egg and dark corn syrup.
Blend in dry ingredients in fourths, mixing well after each addition.
Cover with plastic wrap and refrigerate for several hours or overnight.
Prepare a surface for rolling dough.
If you have a pastry cloth and rolling pin cover, they will do well.
Dust each liberally with flour (or sprinkle flour onto pastry board or clean countertop).

_enrichment.description: Classic Swedish gingersnaps with warm spices, perfect for festive occasions or as a sweet treat with coffee.

title: Swedish Roasted Potatoes
directions: Slash potatoes into thick slices, but do not cut all the way through.
Add seasoning to hot water and pour over potatoes. Baste while cooking.
About 15 minutes before removing from the oven, drain off most of the broth and thicken slighlty with flour. Pour over potatoes.
Sprinkle with grated cheese.
Bake in oven at 300° for 2 to 2 1/2 hours.

_enrichment.description: Hearty Swedish roasted potatoes with a cheesy topping, ideal as a comforting side dish for family meals.

title: Danish Sugar Cookies(Makes About 7 Dozen)
directions: Sift dry ingredients into a bowl.
Cut in butter and shortening until mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
Stir in egg and vanilla; dough is fairly soft.
Shape into tiny balls; roll in granulated sugar and place, 2 inches apart, onto greased cookie sheets.
Flatten to 1/2-inch.
Bake at 350° for 12 minutes, or until delicately browned.
Can be frosted with powdered sugar frosting and decorated with colored sugar.
Or, just roll in colored sugar instead of regular granulated sugar.

_enrichment.description: Buttery Danish sugar cookies rolled in sugar, a simple yet delightful treat for any occasion.

title: Norwegian Coffee Cake
directions: Rub with fingers into crumbs the flour, sugar and butter. Save 2 cups for top.
Mix the remaining ingredients; add remaining crumb mixture.
Pour into greased and floured 8 x 8-inch pan. Sprinkle crumbs on top.
Bake for about 30 minutes in a 350° to 400° oven.

_enrichment.description: A moist Norwegian coffee cake with a crumbly topping, perfect for brunch or a cosy afternoon tea.

title: Danish Sweet Rolls
ingredients: 1 pkg. yeast, dissolved in 1/4 c. water
directions: Mix the first 4 ingredients like pie crust. Add the eggs, milk and yeast mixture.
Beat.
Add 1 cup flour. Dough will be soft. Let set in refrigerator for several hours. Take out. Cut into 4 parts and roll out each part (like pie crust).
Spread with butter.
Cut into triangular wedges.
Roll each wedge from wide side to point. Let rise and bake 20 to 25 minutes at 350°.
Frost and sprinkle with nuts.

_enrichment.description: Soft and buttery Danish sweet rolls, a classic treat often enjoyed with morning coffee or as a snack.

title: Pepparkokar(Swedish Christmas Cookies)
directions: Mix butter, sugar, egg and molasses together in mixer.
Add flour, soda and spices.
Chill dough in refrigerator; roll thin and bake at 350° for 8 to 10 minutes.
(Dough may also be shaped into "logs", frozen and sliced from ends; not as pretty, but much easier!)

_enrichment.description: Traditional Swedish Christmas cookies with a molasses and spice flavour, perfect for festive baking.

title: Finnish Bread
ingredients: 1 cake yeast, softened in 1/4 c. warm water
directions: Mix together the milk, yeast and about 1/3 of the flour.
Beat well and let stand 1 hour.
Stir in the sugar, salt, butter, eggs and cardamom seed; beat well. Add the rest of the flour and knead the dough thoroughly.
Let rise until double in size.
Shape in loaves or rolls and let rise again.
Bake at 375° to 400° for 30 minutes.

_enrichment.description: A classic Finnish bread with cardamom, light and slightly sweet, ideal for breakfast or as a snack.

title: "Kalops" Swedish Beef Stew
ingredients: 1 1/2 lb. lean beef chuck, cut into 1-inch cubes
directions: Dredge meat in flour; set aside.
In Dutch oven, melt butter over medium heat until golden brown.
Add meat and brown well. Sprinkle with any leftover flour and salt, allspice, bay leaves and 1 1/2 cups water.
Stir to loosen pan drippings.
Cover; simmer 1 hour and 15 minutes.
Stir occasionally until meat is almost tender.
Add carrots and onions and more water, if necessary, to cover and simmer until meat and vegetables are tender.
Discard bay leaves.
Serve with mashed potatoes and pickled beets.

_enrichment.description: A hearty Swedish beef stew with tender meat and vegetables, a comforting dish for cold evenings.

title: Norwegian Meat Balls And Noodles
directions: Cut onion in small pieces and saute in 2 tablespoons of oil or butter for a few minutes.
Then add the remaining ingredients. Form into meatballs and brown in oil or butter until done.
Add meatballs to a can of golden mushroom soup and simmer a few minutes.
Serve over your favorite noodles.

_enrichment.description: Norwegian meatballs served with noodles, a comforting and hearty meal for family dinners.

title: Sweet Bread(Scandinavian)
directions: Melt butter in hot milk and set aside.
Add yeast to cool milk and add sugar, cardamom, salt and eggs.
Combine the two sets of liquids in a large bowl. Add approximately 8 cups flour.
Knead into a rather stiff dough.
Let stand overnight.
In the morning divide the dough into 6 pieces.
Roll out into strips, 1 1/2-inches in diameter.
Braid 3 strips together into a loaf.

Set on a greased flat pan or cookie sheet; let dough rise lightly, about 1 hour. Bake at 350° for about 35 minutes (until nicely browned). Remove from oven; brush top with sugar melted in a little hot coffee. Sprinkle with cinnamon and sugar.

_enrichment.description: A traditional Scandinavian sweet bread with cardamom, light and fluffy, perfect for breakfast or tea.

title: Swedish Meat Loaf
directions: Combine beef, milk, 1/3 can soup, dry onion soup mix, parsley flakes, thyme and nutmeg.
Place in a 12 x 9 x 2 1/2-inch baking dish.
Shape into a loaf. Bake at 350° for 1 hour or until done. Heat remaining soup and sour cream, stirring constantly.
Serve over meat loaf.
Serves 8.

_enrichment.description: A moist Swedish meat loaf with a creamy mushroom sauce, a comforting dish for family meals.

title: Swedish Sweet Bread
ingredients: 1/2 c. warm milk, scalded then cooled
1/2 c. shortening or margarine or butter
directions: Dissolve yeast in warm water.
Stir in milk, sugar, salt, eggs, shortening and 2 1/2 cups flour to make dough easy to handle.
Turn dough onto lightly floured board; knead until smooth and elastic about 5 minutes.
Cover and let rise in warm place until double, about 1 1/2 hours.

_enrichment.description: A soft and slightly sweet Swedish sweet bread, perfect for breakfast or as a snack with coffee.

title: Yulekage(Norwegian Christmas Bread)
ingredients: 1/2 c. lukewarm water plus 2 tsp. sugar
directions: Put sugar, salt, butter and milk powder into a large bowl. Bring potato water to a boil and pour over first 4 ingredients. Let cool to lukewarm.
(Potato water is water potatoes have been boiled in.
If you don't have potato water, you can use plain water and add 2 tablespoons instant potato flakes to the first 4 ingredients.
Potato water helps make a moist loaf that doesn't become stale quickly.)
Add eggs and yeast mixture (yeast, water and 2 teaspoons sugar) to bowl.
Beat in about 4 cups of flour plus the cardamon.
Gradually beat in more flour until the dough is stiff enough to handle on the bread board.

_enrichment.description: A moist Norwegian Christmas bread with cardamom and raisins, a festive treat for the holiday season.

title: Danish Cheesecake
directions: Mix the first 3 ingredients together until crumbly, then press in a 9 x 13-inch pan.
Mix the next 3 ingredients and beat with mixer until smooth.
Pour over the first layer.
Sprinkle the top with pecans and bake for 35 minutes at 350°.
Sprinkle with powdered sugar as soon as removed from oven.

_enrichment.description: A creamy Danish cheesecake with a crumbly base and nutty topping, a delightful dessert for any occasion.

title: Norwegian Toast Cookies(Tebrod)
ingredients: 1 tsp. cardamom, vanilla or anise flavoring
directions: Mix all ingredients together in order given.
Dough should be sticky, but not real glossy.
You may have to add a tablespoon or so more flour.
On a greased cookie sheet, spoon and shape into 3 loaves.
Bake at 350° for 25 minutes. Remove from oven; slice loaves.
Arrange slices, cut side down, on cookie sheet and bake another 15 minutes at 375° or until golden brown.
Each loaf makes 18 slices.
To make daintier slices, make loaves larger or narrower.
These are very good.

_enrichment.description: Crispy Norwegian toast cookies with a hint of cardamom, a simple yet delicious treat for any time of day.

title: Norwegian Christmas Bread
ingredients: 1/2 small container mixed fruit (1/2 pineapple; 1/2 cherry)
directions: Cream shortening and sugar; add eggs.
Sift cardamon, flour and baking powder together (save out a small amount to mix with raisins and fruit).
Add to mixture, alternating the flour and milk.
Mix raisins and fruits with flour; add to total mixture. Put into 1 large loaf pan or divide between 2 small loaf pans. Bake at 375° for 10 minutes, then 350° for 40 minutes.

_enrichment.description: A festive Norwegian sweet bread studded with raisins and mixed fruit, flavoured with cardamom and best enjoyed during the Christmas season.

title: Swedish Ginger Cookies
directions: Thoroughly cream butter and sugar.
Add egg and beat until light and fluffy.
Add orange peel, corn syrup and water; mix well.
Sift together dry ingredients; stir into creamed mixture. Chill dough thoroughly.

_enrichment.description: Crisp and aromatic Swedish ginger cookies with a hint of cinnamon and cloves, perfect for festive gatherings or a cosy afternoon treat.

title: Jansson'S Temptation(Swedish Recipe)
ingredients: 6 medium-size potatoes, peeled and cut into 2 x 1/4-inch strips
3/4 c. cream or half and half
directions: Preheat oven to 400°.
Keep potato strip in cold water to prevent discoloration. Melt 2 tablespoons butter in skillet and add onions.
Cook until soft, but not brown, about 5 minutes.
Pat potatoes dry.
Arrange layers of potatoes, onions and anchovies in greased 1 1/2 to 2-quart baking dish.
Begin and end with potatoes.
Dot casserole with butter and sprinkle with pepper and bread crumbs.
Pour cream around the casserole.
Bake in the center of the oven until the potatoes are tender and most of the liquid is absorbed, about 45 to 60 minutes.
Serves 4 to 6.

_enrichment.description: A classic Swedish potato casserole layered with onions, anchovies, and cream, baked until golden and irresistibly savoury.

title: Swedish Apple Pie(Easy, Quick, Yummy Dessert)
directions: Mix the first five ingredients together with a fork.
Grease pie plate.
Peel and core apples (enough to fill to heaping); put into pie plate and sprinkle with sugar and cinnamon.
Spoon dough on top of fruit and bake at 325° until golden brown or about 1 hour.

_enrichment.description: A quick and easy Swedish apple pie with a simple dough topping, perfect for a homely dessert with minimal effort.

title: Norwegian Te-Brod
directions: Melt butter; add sugar, beaten eggs, extract and flour which has been sifted with baking powder, salt and cardamon.
Grease and flour 9 x 13-inch pan.
Pour in and spread with a mixture of sugar and cinnamon.
Bake for 30 minutes in 300° oven.
Remove and cool. Split lengthwise, then into slices.
Put on cookie sheet and put in 350° oven to brown.
Turn over and brown other side.

_enrichment.description: A buttery Norwegian tea bread with a cinnamon-sugar swirl, ideal for afternoon tea or a light snack.

title: Swedish Cabbage Rolls
directions: In bowl, combine egg, salt, pepper, Worcestershire sauce, onion and milk and mix well.
Add ground beef and cooked rice and mix well.
Immerse cabbage leaves in boiling water for 3 minutes or just until limp.
Heavy center vein of leaf may be split about 2 1/2-inches.

_enrichment.description: Swedish-style cabbage rolls stuffed with a savoury beef and rice mixture, served in a tangy tomato soup.

title: Danish Red Cabbage
ingredients: 1 large red cabbage, cored and thinly sliced
2 Tbsp. fat (raw goose fat, salt pork or bacon fat)
1/2 c. wine vinegar (second choice, cider vinegar)
2 to 3 apples, peeled and cut up fine
directions: Put cabbage in a heavy, porcelain lined iron pot with vinegar. Add fat.
Carefully bring to a boil.
Add a little water if necessary.
Add apples, allspice berries, salt, sugar and raisins. Cabbage should be juicy but not soupy.
Taste for salt and give the pepper mill a few turns.

_enrichment.description: A rich and tangy Danish red cabbage dish cooked with apples, berries, and a touch of sweetness, perfect as a side for festive meals.

title: Sot Suppe(A Norwegian Cold, Fruit Soup)
directions: Place tapioca and water in kettle.
Let soak for several hours.
Prepare seedless raisins and prunes as directed on package for stewed fruit.
Remove from burner and cool.
Place kettle of tapioca mixture on burner.
Cook until mixture is almost clear. Add grape juice, lemon juice, sugar and cinnamon. Simmer gently for several minutes or until clear.
Gently stir in stewed fruit. Can be served warm or cold.

_enrichment.description: A refreshing Norwegian cold fruit soup made with tapioca, grape juice, and a blend of dried fruits, served chilled.

title: Swedish Meatballs With Rice
ingredients: 1 lb. ground beef or ground turkey
directions: Combine beef, bread crumbs, egg, onion, salt and paprika. Shape into 1 1/2-inch balls.
Brown the meatballs in a skillet with the melted margarine.
Combine the mushroom soup and water, pouring over meatballs.
Simmer for 15 minutes.
Serve on top of cooked rice.

_enrichment.description: Classic Swedish meatballs served on a bed of rice, smothered in a creamy mushroom sauce for a comforting meal.

title: Swedish Cardamon Bread
ingredients: 1 egg yolk, beaten with 1 Tbsp. milk
directions: Dissolve yeast in warm beer in a large bowl.

Heat milk to warm and add to yeast; stir in sugar and salt. Gradually stir in 2 cups flour; stir the batter with a spoon for 100 strokes or more. Add melted butter, cardamon, orange rind and eggs and stir well. Gradually add remaining flour until 2 more cups have been added. If needed, add 1 more cup flour.
Dough should be soft, but not too sticky.
Turn onto board.
Let rise to twice size.
Rework the dough and form into 4 long strips.
Braid into 2 loaves.
Let raise and brush top with yolk and milk mixture.
Bake until brown.

_enrichment.description: A soft and aromatic Swedish cardamom bread, lightly sweetened and perfect for breakfast or fika with coffee.

title: Fruit-Glazed Easter Ham
directions: Place ham, fat side up, on rack in open roasting pan.
Insert meat thermometer so bulb is centered in thickest part.
Be careful bulb does not rest in fat or on bone.
Do not add water.
Do not cover.
Roast at 325° until meat thermometer registers 130° to 140° for "fully cooked" half ham (18 to 24 minutes per pound) or 160° for a "cook before eating" half ham (22 to 25 minutes per pound).

_enrichment.description: A glazed Easter ham coated in a sweet and tangy fruit glaze, roasted to perfection for a festive centrepiece.

title: Beef Jerky
directions: Mix in a plastic bag; seal and put into refrigerator.
Next day, mix again.
Following day, mix and leave bag open in refrigerator.
The next day, spread out on cookie sheets and put into oven.
For electric ovens, cook on warm approximately 12 hours.
For gas ovens, leave in oven with just pilot light for approximately 24 hours.

_enrichment.description: Homemade beef jerky marinated in a smoky and tangy sauce, dried to a chewy perfection for a protein-packed snack.

title: Barry'S Deer Jerky
directions: Combine all ingredients together and marinate in the refrigerator for 6 to 10 hours.
When ready to dry, remove slices and drain on paper towels; may pat dry.
To dry, spread slices in single layer on oven racks.
Place a pan under racks to catch drippings.
Set temperature at 140° or lowest temperature on your stove.
Open door slightly to allow air to circulate and moisture to escape.
Drying will take 6 to 10 hours.
Turn over after 4 hours.
Jerky is done when meat is shriveled, dark, leathery and chewy.

_enrichment.description: A simple and flavourful deer jerky marinated in soy sauce and spices, dried to a chewy texture for a wild game treat.

title: Caribbean Shrimp And Black Bean Salad
ingredients: 1 lb. cooked, cleaned and deveined medium shrimp
1 can (15 oz.) black beans, rinsed and drained
1 small green pepper, cut into short, thin strips
1/3 c. very thinly sliced small red onion rings
shredded peel and juice from 1 lime (1 tsp. peel and 2 Tbsp. juice)
directions: Combine shrimp, beans, green pepper, celery and onion in large bowl.
Combine remaining ingredients except tomatoes and lettuce; mix well. Pour over shrimp mixture; toss lightly to coat.
Cover and chill at least 2 hours or up to 24 hours, tossing lightly occasionally.
Spoon salad onto lettuce-lined serving platter; garnish with tomatoes.
Serve with additional picante sauce. Makes 6 servings.

_enrichment.description: A vibrant Caribbean-inspired salad with shrimp, black beans, and a zesty lime-honey dressing, served chilled for a refreshing meal.

title: Jamaican Steak
ingredients: 1/3 c. lime juice (fresh is best, about 3 limes)
2 lb. top round, sirloin or flank steak
directions: In a small bowl whisk together all ingredients except steak and lime wedges.
Score steak across top or pierce top of steak with a fork.
Place in a shallow glass baking dish and pour lime juice mixture over the steak; turn to coat both sides. Refrigerate 6 to 8 hours to "marry" the flavors, turning occasionally.
Remove steak from marinade and grill 5 or 6 minutes on each side or to desired doneness.
Slice thinly on the diagonal; serve with lime wedges.
Makes 6 servings.

_enrichment.description: A zesty Jamaican-style marinated flank steak grilled to perfection and served with lime wedges for a bold and tangy meal.

title: Beef Or Deer Jerky
directions: Mix all ingredients and marinate overnight.
Remove meat from juice and dehydrate.
If you do not have a dehydrator, place in oven on 140° with oven door cracked open so meat will dry out without being cooked.
If meat is thin enough, it should dry out overnight.

_enrichment.description: A savoury, smoky snack made by marinating lean beef or venison in a spiced sauce and drying it to create jerky. Perfect for on-the-go energy.

title: Microwave Beef Jerky With Fresh Ginger
ingredients: 1 1/2 lb. flank steak, all fat removed and cut in thin strips
directions: Combine ginger, honey, sesame oil, soy sauce, wine, garlic and cayenne.
Add steak to marinade, tossing to coat well.
Marinate at room temperature, tossing occasionally, for 3 hours.
Remove meat from marinade and arrange half the strips, flat and close together, on a microwave bacon rack.
Cover with paper towels and cook on Medium-low 20 minutes.
Rotate strips so that drier strips are in the center. Cover with fresh paper towel and cook on Medium-low for 15 to 20 minutes until meat is dry but slightly pliable.
Remove to a large wire rack and let stand overnight. Repeat with remaining strips.

_enrichment.description: A quick microwave method for making tender beef jerky with a hint of ginger and sesame, ideal for a spicy, umami-rich snack.

title: Coconut Shrimp
directions: In medium-sized bowl, mix eggs, 1 cup flour, rum, milk, baking powder and spices.
Place remaining flour in small bowl.
Spread coconut on small plate.
Dredge shrimp in flour, then dunk it in the batter, then dredge it in coconut.
Place coated shrimp on waxed paper.
Carefully drop shrimp in hot oil and fry 3 to 5 minutes.
Drain on paper towels and serve hot.

_enrichment.description: Crispy, golden coconut-coated shrimp fried to perfection, offering a sweet and nutty contrast with a light, flaky texture.

title: Jamaican Red Beans And Rice
directions: Break and remove coconut from shell; wash and grate coconut very fine.
Add water to grated coconut; mix and squeeze milk from the grated nut.
Get at least 4 cups of milk.
Place milk in pot. Add salt to taste, onion and green onion and bring to a boil.
Add rice and water from cans of kidney beans.
Return to boil, then cover and simmer on low heat.
When rice is almost cooked, add beans and finish cooking.

_enrichment.description: A hearty, one-pot dish of red kidney beans and rice simmered in creamy coconut milk, seasoned with aromatic spices for a comforting meal.

title: Honey Coconut Loaf
directions: Beat together water, eggs, vegetable oil, honey and sugar. Mix flour with baking soda, salt, cinnamon and allspice.
Add gradually to liquid mixture, stirring just to blend.
Fold in citron and coconut. Spoon into a greased and floured 9 x 5 x 4-inch loaf pan.
Bake at 350° for 60 minutes or until a toothpick inserted in center of loaf comes out clean. Cool on rack in pan 10 minutes.
Carefully remove from pan and cool on rack. Wrap cooled loaf in aluminum foil and store overnight before cutting.

_enrichment.description: A moist, spiced loaf with a tender crumb, sweetened with honey and studded with coconut and citron for a festive treat.

title: Three Layer Jam Cake
directions: Cream together the butter, sugar, vanilla, jam and eggs.
Sift flour together with the other dry ingredients, then combine the two mixtures alternately with buttermilk.
Add raisins, nuts and coconut.
(Take out 1 1/2 cups batter and save.)
Pour remaining batter equally into 3 round cake pans and bake in your oven as you would any other heavy cake. After cake is cool, take the reserved batter and mix in
1/2 cup sugar, 1 cup brown sugar, 1/2 cup butter and 1 cup milk.
Cook until thickened. Cool and spread in between layers and on top.
Frost with Caramel Frosting.

_enrichment.description: A rich, three-layer cake with a moist crumb, filled with blackberry jam and topped with a spiced buttercream for a decadent dessert.

title: Zesty Caribbean Chicken
ingredients: 6 boneless, skinless chicken breast halves (about 1 1/2 lb.)
directions: Combine Crisco, lemon peel, lemon juice, paprika, honey, garlic salt, ginger, oregano and hot pepper sauce in shallow baking dish.
Stir well.
Add chicken.
Coat well.
Refrigerate for 30 minutes or up to 4 hours.
(The longer the chicken marinates, the better the chicken will taste.) Turn chicken occasionally.
Broil or grill 3 to 5 minutes per side or until the center is no longer pink.

_enrichment.description: Juicy chicken marinated in a zesty blend of citrus, honey, and spices, then grilled to perfection for a tangy, flavourful main.

title: Caribbean Grilled Chicken With Citrus
ingredients: 2 red grapefruit, peeled, sliced and seeded
directions: In a non-metallic bowl, stir together chicken broth, orange juice concentrate, vinegar, jerk seasoning, orange peel and garlic. Cover and chill half of the mixture for sauce.
Rinse chicken; pat dry.
In a plastic bag set in a shallow dish, combine chicken and remaining orange mixture.
Close bag and chill for 2 to 24 hours, turning bag occasionally to distribute marinade.

_enrichment.description: Tender chicken marinated in a citrusy jerk seasoning, grilled and served with rice for a vibrant, tropical-inspired meal.

title: Jamaican Coconut Bread
directions: Sift together flour, baking powder and salt.
Add coconut, sugar, vanilla and beaten egg, beating the mixture well after each addition.
Pour in milk, a little at a time, until dough is firm but not sticky.
(Add a little more flour if it should become so.) Knead the dough
for a few minutes.
Shape into loaves to fit your greased loaf pans to about 3/4 full. Dust tops with fine sugar.
Bake at 350° for about 1 hour.

_enrichment.description: A lightly sweetened bread studded with grated coconut, offering a tender crumb and a subtle nutty flavour, perfect with tea.

title: Deer Jerky
directions: Cover bottom of pan with meat strips.
Liberally salt, pepper and garlic.
Sprinkle with soy sauce.
Cover with Worcestershire sauce.
Repeat layers if necessary.
Make sure meat is submerged. Cover and refrigerate 24 to 48 hours.
Place foil on oven racks; shape an edge.
Lay meat in single layer, edge to edge.
Brush with liquid smoke.
Dry in 150° to 175° oven until bends, but does not break, about 5 hours.

_enrichment.description: A classic venison jerky marinated in soy sauce, Worcestershire, and spices for a savoury, umami-rich snack with a chewy texture.

title: Layer Fruit Cake
directions: Run nuts and raisins through food chopper.
Cream sugar, margarine, eggs and milk.
Add dry ingredients.
Add jam, nuts and raisins.
Pour in 3 or 4 cake pans that have been sprayed.
Bake at 350°.

_enrichment.description: A rich, layered fruit cake packed with nuts, raisins, and coconut, offering a moist, spiced crumb for a festive dessert.

title: Caribbean Fudge Pie
ingredients: 1/2 c. halves or large pieces of walnuts
directions: Heat oven to 375°.
Beat margarine and sugar in a large bowl until light and fluffy.
Add eggs, one at a time, beating well after each addition.
Add chocolate, instant coffee and rum extract; mix well.
Stir in flour and 1 cup chopped walnuts; pour into baked pie shell.
Decorate top with 1/2 cup walnut halves or pieces.
Bake in lower third of oven for 25 minutes.
Cool on wire rack.
Refrigerate for at least 1 hour before serving.
Store in refrigerator.

_enrichment.description: A decadent fudge pie with a rich chocolate filling, rum, and walnuts, topped with a crunchy nut layer for a luxurious dessert.

title: Raw Apple Cake
directions: Grease and flour Bundt pan.
Mix oil, sugar and eggs.
Mix dry ingredients and add to oil, sugar and eggs. Add sour cream and coconut.
Add apples and mix well.
Bake in Bundt pan about 50 minutes at 350° or until crust breaks away.

_enrichment.description: A moist apple cake with a tender crumb, studded with coconut and topped with a light sour cream glaze for a comforting treat.

title: Jamaican Fruit Cake
directions: Presoak fruit in wine or rum.
Combine fruits and nuts.
Sift flour and mix 2 cups with fruit and nuts. Cream butter until soft and blend with sugar.
Add eggs, singly beating well. Add jelly.

_enrichment.description: A boozy, fruity cake packed with soaked raisins, nuts, and spices, offering a rich, moist crumb for a festive dessert.

title: "Ladies Night Out" Jam Cake
ingredients: 1 c. black walnut pieces or other nuts
directions: In large mixing bowl cream butter and sugar.
Add eggs and beat until creamy.	Mix flour and soda.
Add to creamed mixture and mix well.
Add buttermilk, jam and allspice.
Mix well.
Stir in walnuts, raisins and coconut.
Pour into lightly greased and floured 10-inch tube pan.
Bake 1 hour in preheated 325° oven. Cool 15 minutes in pan.
Loosen cake and invert onto cooling rack. Serves 12 to 16.

_enrichment.description: A spiced jam cake with a moist crumb, studded with walnuts, raisins, and coconut, perfect for a cosy, festive dessert.

title: Venison Jerky
ingredients: 3 lb. lean round or rump venison (or beef neck meat is also good)
directions: Cut meat in strips 1/2 x 1/4 x 4 to 6-inches long.
Mix all other ingredients and pour over meat.
Marinate overnight in fridge.
Drain meat and pat dry with paper towels.
Let meat cure in oven using pilot heat or lowest setting.
Turn oven off periodically.
Process until done.

_enrichment.description: A savoury, protein-rich snack made from lean venison marinated in a blend of Worcestershire sauce and spices, then dried to a chewy texture. Perfect for on-the-go energy.

title: Jerky(Beef Or Venison)
directions: Mix ingredients together.
Marinate 1 1/2 pounds meat, cut in strips and fat trimmed away.
(Marinate overnight.)
Dry on racks in oven at 140°, about 12 hours.
Keep oven door open slightly.

_enrichment.description: Classic beef or venison jerky with a rich, smoky flavour from liquid smoke and soy sauce, dried slowly for a tender yet chewy texture.

title: Tropical Carrot Cake
directions: Sift flour and all other dry ingredients together, except sugar.
Prepare carrots, pineapple and nuts.
Grease and flour 3 (9-inch) round cake pans.
Preheat oven to 350°.
Beat eggs lightly; beat in sugar.
Stir in oil, dry ingredients, carrots and 3/4 cup pineapple.
Stir in nuts and coconut.
Spread evenly in pans.
Bake for 40 to 45 minutes, until sides shrink and top springs back when touched.
Sprinkle with powdered sugar; decorate with grated orange peel.

_enrichment.description: A moist and spiced carrot cake packed with pineapple, walnuts, and coconut, offering a tropical twist on a classic dessert.

title: Jamaican Curried Goat
ingredients: 2 lb. goat meat (preferably leg), cut in 2-inch pieces
directions: First wash meat, then add curry, scallions, onion, garlic, pepper and salt.
For better taste, meat should be seasoned in advance.
Pot should also be of adequate size and partially covered.
Add water and cook until meat is tender.
Creates its own gravy.
Can be served with any vegetable.

_enrichment.description: A fragrant Jamaican curried goat stew, slow-cooked until tender and served with vegetables, offering bold Caribbean flavours.

title: Mamie Rhodes' Jam Cake
directions: Mix all ingredients together.
Put in a greased and floured pan.
Bake at 350° until toothpick comes out clean.
It will be a little sticky, but not doughy.

_enrichment.description: A sticky, spiced jam cake studded with coconut, raisins, nuts, and cherries, offering a sweet and aromatic dessert experience.

title: Turkey Jerky
ingredients: 4 tsp. hickory salt or liquid smoke
directions: Place strips of turkey in marinade of all ingredients for 6 hours.
Drain, but do not rinse.
Place meat on foil in center of oven.
Set oven on 200°.
Place a wedge in door (clothespin will work).
Bake 10 hours or until meat turns a dirty brown.
Check after 2 hours.
Store in airtight container.

_enrichment.description: Turkey jerky with a sweet and smoky marinade, dried to a chewy texture for a lean, protein-packed snack.

title: Beef Jerky(6 Servings)
ingredients: 1 1/2 lb. flank steak, trimmed and partially frozen
directions: Slice beef across grain in 1/4-inch slices.
Place in a shallow dish.
In a small bowl, combine remaining ingredients; pour over beef.
Cover; refrigerate overnight.
Preheat oven to 150°.
Lay beef strips on a rack set over an aluminum foil lined pan.
Place in oven; prop oven door open with a pencil.
Bake for 8 to 12 hours, or until meat is dry and chewy.
Perfect for snacking.

_enrichment.description: A classic beef jerky recipe with a savoury marinade of soy sauce and Worcestershire, dried for a chewy, flavourful snack.

title: Caribbean Cream Pie
ingredients: 3/4 c. sweetened pineapple, drained (reserve 3 Tbsp. juice)
directions: Bake pie crust (in deep pie plate), and set aside.
Toast 3/4 cup packed coconut in 350° oven.
Watch closely and remove from oven when light brown.
Set aside.

_enrichment.description: A creamy Caribbean-inspired pie with coconut, banana, and pineapple, offering a tropical twist on a classic cream pie.

title: All-Natural Beef Jerky
directions: Trim off all fat from one round steak.
Cut meat into thin strips and place in oblong pan.
Combine the next 7 ingredients in a saucepan.
Bring mixture to boil and simmer 10 minutes.
Cool, pour liquid over meat through strainer.
Let sit in refrigerator at least 8 hours.
Remove marinated pieces and lay across a broiling pan.
Put strips into cold oven and set the temperature at 125°.
Dry meat in oven 10 to 12 hours.
Store in a covered container.

_enrichment.description: A simple, all-natural beef jerky with a savoury marinade of soy sauce and hot sauce, dried for a chewy, protein-rich snack.

title: Ree'S Jerky
directions: Cut the venison or beef into thin strips.
Dip into the mixture.
Then put into dryer or dehydrator.
Dry until it's the way you like it.

_enrichment.description: A quick and easy beef or venison jerky with a spicy, smoky marinade, dried for a chewy, flavourful snack.

title: Apple Harvest Pudding
directions: Cream together sugar, shortening and egg.
Add dry ingredients and spices alternately with 1 cup milk.
Add fruit and nuts.
Pour into 9 x 13-inch glass baking dish and bake at 350° for 25 to 35 minutes.

_enrichment.description: A spiced apple and banana pudding with coconut and raisins, offering a moist and fragrant dessert with autumnal flavours.

title: Ground Deer Jerky
directions: Preparation time:
16 to 20 hours.
Mix meat and ingredients then place in refrigerator for 12 hours.
Then roll in thin strips on cookie sheet.
Bake at 150° for 4 to 8 hours or when dry.
When dry, store in airtight container in refrigerator or freezer.

_enrichment.description: A lean ground venison jerky with a spicy, smoky marinade, rolled thin and dried for a chewy, protein-packed snack.

title: Sweet Potato Bread Pudding
directions: Beat milk, sugar, eggs, cinnamon, allspice and vanilla together.
Mix bread crumbs until soft.
Add sweet potatoes; mix well, then add nuts, coconut and raisins.
Pour into 9 x 12-inch baking pan.
Cook in oven at 350° for 45 minutes.

_enrichment.description: A rich and spiced sweet potato bread pudding with coconut, raisins, and nuts, offering a comforting dessert with autumnal flavours.

title: Jerk Pork Chops
ingredients: 4 bone in loin pork chops (8 oz. each), 1 inch thick
directions: In small bowl, mix together scallions, garlic, oil, lemon juice, honey, allspice, thyme, salt, pepper, cinnamon, nutmeg and hot sauce for jerk seasoning.
Place pork chops in large glass dish.
Rub jerk seasoning over both sides.
Cover and refrigerate for 2 hours.
Heat oven to 400 degree.
Heat grill pan over high heat.
Place chops on grill pan; cook 2 minutes per side.
Place chops in baking dish.
Bake in 400 degree oven for 18 to 20 minutes depending on thickness, or until internal temperature registers 155 degrees.
Let meat rest 10 minutes.
Serve with buttered red skinned potatoes, string beans and carrots.

_enrichment.description: Jerk-marinated pork chops with a blend of allspice, thyme, and scotch bonnet, grilled or roasted for a spicy, aromatic main course.

title: Jason'S Jerky
directions: Mix all spices together.
Trim fat off meat.
Cut into thin strips.
Place meat and spice mix in Ziploc bag.
Let marinate overnight in refrigerator.
Place on broiler pan.
Bake at 200° in oven until tough and dry.
Store in refrigerator for long storage.

_enrichment.description: A simple deer jerky with a smoky, spicy marinade, dried for a chewy, protein-rich snack.

title: Microwave Beef Jerky
ingredients: 1 1/2 lb. meat, cut in strips (top round roast is best)
directions: Mix all ingredients and put in a large bowl.
Add meat strips; let set overnight.
Drain and put on paper towels.
Lay on microwave bacon tray.
Cook on High 3 to 4 minutes.
Turn and cook 3 to 4 minutes.

_enrichment.description: A quick and unconventional method to make beef jerky using a microwave, perfect for a savoury snack with a smoky kick.

title: Caribbean Chicken
directions: Heat oil in skillet.
Brown chicken. Reduce heat to low.
Add remaining ingredients; mix well.
Simmer, covered, for 1 hour, stirring and adding water as necessary. Serve over rice.

_enrichment.description: A simple Caribbean-inspired chicken dish with a rich, slightly tangy sauce, ideal for a comforting family meal.

title: Jerk Chicken
ingredients: 1 Tbsp. minced fresh ginger plus 1 1/2 tsp. ground ginger or 2 tsp. ground ginger
directions: Preheat oven to 400°.
In a large bowl, combine teriyaki sauce, garlic, ginger, pepper and salt.
Add chicken; toss to mix well.
Lift chicken out of sauce and place in oiled baking dish, skin side down.
Bake for 45 minutes, turning once and basting frequently with remaining sauce until brown and crispy.
Can also be cooked on the grill.

_enrichment.description: A fusion of teriyaki and jerk flavours, this chicken dish is baked until tender and glazed with a sweet-spicy sauce.

title: Sweet Potato Snowballs
directions: Preheat oven to 400°.
Lightly butter a cookie sheet.
In a bowl, blend sweet potatoes, brown sugar, butter and allspice. Roll marshmallows separately in mixture, forming balls.
Roll each into coconut.
Place on a baking sheet and cook 5 to 8 minutes or until marshmallows heat but don't melt.

_enrichment.description: Sweet and creamy sweet potato balls rolled in coconut, perfect as a light dessert or afternoon treat.

title: Caribbean Jerk Chicken
ingredients: 4 whole chicken breasts (6 to 8 oz. each)
directions: *Scotch bonnet peppers range from red and orange to yellow green.
Shaped like a Scotchman's bonnet, they are the preferred island pepper and very hot.
They usually can be found only in Hispanic markets in the Milwaukee area.
Jalapeno or Serrano peppers may be substituted.

_enrichment.description: A bold and aromatic Caribbean jerk chicken with a complex blend of spices, best served with rice or flatbread.

title: West African Groundnut Stew
ingredients: 2 lb. beef cubes (1-inch or smaller), rolled in flour
directions: In large heavy kettle, heat oil; add meat.
When meat is browned, add onion, garlic, nutmeg, chili powder, sliced onion, tomato paste, water and red pepper.
Simmer until meat is tender. A half hour before serving, heat peanut butter and oil in small saucepan.
Stir over medium heat 5 minutes.
Add beef stew and simmer over low heat 20 minutes.
Serve over rice.

_enrichment.description: A hearty West African stew with tender beef and peanut butter, offering a rich, nutty flavour and creamy texture.

title: Nigerian African Stew
directions: Cut meat into small cubes and place in pot of water 2 inches above meat.
Next add all ingredients, except tomatoes, tomato paste, tomato sauce, tomato puree and spinach.
When meat is almost cooked, add tomatoes, tomato paste, puree and tomato sauce as well as spinach.
(Cooking time is approximately 1 hour.) Serve over cooked white rice.
Serves 10 to 12.

_enrichment.description: A robust Nigerian stew packed with mixed meats and vegetables, served over rice for a filling meal.

title: Kyenam(African Fish Dish)
ingredients: 1 Tbsp. ground dried shrimp or anchovy
salt and red hot pepper to taste
directions: Soak bay leaves, peppercorns and cloves in 1/3 cup of oil overnight.
Rub cleaned fish with salt and black pepper and then the flavored oil.
Bake in a hot pan or in the oven or grill on a charcoal fire.
Then fry onion, tomatoes, tomato paste and red hot pepper in the rest of the oil.
Add dried shrimp or anchovy. Place the cooked fish on a bed of plain, boiled rice and coat with the tomato, onion and pepper sauce.
Decorate with French-fried onions, sliced green peppers, fried plaintain, sliced hard-boiled eggs, etc.
Serves 4.

_enrichment.description: A flavourful African fish dish with aromatic spices, served with rice and garnished with fried plantains and peppers.

title: African Beef Stew
ingredients: 1/2 c. chunky or smooth peanut butter
1/4 tsp. ground red pepper (cayenne) or to taste
2 1/2 c. frozen chunks butternut squash from 20 oz. bag
directions: Heat oil in a large, preferably nonstick skillet. Stir in onions and cook over medium-high heat 4 to 5 minutes until translucent.
Add beef and cook 5 minutes, stirring with a fork to break up chunks, or until browned.
Drain off fat.
Stir in broth, peanut butter, tomato paste, salt and red pepper until well blended (mixture will thicken).
Stir in squash, reduce heat, cover and simmer 5 to 7 minutes until
squash is hot.
Uncover and simmer 5 minutes longer for flavors to blend.

_enrichment.description: A simple African-inspired beef stew with peanut butter, offering a creamy and slightly spicy flavour.

title: African Stew(International:  Africa)
ingredients: 2 (4 lb.) boneless, skinless chicken breasts, cut into pieces
directions: Brown chicken and onions in oil until completely done, stirring frequently.
Drain chicken.
Retain 2 tablespoons of oil.
Place chicken in oil and remaining ingredients, except flour, in large pot.
Cover the pot and simmer over medium heat for 45 to 50 minutes.

_enrichment.description: A versatile African stew with chicken, potatoes, and tomatoes, simmered in a rich broth.

title: Ibo Stew(Nigerian)
ingredients: Beef stew mix or Onion soup mix
your choice of spices or the items below
directions: *Tomato Puree:
Vegetable oil and tomato paste.
Heat oil in pan. Add tomato paste and stir.
Allow to heat thoroughly while continuously stirring.

_enrichment.description: A Nigerian stew with beef and tomato puree, offering a deep, savoury flavour with a hint of spice.

title: African Stew
ingredients: 3 large sweet potatoes cut in chunks
1 (12 oz.) can condensed low fat milk
directions: Brown beef in skillet then place in stew pot.
Add vegetables, tomatoes and spices.
Cook until tender. Add warm stew juice to the peanut butter.
Add mixture to stew pot.
Before serving, add condensed milk.
1/2 hour prep time.
1 1/4 hours cooking time.

_enrichment.description: A rich African stew with beef, sweet potatoes, and peanut butter, finished with condensed milk for a creamy touch.

title: African Rum Buns
ingredients: 10 oz. coconut flakes, raisins or dried cherries
directions: Mix butter, flour and all dry ingredients.
Add eggs, rum and vanilla extract.
Mix and add milk.
Drop 1 tablespoon batter onto 2 greased cookie sheets.
Bake at 400° for about 20 to 25 minutes.

_enrichment.description: Sweet and spiced buns with rum and coconut, perfect for a festive treat or dessert.

title: African Froth
ingredients: 2 dried apricots, dates or fresh strawberries (optional)
honey or a touch of sugar to taste
directions: Combine in blender the first 4 ingredients.
Add apricots, dates or fresh strawberries.
Blend until smooth, adding honey or a touch of sugar for taste.
Pour into two 8 ounce glasses and serve with shredded coconut sprinkled over the top.

_enrichment.description: A creamy and refreshing African froth made with coconut, milk, and fruit, ideal for a light dessert.

title: South African Gourmet Salad
ingredients: 8 (3 oz.) South African rock lobster tails
directions: Drop frozen rock lobster tails into boiling salted water.
When water re-boils, cook for 3 minutes.
Drain immediately; drench with cold water and cut away underside membrane.
Remove meat from shells.
Chill.
Boil potatoes; drain and peel.
While still warm, cut into cubes in bottom of large bowl.
Add onion, salt and pepper immediately.
Mix mayonnaise with cream until smooth.
Pour over potatoes and onions and leave in a thick top layer.
Cool, then chill.
At serving time, cut chilled rock lobster meat into bite size pieces.
Add to potatoes in bowl and toss together until all ingredients are well blended.
Yield:
8 servings.

_enrichment.description: A gourmet South African salad featuring lobster, potatoes, and a creamy dressing, perfect for a special occasion.

title: African Rice Dishes
ingredients: whole cabbage, 1 or 2 bunches collards, 2 or 3 frozen collards or sweet potato leaves
directions: Wilt greens in hot oil.
Do not brown.
Add water to cover. Cook rice according to the directions on box.
Set aside, keeping rice hot.
Add 2 tablespoons tomato paste.
Add 3 or 4 beef bouillon cubes, crushed.
Add meat, halfway cooked.
Simmer 1 hour or until meat is tender.
Serve over hot rice.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for African Rice Dishes.

title: African Peanut Soup
ingredients: 1/4 bunch fresh spinach, drained and cut in small pieces
1 soup can milk or heavy cream
directions: Saute onion, spinach and mushrooms.
Combine all ingredients and heat.
Serves 2 people.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for African Peanut Soup.

title: Peruvian Potato Salad
ingredients: 3 oz. can ripe pitted black olives
directions: Simmer scrubbed potatoes in large pan with 1-inch of boiling, salted water for 25 to 30 minutes, until tender.
Drain, cool and remove skins.
In large saucepan over low heat, stir cream cheese and half and half until smooth.
Add chopped chili peppers, lemon juice, turmeric, comino and salt.
Simmer 1 minute, stirring occasionally.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Peruvian Potato Salad.

title: Peruvian Lomo Saltado
ingredients: 1/2 c. bell pepper (yellow or red) chopped
1 tsp. salt and pepper to taste
directions: Heat oil in a non-stick pan and saute onion, garlic and bell pepper.
Cook potato, stirring occasionally, until potato just begins to brown.
Add chili and steak.
Saute a few more minutes, stir in tomato, oregano, salt and pepper.
Serve with steamed rice.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Peruvian Lomo Saltado.

title: Peruvian Chicken
directions: Mix together marinade.
Marinate chicken for 2 hours.
Bake at 350° for 25 minutes if boneless, 45 minutes with bone.
Serve over rice or with potatoes.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Peruvian Chicken.

title: Rice Peruvian-Style
ingredients: 8 pieces bacon, fried crisp and cut into fine pieces with scissors
3 eggs, scrambled dry and cut into small squares
directions: In deep pot, saute garlic in oil and salt.
Add water and bring to a boil.
Add rice and return to a boil.
Reduce to a simmer, cover and cook 20 minutes or until rice is cooked.
Mix in onions, bacon and eggs.
Add a little soy sauce, mix well and serve.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Rice Peruvian-Style.

title: Shrimp Ceviche
ingredients: 1/4 medium red onion, sliced paper thin
directions: Add to ingredients, one 6 ounce can shrimp, drained and rinsed.
Serve
over
rice, cooked according to package directions.
Season to taste with Tabasco sauce.
Serves 4.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Shrimp Ceviche.

title: Ceviche
directions: Cut uncooked fish into small pieces.
Add lime juice to cover fish; cover and refrigerate 3 to 4 hours, or until fish become opaque.
Stir occasionally.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Ceviche.

title: Eggless Milkless Applesauce Cake
ingredients: 1 tsp. each: cinnamon, cloves and nutmeg
directions: Mix Crisco with applesauce, nuts and raisins.
Sift dry ingredients and add.
Mix well.
Put in a greased and floured loaf pan or tube pan.
Bake in loaf pan at 350° to 375° for 45 to 60 minutes, layer pan at 375° for 20 minutes or tube pan at 325° for 1 hour.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Eggless Milkless Applesauce Cake.

title: Potato Casserole
directions: Peel and dice potatoes; place in bowl of cold, salted water. Drain potatoes and dry on paper towels.
Mix 1/2 cup butter, onions, soup, sour cream and cheese; stir well.
Add potatoes to mixture and pour into a 2 1/2-quart casserole dish.
Crush cornflakes, mix with remaining butter.
Sprinkle cornflakes and butter on top of potato mixture.
Bake at 350° for 50 minutes.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Potato Casserole.

title: Sesame Ginger Chicken
directions: Combine first 4 ingredients in a small bowl, stir well and set aside.
Place chicken between 2 sheets of waxed paper or heavy duty plastic wrap, and flatten to 1/4-inch thickness, using meat mallet or rolling pin.
Coat grill rack with cooking spray; place on grill over coals.
Place chicken on rack and cook 4 minutes on each side, basting frequently with soy sauce mixture.
Transfer chicken to a serving platter and garnish with green onion, if desired.
Yields 4 servings, about 200 calories.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Sesame Ginger Chicken.

title: Baked Beans
directions: Cook onions and bell pepper in oil until onions are transparent.
Add beef and cook until brown.
Mix all ingredients together.
Bake in 350° oven for 45 minutes, or after mixing together, pour in crock-pot and cook 6 to 10 hours on low.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Baked Beans.

title: Don Juan'S Sangria
