ingredients: 3 chicken breasts, skinned, deboned and halved
directions: Flatten chicken slightly and dust with flour.
Mix eggs with Parmesan cheese, 2 tablespoons parsley, salt and pepper. Dip chicken in egg mixture.
Heat oil in pan and brown chicken on both sides over medium-high heat. Discard oil and wipe pan out.
Melt butter in pan. Add chicken broth and 2 tablespoons parsley. Squeeze juice from lemon in sauce and put lemon in sauce.
Return chicken to pan.
Simmer 10 minutes or until tender.
Add wine and pepper.
Simmer 5 minutes. Remove to serving dish.
Pour sauce over chicken and garnish with lemon and parsley.

_enrichment.description: Juicy chicken breasts coated in Parmesan and herbs, simmered in a lemony Marsala wine sauce.

title: Breakfast Tortillas
directions: Fry tortillas in oil until browned.
Set on paper towel to drain.
Melt butter in frying pan.
In bowl, add all other ingredients except bacon and stir until well mixed.
Add mixture to frying pan, stirring constantly over medium heat until well mixed.
Add mixture to frying pan, stirring constantly over medium heat until cheese is melted.
Put on tortillas and top with bacon. Makes 2 servings.

_enrichment.description: Scrambled eggs with melted cheese, green onions, and chillies, wrapped in warm tortillas for a quick breakfast.

title: Quick And Easy Caesar Salad
ingredients: 1 head Romaine or green leaf lettuce
directions: In a large salad bowl place lettuce (torn into bite-size pieces), cucumber, 1/2 box of croutons, 1/2 of cheese, salt and pepper.
Do not add dressing until you are ready to serve salad. Shake dressing well and pour approximately 1/2 to 3/4 of dressing on salad.
Add salt and pepper to taste.
Toss salad well.
Garnish individual servings with tomatoes and onion.
Sprinkle cheese on top, if desired.

_enrichment.description: A classic Caesar salad with crisp romaine, fresh cucumber, and crunchy croutons, finished with Parmesan cheese and tomatoes for a refreshing starter.

title: Pistachio Marble Cake
directions: Combine all ingredients except for the chocolate syrup in large mixer bowl.
Beat at medium speed of electric mixer for 2 minutes.
Pour 1/3 of batter in a separate bowl and stir in chocolate syrup.
Spoon batters alternately into greased and floured 10-inch tube pan.
Zig zag spatula through batter to marble.
Bake at 350° for 50 minutes or until center springs back when lightly touched.
Cool 15 minutes before removing from pan.

_enrichment.description: A moist and marbled cake combining the nutty flavour of pistachio with rich chocolate, perfect for a simple yet impressive dessert.

title: Hot Milk Cake
directions: Blend sugar and eggs together and beat until creamy.
Put milk and butter in small pan and heat until warm.
Add flour to sugar and egg mix and add milk and butter a little at a time until all used up.
Add baking powder and vanilla; mix a little more.
Put in two 9-inch pans and bake in 350° oven until brown, about 35 minutes.
Use any frosting.

_enrichment.description: A light and tender sponge cake made with hot milk for a delicate crumb, ideal for pairing with any frosting.

title: Arizona Baked Frijoles
directions: Cover beans with cold water; let stand overnight.
In the morning, drain and cook with fresh water to which salt and soda have been added.
Let the beans simmer until tender.
Place onions and honey in the bottom of a casserole and cover them with the drained beans.
Add seasonings.
Rub the lid of the casserole thoroughly with the halved clove of garlic.
Cover and bake at 450° until completely tender.

_enrichment.description: A hearty baked bean dish with tender pink beans, sweet onions, and a touch of honey and spices for a comforting side or main.

title: Sauteed Cabbage And Apples
directions: Peel, core and chop apples.
Melt butter (bacon drippings may be used if you prefer).
Add apples, cabbage, onions, Season-All and nutmeg.

Mix well. Cover and cook over low heat 20 minutes, stirring frequently. Combine vinegar, sugar and red pepper. Add to cabbage. Cook 5 minutes longer. Stir in nuts or serve with nuts sprinkled over top.
This is one of those dishes you will find even better when reheated. Excellent served with pork, duckling, goose and game.
Serves 4 to 5.

_enrichment.description: A sweet and savoury dish of tender cabbage and tart apples, lightly seasoned with nutmeg and vinegar for a unique side.

title: Cheesy Chicken Casserole
ingredients: 2 (10 oz.) pkg. frozen broccoli spears, thawed
10 to 12 slices cooked chicken or turkey
directions: Cut broccoli spears into bite size pieces and place in bottom of greased crock-pot.
In saucepan, blend milk, cream cheese, salt, garlic salt and Parmesan cheese over low heat until melted and smooth.
Pour one cup over broccoli; top with sliced chicken. Pour remaining sauce over chicken.
Cover and cook on low 4 to 6 hours.
Serves 4 to 6.

_enrichment.description: A creamy and cheesy casserole with tender chicken and broccoli, baked until bubbly for a comforting family meal.

title: Whitefish Poached In Wine
ingredients: mushrooms, 1 c. or to taste, sauteed in butter
lemon juice, salt and pepper to taste
directions: In buttered pie pan put 1 tablespoon shallots on bottom, layer fish on top in single layer.
Cover with remaining shallots and the mushrooms, dot with butter, salt and pepper.
Pour wine and water over just barely to cover.
Bring to low simmer on stove; cover with wax paper coated with butter.
Put in bottom third of oven, preheated to 350° for 8 to 12 minutes.

_enrichment.description: Delicate whitefish poached in wine with shallots and mushrooms, finished with a creamy Swiss cheese sauce for an elegant main.

title: Georgia Pecan Pie
directions: Beat eggs until foamy.
Add sugar, syrup, melted butter and vanilla; beat until mixture thickens.
Add nuts and pour into a pie shell.
Bake for 10 minutes at 450°, then reduce heat to 325° and bake until filling is firm (approximately 35 to 40 minutes).

_enrichment.description: A rich and gooey pecan pie with a buttery crust, perfect for a festive dessert with a caramelised filling.

title: Baked Pineapple Chicken
directions: Drain pineapple; reserve juice.
Combine reserved juice with garlic, cornstarch, Worcestershire sauce, mustard and rosemary. Arrange chicken in shallow baking dish on broiler-proof dish, skin side up.
Broil until browned.
Stir sauce; pour over chicken.
Bake at 400° for 30 minutes.

_enrichment.description: Juicy chicken breasts baked with pineapple and a tangy mustard sauce, served with a side of the fruit for a sweet and savoury main.

title: Velvet Crumb Cake
ingredients: fresh fruit and whipped cream or Cool Whip
directions: Grease and flour an 8 x 8 x 2-inch square pan or a 9-inch round pan.
Beat all ingredients until well blended and pour into pan. Bake at 350° for 25 to 30 minutes or until wooden toothpick inserted in center comes out clean.
Cool.
Serve with fresh fruit and whipped cream or with Cool Whip as desired.

_enrichment.description: A simple and moist crumb cake with a tender texture, best served with fresh fruit and whipped cream for a light dessert.

title: Cornpatch Spoon Bread
ingredients: 3/4 c. white or yellow corn meal
directions: Stir boiling water into corn meal, mixing thoroughly.
Add remaining ingredients.
Stir quickly until just mixed.
Place in very hot, generously buttered skillet or casserole.
Bake in moderate oven at 375° about 40 minutes or until nicely done. Serves 5 to 6.

_enrichment.description: A creamy and slightly sweet spoon bread made with cornmeal and eggs, baked until golden for a comforting side dish.

title: Granny'S Tea Cakes
directions: Cream shortening, margarine and sugar in mixer bowl until light and fluffy.
Blend in eggs, vanilla, buttermilk, baking powder, soda and salt.
Add flour gradually until mixture forms dough which can be easily rolled.
Roll to 3/8 inch thickness on floured surface.
Cut as desired.
Place on ungreased cookie sheet.
Bake at 350° for 8 to 10 minutes or until light brown. Cool on wire rack.
Yield: 6 to 8 servings.

_enrichment.description: Classic buttery tea cakes with a tender crumb, perfect for afternoon tea or a simple sweet treat.

title: Butterhorns
directions: Blend the yeast, 1 tablespoon sugar and lukewarm water together in a small bowl; set aside.
Combine the sifted flour, salt, butter and shortening in a large mixing bowl.
Cut butter and shortening into the flour and salt mixture with a pastry blender.
Beat the egg yolks slightly with a fork; add to flour mixture, along with yeast mixture.
Mix well.
Beat the egg whites until foamy.
Begin adding 1/2 cup sugar very gradually; beat until mixture holds a stiff peak.

_enrichment.description: Flaky and buttery crescent-shaped pastries with a light, airy texture, ideal for breakfast or a sweet snack.

title: Low-Fat Hash Brown Casserole
ingredients: 1 (10 oz.) Campbell's fat-free cream of mushroom soup
1 (24 oz.) pkg. frozen potatoes O'Brien with green peppers and onions
1/2 c. Weight Watchers fat-free Parmesan cheese
directions: Combine soup, chicken broth, sour cream, Molly McButter, salt and pepper in a large bowl; mix well.
Add hash browns and mix. Pour into a large casserole dish sprayed with nonstick cooking spray.
Sprinkle Parmesan on top.
Bake at 350° for 40 to 45 minutes.

_enrichment.description: A low-fat casserole with crispy hash browns and a creamy sauce, topped with Parmesan for a comforting side dish.

title: Microwave Maple Nut Divinity
directions: Combine sugars, corn syrup and water in buttered large glass mixing bowl.
Microwave for 15 to 17 minutes on roast or until candy forms a hard ball in cold water (250°).
Meanwhile, beat egg whites in large bowl until stiff peaks.
Pour candy syrup in thin stream into egg whites; beat constantly with mixer.
Add maple flavoring and salt; beat until mixture is stiff and dull.
Fold in nuts.
Drop mixture by teaspoonfuls onto wax paper.
Cool.

_enrichment.description: A quick microwave divinity candy with nuts and maple flavour, perfect for a sweet treat with a chewy texture.

title: Company'S Comin'
ingredients: 1 (8 oz.) pkg. noodles, cooked and drained
directions: Cook beef; add next 2 ingredients and cook 2 to 3 minutes.
Add next 7 ingredients and simmer for a few minutes.
Add next 3 (last) ingredients and mix well.
Put in shallow baking dish (3-quart size) and bake at 350° for 3/4 to 1 hour.

_enrichment.description: A comforting American casserole with ground beef, creamy soups, and noodles, baked until bubbly and golden.

title: Chocolate Marble Cheesecake
ingredients: 3 pkg. (8 oz. each) cream cheese, softened
directions: Prepare Graham Crust; cool.
Combine cream cheese, sour cream, 1 cup sugar and 2 teaspoons vanilla in a large mixer bowl.
Beat on medium speed until smooth.
Add flour, a tablespoon at a time, blending well.
Add eggs, one at a time, beating well after each addition.
Reserve 2 cups of batter; pour remaining batter into crust.

_enrichment.description: A decadent American cheesecake with a marbled chocolate and vanilla swirl, topped with a graham crust.

title: Franks And Sauerkraut
directions: Combine sauerkraut, caraway seed, 1 teaspoon brown sugar, onion slices and margarine.
Place in shallow square baking dish.
Cut unpeeled apples into halves and remove cores.
Brush cut surfaces with margarine and sprinkle with brown sugar.
Arrange apple halves on the sauerkraut in a diagonal line across the center of the casserole.
Place 2 frankfurters on each side of the apple halves. Bake at 350° for 30 to 35 minutes.
Serve immediately.

_enrichment.description: A hearty German-inspired dish of sauerkraut, apples, and frankfurters, baked until caramelised and tender.

title: Bread Pudding For A Crowd(Abington Grange Supper)
directions: Heat the milk in double boiler (I use 2 mixing bowls set in large kettles) until bubbly around the edges.
The butter can be melted in the milk.
Beat eggs lightly, add sugar and flavorings. Put bread cubes in baking pan (10 x 18 x 2 1/2-inches).
Mix hot milk, eggs and flavorings.
Pour over bread.
Sprinkle raisins on top and try to submerge them somewhat with a rubber spatula.
This will prevent them from sinking to the bottom or clumping together. Put your large broiler pan filled with water on lower oven rack and set pudding pan on a rack above.
Bake about 1 hour or until a dinner knife inserted in the middle comes out clean.
Serves 36.

_enrichment.description: A classic British bread pudding, rich with custard and studded with raisins, perfect for feeding a crowd.

title: Oyster Chowder
ingredients: 1 tsp. Old Bay or seafood seasoning
directions: Drain oysters, reserving liquor and chop.
Arrange alternate layers of celery, rice and oysters in a kettle; season with salt, pepper and Old Bay.
Add water and oyster liquid; simmer 1/2 hour. Mix egg yolks and milk and add to chowder.
Heat below boiling point for only a few minutes and serve piping hot.
If allowed to cook too long after adding the eggs, it will curdle.
Serves 6.

_enrichment.description: A creamy American oyster chowder with rice, celery, and a touch of egg yolk for richness.

title: Impossible Vegetable Pie
ingredients: 1 pkg. frozen chopped broccoli, thawed and drained (not cooked)
directions: Mix broccoli, onion and green pepper; place in a lightly greased pie plate, 10 x 1 1/2-inches.
Beat remaining ingredients 1 minute with hand beater.
Pour into pie plate; bake 40 minutes at 400° until knife comes out clean.
Let stand 5 minutes before cutting.

_enrichment.description: A quick and easy American vegetable pie with broccoli, cheese, and a Bisquick batter crust.

title: Easy Microwave Beef Stew
directions: Place first eleven ingredients in microwave covered dish. Microwave at High for 10 minutes, Medium-high for 10 minutes and Low for 30 minutes.
Stir.
Add more water, if necessary. Microwave until tender.
Add vegetables.
Microwave at High until tender.
Gradually sprinkle four tablespoons of flour over stew while stirring.
Cook (microwave) until thickened, stirring once. May also be cooked conventionally.

_enrichment.description: A speedy microwave beef stew with tender beef, vegetables, and a flour-thickened gravy.

title: Two Minute Fudge
directions: In a 1 1/2 quart microwave safe dish, stir together the sugar, cocoa, salt, milk and vanilla.
Mixture will be stiff.
Top with the stick of butter.
Microwave at high for two minutes. Stir until smooth and blend in the nuts.
Pour into 8-inch square dish lined with wax paper and refrigerate until firm.
Cut into squares.

_enrichment.description: A quick American fudge made in the microwave, rich with chocolate and nuts.

title: Chocolate Dip
directions: Break chocolate into a bowl, heat some water in the saucepan over a low heat, until it just begins to bubble.
Keeping the heat on low, place the bowl over the saucepan.
Stir the chocolate with a wooden spoon until it completely melts.
Turn off the heat. Very carefully move the saucepan and the bowl from the stove to a mat or dish towel.

_enrichment.description: A simple American chocolate dip for fruit and nuts, melted gently over a bain-marie.

title: Country-Style Baked Ham
ingredients: 1 c. Alaga syrup (heavy cane syrup, do not substitute)
1 1/2 lb. beef tenderloin or round steak, cut in bite size pieces
directions: Combine the red pepper, garlic powder and seasoned salt in a shallow bowl.
Add the beef and mix with hands to coat each piece. Preheat the broiler (in the bottom of the stove) or prepare the barbecue grill so that you have hot coals.
Thread the meat, alternating with onions, onto 12-inch skewers.
Broil in the oven for 4 to 5 minutes or until the meat is tender.
Cook on the grill, on all sides, until the meat is tender.

_enrichment.description: A sweet and spicy American baked ham with a citrusy glaze, perfect for festive occasions.

title: Park Avenue Cake
directions: Line a 9 x 13-inch pan with double thickness of wax paper. Sift dry ingredients together.
Beat egg whites until frothy and add 1/2 cup sugar making high peaked meringue.
Cream lard and add sugar.
Beat until fluffy and add milk and dry ingredients alternately.
Combine with meringue and put in pan.
Bake at 350° for 30 to 35 minutes.

_enrichment.description: A light and airy American cake with a meringue base, topped with black walnuts.

title: Yum Yum Meat Loaf
directions: Preheat oven to 350°.
Mix meat, chopped celery, green pepper, onion and carrot in a mixing bowl.
Add egg, cracker crumbs, salt, red pepper and black pepper.
Mix thoroughly with generous amounts of ketchup.

_enrichment.description: A classic American meatloaf with a ketchup glaze, packed with vegetables for extra flavour.

title: Fresh Broccoli Soup
ingredients: 1 1/4 c. (5 oz.) shredded sharp American cheese
directions: Trim off large leaves of broccoli and remove tough ends of lower stalks.
Wash broccoli thoroughly and chop.
Place broccoli in a Dutch oven; add water to depth of 1 inch.
Bring to a boil; cover, reduce heat and simmer 10 to 12 minutes or until tender. Drain and set aside.

_enrichment.description: A creamy American broccoli soup with a cheesy finish, perfect for a light meal.

title: Lace Cookies
directions: Mix dry ingredients; add melted butter and stir.
Add egg and vanilla (batter will be runny and can be refrigerated overnight). Put aluminum foil over cookie sheets.
Drop 1/2 teaspoon of batter on foil.
Bake at 350° for 8 to 10 minutes.
(Be sure to keep watch; cookies will burn easily.)
Let cool completely, then peel cookies off foil.

_enrichment.description: Crispy American lace cookies made with oats, perfect for tea time or a sweet snack.

title: New England Clam Chowder
ingredients: 1/2 lb. White Rose potatoes (unpeeled), chopped into 1/2-inch cubes
2 Tbsp. fresh thyme or 1 Tbsp. dried
2 (8 oz.) cans whole baby clams with liquid
directions: Saute the bacon with the onion until onion is translucent (about 3 minutes over medium heat).
Add potatoes.
Cook another 3 to 4 minutes.
Add cream, thyme and bay leaves and bring to boil. Add clams and liquid.
Cook slowly for 5 to 7 minutes over medium heat until heated through and reaches desired consistency.
Add salt and pepper to taste.

_enrichment.description: A classic New England clam chowder with potatoes, bacon, and a creamy broth.

title: Chicken And Asparagus
ingredients: 1 (10 3/4 oz.) can condensed cream of chicken soup (undiluted)
directions: Place partially cooked asparagus in a greased 9-inch square baking dish.
In a skillet over medium heat, brown the chicken in oil on both sides.
Season with salt and pepper.
Arrange chicken over asparagus.
In a bowl, mix soup, mayonnaise and lemon juice; pour over chicken.
Cover and bake at 375° for 40 minutes or until the chicken is tender and juices run clear.
Sprinkle with cheese. Makes 4 servings.

_enrichment.description: A creamy casserole featuring tender chicken and fresh asparagus, baked with a rich cheese sauce for a comforting family meal.

title: Calypso Java Bread
directions: In mixing bowl, combine biscuit mix, brown sugar and flour. Dissolve coffee powder in milk; add all at once to dry ingredients, along with banana and egg.
Beat until blended.
Stir in dates and nuts.
Grease bottoms of two 1 pound coffee cans. Divide batter between cans.
Bake in 350° oven for 50 minutes; cool 10 minutes.
Loosen sides of loaves with spatula; remove from cans.

_enrichment.description: A moist, coffee-infused quick bread studded with dates and walnuts, baked in coffee cans for a rustic, aromatic treat.

title: Dad'S Birthday Cake
directions: Cream shortening or oleo and sugar until fluffy.
Add egg yolks, one at a time.
Beat thoroughly.
Add dry ingredients alternately with milk and vanilla to creamed mixture, beating until smooth.
Fold in stiffly beaten egg whites.
Bake in moderate oven (350°) for 25 minutes in a 9 x 13 x 2-inch pan or two 9-inch round layer pans.

_enrichment.description: A classic vanilla sponge cake with a light, fluffy texture, perfect for birthdays or celebrations.

title: Oatmeal Raisin Cookies
directions: Mix flour with baking soda. Combine butter, sugars and pudding mix in a large mixer bowl. Beat until smooth and creamy.
Beat in eggs. Gradually add flour mixture, then stir in oats and raisins. (Batter will be stiff.)
Drop by rounded measuring teaspoonfuls onto ungreased baking sheets, about 2-inches apart.
Bake at 375° for 10 to 12 minutes.
Makes about 5 dozen.

_enrichment.description: Chewy oatmeal cookies packed with plump raisins, offering a hearty, wholesome snack or lunchbox treat.

title: Fruit Cake Cookies
directions: Mix all ingredients and chill 3 to 4 hours or overnight.
Drop about 1 teaspoon of mixture on lightly greased cookie sheet about 2-inches apart. Bake at 350° for about 8 minutes.
Let cook about 5 minutes before removing from cookie sheet.
Yields 3 to 4 dozen.

_enrichment.description: Soft, cake-like cookies studded with dates, pecans, and candied cherries, offering a fruity, spiced bite.

title: Apple Cinnamon Muffins
ingredients: 1 c. finely chopped apple (1 large), peeled and cored
directions: Preheat oven to 375°.
In a large bowl, combine flour, granulated sugar, baking powder, 1/2 teaspoon cinnamon and salt. In another bowl, beat egg with milk, then stir in apple and melted butter.
Add all at once to flour mixture and stir until just moistened.
Spoon batter into a greased muffin tin(s), filling each cup about 2/3 full.

_enrichment.description: Moist muffins bursting with fresh apple and a warm cinnamon-sugar topping, ideal for breakfast or a snack.

title: Reese Cup Cake
directions: In a saucepan on medium heat, combine and bring to a boil, first 5 ingredients.
In a bowl, combine next 3 ingredients; add to the other ingredients.
Bake at 350° for 25 minutes.
Let cool. Prepare peanut butter and oil.
Mix together and spread over cooled cake.

_enrichment.description: A decadent chocolate cake topped with a rich peanut butter glaze, combining two beloved flavours in one dessert.

title: Barbara'S Sugarless Apple Pie
directions: Combine lemon juice, apple juice, flour and cinnamon in a bowl. Mix with apples and raisins in a large bowl.
Line 10-inch pie plate with half the pastry.
Fill with apple mixture; dot with butter.
Cover with top crust; cut vents.
Bake at 425° for 40 to 45 minutes or until golden brown.

_enrichment.description: A sugar-free apple pie with a buttery pastry, filled with spiced apples and raisins for a lighter dessert option.

title: Lashlee'S Chili
directions: Let meat, hot water and fat cook until meat is tender.
Add garlic, chili powder, carman seed and cracker crumbs and oats. Cook on low for an hour.
Add tomato sauce or paste.
If desired, kidney beans can be added.

_enrichment.description: A hearty chilli with ground meat, kidney beans, and spices, slow-cooked for a rich, smoky flavour.

title: Disappearing Marshmallow Brownies
directions: Melt first 2 ingredients in a 3-quart saucepan over medium heat, stirring constantly.
Remove from heat and cool to lukewarm. Then add flour, brown sugar, baking powder, salt, vanilla and egg. Mix well.
Add marshmallows, chocolate bits and nuts.
Fold into batter until just combined, about 5 strokes.
Spread in a greased 9-inch pan.
Bake at 350° for 20 to 25 minutes.
Do not overbake. Center will be jiggly when done but will be all right when cool.

_enrichment.description: Fudgy brownies with a marshmallow swirl and chocolate chunks, offering a gooey, indulgent treat.

title: Carl'S Chicken Delights
directions: Add margarine or butter to heated large skillet.
Then add chicken, green and red peppers, onion, salt and pepper.
Cover and let simmer for about 30 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Drain most of the juices; leave a small portion of the juice in the pan, then add the barbecue sauce.
Serve over pasta or rice.

_enrichment.description: Tender chicken simmered with colourful peppers and onions in a savoury sauce, served over pasta or rice.

title: Blueberry Buckle
directions: Put all but blueberries in mixing bowl and beat with electric mixer until a smooth batter.
Add and stir in berries.
Then measure together 1 cup sugar, 1 teaspoon cinnamon and 1/2 cup flour and crumble in 1/2 cup soft butter to sprinkle on top.
Pour into greased 13 x 9 x 2-inch pan or three smaller pans.
Bake in 375° oven for 45 to 50 minutes.

_enrichment.description: A blueberry-studded coffee cake with a cinnamon-sugar streusel topping, perfect for brunch or dessert.

title: Lukshen Kugel(Noodle Pudding)
directions: Grease a 9 x 13-inch pan.
Preheat oven to 375°.
Cook the noodles.

While the water is coming to a boil and the noodles are cooking, blend 1 cup cottage cheese, egg yolks, sugar and vanilla. Beat the egg whites until they are stiff, but not too hard (you know, the soft peaks that sort of slump over when they get too high).
By now, the noodles are about done.

_enrichment.description: A traditional Jewish noodle pudding with cottage cheese, raisins, and a cinnamon-spiced crumb topping.

title: Easy Salmon Cakes
ingredients: 1 (7.1 oz.) pouch Chicken of the Sea skinless and boneless pink salmon*
directions: In small mixing bowl, combine chopped red pepper, green onion, mayonnaise, lemon juice, seasoned salt and cayenne pepper. Adjust seasonings to personal preference.
Stir in egg, salmon and 4 tablespoons bread crumbs.
Form mixture into 6 to 8 balls.
Roll salmon balls in remaining bread crumbs; flatten into cakes, about 1/2-inch thick.
Fry salmon cakes in melted butter over medium heat for 3 to 4 minutes per side.
Makes 4 servings.

_enrichment.description: Crispy salmon cakes with a zesty mayonnaise-lemon dressing, served with a fresh, peppery crunch.

title: Tomato Gravy
ingredients: 1 (14-1/2 oz.) can whole or stewed tomatoes, undrained
directions: Heat bacon drippings in a large skillet.
Add flour.
Cook and stir over medium-low heat until a very dark roux forms.
Add tomatoes and mash with fork until smooth, stirring constantly.
Add Worcestershire sauce.
Add a bit of V-8 or tomato juice if more liquid is needed.
Gravy should be a rich red and slightly thick. Add hot sauce and salt and pepper to taste.

_enrichment.description: A rich, smoky tomato gravy made with bacon drippings and a dark roux, perfect for serving over biscuits or mashed potatoes.

title: Tropic Aroma Cake
directions: Need 3 layer pans (I use foil ones).
Mix sugar, shortening and eggs.
Add flour, baking powder and spices alternately with milk.
Pour 2/3 of the batter into 2 greased layer pans. To remaining mixture, add 1 tablespoon cocoa mixed with boiling water. This will be the middle layer.
Pour into third greased pan. Bake at 375° about 20 minutes.
Frost with Mocha Icing between layers and on top.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Tropic Aroma Cake.

title: M.K.C.'S Potato Salad
ingredients: 1/2 c. shredded Swiss cheese or Cheddar cheese
directions: Chop celery and purple onion fine.
Cook bacon and chop up bite size.
Mix first 11 ingredients in large mixing bowl; let set, covered, as you cook the other ingredients.
Boil medium size red potatoes with skin on until done, then slice into 4 to 6 pieces. Then fold both mixtures together; mix well.
Put in serving dish. Top with shredded Cheddar Swiss cheese; cover.
Put in refrigerator for 6 to 8 hours, then serve.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for M.K.C.'S Potato Salad.

title: Drop Cookies
directions: Cream together shortening and sugar; add beaten eggs and blend thoroughly.
Add baking powder and salt to flour and sift.
Add alternately with milk to creamed mixture.
Add nuts and milk only enough to blend.
Drop by teaspoons onto greased baking sheet. Bake at 400° for about 10 minutes.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Drop Cookies.

title: Chocolate Chip Pumpkin Cake
directions: Sift together on waxed paper and set aside flour, baking powder, baking soda, cinnamon and salt.
In separate large bowl, put sugar, eggs, pumpkin and oil.
Beat on high for about 2 minutes and gradually add ingredients that are set aside on waxed paper.
After all the ingredients are blended well, use a big spoon and stir in chocolate chips and chopped walnuts.
Grease and flour Bundt pan (even Teflon) and bake for 1 hour and 15 minutes at 350°.
Cool for 10 minutes and then remove from pan.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Chocolate Chip Pumpkin Cake.

title: Potato Butterhorn Rolls
ingredients: 1 3/4 c. lukewarm milk plus 1/4 c. for softening yeast
directions: In a large bowl, combine sugar, salt and butter with hot mashed potatoes.
Set aside to cool. Add eggs.
Soften yeast in 1/4 cup warm milk. Add the remaining milk to the potato mixture. Stir until blended.
Add flour, a little at a time, beating well after each addition.
Turn dough onto a lightly floured surface. Knead until dough is soft, smooth and elastic.
Gently place ball of dough into a large, well-greased bowl. Turn dough, greased side up, and set in a warm place until doubled in bulk, about 45 minutes.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Potato Butterhorn Rolls.

title: Chicken Yvonne
ingredients: 2 pkg. frozen broccoli (cook approximately 8 to 10 minutes)
directions: Bring to boil whole chicken breast in salted water and then simmer for	one hour. Butter casserole dish and line with cooked broccoli.
Add boned and skinned chicken breasts, cut up into small pieces. To make sauce, mix cream of chicken soup, lemon juice, mayonnaise, curry powder. Pour sauce over chicken and broccoli.
Sprinkle 1 cup shredded cheese (Longhorn or any kind of yellow cheese) over the top and add buttered bread crumbs (1/2 cup to 1 tablespoon melted butter).
Bake in 350° oven until browned on top.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Chicken Yvonne.

title: Peanut Blossoms
directions: Mix all ingredients, except for the 1 bowl of sugar.
Roll dough in 1-inch balls and roll in bowl of sugar.
Place on ungreased cookie sheet and bake at 375° for 10 to 12 minutes. While still hot from oven, press one Hershey's Kiss carefully into the top of each cookie.
Cool and enjoy!
This recipe makes about 2 1/2 dozen cookies.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Peanut Blossoms.

title: Chicken Breasts
directions: Marinate chicken breasts in eggs and garlic salt for 3 hours. Coat chicken in bread crumbs and brown on top of stove.
Lay flat in a 15 x 10-inch pan.
Mix bouillon cubes in 1 cup of water and pour over chicken.
Spread shredded cheeses over chicken.
Bake at 350° for 30 to 40 minutes.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Chicken Breasts.

title: Party Chicken Bake
ingredients: 1 (8 oz.) cream cheese, softened or warmed
1/2 c. cooking sherry or white wine
rice or noodles (great with wild rice pilaf)
directions: Melt butter or margarine in large skillet.
Add chicken breasts and sprinkle half of Italian dressing mix on top.
Saute. Turn chicken and add rest of dressing mix.
Cook until chicken is white throughout, but do not overcook.
Mix softened cream cheese, sour cream, soup, wine and milk with a whisk or beaters until smooth.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Party Chicken Bake.

title: Mother'S Fine Sugar Cookies
directions: In large bowl beat butter, sugars and salt until well blended. Add egg and vanilla and beat until fluffy.
Sift together flour, baking soda and cream of tartar.
Add to creamed mixture and blend well.
Chill for at least 2 hours.
Shape into small balls about 3/4 inch size and place on ungreased baking sheet 2 inches apart.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Mother'S Fine Sugar Cookies.

title: Supper On A Bun
directions: Combine all ingredients and put on half loaves of French bread.
Wrap foil around crust side.
Bake uncovered on cookie sheet for 25 minutes at 350°.
Garnish with strips of cheese and bake 5 minutes longer.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Supper On A Bun.

title: Chicken  Swirl Slices
ingredients: 5 large chicken breast halves, skinned and boned
5 thin slices Swiss cheese (about 4 x 4 inches)
directions: Heat oven to 350°.
Place 1 chicken breast, boned side up between 2 pieces of plastic wrap or waxed paper.
Working from center, gently pound chicken with rolling pin or flat side of meat mallet until about 1/8 to 1/4 inch thick.
Repeat with remaining chicken breast halves.
In a small bowl combine marjoram, thyme and rosemary.
Brush flattened chicken with melted butter; sprinkle each piece with herbs.
Place ham slice on each chicken piece; top with cheese slice.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Chicken  Swirl Slices.

title: Texas Barbecue Sauce
directions: Heat oil in a large saucepan and saute the onion for 5 minutes.
Add garlic and saute 1 more minute.
Add remaining ingredients and bring to boil.
Reduce heat and simmer for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Texas Barbecue Sauce.

title: Easy Macaroni Salad
ingredients: 1 c. Hellmann's or Best Foods real, light or cholesterol free reduced calorie mayonnaise
8 oz. elbow macaroni, cooked, rinsed with cold water and drained
1 c. chopped green or red bell pepper
directions: In large bowl, combine mayonnaise, vinegar, mustard, sugar, salt and black pepper.
Add macaroni, celery, green pepper and onion; toss to coat well.
Cover; chill.
Garnish, as desired. Makes about 8 servings.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Easy Macaroni Salad.

title: Low Cholesterol Mayonnaise
directions: In beater bowl, beat the whites with the sugar, salt, mustard, garlic powder and pepper until quite foamy.
Slowly begin to add 1/2 cup oil while beating on high.
You must add oil slowly or the mixture will not emulsify.
Now add 1 tablespoon of vinegar. Continue beating and adding oil on high until all of the oil is used.
Then beat in the last tablespoon vinegar.
Pour into covered jar and keep refrigerated.
This thickens more on chilling and is excellent for all salads.

_enrichment.description: A tasty recipe for Low Cholesterol Mayonnaise.

title: Ham And Egg Salad
directions: In a bowl, mix together the ham, celery, green pepper, egg, mayonnaise, ginger, lemon juice and mustard.
Stir to blend thoroughly.
Divide ham salad evenly and spread on bottoms of rolls.
Top with lettuce and then with tops of rolls.

_enrichment.description: A classic American salad featuring tender ham, crisp vegetables, and a tangy mayonnaise dressing, served in a soft roll with fresh lettuce.

title: Chocolate-Peanut Bars(Chewy And Crisp At The Same Time.)
directions: Combine syrup, sugar and salt in saucepan. Bring to a full boil.
Stir in peanut butter. Remove from heat.
Stir in vanilla, cereals and chocolate pieces.
Press into buttered 9 x 9 x 2-inch pan. Chill about 1 hour.
Cut in small bars or squares.
(For easy serving, store in refrigerator.) Makes about 2 dozen bars.

_enrichment.description: A delightful American treat combining chewy rice cereal and crisp chocolate-peanut layers, perfect for snacking or dessert.

title: Real Texas Chili
directions: Brown onion and garlic in bacon drippings; add meat and cook until gray.
Add tomato sauce, water and spices.
Bring to boil and then allow to simmer for at least an hour.
When done, add little flour until desired thickness is reached.
When ready to serve, add pinto beans (cooked) or black-eyed peas or hominy. Makes 6 to 8 servings.

_enrichment.description: A hearty Texan chilli with slow-cooked meat, spices, and beans, offering a rich and smoky flavour.

title: Oysters Rockefeller
ingredients: 1/4 c. finely chopped parsley and 1 Tbsp. celery, chopped
directions: Place oysters on a shallow baking tray lined with rock salt. Melt butter in frypan.
Add parsley, celery, onion and garlic. Add spinach and cook 1 minute more.
Blend all in blender.
Add bread crumbs, salt, pepper and sherry.
Sprinkle with grated Gruyere after adding 1 tablespoon of sauce to each oyster.
Bake at 450° for 4 to 5 minutes until oysters are heated through and sauce bubbles slightly.

_enrichment.description: A luxurious French-inspired dish of oysters topped with a creamy spinach and cheese sauce, baked until golden.

title: Bill'S Chicken Salad
ingredients: 1/2 c. fresh onion, minced or finely chopped
directions: Boil the chicken until completely tender (about an hour). Reserve about 1/4 cup of the broth.
Remove the chicken from the pot and allow to cool completely, then skin, bone, and dice or chop very small.
Mix ingredients well in a bowl that can be sealed airtight.
Seal and refrigerate at least four hours for flavors to blend.

_enrichment.description: A creamy American chicken salad with sweet pickles, fresh vegetables, and a tangy mustard dressing.

title: Seafood Linguini
ingredients: 4 shallots or green onions, finely chopped
1 1/2 lb. fresh, shelled shrimp (you can use crab meat and scallops or combination of all 3)
directions: In a 10-inch skillet, cook mushrooms and shallots in butter, uncovered, over medium-high heat for 4 to 5 minutes, or until vegetables are tender, but not brown.
Remove with slotted spoon; set aside.
Stir wine, tomato paste, tarragon, salt and pepper into butter in skillet.
Bring to boil; boil vigorously for 10 minutes, or until mixture is reduced to 1/2 cup.

_enrichment.description: A rich Italian seafood pasta dish with shrimp, mushrooms, and a creamy tarragon sauce.

title: Caramel Popcorn Balls
directions: Measure popped corn into large bowl.
Combine sugars, cornsyrup, water, vinegar and salt in 2 - quart sauce pan.
Heat to boiling over milium - high heat, stirring frequently.
Cook stirring constantly, to 260 degrees on candy thermometer (or until small amount of mixture dropped into very cold water forms a hard ball).
Reduce heat to low stir in butter until melted.
Pour syrup in thin stream over corn in bowl, stirring until corn is well coated.
Cool slightly.
Butter hands; shape mixture into 3 - inch balls and place on waxed paper.
Yields approximately 16 balls.

_enrichment.description: A classic American sweet treat of caramel-coated popcorn, formed into balls for easy snacking.

title: Green Pepper Steak
ingredients: 2 or 3 lb. beef chuck or round steak, fat trimmed
1 c. red and/or green peppers, cut into 1/2-inch squares
2 tomatoes, cut into wedges (can be omitted)
directions: With a very sharp knife, cut beef across grain into thin strips, 1/8-inch thick.
Combine soy sauce, garlic and ginger. Add beef.
Toss and set aside while preparing vegetables.
Heat oil in large frying pan or wok.
Add beef and toss over high heat until browned.
Cover and simmer for 40 to 60 minutes over low heat.

_enrichment.description: A quick and flavourful Chinese-inspired stir-fry with tender beef, green peppers, and a savoury sauce.

title: Aunty Ann'S Vegetable Chowder
directions: In a five-quart Dutch oven, heat 3 tablespoons of corn oil. Add onion, celery and garlic and saute for five minutes.
Add 1 cup chopped tomatoes and the carrots and cook for five minutes.
Add vegetable broth, can of whole tomatoes (chopped), cabbage, salt and pepper and simmer for 1 hour or until vegetables are tender.
Add zucchini and cook 10 minutes.
Add kidney beans and corn and heat.

_enrichment.description: A hearty American vegetable chowder with a mix of fresh vegetables and beans in a light broth.

title: Banana-Nut Raisin Bread
directions: Cream butter; gradually add sugar, beating well.
Add eggs, one at a time, beating well after each addition.
Add bananas, mixing until smooth.
Combine flour, baking powder, soda and salt. Add to creamed mixture, stirring just enough to moisten.
Stir in chopped pecans and raisins.

_enrichment.description: A moist and nutty American banana bread with raisins and pecans, perfect for breakfast or snacking.
